title: What Unilever shares with Google and Apple
summary: Unilever, a consumer-products giant, comes in at No. 3 on LinkedIn's list of most sought-after employers, just behind Google and Apple. CEO Paul Polman attributes this success to Unilever's strong sense of purpose, which attracts talent, especially Millennials, and drives the company's core strategy to positively impact the environment and public health. This purpose-driven approach, despite challenges from short-term investor pressures, has led to increased job applications, improved employee engagement, and ambitious sustainability goals.

title: Can the stylish folks at Warby Parker rescue Google Glass from dork purgatory?
summary: Google is reportedly collaborating with eyewear start-up Warby Parker to redesign Google Glass and improve its public image. The article discusses Google Glass's current image problem, where even models look dorky wearing it, and suggests that stylish design, similar to Warby Parker's aesthetic, could help integrate the technology into everyday life. It also compares Google Glass to Nike's FuelBand, highlighting the importance of subtle design in new technology adoption.

title: Exclusive: Fortune 500’s newest woman CEO talks about her first 90 days on the job
summary: Lisa Su, the first female CEO of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and one of 25 women CEOs in the Fortune 500, discusses her first 90 days leading the company. She outlines her vision for AMD to become a technology leader in diverse applications beyond just PCs, focusing on innovation and differentiating the company in the rapidly changing tech landscape. Su also shares her perspective on being a female CEO in the semiconductor industry and her aspirations for AMD's future success.

title: Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka gifted 3000 sets of iPhone 6 to employees
summary: Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka gifted 3000 sets of Apple iPhone 6 to top-performing employees on January 9, 2015, along with an e-mail. This gesture by Sikka is part of broader efforts to address the high attrition rate at Infosys, which stood at 20.1 percent as of September 2014. The company is also implementing other strategies to retain employees, such as quarterly promotions and increased employee involvement in decision-making.

title: How 7 major tech companies have performed under new CEOs
summary: This article reviews the performance of seven major tech companies—Apple, Google, Yahoo, Hewlett-Packard, AMD, Symantec, and BlackBerry—under their new CEOs appointed in the past two years. It assesses their financial results, stock performance, and strategic directions, highlighting both successes and ongoing struggles. The analysis provides a snapshot of how leadership changes impacted these tech giants.

title: What Microsoft is buying with its $7.2 billion Nokia purchase
summary: Microsoft announced its plan to acquire Nokia's handset and services business for $7.2 billion, aiming to strengthen its position in the mobile market. This move allows Microsoft to control the hardware for its Windows Phone operating system, similar to Apple's strategy, and potentially brings Stephen Elop back into a leadership role within the company. The acquisition is seen as a strategic bet for Microsoft to gain relevance in the mobile age, despite skepticism regarding its potential impact on market share.

title: Apple, Google, Intel, Adobe Reach New Deal in Employee Poaching Suit
summary: Apple, Google, Intel, and Adobe have agreed to pay $415 million to resolve a class-action lawsuit alleging they illegally colluded to prevent employee poaching. This new settlement revises an earlier $324.5 million agreement that was rejected by a U.S. District Judge as inadequate. If approved, it would avert a potentially embarrassing trial for the tech companies.

title: SanDisk Stock Hits Bargain Territory (SNDK)
summary: SanDisk Corp. (SNDK) is identified as an "unloved" stock due to a recent 15% drop, but experts view it as a great value and long-term investment. Despite recent revenue warnings and market turmoil, the company's strong position in the growing flash memory market and expansion into flash data centers, including marquee clients Apple and Facebook, make it an attractive buy. Investors might consider waiting for its Jan. 21 earnings report, though the current price offers a good entry point for a potential 32% gain.

title: Renew blue: Best Buy’s turnaround strategy
summary: Best Buy launched its "renew blue" strategy in 2012 to improve its financial position by strengthening vendor relationships, revamping stores, increasing same-store sales, and growing its online business. This strategy has already generated significant cost reductions and involves unique partnerships with suppliers like Samsung and Microsoft to create "stores-within-a-store." The company continues to focus on cost reduction, online sales expansion, and improving store traffic as part of its ongoing turnaround efforts.

title: CloudOn app to shut down after Dropbox acquisition
summary: Dropbox has acquired CloudOn Inc., an Israel-based startup, and plans to shut down its product offering while absorbing its team. This move follows a partnership between Dropbox and Microsoft to enable mobile editing of Office documents directly in Dropbox, and CloudOn's collaboration tool, which previously offered an Office alternative, faces reduced market viability due to Microsoft's native iOS Office applications. The CloudOn app will discontinue service on March 15 and is no longer accepting new customers.

title: Apple snaps up two large buildings from LinkedIn in Sunnyvale
summary: Apple Inc. has subleased two newly completed office buildings in Sunnyvale from LinkedIn Corp., a move that aligns with the evolving real estate strategies of both tech giants. This deal solidifies Apple's continued expansion in Silicon Valley even as its "spaceship" campus construction progresses. The buildings are part of a 587,000-square-foot campus that will now be shared by Apple and LinkedIn.

title: Western Union Offers Apple Pay Transfers
summary: Western Union has started allowing customers to transfer money via Apple Pay, a move that analysts believe will prompt banks to reconsider their use of the new payment method. The service is available at Western Union locations and kiosks in Walgreens and Duane Reade stores, and targets international money transfer customers who frequently own smartphones. Analysts note that while this could push banks to innovate, Western Union might face adoption challenges given its existing mobile payment app and lack of credit card support for transfers.

title: Phoenix gains in tech jobs, but still lags other cities
summary: The Phoenix metro area is seeing growth in tech jobs, with a 2.9 percent annual increase from 2010 to 2013, slightly above the national average. Despite this growth and efforts to attract advanced industries, Phoenix still lags behind other major U.S. cities and states in the percentage of its workforce in these high-wage fields. The Brookings Institution ranked Phoenix No. 16 in the number of full-time "advanced industry" workers, but only 50th when considering the percentage of the total local workforce.

title: Samsung to produce chips for Nvidia, Qualcomm – analyst
summary: An analyst predicts that Samsung will significantly improve its contract semiconductor manufacturing business by producing chips for Nvidia and Qualcomm starting in Q2 this year, leveraging its 14nm FinFET fabrication process. This move comes as chip designers seek to diversify FinFET chip suppliers due to concerns about yields and manufacturing capacities at TSMC, a long-time Nvidia supplier.

title: 4 IBM Blunders That Gave Big Blue's Competition the Edge (NYSE: IBM)
summary: This article details four significant blunders made by IBM that allowed its competitors, such as Apple, Microsoft, Intel, and HP, to gain a competitive edge in various technology markets. These missteps include abandoning the first smartphone (Simon), ceding control of operating systems to Microsoft, failing to capitalize on microprocessor development, and dismissing the low-margin laser printer market. The author suggests that these strategic errors have contributed to IBM's struggles compared to its more successful tech industry peers.

title: Southwest Airlines App Updated With Passbook Support
summary: Southwest Airlines has updated its iPhone app to include support for Apple's Passbook, allowing users to consolidate their boarding passes into a single application. This update also addresses some bugs in the previous version. The app continues to offer features such as checking in, booking, changing flights, and monitoring flight status.

title: World’s Most Admired companies ranked by key attributes
summary: This article ranks the World's Most Admired companies based on nine key attributes, including management quality, innovativeness, and financial soundness. It provides detailed lists of the top 10 companies for each category, along with their scores. The rankings highlight which companies excel in specific areas of corporate admired status.

title: To Be or Not To BE SEPs
summary: This article discusses recent developments in the law surrounding Standard Essential Patents (SEPs), particularly in China, and how these changes impact patent strategy. It analyzes two key cases: Huawei v. InterDigital, which established a FRAND royalty rate and found abuse of dominant market position, and Hon Hai v. Ge Fang, which clarified essentiality and upheld an injunction for an implementation patent. The author concludes that SEPs may have limited value in China due to low royalty rates and potential refusal of injunctions, while implementation patents retain significant value.

title: Do the ChaCha: better mobile performance with cryptography
summary: Cloudflare has adopted ChaCha20-Poly1305 cipher suites to improve mobile performance and security for HTTPS connections. This new feature, previously only supported by Google services, offers a secure stream cipher option that is more battery-friendly and faster for mobile devices compared to AES, especially for older hardware. It also provides enhanced security with 256-bit encryption and authentication, serving as a robust alternative to existing ciphers.

title: Microsoft rolls out MSN apps suite for iOS & Android
summary: Microsoft has rolled out a suite of MSN apps for iOS and Android devices, expanding its reach beyond Windows platforms. These free apps, including MSN News, Health and Fitness, Money, Sports, Food and Drink, and Weather, allow users to access their personalized preferences across different devices. This move aims to connect with non-Windows Mobile OS users and integrate Microsoft experiences across various platforms.

title: Morgan Stanley sees Apple’s target market growing to $3.4 trillion
summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Katy Huberty projects Apple's total addressable market could expand from $800 billion to $3.4 trillion by 2020, driven by new product categories like the Apple Watch, Apple TV, and Apple Car. Huberty believes Apple can significantly broaden its ecosystem's reach by invading more aspects of users' daily lives. She also raised her 12-month price target for Apple stock to $160, with a bull-case scenario of $190.

title: Apple’s new electric car could give Tesla a run for its money
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly developing an electric car, codenamed "Titan," which could significantly increase competition in the electric vehicle market, especially for Tesla Motors Inc. While Apple has released no details on design or pricing, its entry is expected to make electric cars more mainstream and could prompt Tesla to accelerate its own developments. Despite potential competition, Tesla CEO Elon Musk has historically welcomed new entrants, believing it expands the overall market for electric vehicles.

title: Howard Lorber will make $25M, thanks to franks
summary: Howard Lorber, executive chairman of Nathan's Famous Inc. and chairman of Douglas Elliman Real Estate, is set to receive approximately $25 million from a special dividend issued by Nathan's. This comes as Nathan's Famous, despite being a hot-dog company, has seen its stock more than double in two years, outperforming the Nasdaq, by successfully transforming into a national and international brand. The company's growth, including increased franchise restaurants and supermarket presence, contributed to its financial success, achieving $80 million in revenue and $8 million net profit in the last fiscal year.

title: Amid Apple Pay fraud, banks scramble to fix Yellow Path process
summary: Banks are urgently addressing a rise in Apple Pay fraud, which is linked to lax authentication processes known as "Yellow Path." This issue, where criminals use stolen information to authenticate cards, is raising concerns for all mobile payment systems, as experts emphasize that the responsibility for robust identity verification lies with card issuers. Fraud levels have been significantly higher than anticipated, with some issuers reporting losses of $6 per $100 in transactions, highlighting the need for more secure, scalable, and less reliance on static PII data for provisioning.

title: Samsung loses smartphone race to Apple: Gartner
summary: Apple Inc. surpassed Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. in global smartphone sales for the first time since 2011 in the fourth quarter, driven by the success of its iPhone 6 and 6 Plus models. According to Gartner, Apple sold 74,832 smartphones compared to Samsung's 73,032. Samsung continues to face challenges from both high-end competitors like Apple and low-cost Chinese vendors.

title: The New Tech-Stock Temptation
summary: The Nasdaq Composite is rallying, stirring memories of the dot-com boom, but the article suggests it's a different investing landscape now compared to 2000. The index, including tech giants like Apple and Google GOOGL, is up 4% in 2015 and is on track to outperform the S&P 500 for the fourth consecutive year.

title: Mall Owner Simon Property Bids for Rival Macerich to Aim at High-End Shoppers (Published 2015)
summary: Simon Property Group, a major mall owner, has made an unsolicited bid to acquire rival Macerich Company for approximately $22.4 billion, including debt. This move by Simon aims to consolidate its position in the high-end mall market, which remains lucrative despite a general decline in traditional retail spaces. The bid highlights the increasing polarization in the retail landscape, where high-end malls continue to thrive by catering to wealthier shoppers.

title: BlackBerry Ltd (BBRY) Uses International Business Machines Corp. (IBM)’s Software in its Tablet Computer
summary: BlackBerry Ltd's Secusmart unit has launched the SecuTABLET, a tablet computer that utilizes International Business Machines Corp. (IBM)’s software within a Samsung-based tablet. This device is designed for government and corporate users, allowing access to consumer applications while maintaining the security of confidential work-related data, addressing the need to separate personal and professional digital lives. The SecuTABLET employs a small chip-card for encryption and IBM software to protect applications within a virtual container, aimed at preventing data corruption by malware.

title: U.S. Companies Bring More Foreign Profit Home
summary: U.S.-based multinational companies repatriated or earmarked approximately $300 billion in foreign profits last year, marking the highest amount in nearly a decade. This move is helping to reduce a large offshore cash pile that has attracted regulatory and legislative attention. Companies like eBay Inc., VeriSign Inc., and Stryker Corp. used these repatriated profits for share buybacks, capital improvements, and funding daily operations.

title: Garmin Edge 1010: What we know, what we want
summary: This article discusses the anticipated features and improvements for the potential Garmin Edge 1010, building on the Edge 1000. Key desires include third-party app integration via Garmin's Connect IQ platform, improved battery life, and a better screen-to-total-size ratio. The article also highlights the desire for direct wireless uploads to non-Garmin sites like Strava.

title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT)’s Apps Get Pre-installed in Certain Google Inc (GOOGL)’s Android based Samsung Tablets
summary: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) announced that its apps, including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Skype, OneNote, and OneDrive, will be pre-installed on certain Android-based Samsung tablets set to launch this year. This expands a previous agreement for Samsung's Galaxy S6 handsets and reflects Microsoft's strategy to make its cloud-enabled software available across different platforms, including those by Google Inc (GOOGL) and Apple Inc. (AAPL), to reach a broader user base. Users will have free access to basic features of these apps, with premium features requiring payment.

title: Google hires woman as new CFO, luring Morgan Stanley exec from Wall St. to Silicon Valley
summary: Google has appointed Ruth Porat, Morgan Stanley's chief financial officer, as its new CFO. This move addresses criticisms that Silicon Valley companies, including Google, have faced for not hiring and promoting enough women. Porat's appointment makes her Google's highest-ranking female executive and may signal a more shareholder-friendly approach to Google's significant cash reserves.

title: AMD still has not started to use 20nm process tech
summary: Advanced Micro Devices has not yet begun using 20nm fabrication technology, despite prior announcements to use it for select products like "Skybridge" processors and a low-cost Xbox One SoC. This delay means that any 20nm chips from AMD will not be available until the second half of the year, and upcoming mid-2015 graphics processing units will continue to use the 28nm process. There are also rumors that TSMC's 20nm process may be unsuitable for complex custom designs.

title: Measuring product-development vitality
summary: The article discusses the "Vitality Index," a key corporate R&D performance metric that measures the percentage of total company sales from new products. It explores the challenges in defining "new product" and its lifespan, highlighting the varied approaches companies take. The author also provides benchmarks for different levels of vitality and the impact of the chosen "N" value (length of time a product is considered new) on reported figures and corporate strategy.

title: Jay Z and Friends Challenge Spotify With Tidal Streaming
summary: Rapper Jay Z has launched Tidal, an artist-owned music streaming service, to compete with Spotify Ltd. and Apple Inc. He appeared with numerous equity owners, including Beyonce Knowles and Rihanna, emphasizing Tidal's artist-friendly approach. The service, acquired for $56 million, now offers a standard $9.99 monthly version alongside its $19.99 high-quality option and has partnered with Sprint Corp.

title: The Hartford launches mobile app
summary: The Hartford Financial Services Group has launched a new mobile application called "My Benefits." This app allows customers to enroll in disability insurance and manage their claims directly from their mobile devices. The app is available for free on both Apple and Google's app stores, though traditional claim tracking via phone or website remains an option.

title: No Dividend Reinvestment Plan Is Complete Without These 3 Stocks
summary: This article highlights three stocks, Realty Income, Apple, and American Express, as excellent choices for a dividend reinvestment plan (DRIP). The analysts explain how each stock's unique characteristics, such as monthly dividends, strong growth potential, or consistent dividend growth, make them particularly beneficial for compounding returns over time within a DRIP.
authors: Matthew Frankel|Dan Caplinger|and Jason Hall|The Motley Fool

title: Google, Apple and Microsoft defend tax set-up that shifts revenue offshore
summary: Senior executives from Google, Apple, and Microsoft defended their corporate structures before a Senate inquiry, explaining that revenue from Australian operations is often taxed in offshore jurisdictions due to the global tax system. News Corp Australia also appeared, advocating for a GST on Netflix, which competes with its own streaming service. The article highlights the ongoing debate about multinational corporate tax avoidance and potential "Google taxes" to address profit shifting.

title: Apple's bounce back and 3 more reasons Jabil shines brighter for analysts
summary: Jabil Circuit Inc. has seen a stronger-than-expected recovery in its sales to Apple Inc. This positive development contributes to Jabil shining brighter for analysts, suggesting a favorable outlook for the company.

title: Unlocking App Audiences: The role of content and demographics in platform selection
summary: This article examines the role of content and demographics in platform selection for app audiences. It highlights that apps account for a significant portion of mobile internet minutes and that user engagement with apps is high once the initial download hurdle is overcome. The piece emphasizes that content type often dictates platform choice, with smartphones preferred for location-sensitive activities and tablets for media consumption, and that demographic variances significantly impact these preferences.

title: Worldwide semi foundry market grew 16.1 percent in 2014
summary: The worldwide semiconductor foundry market experienced a 16.1 percent growth in 2014, reaching USD 46.9 billion in revenue, driven by factors such as inventory stocking, ultramobile sales, Apple's iPhone 6 success, and early adoption of wearables. TSMC maintained its lead, increasing market share to 53.7 percent, while UMC returned to the No. 2 position. The market also saw an expansion in 200-mm wafer capacity due to tight supply and increased demand for legacy technology nodes.

title: IBM Launches New Health Unit, Teams With Apple, J&J, Medtronic
summary: IBM has launched a new Watson Health unit to provide data analytics to the healthcare sector, deepening its partnership with Apple for health information gathered by Apple devices. This unit will aggregate health data from various devices and providers in the cloud, offering insights to companies like Johnson & Johnson and Medtronic. IBM has also acquired two health technology firms, Explorys and Phytel, and plans to base the unit in Boston with 2,000 employees.

title: Sony Hackers Used Phishing Emails to Breach Company Networks
summary: A security researcher revealed that phishing emails were used to breach Sony Picture Entertainment's computer networks. Hackers leveraged fake Apple ID verification emails to obtain credentials, then used them in conjunction with LinkedIn profiles to access Sony network login information. Recommendations include memory process injection protection, user credential monitoring, and avoiding password reuse to prevent similar attacks.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL), Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), Google Inc (GOOGL) & Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): 4 Horsemen Of NASDAQ
summary: This article discusses the performance of four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Google, and Microsoft—dubbed the "4 Horsemen of NASDAQ," in early 2015. Amazon showed the most significant growth with over 45% YTD, followed by Apple and Google, while Microsoft had more modest gains. Analysts James Cakmak and Jason Helfstein weighed in on Amazon's impressive turnaround, particularly highlighting its web services business.

title: Number of Mobile-Only Internet Users Now Exceeds Desktop-Only in the U.S.
summary: For the first time in March 2015, the number of mobile-only adult internet users in the U.S. exceeded desktop-only users. While a large majority of the digital population is multi-platform, this milestone signifies the smartphone's rise as the primary internet access point. Desktops still remain important for complex tasks and online retail purchases, but the trend points towards a "mobile first" evolution of digital media.

title: FAO Schwarz Fifth Avenue space to be overhauled as toy store moves
summary: The FAO Schwarz toy store space at New York's General Motors Building is set for a major overhaul as the toy store moves out. Boston Properties Inc. plans to renovate and re-lease the 64,000-square-foot retail location, which could be divided for multiple tenants or offered to a single large tenant. The current rent paid by FAO Schwarz is significantly below market rates for prime Fifth Avenue retail space.

title: Apple, IBM to Work With Japan Post to Serve Elderly
summary: Apple, IBM, and Japan Post are partnering to improve services for elderly Japanese citizens. This collaboration involves Apple's hardware and IBM's software, which will be used by Japan Post employees to enhance their Post Office Watch service. IBM will develop iPad software over the next year to facilitate these new services.

title: Amazon Prime Video takes to the skies via JetBlue
summary: Amazon (AMZN) is partnering with JetBlue to offer its Prime Video streaming service on most JetBlue flights. Amazon Prime members can stream content at no additional cost, while non-members can purchase or rent titles. The service utilizes JetBlue's Fly-Fi satellite broadband technology, offering access to Amazon's full content catalog and allowing passengers to watch on their personal devices or seatback televisions.

title: Lights are computers now, so the founder of Silicon Labs has a lighting startup
summary: Ketra, a six-year-old lighting startup led by Nav Sooch, co-founder of Silicon Labs, is emerging from stealth mode with a focus on the residential market. The company aims to compete with major lighting incumbents by leveraging LED and connectivity technology, developing lights that can self-calibrate for consistent color. While already used by commercial clients, Ketra's sophisticated and self-checking bulbs will be priced at a premium for residential installations.

title: Mint.com Founder on What It's Like to Be an Employee at Your Own Company (Hint: It Stinks)
summary: Mint.com founder Aaron Patzer shares his experiences after selling his startup to Intuit for $170 million, detailing the challenges of being an employee in his former company and navigating leadership changes. He discusses past failures, his new venture Fountain, and offers advice to entrepreneurs on solving their own problems and being fully committed.

title: Teens & Older Demos Driving Gains in U.S. Smartphone Penetration...
summary: U.S. smartphone penetration reached 77% in March, with teens and older age groups showing the fastest growth. Notably, teens aged 13-17, 45-54 year olds, and those 65+ each saw double-digit percentage point increases in penetration. The article suggests that smartphones are now viewed as mainstream rather than a luxury, driving adoption across all demographics.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook’s social media debut in China embraced by competitors
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook launched a personal account on Weibo, China's most popular microblogging site, during his sixth visit to China. His debut was met with enthusiasm, even from competitors like Xiaomi and Huawei, underscoring the importance of the Chinese market for Apple. Cook's first post highlighted new environmental programs, including partnerships with the World Wildlife Fund and increased renewable energy use in Apple's Chinese facilities.

title: American Airlines Center gets NFC tablets for payments on the go
summary: The American Airlines Center in Dallas is deploying NFC tablets equipped with the Agilysys InfoGenesis Flex POS system to enable staff to accept mobile and contactless credit card payments anywhere in the stadium. This solution, running on Dell Venue Pro tablets, aims to improve guest service, increase order volumes and revenue, and support various payment types including Apple Pay. The system is scalable and versatile, allowing for use in both in-seat service and concession areas.

title: Q&A: Deezer CEO sees 'big' upside for music streaming
summary: Deezer CEO Hans-Holger Albrecht discusses the company's strategies in a competitive music streaming market, including partnerships with telcos and high-end audio companies. He emphasizes the vast untapped potential for growth in the global music streaming industry despite concerns about artist compensation. Albrecht also highlights Deezer's extensive track library and efforts to integrate podcasts through its acquisition of Stitcher.

title: China’s Antitrust Crackdown Hits Qualcomm with US$975 Million Fine: What Can Other Host States Learn from the Story?
summary: In February 2015, Qualcomm Inc. was fined US$975 million by Chinese antitrust authorities for anti-competitive practices, marking China's largest-ever antitrust fine. The article details Qualcomm's controversial patent licensing model, the NDRC's investigation, and the terms of the settlement, highlighting how the fine was substantial yet amicable given the potential for harsher penalties. It also discusses the broader implications for emerging market economies tightening their competition law regimes and the challenges companies face with new regulatory bodies.

title: Is It Time to Take Profit on Kroger Co Stock?
summary: This article discusses whether it's time for investors to take profit on Kroger Co stock, given its significant performance over recent years. It highlights Kroger's strategies, including pricing advantages and the success of its Simple Truth organic brand, as key reasons for continued optimism despite competitive pressure and a high valuation. The author believes Kroger will continue to outperform due to its industry-leading sales and profit growth.

title: Censorship In China: Which Words Are Banned From Weibo On The 26th Anniversary Of Tiananmen Square
summary: This article examines online censorship in China, specifically focusing on words banned from Sina Weibo on the 26th anniversary of the Tiananmen Square protest. It analyzes a crowdsourced list of sensitive terms, revealing how the government censors discussions related to the protest, political figures, and other sensitive issues. The analysis highlights the government's sophisticated censorship methods, including detecting synonyms and creative circumventions by users, and categorizes censored terms to show prevalent themes of suppression.
authors: Kuang Keng and John Zongmin Chow

title: Assurant’s Colberg on Innovation
summary: Alan B. Colberg, President and CEO of Assurant, discusses innovation within the P/C insurance industry in this interview. He highlights the impact of telemetrics, external innovators like Apple and Amazon, and Assurant's internal strategies for fostering innovation, including leadership development programs and a strategy learning game. Colberg emphasizes the importance of adaptive leadership, an enterprise mindset, and a willingness to embrace failure to drive innovation.

title: Avnet leads five Arizona companies on Fortune 500 list
summary: Avnet, a Phoenix-based distributor of computer and electronic products, is the highest-ranked Arizona company on the Fortune 500 list, coming in at 108th. Four other Arizona-based companies, Freeport-McMoRan, Republic Services, PetSmart, and Insight Enterprises, also made the list. The rankings are based on annual revenue, with Walmart topping the overall list.

title: HP Pro Slate 8 review
summary: The HP Pro Slate 8 is reviewed as a surprisingly good Android mini tablet for business, offering features like hardware-accelerated encryption and a stylus, despite some quirks. While the stylus features could use refinement, its lightweight design, good battery life, and quality screen make it a solid choice, especially for those deploying in-house Android apps. The tablet is noted for not being a Samsung Galaxy Note imitation but rather a distinct offering by HP.

title: The 10 Most Profitable Companies in the Fortune 500
summary: This article identifies the 10 most profitable companies in the Fortune 500 for 2014, highlighting their profits and key factors influencing their performance. Tech giants Apple and Microsoft, banks Wells Fargo and J.P. Morgan, and oil producers ExxonMobil and Chevron are prominent, with Apple leading in profits. The article details individual company performance, including financial figures and notable events from 2014.

title: The top technology companies of the Fortune 500
summary: This article highlights the top technology companies on the Fortune 500 list, noting that many well-known consumer tech companies are outranked by less familiar biotech and electronics manufacturers. It discusses the financial performance of major players like Apple, Google, Amazon, and Microsoft, alongside other significant companies like Thermo Fisher Scientific and Micron Technologies, emphasizing market shifts and strategic moves. The article also points out that privately held, highly valued companies like Uber are not included in the ranking.

title: UnitedHealth-Aetna colossus would overtake Apple on the Fortune 500
summary: Rumors of a potential merger between UnitedHealth Group and Aetna have ignited the health insurance sector, hinting at a significant industry reshuffle. If realized, this combination would create a colossal entity with $189 billion in sales, ranking it fifth on the Fortune 500, surpassing even Apple, and establishing it as the world's largest healthcare company. Despite the potential for disruption and the unlikely nature of such a large integration, investors are bullish on both companies' stocks, signaling an appetite for consolidation and scale.

title: What General Electric Seeks In MBA Hires
summary: General Electric is seeking MBA hires who possess leadership, a passion to drive change, and learning agility. The company values candidates with a technical undergraduate degree combined with an MBA, especially those with prior entrepreneurial experience. GE emphasizes a culture of speed and simplicity, looking for talent with a "startup style" mindset who can navigate a large organization and possess analytical skills for HR roles.

title: Facebook (Nasdaq: FB) Joins Top 10 Companies by Market Cap
summary: Facebook (Nasdaq: FB) has surpassed Wal-Mart Stores Inc. (NYSE: WMT) to join the top 10 companies by market capitalization, following a strong stock rally. This achievement highlights Facebook's rapid growth since its 2012 IPO, expanding its influence across tech, mobile, advertising, and virtual reality with acquisitions like Oculus. The article also details Facebook's strategic initiatives in mobile advertising and video to drive future growth.

title: Netflix splits stock 7 for 1
summary: Netflix announced a 7-for-1 stock split, which will be distributed as a dividend of six additional shares for each outstanding share. The split is intended to make the stock more appealing to retail investors and more affordable for employees participating in its stock option program. Trading at the post-split price will commence on July 15, following the record date of July 2.

title: Huge deal for Westcott investment cloaked in secrecy
summary: Court Westcott, managing partner of Westcott LLC, reveals that Apple acquired Metaio, a German augmented reality startup in which Westcott LLC had invested $2 million two years prior. The deal, kept secret due to a non-disclosure agreement with Apple, is estimated to have made the Westcott family tens of millions of dollars. Westcott is now focusing on other augmented reality companies in their portfolio, believing AR will be the next major technological wave.

title: Netflix Splits Stock And Icahn Cashes Out, Volatility Ensues
summary: Netflix's stock experienced significant volatility after its board approved a 7-for-1 stock split and then activist investor Carl Icahn announced he had sold his remaining 4.5% stake in the company. The stock split, aimed at increasing accessibility, initially sent shares soaring, but Icahn's unexpected sell-off caused a sharp plummet. Icahn suggested that Apple now offers the same opportunity Netflix did years ago, raising questions about Netflix's future stock performance.

title: Netflix stock drops after billionaire Carl Icahn cashes out stake
summary: Billionaire investor Carl Icahn sold his remaining stake in Netflix, realizing a more than $2 billion profit over three years. Following his announcement, Netflix's stock dropped, partly influenced by Icahn's concerns about an "overheated" stock market. Icahn, however, still believes Netflix is a great company and hopes his investment in Apple Inc. will yield similar returns.

title: Netflix Is So Hot Because It Gives Us What We Want: TV
summary: Netflix's stock is experiencing significant growth due to the public's love for TV and the company's ability to provide content cheaply, easily, and without ads. The article highlights Netflix's impressive subscriber numbers and competitive edge against traditional media and other streaming services, further boosted by an upcoming stock split. Analysts credit Netflix's success to its consumer-first approach, technological investments, and substantial spending on compelling original content.

title: Microsoft to cut 7,800 more jobs; writes off $7.6 billion in Nokia deal
summary: Microsoft announced a major restructuring of its mobile devices business, writing off $7.6 billion from its Nokia acquisition and cutting 7,800 jobs globally, primarily in its phone segment. This marks the second round of significant job cuts under CEO Satya Nadella, as the company shifts its strategy from a standalone phone business to growing a vibrant Windows ecosystem. Microsoft aims to narrow its focus to three key customer segments and run a more effective, narrower phone portfolio.

title: TSMC cuts prices of 20nm and 28nm chips by 10%
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has reduced prices for its 20nm and 28nm chip production by 10% earlier this quarter due to decreased orders and declining utilization rates. This price cut aims to boost capacity utilization above 80% and could benefit companies like AMD, Nvidia, Qualcomm, and MediaTek by lowering their manufacturing costs. The move comes as demand for these chips has softened, particularly in the mobile sector, with Apple significantly reducing its 20nm orders in anticipation of new technologies.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Earnings Growth Slows
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported its slowest earnings growth in a year for the second quarter, attributed to increased competition and a slowdown in global smartphone demand. Despite a 33% rise in net profit to NT$79.42 billion (US$2.56 billion), exceeding analyst expectations, and a 12% revenue increase to NT$205.44 billion, the company is facing challenges as it loses some orders to rivals.

title: Synchrony Powers Through Earnings With New Partnerships
summary: Synchrony Financial reported strong second-quarter financial results, exceeding EPS estimates with $0.65 on $2.7 billion in revenue. The company demonstrated significant growth in loan receivables and deposits and announced new partnerships with Mattress Firm, Newegg, and Stash Hotel Rewards, extending an existing partnership with Chevron, and notably becoming one of the first issuers for private label credit cards in Apple Pay. Shares rose significantly following the announcement, reaching a new post-spin-off high.

title: PayPal makes big splash on first day of trading after eBay spinoff
summary: On its first day of trading as an independent public company, PayPal's market value reached nearly $49 billion, surpassing its former parent company eBay. This move followed pressure from activist investor Carl Icahn for eBay to spin off PayPal. PayPal's shares rose 5.4%, while eBay's gained 2.4%, reflecting their divergent growth paths, with PayPal showing strong revenue and transaction volume increases compared to eBay's challenges.

title: PayPal Back On Nasdaq With Market Value Larger Than eBay
summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:PYPL) returned to the Nasdaq as an independent company after spinning off from eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY). Shares of PayPal soared 11% on its market debut, giving it a market capitalization of $46.6 billion, which is higher than eBay's reduced valuation of $34 billion. Analysts from Goldman Sachs and Wedbush Securities issued "Buy" and "Neutral" ratings respectively, highlighting PayPal's strong position in the digital payments market, despite competitive threats.

title: Dow Jones Industrial Average Today Drops 180 Points as IBM, UTX Slide
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 180 points or 1% on July 21, 2015, primarily due to significant slides in IBM and United Technologies Corp. shares following weak revenue figures and a slashed profit outlook, respectively. Other market movers included a drop in Verizon Communications, marginal gains in oil prices, and upcoming earnings reports from major companies like Apple and Microsoft, while specific stocks like Tesla and Lexmark also experienced notable declines.

title: Infosys Revamp Brings in More Large Deals; Shares Surge
summary: Infosys Ltd (INFY.NS) reported a 5 percent rise in net profit for the first quarter, attributing the success to a business revamp initiated by its first non-founder CEO, Vishal Sikka. The company's shares surged 11 percent, and it raised its U.S. dollar-denominated revenue estimate for the year, indicating positive market response to its new strategy focusing on automation, AI, and digital technology.

title: If Microsoft made laptops, it'd make this: HP Spectre x360
summary: This article reviews the HP Spectre x360 laptop, highlighting its design as a collaborative effort between HP and Microsoft, and comparing it to what a Microsoft Surface laptop might be like. It details the machine's aesthetic, specs, and innovative hinge design, concluding that it offers a solid and stylish convertible experience.

title: Windows 10 downloads available from July 29: New features you should know about
summary: Microsoft's new Windows 10 operating system was made available worldwide on July 29, 2015, as a free upgrade for users of Windows 7 and 8.1. The article highlights several new features including a revamped Start menu, the Continuum feature for cross-device integration, enhanced Cortana functionality, and universal apps for music and video. Microsoft also aimed to attract a billion new users by making it easier for Android and iOS apps to run on Windows 10.

title: Smart Beta: Single Factor vs. Multi-Factor Approaches
summary: The article discusses the differences between single-factor and multi-factor smart beta ETFs, which combine passive and active investing approaches. It explains how single-factor funds focus on one characteristic like value or momentum, while multi-factor funds combine several factors using proprietary algorithms. The piece also compares their performance and considerations like expense ratios and turnover, noting that while single-factor funds can outperform if the right factor is chosen, multi-factor funds may offer more consistent benchmark-beating performance over time.

title: Silicon Valley map wars heat up: $3.1 billion deal between Nokia, carmakers sign of trend
summary: German luxury carmakers BMW, Audi, and Daimler are acquiring Nokia's mapping division, HERE, for $3.1 billion, signaling a heated competition in the Silicon Valley map-making industry. This acquisition aims to secure independence in location-based technology crucial for future autonomous vehicles and smart cities, challenging the dominance of Google and Apple. The deal underscores the importance of highly detailed, updated digital maps for next-generation industries like robot taxis, package delivery, and personalized shopping apps.

title: IBM, Apple’s Rival-Turned-Partner, Plans to Help Other Companies Adopt Macs
summary: IBM, once a bitter rival of Apple, is expanding its collaboration to help other companies adopt Macs. As expected to become the world's largest corporate user of MacBooks, IBM is introducing a service to facilitate this adoption. This partnership highlights the evolving relationship between the two tech giants, who are also working together on iPhone and iPad apps for IBM's clients.

title: Kroger top among supermarkets in customer service
summary: Kroger Co. was recognized for its excellent customer service, earning first place among supermarkets and fifth place overall in 24/7 Wall St.’s annual 2015 Customer Service Hall of Fame. This achievement was based on a survey of over 1,500 adults, where more than 77 percent reported a good or excellent experience with Kroger. Jayne Homco, president of The Kroger Co. of Michigan, highlighted the company's commitment to investing in its employees and providing world-class service.

title: Dow plunges 900 points, TSX tumbles almost 700 points at the open
summary: North American markets experienced a significant plunge at the start of trading, with the Dow falling 900 points and the TSX almost 700 points. This broad selloff was triggered by fears of a global disinflationary shock, particularly stemming from weakness in the Chinese currency and concerns about the Federal Reserve's interest rate hike plans. The S&P 500 is down 7.5 percent from its May record and is on track for its worst August decline in 14 years.

title: Foxconn boost for 'Make in India': Why Taiwan Inc's growing interests in India deserve attention
summary: Foxconn's renewed and significant investment in India, including a $5 billion electronics factory and R&D center in Maharashtra, signals a major boost for the "Make in India" campaign and the broader entry of Taiwanese companies into the Indian market. This move reflects Taiwan Inc's strategy to geographically de-risk from China, capitalize on India's large domestic market, and contribute to building a critical manufacturing ecosystem in the country. The article highlights the potential for increased Taiwanese investment beyond Foxconn, addressing India's need to reduce electronics imports and create jobs.

title: TSMC obtains orders from AMD and Nvidia for 16nm FinFET chips
summary: Despite being behind Samsung Foundry with its first-generation FinFET technology, TSMC has secured orders from major customers, including AMD and Nvidia, for its 16nm FinFET manufacturing process. AMD plans to use TSMC for its next-generation Radeon GPUs ("Greenland") and GlobalFoundries for 14nm microprocessors, while Nvidia will continue its partnership with TSMC for its GP100 graphics chip based on the Pascal architecture, even after adding Samsung Foundry as a partner. TSMC's 16nm FinFET client list also includes Apple, Avago, Broadcom, HiSilicon Technologies, LG Electronics, MediaTek, and Xilinx.

title: Apple Loses ‘Swipe To Unlock’ Patent In German Court
summary: Apple lost a legal challenge in Germany to overturn a previous ruling that voided its 'swipe to unlock' patent in Europe. The Federal Court of Justice ruled the patent did not provide a technical solution and noted that a Swedish company, Neonode, had already developed the feature before the iPhone. This decision could impact ongoing legal battles between Apple and Motorola.

title: Yext Named to the 2015 Inc. 500 List of Fastest-Growing Private Companies in America
summary: Yext, Inc., a leader in Digital Location Management, has been named to the 34th annual Inc. 500 list of the fastest-growing private companies in America. The company secured the #212 spot overall and #23 in the software industry, demonstrating over 2,000% revenue growth and adding 240 jobs in the past three years. This marks the second time Yext has been recognized on the Inc. 500 list, highlighting its significant expansion in revenue, technology, and market share.

title: Johnson & Johnson goes to Silicon Valley with new hardware partnership
summary: Johnson & Johnson has partnered with PCH, a San Francisco hardware specialist, to support startups developing innovative healthcare devices. This collaboration will offer startups office space, funding, product design, distribution, marketing, and regulatory assistance. The initiative aims to help these startups navigate the complexities of manufacturing in the medical field, leveraging PCH's experience and J&J's industry knowledge.

title: Spreads between High-Grade Bonds and Treasury Yields
summary: This article discusses the widening spreads between high-grade corporate bonds and Treasury yields, indicating worsening credit and economic conditions. It explains what high-grade bonds are, how their yields are assessed, and the historical context of these spreads, suggesting that wider spreads make high-grade bonds more attractive despite increased risk.

title: Ralph Lauren Smart Shirt Will Measure Heart Rate And Calories Burned
summary: Ralph Lauren has launched the new PoloTech Smartshirt, a garment that combines fashion and technology to measure heart rate, calories burned, stress levels, and other fitness data. Developed in partnership with OMSignal, the shirt features sensory receivers woven into the fabric and a "black box" to stream data to a mobile device. The smartshirt retails for $295 and was tested live during the 2014 U.S. Open.

title: KeyRaider Malware Targets Jailbroken iPhones
source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
source_domain: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
summary: A sophisticated malware called KeyRaider has compromised over 225,000 Apple accounts, primarily targeting jailbroken iPhones. The malware allows hackers to download apps using stolen credentials, lock devices for ransom, and is considered the largest known Apple account theft caused by malware. Apple advises against jailbreaking devices due to the significant security risks involved.

title: Use 2FA to mitigate KeyRaider iOS malware, says Rapid7
summary: Security firm Rapid7 recommends that jailbroken iPhone users enable two-factor authentication (2FA) to protect against the KeyRaider iOS malware. This malware has stolen over 225,000 Apple accounts, primarily in China but also in 17 other countries including the UK, and poses a significant risk to enterprises due to personal iPhones accessing corporate systems. Jailbreaking iOS devices increases vulnerability to such malware by allowing apps from untested third-party sources.

title: Netflix Stock Clawed by Bears: Are Apple, Hulu, Amazon Threats to Blame?
summary: Netflix stock experienced a significant decline, losing 14% since August 27th, amidst growing fears of increased competition from Apple, Hulu, and Amazon. Investors were spooked by Apple's potential entry into original programming, Hulu's new ad-free tier and a deal to acquire Epix content previously held by Netflix, and Amazon's introduction of offline viewing and expansion into Japan. Despite the sell-off, some analysts remain bullish on Netflix, viewing the competition as beneficial for the streaming market overall.

title: Understanding Tesla Motors’s Product Portfolio
summary: This article examines Tesla's product portfolio, focusing on the currently available Model-S and the upcoming Model-X, expected later in 2015. It also mentions the highly anticipated mass-market Model-3, which is still a few years away. The author highlights Tesla's success in building a strong brand and compares its products to Apple's in terms of customer enthusiasm, while noting the ultimate key to success will be a mass-market electric vehicle.

title: Apple’s iPhone: The Biggest Change Is How You’ll Pay for It
summary: This article discusses that the most significant change with Apple's new iPhone will be the variety of payment options available to consumers, rather than major device alterations. The traditional $200 upfront payment for the latest model is largely over, as carriers introduce new pricing strategies to compete for subscribers in a mature U.S. market. Consumers now have numerous choices for acquiring the new device, even without switching wireless providers.

title: Amazon Is Killing Off the Fire Phone
summary: Amazon has discontinued its Fire Phone, with both 32GB and 64GB models now out of stock and no plans for replenishment. The smartphone, launched last year, failed to gain traction despite Amazon's hopes for the smartphone market. Amazon had previously taken a $170 million charge related to the Fire Phone's poor performance and excessive inventory.

title: iPad envy prompted Microsoft Surface alliance
summary: Microsoft launched its Surface Enterprise Initiative with Dell and HP, among others, likely in response to Apple's business-focused iPad Pro. This initiative aims to push Windows 10-based tablets into corporate accounts, a market where Apple devices have already made significant inroads. Despite internal conflicts and their own hardware offerings, HP and Dell's participation highlights a collective concern among these companies regarding Apple's dominance

title: How to Know When It is Time to Switch to Plan B
summary: Robert Chess, a lecturer of management at Stanford GSB, advises entrepreneurs on recognizing when to pivot their business plans. He emphasizes the need for entrepreneurs to balance optimism with a pessimistic eye for potential problems and to build nimbleness into their company culture from the start. Chess also notes that even large companies need to adapt and that trusting both gut instincts and analytics is crucial for successful pivots.

title: Why Macy's Is Teaming Up With Best Buy
summary: Macy's is partnering with Best Buy to license electronics departments in 10 of its stores, starting in early November. This test aims to bring consumer electronics back to Macy's ahead of the Christmas shopping season, as Macy's simultaneously plans to close dozens of underperforming stores due to sluggish sales. The move is seen as a strategic implication to maximize existing real estate and cater to consumers' shifting spend towards technology.

title: Smart offers mobile payment system for iTunes
summary: Smart Communications Inc., a unit of Philippine Long Distance Telephone Co. (PLDT), has launched "Smart Pay-With-Mobile," a service allowing postpaid subscribers to charge purchases from Apple's App Store, iTunes, and Apple Music to their mobile accounts. This service, backed by BDO Unibank Inc., is the first of its kind in the Philippines and enables subscribers to buy digital content without needing a credit card. Smart plans to expand the service's availability to all users, including prepaid subscribers, as part of PLDT's strategy to increase digital content consumption and mobile data activity.

title: Novel Malware XcodeGhost Modifies Xcode, Infects Apple iOS Apps and Hits App Store
summary: XcodeGhost is a novel compiler malware that repackages Xcode installers with malicious code, leading to the infection of iOS apps compiled with these altered versions. The malware collects device information and uploads it to C2 servers, and has successfully bypassed Apple's App Store review process, infecting at least two popular apps. This highlights a significant security risk for developers who download Xcode from unofficial sources and for enterprise apps distributed outside the App Store.

title: Apple Confirms Discovery of Malicious Code in Some App Store Products (Published 2015)
summary: Apple confirmed on Sunday that malicious code had infiltrated its App Store, affecting approximately 40 apps with potential reach to hundreds of millions of users. Hackers modified a developer tool called Xcode, leading to major apps like WeChat and Didi Kuaidi being infected. Apple has since removed the compromised apps and is collaborating with security firms and developers to address the breach.

title: More Details on the XcodeGhost Malware and Affected iOS Apps
summary: This article provides further details on the XcodeGhost malware, which modifies Xcode to infect iOS apps. It discusses the actions taken by Apple, Amazon, and Baidu to mitigate the threat, the discovery of more infected apps, and a deeper technical analysis of how XcodeGhost modifies Xcode and its potential vulnerabilities. The post also offers security suggestions for both iOS users and developers to protect against similar future attacks.

title: Apple removes malware-infected apps from App Store
summary: Apple Inc. has removed several applications from its App Store after developers in China were targeted with malicious software that injected harmful code into their apps. The malware, dubbed XcodeGhost, spread through counterfeit versions of Apple's Xcode tools. Palo Alto Networks identified 39 infected iOS apps, some of which were very popular in China and other countries, affecting hundreds of millions of users.

title: Apple's Chinese App Store Hit by Malware: What You Need to Know
summary: Apple's App Store in China was compromised by XcodeGhost malware, affecting up to 39 applications and potentially hundreds of millions of users. The malware, a modified version of Apple's development software, could lead to phishing attacks and has been described as a harmful threat to the iOS ecosystem. Apple has removed the affected apps and is working with developers to ensure they use the proper Xcode version.

title: Touch ID Authentication Added To PNC Bank Mobile App For Commercial Customers
summary: PNC Bank has added Touch ID authentication to its PINACLE mobile banking app for commercial customers, allowing secure and convenient access to account information using fingerprint scanning. This feature, available on select iPhone models, enhances security by encrypting the fingerprint data and provides quick access to critical financial details, though some advanced functions may require further verification. Howard Forman, senior vice president of treasury management product management at PNC Bank, emphasized the combination of security and convenience for business leaders on the go.

title: Apple Inc A9 chip cores in iPhone 6s and 6s Plus deliver a processor punch to Samsung, Qualcomm
summary: Preliminary testing of Apple's A9 chip in the iPhone 6s and 6s Plus reveals a significant jump in general CPU performance over the A8, advancing Apple's lead in proprietary ARM core design against Samsung and Qualcomm. The A9's two cores deliver single-core performance that makes the iPhone 6s over 90 percent faster than Samsung's Exynos 7 and over 133 percent faster than Qualcomm's Snapdragon 805 in single-core tasks, with its efficiency being a key advantage over competitors. The new iPhones also offer tangible performance increases for app launching and data processing, alongside faster Touch ID.

title: Rentokil Initial raises the bar for hygiene standards
summary: Paul Clifford, managing director of Rentokil Initial Vietnam, discusses the company's "Protecting People. Enhancing Lives" initiative and its establishment in the Vietnamese market since 2007. He outlines Rentokil Initial's unique approach to pest control and hygiene services, emphasizing their international expertise, innovative products, and strategic expansion plans within Vietnam. The article highlights the growing demand for high hygiene standards and the company's commitment to delivering professional solutions across various sectors.

title: Why the Model X is so important for Tesla
summary: Tesla's Model X launch event marks a significant transition for the company, moving beyond the highly successful Model S to offer multiple products and attract new customer demographics. The Model X is crucial for Tesla to meet its annual shipment goals and generate revenue for future projects like the cheaper Model 3 and its grid battery business, despite facing production challenges and delays. The success of the Model X will be a key indicator of Tesla's ability to scale production and realize its ambitious growth targets.

title: Sonos redesigns its Play:5 speaker, announces Trueplay room-tuning software
summary: Sonos has redesigned its flagship Play:5 speaker and introduced Trueplay, a new room-tuning software. Trueplay uses an iOS device's microphone to analyze a room's acoustics and adjust speaker performance, working with existing Sonos speakers. The new Play:5 features six drivers, can be oriented vertically or horizontally for different sound modes, and retails for $499, while Trueplay will be a free software upgrade available later this year.

title: HP introduces monstrous stackable mini PC
summary: HP has introduced the Elite Desk 800 G2 mini, a powerful and expandable mini PC described as being the size of a paperback book. It features an Intel Core i7 6700 processor, 16GB of memory, and integrated graphics capable of running three monitors. The device also offers optional stackable accessory boxes for expanded functionality, with European pricing starting at 399.

title: SkyWest, Inc. chooses Surface and Windows 10 over iPad for 7,500 pilots
summary: SkyWest, Inc. is deploying Surface 3 devices running Windows 10 Professional to 7,500 pilots across its ExpressJet Airlines and SkyWest Airlines subsidiaries, replacing heavy paper-based flight kits. The decision was driven by the security and management capabilities of Windows 10 on Surface 3, demonstrating a growing trend of commercial airlines adopting Surface tablets for Electronic Flight Bags (EFBs). Surface 3 has also been pre-qualified for FAA and EASA authorization, streamlining the deployment process for airlines.

title: Nelson Peltz's Trian takes $2.5 billion stake in General Electric
summary: Activist investor Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management has acquired a $2.5 billion stake in General Electric, making it a top-10 shareholder and adding pressure for the conglomerate to accelerate its shift towards industrial operations. Trian aims for cost cutting, more share buybacks, and further scaling back of GE Capital, backing GE's ongoing transformation. General Electric's shares rose 4.5% following the disclosure, with Trian projecting its stock could reach $40-$45 per share by the end of 2017.

title: Micron’s Clients and Relationships at a Glance
summary: This article examines Micron Technology's client base and relationships, highlighting its primary focus on PC and storage manufacturers like HP, Intel, and Kingston. It notes Micron's strategic shift to the mobile market through the acquisition of Elpida, and its significant sales concentration in China. The article also discusses Micron's sales channels and challenges in predicting order volumes due to demand fluctuations.

title: List of Carl Icahn Holdings Loaded with Stocks You May Own
summary: This article examines the holdings of activist investor Carl Icahn, highlighting several well-known companies he has invested in. It explains how Icahn's activism can benefit ordinary shareholders, often leading to a significant increase in stock value. The article also touches on the growth of activist investing and its impressive returns compared to the S&P 500.

title: Barra: GM will lead self-driving revolution
summary: GM CEO Mary Barra states that General Motors is among the leaders in self-driving car development, despite competition from Silicon Valley giants like Apple and Google, and electric-vehicle maker Tesla Motors. She emphasizes GM's commitment to innovation, including the development of the "Super Cruise" system and an affordable long-range electric car, while navigating the shifting automotive landscape and addressing investor concerns. Barra also discusses GM's stance on mergers, its relationship with regulatory bodies, and the broader challenges and opportunities in the evolving auto industry.

title: Microsoft Surface Book’s Nvidia GPU is decent, but it’s no gaming laptop
summary: Microsoft's Surface Book features a dedicated Nvidia GPU, which new reports suggest is equivalent to the mid-range GeForce 940M. While this makes the Surface Book capable for average laptop use and some gaming at lower settings, it is not competitive with dedicated gaming laptops due to its focus on portability and battery life. The Surface Book does, however, hold an advantage over the Macbook Pro in gaming thanks to its Windows operating system and a broader game library.

title: Nasdaq may see record with Apple earnings
summary: The Nasdaq 100 index, heavily influenced by US technology stocks such as Apple, is poised to potentially set a new record high, driven by strong earnings anticipated from Apple Inc. Technology shares have led the market's recovery, with gains in Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft following better-than-expected earnings. Despite being an "underperformer" among tech giants recently, Apple's expected revenue increase and positive market sentiment suggest its upcoming earnings report could push the Nasdaq 100 to a record.

title: Review: Should you buy Roku’s new media streaming box?
summary: This article reviews the Roku 4 streaming box, highlighting its new features like 4K streaming, the remote finding feature, and Roku OS7 with enhanced search. The reviewer notes that while it's an excellent device, it's not a significant upgrade from the Roku 3 unless 4K capability is crucial. The article compares it to competitors like Apple TV and Amazon Fire TV, concluding that it's a strong contender for those considering a new streaming box other than the latest Apple TV.

title: Daily Deals: PCM snaps up Edmonton’s Acrodex
summary: PCM Inc. acquired Edmonton-based IT services provider Acrodex for $16.5-million. This article also covers Snyder's-Lance Inc.'s acquisition of Diamond Foods for $1.27-billion, Carl Icahn's push for a breakup of AIG, and the Canadian launch of Apple Pay. Additionally, it highlights David Copperfield's legal issues with the Canada Revenue Agency regarding back taxes.

title: Broadcom in $207 million deal with Boston Properties for North San Jose project
summary: Broadcom is set to finalize a $207 million deal with Boston Properties for a North San Jose project by the end of the year. This agreement will add to the recent significant real estate transactions in North San Jose, a region that has also seen substantial investment from Apple. The deal involves new buildings and parking garages on land that is currently surface lots at Innovation Place.

title: 5 Of HP’s Biggest Gaffes
summary: This article details five major gaffes made by HP leading up to its 2015 split into HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise. These gaffes include Mark Hurd's resignation, the failed Palm acquisition, illegal sales to Iran, the costly Autonomy write-down, and a scandal involving spying on journalists.

title: Amazon opens a physical bookstore, but still hopes you buy online
summary: Amazon opened its first physical bookstore, Amazon Books, in Seattle, 15 years after starting as an online bookseller. The store aims to be a physical extension of Amazon.com, allowing customers to try out Amazon devices and browse books displayed with online customer ratings. Prices in the store match online prices, and customers are encouraged to browse in-store but buy online.

title: 3 Cell Tower Stocks With Huge New Network Upside Potential for 2016
summary: This article highlights three cell tower stocks—American Tower, Crown Castle, and SBA Communications—that Cowen believes have significant upside potential for 2016 due to the impending growth of 5G and Heterogeneous Networks (HetNets). The increasing demand for mobile data is expected to drive substantial spending in mobile infrastructure, benefiting these top companies. Cowen rated all three stocks as Outperform, with specific price targets provided for each.

title: Box’s CEO Is The Latest Silicon Valley Exec To Teach A Stanford Class
summary: Aaron Levie, CEO of Box, is co-teaching a class at Stanford's graduate business school called “Industrialist’s Dilemma.” The course will explore how large companies are disrupted by startups and how all companies are becoming digital, featuring insights from Fortune 500 CEOs and startup founders. Despite never graduating college himself, Levie's class will address crucial questions about innovation and disruption in today's business landscape.

title: Gigafactory lands on Tesla’s fast track to Big Battery era
summary: This article details Tesla's ambitious Gigafactory project in Nevada, a $5 billion, 10-million-square-foot plant aimed at mass-producing lithium-ion batteries to significantly reduce costs and support the widespread adoption of electric cars and renewable energy storage. The Gigafactory, built in partnership with Panasonic, is critical for Tesla to meet its projected production goals and solidify its position as a major manufacturer, facing both significant opportunities and risks in a rapidly evolving market. The article also touches on the intense bidding war between states for the factory, with Nevada ultimately securing the project through a substantial tax incentive package.

title: Can Tesla’s enthusiast customers help it sell the electric car for the everyperson?
summary: This article explores how Tesla's enthusiastic early adopters, like "Darrell," are crucial to its initial success but questions if their fervor can translate to the mass market for the upcoming Model 3. It delves into the challenges Tesla faces in affordability, customer service scalability, and managing the "elephant in the room" – battery degradation and replacement costs, which could hinder its transition to a broader consumer base.

title: SimpliSafe Home Security System
summary: The article reviews the SimpliSafe Home Security System, highlighting its ease of setup, wireless design, and flexible, affordable monthly monitoring plans. It discusses the hardware components, pricing for various packages and individual sensors, and the limitations, such as the lack of camera integration and smart home protocol support. The review concludes that SimpliSafe is a compelling DIY security solution, especially for those seeking affordability and no long-term contracts.

title: Is StubHub Back on Track?
summary: StubHub, acquired by eBay in 2007, faced decline due to new mobile-first competitors and a problematic pricing scheme. The article details how new leader Scott Cutler, formerly of the NYSE, is revitalizing the company by reversing unpopular policies, replatforming its technology, and planning expansion into new markets and the primary ticketing sector. Early results show promise, with record sales and increased gross merchandise volume for eBay.

title: Apple is much more than a hardware company
summary: This article argues that Apple is undervalued because it is perceived as a hardware company, rather than a services company. Goldman Sachs' report supports this view, highlighting Apple's recurring revenue from its "cocoon" of content and services, which could significantly increase per-user revenue. The author also touches on the strong performance of US markets and another favoured stock, Nike, due to increased stock buy-backs and a stock split.

title: Qualcomm signs new licensing pact with Xiaomi
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has signed a licensing agreement with Xiaomi Technology Co Ltd, allowing the Chinese smartphone maker to use Qualcomm's patents for 3G and 4G devices. This pact aligns with Qualcomm's rectification plan submitted to China's National Reform and Development Commission, following earlier disputes over patent royalties. Qualcomm's shares rose significantly after the announcement, recouping some losses from the year.

title: Analyzing the Competitive Landscape of the Medical Device Industry
summary: The medical device industry in the US is characterized by a few major players and numerous small and medium enterprises, making it highly competitive due to specialized niche products and rapid innovation cycles. Large manufacturers are pursuing consolidation through M&A and strategic partnerships to achieve economies of scale and offer integrated solutions, while consumer tech companies are also entering the space, bringing analytics and cloud solutions to medical devices. This dynamic landscape emphasizes breakthrough innovation, data analytics, and cost efficiencies as key factors for success.

title: BackStab: Mobile Backup Data Under Attack from Malware
summary: A whitepaper reveals that malware is stealing private mobile device data by accessing local backup files stored on PCs and Macs, a technique dubbed "BackStab." This attack has been observed globally for over five years, affecting iOS and BlackBerry devices by exploiting unencrypted backup files. Users are advised to encrypt backups, use strong passwords, update their OS, and be cautious when connecting devices to untrusted computers to mitigate this threat.

title: Johnson & Johnson, Medtronic make BCG’s most innovative list
summary: Johnson & Johnson and Medtronic were recognized on Boston Consulting Group's list of the 50 most innovative companies, ranking 20th and 42nd respectively. The rankings were determined by a survey of 1,500 senior executives and three financial measures, with innovation assessed on speed, R&D processes, technological platforms, and market exploration. The report highlighted overly long development times as a major obstacle to innovation returns.

title: Choosing an iPad Pro Keyboard (Published 2015)
summary: This article discusses keyboard options for the iPad Pro, comparing Apple's Smart Keyboard with other alternatives. It highlights the features of Apple's Smart Keyboard, such as its Smart Connector technology and design, and also explores options from Logitech and various Bluetooth keyboards, noting their prices and functionalities. The piece aims to help users decide which keyboard best suits their needs and budget for their iPad Pro.

title: Walmart Launches Walmart Pay
summary: Walmart has introduced Walmart Pay, a new mobile payment solution that allows customers to pay in stores using their smartphones. This service is compatible with both iOS and Android devices, works at any checkout lane, and accepts major credit, debit, prepaid, and Walmart gift cards. The company plans a nationwide rollout by the first half of the following year, leveraging its popular mobile app which already boasts 22 million active monthly users.

title: Wal-Mart to Offer Smartphone Payments in Stores
summary: Walmart is set to launch its own mobile payment system, Walmart Pay, allowing shoppers to use their smartphones to pay at registers using stored payment information from their Walmart.com accounts. This move positions the retail giant as a competitor to existing mobile payment providers like Apple Pay. The service is expected to roll out to 4,600 U.S. stores in the first half of the coming year.

title: Walmart launching mobile payments in stores
summary: Walmart is launching a new digital payment method called Walmart Pay, allowing customers to pay in stores by scanning a QR code with their smartphone. This move aims to simplify the checkout process and build deeper relationships with mobile-savvy shoppers. While it offers convenience, some analysts question its potential to significantly impact Walmart's overall business or attract new customers.

title: Microsoft’s Cortana comes to Android, iOS devices in US and China
summary: Microsoft has rolled out its voice-assistant Cortana for select iPhone and Android devices, as well as Cyanogen OS-powered devices, in the US and China. Cortana, a rival to Google Now and Apple's Siri, will allow Windows PC users to connect their mobile devices to their PCs and offer features like setting reminders and tracking flights, with changes syncing across all devices. While some Windows Phone features like hands-free activation or toggling settings won't be available on Android and iOS, Cyanogen devices will have a more integrated experience allowing "Hey Cortana" activation and control over network modes and Quiet Mode.

title: Apple Adds Omnicom's Critical Mass to Digital Agency Roster
summary: Apple has added Critical Mass, an Omnicom agency, to its roster of digital agencies. This move indicates Apple's continued expansion and diversification of its digital marketing partners.

title: Apple iPhone 7 OLED display to be supplied by LG, Samsung: report
summary: LG Display Co Ltd and Samsung Electronics' panel-making unit are reportedly set to supply OLED screens for Apple Inc's iPhones, starting with the iPhone 7. This move follows years of speculation about Apple adopting next-generation display technology, with the Korean firms planning significant capital expenditure to boost OLED production capacity. Apple may also provide funding to assist with these investments.

title: Goldman Sachs Has a New Model: Apple
summary: Goldman Sachs is adopting a new strategy by allowing rival investment products to be sold through its web application, Securities DataBase, traditionally used for analyzing risk and measuring securities. This move emulates Apple's platform model, aiming to create an online hub for various financial products, including those from competitors. The bank, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS), seeks to broaden its reach and become a central platform for customers and other firms.

title: Samsung, LG join forces
summary: LG Display and Samsung Display are reportedly nearing an agreement to supply OLED screens for Apple Inc.'s iPhones, with a combined capital expenditure of 15 trillion won ($F27 billion) planned for OLED production. Apple is expected to contribute some funding to these investments. The shift to OLED screens for iPhones is anticipated to begin in 2018.

title: General Motors invests $500M in Lyft, forms partnership
summary: General Motors announced a $500 million investment in ride-sharing company Lyft, as part of a $1 billion fundraising round for Lyft. This partnership aims to provide Lyft drivers with more affordable vehicle access through GM's financing and vehicle supply, and to accelerate the development of autonomous vehicle fleets. The collaboration positions both companies to compete in the evolving urban mobility and self-driving car market, with GM gaining a board seat and access to Lyft's software, and Lyft benefiting from GM's manufacturing and global reach.

title: Country Club Plaza will sell for $660 million
summary: Highwoods Properties has announced the sale of Country Club Plaza for $660 million to a joint venture between Taubman Centers and The Macerich Co. The sale, expected to close on February 1st, marks only the second ownership change for the historic shopping district opened in 1923. The new owners plan to acquire substantially all of the Plaza, comprising retail and office space, with intentions to maintain its high-quality status and potentially attract new tenants.

title: GM invests $500 million in Lyft, sets out self-driving car partnership
summary: General Motors announced a $500 million investment in Lyft and a strategic partnership to develop an on-demand network of self-driving cars. This significant deal highlights automakers' efforts to integrate with technology companies and counter Silicon Valley's growing influence in the future of transportation and autonomous vehicles. The collaboration aims to bring self-driving cars to consumers primarily through ride-sharing services, with GM President Dan Ammann joining Lyft's board.

title: Enterprises Go 100% Digital with DocuSign on iOS Devices to Deliver Great Customer Experiences
summary: DocuSign is enabling companies of all sizes to fully digitize transactions using its Digital Transaction Management platform and eSignature solution on iOS devices, enhancing customer experiences, productivity, and security.
