The company highlights its mobility partnership with Apple and showcases several clients like Comcast Business, Tishman Construction, and the Nevada Department of Transportation, who are leveraging DocuSign on iPads and iPhones to streamline operations and accelerate workflows. This digital transformation eliminates reliance on paper processes, leading to faster, more convenient, and trusted business transactions.

title: Apple Plans To Expand In Latin America
summary: Apple Inc. is planning a significant retail expansion across Latin America, starting with Mexico City, with further stores planned for Peru, Chile, and Argentina. This move is seen as an effort to drive revenue amidst an anticipated slowdown in the global smartphone market, particularly in China. The company previously opened its first Latin American store in Brazil in 2014.

title: Diabetes: Insulet launches Glooko-linked data management system
summary: Insulet launched a new data management system for its OmniPod device, partnering with Glooko's diabetes management platform. This system allows patients and healthcare providers to view and share data from OmniPod devices on mobile devices and computers, helping to optimize diabetes management through comprehensive data analysis. The collaboration aims to provide a complete picture of a patient's health, integrating insulin data with blood glucose levels, diet, and exercise.

title: Netflix’s Latest Move Is a Huge Win for Travelers
summary: Netflix announced a major expansion, launching in 130 new countries and bringing its total availability to 190 countries globally. This move, dubbed "the birth of a global Internet TV network" by CEO Reed Hastings, makes Netflix accessible in most parts of the world, excluding China, Crimea, North Korea, and Syria. The company also plans to release two dozen original films and documentaries, new kids' series, and stand-up comedy specials in 2016.

title: It’s True: Apple Will Drop Headphone Jack To Make The iPhone 7 Slimmer, Says Source
summary: A source with knowledge of Apple's plans confirms that the iPhone 7 will drop the 3.5mm headphone jack, making it thinner, and will likely feature wireless charging and waterproofing. The new phone will rely on its Lightning port for wired headphones and will incorporate new noise-canceling technology developed with Cirrus Logic and Wolfson Microelectronics. Apple may also sell separate Lightning-connected, noise-canceling headphones, possibly under the Beats brand.

title: Which IBM Partnerships and Acquisitions Have Boosted Watson?
summary: IBM has strategically used acquisitions and partnerships to enhance its Watson AI platform, particularly in the healthcare sector. Key acquisitions like Merge Healthcare and AlchemyAPI boosted Watson's capabilities, while partnerships with Apple, Johnson & Johnson, and Medtronic leveraged personal health device data. IBM anticipates Watson becoming a $10 billion business, reflecting its significant investment in data analytics, cloud, and mobile technologies.

title: Southwest Airlines Launches Updated Mobile App For iOS Users
summary: Southwest Airlines has launched an updated version of its iOS mobile app, featuring a faster and more streamlined interface with new functionalities like express booking. This update aims to enhance the mobile experience for customers, making it easier to browse and book flights, access trip information, and utilize Rapid Rewards features. The app integrates with Apple Wallet for boarding passes and provides updated interfaces for tracking Rapid Rewards tier status.

title: Al Gore Sees Technology Creating a Global Hive Mind
summary: Al Gore believes that the internet and mobile devices are forming a "global mind" that is transforming politics and human interaction. Speaking at Cheetah Mobile’s Connect 2016 conference, he cited examples like the collective online reaction leading to the destruction of a Mao Zedong statue. Gore urged entrepreneurs to consider their broader influence beyond commercial goals, while also acknowledging the government's enduring role.

title: SKT faces criticism for offering free iPhone 4 promotion
summary: SK Telecom is facing public backlash for offering Apple's 5-year-old iPhone 4 for free as a promotion to attract budget smartphone customers. Critics argue it's a desperate attempt to clear old inventory due to the iPhone 4's outdated features, 3G network limitation, and incompatibility with current iOS versions and popular apps like KakaoTalk. SK Telecom defends the promotion by stating it targets users who want to try an iPhone or need a secondary, price-competitive model.

title: After abandoning iAd revenue, Apple Inc can reintroduce an ad-free internet
summary: Apple's decision to abandon iAd revenue opens the door for a new strategy focused on an ad-free internet, leveraging its strong hardware sales and user ecosystem. The article argues that Apple can starve Google's core advertising business by offering privacy-focused services and content, thereby differentiating itself from ad-supported platforms. This shift capitalizes on the diminishing value of display ads and the increasing demand for high-quality, subscription-based content.

title: SKT to Release New Budget Smartphone, Sol
summary: SK Telecom announced the release of its new budget smartphone, Sol, set to go on sale from January 22nd with pre-orders opening on January 19th. The Sol, priced at 19,800 won (US$16.30), is planned by SK Telecom, produced by TCL's Alcatel, and accompanied by a valuable accessory package. The device is notably lightweight, features a 5.5-inch full HD display, and boasts a 13MP rear camera.

title: Apple acquisition of UC San Diego startup paves way for further robotics research
summary: Apple has acquired Emotient, a UC San Diego startup specializing in AI-based facial emotion recognition, for an undisclosed price. As part of the deal, Emotient's co-founders and several former UC San Diego students have joined Apple. This acquisition is one of several recent AI-related purchases by Apple and leaves the Machine Perception Lab at UC San Diego to reconfigure its research around the Diego-san robot.

title: Times and Sunday Times to launch weekly digital edition
summary: The Times and Sunday Times are launching a new weekly digital edition called "Times of London Weekly," aimed at international subscribers across 46 countries. The app will offer a curated selection of news, business, sports, features, and investigations from both newspapers for £2.99 a month after a free trial. This initiative follows the Times' successful paywall strategy and a recent digital bundle tie-up with the Wall Street Journal.

title: Scarlet Mimic: Years-Long Espionage Campaign Targets Minority Activists
summary: Unit 42's investigation reveals "Scarlet Mimic," a sophisticated cyber espionage group active for over four years, primarily targeting minority rights activists, particularly Uyghur and Tibetan individuals. The group uses advanced malware like FakeM, Psylo, and MobileOrder, expanding its attacks from PCs to mobile devices, and employing spear-phishing and watering hole tactics. This report aims to expose their methods to enhance awareness and improve countermeasures against this well-resourced adversary.

title: Twitter Names American Express Executive Berland as CMO
summary: Twitter Inc. appointed Leslie Berland as its new chief marketing officer as the company undergoes a significant leadership restructuring to boost growth. Berland, formerly a senior vice president at American Express Co., will be tasked with revitalizing Twitter's marketing efforts. This appointment comes as Twitter seeks to recover and expand its user base.

title: The chip guru who built Apple's Ax microprocessors joins Tesla to lead the Autopilot Hardware Engineering team
summary: Tesla has hired Jim Keller, a high-profile microprocessor engineer known for his work on Apple's A4 and A5 processors and AMD's Zen architecture, as Vice President of Autopilot Hardware Engineering. Keller will be responsible for developing advanced autopilot systems, leveraging his expertise in low-power design. This move follows Tesla's search for "hardcore" engineers for its Autopilot team and adds another Apple alumnus to its senior staff.

title: How the 2008 Stock Market Crash Compares to 2016
summary: This article compares the market turbulence of early 2016 to the 2008 stock market crash, noting similarities like inflated real estate bubbles (China in 2016 vs. US in 2008) and expert warnings. While the current downturn is less severe than 2008 due to a lack of deep systemic problems, investors are advised to prepare for continued volatility and consider long-term investments or inverse ETFs.

title: Robert F. Smith (1962- )
summary: Robert F. Smith is celebrated as the founder, chairman, and CEO of Vista Equity Partners, a multi-billion-dollar private equity firm focusing on enterprise software. Ranked among the richest individuals in the U.S., Smith is also a significant philanthropist, known for major donations to educational institutions and initiatives supporting inner-city youth and African American advancement in technology and education. His career transitioned from engineering and patents to finance at Goldman Sachs before establishing his highly successful private equity venture.

title: Four-part video tutorial taps Bob Ross lookalike to promote Photoshop Sketch for iOS
summary: Adobe has launched a four-part video tutorial series featuring a Bob Ross lookalike to promote its Photoshop Sketch app for iOS, specifically with the iPad Pro and Apple Pencil. The series, created in cooperation with Bob Ross Inc., aims to capture the beloved painter's essence while showcasing the app's capabilities. Viewers generally reacted positively to the unique promotional campaign.

title: Future iPhones Could Be Controlled Without Touch
summary: Apple Inc. received a patent for "Proximity and Multi-touch Sensor Detection and Modulation," suggesting future iPhones, possibly the "iPhone 7," could be controlled with touch-free hovering hand gestures. This technology would use existing proximity sensors and additional sensors to detect hand movements. The patent also proposes using this invention in other devices and applications beyond Apple products, such as ATMs and gaming machines, though its implementation timeline remains unknown.

title: Apple Now Accepting Your Banged-up iPhone
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple is now accepting banged-up iPhones as trade-ins for upgrades, a new policy that expands on its previous requirement for intact screens and working buttons. This change, which applies to iPhone 5 models and later, aims to encourage more users to upgrade to newer iPhones. Additionally, customers can now get screen protectors installed in Apple stores.

title: Sundar Pichai gets $199 million in stock grant, biggest ever given to a Google executive
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai has been awarded a record $199 million stock grant from Alphabet, making it the largest ever given to a Google executive and positioning him as one of the highest-paid executives of a publicly traded company for the year. The grant comprises 273,328 Class C shares that will vest quarterly through 2019, contingent on his continued employment. This news comes amidst other executive stock grants and the announcement of Amit Singhal's departure from Google.

title: Hasbro, Backflip Studios and Space Ape Games Bring The Fight Home with TRANSFORMERS: EARTH WARS; Game Coming To iOS And Android in Select Markets Spring 2016
summary: Hasbro, Backflip Studios, and Space Ape Games announced the upcoming release of TRANSFORMERS: EARTH WARS, a real-time combat strategy mobile game for iOS and Android, launching in Spring 2016. Players can choose to battle as AUTOBOTS or DECEPTICONS, utilizing iconic characters like OPTIMUS PRIME and MEGATRON, in a global real-time combat experience. The game will feature authentic TRANSFORMERS storytelling and voice actors, including Peter Cullen and Frank Welker, and will be free to download with in-game purchases.

title: Most Searched Tickers Among Financial Advisors: Apple and Alphabet Share the Lead; Focus on Tech, Consumer Stocks
summary: Gilead Sciences, Inc. (NASDAQ:GILD) emerged as the third-most searched ticker among financial advisors after reporting better-than-expected earnings and revenue. Despite forecasting lower-than-Street-forecasted product sales for 2016, the company's increased dividend and expanded buyback program led to a stock appreciation of 2.5%. Columbus Circle Investors, managed by Clifford G. Fox, remains a significant bullish investor in Gilead, holding 3.23 million shares.

title: Apple, Inc set to open new $25 million tech center in Hyderabad, India by June
summary: Apple is reportedly opening its first technology development center in India, an investment of $25 million in Hyderabad, creating 4,500 jobs. This move signifies Apple's increased focus on the Indian market, following growing sales and efforts to open Apple Stores in the country. The article also details Apple's existing international facilities and R&D centers worldwide, highlighting its global expansion strategy.

title: Customer Letter
summary: Apple released a public letter explaining its opposition to a U.S. government demand to build a "backdoor" into the iPhone for the San Bernardino investigation. The company argues that creating such a tool would compromise the security of all iPhone users, set a dangerous precedent, and weaken encryption that protects personal data from hackers and criminals. Apple emphasizes its commitment to safeguarding customer privacy and states that acceding to the government's request would undermine decades of security advancements.

title: Apple's Tim Cook blasts order to unlock iPhone, vowing to fight court order
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook has vowed to fight a U.S. court order compelling the company to unlock an encrypted iPhone belonging to one of the San Bernardino shooters. Cook argues that creating such a "backdoor" would have far-reaching implications for digital privacy and security, potentially compromising hundreds of millions of devices. This stance sets the stage for a significant legal battle between Silicon Valley and the federal government over technology policy and national security.

title: Apple opposes order to help FBI unlock phone belonging to San Bernardino shooter
summary: Apple Inc. CEO Tim Cook announced the company will resist a federal judge's order to help the FBI unlock an iPhone belonging to one of the San Bernardino shooters, citing concerns that creating such a backdoor could compromise encryption for all users. The FBI seeks access to encrypted data to fill an 18-minute gap in the attackers' timeline and investigate potential accomplices, but Apple views the court order as an "overreach by the U.S. government." This standoff highlights the ongoing conflict between tech companies prioritizing user privacy and law enforcement demands for security access.

title: Security expert: Arm-twisting not the answer in Apple v. FBI case
summary: Indrajit Ray, a CSU professor of computer science, weighs in on the Apple v. FBI case, explaining the iPhone's security technology and the implications of the government's request. He argues that forcing Apple to create software to bypass security sets a dangerous precedent and that a proper legal mechanism, not "arm-twisting," is needed to balance law enforcement needs with privacy concerns.

title: Caterpillar-licensed phone boasts thermal imaging
summary: Caterpillar is releasing the Cat S60 smartphone, which is licensed through British firm Bullitt and features thermal imaging. This rugged and waterproof phone is designed for durability and can function in extreme conditions, appealing to various users like firefighters and skiers. Retailing for $599, it is part of a line of Cat phones known for meeting military standards.

title: Apple Pay Launches in China
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple Pay has officially launched in China, marking its first entry into the Asian market. The mobile payment system is collaborating with UnionPay and 19 local banks, aiming to compete with established local brands like Alipay and WeChat Wallet. The move is seen as beneficial for China's national banks and raises questions about data privacy and encryption, especially given Apple's ongoing resistance to U.S. government requests for iPhone encryption access.

title: Apple and its smartwatch rivals overtake the mighty Swiss watch industry
summary: In the fourth quarter of 2015, smartwatch shipments, led by Apple Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., surpassed the sales of the traditional Swiss watch industry for the first time. This shift indicates a consumer migration towards tech-focused wearable options. While many companies are entering the smartwatch market, Swiss watchmakers are notably lagging.

title: More Support for Justice Department Than for Apple in Dispute Over Unlocking iPhone
summary: A Pew Research Center survey found that 51% of Americans believe Apple should unlock the iPhone for the FBI's San Bernardino investigation, while 38% believe they shouldn't to protect user security. There were slight partisan differences, with Republicans and Democrats showing similar support for unlocking, but independents were divided. Age and smartphone ownership also influenced views, with younger adults divided and smartphone owners more likely to oppose unlocking the device.

title: I got hacked mid-air while writing an Apple-FBI story
summary: A journalist writing about the Apple-FBI standoff experienced a personal wake-up call about online privacy when he was hacked while using Gogo in-flight Wi-Fi. A stranger on his flight accessed his emails, demonstrating the vulnerability of public networks and the broader implications of the Apple-FBI case for everyone's digital security. The incident underscored the importance of encryption and strong privacy practices.

title: Why Alphabet Stock Is Our Pick of the Week
summary: The article names Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG) as Money Morning's Pick of the Week, highlighting its consistent earnings, innovative ventures, and dominance in the search market. Despite a recent slip, analysts see the stock's pullback as a buying opportunity, projecting significant gains. The company's strong free cash flow allows for investment in "moonshot" projects, securing its future growth.

title: First Verily, DexCom Product Launch Slated for 2018
summary: DexCom CEO Kevin Sayer reports that the company is on schedule with its long-term goals for growth in advanced diabetes management, with the first product from its partnership with Verily slated for launch in 2018. This will be followed by a next-generation, disposable CGM system in 2021. DexCom also saw significant revenue growth in 2015 and launched the G5 Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) System sooner than expected, with plans for Android compatibility in 2016 and G6 technology application by May.

title: Autodesk part of software group backing Apple
summary: Autodesk is joining a consortium, including the Business Software Alliance, to file an amicus brief supporting Apple in its standoff with the FBI over iPhone access. Apple, and its supporters, contend that the FBI's request for new software could compromise user data and privacy, especially for international clients. While supporting data privacy, Autodesk's CEO Carl Bass also believes the FBI should get the information it needs in this specific case without setting a precedent for unfettered data access, suggesting a resolution behind closed doors.

title: A Future iPhone Could Be Bendable, Foldable
summary: Apple Inc. has been granted a patent for "Flexible electronic devices" which suggests future iPhones could be designed to bend and fold purposefully. This technology aims to make devices more durable by allowing them to absorb impact through deformation, and could also enable features like sound transmission through the screen or gesture controls. While a flexible iPhone is used for illustration, the patent applies to other Apple products such as the Apple Watch, iPad, iPod, and Mac.

title: Meet The 30 FinTech Startups Shortlisted By HDFC Bank For Digital Innovation Summit
summary: HDFC Bank is organizing a Digital Innovation Summit on March 3-4, 2016, to encourage innovation in the FinTech space, shortlisting 30 startups from 105 applicants. These startups will showcase their ideas in categories such as Payments, Mobile Innovation, and Analytics, with the top three winners getting a chance to partner with HDFC Bank. The initiative aims to enhance customer experience and tap into the rapidly growing digital transaction landscape.

title: New OS X Ransomware KeRanger Infected Transmission BitTorrent Client Installer
summary: Palo Alto Networks detected the KeRanger ransomware infecting the Transmission BitTorrent client installer for OS X on March 4, 2016. This is believed to be the first fully functional ransomware for the OS X platform. The malware bypassed Apple’s Gatekeeper, encrypted user files after a three-day delay, and demanded a one-bitcoin ransom.

title: Steve Wozniak: ‘I Side With Apple’ in FBI Case
summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak has publicly stated his support for Apple in its legal dispute with the FBI, emphasizing concerns about potential demands from other governments like China for "back doors" into phones. Wozniak, also a founding member of the Electronic Frontier Foundation, aligns with Apple's stance against a federal magistrate's order to assist the FBI in accessing data from an attacker's iPhone.

title: Will Apple, Inc. Go Bigger Than "Bigger Than Bigger"?
summary: This article discusses Apple's potential move to even larger iPhone displays, following the success of the iPhone 6 and 6 Plus. It explores rumors of a 5.8-inch AMOLED iPhone for 2017 or 2018, the strategic implications for pricing and product positioning, and the challenges of increasing product breadth while maintaining simplicity.

title: Opera browser now has a built-in ad blocker
summary: Opera has launched a new desktop browser with a native ad blocker, claiming it can cut page-loading times by as much as 90% and is 45% faster than Chrome with AdBlock Plus. The feature is deactivated by default but the browser suggests enabling it if ads are detected, and users can manage an exception list for websites. This development comes as other entities like Google, Samsung, and Apple are also navigating the ad-blocking landscape, impacting publishers and raising discussions about "acceptable ads."

title: General Motors acquires self-driving car startup Cruise Automation
summary: General Motors has acquired Silicon Valley self-driving car startup Cruise Automation to gain a technological advantage in the rapidly evolving autonomous vehicle market. This move allows GM to compete with tech giants like Apple and Alphabet, who are also developing self-driving projects, and positions GM to benefit from Cruise's advanced autopilot systems previously used by competitors. Despite some road-testing incidents and challenges with infrastructure, the acquisition is seen as a strategic step to accelerate GM's autonomous vehicle technology development.

title: AceDeceiver: First iOS Trojan Exploiting Apple DRM Design Flaws to Infect Any iOS Device
summary: Palo Alto Networks has discovered AceDeceiver, a new family of iOS malware capable of infecting non-jailbroken devices by exploiting design flaws in Apple's FairPlay DRM mechanism. This malware uses a "FairPlay Man-In-The-Middle (MITM)" attack, a technique previously used for pirating apps, to install malicious applications without enterprise certificates or user confirmation. Although Apple removed the initial malicious apps from the App Store, the attack remains viable as it relies on the apps having been available once and a PC client called "Aisi Helper" to surreptitiously install them.

title: EeGeo picks up $5M to build better 3D visuals for interiors and VR experiences
summary: London-based eeGeo, a company specializing in interactive 3D mapping for interiors and VR, has raised $5 million in funding. The investment, led by NetSol Technologies, values eeGeo at over $26 million and will be used to expand its business and move into virtual reality environments. eeGeo plans to enhance its mapping data through crowdsourcing and continues to offer flexible 3D visualization solutions to its clients.

title: Apple Using Google’s Cloud, Moving Some Data From Amazon
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly moving some of its data from Amazon.com Inc.'s cloud services to Google's computing-on-demand service. This move represents a significant win for Alphabet Inc.'s Google in its competition with Amazon and Microsoft Corp. for cloud computing customers. It also signals Google's progress in the growing $26 billion cloud computing market.

title: Apple Co-Founder Steve Wozniak Still Doesn’t Quite Get Why Steve Jobs Was a Genius
summary: The article discusses Steve Wozniak's criticism of the Apple Watch, highlighting his view that its high price is based solely on watchbands, which misses the essence of Apple's appeal. The author argues that Wozniak, being a "geek," underestimates the importance of design, usability, and marketing—areas where Steve Jobs excelled. The piece asserts that Jobs's genius lay in creating beautiful, functional products that users desired, a concept Wozniak appears not to fully grasp.

title: 6 People Who Dramatically Influenced Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff’s Life
summary: This article highlights six influential individuals who played a significant role in shaping Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff's career and philanthropic views. From his father and Steve Jobs to Larry Ellison and Colin Powell, these figures provided mentorship, business lessons, and inspired his approach to philanthropy. The piece emphasizes that Benioff's success as a Silicon Valley titan was not accidental but a result of these key relationships.

title: PayPal Brings Money Transfers to Cuba
summary: PayPal announced it will begin allowing money transfers to and from Cuba later this year, using its acquired company Xoom. This move coincides with President Obama's visit to Cuba and follows the loosening of trade restrictions, opening up the Cuban market to U.S. bedrijven. The opportunity for expansion is significant, with an estimated $2 billion sent from the U.S. to Cuba annually.

title: iPhone 6 Series Now Represents Majority of U.S. iPhone Installed Base
summary: Comscore's 2016 U.S. Cross-Platform Future in Focus report reveals that the iPhone 6 and 6 Plus series now constitute the majority of iPhones in the U.S., with over 30 million new users. While most upgraded from older iPhone models, Apple still saw 7.5 million incremental iPhone owners. The introduction of the iPhone SE, with its smaller screen and lower price, is expected to attract both existing iPhone 5 users and first-time iPhone owners, contributing to the overall growth of U.S. iPhone ownership.

title: AutoCAD 360, AutoCAD 360 Pro, Apple iPad Pro: Made for Each Other
summary: This article highlights the full compatibility between AutoCAD 360, AutoCAD 360 Pro, and the Apple iPad Pro, emphasizing how these products are designed to work seamlessly together. It details features like Apple Pencil support in the Pro version and new drawing tools, explaining how they enhance the mobile CAD experience for users on the go. The benefits are further illustrated through a customer testimonial from a civil engineering firm.

title: Supreme Court Decides to Hear Samsung v. Apple, Appears Ready to Weigh-In on Patent Damage Calculations
summary: The Supreme Court has granted certiorari in Samsung Electronics Co. v. Apple Inc., agreeing to hear a design patent case for the first time in over a century. The case will address whether an award of infringer’s profits for a design patent applied to only a component of a product should be limited to profits attributable to that component. This decision could significantly impact patent damage calculations, particularly given the prevalence of design patents in modern smartphones and the potential for a shift in how damages are awarded for patent infringement.

title: Apple Inc.'s campus in Austin
summary: This article provides details and images of Apple Inc.'s Austin campus, highlighting its features and size. The campus spans 38 acres with 1.1 million square feet of office space, including a wellness center and doctor's office. It houses various technology giant workers, with four buildings open and three under construction as of August 2015.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai was paid over $100 MILLION on his first year in the job
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai received over $100 million in compensation during his first year, according to a recent public filing. This included a $652,500 annual salary and $99.8 million in restricted stock in 2015, with an additional $199 million in restricted shares awarded last month. The significant compensation comes as Google's parent company, Alphabet, surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable public company.
authors: MIA DE GRAAF FOR DAILYMAIL.COM and ASSOCIATED PRESS

title: Dr. Michael Burry Of 'The Big Short' Holds Citigroup, Bank Of America In First Portfolio Filing Since 2008
summary: Dr. Michael Burry, famously known from "The Big Short" for his accurate prediction of the subprime mortgage crisis, has revealed holdings in Citigroup and Bank of America in his new firm's first public portfolio filing since 2008. His new fund, Scion Asset Management, is heavily invested in financial services, technology, and healthcare, with financials making up 32.4% of the portfolio. This strategic move into banks, which he once criticized, suggests a shift in his valuation perspective regarding these companies after years of struggling with low valuations and perceived opaqueness.

title: Apple turns 40 today. It almost didn’t make it to 20
summary: On Apple's 40th birthday, this article revisits a 1996 report when the company was struggling, with its stock price around $1 per share and significant layoffs. The piece contrasts Apple's proprietary system with Microsoft's open approach, which initially led to Apple's difficulties, but highlights how changes like opening its OS and the rise of the internet, along with Steve Jobs's return, revitalized the company, making it incredibly successful today. It also notes the ironic shift where Apple now benefits from user "path dependency" similar to what Microsoft once enjoyed.

title: Industrial Design Important Part of Apple's Success
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: This article highlights how industrial design has been a crucial element of Apple's success from its early days, driven by Steve Jobs's vision. It discusses how Apple products, from the iMac G3 to the iPhone, revolutionized consumer electronics with their aesthetic appeal, user-centered design, and integrated hardware and software. The piece also credits designers like Jonathan Ive and influences like Dieter Rams for shaping Apple's distinctive approach to product development.

title: Apple Inc. Plan To Sell Used iPhones In India Faces Challenge As Officials Worry About Electronics Dumping
summary: Apple Inc. is facing significant challenges in its plan to sell refurbished iPhones in India, a strategy aimed at countering falling global sales and increasing market share in developing economies. The Indian government and local smartphone manufacturers oppose the move, citing concerns about "electronics dumping" and their "Make in India" initiative. This opposition pushes Apple to explore alternative solutions, such as local assembly of refurbished parts, given Foxconn's existing presence in the country.

title: HP’s new premium brand logo revealed with Spectre laptop
summary: HP has unveiled its new premium brand logo alongside the launch of a sleek, ultra-thin laptop named Spectre. This move follows HP's split into Hewlett-Packard Enterprise and HP Inc., with the latter targeting Apple's MacBook line-up. The new logo will be reserved for HP's premium laptops, signaling a strong push to regain momentum in the laptop market.

title: Turtle Beach Impact 500 Mechanical Keyboard Review
summary: The Turtle Beach Impact 500 mechanical keyboard is reviewed as a simple, compact, and solid option for TKL fans looking for a no-frills experience. While it works with Macs and older PCs and has little extraneous key noise, its price is considered a bit steep for a basic model, especially given the use of Costar stabilizers. The article delves into its design, specifications, and performance, noting Turtle Beach's entry into the gaming peripherals market.

title: Amazon Slashes Kindle Price, Adds Free 2-Hour Delivery
summary: Amazon has reduced the prices of some Kindle e-readers by up to $50 and introduced a two-hour delivery service, with an expiration date attached, for its Prime members. This strategic move appears designed to attract more customers to other Amazon products, particularly those available through its Prime Now service. The article speculates that the Kindle offer is a calculated gamble by CEO Jeff Bezos to gain and retain customers, despite the possibility that Amazon doesn't directly profit from Prime or Kindle itself.

title: The Two Women Who Kicked Off Salesforce's Company-Wide Salary Review
summary: Leyla Seka and Cindy Robbins, two senior Salesforce employees, successfully convinced CEO Marc Benioff to conduct an internal salary audit that revealed and corrected gender-pay disparities, costing the company $3 million. Their initiative, born from their involvement in the Women’s Surge program, led to 6% of employees receiving raises for equal pay. Seka and Robbins hope their experience encourages other companies to address pay equality, emphasizing the importance of transparency and courage.

title: Ford’s 10-Year Plan To Transform Its Headquarters Into A High-Tech Campus
summary: Ford is undertaking a 10-year plan to transform its 7.5-million-square-foot headquarters in Dearborn, Michigan, into a high-tech campus, aiming to reframe itself as a technology company. The renovation will consolidate over 30,000 employees into two main locations, fostering collaboration and connectivity. This move reflects Ford's broader strategy to evolve beyond car manufacturing into a technology and information company, focusing on user experience and smart mobility.

title: Apple Learns an Important Lesson on Integrating Ethics Into Operations
summary: This article discusses how Apple learned a critical lesson about integrating ethics into its operations following a factory explosion at its supplier, Foxconn, in 2011. The author, Gang Li, argues that unethical decisions ultimately lead to higher costs and lower profits in the long run, challenging the traditional economic assumption for low costs. He introduces the "Bullwhip Effect due to Unethical Operations" (BEUO) and proposes a decision-support model to help companies balance cost minimization with sustainability maximization, as demonstrated through Apple's shift in supplier strategy.

title: Apple may soon cut production of iPhone SE: reports
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly planning to continue reducing iPhone production, including the newly launched iPhone SE, through the quarter ending June due to sluggish sales. This follows previous production cuts for iPhone 6s and 6s Plus models. The news caused a dip in Apple's stock and shares of some of its suppliers.

title: Silicon Valley Mourns Loss of ‘Coach’ Bill Campbell
summary: Silicon Valley is mourning the death of Bill Campbell, a renowned mentor and former football coach, who passed away after a long battle with cancer. Campbell was celebrated for his ability to coach and counsel top tech executives at companies like Apple, Google, and Intuit, and for his quiet advocacy for women in technology. Many prominent figures in the tech industry, including Tim Cook and Eric Schmidt, shared their condolences and memories of Campbell on social media, highlighting his significant impact on their careers and the industry.

title: Bill Campbell ‘The Coach’, Signs Off From The World
summary: Bill Campbell, known as 'The Coach' and a legendary mentor to numerous Silicon Valley leaders including Steve Jobs and Larry Page, has passed away at the age of 75 after battling cancer. Campbell was well-known for advising tech executives and served as president, CEO, and chairman of Intuit Inc. His influence on the company's culture and the development of major tech figures is widely recognized.

title: Apple Inc. MacBook Updated With Faster Processors, Longer Battery Life And Rose Gold Option
summary: Apple has refreshed its 12-inch MacBook with the latest Intel Skylake processors, offering improved performance and an additional 30 minutes of battery life. The new models also feature 8GB of RAM as standard and introduce a rose gold color option, bringing the laptop's aesthetic in line with Apple's iOS devices. Configurations start at $1,299 for the 1.1 GHz Core m3 model and $1,599 for the 1.2GHz Core m5 version.

title: WARNING – Don’t Buy Cannabis Stocks!
summary: This article strongly advises against investing in over-the-counter (OTC) cannabis stocks, citing FINRA warnings about scams and pump-and-dumps. While acknowledging the viability of cannabis as an investment theme, it emphasizes avoiding risky OTC companies and instead focusing on legitimate, major exchange-traded companies like GW Pharmaceuticals, Aphria, and Canopy Growth. The piece highlights the significant capital raised by cannabis startups, suggesting future opportunities for retail investors but cautioning against current OTC options.

title: Nine things you may have missed Thursday from the world of business
summary: This article compiles nine key business news items from Thursday, including Hologic's FDA approval for a new breast biopsy system, Biogen exceeding earnings expectations, the European Central Bank maintaining interest rates, and the FBI's expenditure to hack an iPhone. Other topics covered are Sears' store closings, millennial women's retirement savings habits, the low number of female CEOs appointed in 2015, sustained low mortgage rates, and a significant drop in US jobless claims.
authors: ROBERT WEISMAN|ASSOCIATED PRESS|NEW YORK TIMES|BLOOMBERG|WASHINGTON POST

title: Hey, Nokia Isn't Just a Company That Used to Make Phones
summary: This article discusses Nokia's evolution beyond its former mobile phone business, highlighting its successful transition into selling high-end networking gear and software. It examines Nokia's recent acquisition of Withings, a company specializing in smart home and wearable products, and contextualizes this move within Nokia's long history as a diversified holding company making strategic investments in emerging markets. The piece suggests that Nokia's ventures into consumer electronics and health technologies, like the Withings acquisition and the OZO VR camera, are akin to Alphabet's "other bets," positioning Nokia to capitalize on future big trends.

title: Google Chrome Edges Internet Explorer As Most Popular Web Browser
summary: Google Chrome has surpassed Microsoft's Internet Explorer to become the most popular web browser globally, according to NetMarketShare.com data for April. Chrome controlled 41.66% of the market, while IE and Microsoft Edge collectively held 41.35%. This marks a significant shift, largely driven by Microsoft's decision to discontinue IE development in favor of Edge, benefiting Chrome's market share.

title: Oracle’s Larry Ellison urges graduates to pursue their own dreams as USC marks 133rd commencement
summary: Larry Ellison, founder of Oracle Corp., addressed USC's 133rd commencement, encouraging graduates to pursue their own dreams rather than those imposed by others. He shared personal anecdotes, including advice from Steve Jobs, and highlighted his journey from dropping out of college to becoming a tech titan and expert sailor. The ceremony also honored several individuals with honorary degrees and recognized outstanding student achievements.

title: Warren Buffett’s company buys 9.8 million Apple shares
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. purchased 9.81 million shares of Apple Inc. in the first quarter, an investment reportedly made by one of Buffett's stock pickers, not Buffett himself. This stake, valued at $1.07 billion as of March 31, comes despite Buffett's general aversion to technology stocks and Apple's recent stock tumble due to slowing iPhone sales. The move highlights the growing influence of Berkshire’s other portfolio managers.

title: Update | Iconic billionaire Warren Buffett’s Berkshire unveils over US$1 billion stake in Apple
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has disclosed a more than US$1 billion stake in Apple, marking a rare venture into the technology sector for the investment firm. This investment comes despite Apple reporting its first quarterly revenue decline in 13 years, and spurred a 3.71% rise in Apple's shares.

title: Oracle's Safra Catz becomes top-paid female executive
summary: Safra Catz, co-CEO of Oracle Corp., became the highest-paid US female executive in fiscal 2015 with a $56.9 million pay package, topping the Bloomberg Pay Index. Her compensation included special equity awards and an annual salary. Ruth Porat of Alphabet Inc. and Mary Barra of General Motors Co. ranked second and third, respectively, highlighting that once women reach the C-suite, their pay is largely comparable to men.

title: Nokia returns to the phone market as Microsoft sells brand
summary: Microsoft has sold the Nokia brand name, which it had previously acquired, in two parts for $350m, enabling Nokia to re-enter the mobile phone market. A new Finnish company, HMD, formed by ex-Nokia employees, acquired the brand, while iPhone manufacturer Foxconn bought the manufacturing and distribution arms and will build new Nokia phones. The new entity plans to develop Nokia-branded smartphones and tablets running Android, appealing to consumers nostalgic for the brand.

title: Android Pay Arrives at Bank Of America ATMs
summary: Android Pay is expanding its services to Bank of America ATMs, allowing users to withdraw cash by waving their NFC-enabled Android smartphones instead of using a physical card. This technology will first be rolled out in San Francisco and the Bay Area, with plans to expand to 5,000 ATMs by the end of the year. The feature aims to offer a cardless and more convenient way to access ATM services, similar to how mobile payment systems simplify in-store purchases.

title: Applied Materials: Dialed In to the iPhone Rumor Mill
summary: Applied Materials' stock has surged by 17% following its fiscal second-quarter report, which revealed a significant increase in new orders for display panel production equipment. This rise is attributed to rumors that future iPhone models may incorporate OLED displays, which would boost demand for Applied Materials’ manufacturing tools. The company's stock is approaching a 10-year high, up 25% for the year.

title: Fraud Alert: Scammers Get Victims to Pay With iTunes Gift Cards
summary: Scammers are increasingly using iTunes gift cards as a payment method for various scams, including phony debt collection and fake IRS agents, because they are quick, convenient, and untraceable. Both the Federal Trade Commission and the U.S. Treasury Department have issued warnings about this trend, advising consumers that requests for payment via iTunes gift cards are indicative of fraud. Apple has posted a warning on its website, and victims who realize they've been scammed before the money is drained may be able to get a refund.

title: Omnicom and Interpublic make the Fortune 500 list
summary: Omnicom Group and Interpublic Group were the only two advertising industry companies to be included in this year's Fortune 500 list. Omnicom ranked 186 and Interpublic 355, with both companies moving up from their previous year's positions. This marks the 20th year for Omnicom and 19th year for Interpublic on the list, highlighting the continued influence of marketers at big brands within the advertising industry.

title: Supporting the transition to IPv6-only networking services for iOS
summary: This article discusses Apple's requirement for all new iOS apps to support IPv6-only networking services and the ongoing global transition from IPv4 to IPv6 due to IPv4 address exhaustion. It explains the complexities of this transition, common pitfalls for developers, and how Cloudflare assists with IPv6 compatibility through various features like Pseudo IPv4. The article emphasizes the security and performance benefits of adopting IPv6.

title: Salesforce to open centre in Hyderabad
summary: Salesforce.com Inc. is expanding its operations in India by opening a centre of excellence in Hyderabad, creating 1,000 jobs. This will make Hyderabad its biggest hub in India and one of its largest engineering and customer service centres globally, also serving as the headquarters for its India operations. Salesforce's decision mirrors similar moves by other tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Uber to establish significant presences in the city.

title: Apple starts a company to sell excess solar power
summary: Apple has formed a subsidiary named Apple Energy LLC to sell excess electricity generated from its solar panels and other clean energy facilities. The company has applied to the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to sell power at market rates, aiming to power its data centers, Apple Stores, and corporate offices with clean energy and subsidize costs by selling surplus power. If approved, Apple could begin reselling power within 60 days of June 6, directly to customers and eliminating the need for utility power.

title: Birth Of IBM, Oracle, And Calculating Machines: This Week In Tech History
summary: This article chronicles significant moments in tech history corresponding to specific dates, focusing on the origins of IBM, Oracle, and early calculating machines. It details Charles Babbage's Difference Engine, the controversial meeting between John Mauchly and John Atanasoff regarding early electronic computers, and the incorporation of companies that would become IBM and Oracle. It also touches upon early visions for television and the introduction of the LP record, highlighting foundational events and philosophies in technology and business.

title: Jabil Circuit Further Cuts Outlook, Citing Weakness in Mobility
summary: Jabil Circuit Inc. further reduced its annual outlook due to ongoing weakness in its mobility business, which is negatively impacting earnings. The electronics supplier, whose services are used by technology companies worldwide and include Apple Inc. as a major client, announced a stock repurchase program of up to $400 million over the next two fiscal years to reassure shareholders. This marks another cut to its forecast as the company grapples with deteriorating conditions in the mobility sector.

title: Why Apple doesn’t have a venture-capital arm
summary: Apple Inc. stands out among its tech competitors for not having a venture-capital arm, a decision largely attributed to its culture of secrecy, according to experts. Despite a substantial $1 billion investment in Didi Chuxing, Apple has shown no signs of forming a separate investment vehicle. Experts suggest that while Apple's various advantages might make a venture capital arm seem unnecessary, such an arm could potentially offer tax benefits if Didi were to go public or be sold.

title: Spotify Just Surpassed a Major Milestone Despite Lack of Profits
summary: Spotify announced its user base has grown to 100 million, including 30 million paid subscribers, despite fierce competition from Apple Music and Google, and a lack of profitability. The company continues to expand internationally, investing heavily to grow its market share in the music streaming industry.

title: Mossberg: HP aims at the Mac with super-slim laptop
summary: Walt Mossberg reviews the new HP Spectre, which is HP's attempt to challenge Apple's MacBooks with a super-slim, premium Windows laptop. While acknowledging its attractive design and powerful processors, Mossberg raises concerns about the Spectre's battery life, trackpad, and fan noise, ultimately finding it a good option for Windows users willing to compromise on some aspects. He notes that the Spectre's focus on being a traditional, thin clamshell laptop is a departure from recent Windows trends toward 2-in-1 devices.

title: Mossberg: HP aims at the Mac with a super-slim laptop
summary: Walt Mossberg reviews the new HP Spectre, a super-slim laptop designed to compete with Apple's MacBooks. While praising its premium design and powerful processors, Mossberg raises concerns about its battery life, trackpad, and fan noise. He concludes that it's a strong contender for Windows users willing to compromise on battery life, distinguishing itself from 2-in-1 hybrid devices by focusing on being a traditional, thin laptop.

title: J.P. Morgan Leads $52.5 Million Investment In This Hot Robotics Company
summary: J.P. Morgan led a $52.5 million investment round in San Francisco-based robotics startup Anki, with additional participation from Andreessen Horowitz, Index Ventures, and Two Sigma. Anki, known for its robotic race cars and new toy robot designed by former DreamWorks and Pixar animators, has now raised a total of $182.5 million in funding. This investment aligns with J.P. Morgan's broader technology strategy, which includes a focus on robotics.

title: Cirrus Logic unveils Apple-authorized Lightning headphone development kit
summary: Cirrus Logic has released a new development kit to help manufacturers create Apple-authorized Lightning headphones, anticipating Apple's move to remove the traditional 3.5mm headphone jack from future iPhones. The kit facilitates the transition from analog to digital audio connectivity, supporting various headphone form factors. This development comes amid rumors that the "iPhone 7" will drop the headphone jack, pushing accessory makers towards Lightning and Bluetooth solutions.

title: HP EliteBook Folio G1 review
summary: The HP EliteBook Folio G1 is reviewed, highlighting its sleek design and comfortable keyboard as positives, but criticizing its problematic touchpad, awkward port placement, overheating issues, and inconsistent battery life. Despite its attractive appearance and portability, the numerous functional flaws make it difficult to recommend wholeheartedly. The article details its specifications and compares it to other ultra-portable laptops.

title: Apple, Microsoft, HP among companies shunning GOP convention
summary: Several major corporations, including Apple, Microsoft, and HP Inc., are withholding financial and product support from the 2016 Republican National Convention, a departure from their past donations. While companies cite various reasons, their decisions are widely seen as a response to Donald Trump's controversial rhetoric. Many of these companies are also not supporting the Democratic convention, but their withdrawal from the GOP event is more significant given their past support.

title: Amid frenzy, Pokemon Go leads to robberies and injuries
summary: The new augmented reality mobile game Pokemon Go has become an overnight sensation in the US but has also led to several dangerous incidents. These include armed robberies in Missouri where teens used the game's geolocation feature to ambush victims, and the discovery of a body in Wyoming by a player. Additionally, officials and media have reported minor injuries to players who were distracted by the app while searching for characters.

title: Ericsson v. Micromax - A Kick-Start to SEP-FRAND Antitrust Jurisprudence in India
summary: The article discusses the Delhi High Court's decision to uphold the Competition Commission of India's (CCI) jurisdiction over abuse of dominance allegations against Ericsson concerning Standard Essential Patents (SEPs). This ruling clarifies that intellectual property rights and competition law are complementary in India, setting a precedent for antitrust jurisprudence in SEP-FRAND disputes. It further examines issues such as excessive royalty demands and injunctions by SEP holders.
authors: Sahithya Muralidharan (West Bengal National University of Juridical Sciences|Kolkata)

title: Lowe's signs on to Apple TV with its own DIY series
summary: Lowe's is launching its own DIY series called "The Weekender" on Apple TV. The first episode, "Concrete Jungle Patio," features a Manhattan couple transforming their backyard. The show has a similar feel to "This Old House" and includes a segment with Monica Mangin discussing motherhood.

title: McDonald’s Just Made Surfing for Porn Harder
summary: McDonald's has implemented WiFi filtering across the majority of its nearly 14,000 U.S. restaurants to block pornography. This change follows years of pressure from the internet safety advocacy group Enough is Enough. The new policy aims to provide a safer and more family-friendly WiFi experience for customers, similar to competitors like Chick-fil-A and Panera Bread.

title: ARM Holdings in £24bn Japanese takeover deal
summary: ARM Holdings, a leading UK technology company known for its microchip designs, is being acquired by Japan's Softbank for £24bn ($32bn). This deal represents close to a 50% premium on ARM's market value and is seen as Softbank's move to capitalize on the "internet of things." The acquisition poses a dilemma for the UK government regarding foreign takeovers but Softbank has committed to doubling ARM's UK workforce over the next five years.

title: Japan’s Softbank to buy chip-design powerhouse ARM for $32 billion
summary: Japan's Softbank has agreed to acquire U.K. semiconductor firm ARM Holdings for over $32 billion. ARM supplies chip designs for major tech companies like Apple, Samsung, and Huawei, and Softbank aims to strengthen its presence in the growing internet of things (IoT) sector. The deal, which offers a 43 percent premium on ARM's stock, is Softbank's bid to aggressively expand into new markets like IoT and connected cars.

title: How the Geek Squad Could Be Best Buy’s Secret Weapon
summary: Best Buy, under CEO Hubert Joly, transformed from a struggling retailer into a viable competitor against Amazon by implementing strategic changes such as price matching, turning stores into fulfillment centers, and partnering with gadget makers. With smartphone sales tapering off and commoditization of TV prices, Joly now plans to leverage the Geek Squad as Best Buy's next secret weapon. The company envisions the Geek Squad providing in-home tech support, creating a crucial service that Amazon isn't well-positioned to offer and securing a new area of steady growth.

title: The Rebirth of Nokia: An Interview with Chairman Risto Siilasmaa
summary: This article features an interview with Nokia Chairman Risto Siilasmaa, discussing the company's dramatic transformation from near bankruptcy in 2012 to a renewed focus on networking and the "programmable world." Siilasmaa details the strategic decisions, including selling the phone and mapping businesses, and the internal changes that led to Nokia's enterprise value growing twenty-fold. He also shares key advice for leaders undergoing major transformations, emphasizing independent thinking, paranoid optimism, and building a strong team.

title: Apple Taps Bob Mansfield to Oversee Car Project
summary: Apple Inc. has appointed highly regarded senior executive Bob Mansfield to oversee its secretive autonomous, electric-vehicle initiative, code-named Project Titan. Mansfield, who previously led hardware engineering for many key Apple products like the MacBook Air, iMac, and iPad, had stepped back from a day-to-day role a few years ago and was serving as an adviser. His new role places him in charge of one of Apple's most ambitious new projects.

title: Is the sale of ARM a 'surrender' or an 'endorsement' for the UK?
summary: The article discusses the controversial £24bn acquisition of UK tech firm ARM Holdings by Japanese company Softbank. It explores arguments from critics who view it as a surrender of British innovation and warn of potential job losses and compromised independence, contrasting these with perspectives that see the investment as an endorsement of UK tech and a necessary step for ARM to compete globally. The deal’s impact on Softbank’s share price and potential rival bids are also examined.

title: The new Fortune Global 500 is out. It shows a shift in the world's business landscape
summary: The 2016 Fortune Global 500 list reveals a significant shift in the global business landscape, highlighted by the extraordinary rise of Chinese companies. China now hosts over 100 of the world's biggest companies on the list, trailing only the United States, with three Chinese firms occupying spots in the top five. The article discusses the continued dominance of Walmart, the rapid ascent of China's State Grid, and Apple's return to prominence, while noting an overall decline in revenue for the top 500 companies due to global economic factors.

title: LabStyle Innovations rebrands as DarioHealth
summary: LabStyle Innovations Corp. is rebranding as DarioHealth Corp. to better reflect its growth in the digital health and mHealth space. The company will continue to trade under the ticker "DRIO" on Nasdaq, and the new name will extend to all its products and services. CEO Erez Raphael stated this rebrand aligns with their goal to revolutionize health management with personalized, user-centric digital solutions.

title: Chinese consortium agrees $4.4 billion deal for Caesars online games
summary: A Chinese consortium, including Shanghai Giant Network Technology Co Ltd and Alibaba founder Jack Ma, has agreed to acquire Caesars Interactive Entertainment Inc's online games unit, Playtika, for $4.4 billion in cash. This deal will provide a significant boost to Caesars Acquisition Co and Caesars Entertainment Corp as they navigate a complex merger and a large bankruptcy restructuring. The acquisition highlights Chinese companies' growing interest in expanding their presence in the global online gaming market.
authors: Liana B. Baker and Allison Lampert

title: Apple Says It Has Fixed Gender Pay Gap For U.S. Employees
summary: Apple has announced that it has closed the gender wage gap for its employees in the United States, with women and underrepresented minorities earning one dollar for every dollar male and white employees earn, respectively. The company is now analyzing its global workforce to address any existing pay disparities worldwide. This achievement aligns with similar announcements from Facebook and Amazon and reflects Apple's focus on hiring more women and diverse candidates, with a slight shift in its workforce composition and over half of new hires being minorities.

title: Best Places to Work: Intuit, Reno
summary: Intuit's Reno office, employing over 400 people, has been recognized multiple times as one of Northern Nevada’s Best Places to Work. The company focuses on financial software like QuickBooks and TurboTax, with the Reno site specializing in Intuit Payroll services. Intuit attributes its success in attracting talent to its recognition as a top workplace, offering various employee perks and demonstrating a strong commitment to community outreach.

title: A focus on shareholder value for long-term shareholders
summary: J.M. Smucker utilizes a tenure stock-voting structure to reward long-term investors, directly addressing the prevalent issue of short-termism in corporate America. This system grants shareholders increased voting power the longer they hold stock, aiming to prioritize sustained growth and innovation over immediate profits. The article explores both the benefits and controversies of tenure voting, highlighting its potential to foster patient capital and founder vision, while also raising concerns about shareholder democracy.

title: Applied Materials reports record earnings
summary: Applied Materials reported record earnings per share and new orders for the third quarter, ending July 31. The semiconductor tools giant is optimistic about the potential for Apple to use new organic light-emitting diode (OLED) display screens, for which Applied Materials produces manufacturing tools.

title: Lyft Is Said to Seek a Buyer, Without Success (Published 2016)
summary: Lyft, the second-largest ride-hailing company in the U.S., has reportedly sought a buyer but ultimately failed to secure a deal, despite holding talks with several major companies including General Motors, Apple, and Google. The company remains independent with a $1.4 billion cash reserve, highlighting the capital-intensive and challenging nature of the ride-hailing market. Though G.M. was considered the most serious suitor, no written offer was ever made.

title: Why Warren Buffett is grabbing Apple stock while other big names run away
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. has significantly invested in Apple Inc. stock, a move that suggests Apple is now seen as a value stock. This comes as other investors like Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management sold off their holdings. The shift indicates a new type of Apple investor who prioritizes companies with stable growth, substantial cash reserves, and dividend payouts over high-growth, high-risk opportunities.

title: Apple Inc sends invitations for September 7 event, expected to introduce new iPhone and Watch
summary: Apple Inc. has sent out invitations for a September 7th event in San Francisco, where it is anticipated to unveil new iPhone and Apple Watch models. The new iPhones are expected to feature advanced photography capabilities and upgraded hardware, maintaining a design similar to previous models. This product refresh is crucial for Apple as it faces a revenue decline with customers upgrading their smartphones less frequently.

title: Customer Letter
summary: Apple's August 30, 2016, message to its European community addresses the European Commission's ruling regarding its tax arrangements in Ireland. The company asserts its long-standing commitment to Europe, its role as a major taxpayer globally, and that it has always followed tax laws. Apple strongly refutes the Commission's claim of a "special deal" and argues that the ruling sets a dangerous precedent by retroactively applying new interpretations of tax law, which could harm investment and job creation in Europe.

title: Why Apple Was Taxed $14.5 Billion for Ireland, Which Doesn't Want the Money
summary: The European Union ordered Apple to pay $14.5 billion in back taxes to Ireland after a three-year investigation into the company's tax dealings, finding that Apple received unfair tax advantages from the country. Surprisingly, Ireland's finance minister disagreed with the ruling and plans to appeal, as the country fears losing its appeal to foreign investment. The U.S. Treasury also criticized the EU's decision, calling it unfair and a threat to international tax principles and foreign investment in Europe.

title: Google Is About to Take On Uber in a Big Way
summary: Google is reportedly expanding its ride-sharing service through the Waze navigation app, directly competing with Uber and Lyft. This service will function as a digital carpooling platform, connecting passengers with drivers heading in the same direction, initially expanding across San Francisco. This move signals a further deterioration of the relationship between Google and Uber, especially given Google's past investment in Uber and its own development of autonomous driving technology.

title: Dropping the F-bomb when you're a CEO: Is it ever OK?
summary: This article discusses the appropriateness of CEOs using profanity publicly, sparked by Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary's recent expletive-laden comments. Experts weigh in on whether swearing can legitimize profanity in the workplace or if it sets an inappropriate example, noting that while some argue it can add power to a message or attract attention, others view it as unprofessional, especially for a company's public face. The piece also cites other executives known for their colorful language and the various repercussions they faced.

title: Bill Campbell, tech mentor, dies
summary: Bill Campbell, a former Ivy League football coach turned Silicon Valley management guru, has died at 75 after a long battle with cancer. Known as "Coach," Campbell played a pivotal role in shaping Apple and Google's direction, serving as a sounding board for Steve Jobs and mentoring Eric Schmidt, Larry Page, and Sergey Brin. Before his tech mentorship, Campbell was CEO of Intuit Inc. and coached football at Columbia University.

title: Software giant Oracle to build major Austin campus, add employees
summary: Oracle Corp. plans to construct a new corporate campus in Austin, Texas, on 27 acres overlooking Lady Bird Lake, aiming to increase its Austin workforce by 50 percent. This expansion includes the purchase of a 295-unit luxury apartment complex for employee housing. The move is part of Oracle's global initiative to keep pace with demand for cloud services and attract top millennial talent, leveraging Austin's growing tech community and lower cost of living compared to California.

title: Introducing Michael Kors Access Wearable Technology: Time for Fashion to Meet the Future
summary: Michael Kors has introduced MICHAEL KORS ACCESS, a new line of wearable technology accessories, including display smartwatches and activity trackers. This collection combines fashion with innovative technology, featuring customizable displays, social media updates, fitness tracking, and interchangeable bands. The smartwatches, powered by Android Wear through a partnership with Google, are designed for the fashion-focused consumer who wants to stay connected and stylish.

title: Johnson Controls International debuts
summary: The sale of Johnson Controls to Tyco International plc has been completed, forming Johnson Controls International plc, an Ireland-based company with $30 billion in annual sales. The new entity's shares began trading Tuesday under the JCI ticker symbol. The merger aims for $1 billion in savings from synergies and productivity, in addition to an expected $150 million in tax savings from the inversion to Ireland.

title: Apple & Nike launch the perfect running partner, Apple Watch Nike+
summary: Apple and Nike have partnered to launch the Apple Watch Nike+, an ultimate running tool that combines Apple Watch Series 2 features with exclusive Nike Sport Bands and deep integration with the new Nike+ Run Club app. This collaboration aims to provide motivation, coaching, and a seamless tracking experience for runners of all levels. The Apple Watch Nike+ will be available in two sizes and multiple color combinations, with pricing starting at $369 (US) for the 38mm model.

title: AirPods 4
summary: This article from Apple details the features and pricing of various AirPods models, including the new AirPods 4, AirPods Pro 3, and AirPods Max. It highlights key features such as Active Noise Cancellation, Adaptive Audio, Personalized Spatial Audio, Heart Rate Sensing, Live Translation, and Hearing Health capabilities, while also providing purchasing options and promotional offers related to Apple Music and Apple Fitness+. The page also includes detailed disclaimers and technical specifications for each product.

title: Apple event unveils iPhone 7 and Apple Watch Series 2
summary: Apple unveiled its new iPhone 7 and second-generation Apple Watch Series 2 at a major event, highlighting significant upgrades such as improved cameras, water resistance, and faster processors. The new products are poised to be key offerings for the upcoming holiday shopping season. Additionally, the event featured the introduction of Apple AirPods, the announcement of Pokemon Go for Apple Watch, and Super Mario coming to the App Store.

title: IPhone 7, New Apple Watch Unveiled
summary: Apple unveiled the iPhone 7 and new Apple Watch models, focusing on fitness-minded consumers. The iPhone 7 features a dual camera system, improved battery life, waterproof design, and removes the traditional headphone jack. The updated Apple Watch is "swimproof," includes GPS, and will have a special Nike-branded version, along with "Pokemon Go" coming to the device.

title: 5 Changes Coming to the Apple Watch
summary: Apple unveiled the Apple Watch Series 2 and the Apple Watch Nike+, featuring significant internal tweaks for enhanced speed and utility. Key changes include waterproofing, built-in GPS, a faster processor, a brighter display, and the upcoming integration of Pokemon Go. The new models start at $369, with preorders beginning soon and a price cut for the first-generation Apple Watch.

title: Apple Made 2 Surprising Moves With the New iPhone
summary: Apple announced two unexpected changes with the new iPhone 7: increasing the base storage to 32GB from 16GB, and including an adapter to allow users to continue using old headphones despite the removal of the headphone jack. These moves suggest Apple is responding to consumer feedback and aiming to ease transitions, particularly as the company faces its first-ever decline in iPhone sales. Minor adjustments like these may help Apple compete more effectively in the smartphone market.

title: Apple Says Jet Black iPhone 7 Could Scratch and Might Need a Case
summary: Apple has revealed a potential design flaw in its new Jet Black iPhone 7, stating that its high-gloss finish may show "fine micro-abrasions with use" despite a precision nine-step manufacturing process. The company suggests that customers concerned about scratches consider using a protective case. This revelation could deter some buyers attracted to the new color, as a case would obscure the device's aesthetic appeal which Apple heavily promoted.

title: Apple Watch Nike
summary: Apple and Nike have collaborated to reimagine Apple Watch bands, focusing on comfort and performance for active users. The article details features of the Nike Sport Band and Nike Sport Loop, both designed to be lightweight, quick-drying, and durable. It also highlights the integration with the Nike Run Club app for training plans, tracking, and safety features like location sharing and extreme weather alerts, alongside unique Nike-themed watch faces.

title: Why Apple Misses Steve Jobs More Than Ever
summary: The article argues that Apple misses Steve Jobs primarily due to his unparalleled passion and ability to inspire focus, which made his product keynotes captivating and memorable. Without Jobs, recent Apple keynotes feel boring and lack the genuine enthusiasm he brought, even though the products themselves are incremental improvements. The author believes Jobs would have continued to innovate and steer Apple away from becoming boring, demonstrating a unique vision that current leadership lacks.

title: Intel and Apple Partner to Create Supply Chain Flexibility
summary: Intel Corporation is reportedly supplying wireless chips for some of Apple Inc.'s new iPhone 7 models. This partnership aims to add critical flexibility to both companies' supply chains, with Intel's baseband modern semiconductors replacing those previously supplied by Qualcomm Inc. This collaboration marks a significant breakthrough for Intel in the smartphone market.

title: Morning Agenda: A.I.G.’s Ex-Chief on Trial, Apple Taxes, Driverless Updates (Published 2016)
summary: Maurice Greenberg, former chief executive of A.I.G., is going to trial on civil charges related to alleged bogus reinsurance transactions and orchestrating other transactions to convert insurance losses into investment losses. Meanwhile, the Irish government is appealing a European ruling regarding Apple's taxes, amounting to about $21.3 billion including interest, despite some domestic calls for them to accept the funds.

title: HP Inc. Declares It Has Beaten Apple At Its Own Game
summary: HP Inc. announced at its Global Partner Conference that it has surpassed Apple in innovation and market share in the personal systems space. Ron Coughlin, President of Personal Systems, stated that Apple is no longer untouchable and highlighted HP Inc.'s rebound in Q2 2016 PC shipments where it held 20.8 percent of the market compared to Apple's 7.1 percent. This declaration positions HP Inc. as a leader, challenging Apple's long-held dominance in design and innovation.

title: Do Tech Companies Know What It Takes To Make A Car?
summary: Magna International Inc., the world's largest contract manufacturer of cars, is positioning itself to build vehicles for Silicon Valley tech companies like Apple, Google, and Uber. While tech companies have expertise in software, they lack the experience in manufacturing complex vehicles, making partners like Magna essential. Magna expects this new demand to significantly boost its revenue from contract manufacturing, with a focus on autonomous and electric vehicles.
authors: Alex Webb|Elisabeth Behrmann and Gerrit De Vynck

title: JPMorgan Chase Card Services CEO: ‘We Need Great General Managers’
summary: Kevin Watters, CEO of JPMorgan Chase Card Services, spoke at the University of Virginia Darden School of Business about the diverse opportunities at the banking giant, emphasizing its blend of large-company scale with a startup mentality in fintech. He highlighted the company's significant investment in digital wallet technology like Chase Pay and the unexpected success of the Chase Sapphire Reserve card. Watters also stressed the need for "great general managers" and the value of cross-disciplinary expertise from institutions like Darden.

title: Why Apple Inc. Didnt Include OLED Displays on iPhone 7 and iPhone 7 Plus
summary: This article explores why Apple did not integrate OLED displays into the iPhone 7 and iPhone 7 Plus, despite their advantages over LCDs. The author suggests that Apple's decision was likely due to technological readiness, as current OLED technology might have required significant sacrifices in brightness and color accuracy compared to the advanced LCDs used in the new iPhones. However, the author anticipates Apple will transition to OLED in future models, citing the Apple Watch 2's bright OLED display as a promising indicator.

title: Apple patents retail store developments from shopping bags to architectural designs
summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) continues to patent various aspects of its retail stores, ranging from shopping bag designs and lighting systems to architectural structures and display stands. These patents underscore Apple's significant investment in creating a distinctive customer experience and the unique design elements of its high-profile retail locations worldwide. The company's patent filings highlight innovations intended to enhance both the aesthetic and functional aspects of its physical stores, including new features like "The Avenue," "The Forum," and "Genius Grove."

title: Blackberry to stop making its own phones
summary: BlackBerry Ltd. announced it will cease internal development of its brand phones, shifting this function to outside partners to reduce capital requirements and enhance return on invested capital. The company plans to focus on developing security and device software, a strategic move by CEO John Chen to turn around the once-dominant smartphone maker. This decision reflects BlackBerry's ongoing efforts to adapt in a market now led by companies like Apple and Samsung.

title: Apple, Inc. Sold Over $2.5 Billion Worth of Apple Watches in 2015
summary: In 2015, Apple sold over $2.5 billion worth of Apple Watches, positioning the company as the world's No. 2 watchmaker by revenue, despite the product only shipping in volume for half the year. The article estimates Apple Watch revenue to be between $2.5 billion and $4.5 billion, and with an estimated average selling price of $500, this suggests 5 million to 9 million units sold in 2015. This makes Apple a significant competitor in the watch market, surpassing publicly traded Fossil.

title: In-House Lawyer Lays Out Discrimination Claims Against Apple
summary: A former in-house attorney at Apple, identified as "Jane Doe," has filed a lawsuit accusing Apple of age discrimination, gender discrimination, and wrongful termination. She alleges that male colleagues were given more flexible work arrangements and that she faced conflicting directives that created a "no-win" situation, ultimately leading to her termination. The case was filed in Los Angeles Superior Court, and a request by Apple to move the lawsuit to Santa Clara was denied.

title: An Apple a day
summary: Liberty University's IT Department celebrated the grand opening of its IT Marketplace, the university's first on-campus computer supply store, on September 28. The marketplace offers Apple products like the MacBook Pro, iPad, and iPhone, with plans to expand to Dell and Windows hardware in the future. The store provides education discounts and is Apple-authorized, having been conceived four years prior to its opening to provide value to students and build academic reputation.

title: Remembering Steve Jobs: 5 Years Later
summary: This article reflects on the five-year anniversary of Steve Jobs' death, examining the changes at Apple under CEO Tim Cook. It highlights how Cook has maintained Apple's profitability and innovation while exhibiting a different leadership style, and speculates on what Apple might be like if Jobs were still alive. The piece concludes that despite the differences, Cook learned critical lessons from Jobs and has solidified Apple's position as a global force.

title: Apple unveils new store design in Indy
summary: Apple Inc. rolled out its new store in Indianapolis, featuring a new design spearheaded by Indiana native Angela Ahrendts, Apple’s senior vice president of retail and online stores. This Indianapolis location is the second in the country to receive the updated storefront, which includes doubled space, a parking lot entrance, and distinct areas for different products and services, aiming to create a more customer-friendly environment and increase traffic. The new store design emphasizes human interaction with Apple products beyond just screens, with plans for creative professionals and events to engage customers.

title: Apple’s iPhone ‘Touch Disease’ Lawsuit Is Heating Up
summary: A lawsuit against Apple regarding its handling of the iPhone's "Touch Disease" has intensified, with three more law firms joining the suit and another class-action lawsuit filed in Utah. The "Touch Disease" refers to a defect in the iPhone 6 and 6 Plus where soldered balls under the screen crack, making the touchscreens inoperable. Plaintiffs allege Apple kept the defect secret and charged for replacements, sometimes with refurbished models that also had the same problem, leading to growing legal pressure on the company.

title: Here’s how much Apple may profit from Samsung’s exploding phone disaster
summary: Apple shares are trading at their highest prices of 2016 following Samsung's escalating Galaxy Note 7 issues, which analysts believe could lead to millions of additional iPhone sales for Apple.
