Samsung has halted sales and stopped production of the Note 7 after replacement devices continued to experience overheating and catching fire. This disaster for Samsung is expected to significantly benefit Apple.

title: Chromebooks destroyed Apple in back-to-school shopping
summary: Chromebooks had a very successful back-to-school shopping season, while Apple's Mac sales experienced significant declines. Two separate analyses by IDC and Gartner Inc. reported drops of 13% and 13.4% respectively, for Apple during the third quarter. This indicates a growing popularity of Google's low-priced PCs among students.

title: E-payment firm ACI opens Limerick, Ireland data center
summary: Electronic payment and banking solutions company ACI Worldwide has opened its European data center in Limerick, Ireland, creating 50 new jobs and expanding its existing team. This fully redundant facility highlights Ireland's appeal for data center investment due to low taxes, strong privacy, and good fiber links. The move was supported by IDA Ireland and various political figures, emphasizing the long-term commitment and value of hosting data centers in the region.

title: Mary Barra Is Remaking GM’s Culture—And The Company Itself
summary: Mary Barra, CEO of General Motors, is working to transform the company's culture and perception despite investor skepticism and the company's past challenges. The article questions whether Barra, a long-time GM employee, is the ideal leader to make GM a world-beating brand against tech giants like Uber, Tesla, and Google. It highlights the dramatic shift needed for GM to compete in the reinvented transportation landscape.

title: Why Corbus Pharmaceuticals Holdings Inc Jumped Higher Today
summary: Corbus Pharmaceuticals' stock rose over 13% today in anticipation of its CEO appearing on Jim Cramer's Mad Money. The company is developing Resunab, an anti-inflammatory drug, and currently has it in three phase 2 clinical trials with results for systemic sclerosis expected by year-end. Positive outcomes could validate the drug for other conditions like cystic fibrosis and dermatomyositis, influencing its future valuation.

title: Kodak continues push into crowded smartphone market
summary: Eastman Kodak Co. is re-entering the smartphone market with its new camera-focused Kodak Ektra smartphone, set to launch overseas later this year and in the U.S. next year. The company hopes this device, boasting advanced camera features, will compete with established players like Apple and Samsung. This marks Kodak's second attempt in the smartphone market, aiming to leverage its brand heritage in photography despite a flattening global smartphone market.

title: Behind the Fidelity Blue Chip Growth Fund’s Disappointing Performance in 2016
summary: The Fidelity Blue Chip Growth Fund (FBGRX) had a below-average performance in 2016, placing seventh among 12 peer funds for point-to-point returns. While tech, energy, and consumer discretionary sectors contributed positively, healthcare, industrials, and materials significantly dragged down the fund's overall performance. This prompted a call for investors to carefully review their allocations to FBGRX.

title: NVIDIA and FANUC Join Forces to Implement AI Robotics System
summary: NVIDIA and FANUC have announced a collaboration to integrate AI into FANUC's Intelligent Edge Link and Drive (FIELD) system, aiming to boost robotics productivity in automated factories. This partnership will leverage NVIDIA's GPUs and deep learning software to enable robots to learn tasks faster and more efficiently, utilizing an edge computing architecture to process sensor data closer to the source. The initiative is set to enhance manufacturing capabilities and increase competition in the industrial robotics market.

title: Qualcomm is buying NXP Semiconductors in a $47 billion deal
summary: Qualcomm announced it is acquiring NXP Semiconductors NV for approximately $47 billion, including debt, a move aimed at expanding its chip business beyond phones into the automotive industry. This acquisition will make Qualcomm a major supplier to the rapidly growing market for automotive chips and is expected to significantly boost earnings and generate substantial cost savings. The deal follows other major consolidations in the semiconductor industry and positions Qualcomm to compete with Chinese and Taiwanese rivals amid slowing smartphone sales.

title: Goldman Sachs Has a New Model: Apple
summary: Goldman Sachs is adopting a new strategy, mirroring Apple's model, by allowing rival firms to sell their investment products through its web application. This move, which marks a significant shift for the bank, enables Goldman to broaden its reach by creating an open online platform for various financial products, including those from competitors. The bank aims to become a central hub for investment product distribution, similar to how Apple's App Store hosts various third-party applications.

title: PLAY-DOH Touch App and SHAPE TO LIFE STUDIO Bring Kids' Creations to Life for the First Time Ever
summary: Hasbro has launched the PLAY-DOH Touch app and the PLAY-DOH Touch SHAPE TO LIFE STUDIO set, allowing children to bring their physical PLAY-DOH creations to life digitally. This interactive experience, debuting exclusively at Apple Stores, merges hands-on play with digital exploration, enhancing creativity for the brand's 60th anniversary. The app and studio set will be available at additional retailers and platforms early next year.

title: Beware, iPhone Users: Fake Retail Apps Are Surging Before Holidays (Published 2016)
summary: Hundreds of fake retail and product apps have appeared in Apple's App Store, coinciding with the holiday shopping season. These counterfeit apps mimic major retailers and luxury brands, posing risks such as financial fraud, malware infection, and exposure of personal information to unsuspecting iPhone users. Chris Mason, CEO of Branding Brand, noted this surge as unprecedented in a short period.

title: Eric Schmidt’s Next Book Is About Silicon Valley’s Secret Coach
summary: Alphabet chairman and former Google CEO Eric Schmidt is writing a new book about Bill Campbell, the legendary executive coach who mentored many prominent Silicon Valley figures, including Steve Jobs, Larry Page, Sergey Brin, and Jeff Bezos. Campbell, who passed away in April, was known as the "secret coach" and played a crucial role in Google's organizational structure and growth. This will be Schmidt's third book, following "How Google Works" and "The New Digital Age."

title: Citibank Launches Challenge to Apple and Google With Citi Pay
summary: Citibank has launched Citi Pay, a new mobile wallet for Android users, entering the competitive mobile payment market dominated by Apple Pay and Android Pay. The service allows customers to use Citibank credit and debit cards for in-store, online, and in-app purchases, and will be rolled out internationally before its 2017 U.S. launch. Citi Pay aims to connect with customers through their smartphones, a strategy also pursued by other financial institutions like Chase Bank and Wells Fargo.

title: PayPal’s Siri Integration Makes Parting With Money Even Easier
summary: PayPal has integrated with Apple's Siri, allowing users to send and request money using voice commands on iPhones and iPads. This new feature enables easier money transfers by simply asking Siri to send or request funds from contacts via PayPal. The integration simplifies the process of digital payments for PayPal users.

title: Penumbra, Inc. Announces Resignation of Board Member
summary: Penumbra, Inc. announced that Kevin Sullivan has resigned from its Board of Directors due to personal health reasons, effective immediately. Kevin Sullivan was an early investor and served on the board, with the CEO Adam Elsesser praising his strategic thinking and contributions. Sullivan previously held senior human resources executive positions at Wells Fargo and Company and Apple Computer.

title: “Designed by Apple in California” chronicles 20 years of Apple design
summary: Apple announced the release of a new hardbound book titled “Designed by Apple in California,” chronicling 20 years of Apple’s design through 450 photographs of its products, from the 1998 iMac to the 2015 Apple Pencil. The book is dedicated to Steve Jobs and aims to shed light on how and why Apple's products exist, serving as a resource for design students. It is available in two sizes, starting at $199 and $299 respectively, from November 16th.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Angers New MacBook Pro Fans Yet Again
summary: Apple's new MacBook Pro, despite strong initial sales, is drawing criticism for its lack of upgradability, as both RAM and SSD are soldered to the motherboard. This change eliminates traditional ways users extended their laptop's lifespan, forcing them to buy new units for upgrades. The move is causing frustration among core MacBook Pro customers and could open a door for competitors like Microsoft.

title: Citigroup, Apple No Longer ‘Forever’ Holdings of Saudi Prince
summary: Saudi Prince Alwaleed bin Talal indicated that his long-held "forever" investments, including Citigroup Inc. (NYSE: C) and Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL), are now under review for potential divestment. This shift in strategy, influenced by low oil prices impacting Saudi Arabia's economy, marks a departure from his previous stance of never selling his company shares. The article discusses potential reasons for this change, including the country's economic pressures and the stability of the Saudi riyal.

title: Disney Brings Tech and Storytelling to Grace Hopper Celebration of Women in Computing
summary: Disney showcased its integration of technology and storytelling at the Grace Hopper Celebration of Women in Computing. The company highlighted how tech is crucial for its products and films, emphasized the importance of diversity in driving innovation, and aimed to inspire more women to pursue careers in technology. Disney participated in panels and presentations, demonstrating its commitment to advancing women in computing.

title: Apple faces decade of uncertainty after next year’s iPhone
summary: Apple Inc. may face a "decade-long malaise" despite being the world's most valuable company. Analysts, including Andrew Uerkwitz of Oppenheimer, predict iPhone sales will peak with the 10th-anniversary iPhone next year, followed by uncertainty due to shrinking differentiation from rivals and a growing used smartphone market. The company is projected to sell 245 million iPhones in its 2018 fiscal year, but risks are considered higher than ever.

title: Is Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT) Worthy of Your Portfolio?
summary: This article analyzes hedge fund sentiment towards Lockheed Martin Corporation (NYSE: LMT), noting a decrease in hedge fund support but highlighting significant positions held by top money managers. It discusses Insider Monkey's investment strategy that has outperformed the market and identifies key hedge fund activity related to LMT.

title: Watch Out Apple, Samsung: Former Nokia Execs Plot Smartphone Comeback
summary: Former Nokia managers are planning a smartphone comeback under the Nokia brand, licensing it from Microsoft and partnering with Google and Foxconn. Their new company, HMD Global, aims to launch its first Android-based Nokia smartphone early next year, targeting a share of the competitive smartphone market currently dominated by Apple and Samsung. Despite Nokia's past struggles in smartphones, HMD believes the strong brand recognition, especially in feature phone markets, and their experienced management team will drive success.

title: Apple Said to Fly Drones to Improve Maps Data and Catch Google
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly planning to use drones to enhance its Maps service, aiming to gather and update map information more quickly than its current minivan fleet. This initiative is part of Apple's strategy to improve its mapping data and compete more effectively with Google. The company is assembling a team of robotics and data-collection experts for this purpose and previously acquired Indoor.io for interior mapping.

title: Simon Property Group Fights to Reinvent the Shopping Mall
summary: Simon Property Group is actively reinventing its shopping malls to combat the decline of department stores and the rise of online shopping. The company is investing heavily in renovations, diversifying tenant mixes to include more restaurants, entertainment, and non-traditional retail, and leveraging technology and customer pampering to maintain high sales per square foot and occupancy rates in its A-malls. This strategic overhaul aims to keep Simon at the forefront of the evolving retail landscape despite industry challenges.

title: Netflix’s Ted Sarandos: Acquisition by Larger Media Company Would Hamstring Us
summary: Netflix chief content officer Ted Sarandos dismissed the idea of the company being acquired by a larger media conglomerate, stating it would hinder Netflix's focused business model. He noted that large companies have inter-company conflicts that would make it difficult for Netflix to maintain its discipline. Sarandos also mentioned that Netflix plans to double its original series slate in 2017, including about 20 unscripted TV shows.

title: Why Apple Will Never Merge iOS and MacOS
summary: This article argues that Apple will not merge its iOS and macOS operating systems. The author contends that macOS will remain for professionals and creatives ("trucks"), while iOS will evolve as Apple's mainstream computing software through iPads, catering to everyday users ("cars"). The article highlights iOS's significant contribution to Apple's services revenue, solidifying its importance to the company's financial future and strategy of maintaining two distinct operating systems.

title: Supreme Court Gives Samsung a Reprieve in Apple Patent Case (Published 2016)
summary: The Supreme Court unanimously ruled that Samsung may not have to pay the full $399 million in profits to Apple for infringing design patents. The decision clarifies that liability could apply to specific components rather than the entire product ("article of manufacture"), potentially reducing damages owed by Samsung. This case is significant as it's the first design patent case the Supreme Court has heard in over a century.

title: Trump’s Team Says He Sold All His Stocks in June (Published 2016)
summary: President-elect Donald J. Trump's team announced that he sold all his stock holdings in June, a move that could alleviate some conflict of interest concerns as he prepared to enter the White House. Previously, his portfolio was valued at least $22 million and included significant stakes in companies like Apple, Walmart, and MasterCard, as well as a large holding in a BlackRock-managed hedge fund. While the sale cannot be independently verified immediately, future financial disclosures will provide more details.

title: Netflix Launches Video Previews: How The Company Landed On Its Biggest Redesign Ever
summary: Netflix is rolling out a major UI redesign that replaces static images with auto-playing video previews to enhance user experience and cut down on browsing time. This change, influenced by how Hollywood merchandises content, is based on extensive testing showing that video is more engaging and helps users confidently select content, thereby improving subscriber retention. The update will be progressively rolled out across various platforms, with Netflix continuing to refine the feature based on user interaction.

title: Phones and the city
summary: This article showcases Reuters photographer Stefan Wermuth's visual documentation of Londoners' interactions with their smartphones. It also reports on the comeback of Nokia-branded mobile phones through a new Finnish company, HMD Global, which has taken over Nokia's basic phone business and aims to enter the smartphone market. The company faces the challenge of breaking into a competitive market currently dominated by several players using Google's Android platform.

title: Goodbye, My Sweet: Hershey's Michigan Avenue Store to Close in January
summary: Hershey's Chocolate Chicago store on Michigan Avenue is set to close in January 2017 after being a part of the Magnificent Mile since 2005. The company plans to focus on larger, more experiential retail footprints elsewhere, such as their Las Vegas store and an upcoming New York Times Square location. This closure will add another vacant storefront to North Michigan Avenue, an area already experiencing significant retail vacancies.

title: Hershey’s closing Michigan Avenue store
summary: Hershey's is closing its Hershey's Chocolate World Chicago store on North Michigan Avenue in January, a move prompted by the company's focus on larger, more experiential stores. The Chicago location, which opened in 2005, is significantly smaller than new flagships in Las Vegas and a planned relocation in New York's Times Square. Its closure will add to the increasing number of vacant storefronts on the Magnificent Mile.

title: Stuck on Hold? The Worst Offenders Include Apple, Amazon, Airlines
summary: A new study by Fonolo, based on Twitter complaints, identifies the top companies and industries with the worst customer service hold times, with Apple and Amazon appearing as unexpected offenders. The article highlights customer frustration with long hold times and suggests technological solutions like call-backs or queue updates to improve customer satisfaction. It emphasizes that good post-sale service is crucial for customer retention and brand loyalty.

title: Report: Comcast Is Pennsylvania’s Most Valuable Company
summary: A new report by Broadview Networks identifies Comcast Corporation as Pennsylvania's most valuable company based on market capitalization. While Pennsylvania's economy was historically dominated by traditional industries, Comcast's $147 billion market cap now positions it as a leader, though it ranks below companies in thirteen other states, including New Jersey's Johnson & Johnson and California's Apple. The report highlights the shift in economic drivers for "rustbelt" states like Pennsylvania.

title: How the Twinkie made the super-rich even richer
summary: Private equity firms Apollo Global Management and Metropoulos & Co made 13 times their initial investment in Hostess, valuing the company at US$2.3 billion. The article examines how such deals enrich private equity executives, contrasting their significant profits with the impact on workers, many of whom lost jobs despite the company's "comeback." It also highlights the financing tactics used, such as dividend recapitalizations and tax arrangements, and the reliance on pension funds for investment capital.

title: Jabil Circuit’s Quarterly Results Largely Beat Targets
summary: Jabil Circuit's first-quarter results surpassed Wall Street projections, primarily driven by stronger performance in its diversified manufacturing services segment, which includes Apple. Despite a projected 12% decrease, segment revenue declined only 3% from the prior year. The electronics supplier anticipates an adjusted profit of 35 cents to 57 cents per share in the second quarter.

title: Samsung v. Apple – The Supreme Court Limits Damages in Design Patent Cases
summary: The Supreme Court unanimously reversed a nearly $400 million damages award against Samsung in a design patent infringement case brought by Apple, shifting the landscape for design patent damages. The Court clarified that "article of manufacture" in patent law can refer to a component rather than solely the entire sold product, introducing a two-step test for determining damages but remanding the case for the Federal Circuit to establish further guidelines. This decision creates uncertainty in damages calculation for multicomponent products and may influence future design patent application strategies.

title: Saudi Aramco and General Electric announce joint wind turbine venture
summary: Saudi Aramco and General Electric have partnered to install the first wind turbine in Saudi Arabia, aiming to power approximately 250 Saudi households and reduce diesel consumption. This initiative is a significant step towards renewable energy production in the oil-dependent kingdom, aligning with its Vision 2030 goal of diversifying its economy and reducing reliance on oil exports amidst falling oil prices. The move underscores Aramco's pivot towards becoming a global industrial conglomerate and a key player in sustainable energy solutions.

title: Nokia sues Apple over patent infringement
summary: Nokia has filed lawsuits against Apple in German and American courts, accusing the company of infringing on 32 patents related to display, user interface, software, and other technologies. This legal action follows Nokia's claims that Apple declined to license these inventions after an earlier agreement in 2011. The news comes shortly after Apple sued two licensing firms, alleging they colluded with Nokia to extort funds.

title: Neonode AirBar review: Transform your plain-Jane display into a ‘touchscreen,’ for just $69
summary: The Neonode AirBar is an inexpensive USB peripheral that can turn an ordinary laptop or monitor into a "touchscreen" for $69. It uses infrared light to detect touches, and while not as precise as a genuine touchscreen, it offers a serviceable alternative, especially for laptops. The AirBar's effectiveness is highly dependent on matching its size to the display, and it has a short USB cord.

title: TDK Agrees to Buy InvenSense for About $1.3 Billion in Cash
summary: TDK Corp. has agreed to acquire InvenSense Inc., a supplier of sensors to Apple Inc. and other smartphone makers, for approximately $1.3 billion in cash, valuing the company at $13 per share. This acquisition aims to strengthen TDK's position in the sensor business, with InvenSense's technology for motion and rotation tracking playing a key role, and includes retention bonuses for InvenSense executives.

title: Photoshop is hard to learn. Adobe thinks artificial intelligence can help
summary: Adobe is investing heavily in artificial intelligence with its new Sensei platform to make its powerful editing tools, like Photoshop, more accessible to a wider audience. Sensei aims to act as a virtual assistant, automating rote tasks, suggesting personalized solutions, and guiding first-time users through complex software, ultimately broadening Adobe's user base beyond professionals. This initiative is crucial for Adobe's growth in an era of popular, easy-to-use mobile editing apps, allowing the company to maintain its dominance and potentially increase annual sales.

title: South Korea fines Qualcomm $854 million for violating competition laws
summary: South Korea's antitrust regulator fined Qualcomm Inc 1.03 trillion won ($854 million) for unfair business practices in patent licensing and modem chip sales, the largest fine ever levied in South Korea. Qualcomm plans to challenge the decision, which alleges the company abused its dominant market position and restricted competition by refusing or limiting licensing of its standard essential patents to rival chipmakers. This ruling could significantly impact Qualcomm's business model and dealings with major tech companies globally.

title: South Korea fines Qualcomm $854 million for violating competition laws
summary: South Korea's antitrust regulator has fined Qualcomm Inc. 1.03 trillion won ($854 million) for unfair business practices in patent licensing and modem chip sales, the largest such fine ever in South Korea. Qualcomm plans to challenge the decision in court. The ruling could significantly impact Qualcomm's business model and lower costs for mobile phone makers, while also setting a precedent for other regulators globally investigating the chipmaker.
authors: Se Young Lee and Stephen Nellis

title: Apple Said to Seek Lower Taxes to Start Manufacturing in India
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly seeking tax concessions from the Indian government, including lower import and manufacturing duties, to begin manufacturing iPhones in the country. The company sent a letter to the industry ministry requesting these concessions, which it wants to continue even after the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax. This move indicates Apple's intention to establish a manufacturing presence in India.

title: CES 2017: Amazon Echo-Style Voice Control Is Coming to More Appliances
summary: At CES 2017, Whirlpool announced that its new home appliances will be compatible with Amazon's Alexa voice assistant, allowing users to control tasks like laundry and oven settings with voice commands. This partnership signifies Amazon's strategy to integrate Alexa deeper into the smart home ecosystem, facing competition from other tech giants like Apple and Samsung who are also developing their own voice-controlled platforms.

summary: This page provides comprehensive information on the WisdomTree Barclays Yield Enhanced US Aggregate Bond ETF (AGGY), including current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), key statistics like expense ratio, total assets, and dividend yield, and historical performance data. It also features a company profile for WisdomTree Trust and a collection of news and analysis relevant to bond markets and the ETF.

title: A Touchscreen MacBook Air? It's Possible with This
summary: Neonode has introduced a new version of its AirBar specifically designed for the MacBook Air, which will allow the laptop's screen to become a touchscreen. Priced at $99 and launching in March, this accessory uses infrared light to detect finger movements, fitting the 13.3-inch display model. While the prototype at CES had some limitations like single-finger input, Neonode promises multi-touch gestures at launch.

title: Amazon to surpass 1 GW of clean energy capacity. Is it enough?
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to significantly expand its renewable energy capacity, nearing 1 GW of wind and solar power in 2017, positioning itself as a major player in clean computing infrastructure. Despite these substantial investments, the company faces criticism for a lack of transparency regarding its overall energy consumption and carbon footprint compared to its competitors. Amazon is focused on broadening renewable energy adoption, particularly in regions where it has been slower to catch on, and is actively participating in clean energy policy debates.

title: iPhone Turns 10
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: The article celebrates the 10th anniversary of the Apple iPhone's launch, highlighting its revolutionary impact on communication, entertainment, and daily life. It recounts Steve Jobs's original introduction and includes statements from current Apple CEO Tim Cook and Sprint CEO Marcelo Claure, emphasizing the iPhone's influence and Apple's continued innovation. Apple has sold over one billion iPhones since its debut.

title: Julian Robertson – Start With Management & Avoid REIT’s
summary: Julian Robertson, founder of Tiger Management, shares his investment philosophy, emphasizing the importance of excellent management and growth potential. He discusses his start in investing, his success through hiring talented young people, and his views on specific stocks like Apple, while cautioning against investments like REITs. Robertson also advises young analysts to focus on fundamentally sound companies in overlooked markets.

title: Live Nation Headquarters Moving to NYC’s Meatpacking District
summary: Live Nation, the world's largest music promoter, is relocating its headquarters from Times Square to a 100,000-square-foot building in New York City's Meatpacking District. This move follows Live Nation's record-breaking third quarter with $3.21 billion in revenues, a 23% increase year-over-year. The new location places Live Nation among other major tech and retail companies in the increasingly gentrified neighborhood.

title: USPX Stock Price | Franklin U.S. Equity Index ETF
summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the Franklin U.S. Equity Index ETF (USPX), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and various key statistics such as expense ratio, dividend yield, and asset class. It also offers insights into its top holdings like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, and presents technical analysis ratings. The ETF aims to track the performance of the Morningstar US Target Market Exposure Index.

title: Why a Former Employee Thinks Tim Cook Made Apple ‘Boring’
summary: A former Apple engineer, Bob Burrough, claims that CEO Tim Cook has transformed Apple into a "boring operations company," stifling innovation and altering the corporate culture established by Steve Jobs. While Cook has significantly improved Apple's financial health, critics argue the company has lost some of its former allure and innovation drive under his leadership. Current Apple executives acknowledge Cook's different leadership style compared to Jobs but maintain that innovation is still strong.

title: Netflix is developing an emotional bond with customers that will help its stock price
summary: Netflix has significantly climbed in brand intimacy rankings, moving from 25th to 5th place on MBLM's 2017 list due to the strong emotional bond formed with consumers through binge-watching. This heightened brand intimacy is linked to greater financial success, as brands on this list have historically outperformed the S&P 500 index. Apple also notably topped the rankings in various categories.

summary: This article provides comprehensive information on the Invesco Global Short Term High Yield Bond ETF (PGHY), including its current stock price, historical data, key statistics, and technical analysis. It details the fund's profile, holdings, performance, and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF. title: Swift: Most In-Demand Language for Developers
summary: Upwork's Q4 2016 report on fastest-growing skills identifies Swift as the most in-demand language, even though natural language processing holds the top spot for overall desirable technology. The article attributes Swift's rise to the popularity of Apple products, including the Apple Watch, and notes its growing dominance over Objective-C among Apple developers. It also highlights other top skills like natural language processing, Tableau, Amazon Marketplace, and Stripe, indicating trends in employer demand for freelancers.

title: PBD Stock Price | Invesco Global Clean Energy ETF
summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the Invesco Global Clean Energy ETF (PBD), including its current stock price, historical data, key statistics like expense ratio and dividend yield, and technical analysis. It also lists top holdings and recent news related to the clean energy sector and associated companies, even though some news appears to be mismatched with the ETF itself (e.g., Protalix BioTherapeutics). Investors can find performance metrics, company profile, and frequently asked questions about PBD.

title: FXF Stock Price | Invesco CurrencyShares Swiss Franc Trust ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information about the Invesco CurrencyShares Swiss Franc Trust ETF (FXF), including its current stock price, historical data, and key statistics. It outlines the ETF's profile, performance metrics, and technical analysis, indicating a "Strong Buy" signal. The fund aims to track the Swiss Franc's spot price against the USD and is managed by Invesco Specialized Products, LLC.

title: How we started the Arab world's biggest music service
summary: This article details the founding and growth of Anghami, the Arab world's biggest home-grown music streaming service, by Eddy Maroun and Elie Habib. It covers their journey from composing ringtones to overcoming challenges like fundraising in the Middle East and poor internet infrastructure, to building a platform with 30 million users and 20 million songs. The founders' initial inspiration came from the prevalence of music piracy, and they aimed to provide a convenient and affordable legal alternative.

title: Tech Companies Fight Trump Immigration Order in Court (Published 2017)
summary: Amazon and Expedia have intensified their opposition to President Trump's immigration order by filing declarations in a federal lawsuit, arguing that the order would negatively impact their businesses and operations. This move represents an escalation of the tech industry's efforts to challenge the executive order, which restricts immigration from several Muslim-majority countries. Other tech companies like Google also saw employee demonstrations against the order.

title: Laurene Powell Jobs Cuts Disney Stake in Half
summary: Laurene Powell Jobs, widow of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, has reduced her ownership stake in Disney by half, according to recent regulatory filings. She now beneficially owns 64.3 million Disney shares, down from 128.3 million shares at the end of 2015, making Vanguard Group the largest Disney shareholder. The reduction is part of her normal long-term financial planning and portfolio diversification through Emerson Collective, which focuses on philanthropy and impact investing.

title: Apple Kicks Off 2017 With Big Hints About the Future
summary: Apple started 2017 with strong financial results, including nearly $18 billion in profits and a record $78.4 billion revenue in Q4 2016. CEO Tim Cook hinted at a potential major media acquisition and an increased focus on the smart home market. The company also saw updates on its new headquarters, plans for iPhone production in India, and faced a class-action lawsuit regarding FaceTime.

title: 'Creating an icon': Apple Inc's new 'spaceship' HQ keeps getting delayed as company seeks design perfection
summary: Apple Inc.'s new "spaceship" headquarters in Cupertino, Calif., is experiencing delays as the company pursues design perfection, mirroring co-founder Steve Jobs's meticulous attention to detail. The futuristic campus, estimated to cost US$5 billion, emphasizes flawless construction and adherence to specific design principles, causing some friction with traditional construction practices and leading to extended timelines. Despite the high cost and strict demands, the project aims to create a symbolic icon for the global company rather than just maximizing office productivity.

title: Apple Inc. gears up to distribute $3.1 billion in dividends to shareholders
summary: Apple Inc. is preparing to pay $3.1 billion in quarterly dividends to shareholders on February 16, with investors needing to own stock by February 9 to qualify. This payment is part of Apple's ongoing capital return program, which heavily prioritizes stock buybacks, having spent $144 billion on buybacks and $250 billion total on capital return since mid-2012. The article also touches on Apple's international expansion and its strategy regarding its substantial overseas cash reserves.

title: Quanta Maintains Position as Largest Notebook ODM for HP
summary: Quanta Computer is set to remain HP's largest notebook ODM in 2017, securing 35% of HP's total orders, an increase from 32% in 2016. This allocation translates to 12.25 million units out of an estimated 35 million notebooks HP plans to ship. Quanta is also noted as the largest notebook ODM for Apple, Acer, and Asustek Computer, even though its total notebook shipments dropped in 2016.

title: Why Apple Still May Need to Do a Big Media Deal
summary: Apple is facing a significant challenge in doubling its $24 billion annual services revenue within four years as pledged, with analysts estimating a multi-billion dollar shortfall if growth is purely organic. This gap suggests Apple might pursue a major media acquisition, despite mixed messages from executives and a current focus on financing smaller content projects for Apple Music. Potential targets discussed include Netflix, CBS, Walt Disney, Activision Blizzard, or PayPal, leveraging Apple's substantial overseas cash reserves.

title: BlackBerry’s Global Market Share Is Now 0.0 Percent
summary: Research firm Gartner, Inc., reported that BlackBerry's global smartphone market share has fallen to 0.0 percent in the fourth quarter of 2016, a significant decline from its past dominance. This follows BlackBerry's 2016 announcement to cease manufacturing its own phones and focus on software. Despite this, a new BlackBerry phone, the "Mercury," is anticipated from TCL, which acquired the brand name.

title: Soros gets out of gold, Paulson cuts SPDR Gold shares
summary: Soros Fund Management completely sold off its gold holdings in Barrick Gold during the fourth quarter of 2016, while Paulson & Co. reduced its stake in the SPDR Gold Trust. This move by major investors followed a significant 12.5 percent drop in spot gold prices, marking its weakest quarterly performance in three and a half years, largely influenced by the U.S. presidential election and a hawkish Federal Reserve. Other firms, such as CI Investments Inc., also made similar reductions in gold-related investments.

title: Latest Gartner data shows Apple edge out Samsung in market share during Q4 2016
summary: Gartner's latest report indicates that Apple surpassed Samsung in global smartphone market share during Q4 2016, marking the first time in eight quarters Apple held the top spot, driven by strong iPhone 7 sales. However, Samsung remained the overall leader for 2016. Chinese manufacturers like Huawei, Oppo, and BBK continued to gain market share from both Apple and Samsung.

title: Here’s Why Apple Isn’t Building an Amazon Echo Rival
summary: The article explains why Apple is not directly competing with Amazon's Echo device despite the success of voice-activated AI. Apple's strategy focuses on making Siri an omnipresent AI assistant across all its devices, rather than creating a dedicated smart speaker. The author argues this approach, exemplified by Siri's integration with AirPods and HomeKit, will prove more effective in the long run for winning the AI wars.

title: Episode 755: The Phone At The End Of The World : Planet Money
summary: In the late 2000s, populist Argentinian President Cristina Kirchner mandated that companies selling goods in Argentina must manufacture them there to create jobs. While Apple refused, companies like Blackberry's maker set up production in a remote, southern tip of Argentina called Tierra del Fuego. This episode explores the challenges and outcomes of this ambitious plan to boost manufacturing and employment.

title: Warren Buffett loves cheap airline stocks, which could get a boost from Trump
summary: Warren Buffett has invested in four airline stocks, and these holdings could become even more attractive if President Donald Trump's corporate tax plan is implemented. Airlines, which currently face high income tax rates, stand to benefit significantly from a reduction in corporate taxes. This prospective tax cut could dramatically increase their profitability, enriching Buffett and other airline investors.

title: Why did Emerson Network Power rebrand itself as Vertiv?
summary: Emerson Network Power rebranded as Vertiv in January 2017, following its sale by Emerson Electric Co. to Platinum Equity. This transition to a privately-held company allows Vertiv greater flexibility in business strategy, investment decisions, and the ability to focus on long-term growth as a standalone entity. Vertiv aims to capitalize on growth opportunities in Southeast Asia, particularly in cloud computing, data centers, and advanced network infrastructure.

title: Apple Park opens to employees in April
summary: Apple announced that its new 175-acre campus, Apple Park, would begin opening to employees in April 2017. The campus, envisioned by Steve Jobs, emphasizes creativity, collaboration, and environmental sustainability, featuring a ring-shaped main building, extensive parklands, and 100 percent renewable energy. The theater at Apple Park will be named the Steve Jobs Theater in honor of Apple's co-founder.

title: Apple to Open New Campus in April
summary: Apple Inc. announced that its new campus, to be named Apple Park, will open in April after six months of employee relocation. The 175-acre campus, reminiscent of a "spaceship," was one of Steve Jobs's final projects. Reports estimate the cost of the campus to be between $3 billion and $5 billion.

title: AMD's Pricing Strategy To Deeply Undercut Intel
summary: AMD introduced its new Ryzen chip line, claiming they match the performance of high-end Intel chips at half the price, aiming to regain market share from Intel. The flagship Ryzen 7 1800X chip, priced at $500, offers comparable performance to Intel's $1,000 Broadwell chip. This aggressive strategy and strong initial analyst reception have significantly boosted AMD's stock price over the past year.

title: Array Technologies to supply trackers for Facebook solar plants in New Mexico
summary: Affordable Solar will utilize Array Technologies' DuraTrack HZ v3 trackers for three 10 MW-AC solar projects in New Mexico, which will supply power to Facebook's new data center. The $37 million contract was awarded by Public Service Company of New Mexico (PNM), and the solar plants are expected to help the data center achieve 100% renewable energy use. This deal highlights the growing trend of corporate renewable energy procurement through "Green Tariff" arrangements in regulated utility areas.

title: Cisco’s John Chambers Bets on Google and Citi-Backed Voice Security Startup
summary: John Chambers, executive chairman of Cisco Systems, has invested in Pindrop Security, a startup focused on voice authentication and phone fraud protection. Chambers believes voice will be the next major technology interface, similar to how he anticipated the shift to IP telephony during his time as Cisco CEO. Pindrop, backed by Alphabet's CapitalG, GV, Citi Ventures, and Andreessen Horowitz, aims to provide an agnostic voice security solution for various tech giants' virtual assistants and voice-activated devices.

title: Exclusive: Apple expanding its Seattle engineering center, making a big bet on artificial intelligence
summary: Apple is significantly expanding its engineering operations in Seattle, with a focus on artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies for its products. The expansion is marked by a commitment to take additional floors at Two Union Square and is supported by Apple's acquisition of Seattle startup Turi, which also led to a new $1 million endowed professorship at the University of Washington. This move highlights Seattle's growing importance as a hub for AI development, attracting talent and investment from tech giants and startups alike.

title: Apple Cut Ties With Supplier Super Micro Computer Over Server Security Concerns
summary: Apple ended its business relationship with server supplier Super Micro Computer in 2016 due to a potential security vulnerability found in one of Super Micro's data center servers. While Super Micro suggests Apple received bad firmware from an infiltrated FTP site, Apple denies receiving bad firmware or any customer data being stolen. Apple has since diversified its server suppliers, increasing orders from ZT and purchasing from Inspur.

title: FVAL Stock Price | Fidelity Value Factor ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Fidelity Value Factor ETF (FVAL), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and key financial statistics such as market capitalization, expense ratio, dividend yield, and inception date. It also lists top holdings and offers performance data, aiming to help investors assess FVAL's potential.

title: Apple Shareholder Proposal Seeks More Diverse Company Leadership
summary: A group of Apple investors is pushing for a shareholder proposal to increase diversity in the company's senior management and board of directors. Lead investor Tony Maldonado argues that a lack of diversity poses a business risk for Apple, especially as it seeks new growth avenues beyond the iPhone. Despite Apple's recommendation to reject the proposal, supporting investors like Zevin Asset Management believe current diversity initiatives haven't adequately changed the composition of the company's leadership.

title: Faces of HCA Healthcare: Dr. Kenneth Sands
summary: This article introduces Dr. Kenneth E. F. Sands, HCA Healthcare's new chief epidemiologist and chief patient safety officer. It covers his career journey, his philosophy of care focusing on patient experience and dignity, and his vision for improving care reliability at HCA Healthcare. Dr. Sands also shares personal insights and advice, emphasizing open-mindedness and continuous improvement in healthcare.

title: Apple deleted server supplier after finding infected firmware in servers [Updated]
summary: Apple reportedly purged its data centers of servers from Supermicro after detecting malware-infected firmware in an internal App Store development environment and some Siri production servers. While an Apple spokesperson denied a security incident, Supermicro's senior VP confirmed Apple ended their relationship due to the compromised systems. An update clarified the compromised firmware affected Apple’s design lab servers, not active Siri servers, and was downloaded from Supermicro’s support site.

title: Apple’s Next iPhone Will Have a Curved Screen
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly adopting a flexible OLED display for one of its new iPhone models this year, coinciding with the iPhone's 10th anniversary. Rival Samsung Electronics Co. is expected to be the initial supplier of these curved screens. Apple has ordered sufficient components to support mass production of this new design.

title: Apple Ranked Behind Google on This Corporate Reputation Study
summary: Apple's corporate reputation has fallen, landing at 20th place in the Reputation Institute’s Global RepTrak 100 study in early 2017, a drop from 10th place the previous year. While still strong in products and corporate performance, Apple's score was notably hurt by its perceived "citizenship." Competitors like Google and Microsoft ranked higher, with Samsung experiencing an even more significant decline in its reputation, likely due to the Galaxy Note 7 issues.

title: Meg Whitman: Why I decided to shrink Hewlett-Packard
summary: Meg Whitman, former CEO of Hewlett-Packard, explains her decision to split the technology giant into two companies, Hewlett Packard Enterprise and HP Inc., and her subsequent further spinning off of businesses within HPE. She argues that speed and focus are critical in the modern technology landscape, where smaller, nimble start-ups often outperform larger, slower corporations. This strategy allows the separated entities to pursue their own agendas without being hindered by conflicting business characteristics.

title: Here’s How Many iPhones Are Currently Being Used Worldwide
summary: A new analyst report estimates there are over 700 million iPhones currently in use globally, with potential to reach 1 billion within a couple of years. This figure includes a significant portion of second-hand devices, highlighting the iPhone's enduring popularity and suggesting future upgrade cycles could boost Apple's sales and services revenue. The increasing longevity of iPhones in use could also be a tailwind for Apple's Services business, despite Google's Android having a larger global user base.

title: IBM's Deal With Salesforce Highlights the Company's Unique AI Position
summary: IBM's new deal with Salesforce.com showcases its unique position in the AI market, focusing on industry-specific solutions rather than broad consumer applications. This partnership leverages IBM Watson's expertise in specialized data analysis with Salesforce's Einstein platform to deliver targeted insights for various sectors. The article highlights IBM's strategy of using less demanding machine learning techniques and domain-specific knowledge to serve a wide range of enterprise use cases.

title: Capital One launches Eno the gender neutral AI assistant
summary: Capital One has launched Eno, a gender-neutral virtual assistant designed to help customers manage their money through conversational text messaging. Eno is powered by AI and responds as "binary" when asked about its gender, a deliberate choice by Capital One to avoid the criticism often directed at female-voiced digital assistants like Siri and Alexa. The assistant can respond to natural language and emojis, allowing users to check balances or pay bills simply.
authors: REUTERS and STACY LIBERATORE|US SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY EDITOR

title: Tesla's 'Vision' and Autopilot chip efforts validated by Intel's $15 billion acquisition of Mobileye
summary: Intel's $15 billion acquisition of Mobileye for its autonomous driving capabilities validates Tesla's in-house approach to developing its own vision system and chip efforts for Autopilot. Tesla had previously discontinued using Mobileye's system and is working on its own SoC, with a world-class team and rumors of a partnership with Samsung. While Tesla's current vision system isn't yet on par with Mobileye's, a software update is expected to bring parity, and future self-driving capabilities could even surpass Mobileye's timeline.

title: Taiwan's TSMC Tops Intel by Market Value for First Time: Chart
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) surpassed Intel Corp. in market value for the first time, driven by its success in mobile computing and Intel's struggles in the declining personal computer market. TSMC's profits have benefited from partnerships with major customers like Apple Inc. and Qualcomm Inc.

title: OUTFRONT Media CMO says her job is to keep moving the ball down the field
summary: Jodi Senese, CMO of OUTFRONT Media, discusses her 36-year career in out-of-home advertising, starting from an internship and rising through the ranks. She emphasizes the importance of continuous learning and adaptation in the evolving digital marketing landscape, highlighting her role in transitioning OUTFRONT Media from a static medium to a dynamic OOH advertising company. The article delves into her daily routine, career influences, challenges, and insights into the industry.

title: QQQ ETF Pegs New High: Best Large-Cap Growth Fund Or Tech Sector Star?
summary: The PowerShares QQQ ETF has been the best-performing large-cap growth fund over the past 10 years, with an average annual gain of 12.8%. While it has strong performance, some experts view it more as a technology sector fund due to its heavy tech weighting (over 50%), suggesting it's better suited for a satellite role in a diversified portfolio rather than a core holding. The article also highlights QQQ's broader exposure to "tech innovators" that blur traditional sector lines and its potential benefits from cash repatriation under President Trump.

title: Apple Shares Shine, Rising 24%
summary: Apple Inc. shares experienced their best quarterly performance in five years, climbing 24% in the first three months of 2017, demonstrating a strong resurgence for formerly lagging technology trades. The tech giant increased its market value by $144.8 billion during this period, with its shares reaching all-time highs. This significant capital appreciation in a single quarter has only been surpassed by Apple itself, in the first quarter of 2012.

title: Apple ditches UK's Imagination Technologies to make its own chips
summary: Apple Inc. announced it will stop using Imagination Technologies' graphics designs for its iPhone, iPad, and Apple Watch, opting to create its own chips instead. This decision has led to a significant drop in Imagination Technologies' share price and could result in a legal battle, as Apple's license fees and royalties accounted for about half of Imagination's revenue last year. Imagination Technologies disputes Apple's ability to develop its own GPU without infringing on existing intellectual property.

title: Here's What a $1,000 Investment in Amazon's 1997 Debut Is Worth Today
summary: This article examines the impressive growth of an early investment in Amazon.com Inc. since its 1997 IPO. It details how a $1,000 investment, despite early operating losses and multiple stock splits, would have grown to over half a million dollars by 2017 due to Amazon's aggressive expansion and market dominance. The piece highlights Amazon's journey from an online bookseller to a major e-commerce and tech giant, contrasting its performance with the broader retail sector.

title: Amazon Alexa has become one of my BFFs, says Omnicom Media chief
summary: Omnicom Media Group's Philippa Brown highlights how Amazon's Alexa has transformed her work by enabling multitasking and streamlining daily tasks. She notes Alexa's continuous learning and expanding functionalities, impacting sectors like automotive, energy, and travel. Brown emphasizes the growing importance of voice-activated search for brands and anticipates significant developments, particularly in the automotive industry.

title: Flo TV to Launch Sports Datacasting This Month, as Parent Qualcomm Studies Datacasting for Magazines and Other Opportunities
summary: Qualcomm's Flo TV is set to launch sports datacasting this month, aiming to offload data traffic and enhance its mobile TV services with sports stats and interviews. The company is also exploring datacasting opportunities for digital magazines, addressing the high data transfer costs of current offerings like the iPad version of Wired magazine. While Flo TV has seen slower than expected subscriber growth, Qualcomm is investigating new business models and international expansion, with a focus on improving the service's value proposition.

title: The five-minute CIO: Dharmesh Shah, CTO, HubSpot
summary: Dharmesh Shah, co-founder and CTO of HubSpot, discusses the company's origins, its product development ethos, and the expansion of its European headquarters in Dublin. He highlights HubSpot's focus on creating intuitive software and its optimistic view on the future of AI and conversational user interfaces, particularly with the GrowthBot product. Shah also touches upon the challenges and successes of building a strong employer brand in Dublin and the technical hurdles of scaling their platform.

title: Amazon, Wal-Mart and Apple Top List of Biggest E-commerce Retailers
summary: Amazon, Apple, and Wal-Mart lead the list of top e-commerce retailers in the U.S. according to eMarketer data, with Amazon dominating online sales. While Amazon alone generated $94.7 billion in online sales, surpassing the combined total of the next 49 retailers, companies like Hudson's Bay Co. and Ulta Beauty showed significant e-commerce sales growth. The article also highlights how e-commerce profitability challenges affect traditional retailers.

title: BIL ETF Stock Price History
summary: This article provides comprehensive historical stock price data for the State Street SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF (BIL). It includes daily, weekly, or monthly intervals of closing, opening, high, low, price change, and percentage change. The data covers a specific date range, offering statistics on performance over that period, along with frequently asked questions regarding BIL's performance.

title: Why contract manufacturing is getting into design
summary: Sanmina Corp.'s executive vice president for Europe, Middle-East and Africa, Dietmar Guenther, explains how contract manufacturing has evolved beyond pure manufacturing to include design work, technology selection, and supply chain management. This shift is driven by clients seeking to offload hardware development and the increasing digitalization of products, allowing EMS companies like Sanmina to add significant value and provide end-to-end system solutions, leveraging their global footprint and engineering expertise.

title: Yelp Is Using Robots to Deliver Food in San Francisco
summary: Yelp Eat24 is partnering with robotics startup Marble to use self-driving robots for food deliveries in San Francisco's Mission and Potrero Hill neighborhoods. These robots will partially take over deliveries previously handled by human couriers, with Marble's team including former Apple and Google employees. The initiative aims to make delivery more efficient and affordable, with Marble also announcing a $4 million seed funding round to expand its robot fleet.

title: Yext Joins $1B Club With Successful IPO, Continuing Software's Hot Market Run
summary: Yext, a software company specializing in digital knowledge management, achieved a market capitalization exceeding $1.1 billion after a successful IPO on the New York Stock Exchange. Despite being valued at $525 million in its last private funding round, shares surged 25% on its first day of trading. CEO Howard Lerman views the public offering as a strategic marketing event to accelerate the company's growth in a "winner-take-all market" for structured answers across digital platforms.

title: Qualcomm strikes back, sues Apple for tortious interference and unlawful business tactics
summary: Qualcomm has filed a lawsuit against Apple, accusing it of tortious interference and unlawful business tactics. The suit seeks a declaration that Qualcomm has not violated FRAND obligations concerning SEP licensing and denies Apple's prior allegations, arguing that Apple has mischaracterized Qualcomm's business model and pressured regulatory bodies and contract manufacturers. Qualcomm alleges Apple intentionally limited the performance of iPhones using Qualcomm chips and breached agreement obligations, seeking a jury trial.

title: Ally Bank customers can now add their debit cards to Apple Pay
summary: Ally Bank has announced that its customers can now add their debit cards to Apple Pay, joining over 1,000 other financial institutions supporting the contactless payment system. The bank also stated plans to support Android Pay and Samsung Pay in the future to further expand mobile payment options for its customers. This move aims to make banking more convenient and secure for Ally Bank users.

title: New iPhone screen puts blue-colored spotlight on Japan supplier
summary: Apple's adoption of OLED screens for its new iPhone is highlighting Japanese suppliers, particularly Idemitsu Kosan Co., which has a long history in OLED material development. This move is expected to significantly boost demand for OLED displays and intensify competition among material suppliers like Dow Chemical, Merck & Co., and Universal Display Corp.

title: Apple Isn't a Tech Company
summary: This article argues that Apple is fundamentally a design company rather than a technology company, viewing technology as ingredients for enhancing user experience rather than as standalone products. It highlights Apple's organizational structure which prioritizes designers, its public storytelling through design, and its product strategies, such as Apple Watch bands and AR development, as evidence of this design-centric approach. The author contends that Apple's risk lies in becoming too much of a tech company, moving away from its design-led philosophy.

title: Live Nation Productions And Sean "Diddy" Combs' Can't Stop Won't Stop: A Bad Boy Story* Coming To Apple Music
summary: Live Nation Productions announced that the film "Can't Stop Won't Stop: A Bad Boy Story" will be exclusively available on Apple Music starting June 25, 2017, following its World Premiere at the 2017 Tribeca Film Festival. The documentary explores the history and legacy of Sean "Diddy" Combs' Bad Boy Records, detailing its impact on music and culture and culminating in the 2016 Bad Boy Family reunion concert. This partnership between Live Nation, Bad Boy, and Apple Music aims to share the story of the iconic label and its influence.

title: Apple’s Cash Hoard Set to Top $250 Billion
summary: Apple Inc. is projected to announce an unprecedented cash reserve exceeding a quarter of a trillion dollars, surpassing the market value of companies like Wal-Mart or Procter & Gamble, and even the combined foreign-currency reserves of the U.K. and Canada. The vast majority of this cash is held outside the U.S., which could intensify pressure on Apple to distribute more funds to shareholders or pursue major acquisitions if proposed corporate tax reforms and a tax holiday on repatriated cash are enacted by President Trump. This immense cash pile comes after Apple has sold over a billion iPhones, securing 91% of smartphone sector profits.

title: Apple, Inc. Earnings: 3 Questions for Tim Cook
summary: Ahead of Apple's second-quarter earnings release, investors should focus on three key questions for CEO Tim Cook: the sustainability of Apple's recent growth, potential new product segments, and the long-term momentum of its services business. After several quarters of decline, Apple has returned to growth, and investors are keen to understand if this trend will continue. The article also highlights the importance of new product categories to drive future growth, given the iPhone's maturing sales, and the robust performance of the services segment.

title: Buffett Says His IBM Thesis Was Flawed, He Blew It on Google
summary: Warren Buffett admitted that his investment thesis for IBM was flawed, acknowledging that he "blew it" by not investing in Google. He also clarified that he views Apple as a consumer-products business, distinct from IBM, and believes it offers different opportunities. Buffett, chairman of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., discussed these points at the company's annual meeting.

title: Abbott's Infinity Deep Brain Stimulation System is rare technology advance
summary: Abbott's Infinity Deep Brain Stimulation System, touted as the first major technological advancement in 20 years for treating movement disorders, allows doctors to precisely "sculpt" electrical fields in the brain. This system, which can be controlled via an iPod Touch, directly targets specific brain areas while avoiding others, reducing side effects. Patients like John Alexander have experienced significant improvements in symptoms and quality of life after receiving the implant.

title: Elon Musk: Robot software will make Tesla worth as much as Apple
summary: Elon Musk believes that the key to Tesla achieving a $700 billion market capitalization comparable to Apple Inc. lies in its robot manufacturing software. With new production lines for Model 3, batteries, and solar panels launching soon, Musk has the opportunity to prove Tesla's advanced automated manufacturing capabilities. If successful, Tesla's manufacturing prowess could give it a significant advantage in the market.

title: Apple becomes first $800 billion company with stock price increase
summary: Apple Inc. briefly surpassed an $800 billion market capitalization, becoming the first company to do so, following a stock price increase to $153.70 per share on Monday. The surge was attributed to a positive research note from Drexel Hamilton's Brian White, who set an ambitious price target of $185 to $202 per share, suggesting Apple could reach a trillion-dollar valuation. Despite concerns about the consumer electronics sector, Apple's ability to seamlessly integrate hardware, software, and services has demonstrated its market resilience.

title: Apple announces $1 billion data center expansion east of Reno
summary: Apple announced a $1 billion expansion of its data center east of Reno, bringing its total investment there to $2 billion. The company secured approval from the Reno City Council for sales tax abatements by agreeing to build a warehouse in downtown Reno, which will further reduce its sales tax rate on computer equipment. This expansion is expected to create 100 permanent jobs and 300 construction jobs, contributing to Northern Nevada's growing technology sector.

title: Apple awards Corning first Advanced Manufacturing Fund investment
summary: Apple has awarded Corning Incorporated $200 million from its new Advanced Manufacturing Fund to support the company's R&D, capital equipment, and glass processing. This investment highlights the decade-long collaboration between Apple and Corning, particularly focusing on Corning's Harrodsburg, Kentucky facility which produces the cover glass for iOS devices. The initiative is part of Apple's commitment to invest at least $1 billion in US-based companies to foster innovative production and high-skilled jobs in American manufacturing.

title: Small Kentucky city hails Apple investment but wonders, 'How many jobs?'
summary: Apple Inc. announced a $200 million investment in Corning Inc.'s Harrodsburg, Kentucky, plant, which manufactures Gorilla Glass for iPhones. While local residents and officials welcome the investment, they are eager to learn how many new jobs it will create in the small city. The investment is seen as a way to sustain and potentially expand the 400 existing jobs at the decades-old factory.

title: Apple Investing $200 Million In American Facility For Scratch-Resistant Glass
summary: Apple announced an investment of $200 million in Corning Inc.'s Harrodsburg, Kentucky facility, dedicated to producing scratch-resistant Gorilla Glass for its iPhones and iPads. This investment is the first from Apple's $1 billion Advanced Manufacturing Fund, aimed at supporting U.S.-based companies, and acknowledges Corning's crucial role in developing the glass that replaced plastic screens on early iPhones. The partnership has created 1,000 U.S. jobs, and this funding will sustain the 400 employees in Harrodsburg, adding to Kentucky's recent significant business investments.

title: Top 20 companies in Houston for job-seeking graduates
summary: This article lists top companies in Houston for recent graduates looking for jobs, highlighting the city's strong economy in oil/energy, healthcare, and IT. It names companies identified by LinkedIn and Glassdoor as top hirers of new graduates and best places to work. The article also mentions the Memorial Hermann Hospital System as the area's largest employer.

title: Wal-Mart experiments with tech-enhanced ‘Next-Gen’ stores
summary: Wal-Mart is testing new "Next-Gen" supercenter layouts in Tomball, TX, and Lake Nona, FL, integrating technology and revised merchandising to enhance the shopping experience. These stores feature improved "Scan and Go" checkout, interactive displays for connected devices and an "endless aisle" for online items, and Wi-Fi buttons to page staff. This initiative is part of a broader effort by Wal-Mart and other traditional retailers like Target to blend e-commerce and brick-and-mortar experiences, as research indicates physical stores can boost online sales and serve as crucial fulfillment hubs.

title: Legal and Regulatory Issues Challenge Qualcomm’s Business Model
summary: Qualcomm, a favored stock for dividend investors due to its strong financials and effective business model, faces significant legal and regulatory challenges. These issues, including antitrust fines and disputes with key customers like Apple and BlackBerry, are threatening its profitable licensing business and have caused a 15% stock drop since the beginning of 2017. This article examines the impact of these challenges on Qualcomm's future earnings.

title: Surprise, surprise: Most iPhone users stick with the iPhone
summary: A Morgan Stanley survey revealed that 92 percent of iPhone users are likely to purchase another iPhone, marking a significant retention rate of Apple's customer base. This figure is a notable increase from the previous year and surpasses competitor retention rates, signaling strong brand loyalty despite a slight slowdown in recent iPhone sales. Morgan Stanley has high confidence in Apple, raising its target stock price and dismissing rumors of an iPhone 8 delay.

title: Spotify buys AI startup Niland to develop its music personalization and recommendations
summary: Spotify has acquired Paris-based AI startup Niland, marking its fourth acquisition of the year. Niland will join Spotify's R&D team in New York to enhance its music personalization and recommendation features, which are a key part of its service. This move further strengthens Spotify's technology as it competes with rivals like Apple Music and prepares for a potential IPO.

title: What Qualcomm Inc.'s New Snapdragon 660 Means for TSMC and Samsung
summary: Qualcomm recently announced its new Snapdragon 660 and 630 processors, which will be manufactured using a 14-nanometer technology by Samsung, not TSMC. This move shifts production of Qualcomm's high-end and premium-tier processors away from TSMC to Samsung. The article discusses the implications for both foundries, suggesting Samsung benefits from increased volumes while TSMC faces the challenge of winning back orders or strengthening relationships with Qualcomm's competitors and other chip designers like Apple and Huawei.

title: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) holdings
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) holdings. It details the top 10 holdings, sector weightings, overall portfolio composition, and key ETF summary metrics. The VOO ETF aims to track the performance of the S&P 500 Index.

title: New! Denny’s on Demand: Whatever. Whenever. Now, Wherever.
summary: Denny's has launched 'Denny's on Demand,' a new online ordering platform that allows customers to order food for takeout or delivery 24/7. This initiative expands Denny's "always open" philosophy to digital channels, making their full menu available anytime, anywhere. Denny's also incorporated social media integrations and new custom-designed packaging to enhance the customer experience.

title: How Apple Has Contributed to Green Bonds
summary: Apple has emerged as a leader in green bonds, being the first and largest US corporation to issue them. The company's $1.5 billion green bond is focused on reducing climate change hazards through renewable energy, energy efficiency, safer materials, and resource preservation. Apple's initiatives include powering its headquarters with 100% renewable energy and using robots to disassemble iPhones for material reuse.

title: BlackBerry settles arbitration with Qualcomm, will receive $940 million for contract dispute over patent royalties
summary: BlackBerry Ltd (NASDAQ:BBRY) announced a settlement with Qualcomm, Inc. (NASDAQ:QCOM) regarding a contract dispute over patent royalties, with Qualcomm agreeing to pay BlackBerry $940 million. This settlement resolves arbitration proceedings related to royalty payments from 2016 and 2017. The payment is more than the initial $814.9 million arbitration award and comes amidst other legal disputes involving Qualcomm and increased patent assertion activities by BlackBerry.

title: How former Apple CEO Sculley helped usher in the personal computing era
summary: Former Apple CEO John Sculley discusses his tenure at Apple, highlighting how he and Steve Jobs focused on making computers personal and accessible to non-technical users. He also reflects on Apple's challenges against industry standards and shares his current work in fintech and healthcare, applying technology to disrupt traditional industries.

title: Apple drops Twitter, Facebook, Flickr, Vimeo integration in iOS 11
summary: Apple has removed system-level integration with Twitter, Facebook, Flickr, and Vimeo from iOS 11, a change from previous iOS versions where users could sign in once and share credentials across third-party apps. This decision means third-party apps will no longer have access to stored social media account credentials. However, Apple may be offering a workaround with an upcoming WWDC session on Password AutoFill for Apps.

title: GM wards off stock split as investors demand higher profits
summary: General Motors shareholders rejected a stock split plan as investors push for higher profits amidst a challenging automotive industry. While Ford ousted its CEO due to similar pressures, GM, under CEO Mary Barra, has shown better recent profits and made significant investments in new automotive technologies. The article highlights the ongoing tension between company leadership navigating industry disruption and shareholders demanding predictable, profitable growth.

title: Alphabet, Google's Parent Company, Loudly Announces It Is a Company of Liberals, by Liberals and for Liberals
summary: At Alphabet Inc.'s shareholder meeting, the National Center for Public Policy Research's Free Enterprise Project (FEP) challenged Google executives on the company's perceived liberal bias and lack of diversity for conservative viewpoints. FEP Director Justin Danhof stated that Alphabet and Google appear to be companies "of, by and for liberals," with their executives repeatedly taking positions against President Trump and conservative interests. FEP also presented a shareholder resolution seeking greater transparency for Alphabet's charitable donations, highlighting controversial contributions to groups like the Center for American Progress and the Clinton Foundation.

title: Fortune 500 list has one Long Island company: Henry Schein
summary: Henry Schein Inc., a health care products distributor, is the sole Long Island representative on the 2017 Fortune 500 list, moving up to No. 243. The article notes the decreasing presence of Long Island companies on the list due to relocations and acquisitions, despite the strong revenue of some local entities like Northwell Health which is ineligible as a nonprofit. It also details the top 10 Fortune 500 companies and the most profitable ones for the year.

title: Smartflash v. Apple: A poster child of the current ills wrecking the U.S. patent system
summary: This article criticizes the U.S. patent system and Apple's actions in the Smartflash v. Apple lawsuit. It details how Smartflash, initially awarded $533 million, had its patents found ineligible by the Federal Circuit after a judicial panel change, despite evidence suggesting Apple's willful infringement and theft of technology. The piece also highlights the media's portrayal of Smartflash as a "patent troll" and argues for a deeper examination of the case's implications for innovators.

title: DuPont Fabros Deal Gives Digital Realty More Firepower in the War for Cloud Deals
summary: Digital Realty Trust's acquisition of DuPont Fabros Technology for $7.6 billion significantly expands its footprint in key North American data center markets, strengthening its ability to secure large deals with hyper-scale cloud platforms like Microsoft, Apple, and Salesforce. This merger also preempts competitors and makes Digital Realty the largest multi-tenant data center provider in North America. Despite this consolidation, intense competition within the hyper-scale data center market is expected to continue.

title: How Might Apple’s iPhone 8 Impact Foxconn?
summary: The article discusses how Hon Hai Precision Industry (Foxconn), a major Taiwanese manufacturer, is expected to benefit significantly from Apple's iPhone 8 super-cycle, despite Apple's dual-sourcing strategy. Foxconn, which earns over 50% of its revenue from assembling iPhones, is diversifying its business model by acquiring companies like Sharp and bidding for Toshiba's NAND flash business to reduce its dependence on Apple and increase its "dollar content" in server and storage products. Additionally, Foxconn is anticipated to be the sole assembler for the high-end iPhone 8 due to its expertise and capacity, while other models like the iPhone 7s and 7s Plus will be handled by partners such as Pegatron and Wistron.

title: Jury Hasn’t Decided on ETFs’ Role in Stock Market Rise
summary: This article examines the impact of ETF ownership on stock prices, concluding that despite substantial growth, ETF holdings are still too small to significantly influence overall market valuations. Data shows ETFs own only a small percentage of US equity market capitalization compared to other investment vehicles and household investors. Both quantitative analysis and Citigroup's research found no conclusive evidence linking ETF growth to stock price bubbles.

title: Newegg makes big global push
summary: Newegg announced plans to expand its online electronics retail operations to dozens of new countries across Asia Pacific, Europe, Latin America, and the Middle East, aiming to reach customers in 50 countries. This global push, operating in beta, includes country-specific storefronts, local sellers, and native-language customer support. The move comes as Newegg faces stiff competition, particularly from Amazon, and seeks growth opportunities abroad by offering services to international marketplace sellers.

title: PG&E’s Bolt of Energy
summary: This article profiles Geisha Williams, the first Latina CEO of a Fortune 500 company, PG&E Corp. It details her journey from Cuba as a child to leading California's largest utility, focusing on her efforts to embrace renewable energy, modernize the grid, and navigate the complex challenges facing the power industry. The piece highlights her role in transforming PG&E into a leader in clean energy and her strategic vision for its future as a grid administrator.

title: How to Short Technology Stocks with ETFs
summary: The technology sector has shown signs of losing steam due to overvaluation concerns and profit-taking, leading investors to consider short positions. This article explores several ETFs that offer inverse exposure to the tech sector for investors looking to capitalize on a bearish outlook. It highlights ETFs with varying inverse exposures, including those focusing on the broader tech sector and the semiconductor industry, noting their suitability for short-term traders.

title: Qorvo helps advance 5G efforts with RF filter innovations for smartphones
summary: Qorvo is making significant strides in accelerating 5G development, particularly with its RF filter technologies for smartphones. The company is addressing critical challenges posed by 5G, such as increasing RF front-end complexity, managing multiple frequency bands and antennas, and enabling Gigabit speeds in a small form factor. Qorvo's RF Fusion solutions and advanced BAW-SMR filter technology are key to achieving these goals, catering to both flagship and mid-tier devices.

title: Apple has issued a 'green bond' to help tackle climate change
summary: Apple has issued a $1 billion 'green bond' to finance clean energy and environmental projects, marking its second such bond issuance. This move comes after the U.S. withdrew from the Paris climate agreement, with Apple aiming to demonstrate continued business commitment to addressing climate change. The proceeds will fund renewable energy, energy efficiency, and material procurement, including efforts to green its supply chain and use recycled materials.

title: New Mobile Payment Option For PNC Bank's Visa® Commercial Cardholders
summary: PNC Bank announced a new mobile payment option for its Visa commercial cardholders, allowing them to use Apple Pay, Android Pay, and Samsung Pay for corporate purchases. This initiative aims to increase security, convenience, and ease of reconciliation for businesses, addressing the changing demographics of the U.S. labor force. PNC is among the first U.S. banks to offer mobile wallet payments for commercial cards, incorporating multiple security features like virtual account numbers and two-step authentication.

title: Behind the charts: Amazon’s incredible impact on the consumer discretionary sector
