summary: Amazon, often considered a tech stock, is actually the largest component of the consumer discretionary sector, making up about 15 percent of the sector and 1.8 percent of the S&P 500. Its 32 percent share price rise this year has significantly driven the sector's performance, contributing about a quarter of its nearly 11 percent rise. However, Amazon's success has also negatively impacted traditional retailers within the same sector.

title: Among the iPhone’s Biggest Transformations: Apple Itself
summary: The article discusses how the iPhone has not only revolutionized people's lives and industries but also profoundly transformed Apple Inc. itself over the past decade. With 1.3 billion units sold and over $800 billion in revenue, the iPhone propelled Apple into the business stratosphere, making it the world's most valuable publicly traded company and fostering an enormous services business. Former Apple executives recount the arduous process of realizing Steve Jobs's vision for the device.

title: Apple’s Obsession With Secrecy May Be A Self-Fulfilling Prophecy
summary: Apple's intense secrecy, enforced by a New Product Security team composed of ex-NSA and FBI personnel, is examined as a core part of its culture and marketing strategy. The article explores how this secrecy, while central to Apple's brand and product reveals, may also be contributing to the very problem of leaks and potentially impacting sales of existing products. Despite efforts to curb them, leaks are now integral to the hype cycle of new Apple products, yet also devalue information for journalists and readers.

title: Chipmaker AMD Makes a Big Bet on Brand-New Tech
summary: This article details AMD's comeback under CEO Lisa Su, focusing on the development of new chips like Ryzen, Epyc, and Vega. It highlights how Su's strategy, involving radical redesigns and a move towards supercomputer-like processors and gaming consoles, aims to challenge rivals Intel and Nvidia. The piece also covers the initial challenges with the "Zeppelin" chip, Su's background, and the company's renewed focus on graphics technology for AI and machine learning.

title: The 50 Smartest Companies in 2017, According to MIT Experts
summary: MIT Technology Review released its annual list of the world's 50 smartest companies for 2017, based on business savvy and creative use of emerging technology. Nvidia topped the list for its AI software and growing AI businesses, with a valuation of $90.9 billion. The list also includes major companies like Apple and Facebook, alongside newcomers such as Face++, Carbon, and Desktop Metal.

title: Micron DRAM Production Facility Closed Due to Contamination
summary: Micron's Fab 2 in Taiwan, responsible for 5.5% of global DRAM supply and specializing in LPDDR4 memory for mobile phones like the iPhone 8, closed due to contamination from a nitrogen gas dispenser malfunction. This incident reduced Inotera's production capability by 60,000 wafers, despite Micron officially downplaying it as a "minor accident" with "no impact" on business. The article highlights the potential significant consequences for Micron if major customers like Apple were to seek alternative suppliers due to perceived supply issues.

title: Gartner Says Worldwide Device Shipments Will Decline 0.3% in 2017
summary: Gartner forecasts worldwide shipments of PCs, tablets, and smartphones to decline by 0.3% in 2017 to over 2.3 billion units. Growth is expected to return in 2018 with a 1.6% increase. The report highlights a slowing decline in PC shipments due to Windows 10 adoption and strong growth in the smartphone market driven by premium devices like the Samsung S8 and the anticipated new iPhone.

title: Qualcomm accuses Apple, Intel of infringement
summary: Qualcomm is filing a legal challenge against Apple, accusing the company of infringing on six Qualcomm patents with iPhones and iPads that use Intel chips. Qualcomm will ask the U.S. International Trade Commission to ban imports of these infringing Apple devices and has also filed a lawsuit seeking monetary damages. This action is part of an escalating legal battle between the two tech giants concerning patent licensing and alleged anti-competitive practices.

title: Apple sets up China data center to meet new cyber-security rules
summary: Apple is establishing its first data center in China, partnering with Guizhou-Cloud Big Data Industry Co Ltd (GCBD) to comply with new cyber-security laws. This move is part of a planned $1 billion investment and aims to improve service speed and reliability while adhering to regulations requiring cloud services to be operated by Chinese companies. The company emphasized its strong data privacy and security protections, stating that no backdoors would be created into its systems.

title: Amazon’s Prime Day Proves to Be Biggest Shopping Day Ever
summary: Amazon's third annual Prime Day was its biggest shopping day ever, with sales surpassing Black Friday and Cyber Monday. The event, which included significant discounts on Echo devices, drove a substantial increase in new Prime members and sales, growing by more than 60% compared to the previous year. Amazon uses Prime Day to promote its membership and expand the reach of its Alexa platform, facing increasing competition from retailers like Walmart and Macy's.

title: Amazon Prime Day generates estimated $1 billion in sales
summary: Amazon's third annual Prime Day, a 30-hour sales event, generated an estimated $1 billion in revenue, making it the e-commerce giant's biggest sales day ever. Analysts from Cowen & Co. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. estimate this revenue, which was triple Amazon's average daily e-commerce revenue in 2016. The event saw "tens of millions" of Prime members making purchases, with the Echo Dot mini-speaker being the most popular item.

title: The No. 1 reason Apple has been so successful can be traced to Steve Jobs
summary: Apple's extraordinary success is primarily attributed to its history of innovation, a legacy heavily influenced by its co-founder Steve Jobs. The company consistently evolves by pairing new technology with innovative business models, like iTunes and the App Store, and by vigilantly observing and surpassing competitors. Jobs's willingness to "go thermonuclear war" on perceived rivals and Apple's readiness to discard old models and pivot to new products are hallmarks of its sustained growth.

title: Qualcomm CEO sees settlement with Apple
summary: Qualcomm CEO Steve Mollenkopf expressed confidence on Monday that the company's legal battle with Apple, centered on the price of technology and licensing, would ultimately be resolved out of court. Mollenkopf stated that Qualcomm has faced similar disputes in the past that were settled without litigation and expects a similar outcome this time. The dispute involves Qualcomm's allegations of patent infringement against Apple and Apple's claims of unfair royalties from Qualcomm.

title: New App Helps Blind People Know the World Around Them
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Microsoft has released a new, free app called "Seeing AI" for Apple's iPhone and iPad, designed to assist blind and visually impaired individuals. The app utilizes a camera and artificial intelligence to identify objects, people, documents, and even emotions, announcing them aloud to the user. Seeing AI also features capabilities like reading product bar codes, menu items, and soon, banknote values, significantly enhancing independence for its users.

title: Companies seize technology to help us sleep
summary: Companies are increasingly integrating technology into products like pillows and beds to help consumers improve their sleep, driven by growing interest in sleep quality and its health implications. While some products, like Sleep Number's Smart Bed and the Zeeq pillow, offer sophisticated monitoring, experts caution that many consumer devices may not yet accurately detect sleep stages. Despite current limitations, the market is expanding, with major players like Apple entering the field, and experts anticipate significant improvements in sleep technology within the next decade.

title: Amazon acquires Santa Barbara start-up Graphiq to try to bolster Alexa
summary: Amazon acquired Santa Barbara start-up Graphiq in May 2017 for an estimated $50 million to enhance its Alexa virtual assistant and other services. Graphiq, founded as FindTheBest, specialized in organizing and connecting data to simplify online research, having previously developed tools for news publishers and an Alexa app. The acquisition brings Graphiq's data analysis technology and its employees into Amazon, strengthening Amazon's competitive position against other major tech companies in delivering quick answers to consumer queries.

title: QID Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:QID
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the ProShares UltraShort QQQ (QID) ETF, including its current price, performance metrics, and key statistics like AUM and expense ratio. It explains that QID offers -2x exposure to the Nasdaq 100 Index, making it a more aggressive inverse fund primarily designed for daily trading. The article also includes analysis, news, ideas from traders, technical indicators, and frequently asked questions about the ETF.

title: Live Nation Productions and Sean 'Diddy' Combs' Can't Stop Won't Stop: A Bad Boy Story hits #1 on iTunes around the world!
summary: Live Nation Productions and Sean 'Diddy' Combs' documentary "Can't Stop Won't Stop: A Bad Boy Story" has reached #1 on the iTunes documentary charts in multiple countries including the US, Canada, UK, France, and Australia. The film explores the history of Bad Boy Entertainment and its founder Sean "Diddy" Combs, including the preparation for the label's 20th-anniversary celebration. Its success also led to significant increases in plays for several Bad Boy catalog songs on Apple Music.

title: MILN Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:MILN
summary: This article provides an overview of the Global X Millennial Consumer ETF (MILN), detailing its price performance, key statistics like AUM and expense ratio, and its investment strategy. MILN invests in companies linked to millennial spending habits, tracking the Indxx Millennials Thematic Index. The fund's profile includes its issuer, inception date, and asset allocation, alongside technical analysis and frequently asked questions for potential investors.

title: Tech group sides with Apple in Qualcomm dispute
summary: A group representing major technology companies, including Alphabet Inc.'s Google, Amazon.com Inc., Microsoft Corp., and Facebook Inc., has aligned with Apple Inc. against Qualcomm Inc. in their legal dispute. The group urged regulators to reject Qualcomm's request to ban iPhone imports, arguing it would disrupt supply and harm consumers. This move comes amid a broader legal battle where Apple objects to Qualcomm's patent licensing model, and Qualcomm has accused Apple of withholding payments and infringing patents.

title: Your Roomba May Be Mapping Your Home, Collecting Data That Could Be Shared (Published 2017)
summary: High-end Roomba models collect spatial data to map users' homes, raising privacy concerns about how this data could be shared with companies like Amazon, Apple, or Google. While iRobot stated they have no plans to sell data and Reuters issued a correction regarding previous reports, the potential for targeted advertising based on home layouts and contents remains a sensitive issue in smart home data privacy. Digital rights groups warn that such data could allow advertisers to deduce income levels and even identify brands used by homeowners.

title: Smart Vacuum Cleaners Making Map Of Your Home — And Wants to Sell It
summary: iRobot, the company behind the Roomba vacuum cleaner, is reportedly planning to sell the home mapping data collected by its devices to third-party companies like Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. While this raises significant privacy concerns, proponents suggest the data could enhance the functionality of other smart home devices. iRobot CEO Colin Angle states that this would only happen with users' consent and could lead to a more integrated smart home ecosystem.

title: JCPenney adds credit cards and loyalty rewards to Apple Pay
summary: JCPenney customers can now add the retailer's credit card to Apple Pay to make contactless payments at any of its stores nationwide and earn shopping points through JCPenney Rewards. The integration extends to both JCPenney's credit card and co-branded Mastercard, issued by Synchrony Bank. This move aims to offer a faster, more seamless checkout process and provide an additional incentive for customers to join JCPenney's credit card and loyalty programs.

title: Over-the-air software updates coming to GM cars and trucks
summary: General Motors announced plans to introduce a new electrical vehicle architecture and infotainment system capable of over-the-air (OTA) updates before 2020. This move will bring GM closer to competitors like Tesla, which has been utilizing OTA updates for years, and is expected to save automakers billions and alter their relationship with drivers and dealerships. GM also recently hired a chief data officer to strengthen data analytics for customer experience and business-to-business opportunities.

title: JPMorgan Chase goes all in on renewable energy
summary: JPMorgan Chase announced plans to source 100% of its global energy needs from renewables by 2020 through on-site installations and utility-scale projects. Additionally, the financial services giant committed to facilitating $200 billion in clean energy financing by 2025, marking the largest pledge of its kind by a global financial institution. This initiative includes installing solar on many of its retail and commercial buildings and providing various financial solutions for clean energy projects.

title: Real estate start-up Redfin surges over 44% in IPO: 'We are the Apple of real estate,' CEO says
summary: Online real estate broker Redfin surged over 44% in its IPO, raising $138.5 million. CEO Glenn Kelman compared Redfin to "the Apple of real estate" and highlighted its technology-powered approach, commission-free agent salaries, and focus on customer satisfaction. Despite a small market share and inventory shortages, Kelman is confident in Redfin's ability to change the real estate sales model.

title: Apple Accused of Bowing to Chinese Censors
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple Inc. has confirmed it is removing Virtual Private Network (VPN) applications from its China App Store to comply with new Chinese regulations that require a government license for VPN providers. Critics, including VPN companies like Express VPN and Golden Frog, accuse Apple of capitulating to internet censorship, highlighting that this is a significant move by China to control internet access. This action comes despite Apple's previous stance against government pressure regarding encryption, raising concerns about its role in aiding China's censorship efforts.

title: The Elaborate Ways Apple Hides Product Secrets from the Public
summary: Apple employs various tactics to conceal its product names, including registering trademarks in countries with non-searchable databases like Jamaica and Liechtenstein. These strategies aim to prevent leaks before official announcements, although "trademark ninja" Brian Conroy previously exploited loopholes in Jamaica to unearth product names like iPhone 7 and AirPods. Following these leaks, Jamaica's trademark office implemented rule changes making such searches significantly harder.

title: Industry Focus: Manufacturing
summary: This article discusses the growth spurt in Nevada's manufacturing industry, highlighting challenges and opportunities identified by industry leaders. Key issues include workforce shortages due to rapid expansion and competition from large companies like Tesla and Panasonic, as well as regulatory hurdles. Automation is presented as a significant factor in increasing efficiency and enabling companies to remain competitive in the US.

title: Dow at 22,000: All eyes on an index few investors actually put money in
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average reached 22,000, setting a new record, but index funds and ETFs tracking the Dow have seen significantly less investor interest compared to those tracking the S&P 500. Despite its prominence as a market indicator, many experts view the Dow as an outdated and less diversified investment option due to its price-weighting methodology and limited number of stocks. The article highlights the discrepancy between the Dow's status as a widely watched market snapshot and its minimal role in actual investment portfolios.

title: Microsoft’s new corporate vision: artificial intelligence is in and mobile is out
summary: Microsoft's annual report reveals a significant shift in its corporate vision, highlighting artificial intelligence as a core focus while diminishing the emphasis on "mobile-first." This change aligns with the company's recent investments in AI, including a 5,000-person engineering team and AI-focused product announcements, and its retreat from the smartphone market. The new vision emphasizes an "intelligent cloud and an intelligent edge" where AI drives insights and user experiences span across devices.

title: Tesla 3 official launch depends on suppliers
summary: Tesla's ambitious Model 3 launch timeline is heavily reliant on its suppliers' ability to meet high production demands, with CEO Elon Musk stating the company needs to produce 10,000 cars per week. The article highlights the critical role of strong supplier relationships for manufacturers with tight deadlines, citing issues faced by Apple due to supply chain delays. Despite past production struggles and what Musk calls "manufacturing hell," Tesla's proximity to suppliers in Fremont, California, is expected to help them achieve their Model 3 production goals.

title: GrubHub Buys Yelp's Eat24 for $288 Million
summary: GrubHub Inc. has acquired Yelp's Eat24 for $288 million. This acquisition marks another instance of GrubHub purchasing smaller competitors, even as larger industry players continue to pose a challenge. The deal was announced on August 3, 2017.

title: Busting the myths of successful CEOs
summary: A 10-year study, The CEO Genome Project, examined thousands of CEOs to debunk common myths surrounding successful leadership. The research revealed that qualities like charisma, sector-specific experience, ruthlessness, and top-tier education are often overrated, while humility, decisiveness, and learning from mistakes are key. The study also found that individuals from ordinary backgrounds can indeed reach the top executive levels.

title: Garmin beats profit estimates as outdoor device sales soar
summary: Garmin reported better-than-expected quarterly profits driven by robust demand for outdoor devices like pet trackers and sports watches, which compensated for declining sales in its automobile products business. The outdoor segment sales jumped 46.3 percent to a record $194.8 million, helping net income attributable to Garmin rise to $171 million, or 91 cents per share. The company also raised its annual revenue forecast to $3.04 billion from $3.02 billion.

title: Green Dot Provides Some Details About its Role in the Upcoming Apple P2P Service
summary: Green Dot Corp. has revealed more details about its involvement in Apple Inc.’s upcoming person-to-person payment service within the Messages app. Green Dot will act as the program manager for the virtual Apple Pay cash card and the merchant of record for funding transactions. The company anticipates generating interchange revenue from users spending with Apple Pay cash but does not expect a material impact on its financial performance this year, despite strong second-quarter results.

title: LA 500: Ronald D. Sugar
summary: Ronald D. Sugar, former Chairman and CEO of Northrop Grumman Corp., has remained highly active in business since his 2010 retirement. He serves on the boards of Chevron, Air Lease Corp., Amgen Inc., and Apple Inc., and acts as a senior adviser to several firms including Ares Management and Bain & Co. Sugar also remains involved with nonprofit organizations like Boys & Girls Clubs of America and the Los Angeles Philharmonic Association.

title: Two years in, Sundar Pichai facing his toughest moment as CEO
summary: Sundar Pichai, two years into his tenure as Google CEO, has overseen significant growth in AI and hardware, including the Pixel smartphone launch, and rapid Android expansion. Despite these successes, he is currently navigating significant internal controversies surrounding a sexism debate and a record fine from the European Commission. The article explores his achievements and challenges, particularly the recent internal cultural issues.

title: Blackout Hits Taiwan, Leaves Millions in Dark
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Taiwan experienced a significant power blackout affecting nearly 7 million households due to a human technical error at a state-owned gas supplier, which impacted a power plant. The incident led to the resignation offer of the Minister of Economics Affairs and highlighted the need for systemic reforms in the country's electricity supply. Despite the widespread outage, major technology manufacturers reported limited impact on their operations due to the swift restoration of power.

title: Apple Readies $1 Billion War Chest for Hollywood Programming
summary: Apple Inc. is preparing to spend approximately $1 billion on original video content over the next year, signaling its serious intent to compete in the Hollywood programming market. This substantial investment positions Apple as a considerable contender, despite being a late entrant to a sector already crowded with both new and traditional media players. The budget is roughly half of what HBO spent on content last year and comparable to Amazon's spending in 2013, highlighting Apple's aggressive entry strategy.

title: Equinix to install Bloom Energy fuel cells at 12 data centers
summary: Equinix has signed a 15-year Power Purchase Agreement with a subsidiary of Southern Company to install Bloom Energy fuel cells at 12 of its US data centers. This phased installation, beginning in late 2017 and finishing by 2019, will provide over 37MW of power and aims to significantly reduce carbon emissions and water usage. The project expands on a previous trial and will bring Equinix's total fuel cell capacity to over 40MW across 15 locations.

title: How Does Bitcoin's Market Cap Stack Up Next to Gold, the S&P 500, and U.S. Dollar?
summary: This article examines Bitcoin's market capitalization in comparison to gold, the S&P 500, and the U.S. dollar in August 2017. Despite a significant price surge, Bitcoin's market cap remains substantially smaller than these traditional assets, highlighting its relative youth and volatility. The author advises caution regarding Bitcoin investments, citing a lack of fundamentals for valuation and the potential for a market bubble.

title: SHY Stock Price | iShares 1-3 Year Treasury Bond ETF
summary: This article provides comprehensive information on the iShares 1-3 Year Treasury Bond ETF (SHY), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key performance metrics. It details the ETF's dividend yield, inception date, asset class, and top holdings in U.S. Treasury Notes. The article also includes news and analysis regarding the treasury market and investor sentiment.

title: 7 things to know about the Apple data center in Waukee
summary: Apple Inc. unveiled plans for a $1.375 billion data center in Waukee, Iowa, with CEO Tim Cook attending the announcement. The facility, slated to open in 2020, will be built on a 2,000-acre site, create 50 permanent jobs, and be powered by 100 percent renewable energy. Apple will receive over $213 million in state and local incentives, including tax credits and property tax abatements similar to those offered to other tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and Facebook for their Iowa data centers.

title: Apple puts 'faith' in Waukee with $100 million pledge
summary: Apple Inc. pledged up to $100 million to Waukee, Iowa, as part of a multi-decade partnership for two new data centers. In return, Waukee will provide $194 million in tax abatements and infrastructure improvements, including a 71% property tax abatement for 20 years. Apple's investment will support local quality-of-life amenities and civic projects, with the first beneficiary being a youth sports complex.

title: Apple's billion-dollar data center 'puts Iowa on world stage'
summary: Apple announced plans to build a $1.375 billion data center in Waukee, Iowa, following over $213 million in state and local incentives. While critics question the high cost per job created (50 full-time jobs are expected), officials argue the investment puts Iowa on the "world stage" and strengthens its tech ecosystem, with Apple committing to $100 million for public improvements and powering the facility with 100% renewable energy. The deal is touted as a significant step for Iowa's economic development, despite the state facing a budget shortfall.

title: Apple CEO talks Iowa, RAGBRAI and the Iowa State Fair
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook made an unannounced appearance at Iowa's only Apple Store in West Des Moines after announcing a new data center in Waukee. He discussed the company's investment in Iowa, the role of renewable energy, and his past experiences with RAGBRAI and the Iowa State Fair during an interview with The Des Moines Register. Cook emphasized Apple's commitment to expansion in the state, driven by the growing need for data centers to support the App Store.

title: Stock market closes little changed as Hurricane Harvey hits energy sector
summary: U.S. stock markets ended mixed on Monday as investors assessed the impact of Hurricane Harvey on the energy sector. The Dow Jones Industrial Average saw a slight decline, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted minor gains. Insurance companies like Travelers Cos. Inc. were among the biggest losers, reflecting concerns over hurricane-related damages.

title: Stock market closes little changed as Hurricane Harvey hits energy sector
summary: U.S. stock markets ended mixed on Monday as investors assessed the impact of Hurricane Harvey on the energy sector. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped slightly, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted minor gains. Travelers Cos. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. saw losses, while Home Depot Inc. and Apple Inc. were among the gainers.

title: Novonix secures Canadian funding for its lithium battery technology
summary: Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) has secured C$500,000 in funding from the Government of Canada to advance its innovative battery testing technologies. The company, a developer and supplier for the lithium-ion battery industry, will use the funds for research and development of battery chargers and cell testing, aiming to expand its international marketing efforts. This investment is timely as Novonix expects growing demand for energy-efficient batteries, with its expertise already sought by major companies like Apple and Tesla Motors.

title: Apple's Real Reason for Finally Joining the Net Neutrality Fight
summary: Apple has historically been silent on net neutrality, but finally filed a comment in support of strong protections on the last day for public input to the FCC. This shift is likely driven by Apple's growing investment in streaming video and its desire to prevent internet service providers from throttling its content in favor of their own. While its late entry may not sway the FCC, it signifies the increasing importance of net neutrality to Apple's future business strategy.

title: About the Author – Ndubuisi Ekekwe, PhD
summary: This article provides a biography of Prof. Ndubuisi Ekekwe, highlighting his extensive academic and professional achievements. It details his engineering background, significant inventions, and roles in various institutions and companies, including his work with the iPhone accelerometer and founding Fasmicro Group and Zenvus. The bio also notes his contributions as an author, TED Fellow, and World Economic Forum Young Global Leader.

title: Disassembling Kodak
summary: This article analyzes Kodak's employment history, comparing its growth trajectory to modern tech giants like Apple, Amazon, and Google. It highlights how long it took Kodak to reach certain employment milestones versus the much faster growth experienced by today's leading tech companies, challenging the narrative that Kodak was a superior "innovation company" compared to Apple.

title: 3 Reasons Apple Inc. Stock Could Rise After Its Big iPhone Launch
summary: This article explores three reasons why Apple Inc. stock could rise following its upcoming iPhone launch. These reasons include the potential for iPhone sales to exceed Apple's already high expectations, the growth of Apple's other business segments like Services and "Other Products," and positive investor sentiment regarding the 2018 iPhone models. The article suggests that strong performance in these areas could drive up earnings expectations and stock price.

title: Apple’s iPhone Event: What You Need to Know
summary: Apple Inc. is holding its most important product event in years, unveiling new iPhones, Apple Watch, and Apple TV at its new headquarters. This event is significant because it marks the first time Apple will unveil three iPhones at its fall showcase, including a high-end iPhone X model. It's also the inaugural event at the new $5 billion campus, taking place at the Steve Jobs Theater.

title: Investment fail: Why Apple’s little-known third co-founder sold his 10% stake for $800
summary: Ronald Wayne, Apple's third co-founder, sold his 10% stake in the company for $800 after just 12 days, fearing personal financial liability for the company's debts. This decision cost him an estimated $80 billion today, but Wayne maintains he has no regrets about leaving, believing he would not have thrived at Apple. He also sold the original Apple contract years later for $500, which later auctioned for $1.59 million.

title: Apple Is Really Good at Making You Think $1,000 Isn’t That Much for an iPhone
summary: The article explains Apple's strategy behind pricing the iPhone X at $1,000, arguing that the company skillfully mitigates the perceived cost through strategic naming, design, installment plans, and by making the iPhone 8 appear as a bargain. It highlights the unique aspects of the smartphone market where consumers expect annual upgrades and accept price increases for perceived innovation. The piece concludes that Apple's loyal customer base and lack of iOS competition enable premium pricing, anticipating that the $1,000 price point for smartphones may soon become a new norm.

title: FURBY CONNECT World by Hasbro, Inc.
summary: The "FURBY CONNECT World" app, developed by Hasbro, Inc., is a free adventure and family game for iPhone and iPad released on May 27, 2016. It allows users to connect to a virtual world to hatch, care for, and collect virtual Furblings, and offers enhanced interactive features when paired with a separately sold Furby Connect toy. The app includes optional in-app purchases ranging from $1.99 to $24.99 and requires an internet connection, with compatibility for iOS 9.0 and above on specific Apple devices.

title: Interbrand Releases 2017 Best Global Brands Report: Apple and Google Hold the Top Two Spots, while Ferrari, Netflix and Salesforce.com Enter the List
summary: Interbrand's 2017 Best Global Brands report names Apple and Google as the top two most valuable brands for the fifth consecutive year, with Microsoft coming in third. The report, themed "Growth in a Changing World," highlights technology as the dominant sector and includes new entrants Ferrari, Netflix, and Salesforce.com. The combined value of the Top 100 brands increased by 4.2% from 2016, reaching USD $1,871,730m.

title: Carnival Corporation Debuts PlayOcean Mobile and Interactive Games
summary: Carnival Corporation has launched PlayOcean, a portfolio of original mobile games and interactive experiences, making it the first travel company to create mobile games for the vacation market. The PlayOcean Everywhere app offers free-to-play games for fun, while the PlayOcean Casino app allows real-money wagering onboard select Princess Cruises Medallion Class ships. This initiative aims to engage guests with gaming options accessible anytime, anywhere, enhancing the cruise experience through both mobile and location-based interactive games.

title: Western Digital to seek injunction to block Toshiba’s chip unit sale
summary: Western Digital announced its intention to seek an injunction to prevent the sale of Toshiba's semiconductor business to a consortium led by Bain Capital LP and SK Hynix. This move escalates the dispute between Western Digital and Toshiba, with Western Digital arguing that any deal requires its consent and expressing concerns about SK Hynix's involvement. Toshiba is under pressure to finalize a deal quickly to cover liabilities from its bankrupt US nuclear unit.

title: Roku just priced its IPO at the top of its range, fetching a $1.3 billion market cap before trading on Thursday
summary: Roku priced its initial public offering at $14 per share, the high end of its expected range, valuing the company at $1.3 billion. The streaming video player maker will debut on Nasdaq under the symbol "ROKU" and plans to raise $126 million in funding. Despite rapid revenue growth from its devices, Roku still faces challenges in the competitive hardware business and recorded a loss of $24.2 million in the first half of 2017.

title: Amazon says Google has pulled YouTube from Echo Show device
summary: Amazon announced that Google has inexplicably pulled YouTube from its Echo Show devices, causing a rare public dispute between the two tech giants. Google countered that Amazon's implementation of YouTube violated its terms of service, leading to a "broken user experience." This incident affects the Echo Show's video content and highlights ongoing competitive tensions in Silicon Valley.

title: Manulife’s new CEO Roy Gori sets sights on shaking up insurance industry
summary: Roy Gori, Manulife's new CEO, aims to transform the 130-year-old insurance giant into a technology-driven company, prioritizing digital disruption, capital efficiency, and customer obsession. Having spent most of his career in Asia with Citigroup, Gori plans to shake up the industry by focusing on growth in wealth and asset management and Asian markets, while tackling legacy businesses and fostering an insurgent mentality within Manulife. His strategic vision includes leveraging technology for customer experience, cutting costs, and building a new leadership team, despite the challenges of transforming a large, established institution.

title: Apple’s ‘Face ID’ Made in Taiwan｜Industry｜2017-10-02｜web only
summary: This article reveals that two Taiwanese companies, Himax and TSMC, are key suppliers for Apple's iPhone X Face ID technology. Himax provides tiny optical lenses for 3D facial recognition, while TSMC manufactures the near-infrared imaging sensor. The article also discusses Qualcomm's strategic efforts with Himax and TSMC to develop a competing 3D depth-sensing camera system for Android phones, indicating significant market opportunity for these Taiwanese firms.

title: Vuforia’s new ARKit app, Chalk, helps you help others using augmented reality
summary: Vuforia's new ARKit app, Chalk, enables users to provide remote assistance by making augmented reality "chalk marks" over a live video feed. This practical application of AR allows users to visually guide others through tasks, such as operating a TV remote, bridging distances and reducing confusion. Chalk is free, available on iOS 11, and represents a shift towards problem-solving AR applications beyond entertainment.

title: Semiconductor Industry To Lose Titan As Morris Chang Sets TSMC Retirement Date
summary: Morris Chang, the influential chairman of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), has announced his retirement effective June 2018. Chang, 86, is recognized as a titan in the chip industry, having founded TSMC and built it into a leader in specialized semiconductor contract manufacturing for companies like Apple, Nvidia, and Qualcomm. He was previously named Forbes Asia's Businessman of the Year in 2012 and selected as one of the world's 100 greatest living business minds by Forbes.

title: Battle for Toshiba: A tale of threats, fury and culture clashes
summary: This article details the intense eight-month battle for Toshiba Corp's semiconductor unit, highlighting the aggressive tactics of Western Digital CEO Steve Milligan and the cultural clashes that ensued. Ultimately, Bain Capital, backed by Apple, secured the US$18 billion deal, leaving Western Digital in a precarious position with a damaged relationship with Toshiba and ongoing legal challenges. The acquisition was crucial for Toshiba to cover massive losses from its nuclear reactor business.

title: PTC Announces a Major New Release to Vuforia Augmented Reality Platform
summary: PTC has announced Vuforia 7, a major new release to its Vuforia Augmented Reality Platform, introducing significant advancements like ground plane detection, next-generation object recognition with Model Targets, and broad support for Apple’s ARKit and Google’s ARCore via Vuforia Fusion. The update also launches Vuforia Chalk, an innovative app for collaborative visual communication directly on real-world objects. These enhancements aim to expand AR capabilities across more device types and environments, making AR development more accessible and powerful for developers.

title: Paul S. Otellini, former Intel CEO, dies at 66
summary: Paul S. Otellini, former CEO of Intel Corp., died at 66. He led the company from 2005 to 2013, overseeing revenue growth and important shifts like the adoption of Intel chips by Apple. Otellini was known for his customer-first approach and presided over significant product introductions and financial successes.

title: Amazon said to face EU order to pay millions in back taxes to Luxembourg
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is expected to face an order from the European Union to pay millions of euros in back taxes to Luxembourg, becoming the latest US giant targeted by the bloc's state-aid rules. This decision follows similar actions against Apple and is part of EU Competition Commissioner Margrethe Vestager's crackdown on tax loopholes, aiming to ensure fair competition. The EU has been investigating various individual tax deals and broader tax avoidance schemes across member states since 2013.

title: Apple and Qualcomm’s Billion-Dollar War Over an $18 Part
summary: The article details the ongoing financial dispute between Apple and Qualcomm, specifically highlighting Apple's dissatisfaction with the $2 billion annually it pays in patent fees to Qualcomm. This conflict is exemplified by the importance of an $18 part in the iPhone, which is at the heart of their billion-dollar legal battle. The article also touches upon the launch of new iPhone models, including the iPhone X, and their expected impact on the market.

title: Wafer fabs are notoriously hard to set up and run: Cadence Design Systems CEO Lip-bu Tan
summary: Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Cadence Design Systems, discusses the challenges of setting up and running wafer fabs in India, highlighting the significant investments, technical expertise, and developed ecosystem required. He notes India's strength in IP, design services, and R&D within the semiconductor industry, with Cadence having a significant R&D presence there for 30 years. Tan also emphasizes the need for accelerated implementation of government initiatives to boost the local electronic system design and manufacturing (ESDM) sector and addresses the crucial aspect of skilling the workforce with "design aware" manpower for the semiconductor industry.

title: Bain hoping to settle with Western Digital on Toshiba deal
summary: Bain Capital, leading a consortium to acquire Toshiba's memory chip business for 2 trillion yen ($18 billion), announced its intention to seek a speedy settlement with Western Digital, which is opposing the sale through litigation. Despite the legal dispute, Bain Capital's Japan head, Yuji Sugimoto, stated the deal would proceed and emphasized Western Digital's continued importance as a partner. Toshiba, facing significant losses from its nuclear business, urgently needs the sale to ensure its survival.

title: Apple clean energy project fined for environmental violations
summary: Apple Computer's data center in Maiden, North Carolina, has been fined over $40,000 by the N.C. Division of Environmental Quality for multiple violations of the state’s Solid Waste Management Act, related to the handling of hazardous waste from its custom-built fuel cells. The violations include improper waste determination for spent fuel filter materials and failure to maintain proper records and manifests. The article also scrutinizes Apple's renewable energy claims, suggesting they are misleading as the facility primarily draws power from Duke Energy, with the generated renewable energy sold back to the grid.

title: Apple names former Honeywell exec as new general counsel
summary: This article is a cookie consent pop-up related to Yahoo's use of cookies and personal data on their sites and apps. It describes the types of data collected, how it's used for analytics and personalized advertising, and provides options for users to manage their privacy settings.

title: Motorola Z2 Force review: MotoMod accessories make this the Swiss Army Knife of smartphones
summary: The Motorola Z2 Force boasts accessories and excellent build quality, making it a strong contender in the Android market. While sharing a similar design with the Z2 Play, the Z2 Force prioritizes power and durability, though it comes with a premium price tag of £719. Its standout feature is compatibility with Moto Mods, clip-on accessories that enhance battery or camera performance, offering a unique level of customization despite the added cost.

title: A Unique Apple ETF Play in Emerging Markets
summary: This article highlights the iShares MSCI Taiwan ETF (NYSEArca: EWT) as a unique way to invest in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) through its supply chain in emerging markets. Despite not directly holding Apple stock, EWT's significant allocation to Apple suppliers like Taiwan Semiconductor and Hon Hai Precision Industry makes it a credible play on the tech giant. The ETF's strong performance, driven by its technology sector weighting, offers an indirect route to Apple exposure with a seemingly low correlation to Apple's stock itself.

title: Now Open: Lemonade Shares API Platform With ‘The World’
summary: Lemonade announced the launch of a public API allowing developers to integrate insurance offerings into their own apps and websites. This initiative aims to make insurance more accessible and hassle-free, allowing partners to offer instant coverage for renters, condo, and homeowners insurance in Lemonade's active markets. The company likens this move to "liberating technology" adopted by companies like Amazon and Apple, enabling a broader ecosystem for insurance services.

title: Taiwan fines Qualcomm $774 million for antitrust violations
summary: Taiwan has fined Qualcomm Inc $774 million for antitrust violations related to its chip technology, marking the latest regulatory setback for the company. The Taiwan Fair Trade Commission accused Qualcomm of monopolizing the chip market for modem technologies and refusing to license its technology. Qualcomm disputes the decision and plans to challenge it in court.

title: What's in a name? Handbag icon Coach to become Tapestry
summary: Handbag brand Coach Inc. announced its corporate name change to Tapestry Inc., effective end of October, aiming to interweave brands like Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman. The announcement met with negative market reactions and mixed public opinion, prompting a discussion on the challenges and successes of corporate rebranding, especially in global markets like China, using examples like Google/Alphabet and Apple. The article explores how companies tackle rebranding and naming strategies across different cultures and languages.

title: Sonos One review: Sweet sound mixed with Alexa voice and smart home controls
summary: The Sonos One is reviewed as a smart speaker offering excellent sound quality and integrated Alexa voice controls without needing an Amazon Echo. While it functions well as a smart home assistant and music player for numerous services, it lacks some Alexa features like Drop In and voice calling due to not being an Amazon device. The speaker is also slated to receive Google Assistant integration in 2018, expanding its voice assistant compatibility.

title: Patent lawsuit turns up heat on Apple
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has filed a patent lawsuit against Apple Inc. in China, seeking to halt the manufacturing and sale of iPhones, which analysts say adds pressure on Apple amidst stiff competition from local rivals. The lawsuit escalates a prolonged legal dispute over Qualcomm's technology licensing fees, with Apple accusing the chipmaker of overcharging. This development comes as Apple prepares to launch its iPhone X and faces competition from Huawei's new Mate 10.

title: Qualcomm CEO still expects NXP deal to close later this year
summary: Qualcomm CEO Steve Mollenkopf reaffirmed his expectation that the company's $39 billion acquisition of NXP Semiconductors NV will close by the end of the year. He stated that the deal is still on track, dismissing speculation about a potential price increase for NXP. This acquisition is anticipated to establish Qualcomm as a significant force in the autonomous driving sector.

title: Honeywell Names Anne T. Madden General Counsel
summary: Honeywell International Inc. has named Anne T. Madden ’92 as its new senior vice president and general counsel. Madden, who previously served as Vice President of Corporate Development and Global Head of M&A since 2002, succeeds Kate Adams in this role. She has a strong track record of facilitating transactions and supporting Honeywell's growth through numerous acquisitions and divestitures.

title: Cramer Remix: Why Oct. 20, 1987, was worse than Black Monday
summary: Jim Cramer reflects on the 30th anniversary of the 1987 market crash, arguing that "Terrible Tuesday" (October 20, 1987) was more frightening than Black Monday due to widespread investor confusion and market uncertainty. He also discusses his sell-off strategy, an interview with Skyworks Solutions CEO Liam Griffin about mobile network congestion, and Winnebago Industries CEO Michael Happe on the growth of the RV market. The article concludes with Cramer's lightning round picks, including USG Corporation and Enterprise Products Partners.

title: Drop in AT&T profit
summary: AT&T Inc.'s quarterly results missed Wall Street estimates due to a loss of video subscribers and fewer device upgrades ahead of the iPhone X launch. The company is actively pursuing a strategy of bundling wireless and video services, bolstered by its pending acquisition of Time Warner Inc. AT&T reported a loss of 89,000 US video subscribers and 97,000 phone subscribers, partly attributed to intense competition and consumers delaying upgrades for the new iPhone.

title: Payne: First Tesla Michigan gallery opens at Somerset mall
summary: Tesla Inc. has opened its first stand-alone gallery space in Michigan at the Somerset Collection mall in Troy. This gallery, similar to an Apple store and located next to one, allows customers to learn about Tesla vehicles and energy products but not purchase cars directly due to Michigan's direct-to-consumer sales ban. The opening comes as Tesla battles state laws restricting its direct sales model, showcasing Model S and Model X vehicles, and highlighting its maintenance approach for electric vehicles.

title: VanEck Long/Flat Trend ETF Historical Data
summary: This article provides historical data for the VanEck Long/Flat Trend ETF (LFEQ) in USD. It includes closing prices, open, high, low, change, and percentage change for various dates, indicating its performance and trading ranges. The ETF's 52-week range and recent daily trading activity are also highlighted.

title: A new ETF tries turning market-cap weighting on its head
summary: A new exchange-traded fund, the Reverse Cap Weighted US Large Cap ETF, is challenging traditional market-cap weighting by inversing the proportions of its S&P 500 components. This means that while Apple is the largest stock in the benchmark index, it will be the smallest component in this new fund, launching on Wednesday. The fund aims to flip the time-tested approach of passive investments that typically rank stocks by size.

title: Corning says it is the secret buyer of Hynix's shuttered Eugene factory
summary: Corning announced it is the mystery buyer of the former Hynix computer chip factory in Eugene, which it purchased in September. While the industrial manufacturer has no immediate plans for the site, its acquisition by an established company like Corning increases the likelihood of the factory eventually reopening. The 300-acre property was sold for $13.4 million and could be eligible for significant property tax breaks as it sits inside an enterprise zone.

title: Trump says Broadcom will move headquarters from Singapore to US
summary: President Donald Trump announced that semiconductor company Broadcom Ltd. will move its headquarters from Singapore to the United States. Broadcom CEO Tan Hock E. stated this move would bring $20 billion in revenue to the US. This decision comes as Broadcom seeks regulatory approval for its acquisition of Brocade Communications Systems Inc. and amidst its strategy of growth through acquisitions.

title: Apple has $900 billion valuation in sight as earnings allay iPhone fears
summary: Apple Inc. is nearing a $900 billion valuation after its fiscal fourth-quarter results surpassed analyst expectations, driven by strong net profit. Executives expressed confidence in significant sales of the $1,000 iPhone X, forecasting record revenue for the current holiday quarter and squashing previous production fears. CEO Tim Cook stated it would be the "best holiday season yet," indicating strong optimism for Apple's future performance.

title: From ‘skinny kid’ in Malaysia to presidential welcome in Oval Office
summary: Hock E. Tan, the Malaysian-born CEO of semiconductor giant Broadcom, received a presidential welcome from Donald Trump as the company announced its headquarters would move from Singapore back to the United States. Tan, an MIT scholarship recipient, expressed gratitude for the American education system and saw the move as giving back to the country. Broadcom's return to the US follows its acquisition of Broadcom Corp for US$37 billion in 2015.

title: Chipmaker Broadcom plans blockbuster bid for Qualcomm
summary: Broadcom is planning a blockbuster bid for Qualcomm, potentially creating a $200-billion technology company. The proposed acquisition would consolidate two major wireless communication chipmakers and significantly impact competitors like Intel. Analysts suggest a bid in the range of $70 to $80 per share, valuing Qualcomm at over $100 billion.

title: Commerce Secretary’s Offshore Ties to Putin ‘Cronies’ (Published 2017)
summary: Commerce Secretary Wilbur L. Ross Jr. maintained investments in a shipping firm with significant business ties to a Russian oligarch and President Vladimir V. Putin's son-in-law even after taking office. These ties were revealed through leaked documents from the offshore law firm Appleby, highlighting how Ross stood to profit from a company doing business with Russia's energy sector despite Western sanctions. His investments were held through a chain of companies in the Cayman Islands.

title: Broadcom Offers Qualcomm $130 Billion In Takeover Bid
summary: Broadcom has made an unsolicited takeover bid for Qualcomm, offering $130 billion in cash and stock ($70 per share). Qualcomm's board stated they would assess the offer, but reports suggest they are preparing to reject it as undervalued. The deal, if successful, would create the third-largest chipmaker globally and could simplify regulatory approvals due to Broadcom's announced relocation of its headquarters to the U.S.

title: Israeli talent ‘very important’ for Cadence business, CEO says
summary: Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Cadence Design Systems, emphasized the critical role of Israeli talent and technology for the company's global business. Cadence, a developer of software for chipmakers, actively seeks Israeli acquisitions and employs around 250 people in its Israeli R&D center, citing the country's skilled workforce and vibrant semiconductor industry. Tan also highlighted the importance of a humble, innovative, and inclusive company culture for success, drawing parallels to teamwork in basketball.

title: Place matters: Janesville: An American Story wins 2017 Business Book of the Year Award
summary: Amy Goldstein's "Janesville: An American Story" won the 2017 Financial Times and McKinsey Business Book of the Year Award for its examination of economic disruption in a small American town. The Bracken Bower Prize went to Mehran Gul's "The New Geography of Innovation," highlighting the rise of global start-up ecosystems. The awards recognize books that offer compelling insights into modern business issues.

title: Apple's iPhone X Parts Cost $115 More Than iPhone 8, IHS Says
summary: According to IHS Markit Inc., the components for Apple's new iPhone X are $115 more expensive than those of the iPhone 8, primarily due to its new OLED screen and facial recognition scanner. The bill of materials for the iPhone X 64GB model is $370.25, compared to $255.16 for the iPhone 8 base model. These figures exclude other costs like R&D, manufacturing, and software.

title: Raja Koduri Joins Intel as Chief Architect to Drive Unified Vision Across Cores and Visual Computing
summary: Intel announced the appointment of Raja Koduri as chief architect, senior vice president of the newly formed Core and Visual Computing Group, and general manager of a new initiative for edge computing solutions. Koduri, formerly of AMD and Apple, will lead the unification and expansion of Intel's differentiated IP across various computing segments, including graphics, media, imaging, and machine intelligence. His role aims to strengthen Intel's position in integrated graphics and drive high-end discrete graphics solutions.

title: How Corning’s Crash Project For Steve Jobs Helped Define The iPhone
summary: This article details how Corning developed Gorilla Glass for the iPhone under a tight deadline from Steve Jobs, a critical decision that replaced the original plastic screen. The introduction of Gorilla Glass significantly enhanced the iPhone's user experience and durability, influencing the entire smartphone industry to adopt glass screens. Apple has since invested $200 million in Corning, recognizing its crucial role in the iPhone's success.

title: Qualcomm unequivocally rejects Broadcom's advances
summary: Qualcomm's Board of Directors has unanimously rejected Broadcom's unsolicited US$130 billion acquisition proposal, citing significant undervaluation and regulatory uncertainty. Qualcomm executives expressed confidence in the company's standalone growth prospects and leadership position in mobile technology and emerging segments like 5G and IoT. The decision dismisses speculation that a takeover could resolve Qualcomm's lawsuit with Apple and is expected to be well-received by the Android ecosystem.

title: Qualcomm draws up plans to rebuff Broadcom's $103 billion offer: sources
summary: Qualcomm is preparing to reject Broadcom's $103 billion acquisition offer as early as this week, with its board deeming the bid insufficient and undervaluing the company. Broadcom is reportedly ready to launch a takeover battle by submitting a slate of directors by Qualcomm's nomination deadline and is also considering raising its bid.
authors: Greg Roumeliotis and Liana B. Baker

title: Why Mobile Payments Are Still So Hard for J.P. Morgan
summary: JP Morgan has struggled to gain traction in the mobile payments market despite spending $100 million on its latest attempt. This market is currently dominated by technology firms and non-banks, making it challenging for traditional banks like JP Morgan to compete effectively. The article highlights that many U.S. companies, including startups and Apple Inc., have faced similar difficulties in this fast-growing sector.

title: Reprints Desk Recognized as "One of the Companies that Matter Most" in the Digital Content Industry
summary: Reprints Desk, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Research Solutions, Inc., has been recognized by EContent Magazine for the seventh consecutive year as a top digital content industry leader. The company was named to the "100 Companies that Matter Most in the Digital Content Industry" list for its Article Galaxy platform, which provides on-demand access to scientific documents. This recognition highlights Reprints Desk's commitment to advancing technologies for scientific research and its significant impact on digital content creation and delivery.

title: Uncanny Green Line on iPhone X OLED Display
summary: The iPhone X is reportedly experiencing a hardware issue where a vertical green line appears on its OLED display, leading to speculation that it might be an electrical flaw or related to the RGB sub-pixel pattern. This issue is reminiscent of a similar problem faced by the Samsung Galaxy S7 and highlights potential quality control challenges for OLED technology as its popularity grows. Apple has not yet officially responded, though some users have had their phones replaced.

title: An On-device Deep Neural Network for Face Detection
summary: This article details Apple's development of an on-device deep neural network for face detection, addressing challenges like user privacy and limited mobile resources. It outlines the transition from traditional computer vision methods to deep learning, including a "teacher-student" training approach and extensive optimization for performance and power efficiency within the Vision framework. The goal was to provide a performant and easy-to-use face detection API for developers.

title: Deere & Co.'s $32.6 million facility adds to list of central Iowa high-tech gets
summary: Deere & Co. plans to build a $32.6 million intelligent solutions facility in Urbandale, Iowa, adding to the region's growing list of high-tech investments. This facility will house operations for its intelligent solutions group, focusing on agricultural technology and creating 31 new jobs while retaining 20 existing positions. The project received state tax credits and local incentives, highlighting Iowa's appeal to IT companies and contributing to Urbandale's economic development.

title: Microsoft Windows: Facts on the evolution of the popular OS
summary: This article details the evolution of Microsoft Windows from its announcement in 1983 and first release in 1985 to Windows 10. It highlights key features and changes introduced in different versions, including the shift from MS-DOS, the introduction of GUI, built-in internet support, and the integration of digital assistants. The article also mentions Microsoft's announcement that Windows 10 would be the last version, receiving regular updates rather than large-scale revisions.

title: Why Apple’s HomePod Is Three Years Behind Amazon’s Echo
summary: Apple's HomePod speaker project was significantly delayed and underwent multiple revisions after Apple engineers discovered Amazon's Echo in 2014. Despite initially dismissing the Echo's sound quality, Apple struggled internally to define the HomePod's place in its ecosystem while Amazon gained a substantial lead with its voice-activated assistant, Alexa. The article suggests that the HomePod's late arrival and limited functionality compared to the Echo are due to Apple's internal indecision and project cancellations.

title: Disruptive Stocks, by Malcolm Berko
summary: Malcolm Berko advises a reader on their Universal Display stock, which has significantly appreciated. He introduces "disruptive technology" and recommends selling some Universal Display (OLED) shares to invest in Accelerate Diagnostics (AXDX), highlighting AXDX's potential for revolutionizing medical diagnostics and significant insider ownership.

title: The Strategic Decisions That Caused Nokia’s Failure
summary: This article examines the strategic decisions that led to Nokia's downfall in the mobile phone market, attributing its failure to internal issues rather than external competition. It highlights how initial success fostered conservatism, a loss of agility due to poorly implemented reorganizations, and a failure to adapt to the shift from product-based to platform- and application-centric competition. The author, Yves L. Doz, uses Nokia's story as a cautionary tale for successful companies regarding the dangers of complacency and the importance of continuous innovation.

title: ‘Pokémon Go’ Studio Niantic Completes $200M Funding Round
summary: Niantic, the developer of Pokémon Go and an upcoming Harry Potter AR game, has successfully closed a $200 million Series B investment round. The funding was led by Spark Capital with participation from several other investors including NetEase, Inc. The article also discusses the distinction between "true" AR and the AR experiences currently offered by games like Pokémon Go, with a nod to the potential of Apple's ARkit and Google's ARCore.

title: Pottery Barn launches AR app for iOS
summary: Pottery Barn has launched the iOS version of its augmented reality app, 3D Room View, allowing users to virtually place life-sized products in their rooms. This expands the app's reach significantly compared to its Android predecessor, which required Google's Tango technology. The app aims to enhance customer engagement by offering shopping capabilities and connecting users with design consultants.

title: Global smartphone sales return to growth; Apple up 5.7% year-on-year in Q3 – Gartner
summary: Global smartphone sales returned to growth in Q3 2017 sooner than expected, increasing by 3% year-on-year to 383 million units. Apple's sales grew 5.7% in Q3, constrained by users awaiting the iPhone X launch, and its market share slightly increased to 11.9%. Samsung maintained its top position with 22.3% market share.

title: Why Smart Speakers are Ready for Primetime this Holiday Season...
summary: Smart speakers are poised to go mainstream this holiday season, having already surpassed a critical adoption threshold of 11% penetration in U.S. connected households and likely to hit 15% by early next year. This adoption is a key indicator for the emergence of the 'smart home' as smart speaker households are significantly more likely to own other smart devices. The article suggests that smart speakers are a gateway device for a broader smart home trend that will reshape consumer technology over the next few years.

title: The language of choice
summary: Vodafone Group CFO Nick Read discusses how the telecoms giant has adapted its business model to navigate industry disruptions like messaging apps and free voice services. The company has shifted its focus from metered services to unlimited plans and invested heavily in high-speed networks and new technologies like the Internet of Things, emphasizing innovation and strategic investments to maintain market relevance and growth. Finance plays a crucial role in shaping the business model, owning data standards, and driving efficiency through initiatives like AI.

title: Israel: An Innovation Powerhouse with Exponential Growth Potential
summary: This article highlights Israel's status as a global innovation powerhouse, leading in technological advancements and entrepreneurship despite its small economy. It discusses how Israel attracts significant investor interest, boasts a high concentration of tech R&D centers, and has seen substantial M&A activity even amidst geopolitical concerns. The piece also addresses Israel's underrepresentation in global equity indices due to its reclassification as a developed market, proposing the ARK Israeli Innovation Index to capture its growth potential.

title: Visa Fires Top Executive Who Handled PayPal, Apple Partnerships
summary: Visa Inc. fired Jim McCarthy, a high-profile executive who handled innovation and strategic partnerships with companies like PayPal Holdings Inc. and Apple Inc. The termination was due to unspecified behavior that violated company policy, announced in a memo to staff by Visa President Ryan McInerney. McCarthy had been with Visa for approximately 18 years and had a prominent presence in Silicon Valley.

title: GE Struggles To Show It Still Has Magic Touch
summary: General Electric, a historically influential American corporation, is facing significant struggles, with its stock plummeting over 40% in the last year. New CEO John Flannery announced a major restructuring plan, including selling off $20 billion in assets and cutting the dividend by 50%, to refocus the company on energy, aviation, and medical technology. This shift marks the end of GE's light bulb manufacturing, a legacy of co-founder Thomas Edison, and has left many analysts and employees concerned about the company's future and its impact on towns like Schenectady.

title: iShares Russell 3000 ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:IWV) $IWV

title: iOS updates: Why are some Apple products behind on updates?
summary: A Zimperium study revealed that over 23% of iOS devices aren't running the latest software, despite Apple's integrated hardware and software ecosystem. This article explores the reasons behind this discrepancy, including user preferences to delay updates, incompatibility with older devices, jailbreaking, disabled automatic updates, and network restrictions.

title: Ireland to Start Collecting $15 Billion in Tax From Apple
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Ireland has reached an agreement with Apple to collect up to 13 billion euros ($15 billion) in back taxes, which will be held in an escrow account pending an appeal before the Court of Justice of the European Union. The European Commission had ordered Ireland to collect the taxes, claiming that two Irish tax rulings gave Apple an unfair advantage. Apple and the Irish government disagree with the Commission's analysis and are confident the decision will be overturned.

summary: This article provides detailed information about the iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW), including its current price, key statistics, overview, expenses, and top holdings. summary: The iShares Global Tech ETF (IXN) is an exchange-traded fund that tracks the S&P Global 1200 Information Technology Sector Index, investing primarily in information technology companies. title: P&G reshuffles top execs, more to come?
summary: Procter & Gamble announced an executive reshuffle, appointing Gary Coombe as the new head of Gillette and its grooming business, succeeding Charlie Pierce who moves to an adviser role. Magesvaran Suranjan will expand his responsibilities to include India, Middle East, and Africa marketing operations. These changes, aimed at optimizing management efficiency, come amidst ongoing challenges in P&G's razor business from online competitors and a desire to balance internal promotions with external hires for new ideas.

title: ClearBridge Large Cap Growth ESG ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:LRGE)
summary: The ClearBridge Large Cap Growth ESG ETF (LRGE) focuses on global large-cap growth stocks with strong environmental, social, and governance (ESG) characteristics, aiming for long-term capital appreciation. summary: This article provides detailed information on the Schwab US Broad Market ETF (SCHB), including its current price, key statistics, overview, and expense ratios. summary: This article provides detailed information on the iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO), including its current price, key statistics, overview, and expenses. title: Sharp seeks alliance with Japan Display Inc to challenge LG, Samsung in OLED displays
summary: Sharp's CEO has announced plans to seek an alliance with Japan Display Inc regarding OLED technology, aiming to better compete with South Korean manufacturers like LG and Samsung. The company intends to approach the Japanese government, particularly the industry ministry and INCJ, to facilitate this collaboration, which could also include investment in JOLED, a Japan Display affiliate. This move comes as Sharp's shares returned to the Tokyo Stock Exchange's first section, following strategic changes and cost-cutting measures implemented after Foxconn's acquisition.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:ACWI)
summary: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF tracks a market cap-weighted index of large- and mid-cap global stocks, covering a significant portion of developed and emerging markets. As of December 26, 2025, ACWI trades at $142.90, representing a 21.6% increase since January 1, 2025. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.

title: Katherine Adams
summary: Katherine Adams serves as Apple's Senior Vice President and General Counsel, overseeing all legal matters, global security, and privacy for the company. She reports to CEO Tim Cook and is a member of the executive team. Prior to joining Apple in 2017, Adams held significant legal roles at Honeywell, Sidley Austin LLP, the United States Department of Justice, and as a law clerk for Supreme Court Justice Sandra Day O’Connor.

title: Spotify and Tencent Music buy stake in each other
summary: Spotify and China's Tencent Music Entertainment announced they would buy minority stakes in each other. This deal gives Spotify exposure to the Chinese market and provides a strategic advantage as it plans to list its shares next year. The investment also offers a potential "Plan B" for Spotify's core business and mitigates risks associated with entering the Chinese market independently.

title: PepsiCo to move to Nasdaq after nearly a century with NYSE
summary: PepsiCo Inc. is moving its stock listing to the Nasdaq exchange, ending a nearly century-long association with the New York Stock Exchange. The company believes this move will offer greater cost-effectiveness and access to Nasdaq's investor engagement tools. Shares are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq on December 20, where Pepsi will join other major companies like Apple and Alphabet.

title: Apple Buys Shazam to Boost Apple Music
summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to acquire the music-identification service Shazam for an estimated $400 million, one of its largest acquisitions ever. The deal aims to boost Apple Music and integrates Shazam, an early iPhone app success, which already had ties to Apple through Siri. This purchase underscores Apple's strategy to enhance its music services and leverage popular technology.

title: Amazon, the Music Prodigy
summary: Amazon Music has rapidly become a significant player in the digital music streaming market, positioning itself as the third-largest service by subscribers behind Spotify and Apple Music. The company has also achieved the second-highest weekly listening engagement among paid music services, surpassing Apple Music. This growth highlights Amazon's expanding influence beyond its traditional e-commerce and device ventures into the entertainment sector.

title: Amazon's cloud unit expands in China, with new partner in Ningxia
source: FashionNetwork - The World's Fashion Business News
source_domain: FashionNetwork - The World's Fashion Business News
summary: Amazon's cloud computing unit is expanding its operations in China through a new partnership with Ningxia Western Cloud Data Technology Co Ltd (NWCD). This move aims to navigate China's strict new regulations, which require foreign firms to store data locally and outsource hardware to local partners, amidst a crowded and highly regulated market. The expansion follows Amazon Web Services' recent sale of hardware assets to Beijing Sinnet Technology Co Ltd to comply with these regulations.

title: Apple awards Finisar $390 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund
summary: Apple announced a $390 million award to Finisar from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund, aimed at supporting innovation and job creation by American manufacturers. This investment will enable Finisar to significantly ramp up R&D and high-volume production of VCSELs, crucial components for Apple's Face ID and other features, and will create over 500 high-skill jobs at Finisar's new manufacturing plant in Sherman, Texas. This initiative is part of Apple's broader commitment to invest at least $1 billion in US-based companies to foster advanced manufacturing and skilled jobs within the country.

title: What Is Layer3 TV and How Does It Work?
summary: T-Mobile is acquiring Layer3 TV, a cable TV service that streams hundreds of channels over existing internet connections, eliminating the need for new cables. Layer3 TV uses High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) to deliver up to 275 channels, including 4K content, at reduced bandwidth. The service offers a Platinum allHD package for $75/month for about 150 channels, includes a 1TB DVR cable box, and supports customizable interfaces and integration with other streaming services.

title: Toshiba Settles Fight With Western Digital That Imperiled a Major Deal (Published 2017)
summary: Toshiba settled a legal dispute with Western Digital, removing a major obstacle to the planned $14 billion sale of its microchip unit. This agreement allows Toshiba to proceed with the sale to a Bain Capital-led consortium, providing much-needed cash after losses from nuclear power projects. Both companies agreed to withdraw their lawsuits and arbitration claims, paving the way for renewed collaboration.

title: Finisar and Caterpillar jump while Diebold and Lumentum fall
summary: Finisar and Caterpillar saw their stock prices jump, with Finisar benefiting from a $390 million Apple investment for facial recognition lasers, and Caterpillar updating investors on strong sales. Conversely, Diebold Nixdorf and Lumentum Holdings experienced drops, with Lumentum directly impacted by Apple's investment in a competitor and Diebold's CEO resigning. Other companies like Western Digital, Overstock.com, Honeywell, and Eli Lilly also saw notable stock movements.

title: The Bank of Montreal Group is embracing collaboration in the digital era
summary: The Bank of Montreal Group (BMO) is actively engaging in collaborations with fintech companies, viewing them not as threats but as vital partners for driving innovation and adapting to the evolving digital banking landscape. BMO leverages these partnerships to enhance customer experience, accelerate business outcomes, and develop new technologies, while fintechs benefit from BMO's regulatory expertise and customer base. The bank's strategy focuses on finding complementary strengths to improve services and remain a powerful business partner for its clients beyond just providing capital.

title: Disney Makes $52.4 Billion Deal for 21st Century Fox in Big Bet on Streaming (Published 2017)
summary: The Walt Disney Company announced a deal to acquire most of 21st Century Fox for approximately $52.4 billion in an all-stock transaction. This acquisition marks Disney's significant move into streaming services to compete with tech giants and accelerate its direct-to-consumer strategy. The agreement will reshape Hollywood, bolstering Disney's content library while downsizing 20th Century Fox.

title: 3 ways the Disney-Fox megamerger could affect you
summary: The Walt Disney Company announced a $52.4 billion deal to acquire a significant portion of 21st Century Fox, a move expected to reshape the media landscape. This megamerger could lead to higher cable bills due to Disney's increased leverage through ESPN, alter streaming content availability as Disney gains control of Hulu and pulls content from Netflix, and expand Disney's global reach, especially in Europe and India. The acquisition positions Disney to compete more effectively with major streaming services and expand its influence in international markets.

title: Synaptics Shipping First In-Display Smartphone Fingerprint Reader With Vivo
summary: Synaptics announced it is shipping the first in-display smartphone fingerprint reader, Clear ID FS9500, with top-five smartphone vendor Vivo. This technology integrates a secure and fast fingerprint sensor directly into the smartphone's OLED display, addressing industry challenges with in-display sensor manufacturing. The Clear ID sensor is designed to improve user experience by eliminating rear sensors and notches, offering a secure and convenient alternative to other biometric authentication methods like 3D facial recognition.

title: Cord-cutting tips for setting up your new Roku, Amazon Fire TV and Apple TV
summary: This article provides essential setup tips for new Roku, Amazon Fire TV, and Apple TV streaming devices. It recommends using an Ethernet connection for better reliability, utilizing Plex for accessing personal media files across different platforms, and installing remote-control apps on smartphones for easier text entry. These tips aim to help users quickly and efficiently set up their new streaming media players.

title: CSX CEO's death raises disclosure issues
summary: The death of CSX Corp. CEO Hunter Harrison just two days after his medical leave was announced has ignited a debate about company disclosure requirements regarding the health of top executives. Harrison, 73, had a history of health issues and had declined an independent health review before being hired by CSX for $84 million. This situation highlights the tension between an executive's right to privacy and the public's need for information that significantly impacts a company's stock and future.

title: How to buy a streaming TV player: Apple TV vs. Roku vs. Amazon
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to choosing a streaming TV player, comparing options from Apple TV, Roku, and Amazon. It details the features, pricing, and pros and cons of various models, considering factors like 4K streaming, remote functionality, and content availability. The author also offers specific recommendations based on user needs and budget.

title: Dating App For Latinos: Match, Univision Launch 'Chispa' To Connect Hispanics Across The US
summary: Match Group and Univision Communications Inc. have partnered to launch Chispa, a new free dating app aimed at connecting Latino singles across the U.S. The app leverages Univision's knowledge of the Hispanic community and Match Group's expertise in dating platforms. Chispa offers an easy-to-use swipe functionality, allowing users to create profiles, share interests, and connect with potential matches from similar backgrounds.

title: Special Report - How Philip Morris is selling regulators on its hot new smoking device
