summary: This report details how Philip Morris International is aggressively promoting its iQOS heated tobacco device to regulators worldwide, portraying it as a less harmful alternative to traditional cigarettes. The company is lobbying governments to classify iQOS differently than cigarettes to secure lower taxes and fewer marketing restrictions, despite facing scrutiny over its scientific claims and marketing practices in various countries. The article highlights case studies in Japan, Israel, and Colombia, illustrating the varying success of these strategies.
authors: Paritosh Bansal|Tom Lasseter|Duff Wilson Thomas Wilson|Ami Miyazaki and Aditya Kalra

title: Lawsuit against Apple alleges 'older iPhone models slow down when new models come out'
summary: A class-action lawsuit has been filed against Apple, alleging that the company deliberately slows down older iPhone models when new ones are released, without user consent. Apple has admitted to slowing down older iPhones, stating it is to prevent unexpected shutdowns due to aging batteries and to prolong device life. However, some users and experts suggest this practice encourages the purchase of new devices, although battery replacement is presented as an alternative.

title: Shopping procrastinators, Amazon wants to tell you: there's still time to buy
summary: This article for shopping procrastinators highlights Amazon's last-minute shipping deadlines for Christmas gifts, noting Saturday for free one-day shipping and Sunday for free same-day delivery for Prime members on select items. It also covers other tech news, including Apple's admission about slowing down older iPhones, Google Chrome's upcoming ad-blocking feature, Amazon's potential YouTube competitor, and Facebook's new alliance with Universal Music Group for licensed music.

title: Target And Walmart Stores Are Turning Into Mini Distribution Centers To Compete With Amazon
summary: Traditional retailers like Target and Walmart are transforming their physical stores into mini distribution centers to compete with Amazon's dominant e-commerce presence and offer faster shipping. This strategy helps them leverage existing assets to reduce shipping costs and delivery times, enhancing their online fulfillment capabilities. The move also aids in efficient management of returns and inventory, boosting online sales for these brick-and-mortar giants.

title: Apple slowed iPhones, forcing owners to buy new ones, lawsuit claims
summary: iPhone owners from several states have sued Apple Inc., alleging the company deliberately slowed older iPhone models through software updates, leading consumers to believe they needed to buy new phones. Apple claims the slowdown was a fix for degraded lithium-ion batteries that could cause devices to shut down unexpectedly. The plaintiffs argue that Apple's silence about simply replacing batteries led them to purchase newer, pricier iPhones unnecessarily.

title: Kim Kardashian’s stocking fillers from Kanye West included Amazon, Disney and Apple stock
summary: Kanye West gifted Kim Kardashian stocks in major U.S. corporations, including Disney, Netflix, Amazon, Apple, and Adidas, for Christmas. Kardashian received 920 Walt Disney shares worth approximately $100,000 and 995 shares of Adidas stock. This unique gift followed a strong year for the U.S. stock markets, with several of these companies showing significant year-to-date performance gains.

title: Apple’s Not-Quite Trillion-Dollar iPhone
summary: Apple Inc.'s stock price has soared this year, achieving its best annual performance since 2010, driven by anticipation of a new iPhone "supercycle." However, recent data suggest that sales of the new iPhone models may not be as strong as initially expected, potentially making the company's continued growth toward a trillion-dollar market value more challenging.

title: Apple apologizes for slowing down old iPhones, drops price for battery replacements
summary: Apple has issued an apology for intentionally slowing down older iPhone models and announced a significant price reduction for replacement batteries, from $79 to $29, for the next year. This decision comes after a period of user complaints, social media backlash, and multiple lawsuits accusing the company of deliberately degrading performance to encourage new sales. Apple explained that the slowdowns were due to software updates aimed at preventing unexpected shutdowns caused by aging batteries, and it plans to release an iOS update to provide users with more visibility into battery health.

title: Apple Apologizes After Outcry Over Slowed iPhones
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple has apologized for slowing down older iPhones with flagging batteries and announced measures to address consumer outrage. The company will cut the price of out-of-warranty battery replacements from $79 to $29 and update its iOS software to show battery health and performance impact. This comes amidst lawsuits and concerns regarding product quality, especially with the high price of its new iPhone X.

title: Look Inside This Chinese Smartphone and See It’s Made in America
summary: A teardown of ZTE Corp.'s Axon M smartphone reveals its significant reliance on U.S. technology, with U.S. companies supplying 60% of its electronic components by dollar value. Qualcomm Inc. is identified as the largest supplier of key parts, including the modem and power-management unit. The findings highlight China's dependence on U.S. tech in the face of increasing trade tensions.

title: Here’s Why Apple Won’t Buy Netflix When It Repatriates Its Billions
summary: Despite analyst predictions, this article argues that Apple is unlikely to acquire Netflix, even with the ability to repatriate billions of dollars in overseas cash under the new tax code. The reasons cited include Apple not bringing back its entire cash hoard and its acquisition strategy focusing more on technology and engineering needs for its product development rather than cash position.

title: Biggest Dow winners and losers in 2017: Boeing soars to record levels, GE slumps
summary: Boeing Co. significantly outperformed other companies in the Dow Jones Industrial Average in 2017, achieving an 89.4% stock price surge. This gain surpassed tech giants like the FAANG stocks and even fellow industrial company Caterpillar Inc., which saw a 69.9% gain. General Electric (GE) on the other hand, slumped in comparison

title: Intel Confirms Long-Standing Microprocessor Chip Hacking Vulnerability
summary: Intel confirmed a security vulnerability in its microprocessor chips, affecting devices dating back to 1995, which could allow hackers to obtain personal information. The flaw, also found in chips by Arm and AMD, exploits predictive processing algorithms. While Intel asserts the exploits cannot corrupt or delete data, the initial patch is expected to slow operating times, and the company is working with others to provide updates.

title: Hacking fears: Security flaws put virtually all phones, computers at risk
summary: Security researchers have disclosed major flaws, Meltdown and Spectre, affecting chips from Intel, AMD, and ARM, which could allow hackers to steal sensitive information from nearly all modern computing devices. While Meltdown, specific to Intel, is more severe in the short term but patchable, Spectre affects a broader range of devices and is harder to fix, posing a long-term challenge. Companies like Intel, ARM, Google, Apple, and Microsoft are working on and releasing patches, though some suggest potential performance impacts on Intel chips.

title: Intel Shares Fall as Investors Worry About Costs of Chip Flaw
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Intel's shares fell due to investor concerns about the financial and reputational fallout from recently disclosed security flaws in its microprocessors. The flaws could allow hackers to steal sensitive information, leading to potential lawsuits and demands for compensation from major customers. The incident is also expected to increase Intel's chip development spending on security and could lead cloud companies to seek lower chip prices.

title: U.S. fund managers expect value stocks to jump in 2018
summary: U.S. fund managers are shifting their focus to value stocks in early 2018, anticipating a jump in overlooked shares after a strong year for growth stocks in 2017. Despite past trends where growth stocks continued to lead after strong market years, some managers are actively underweighting technology and increasing positions in financials, industrial companies, and retailers with attractive valuations. They see these value-oriented investments as having significant upside potential over the next couple of years.

title: COLGATE ADVANCES THE FUTURE OF ORAL CARE
summary: Colgate-Palmolive is launching its first app-enabled electronic toothbrush, the Colgate® Smart Electronic Toothbrush E1 with Artificial Intelligence, exclusively on Apple.com and in select Apple Stores. This new toothbrush provides real-time feedback and uses Apple ResearchKit to improve brushing habits and gather data for future oral care innovations. The product aims to help users achieve healthier smiles by providing personalized guidance and integrating with a 3D brushing coach via the Colgate Connect app.

title: Vuzix launches first augmented reality sunglasses with Alexa integration at CES
summary: Vuzix Corporation unveiled the Vuzix Blade, the first augmented reality sunglasses with Amazon's Alexa voice assistant built-in, at CES 2018. These smart glasses feature a sleeker design, an 8-megapixel camera, and the Android operating system, allowing users to display various information within their field of vision and connect to the internet or smartphones. A developer version will be available for $1,997, with a consumer version expected in Q2 for $1,000, aiming for a price under $500 in future iterations.

title: The global LiDAR market is booming
summary: The global LiDAR market is projected to reach USD 5,842.37 million by 2024, growing at a CAGR of 24.1% from 2017 to 2024, driven by increased demand for drones and technological advancements. Key players include Teledyne Optech, Hexagon, Sick AG, and Trimble Inc., with the market being highly concentrated. The report highlights market drivers, restraints, competitive landscape, and segmented forecasts across various applications and geographies.

title: Whirlpool, Apple partner on home appliance front
summary: Whirlpool and Apple have partnered on the home appliance front, a move that highlights the increasing integration of technology into everyday life. This partnership was announced amid other news concerning Whirlpool, including calls for trade relief for its Ohio workers and further investment in Dayton-area operations, as well as a recent split with Sears. The collaboration signifies a deepening trend of smart home technology.

title: Gartner estimates Apple Mac grew shipments 1.4% in holiday quarter; computer market declined in Q417 and for the year
summary: Gartner reported that worldwide PC shipments declined by 2% in Q4 2017 and 2.8% for the full year, marking the 13th consecutive quarter of decline. Despite this, Apple's Mac shipments saw a modest 1.4% growth in the holiday quarter. The report highlighted a shift in the PC market towards specialized, high-quality devices and a significant decline in the U.S. market due to competition from other holiday gift items.

title: Equinix to Build More Owned Data Centers with “Nodes” for Cloud Giants
summary: Equinix plans to build more owned data centers with 1MW to 2MW "nodes" specifically for hyper-scale cloud and SaaS providers, including Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform, Facebook, and Apple. This strategy aims to capture the next wave of cloud deployments and secure competitive advantage by attracting profitable enterprise customers, despite lower margins compared to retail cabinet deployments. The company also continues its M&A strategy, including recent acquisitions of Verizon data centers and Metronode in Australia, expanding its global footprint.

title: UnitedHealth Group Revenue Topped $200B in 2017, Net Earnings Grew 50 Percent
summary: UnitedHealth Group announced record-breaking revenue exceeding $200 billion in 2017, with net earnings growing by 50 percent to $10.6 billion. The company attributes this success, in part, to strong fourth-quarter performance and anticipates a further $1.7 billion jump in earnings and cash flow due to federal tax reform in 2018. UnitedHealth plans to invest these anticipated savings into existing initiatives, artificial intelligence, data analytics, and digital health, among others, while raising its 2018 earnings outlook.

title: Intel Corp. Is Now Sampling XMM 7560 LTE Modem
summary: Intel is currently sampling its XMM 7560 cellular modem to multiple customers, with products featuring the modem expected to be available later in 2018. This modem is significant as it supports the CDMA network, a key feature missing from previous Intel modems that limited their market share, particularly with Apple's iPhones. While Intel aims to expand the modem's use beyond smartphones to areas like always-connected PCs and autonomous driving, its primary success in the near future will likely depend on its adoption in upcoming Apple iPhone models due to Apple's large shipment volumes.

title: Apple accelerates US investment and job creation
summary: Apple announced plans to contribute over $350 billion to the US economy over the next five years, fueled by repatriation tax payments, capital expenditures, and increased spending with domestic suppliers. This initiative is expected to create over 20,000 new jobs, establish a new Apple campus, and expand Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund to $5 billion. The company also highlighted its commitment to STEM education and preparing students for the app economy.

title: Dealertrack Canada upgrades mobile app for dealers
summary: Dealertrack Canada has significantly upgraded its mobile application to improve and evolve dealer productivity. The new app allows dealers to securely track deals, sales leads, credit application statuses, and lender notes on their mobile devices, whether on or off-site. This enhancement aims to position dealers, lenders, and OEMs for the future of mobile digital retailing by providing immediate access to critical transaction information.

title: Apple, Oracle and Microsoft are expected to issue less debt in 2018
summary: Following five years of significant growth in corporate bond issuance, technology companies like Apple Inc., Broadcom Ltd., and Microsoft Corp. are projected to issue less debt in 2018. This shift is primarily due to the expectation that these companies will begin repatriating cash held overseas back to the U.S. after the new tax bill, alleviating the need to issue bonds for shareholder rewards.

title: Xiaomi, Vivo, Oppo protest Broadcom’s $105 billion Qualcomm bid
summary: Three major Chinese smartphone manufacturers, Xiaomi, Oppo, and Vivo, have voiced their opposition to Broadcom's $105 billion bid to acquire Qualcomm. They argue that such a merger would create a monopoly in the smartphone chip market, stifle innovation, and negatively impact research and development in the wireless technology sector. The companies, heavy users of Qualcomm chips, emphasized their support for Qualcomm against Broadcom's unsolicited acquisition attempt.

title: Buy HomePod (2nd generation)
summary: This page provides information on purchasing the HomePod (2nd generation) and HomePod mini, detailing their features and prices. It compares the two models, highlighting their audio capabilities and smart home functionalities. Additionally, it outlines an offer for free Apple Music with HomePod purchase and provides extensive legal disclaimers regarding AppleCare services, including terms for accidental damage, theft/loss coverage, and service fees.

title: LG Display, Sony Expand OLED Partnership into Smartphone Sector
summary: LG Display is expanding its OLED partnership with Sony beyond large OLED TVs into the smartphone sector, supplying flexible OLEDs to Sony. This collaboration is driven by Sony's success with LG Display's large OLED panels in its TV business and its need to maintain its smartphone business despite sluggish sales. For LG Display, Sony represents a crucial partner as it ramps up flexible OLED mass production and seeks to diversify its customer base beyond major buyers like Apple.

title: Gorilla Glass, optical fiber drive improved Corning results
summary: Corning Inc. reported a 12 percent increase in fourth-quarter core earnings, driven by strong demand for Gorilla Glass and optical fiber, exceeding Wall Street estimates. The company plans continued expansion with 23 new projects, including 11 new plants, and expects further performance improvements in late 2018. Despite a fourth-quarter GAAP loss due to a tax law charge, core earnings remained strong, and Corning shares were down 5.6% on the day but up 7% year-to-date.

title: Cracks surface in the bull trend, S&P 500 pulls in from uncharted territory
summary: The S&P 500 is pulling back from uncharted territory as cracks begin to surface in the bull trend. This comes as an unusually strong month of January nears its end for U.S. stocks, which are now on the defensive. The article also notes the 10-year yield hitting a 45-month high and mentions Apple retesting major support.

title: Cracks surface in the bull trend, S&P 500 pulls in from uncharted territory
summary: The article reports that cracks are beginning to appear in the bull market trend as the S&P 500 pulls back from uncharted territory. This comes at the end of an unusually strong month of January for U.S. stocks, which are now on the defensive. The report also highlights a 45-month high for the 10-year yield and retesting of major support by Apple, while Intel takes flight.

title: ASE and Cadence Deliver First System-in-Package EDA Solution
summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering, Inc. (ASE) and Cadence Design Systems have collaborated to release a System-in-Package (SiP) EDA solution. This solution, called SiP-id, addresses the challenges of designing and verifying Fan-Out Chip-on-Substrate (FOCoS) multi-die packages, significantly reducing design iteration time. It offers a streamlined methodology and enhanced tools tailored for ASE's advanced IC package technologies, benefiting both companies' customers.

title: Broadcom to raise $120 billion Qualcomm bid in push for talks: Sources
summary: Broadcom is planning to raise its bid for smartphone chipmaker Qualcomm to approximately $120 billion, aiming to pressure Qualcomm into negotiations before a crucial shareholder meeting on March 6. The new offer would value Qualcomm between $80 and $82 per share, up from the previous $70, and include a higher breakup fee to address regulatory concerns. Meanwhile, Qualcomm is pursuing its own acquisition of NXP Semiconductors NV for $38 billion while also dealing with a patent infringement dispute with Apple.

title: Tech Talk: Innovations That Have Changed How We Bank
summary: This article discusses how technology has transformed banking, shifting most operations to digital platforms. Dominic Venturo, Chief Innovation Officer at U.S. Bancorp, highlights efforts in mobile payments and the approach to innovation in a risk-averse industry. He also speculates on the future of banking, envisioning less paper, seamless cross-channel transitions, and invisible payments within the next five years.

title: Apple, Cisco team up with insurance companies to offer cyber policy discounts
summary: Apple Inc. and Cisco Systems Inc. have partnered with insurer Allianz SE and broker Aon Plc to offer discounts on cyber insurance policies for businesses using their equipment. This initiative aims to strengthen cyber security defenses and provide more favorable insurance terms, such as lower deductibles and support services, for policyholders in the event of an attack. The offering targets the growing market for cyber crime insurance, which has seen increased demand due to costly and frequent hacks.

title: Williams-Sonoma taps new CTO
summary: Williams-Sonoma Inc. has appointed Yasir Anwar as its new Chief Technology Officer. Anwar, formerly CTO at Macy's and with experience at Walmartlabs, will lead all technology aspects, including digital experience, retail stores, and supply chain, and oversee the company's Outward Inc. subsidiary. His appointment aims to accelerate initiatives for customer satisfaction and supply chain efficiencies, especially given the company's focus on integrating technologies like augmented reality.

title: Micro LED vs OLED: Competition between the Two Display Technologies
summary: This article compares Micro LED and OLED display technologies, highlighting their similarities in self-emission but noting Micro LED's advantages in brightness, stability, and inorganic nature. It discusses the significant challenges Micro LED faces in terms of high production cost, difficulty in manufacturing, and downsizing for consumer markets, contrasting it with OLED's growing maturity, flexibility, and increasing affordability. The piece concludes that while Micro LED shows promise, it is still in the R&D phase and not yet ready to supersede the more established and continuously evolving OLED technology.

title: Qualcomm and Samsung announce strategic partnership as Apple looks to building its own chips
summary: Qualcomm Technologies and Samsung Electronics have announced a multi-year strategic partnership to transition the industry from 4G to 5G across various mobile devices. This comes as Apple is reportedly developing its own custom chips, potentially reducing its reliance on Qualcomm and Intel for modems. The collaboration aims to leverage both companies' strengths in mobile technology.

title: Waymo accepts $245 million and Uber's 'regret' to settle self-driving car dispute
summary: Uber Technologies Inc. settled a legal dispute with Waymo, Alphabet Inc.'s self-driving unit, agreeing to pay $245 million in Uber shares. The settlement resolves a trade secrets lawsuit concerning a former Waymo engineer who joined Uber, allowing Uber's CEO to move past significant past controversies and focus on self-driving technology development.
authors: Alexandria Sage|Dan Levine and Heather Somerville

title: Qualcomm rejects Broadcom offer
summary: Qualcomm has rejected Broadcom's revised $121 billion buyout offer, citing "serious deficiencies in value and certainty" while still proposing a meeting to discuss the bid further. Qualcomm believes the $82 per share offer undervalues the company and lacks the necessary regulatory commitment given significant antitrust risks. The company also raised concerns about Broadcom's proposed divestitures and the potential disruption during a critical period of innovation for Qualcomm.

title: Qualcomm, Broadcom plan to meet on Feb. 14 - sources
summary: Qualcomm Inc and Broadcom Ltd are scheduled to meet on February 14 to discuss Broadcom's $121 billion acquisition offer, marking their first direct discussion on the potential deal. This meeting follows Broadcom's increased offer and concessions, which Qualcomm still deems an undervaluation and lacking firm regulatory commitments. Both companies are also engaging with proxy advisory firms ahead of a shareholder vote on March 6.

title: Nearly 70 Percent of EU5 Mobile Banking Users Own Smartphone
summary: In the EU5 region (France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the UK), nearly 70 percent of mobile banking users own smartphones. Apple users showed the highest relative usage of mobile banking, accounting for 27.5 percent of users and 5.5 million mobile bankers. Google's Android software users followed with 2.7 million mobile bankers, while Symbian had 3.3 million users but a lower index due to its widespread platform presence.

title: HP Is Bringing Apple's iPhones, iPads And MacBooks To Its Device-As-A-Service Offering
summary: HP Inc. is expanding its Device-as-a-Service (DaaS) offering to include Apple products such as iPhones, iPads, and MacBook Pros, with plans to extend this to channel partners in the coming year. This move aims to simplify device procurement, management, support, and services for commercial customers who manage both Windows and Apple devices. HP's Apple DaaS will initially be offered directly in North America, with partners being onboarded after ensuring all functional capabilities are robust.

title: Warren Buffett buys Teva shares, cuts Phillips 66 stake
summary: Billionaire Warren Buffett's company, Berkshire Hathaway, disclosed new investments in Teva Pharmaceuticals and a reduction in its holdings of Phillips 66. Berkshire sold 35 million shares of Phillips 66 back to the company for $3.3 billion to reduce its stake below the 10 percent regulatory threshold, while also increasing its investment in Apple and continuing to decrease its IBM stake. The move into Teva comes as the drugmaker struggles with competition and debt.

title: HP’s Device as a Service now featuring, er, Apple products
summary: HP Inc. is expanding its Device as a Service (DaaS) program to include Apple products, from iPhones to Macs, in response to customer demand for managing mixed operating system environments. This move allows HP to tap into the mobile device market dominated by Apple and helps businesses manage the increasingly diverse endpoint landscape. The partnership highlights Apple's strategy of making further inroads into the enterprise by collaborating with companies that have robust channel programs.

title: Qualcomm should negotiate sale to Broadcom, says ISS
summary: Proxy advisory firm Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) has recommended that Qualcomm negotiate a sale to Broadcom following Broadcom's sweetened $121 billion offer. ISS advised Qualcomm shareholders to vote for four of Broadcom's six director nominees at the upcoming shareholder meeting, suggesting this would encourage a negotiated deal despite Qualcomm's previous resistance. While ISS believes Broadcom's current offer isn't clearly superior to Qualcomm's standalone value in the short term, it views the offer as a reasonable starting point for negotiations.

title: This new locally produced app will help you when you’ve ‘Got to Go’
source: Hamilton In-depth, Investigative News from Journal-News
source_domain: Hamilton In-depth, Investigative News from Journal-News
summary: Cintas Corporation has launched a free mobile app called "Got to Go" which helps users locate and rate public restrooms. The app displays restrooms on a map with color-coded icons indicating cleanliness, and allows users to filter results and get directions via Google Maps or Apple Maps. It also integrates nominees and winners of the Cintas Sponsored Best Restroom awards.

title: Qualcomm raises offer for NXP Semiconductors
summary: Qualcomm has raised its offer to buy NXP Semiconductors to $127.50 per share from $110, aiming to secure the deal after pressure from shareholders including Elliott Management. This revised bid puts pressure on Broadcom concerning its own offer for Qualcomm and has been backed by key NXP stockholders. The acquisition seeks to expand Qualcomm's presence in the automotive chip market.

title: HP starts slinging Apple hardware, tech support vending machines
summary: HP, a leading PC maker, has expanded its Device as a Service offering to include Apple hardware like Macs, iPads, and iPhones, catering to customers with multi-OS environments. This move is part of HP's strategy to handle all aspects of hardware fleet management for businesses. Additionally, HP has introduced "Tech Café Market" vending machines, which dispense PC supplies and accessories for easy access.

title: Roku CEO says memory shortage affected earnings
summary: Roku CEO Anthony Wood stated that memory shortages impacted the company's hardware profitability, despite reporting its most profitable quarter. The company was forced to use more expensive air shipping for NAND memory chips, which are crucial for its hardware and are experiencing rising prices and shortages across the tech industry. This issue may have contributed to a disappointing forecast for Roku.

title: Intel Expands Its 5G Ecosystem With Dell, HP, Lenovo PCs And Spreadtrum Alliance
summary: Intel is significantly expanding its 5G ecosystem, forging partnerships with Dell, HP Inc., and Lenovo to bring 5G connectivity to consumer PCs by late 2019. The company is also collaborating with Spreadtrum to deliver 5G phones and has completed the first public 5G NR interoperability test with Huawei, showcasing its end-to-end leadership in 5G. These strategic alliances highlight Intel's commitment to enabling 5G across various devices and infrastructure components.

title: Avigilon’s US$1B sale yanks potential anchor from Vancouver tech sector
summary: Motorola Solutions Inc. acquired Vancouver-based Avigilon Corp. for US$1 billion, raising concerns about the Vancouver tech sector's ability to foster anchor companies. While the sale provides a significant payout for Avigilon CEO Alexander Fernandes and benefits the local start-up ecosystem with experienced talent and capital, it highlights a trend of successful B.C. tech firms being acquired by larger foreign entities before reaching anchor company status. This trend, coupled with increased M&A activity and U.S. tax cuts, poses challenges to Vancouver's goal of developing major homegrown tech players.

title: BYD Bumped IBM From Berkshire Hathaway's 15 Most Valuable Holdings Last Year
summary: Chinese automaker BYD Co. entered Berkshire Hathaway Inc.'s top 15 most valuable holdings in 2017, replacing International Business Machines Corp. Warren Buffett's initial USD230 million investment in BYD in 2008 grew to HKD17 billion (approximately USD2.17 billion) by last year, reflecting his long-term investment philosophy. Berkshire Hathaway also significantly increased its holdings in Apple Inc. during the same period.

title: Apple's surprising cobalt move signals a controversial buy on metal commodities
summary: Apple's decision to directly buy cobalt has brought attention to this critical battery metal, signaling that commodities might be a good investment despite ethical concerns surrounding its sourcing from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The article discusses the financial opportunities in "new school commodities" like cobalt and lithium, while also highlighting the social responsibilities investors face regarding human rights and child labor in the mining industry. It also emphasizes that while profits can be made, responsible investing in such sectors requires careful consideration of the ethical implications.

title: Apple iPhone X was to start OLED boom, but here’s why it is not working
summary: The iPhone X was expected to drive a boom in OLED screen technology, with suppliers like Samsung ramping up production. However, lower-than-anticipated sales of the iPhone X and high costs of OLED panels compared to LCDs have led to concerns about oversupply and price cuts. Chinese phone makers are also not rushing to adopt OLED, impacting the widespread adoption initially forecasted.

title: The best sector of this bull market is the ‘greatest investment story ever told’
summary: This article highlights that internet stocks have been the best performing sector during the current nine-year bull market, which began after the financial crisis bottom. It notes that while internet investing is often associated with the dot-com era, the current extended bull market has also seen the sector significantly influence nearly every aspect of the economy. The piece was published on the ninth anniversary of the financial crisis bottom.

title: DowDuPont's Andrew Liveris makes way for Jim Fitterling as new Dow CEO
summary: Andrew Liveris is stepping down from DowDuPont after four decades, with Jim Fitterling set to become the new CEO of Dow when it spins off in 2019. Fitterling, a longtime Dow employee, will be the first openly gay CEO of a major chemical company and has been a proponent of LGBTQ+ rights in corporate America. Howard Ungerleider will serve as president and CFO of the new Dow spinoff.

title: Apple buys Next Issue Media
summary: Apple Inc. is acquiring Next Issue Media from private equity firm KKR & Co. LP. Next Issue Media is known for offering unlimited access to magazine titles through its monthly, multi-title subscription service.

title: Huge energy corporation, Halliburton ditching BlackBerry for iPhone
summary: Halliburton, a major energy corporation with over 70,000 employees, is transitioning from BlackBerry devices to iPhones for its workforce. This decision follows extensive research into both Apple's platform and Android, indicating a shift towards mobile computing platforms beyond basic communication in the enterprise sector. The move highlights the increasing penetration of iOS devices in large corporations, despite BlackBerry's historical dominance in enterprise.

title: Mazda Readies Android Auto, Apple CarPlay Capability
summary: Mazda announced that it will add Android Auto and Apple CarPlay capability to its lineup of cars and SUVs. This capability will be retroactively upgradeable onto all Mazda Connect systems with a minimal hardware addition. The company has not yet provided an official timeline or cost for the upgrades, leaving Toyota as one of the last holdouts against these technologies.

title: Here’s How We’re Making Checkout Faster in Our Stores
summary: Gap Inc. is implementing several new technologies to improve and speed up the in-store checkout experience for customers. These initiatives include price-check stations at Old Navy, mobile point-of-sale devices for associates, "Buy Online, Pick-Up In Store" and "Reserve in Store" options, and contactless payment methods like Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Android Pay. The goal is to reduce wait times and offer a more seamless shopping experience by merging physical and digital solutions.

title: With Qualcomm saga behind it, Broadcom tops Q4 estimates
summary: Broadcom exceeded its Q4 estimates due to strong demand for its chips from smartphone makers, even after its $117 billion hostile bid for Qualcomm was blocked by US President Donald Trump. The company reported a significant increase in net income, bolstered by its Wi-Fi chip sales and a $5.79 billion gain from the US tax law, while planning to redomicile to the United States and scout for smaller acquisition targets.

title: 3 Lessons Learned From the Rise and Fall of Once-Mighty Nokia
summary: This article examines the rise and fall of Nokia, once a dominant force in the mobile phone industry, and extracts three key lessons for businesses, particularly family-owned ones. It discusses Nokia's failure to adapt to changing market trends like smartphones, its neglect of key regional markets, and the detrimental impact of a fear-driven company culture. The piece emphasizes the importance of market presence, adaptability, and fostering an open communication environment within a company to avoid similar pitfalls.

title: Apple Is Using a Secret Facility to Do Something It’s Never Done Before
summary: Apple is developing its own MicroLED device displays for the first time at a secret facility near its California headquarters, aiming to reduce reliance on suppliers like Samsung and differentiate its products. This ambitious project, though still years away from consumer release, represents a significant investment in next-generation screen technology that promises slimmer, brighter, and more power-efficient gadgets, with initial prototypes for Apple Watch already developed. The move could disrupt the display supply chain and provides Apple with proprietary control over a key component.

title: Will take Android brands two years to catch up with Apple’s Face ID, say parts suppliers
summary: Parts suppliers predict it will take Android brands two years to catch up with Apple's Face ID technology due to parts shortages and Apple's supply-chain control. Companies like Samsung, Huawei, and Xiaomi are expected to be significantly behind, with mass adoption of 3D sensing not happening for Android until 2019. Apple's strategic deals, such as the $390M investment with Finisar, have secured key components, further limiting competitors' access.

title: Apple Face ID TrueDepth 3D Camera System Allegedly Has 2-Year Lead Over Android Competitors
summary: Apple's Face ID TrueDepth 3D camera system is believed to hold a two-year lead over Android competitors due to hardware shortages and Apple's significant R&D investment. Component makers cite difficulties for Android manufacturers in sourcing critical parts like vertical-cavity surface-emitting lasers (VCSELs). While Android OEMs struggle to replicate 3D facial scanning, many have adopted the display notch design.

title: iPhone: Designed in California but imported from China
summary: The article explores how Apple's iPhone, despite being designed in California, is assembled in China, significantly contributing to the US trade deficit with China. It highlights that while components come from various countries, current trade statistics count most of the manufacturing cost under China's exports. This complex global supply chain suggests that a US-China trade war could have wide-ranging economic damage.

title: After raising $756M in its IPO, Dropbox sees shares jump 36% in market debut
summary: Dropbox Inc. saw its shares jump 36% in its market debut after raising $756 million in its IPO, pricing shares at $21, higher than initial estimates. The company's valuation swelled to over $11 billion, marking the largest tech IPO since Snap Inc. last year. Despite not yet being profitable, the IPO's success is attributed to a Salesforce investment and a relatively small percentage of shares offered, creating scarcity.

title: Motorola Solutions closes US$1b acquisition of Vancouver’s Avigilon
summary: Motorola Solutions Inc. has completed its US$1 billion acquisition of Vancouver-based Avigilon Corporation. The deal, announced in early February, involves Motorola acquiring 44.6 million Avigilon shares at C$27 each, leading to Avigilon's delisting from the Toronto Stock Exchange. Avigilon founder Alex Fernandes, who owned 9.89% of the company, is set to retire and will receive C$116 million from the sale.

title: Younger viewers now watch Netflix more than the BBC, says corporation
summary: The BBC reports that younger viewers (16-24) now spend more time watching Netflix than all BBC TV services combined, and prefer music streaming services like Spotify over BBC radio stations. The corporation is concerned about being overtaken by competitors, particularly American tech companies, and is working to reinvent its approach to engage media-savvy young audiences. This shift highlights a rapid generational change in media consumption, prompting the BBC to focus on adapting to digital platforms and addressing issues like "fake news."

title: Doctors put patients in charge with Apple’s Health Records feature
summary: Apple's Health Records feature allows patients to access their medical records from multiple institutions directly on their iPhone, improving patient empowerment and doctor-patient communication. Doctors like Robert Harrington and Paul Testa highlight the challenges of current record-keeping and the benefits of consolidated digital health data for better-informed decision-making and emergency care. The feature is available to patients from nearly 40 health institutions across the United States.

title: DexCom’s glucose monitoring system gets FDA approval
summary: The FDA has approved Dexcom’s glucose monitoring system, the Dexcom G6, for use in children and adults with diabetes. This system is the first to be fully interoperable with other medical devices and electronic interfaces, streamlining future approvals for similar devices. The approval was granted via the de novo premarket review pathway for novel, low-to-moderate-risk devices.

title: 3 Reasons to Buy Apple Inc.'s 10.5-Inch iPad Pro Instead of the New 9.7-Inch iPad
summary: This article argues why consumers should opt for Apple's 10.5-inch iPad Pro over the newer, lower-cost 9.7-inch iPad, despite the latter gaining Apple Pencil support. The author highlights three key differentiators: the iPad Pro's ProMotion display, more colorful display (DCI-P3 wide color gamut), and a significantly faster A10X Fusion processor. These features provide a superior user experience for performance, visuals, and responsiveness.

title: HSBC Holdings PLC eyes robust growth in retail banking and wealth management in Taiwan
summary: HSBC Holdings PLC anticipates strong growth in its retail banking and wealth management (RBWM) offerings in Taiwan for the current year, marking Taiwan as one of its eight priority RBWM markets in the Asia Pacific. The company plans service enhancements through digital transformation investments and is focusing on digital initiatives to meet customer needs, following record pretax profits of US$43 million from its Taiwan RBWM businesses last year. These initiatives include launching Google Pay, with Samsung Pay and Apple Pay expected soon, and streamlining credit card approvals and insurance sales processes.

title: Why isn’t there a turkey burger at McDonalds? | WATTAgNet
summary: This article discusses why turkey burgers aren't commonly found on fast-food menus, particularly at McDonald's. According to a former McDonald's executive, the turkey industry hasn't made a compelling argument with facts and passion for why turkey should be included on fast-food menus or exported more widely. The article suggests that the turkey industry should adopt a strategy similar to successful companies like Apple and Starbucks, emphasizing product quality and consumer experience with passion.

title: Chips are down: Apple to stop using Intel processors in Macs, reports say
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to transition its Mac computers from Intel processors to in-house designed chips by 2020, mirroring its strategy for iPhones and iPads. This potential shift, which has been rumored for several years, led to a 6% drop in Intel's share price and aims to create a unified architecture across Apple's device ecosystem. The move would mark a significant change similar to Apple's transition from IBM's PowerPC to Intel processors in 2005.

title: How These Major Companies Are Getting Equal Pay Right
summary: This article examines how major corporations like Starbucks, Apple, Salesforce, Intel, and Adobe have achieved pay parity for women and underrepresented minorities in the U.S. It highlights the strategies these companies employ, including payroll audits and committed leadership, as well as the broader societal push for equal pay fueled by movements and evolving employee expectations. The piece emphasizes that while progress on wage equality has been slow, these companies are demonstrating that achieving pay parity is possible and crucial for future workforce attraction.

title: Apple Co-Founder Closing Facebook Account in Privacy Crisis
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak is closing his Facebook account due to privacy concerns, stating that Facebook profits from user data without sharing those profits. He criticized Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg for not valuing user privacy while securing his own. Wozniak believes Facebook is unlikely to change its policies regarding user data.

title: How Pizza Night Can Cost More in Data Than Dollars
summary: This article examines how a seemingly simple activity like ordering pizza can involve sharing a significant amount of personal data with tech companies. The Wall Street Journal reviewed privacy statements from companies like Amazon, Apple, Domino's, Facebook, and Google to illustrate how much information is collected beyond the cost of the meal. The piece highlights a growing awareness of data privacy in light of recent events, prompting users to question what data they are truly giving up.

title: Energous Corp Stock Is at Risk for a Reality Check
summary: This article discusses the risks associated with Energous Corp (WATT) stock despite its recent surge. The author questions the safety, efficiency, and real-world utility of Energous' WattUp wireless charging technology, emphasizing that first-to-market advantage and FCC certification do not guarantee success or practicality. The piece warns investors about potential health concerns and the inevitable competition from larger tech companies like Apple and Samsung if the technology proves viable.

title: If You File Taxes Using TurboTax, You Could Be Seriously Overpaying
summary: The article highlights that many TurboTax users might be overpaying for tax filing services, as the free federal tax filing option through the IRS Free File program is only available for those with an adjusted gross income of $33,000 or less. This AGI threshold is significantly lower than most other free tax-filing software options, which typically range from $60,000 to $66,000. Millions of taxpayers with AGIs between $30,000 and $75,000 could be affected by this discrepancy and might be able to file for free elsewhere.

title: VirnetX Awarded $502.6 Million in Fourth Jury Trial against Apple
summary: A federal jury in Eastern District of Texas awarded VirnetX Holding Corp. $502.6 million in a fourth jury trial against Apple Inc. for infringing four secure communications patents related to FaceTime and iMessage. This latest verdict continues an eight-year legal battle between the companies, though the celebration for VirnetX may be short-lived due to ongoing Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) challenges suggesting the patents are invalid. VirnetX's stock shot up over 40% after the verdict.

title: HP's Executive Forum Gives Insights Into Business, Products, And Just As Important, Company Culture
summary: This article discusses key insights from HP's Executive Forum, highlighting new product announcements in gaming, personal systems, and printing, along with updates on their 3D printing initiatives. Beyond product reveals, the author emphasizes the strong company culture, particularly noting the heartfelt tribute to the late COO Jon Flaxman, which revealed the close-knit relationships within HP's leadership. The forum served to showcase HP’s strategic roadmap and its focus on both innovation and internal values.

title: This 29-Year-Old Thinks Steve Jobs Is Wrong About Design. So He Built a Company to Prove It
summary: Suhail Doshi, founder of Mixpanel, built an analytics company to challenge the intuition-based product design philosophy, advocating for a data-driven approach instead. Mixpanel provides tools for mobile and Web apps to track user behavior, helping clients design products based on concrete data rather than "gut feelings." Despite starting during a recession and facing initial investor rejections, Mixpanel has grown significantly, raising $77 million and employing over 300 people, proving the value of its data-centric model.

title: 3 Rules for Dealing With Thinly Traded ETFs
summary: This article addresses the common concern about investing in thinly traded ETFs, offering three rules to navigate them effectively. It emphasizes that the liquidity of underlying holdings is more crucial than the ETF's trading volume, and highlights how brokers can assist with execution. The author shares a personal experience with SPDR MSCI USA StrategicFactors ETF (QUS) to illustrate these points.

title: iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:IUSV)
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV), including its current price, key statistics, holdings, and recent news headlines. It details the ETF's investment strategy, expense ratios, and significant institutional shareholders, while also answering frequently asked questions about its performance and how to invest.

title: JPMorgan U.S. Quality Factor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:JQUA)
summary: The JPMorgan U.S. Quality Factor ETF (JQUA) is an exchange-traded fund that tracks the JP Morgan US Quality Factor index, focusing on profitability, earnings, and solvency from the Russell 1000 companies. JQUA was launched on November 8, 2017, and is managed by JPMorgan Chase, with holdings including major tech companies like Alphabet, Broadcom, and Apple. The ETF has performed well, with its stock increasing by 10.6% this year to $63.34 as of the latest data.

title: Motley Fool 100 Index ETF (TMFC) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Motley Fool 100 Index ETF (TMFC), including its current price ($68.40), key financial stats like assets under management ($1.84 billion) and expense ratio (0.50%), and top holdings such as NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet. It also offers recent news headlines and frequently asked questions about the ETF's performance and ownership.

title: JPMorgan U.S. Momentum Factor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:JMOM)
summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the JPMorgan U.S. Momentum Factor ETF (JMOM), including its current price, dividend history, expense ratio, key statistics, and top holdings. It outlines the fund's strategy of tracking large-cap U.S. companies with strong momentum and discusses its performance and major institutional shareholders. The fund's top holdings include major tech companies like Alphabet, NVIDIA, and Meta Platforms.

title: Apple adds Earth Day donations to trade-in and recycling program
summary: Ahead of Earth Day, Apple announced a donation program to Conservation International for every device received through its Apple GiveBack program until April 30. The company also unveiled Daisy, a new robot designed to more efficiently disassemble iPhones to recover valuable materials, supporting Apple's goal of using only recycled or renewable materials. These initiatives underline Apple's commitment to environmental sustainability and resource conservation.

title: eBay’s App Update Helps Sellers List in Under One Minute
summary: eBay has updated its Android and iOS apps to simplify the listing process for sellers, allowing items to be listed in under one minute. This enhancement leverages structured data to pre-populate item details, pricing, and shipping information, making selling easier for both new and experienced sellers. The company highlights popular items with current average selling prices, encouraging users to leverage the app for selling.

title: Qualcomm Starts Massive Layoff As Part of Cost Cutting Strategy
summary: Qualcomm has begun massive layoffs as part of its strategy to cut $1 billion in annual costs, affecting both temporary and full-time employees, who will receive severance packages. The headcount reduction comes amidst its ongoing legal dispute with Apple and follows a prior announcement to streamline operations to improve revenue and appease investors. The company expects to file Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) in California, indicating a significant number of job eliminations.

title: U.S. Investigating AT&T and Verizon Over Wireless Collusion Claim (Published 2018)
summary: The Justice Department has launched an antitrust investigation into AT&T and Verizon regarding claims of collusion to restrict a new technology called eSIM. The investigation stems from accusations that the carriers worked with a standards-setting group to hinder consumers from easily switching wireless providers, potentially locking devices to their networks. This inquiry was initiated after complaints from at least one device maker, identified as Apple.

title: Apple Introduces New iPhone Disassembly Robot and Recycling Program
summary: Apple has introduced Daisy, a new robotic iPhone disassembly system capable of taking apart 200 iPhones per hour, an upgrade from its predecessor Liam as it can work with more iPhone models and harvest higher-grade aluminum. Alongside this, Apple launched the GiveBack recycling program, which streamlines device trade-ins and will donate to Conservation International for each device accepted until April 30, 2018. The initiative aims to improve Apple's environmental responsibility and device recycling efforts.

title: Analyst Says Apple Inc. iPhone X is “Dead” — But Don’t Panic Yet
summary: An analyst suggests the Apple Inc. iPhone X is "dead" due to poor sales and high pricing, leading Apple to stop production and abandon the ultra-premium market with cheaper replacements. However, a reality check points out flaws in this theory, noting the iPhone X's significant profit share and Apple's likely strategy to continue in the premium market with new models. The article concludes that the iPhone X might become a one-year model rather than being truly "dead."

title: Apple Takes a Step on Payment of Back Taxes to Ireland
summary: Apple Inc. has completed an agreement with Ireland to transfer approximately €13 billion ($16 billion) into a Dublin escrow account. This action is a step toward complying with a 2016 European Commission tax order which ruled that Apple had underreported profit in Ireland due to illegal state aid from two tax deals. Both Ireland and Apple are appealing the decision, but the company is required to remit the back taxes in the interim.

title: Sherwin-Williams’ AR app takes guesswork out of choosing paint
summary: Sherwin-Williams has enhanced its ColorSnap Visualizer mobile app with an augmented reality (AR) feature called "Instant Paint." This tool allows users to virtually preview any of the company's 1,500 paint colors on their walls in real-time using a smartphone camera. The feature aims to help customers confidently select paint colors and avoid costly repainting errors, leveraging the growing trend of AR try-before-you-buy applications in home improvement and other industries.

title: Eastman touts 10th anniversary of Eastman Tritan™ copolyester at NPE
summary: Eastman is celebrating the 10th anniversary of its Eastman Tritan™ copolyester, a clear, tough, chemical-resistant polymer. Tritan is being utilized in new smart technology applications, including digital agriculture with Arable Labs, the SproutsIO smart microgarden, and Hidrate Spark smart water bottles. The company highlights Tritan's versatility and performance properties, positioning it as a key material for the Internet of Things (IoT) revolution.

title: Jim Keller Joins Intel to Lead Silicon Engineering
summary: Intel announced that Jim Keller will join the company as a senior vice president to lead silicon engineering, including system-on-chip (SoC) development and integration. Keller brings over two decades of experience in x86 and ARM-based microarchitecture design from companies like Tesla, AMD, and Apple, where he contributed to the Zen architecture and early A-series processors. His appointment is expected to accelerate Intel's transformation in heterogeneous process and architectures for the data-centric computing era.

title: Lear's CEO sees an electronic sweet spot
summary: Lear Corp. CEO Ray Scott, who took over in March 2018, envisions a future where the company leverages its electronics division to create "smart seats" that integrate advanced technology with vehicle interiors. Scott anticipates rapid adoption of electric vehicles and sees significant growth opportunities in electrification, connectivity, and the convergence of seating and electronics. Lear is developing innovations like powered seat rails and health monitoring systems within seats, with some programs expected to launch by 2022-2023.

title: Why We Built Toast Go® 2: The Future of the POS is in Your Hands
summary: Toast Go® 2 is introduced as the next generation handheld POS system, designed specifically for the restaurant industry to enhance speed, safety, and flexibility. The article details how this new device improves on previous models by offering features like contactless payments, extended battery life, and increased durability, ultimately aiming to boost restaurant efficiency and guest satisfaction. It highlights the positive impact of handheld POS systems on various restaurant models and provides statistics on increased revenue and efficiency.

title: Berkshire Hathaway Has Exited IBM: Buffett
summary: Warren Buffett announced that Berkshire Hathaway has completely sold off its stake in IBM, a position once valued at $10.7 billion. This decision followed IBM's continued underperformance, with Berkshire previously having dumped 94.5% of its shares by the end of 2017. Concurrently, Buffett reallocated funds into Apple, significantly increasing Berkshire's holdings in the smartphone maker.

title: Announces Energizer Bunny ™ Stickers for iMessage
summary: Energizer Holdings, Inc. has launched a free Energizer Bunny™ sticker pack for iMessage, featuring thirteen animated and static stickers. This release aims to enhance text messages with the iconic symbol of long-lasting power, reflecting the brand's ongoing digital media engagement efforts. The stickers showcase the Energizer Bunny™ with popular catchphrases and creative animations.

title: Apple paves the way for breakthrough carbon-free aluminum smelting method
summary: Apple has played a crucial role in the development of a joint venture between Alcoa Corporation and Rio Tinto Aluminum to commercialize a patented technology that eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from the traditional aluminum smelting process. This revolutionary advancement could change global manufacturing, and Apple has partnered with the aluminum companies and the Governments of Canada and Quebec, investing a combined $144 million in future research and development. The new method releases oxygen instead of greenhouse gases and will be commercialized by a joint venture called Elysis, with a package planned for sale beginning in 2024.

title: Goldman Sachs cuts two leaders at its securities unit
summary: Goldman Sachs is reportedly losing two leaders from its securities unit, Pablo Salame and Isabelle Ealet, who are expected to leave the firm next month. This departure will leave Ashok Varadhan as the sole head of the securities division. The Wall Street Journal reported on these impending changes at the prominent financial institution.

title: Zebra (Xplore Technologies) M60
summary: Xplore Technologies, known as the "Rugged Tablet Authority," has launched its first rugged handheld computer, the M60, in response to the growing demand for powerful yet ultra-mobile devices. Running on Android 8.0 "Oreo" with a 6-inch screen, the M60 distinguishes itself from consumer smartphones through its inherent ruggedness, industrial-grade scanner, extensive accessories, and enterprise-focused software features like Android for Work and MDM/EMM certification. Its robust specifications, including a long-lasting replaceable battery and high IP68 sealing, make it suitable for demanding professional environments where consumer devices fall short.

title: Better Buy: TSMC vs. Qualcomm, Inc.
summary: This article compares Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) and Qualcomm (QCOM) as investment opportunities, analyzing their business models, growth rates, and profitability. While TSMC is presented as a safer, more diversified investment due to its contract manufacturing model and consistent profitability, Qualcomm offers a higher-risk, potentially higher-reward scenario tied to the outcome of its legal battle with Apple regarding licensing revenue. The author concludes that the better buy depends on an investor's personal risk profile.

title: New Real-Time Architecture And UI Innovations In Veeva CRM Deliver Information To Any Device For Greater Field Productivity
summary: Veeva Systems announced new mobile innovations in Veeva CRM, incorporating a real-time architecture and the Sunrise user interface. These enhancements allow field teams to access critical customer and product information seamlessly across all Apple and Windows devices, improving execution and productivity. The updates aim to provide a consistent mobile experience, enabling real-time insights and actions from anywhere, supporting over 280 companies including those that launched 83% of new FDA-approved drugs in the last three years.

title: Tracking Firm LocationSmart Leaked Location Data for Customers of All Major U.S. Mobile Carriers Without Consent in Real Time Via Its Web Site
summary: LocationSmart, a company aggregating real-time mobile phone location data, was found to be leaking this sensitive information through a vulnerable component of its website without requiring authentication. A security researcher discovered that the demo site could be abused to track the location of individuals using major U.S. mobile carriers like AT&T, Sprint, T-Mobile, and Verizon, raising serious privacy concerns following similar revelations about Securus Technologies. The vulnerability has been resolved and the demo disabled after being contacted by KrebsOnSecurity.

title: Look Inside This Chinese Smartphone and See It’s Made in America
summary: An examination of ZTE's Axon M smartphone reveals a significant reliance on U.S. technology, with U.S. companies supplying 60% of its electronic components by dollar value. This dependence highlights the critical need for ZTE to resolve the U.S. ban on shipments of phone parts amid increasing trade tensions. Major U.S. suppliers include Qualcomm, SanDisk, Skyworks Solutions, and Corning.

title: Look Inside This Chinese Smartphone and See It’s Made in America
summary: A teardown of ZTE's Axon M smartphone reveals a significant reliance on U.S. technology, with American companies supplying 60% of its electronic components by dollar value. This highlights China's dependence on U.S. tech amidst increasing trade tensions and a U.S. ban on shipments of phone parts to ZTE. Key suppliers include Qualcomm Inc., SanDisk Inc., Skyworks Solutions Inc., and Corning Inc.

title: Southwest Airlines and iHeartRadio team up for free in-flight music streaming
summary: Southwest Airlines announced a partnership with iHeartRadio to offer free in-flight music streaming to its customers. Passengers will be able to access live radio and custom stations via the onboard Wi-Fi, marking Southwest's first streaming music offering since 2015. This collaboration enhances customer travel experiences and ties together two companies with Texas roots, despite iHeartMedia's recent Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing.

title: Netflix overtakes Disney to become most valuable US media company
summary: Netflix surpassed Disney to become the most valuable US media company with a market capitalization of nearly $162bn, driven by its subscriber growth and a 70% stock increase since January. This shift is also influenced by Disney's potential acquisition challenges with 21st Century Fox and changing media consumption habits. The streaming giant has also secured content deals with high-profile figures like Barack and Michelle Obama, Shonda Rhimes, and Ryan Murphy, intensifying competition with other tech companies investing heavily in content.

title: Samsung must pay Apple $539 million in iPhone patent case
summary: A federal jury has ordered Samsung Electronics Co. to pay Apple Inc. $539 million for infringing patents related to the iPhone's design. This decision increases the damages Samsung owes Apple from $399 million to the new amount in a seven-year-old legal dispute. The bulk of the award, $533.3 million, is for three design patents, with an additional $5.3 million for two utility patents.

title: US jury awards Apple $539 million in Samsung patent retrial
summary: A U.S. jury ruled that Samsung must pay Apple $539 million for copying patented smartphone features, bringing a years-long legal dispute to its final stages. This award includes $533.3 million for design patent violations and $5.3 million for utility patent violations. While Apple expressed satisfaction, Samsung indicated it would explore all options to contest the verdict, which it claims goes against a Supreme Court ruling on design patent damages.

title: Urban Outfitters launches third-party marketplace, tests self-checkout
summary: Urban Outfitters has launched an online marketplace for third-party sellers and is testing a self-checkout experience in one of its stores. The retailer is also investing in its digital infrastructure, including new iOS apps and accepting Apple Pay and Afterpay, following strong digital growth in Q1 2019. These moves aim to broaden product selection, enhance customer experience, and increase sales.

title: Microsoft is now more valuable than Alphabet — by about $10 billion
summary: Microsoft has surpassed Alphabet in market capitalization, becoming the world's third most valuable company. This shift occurred as Microsoft's market value reached $749 billion compared to Alphabet's $739 billion, largely driven by significant growth in Microsoft's cloud computing business. Microsoft's stock surged 40 percent over the past 12 months, greatly outpacing Alphabet's gains.

title: Sanofi Chooses Accenture Interactive to Create UWell’s Healthcare Platform to Better Serve Consumers
summary: Sanofi has partnered with Accenture Interactive to develop UWell, a new healthcare platform designed to enhance the consumer experience. UWell aims to simplify health management, provide easy access to information and insights, and offer personalized services. Initially launching in Italy, the platform will allow users to upload health data, access doctor and pharmacy services, and receive educational health advice.

title: Judge approves a $142-million settlement over the Wells Fargo accounts scandal, calling it 'rough justice'
summary: A U.S. District Judge has approved a $142-million class-action settlement for Wells Fargo customers affected by the unauthorized accounts scandal. The settlement aims to compensate account holders who had accounts created without their consent, with an average payout of $35 per customer. Despite objections and acknowledgments of its imperfections, the judge deemed it "rough justice" and a better alternative than prolonged litigation, while some consumers plan to appeal the decision.

title: Assurant adding 300 jobs to bulk up tech center in Gwinnett
summary: Assurant, a $6 billion-a-year insurance company, is adding 300 jobs to expand its technology arm in Gwinnett County, Georgia. This expansion involves a new tech support center in Duluth, catering to the growing demand for device insurance and support for smart devices. The company plans to recruit primarily from the local Gwinnett County and metro Atlanta talent pool for positions including technical support analysts, supervisors, and trainers.

title: Sector Watch: Good times for American growth
summary: Despite trade tensions, rising interest rates, and stock market volatility, U.S. stocks are showing continued optimism driven by strong economic growth, lower taxes, and declining unemployment. Fund managers Tony Genua of AGF Investments Inc. and Noah Blackstein of 1832 Asset Management LP are identifying investment opportunities in innovative companies across technology, financial services, and healthcare sectors, focusing on high-growth potential and sustainable expansion. Both managers emphasize active portfolio management and selective stock-picking, with a focus on market leaders and disruptive technologies while managing risk through diversification and profit-taking.

title: Short Seller Goes Long on Snap Stock
summary: Notorious short seller Andrew Left of Citron Research has gone long on Snap Inc (SNAP) stock, arguing that the market's extreme negativity and high short interest create a compelling bullish case. Despite a 45% year-to-date decline due to app redesign issues and negative celebrity reviews, Left believes even a lack of further bad news could be a catalyst. He suggests Snap is undervalued compared to peers like Facebook regarding daily active users and could be a buyout target for companies like Alphabet or Apple.

title: Apple opens Health Records API to developers
summary: Apple has released a Health Records API for developers and researchers, enabling the creation of apps that utilize health record data for managing medications, nutrition, and diseases. This initiative allows patients from over 500 hospitals and clinics to consolidate their medical information and share it securely with trusted applications. The API aims to provide consumers with a more holistic view of their health and facilitate more comprehensive medical research.

title: Apple says big changes to the Stocks app are coming
summary: Apple Inc. announced significant updates to its Stocks app for iPhone during its WWDC keynote. The revamped app will integrate Apple News stories and feature more prominent charts, indicating Apple's focus on enhancing the investor experience. This move highlights the company's interest in its investor users, even without direct shareholder-return announcements.

title: 12 American Companies That Control Tech
summary: This article lists and details the 12 American companies that dominate the tech sector, focusing on their market capitalization, revenue, net income, and number of employees as of May 2018. It highlights the race among top tech companies to reach a $1 trillion valuation and discusses how many of these giants established their market positions by creating innovative, industry-standard products. The analysis also touches on their current strategies and future outlook, particularly regarding emerging technologies like AI, blockchain, and autonomous driving.

title: Lennox iComfort Smart Thermostats Now Compatible With Apple HomeKit
summary: Lennox announced that its iComfort S30 and E30 Smart Thermostats are now compatible with Apple HomeKit, allowing users to control their systems via iOS devices, the Home app, or Siri. This integration enhances security and provides remote access to heating and cooling. The iComfort S30 is also notable as the first communicating thermostat to offer HomeKit compatibility, actively monitoring home conditions and communicating issues to service providers.

title: Procore introduces fully redesigned iOS app for iPhone and iPad
summary: Procore Technologies Inc. has released a completely redesigned iOS app for iPhone and iPad, optimizing the mobile experience for its construction cloud-based applications. The updated app includes new features like drag and drop functionality for photos and documents, multi-tasking support, and redesigned tools, aiming to simplify technology use for construction professionals. Procore has also become an Apple Mobility Partner, offering customized iOS device bundles to its 2.5 million users.

title: Evolution of the Digital Document: Celebrating Adobe Acrobat’s 25th Anniversary
summary: This article celebrates the 25th anniversary of Adobe Acrobat and the Portable Document Format (PDF), highlighting its origins in Dr. John Warnock's "The Camelot Project" white paper from 1990. It details how Acrobat revolutionized document exchange, achieving ubiquity with 200 billion PDFs opened annually and millions of users. The piece also touches on continuous innovations, including Adobe Sensei and Adobe Scan, and the evolving definition of a document envisioned by Warnock.

title: Apple to Netflix: We see your two Obamas and raise you an Oprah
summary: Apple has signed a multi-year content partnership deal with Oprah Winfrey, marking a significant move for the tech giant's media ambitions. This deal follows similar efforts by other tech companies like Netflix and Facebook to secure top talent and original programming. Oprah will create original content for Apple's upcoming platform while remaining CEO of OWN, showcasing the increasing competition for content among tech companies.

title: Why Did Google Remove Uber Booking From Maps?
summary: Google has quietly removed the ability to book Uber rides directly from its Google Maps app on Android. While users can still see price ranges for ride-hailing services, they must now open the Uber app separately to book a ride. This move follows a similar removal of the feature from Google Maps on iOS last summer, effectively demoting Uber's integrated status to that of other ride-hailing competitors.

title: Why Spotify Technology SA Stock Looks Like a Bubble
summary: This article argues that Spotify Technology SA (NYSE: SPOT) stock appears to be a bubble, primarily due to its lack of original content, which is seen as Netflix's key differentiator. The author believes Spotify is a commoditized business susceptible to price wars and that even in a best-case scenario of subscriber growth, its stock is overvalued. The analysis concludes that while Spotify could reach 200 million users, its present-day valuation of over $170 per share is not justified.

title: The Supreme Court Will Decide If Apple's App Store Is a Monopoly
summary: The Supreme Court is set to hear Apple Inc. v. Pepper, a case that questions whether Apple's App Store constitutes a monopoly due to its 30 percent commission fee and exclusive distribution model for iOS apps. The outcome could significantly impact antitrust legal precedent and have wide-reaching implications for consumers and other tech companies like Amazon. Central to the case is the 1977 Illinois Brick Doctrine, which Apple argues shields it from consumer lawsuits as developers, not Apple, are the direct sellers of apps.

title: What Happened to Apple’s AirPower Wireless Charger?
summary: Apple's AirPower wireless charger, unveiled in September 2017, has faced significant technical delays, pushing its tentative release to September after an internal delay from June. The company is reportedly grappling with issues such as overheating and ensuring devices can be charged anywhere on the pad, aiming to differentiate it from competitors. If successful, the AirPower is expected to be announced at the annual iPhone unveiling.

title: ETF of the Week: Paul Tudor Jones' Goldman Sachs 'JUST' ETF
summary: This article features the Goldman Sachs JUST U.S. Large Cap Equity ETF (JUST), an exchange-traded fund based on Paul Tudor Jones' nonprofit foundation, Just Capital. The ETF focuses on socially responsible investing by selecting companies engaging in "just business behavior" based on 85 ESG metrics. It aims to provide attractive returns by investing in socially conscious companies, which historically exhibit better financial performance and lower volatility.

title: Charting a market divergence, Dow industrials violate 50-day average
summary: U.S. stocks were down on Thursday due to trade tensions and a Supreme Court ruling on Internet sales tax. This pressure led the Dow Jones Industrial Average to fall below its 50-day moving average, which is currently at 24,859. In contrast, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite showed greater resilience.

title: Charting a market divergence, Dow industrials violate 50-day average
summary: On Thursday, U.S. stocks, particularly the Dow Jones Industrial Average, were under pressure due to trade tensions and a Supreme Court ruling on Internet sales tax. The Dow Industrials violated its 50-day moving average, which stands at 24,859. In contrast, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite demonstrated greater resilience amidst the prevailing market conditions.

title: PayPal to Buy Hyperwallet for $400 Million
summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. has agreed to acquire financial-technology company Hyperwallet Systems Inc. for approximately $400 million, a move aimed at enhancing its offerings for managing online and mobile payments for businesses. Hyperwallet helps individuals and small firms receive payments in over 200 countries, and this acquisition is expected to bolster PayPal's growth, especially in the booming online marketplace sector, by connecting both buyers and sellers. The deal follows PayPal's earlier $2.2 billion acquisition of iZettle AB and aligns with its strategy to spend between $1 billion and $3 billion annually on acquisitions.

title: Here’s what could happen to Apple’s average selling prices in the next iPhone cycle
summary: This article discusses the potential trajectory of Apple's average selling prices (ASPs) for its iPhones in the upcoming product cycle. It highlights that while Apple has faced challenges meeting iPhone unit sales expectations, the company has successfully encouraged consumers to pay more for its devices. The future pricing strategy for the next iPhone launch will be crucial in determining if this trend of increasing ASPs continues.

title: Medtronic, Abbott push new diabetes monitoring systems
summary: Medtronic and Abbott Laboratories are promoting their new continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems to help diabetics manage blood-sugar levels more effectively. Abbott's FreeStyle Libre offers cost-effective monitoring, while Medtronic's Sugar.IQ app and Guardian Connect sensor utilize AI for predictive alerts. Both companies highlighted their systems' benefits at the American Diabetes Association annual meeting, addressing the rising costs and challenges of diabetes management.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook has been monitoring his phone usage — and he’s ‘slashing’ this
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook has been monitoring his own iPhone usage using a new feature called Screen Time, which will be part of the upcoming iOS 12. Cook discovered he was receiving an overwhelming number of notifications and has significantly reduced them. This new feature aims to help users understand and manage their device usage, addressing concerns about potential overuse and addiction to smartphones, which was also highlighted by major Apple shareholders earlier in the year.

title: Apple, Samsung settle U.S. patent dispute
summary: Apple Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. have settled their seven-year patent dispute regarding Apple's allegations of design copying. While terms were not disclosed, the settlement follows a U.S. jury awarding Apple $539 million in May, partially offsetting previous payments by Samsung. The case, though not impacting the companies' bottom lines financially, has had significant implications for U.S. patent law.

title: Sands Cotai Central ‘phase four’ adds Apple Store
summary: Sands China Ltd has announced the launch of "phase four" of its Sands Cotai Central resort, adding nearly 100,000 square feet of retail space and over 25 stores, including an Apple Store. This expansion comes as the company plans to rebrand and partly redevelop the property as the "Londoner Macao." The investment is part of a larger US$1.1 billion refurbishment across its Macau properties.

title: Apple gets second supplier for OLED iPhone screens
summary: Apple is securing a second supplier, LG Display, for OLED screens used in its high-end iPhones, diversifying away from Samsung and aiming to reduce manufacturing costs. This move is expected to give Apple more leverage in price negotiations and help LG Display by providing a new revenue stream as it battles declining LCD prices. Analysts believe this will accelerate broader adoption of OLED screens due to increased supply and lower pricing.

title: Apple Concealed Battery Defects with Code Hidden in iOS Updates to Throttle Device Performance, Class Action Alleges
