summary: A class action lawsuit alleges that Apple concealed battery defects in its devices and intentionally throttled performance through hidden code in iOS updates. The suit claims Apple misrepresented the cause of unexpected device shutdowns, attributing them to "ambient air" exposure rather than a fundamental mismatch between hardware capabilities and the demands of its evolving operating system. The lawsuit argues that Apple's actions were driven by monetary motives, forcing consumers to upgrade or purchase new devices because their existing ones were rendered less effective.

title: IBM, Aramark partner to secure connected medical device ecosystem
summary: IBM and Aramark have formed a partnership to enhance the maintenance and security of connected medical devices in healthcare. They plan to use predictive analytics to identify cyber threats, device malfunctions, and maintenance needs before they impact patient care. This collaboration aims to optimize, secure, and support medical device ecosystems, ultimately improving patient health and operational efficiency for healthcare providers.

title: Intel co-founder Gordon Moore reflects on company’s 50-year history
summary: Intel co-founder Gordon Moore reflects on the company's 50-year history in an interview. He discusses the origin of Intel's name, its location in Santa Clara, and his mindset during his tenure as CEO. The article notes that Intel is celebrating its 50th anniversary on July 18, 2018.

title: Apple in early phases of business alliance with Hewlett Packard Enterprise
summary: Apple appears to be expanding its enterprise operations through a new alliance with Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), indicated by a job advertisement for an "Enterprise Strategic Partner Manager" for HPE. This potential partnership would add HPE to Apple's existing list of enterprise collaborators, further strengthening its business solutions offerings. Apple has previously partnered with companies like IBM, Cisco, and SAP to enhance its enterprise services and develop specialized solutions like iOS apps for businesses.

title: Sonos Beam Wireless Soundbar Review
summary: This review covers the Sonos Beam wireless soundbar, highlighting its affordable price, Alexa voice control, and integration with other Sonos speakers. The article details its design, internal technology, and performance for both TV sound and music, positioning it as a significant product for Sonos and the audio industry. It also discusses the Beam's smart features, such as HDMI ARC connectivity for TV control and Alexa integration for various functions.

title: Philo Banks $40 Million in Funding Led by AMC Networks, Discovery, Viacom
summary: Philo, a low-cost live-streaming TV provider, has secured $40 million in Series C funding led by existing investors AMC Networks, Discovery, and Viacom, bringing its total funding to over $90 million. The company plans to use the new capital primarily for marketing and expanding its service to Apple TV and Amazon Fire TV, targeting "cover-nevers" and cord-cutters with its affordable entertainment-focused bundles. Philo aims to attract new consumers to the pay-TV ecosystem by omitting sports and broadcast TV to keep costs low.

title: Apple launches new clean energy fund in China
summary: Apple announced the launch of a new first-of-its-kind investment fund in China to connect suppliers with renewable energy sources. Apple and 10 initial suppliers will jointly invest nearly $300 million over the next four years into the China Clean Energy Fund, aiming to develop more than 1 gigawatt of renewable energy in China. This initiative is part of Apple's broader commitment to address climate change and transition its supply chain to 100 percent renewable energy.

title: Chipmaker Broadcom inks $19 billion deal to buy software company CA
summary: Broadcom Inc. announced an $18.9 billion deal to acquire U.S. business software company CA Inc., marking a significant venture beyond its core semiconductor business. This acquisition comes after U.S. President Donald Trump blocked Broadcom's hostile bid for Qualcomm, and the deal has surprised Wall Street due to the apparent lack of immediate synergies between the chipmaker and the mainframe software specialist. Broadcom aims to capitalize on CA's recurring revenue and mission-critical technology, financing the deal with cash and $18 billion in new debt.

title: Apple has announced a $300 million green energy fund in China
summary: Apple Inc. is launching a $300 million clean energy fund in China, collaborating with its suppliers to invest in renewable energy projects capable of powering nearly 1 million homes. This initiative, made with 10 suppliers over four years, aligns with China's focus on pollution reduction and Apple's broader push into renewable power. The announcement comes amidst trade tensions between the U.S. and China, with Apple CEO Tim Cook previously advocating for calm.

title: Adobe to launch Photoshop for iPad in strategy shift
summary: Adobe is planning to launch a full version of its Photoshop app for Apple's iPad, marking a strategic shift to expand its Creative Cloud services across multiple devices and boost subscription sales. This move aims to cater to professionals and hobbyists who increasingly use mobile devices, and it also positions Adobe to better compete with tablet-optimized software. The new app is expected to be unveiled in October and released in 2019, leveraging the increased power of newer iPad models.

title: Berkshire Hathaway shares jump after Warren Buffett loosens policy on stock buybacks
summary: Berkshire Hathaway's board eliminated a restriction on its ability to buy back its own stock, allowing Chairman Warren Buffett and Vice Chairman Charlie Munger to authorize buybacks when the price is "below Berkshire's intrinsic value." This change addresses pressure on Buffett to deploy the conglomerate's substantial cash reserves and led to a jump in Berkshire's Class B shares. The company stated it would not buy back stock under the new policy until after releasing second-quarter results and will maintain at least $20 billion in cash and equivalents.

title: Philadelphia Phillies & Aramark Launch Apple Business Chat at Citizens Bank Park
summary: The Philadelphia Phillies and Aramark have launched Apple Business Chat at Citizens Bank Park, allowing fans to use the Messages app to communicate with customer service, ask questions, and request assistance. This initiative aims to enhance the fan experience by providing convenient and real-time support.

title: GLW on the rise: Corning stock price surge adds $3 billion in market value
summary: Corning (GLW) stock surged 11% on Wednesday, adding $3 billion to its market value, driven by an optimistic Q2 earnings outlook. The company expects to meet increased demand, grow sales, and improve profitability, projecting $11.3 billion in full-year 2018 sales. Two Wall Street firms raised their price targets for Corning stock following the positive news.

title: Qualcomm ends $44 billion NXP bid after failing to win China approval
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has terminated its $44 billion bid to acquire NXP Semiconductors after failing to secure approval from Chinese regulators, a casualty of escalating Sino-U.S. trade tensions. The collapse of the deal, which would have been the largest semiconductor takeover globally, was publicly confirmed by both companies. Qualcomm will now proceed with a $30 billion share buyback and pay NXP a $2 billion termination fee.

title: Life Fitness Reports 1 Percent Q2 Increase, Amid Spinoff and Planet Fitness Decision
summary: Life Fitness reported a 1 percent Q2 revenue increase to $252.2 million but saw a decrease in operating earnings. This comes as the company prepares for a spinoff from Brunswick Corp. by Q1 2019 and adjusts to Planet Fitness's decision to broaden equipment choices for franchisees, ending Life Fitness's exclusive vendor status.

title: Uber Names Ex-Northrop CEO Ronald Sugar as Chairman After Yearlong Hunt
summary: Uber Technologies Inc. has appointed Ronald Sugar, former Northrop Grumman CEO and current director at Apple and Amgen, as the independent chairman of its board. This move follows a year-long search and is part of an effort to revamp the company's corporate governance after the resignation of Travis Kalanick. Sugar's appointment is seen as a victory for CEO Dara Khosrowshahi as Uber prepares for an initial public offering expected late next year.

title: Tech Pullback Signals Buying Opportunity
summary: Despite a broader tech sector pullback, ETF investors are showing confidence in semiconductor stocks, funneling significant money into semiconductor ETFs. This trend is driven by strong earnings from chipmakers and continued demand fueled by companies like Apple, suggesting a potential buying opportunity in the sector even as other tech giants face headwinds. The divergent performance indicates a belief in the resilience of semiconductor companies amidst overall market volatility.

title: Apple Is Worth $1,000,000,000,000. Two Decades Ago, It Was Almost Bankrupt. (Published 2018)
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a market capitalization of over $1 trillion on Thursday, marking a remarkable turnaround from near-bankruptcy two decades prior. The article credits its ascent to rapid innovation, smash-hit products like the iPod and iPhone, and a sophisticated supply chain, initially spearheaded by Steve Jobs and continued by Timothy D. Cook. Despite its success, Apple faces challenges related to global manufacturing, continued growth, and the need for new hit products beyond the aging iPhone.

title: How Apple got to $1T market value
summary: Apple Inc. became the first U.S.-based company with a market value of $1 trillion, four decades after its inception. The article details Apple's journey from its co-founding by Steve Jobs to its current status as a tech giant, highlighting key products like the iPod and iPhone, Jobs's return to save the company, and Tim Cook's leadership in expanding its market value and business strategies. It also touches on future challenges and opportunities for Apple, particularly concerning the iPhone and new services.

title: Apple's first hires for its health clinics show how it's thinking differently about health care
summary: Apple's AC Wellness, a new subsidiary for employee health, has rapidly hired over 40 professionals, focusing on "well care" specialists like nutritionists and exercise coaches rather than just doctors. This approach emphasizes preventative health and patient experience over traditional fee-for-service primary care, aligning with a broader industry shift towards value-based care. The initiative highlights Apple's unique strategy in healthcare, with a significant number of hires from health-tech startups and a focus on holistic wellness.

title: Apple hangs onto its historic $1 trillion market cap
summary: Apple officially hit a $1 trillion market capitalization on Thursday, becoming the first publicly traded U.S. company to reach this milestone and maintaining it by market close. The stock gained 2.92 percent after a strong fiscal third-quarter earnings report, closing at $207.39 and surpassing the $1 trillion mark based on adjusted share counts. Analysts credit Apple's powerful ecosystem and growing software and services revenue for driving this valuation, with the stock having gained 22 percent in 2018.

title: From Macs to iPods and apps: how Apple revolutionised technology
summary: This article chronicles Apple's 42-year history, highlighting its revolutionary impact on technology through products like the Apple II, Macintosh, iPod, and iPhone. It explains how Apple, not always the first to innovate, excels at bringing concepts to the mainstream and has built an "app economy." The company's evolution from a computer manufacturer to a consumer electronics designer, eventually becoming a trillion-dollar company, is also detailed.

title: How Apple became the first American publicly traded company to reach $1 trillion valuation
summary: Apple became the first American publicly traded company to reach a $1 trillion valuation on August 2, 2018, with shares hitting $207.05. This milestone reflects the company's strong performance, particularly with iPhone sales and a significant increase in software and services revenue. The article highlights Apple's journey from a garage startup to a global tech giant, emphasizing the visions of co-founder Steve Jobs and current CEO Tim Cook.

title: Facebook & The Perils Of Market Cap ETFs
summary: This article discusses the impact of FAANG stocks on market-cap-weighted ETFs, highlighting how their significant movements can disproportionately affect portfolio returns. It explores alternative weighting schemes like equal weighting as a way for passive investors to manage single-stock risk, comparing the performance of ETFs like SPY and RSP. The piece emphasizes the flexibility and diverse choices available within the ETF market for investors to tailor their exposure.

title: Is Apple now too big to fail?
summary: This article discusses Apple Inc. reaching a $1 trillion valuation, highlighting the immense power accumulated by a few tech giants and the lack of scrutiny they face. It draws parallels to the "too big to fail" concept from the banking crisis, arguing that the collapse of these tech companies would have disastrous economic and social consequences. The author urges for greater accountability through legal and economic means.

title: From U.S. Steel’s $1 billion market cap to Apple’s $1 trillion: a brief history of valuation milestones
summary: This article marks Apple Inc.'s achievement of a $1 trillion market capitalization, making it the first U.S.-listed company to reach this milestone. It contextualizes this event against historical valuation achievements, starting from U.S. Steel's $1 billion market cap. The piece highlights Apple's journey to this significant financial landmark.

title: Jobs, Wozniak -- and Wayne? What happened to the third co-founder of Apple
summary: This article details the story of Ronald Wayne, the often-forgotten third co-founder of Apple, who signed the company's founding document alongside Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak. Wayne sold his 10% share for $2,300 just 12 days after signing the contract, a share that would be worth approximately $100 billion today. Despite this, Wayne lives modestly and expresses no major regrets, though he did sell the original founding document for a mere $500, which later auctioned for $1.3 million.

title: Speaker maker Sonos goes public, but acknowledges vulnerability
summary: Speaker maker Sonos Inc. went public with its stock surging 33% above its IPO price, achieving an implied market value of $1.95bn. Despite a successful market debut, the company acknowledges a vulnerability stemming from its reliance on tech giants like Amazon, Apple, and Google, who could potentially enter the hardware market or alter their integration terms, impacting Sonos's future profitability.

title: Apple officially becomes first U.S. company with $1 trillion market cap
summary: Apple Inc. officially became the first U.S. publicly listed company to achieve a $1 trillion market capitalization on Thursday, August 2, 2018. Apple shares closed at $207.39, surpassing the $207.04 threshold required, based on its 4.83 billion shares as of July 20. The company beat out Amazon to reach this financial milestone.

title: Woburn's Skyworks to buy Oregon circuit-maker for up to $425M
summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc., a Woburn-based semiconductor manufacturer, is acquiring an Oregon circuit-maker for up to $425 million. This acquisition aims to expand Skyworks' presence in the emerging voice computing market. The company is already one of Massachusetts' most valuable tech firms, partly due to its strong relationship with Apple.

title: Apple chip supplier TSMC resumes production after WannaCry attack
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (TSMC) announced it has fully resumed production after a WannaCry ransomware attack temporarily shut down some of its computer systems. The incident, caused by an "operational error" during installation, is expected to cause a 2% impact on its third-quarter revenue, down from an initial 3% estimate, but is not expected to affect its annual revenue growth forecast. TSMC assured that neither its data manufacturing base nor client information were compromised.

title: Exclusive: Spotify CEO Daniel Ek on Apple, Facebook, Netflix--and the future
summary: Spotify CEO Daniel Ek discusses his vision for the music industry and the challenges he faced in gaining trust. He emphasizes the collaborative nature of innovation at Spotify, downplaying his individual role. The interview provides an intimate look into Ek's perspectives on the company and the broader music business dynamics.

title: Elon Musk Says Tesla May Go Private, and Its Stock Soars (Published 2018)
summary: Elon Musk announced via Twitter that he was considering taking Tesla private at $420 per share, claiming "Funding secured." This cryptic declaration led to a frenzy in the stock market, especially after Tesla's stock had already risen due to reports of a sizable Saudi Arabian stake in the company. Investors were left to decipher the implications of Musk's tweet and its relationship to other news.
authors: Neal E. Boudette and Matt Phillips

title: TSMC Chip Maker Blames WannaCry Malware for Production Halt
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) experienced a production halt across several chip-fabrication factories due to a variant of the WannaCry malware. The cyberattack, which was not a direct hack but rather an infection spread through tainted software installed by a supplier, is expected to impact revenue by approximately $256 million and cause shipment delays. Although full operations have resumed, the incident raises concerns, especially for key customers like Apple, for whom TSMC is a sole supplier of SoC components.

title: Amex Pulls Ads From Australia’s Sky News After Interview With Far-Right Extremist
summary: American Express has withdrawn its advertising from Australia's Sky News channel following an interview broadcast with far-right extremist Blair Cottrell. Sky News, controlled by Rupert Murdoch's News Corp., is taking measures, including suspending the show that aired the interview and restructuring management, after facing widespread criticism and an activist campaign. Other tech platforms like Apple, Facebook, YouTube, and Spotify have also recently cracked down on hate speech by removing content from figures like Alex Jones.

title: 3 Key Factors That Led to Apple Becoming the World’s First Trillion-Dollar Company
summary: This article examines three key factors contributing to Apple's success in becoming the world's first trillion-dollar company. These factors include breaking down customer segment barriers through inclusive marketing, creating a strong brand culture, and consistently avoiding price wars by focusing on value proposition and user experience.

title: Apple’s $1 trillion value doesn’t mean it’s the ‘biggest’ company
summary: This article argues that market capitalization alone is an insufficient measure of a company's "bigness," especially in today's economy. While Apple reached a $1 trillion valuation, it is relatively light on employees and tangible assets compared to older industrial giants. The author compares Apple and Facebook to companies like Kroger and Walmart to illustrate how Wall Street values lean, employee-light companies over traditional job creators.

title: Apple’s Declaratory Judgment Backfires, Turns Into $145.1M Damages Verdict Wi-LAN
summary: A jury in the Southern District of California awarded Canadian IP licensing firm Wi-LAN $145.1 million in damages in a patent infringement case against Apple Inc. The case stemmed from Apple's 2014 complaint seeking a declaratory judgment of invalidity on Wi-LAN's patents, which Apple was found to have infringed upon regarding 4G LTE-compliant devices. Wi-LAN alleged that Apple incorporated its wireless technologies despite dismissing Wi-LAN's vision years prior to Apple developing its own 4G LTE devices.

title: Agora é oficial: Bradesco e Banco do Brasil entram no Apple Pay!
summary: Bradesco and Banco do Brasil have officially joined Apple Pay, allowing their Visa credit cards (and debit cards for BB) to be used with the service. This expansion comes after much anticipation and removes the exclusivity previously held by Itaú. The article expresses hope for more banks and fintechs to adopt Apple Pay soon.

title: When Can a Company Be Liable for Its Vendor’s Copyright or Patent Infringement?: Hollywood Studios’ IP Headache
summary: This article analyzes the Rearden LLC v. The Walt Disney Company case, where Hollywood studios faced copyright and patent infringement claims due to intellectual property issues with a vendor's technology. It explores the complexities of vicarious and contributory liability for copyright infringement and limitations on direct patent infringement when a company uses an external vendor. The case highlights crucial considerations for companies engaging with vendors regarding potential intellectual property liabilities.

title: Apple Is Planning a New Low-Cost MacBook, Pro-Focused Mac Mini
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly planning to release a new low-cost laptop and an upgraded, professional-focused Mac mini desktop later in the year. These new products aim to boost Mac sales, which have been lagging, with the laptop featuring a higher-resolution "Retina" display and a design similar to the MacBook Air but with thinner bezels.

title: Eli Lilly CEO: Why Consumers Are Key to Bringing Down Health Care Costs
summary: Eli Lilly CEO David Ricks argues that the U.S. healthcare system, designed for a different era, needs dramatic change to address modern chronic illnesses and unsustainable spending. Ricks proposes three key reforms: digitizing everything, empowering consumers with transparency, and paying for value. He emphasizes the role of consumers and risk-based insurance programs in driving these changes and calls for reforms to government regulations that hinder value-based pricing.

title: Apple to Embrace iPhone X Design With New Colors, Bigger Screens
summary: Apple plans to launch three new iPhones with the iPhone X's edge-to-edge screen design, offering a wider range of prices, features, and sizes. This strategy aims to broaden the appeal of their smartphone lineup. New iPad Pros and Apple Watches with larger screens are also anticipated.

title: Which Companies Have The Highest Gross Profit Per Employee?
summary: This article ranks S&P 500 companies by Gross Profit Per Employee (GPPE) for 2017 to assess human capital efficiency after product costs. It highlights that the Energy sector has the highest GPPE, with Financial and Healthcare companies also performing strongly. The analysis also notes an improved ranking for Technology companies like Facebook and Apple in GPPE compared to Revenue Per Employee, due to their high gross margins.

title: 10 Great Tech ETFs to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies 10 excellent technology-focused ETFs for investors, ranging from broad-based funds to those specializing in specific tech subsectors like cybersecurity, cloud computing, and robotics. It highlights the significant role of the information technology segment within the S&P 500 and provides details on each ETF's holdings, expense ratios, and investment focus.

title: China’s Belt and Road Initiative Opens Up Unprecedented Opportunities - U.S. Global Investors - Commentaries
summary: This article contrasts the economic policies of the U.S. and China, highlighting China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) as a significant global infrastructure and trade endeavor. It details how the BRI is expanding China's influence, particularly in Africa and Eastern Europe, and discusses the implications for commodities markets. The author also analyzes China's economic strength despite trade tensions and investor interest in Chinese equities.
authors: Frank Holmes of U.S. Global Investors

title: Charter's Spectrum Mobile goes full market
summary: Charter Communications is launching Spectrum Mobile nationwide after a soft launch, bundling the service with broadband and utilizing an MVNO agreement with Verizon Wireless. The service now supports several Apple iPhone models in addition to Android devices and offers two pricing tiers: Unlimited and By the Gig. This move follows Comcast's similar Xfinity Mobile strategy, with both companies forming a mobile operating platform deal.

title: Apple's New iPhones Will Boost Market Share: Canaccord
summary: Canaccord Genuity analyst Michael Walkley predicts that Apple's launch of three new iPhones will significantly boost the company's global premium smartphone market share and drive strong replacement sales and earnings. Walkley upgraded his earnings per share estimates for 2018 and 2019, raising his price target for Apple stock to $250, primarily due to the new iPhone models and anticipated growth in services revenue. This projected growth is expected to generate additional cash flow for shareholder returns and solidify Apple's market position.

title: Cisco a Better Bet Than Apple, Amazon, Tech Giants: Piper Jaffray
summary: Piper Jaffray's Chief Market Technician Craig Johnson argues that Cisco Systems (CSCO) is a better investment than tech giants like Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN), which may need a pullback. Johnson suggests buying Cisco due to its recent breakout from a consolidation range, anticipating a run to the $51-$52 range. He views Cisco as a value play, currently trading under $50 with a modest market capitalization.

title: Amazon becomes the second US company to achieve a $1 trillion valuation
summary: Amazon briefly reached a $1 trillion public valuation, becoming the second US company to do so after Apple. The article highlights Amazon's rapid growth through retail and cloud computing, contrasting its 21-year journey to the milestone with Apple's 38 years. Analysts attribute Amazon's dynamic growth to its diversification and the success of its cloud services, which are its main profit driver, potentially positioning it to overtake Apple's market valuation.

title: Kopilot İndir - Ücretsiz İndir
summary: The Kopilot application, offered by Turkcell, provides solutions to simplify driving by connecting to a device in the car's OBD service socket. It enables users to track vehicle details, review travel information like distance, fuel consumption, and driving scores, and receive notifications for various events. The app is available for both Android and iOS devices, offering features such as parental and valet modes.

title: Top SUNY Upstate official leaves to take job in Florida
summary: Dr. Ramesh Sachdeva, a senior vice president at SUNY Medical University, has left his position to join the Nicklaus Children's Health System in Miami. Hired two years prior to develop a strategic plan for Upstate, Sachdeva was involved in projects to connect faculty with primary care providers in Central New York. His departure leads to a review of Upstate's Office of Strategic Affairs reporting structure, which Sachdeva developed.

title: Expect big changes with Apple’s iOS 12
summary: Apple's iOS 12 update, expected to roll out soon, promises significant enhancements in speed, responsiveness, and user experience for iPhones and iPads. New features include faster performance, improved photo search, group FaceTime with up to 32 people, Memoji, and "Screen Time" to help users manage device addiction. The update also emphasizes user privacy with built-in encryption and tracking prevention in Safari.

title: Unleash Creativity with Logitech Crayon Exclusively for New 9.7-inch iPad
summary: Logitech announced that its Crayon digital pencil, previously only for schools, will be available for the new 9.7-inch iPad starting September 12 at Apple Retail. The Logitech Crayon offers precise and comfortable writing with Apple Pencil technology, making it suitable for creativity, note-taking, and work markup using various iPad apps. It will retail for $69.99, with a special price of $49.99 for education institutions in the U.S. and Canada.

title: Third Point Seeks to Oust Campbell Soup’s Board (Published 2018)
summary: Third Point, an investment firm led by Daniel S. Loeb, is attempting to replace the entire board of the Campbell Soup Company by nominating 12 candidates. This aggressive move comes as Campbell Soup has faced stagnant sales and declining popularity of its main products. The bid aims to pressure the company into a sale, with one of the nominees being a scion of Campbell's controlling family who has been critical of the company's current direction.

title: Apple says Trump's China tariffs are going to hurt the company
summary: Apple has stated that President Trump's proposed tariffs on $200 billion worth of Chinese goods would impact a wide range of its products, including the Apple Watch, AirPods, and various adapters, leading to increased prices for consumers. The company urged the U.S. trade representative to reconsider these measures, while President Trump suggested Apple should move production to the United States to avoid tariffs. Despite previous assurances from CEO Tim Cook that tariffs wouldn't affect the iPhone, Apple shares declined following the news.

title: 5 lessons of the AI imperative, from Netflix to Spotify
summary: This article discusses the increasing importance of artificial intelligence and algorithms in business, using Netflix and Spotify as key examples. It highlights how companies are committing to AI to enhance efficiency, customer experience, and competitive advantage. The author emphasizes that understanding and leveraging AI is crucial for businesses across various sectors to thrive in the current technological landscape.

title: Apple unveils big, $1,099 iPhone XS Max and pitches upgraded Apple Watch as health device
summary: Apple unveiled three new iPhones, including the high-end iPhone Xs Max starting at $1,099, and a cheaper iPhone Xr ($749) to broaden its market reach. The company also introduced an upgraded Apple Watch Series 4 with larger screens and new health capabilities, such as an ECG monitor and fall detection, positioning it as an "intelligent guardian of your health." This strategy aims to raise average prices and expand the active user base to support sales of accessories and digital services.

title: Does iPhone XR have a headphone jack? New 2018 phones could ditch the headphone adapter entirely
summary: Apple's new iPhone XR is not expected to include a headphone adapter, following the company's previous move to remove the headphone jack with the iPhone 7. This decision is anticipated to push users towards Bluetooth headphones, and reports suggest the free adapter previously provided will no longer be included. A Barclays analyst report confirms Cirrus Logic, a supplier for the adapter, will cease providing the dongle.

title: Amazon's rising stock gives Jeff Bezos 'financial muscle' in outer space equal to whole countries
summary: Morgan Stanley highlights Jeff Bezos' growing financial power through his Amazon shares, which he dedicates significantly to his space company, Blue Origin. His substantial wealth gives Blue Origin a unique advantage in the burgeoning space industry, potentially accelerating its development and competition with companies like SpaceX. Morgan Stanley advises investors to recognize Bezos' increasing influence in the space economy.

title: Time Magazine Is Bought by Marc Benioff, Salesforce Billionaire (Published 2018)
summary: Marc Benioff, the co-founder of Salesforce, and his wife, Lynne, have agreed to purchase Time magazine from Meredith Corporation for $190 million. This acquisition marks another instance of a tech billionaire investing in an iconic media brand, following Jeff Bezos's purchase of The Washington Post and Laurene Powell Jobs's stake in The Atlantic. The Benioffs emerged as the preferred buyers due to their commitment to journalistic integrity amidst the struggles faced by the magazine industry.

title: Amazon to Launch 8 New Alexa-Powered Devices: Report
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) is reportedly planning to launch at least eight new Alexa-powered smart home devices, including a voice-controlled microwave oven, an amplifier, and an in-car gadget. This expansion is expected to intensify competition with rivals like Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft in the smart speaker market, and marks Amazon's significant entry into the home appliance space. The move aligns with CEO Jeff Bezos's vision of integrating Alexa more deeply into everyday life, further expanding Amazon's presence in the smart home ecosystem.

title: ‘We Do Not Support This Conduct.’ PayPal Joins the Growing Number of Sites to Ban InfoWars
summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. has announced it will no longer offer its services to Alex Jones' InfoWars website due to instances of promoting hate and discriminatory intolerance. This decision aligns PayPal with other major tech companies like Apple, Facebook, Google's YouTube, and Twitter, which have also banned Jones from their platforms. InfoWars claims the ban is a "political ploy designed to financially sabotage" them.

title: Garmin Watches & Activity Tracker Now Available in Nepal
summary: Oliz store has introduced Garmin Watches and activity trackers to Nepal, featuring 16 different models from Garmin's extensive inventory. The offerings include the Garmin Vivosport Activity Tracker at Rs. 23,000 and the high-end Garmin Fenix 5X Plus Sapphire Edition priced at Rs. 110,000. These smartwatches boast features like wrist-based heart rate tracking, activity tracking, and cycling functionalities, catering to fitness-conscious consumers.

title: Apple and Salesforce partner to help redefine customer experiences on iOS
summary: Apple and Salesforce announced a strategic partnership to enhance customer experiences on iOS. This collaboration will bring a redesigned Salesforce Mobile App with exclusive iOS features and new tools for developers to build native apps with a Salesforce Mobile SDK for iOS and an iOS app development course on Trailhead. The initiative aims to create powerful new mobile apps for businesses and empower developers with skills for the future.

title: Jabil, Johnson & Johnson strike ‘transformational’ deal
summary: Jabil Inc. announced a "transformational" deal to acquire 14 sites from Johnson & Johnson Medical Devices Companies, significantly expanding their 12-year partnership. This strategic collaboration is expected to generate over $1 billion in annual revenue for Jabil and is part of its initiative to diversify revenue streams. Jabil aims to add $2 billion in new business in the coming year, with a focus on healthcare, cloud services, 5G, and automotive sectors.

title: Weight Watchers' rebranding won't help save the company that built its business around fat-shaming
summary: Weight Watchers has rebranded as WW, shifting its focus from weight loss to broader "wellness that works." The article argues this rebranding is a desperate attempt to stay relevant as public perception of body image and health has changed, moving away from the "fat-shaming" business model. The author suggests that despite the new focus on holistic wellness, the company's legacy of promoting thinness makes it untrustworthy to lead the modern health movement.

title: Regions Bank branch in South Pasadena is first ‘bank of the future’ in Florida
summary: Regions Bank has opened its first "Nexus" branch in Florida in South Pasadena, designed to offer a modern, tech-focused banking experience similar to an Apple store. This new branch emphasizes personalized customer interaction through workstations instead of traditional teller lines and features video bankers and biometric access for safe deposit boxes. It represents Regions Financial's commitment to investing in growth markets like Tampa Bay, with future Nexus branches planned.

title: Secretive startup Aeva unveils a sensor box for self-driving cars
summary: Aeva, a secretive Silicon Valley startup founded by former Apple engineers, has unveiled its first product: an integrated sensor box for self-driving cars. This box combines camera, lidar, and radar sensors and uses a continuous light wave approach for lidar, allowing it to directly measure velocity. The company has raised $45 million in funding and says its sensor boxes cost "several hundred dollars," with unnamed major automakers and ride-hailing companies already incorporating them.

title: Malicious Component Found On Server Motherboards Supplied To Numerous Companies
summary: Bloomberg reports a bombshell in hardware security: Amazon and Apple allegedly found malicious chips on server motherboards manufactured by Supermicro in China for Elemental Technologies. These tiny, hard-to-spot chips, disguised as signal couplers, may be able to compromise baseboard management controllers (BMCs), providing remote access to servers. While Amazon and Apple deny the claims, the report raises significant questions about supply chain security for high-security applications.

title: Tiny Chinese Microchips Reportedly Found Hidden in Amazon, Apple Servers
summary: A Bloomberg Businessweek report claims that tiny Chinese-made microchips were found hidden in servers used by Apple, Amazon, and government contractors, originating from Super Micro Computer Inc. The chips, allegedly hidden during manufacturing in China, were discovered by Amazon in 2015 and sparked a U.S. government investigation. Both Amazon and Apple have strongly disputed Bloomberg's reporting, denying any such discoveries or investigations.

title: Compromised Supermicro chips reportedly infiltrated US
summary: A Bloomberg report claimed that Chinese government-backed actors infiltrated U.S. companies and government organizations by implanting tiny microchips in Supermicro server motherboards, creating backdoors. Amazon, Apple, and Supermicro have vehemently denied these claims. The article also touches on other cybersecurity news, including a Facebook data breach, Google's new Chrome Web Store rules, and a Telegram vulnerability.

title: Report of Chinese chip implants into servers raises alarm on Capitol Hill
summary: A report alleging Chinese chip implants in servers for companies like Amazon and Apple has caused alarm on Capitol Hill. One representative called for increased scrutiny of supply chains for sensitive electronics from China. The companies implicated, including Super Micro, have disputed the findings.

title: The big hack: How China used a tiny chip to steal data from US companies like Amazon, Apple
summary: Bloomberg reported that China allegedly used tiny malicious chips inserted into computer equipment manufactured for about 30 US companies, including Apple and Amazon, to steal technology secrets. Both Apple and Amazon denied these reports, with Apple stating they conducted rigorous internal investigations and found no evidence to support the claims. The report suggested these chips allowed attackers to create a "stealth doorway" into networks.

title: How to invest in UAE stock markets
summary: This article provides guidance on how retail investors can invest in UAE stock markets, including advice on finding brokers, available products like ETFs and REITs, and considerations for investing in local stocks. Issam Kassabieh, a senior financial analyst at Menacorp, offers insights into defensive sectors, high-growth stocks, and risk management specific to the UAE market.

title: Wall Street closes lower amid fears over trade, oil and rates – as it happened
summary: Wall Street closed lower despite an earlier rally, as fears over US-China trade tensions, rising US interest rates, and the disappearance of Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi weighed on markets. Technology stocks were particularly affected, and the article also highlights the jump in America's budget deficit and warnings about an upcoming global economic downturn. European markets also saw declines, though the FTSE 100 managed a modest recovery.

title: Tech Moves: Expedia hires Cortana design lead; Saykara adds VP of growth
summary: This article reports on significant personnel changes in the Pacific Northwest tech community. Sam Moreau, a former Microsoft design leader, has joined Expedia as VP of design operations for the Expedia Group brand team. Additionally, Saykara, a Seattle health tech company, appointed Ryan Plasch as its new vice president of growth, following a recent $5 million funding round.

title: Zimmer Biomet and Apple Collaborate to Launch Major Clinical Study Detailing Patient Experience and Improving Joint Replacement Journey
summary: Zimmer Biomet and Apple have partnered to launch a major clinical study utilizing the Zimmer Biomet mymobility™ app, running on Apple Watch and iPhone, to enhance the patient journey for knee and hip replacement surgeries. The study aims to gather evidence on the app's impact on patient outcomes and costs, enrolling up to 10,000 participants in the United States. This collaboration seeks to transform the traditional in-person care model by providing continuous support and data to both patients and surgeons.

title: Stocks fall, led by tech, as Wall Street fails to bounce back from last week's rout
summary: Wall Street struggled to recover from last week's rout as major indexes, led by tech stocks, closed lower on Monday. Investors faced "crosscurrents" including rising interest rates and inflation anxieties, which prevented the market from finding a bottom despite oversold conditions. Tech giants like Apple and Netflix saw significant declines, contributing to the broader market downturn.

title: Time to Look Under the Hood of Your Alternatives
summary: This article discusses the sudden return of market volatility in October, causing a decline in both stocks and bonds. It highlights the significant drop in FAANG stocks and emphasizes the importance for portfolio allocators to reassess the alternative investment landscape during such turbulent times. The piece focuses on examining structurally diversified alternative investments, excluding commodity, VIX, leveraged, allocation, and tactical funds.

title: ServiceNow sets plans for big expansion in Santa Clara
summary: ServiceNow is planning a significant expansion of its headquarters in Santa Clara, including leasing a new 269,000-square-foot office building expected to be completed in 2021. This expansion reflects the company's rapid growth, with a 28% increase in employee headcount globally in the past year, and is projected to accommodate over a thousand new employees in Santa Clara. The tech company has also extended leases on its existing buildings and currently occupies an additional facility on Jay Street.

title: Motorola Solutions wins $17.3M number plate scanning deal with Vic Police
summary: Motorola Solutions has secured a $17.5 million deal with Victoria Police to install number plate recognition technology in 220 highway patrol vehicles. This five-year agreement provides a cloud-based solution for real-time scanning of number plates to detect stolen and unregistered vehicles, with footage admissible as evidence. This initiative is part of a larger $43.8 million government investment aimed at enhancing road safety and officer protection across Victoria.

title: You Probably Bought Your iPhone With This Bank’s Help. Its Stock Is Worth Buying, Too
summary: Citizens Financial Group, a regional bank based in Providence, R.I., is highlighted for its exclusive financing partnership with Apple for iPhone purchases. With over half of iPhone buyers opting for installment plans, Citizens' Apple portfolio has rapidly grown to approximately $1 billion since 2015. The article suggests that the bank's stock is worth buying due to this successful and growing lending niche.

title: eBay Launches New Program for Consumers to Instantly Sell Their Smartphones
summary: eBay has launched "Instant Selling," a new program enabling consumers to quickly sell their unwanted smartphones for an eBay voucher. The program aims to offer a higher return compared to typical trade-in providers, allowing users to instantly convert unused devices into funds for new purchases on eBay. This initiative addresses the large number of Americans holding onto unused smartphones without realizing their potential value.

title: Ford's New 'Built Ford Proud' Campaign Takes Telling Shots At Silicon Valley Competitors
summary: Ford's new "Built Ford Proud" ad campaign directly challenges Silicon Valley tech and EV companies, positioning traditional manufacturing against digital innovation. The campaign, starring Bryan Cranston, uses dismissive remarks and imagery to highlight Ford's heritage of building tangible products. This strategy subtly appeals to mainstream Americans' concerns about tech giants and signals a battle for control of the automotive industry's future.

title: ‘This Is Surveillance.’ Apple CEO Tim Cook Slams Tech Rivals Over Data Collection
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook criticized tech rivals like Facebook and Google for their data collection practices, equating them to "surveillance" and prioritizing profits over user privacy. This strong rebuke came at an EU privacy conference, highlighting a growing tension between user privacy and advertising-based business models, especially after recent data breaches at Google and Facebook. Cook also called for federal privacy laws in the U.S. similar to Europe's GDPR.

title: Invesco MSCI USA ETF
summary: The Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS) tracks a U.S. large- and mid-cap equity index, excluding small-caps, making it comparable to an S&P 500 ETF. It is part of Invesco’s low-cost PureBeta lineup, offering competitive fees. The fund has attracted significant assets, but its daily trading volume is lower than some longer-established large-cap U.S. equity ETFs.

title: iShares S&P 500 Value ETF
summary: This article provides a comprehensive profile of the iShares S&P 500 Value ETF (IVE), detailing its price, dividends, expenses, holdings, and performance. It highlights IVE's focus on large-cap U.S. companies with value characteristics, emphasizing its diversification, low expense ratio, and stability benefits for long-term investors. The profile also includes ESG scores, technical analysis, and comparisons to alternative ETFs.

title: Invesco RAFI US 1000 ETF
summary: The Invesco RAFI US 1000 ETF (PRF) tracks a fundamentally-weighted index based on book value, cash flow, sales, and dividends, differentiating it from market capitalization-weighted funds. It offers exposure to large U.S. equities with significant holdings in financials and industrials/energy. The article provides detailed performance metrics, holdings analysis, expense ratios, and ESG scores for PRF, comparing it against peer ETFs in the Large Cap Blend Equities category.

title: Qualcomm Says Apple Is $7 Billion Behind in Royalty Payments
summary: Qualcomm Inc. claims Apple Inc. is $7 billion behind in royalty payments for essential patented technology used in iPhones and iPads. A Qualcomm lawyer stated in federal court that Apple is "trying to destroy our business" and that the situation represents significant financial damage. Qualcomm CEO Mollenkopf sees a chance of settlement this year.

title: Qualcomm says Apple $7 billion behind in royalty payments
summary: Qualcomm Inc. stated in a court hearing that Apple Inc. is $7 billion behind in patent royalty payments. The two companies are engaged in a series of legal disputes, with Apple accusing Qualcomm of unfair patent licensing practices and Qualcomm accusing Apple of patent infringement. Apple disputes the amount owed, arguing Qualcomm is forcing it to pay for the same patents twice, while Qualcomm maintains its practices are legal.

title: All-new MacBook Air takes flight
summary: Apple announced an all-new MacBook Air featuring a 13-inch Retina display, Touch ID, the latest processors, and an even more portable design with all-day battery life. The new model is crafted from 100 percent recycled aluminum, making it the greenest Mac ever. Apple also introduced new Radeon Pro Vega graphics options for the MacBook Pro.

title: Apple Revamps MacBook Air Laptop for First Time in Eight Years
summary: Apple Inc. has overhauled its MacBook Air and Mac mini models, marking the first major update for the MacBook Air in eight years. This comes after Mac sales reached their lowest point since 2010 in the previous quarter. The new MacBook Air features a sharper screen, while the Mac mini has been redesigned for professional users, with both updates announced at an event in Brooklyn, New York.

title: Cisco joins other tech giants by emphasizing fertility, adoption benefits
summary: Cisco is expanding its family-friendly benefits in 2019 to include egg storage reimbursement, surrogacy reimbursement, and genetic testing benefits for its 38,000 U.S. employees. This move follows other tech companies in Silicon Valley that are enhancing family-oriented perks to attract and retain talent. The company will reimburse up to $20,000 for egg harvesting or storage, increase adoption and surrogacy reimbursement to $20,000, and cover genetic testing for cancer risks, even if not medically necessary.

title: Apple’s Q4 2018 Results Show Strong End to 2018, Despite Flat or Declining Unit Sales
summary: Apple announced strong Q4 2018 financial results with $14.1 billion in net profits and $62.9 billion in revenues, driven significantly by its Services division and increased iPhone selling prices. Despite growth in revenue, iPhone and Mac unit sales were flat or declined, and iPad saw declines in both unit sales and revenue. The company also announced it would no longer report quarterly unit sales, making future product health assessment more challenging.

title: Eye on the Checkout: Target Adds Mobile Checkout; Harry & David Updates Online Checkout
summary: Target Corp. is implementing mobile checkout in its stores for the holiday season, allowing employees with handheld devices to process card payments anywhere in the store. Concurrently, Harry & David has updated its mobile and desktop checkout experiences to improve speed and streamline shipping and payment forms, anticipating significant mobile online traffic. Adobe Inc. estimates U.S. online sales will reach $124.1 billion this holiday season.

title: Apple’s first 5G iPhone will arrive in 2020
summary: Apple is planning to launch its first 5G iPhone in 2020, exclusively using Intel's 8161 5G modem chip despite challenges with heat dissipation and Apple's current dissatisfaction with Intel. While Intel is the primary provider, Apple has also conversed with MediaTek as a "Plan B," but is confident its issues with Intel can be resolved in time. This launch timeline makes sense given the 5G standard was only recently finalized and widespread 5G infrastructure will still be developing in 2019.

title: Apple’s Social Network
summary: This article discusses Apple's decision to stop reporting unit sales for iPhones, iPads, and Macs, interpreting it as a shift towards becoming more of a services business and a way to manage perceptions of slowing growth in its hardware divisions. The author argues that Apple is attempting to build a "real social network" through its "Today at Apple" sessions, focusing on customer engagement and an ongoing relationship with the brand rather than solely on device sales. The piece also criticizes Apple for not providing clearer metrics like active users and revenue per user, which would offer better insight into its new growth strategy.

title: HP Spectre Folio: One Of The Most Unique Devices I've Ever Reviewed
summary: The author reviews the HP Spectre Folio, highlighting its unique design as a leather-bound PC with three distinct usage modes. He praises its premium packaging, durable leather, and satisfying performance for productivity tasks, despite average battery life for its class. The article also details the device's connectivity, audio quality, and justifies its premium price point.

title: EHR, PHR or something in between? UnitedHealth’s tech venture prompts skepticism and intrigue
summary: UnitedHealth is launching a new medical records platform, prompting both skepticism and intrigue in the health IT sector. While the exact nature of the offering remains ambiguous, experts believe it's likely targeting consumer engagement rather than the traditional EHR market, leveraging its past acquisitions like Rally and Humedica. However, concerns remain about its ability to overcome historical challenges in usability and interoperability within the healthcare IT landscape.

title: Green Dot Says It’s the Go-Between in the New Walmart-PayPal Cash Service
summary: Green Dot Corp. announced its role as the technology provider behind the new Walmart and PayPal cash withdrawal and deposit service. The service, utilizing Green Dot's Banking-as-a-Service platform, allows PayPal users to manage their accounts at Walmart stores. Green Dot also reported strong third-quarter results, with purchase volume up 13% and operating revenues increasing 14%.

title: Qualcomm asked by US federal judge to license its tech to competition such as Intel
summary: A US federal judge has ruled that chip seller Qualcomm Inc must license some of its modem chip technology to competitors, such as Intel Corp. This preliminary ruling emerged from an antitrust lawsuit filed by the US Federal Trade Commission against Qualcomm in early 2017. Qualcomm's shares dropped slightly following the news, as settlement talks between Qualcomm and the FTC continue.

title: MyQuest from Quest Diagnostics Now Supports Health Records on iPhone
summary: Quest Diagnostics announced that its MyQuest mobile app, used by over six million people, now supports Health Records on iPhone. This integration allows patients to consolidate their lab data with other medical information from various providers within the Apple Health app. The update is part of Quest Diagnostics' ongoing efforts to empower consumers with direct access to their health data and enhance their ability to manage healthcare decisions.

title: Amazon strikes deal with Apple to sell iPhones, iPads
summary: Amazon.com Inc has announced a deal with Apple Inc to sell a wider range of Apple products globally, including iPhones and iPads, starting in time for the holiday shopping season. This agreement marks a shift for brands like Apple and Nike, which previously limited distribution through Amazon, as they increasingly recognize the e-commerce giant as a crucial channel for customer reach. The partnership also means Amazon will remove unauthorized third-party sellers of Apple products from its site.

title: Apple Says Some IPhone X Units Have Faulty Touch Screens
summary: Apple Inc. has acknowledged issues with touch screens on some iPhone X units, stating they may not respond or respond intermittently when touched, or react without being touched. Additionally, Apple also announced an issue affecting the 13-inch MacBook Pro. The iPhone X was discontinued in September when newer models were released.

title: Dividends in Focus: The Dow 30
summary: This article analyzes the dividend performance of the 30 stocks in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, highlighting those with attractive yields and long histories of consecutive dividend increases. It summarizes the Dow 30 using five charts focusing on dividend yield, consecutive increases, industry representation, best and worst payers, and total cash dividends paid in 2018. The article also provides detailed profiles of individual Dow components and their dividend growth history.

title: Qualcomm’s stock sinks after ‘messy’ report, but analysts pin hopes on 5G, Apple settlement
summary: Qualcomm's stock experienced a significant drop following a disappointing quarterly report. Despite the "messy" report, analysts are hopeful for the company's future, pinning expectations on the ramp-up of 5G wireless technology and a potential settlement with Apple Inc. The legal dispute with Apple and weakness in China continue to weigh on Qualcomm's results.

title: Quest Diagnostics adds Apple Health integration for lab results
summary: Quest Diagnostics has announced that its customers can now integrate lab test results from their MyQuest accounts into Health Records on Apple iPhones, following a similar move by rival LabCorp. This integration allows consumers to view their lab data alongside other medical information, empowering them to take a more active role in managing their healthcare decisions. The company also expects its partnerships with Safeway and Walmart to augment this new service, with plans for over 200 patient service centers in retail stores by the end of 2018.

title: Fears of peak iPhone rattle Asian Apple suppliers
summary: Asian suppliers and assemblers for Apple Inc experienced a significant drop in shares following warnings of weaker than expected results from several component manufacturers. This has fueled concerns among analysts and investors that iPhone sales may be stagnating, potentially impacting the entire supply chain. The situation is exacerbated by a perceived lack of technological breakthroughs, leading to a bearish outlook for several quarters.
authors: Jess Macy Yu and Heekyong Yang | Reuters

title: Apple Watch Now Part of UnitedHealthcare Wearable Device Program That Helps Motivate People to Walk Nearly 12,000 Steps per Day
summary: Apple Watch is now integrated into UnitedHealthcare Motion, a wearable device program encouraging nearly 12,000 steps per day for participants, double the national average. Eligible employer-sponsored participants can use program rewards toward the purchase of an Apple Watch. This program has proven effective in increasing physical activity, especially among individuals with chronic conditions.

title: Super Micro Computer reports higher 1Q fiscal revenue and earnings amid Chinese spying controversy
summary: Super Micro Computer reported higher fiscal first-quarter earnings due to stronger sales of computer chips, despite being embroiled in a controversy regarding alleged Chinese spy chips in their motherboards used by Apple and Amazon. The company also announced a restatement of prior financial statements concerning revenue recognition and inventory classification, which they believe had an immaterial effect on cash flows. Shares of Super Micro rose following the earnings report.

title: Google parent Alphabet’s stock becomes 2nd FANG to produce ‘death cross’ and Netflix is next
summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc.'s stock (GOOGL) has formed a "death cross" chart pattern for the first time in over two years, indicating a potential bearish trend. This marks Alphabet as the second FANG stock to experience this pattern, with Netflix expected to be next. The death cross occurs when the 50-day moving average falls below the 200-day moving average, which chart watchers interpret as a long-term bearish signal.

title: Abbott Nabs Approvals for DRG Invisible Trial System
summary: Abbott Laboratories has received CE Mark and FDA approval for its Dorsal Root Ganglion (DRG) Invisible Trial System, a chronic pain treatment device. This system allows patients to "test drive" neuromodulation therapy with an external device and Apple technology before committing to a permanent implant. The company highlights the system's effectiveness for chronic focal pain and its potential to reduce opioid use.

title: Anghami bring Shazam rival to the Middle East
summary: Anghami, the Middle East's largest music streaming service with 50 million users, has integrated ACRCloud into its platform, introducing a Shazam-like feature called Anghami Radar. This allows users to scan and discover songs playing around them and find them within the Anghami service. This development comes as Apple moves forward with its acquisition of Shazam's song recognition services.

title: Apple's stock rout starts and ends with the iPhone
summary: Apple's stock is experiencing a significant downturn, falling more than 20 percent from its all-time highs and shedding over $100 billion from its market valuation. This rout is primarily attributed to weakening iPhone sales, lower-than-expected shipments, and warnings of lighter holiday sales, leading to negative sentiment among investors. Despite growth in Services and Other Products, the iPhone remains Apple's largest revenue segment, and its struggles are impacting the company's overall performance.

title: IBM Stock History: 1 Chart You Have to See
summary: This article examines IBM's long history as a blue-chip stock, tracing its evolution from its origins to its current state. It highlights IBM's challenges in adapting to mobile and cloud computing revolutions, which led to underperformance, but also notes the emerging growth in its "strategic imperatives." The author suggests that despite current struggles, IBM's financial resources and research capabilities make it an interesting long-term investment.

title: Probe Card Market Trend Analysis by Top Manufacturers
summary: This report analyzes the global Probe Card market, detailing key manufacturers, market segmentation by type, application, and region, and future growth projections. It highlights leading companies like FormFactor and Micronics Japan (MJC), and forecasts the market to reach $1900 million by 2023 with a CAGR of 4.0%. The demand for probe cards is driven by high-end assembly for advanced semiconductor applications.

title: iPhone users are taking on Apple’s App Store at the Supreme Court. Here’s what it means.
summary: The Supreme Court is hearing oral arguments in a lawsuit against Apple concerning monopolistic prices in the iOS App Store. iPhone users allege that Apple's commissions drive up app prices, while Apple argues that developers set the prices. The outcome could have significant implications for the tech industry regarding app store policies and consumer rights.

title: Apple brings coding education to more students for Computer Science Education Week
summary: Apple is expanding its coding education initiatives for Computer Science Education Week with new resources through its Everyone Can Code program. This includes free Hour of Code sessions at all Apple Store locations, new Swift Coding Club materials, and an App Development with Swift certification to prepare students for the workforce.

title: Argument analysis: Justices poised to allow antitrust dispute against Apple over apps to go forward
summary: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments in an antitrust dispute between Apple and iPhone users regarding app purchases from the App Store. The iPhone users allege Apple's mandatory App Store purchases and 30-percent commission violate federal antitrust laws. Justices appeared inclined to allow the lawsuit to proceed, with particular skepticism regarding Apple's argument based on the 1977 Illinois Brick Co. v. Illinois decision.

title: Apple will tell the Supreme Court that it can't be sued by iPhone users who say the App Store is a monopoly
summary: Apple is set to argue before the U.S. Supreme Court that it cannot be sued by iPhone users who allege the App Store operates as an unlawful monopoly. The case, Apple Inc. v. Pepper, centers on whether iPhone users have standing to sue over Apple's 30 percent commission on app sales, which they claim is passed on to consumers as price gouging. A decision could have significant financial implications for Apple and other tech companies operating electronic marketplaces.

title: Applying Newton’s First Law of Motion to an ETF
summary: This article discusses the Fidelity Momentum Factor ETF (FDMO), an exchange-traded fund that invests in large and mid-cap companies demonstrating positive momentum, applying Newton's First Law of Motion to investing. The ETF's strategy is based on the theory that stocks in motion tend to stay in motion, aligning its investments with top-performing sectors like information technology and financials. It highlights FDMO's performance, expense ratio, yield, current assets, and top holdings, suggesting it for investors who believe recent upward trends will continue.

title: Antitrust, the App Store, and Apple
summary: This article discusses the antitrust case *Apple Inc. v. Pepper* concerning Apple's 30% App Store commission, examining the legal concept of standing, whether the App Store constitutes a monopoly, and the implications for Apple's business strategy. The author argues that Apple likely has the stronger legal position regarding standing but is undeniably an "abusive monopoly" in terms of iOS apps, leveraging its hardware dominance into the digital content market. Ultimately, the article suggests that Apple's "Services Narrative" and rent-seeking behavior could be detrimental to its product culture, even if not immediately illegal.

title: Quest closes buy of Midwest lab services business
summary: Quest Diagnostics has acquired the clinical laboratory services assets of Boyce and Bynum Pathology Laboratories in Columbia, Missouri. This acquisition is part of Quest's strategy to boost revenue through M&A and expand its diagnostic offerings in the Midwest, especially in a challenging reimbursement environment. The deal is expected to close in Q1 2019, with financial terms undisclosed.

title: Which Are Better, Equal-Weighted or Cap-Weighted Index Funds?
summary: This article compares equal-weighted and market capitalization-weighted index funds, discussing their advantages and disadvantages for investors. While market cap-weighting favors larger, outperforming stocks, equal-weighting gives more exposure to medium and smaller companies, potentially offering better diversification and long-term outperformance. However, equal-weighted funds often come with higher expense ratios.

title: Apple Puts Music Service on Amazon Echo in Subscription Push
summary: Apple Inc. and Amazon.com Inc. have announced a partnership that will bring Apple Music to Amazon's Echo devices in December. This collaboration allows Apple Music subscribers to control the service using Amazon's Alexa, marking the first time Apple has opened its music service to full voice control outside of its own Siri technology. The move aims to expand Apple Music's distribution beyond Apple's own hardware by making it available on the popular voice-controlled speakers.

title: To handle the data tsunami, Juniper’s founder turns to custom silicon
summary: Fungible Inc., founded by Juniper Networks Inc. founder Pradeep Sindhu, is developing custom silicon in the form of a data processing unit (DPU) to address the challenge of booming data volumes and the slowdown of Moore's Law. The company aims to optimize data movement and storage in data centers, focusing on data-centric computing and hardware/software integration to meet the demands of massively scalable data centers and edge computing. Despite initial venture capital skepticism, Fungible secured $32 million in funding, and its approach is validated by other tech giants also pursuing custom silicon solutions.

title: Microsoft surpasses Apple as most valuable U.S. company
summary: Microsoft Corp. officially retook its title as the most valuable U.S. company by market capitalization on Friday, unseating Apple Inc. after more than two years. Microsoft's market cap closed at $851.22 billion, while Apple's was $847.43 billion. This marks the first time in 15 years Microsoft has held this position.

title: Fortune 500 company to move headquarters from California to DFW
summary: McKesson Corp., a Fortune 500 company, is officially relocating its headquarters from San Francisco to North Texas. This move follows an expansion of their operations in North Texas in 2016, spurred by $9.75 million in state incentives. Historically, McKesson was the second-highest ranked public company by revenue in the Bay Area, behind Apple.

title: Apple iPhone and McDonald’s fast food have the same problem
summary: Apple Inc. and fast-food chains like McDonald's are facing a similar "demand problem" despite healthy sales figures. Both businesses are relying on higher prices or increased average checks to offset declining customer traffic or flat sales volumes, which the article argues is an unsustainable strategy given market saturation and economic cracks. The article suggests innovative tactics like embracing delivery for restaurants and aggressive marketing of add-on services for Apple to address these challenges.

title: Command & Conquer: Rivals Launches Worldwide, Brings Competitive Real-Time Strategy Excitement to Mobile
summary: Electronic Arts Inc. has officially launched Command & Conquer: Rivals, a fast-paced, real-time strategy action game for Android and iOS devices, aiming to bring competitive RTS excitement to mobile. The game emphasizes player versus player gameplay with intuitive controls, offering factions like the Global Defense Initiative and the Brotherhood of Nod. EA is also expanding its competitive gaming roster with Rivals Champions, an exclusive in-game weekend event and a planned multi-continent esports program in 2019.

title: Apple shifts marketing staff to boost sagging iPhone sales
summary: Apple is implementing aggressive marketing strategies, including discount promotions and moving staff, to boost sagging iPhone sales, especially for the iPhone XS and XR models. This comes amidst waning demand, a cut sales forecast from a supplier, and Apple's decision to stop reporting iPhone unit sales, leading to a drop in its stock value and concerns about market saturation. The company is actively exploring new growth engines beyond the iPhone, such as augmented reality, driverless cars, and subscription services, while also facing challenges in emerging markets like India.

title: China block of iPhone sales, memory outlook don’t hurt chip stocks
summary: Chip-related stocks saw a rebound despite news of China blocking sales of older iPhone models due to Qualcomm patent violations and ongoing tariff concerns. The PHLX Semiconductor Index outperformed broader market indexes, suggesting that investors are not significantly swayed by these developments regarding the chip sector's outlook. This performance indicates a recovery for chip stocks which had previously experienced selloffs throughout the year.

title: Janus Henderson: Tech market sell-off - the bigger picture
summary: Alison Porter, Richard Clode, and Graeme Clark from Janus Henderson’s Global Technology Team discuss the recent tech market sell-off, attributing it to a combination of factors including rising bond yields, trade war concerns, and inventory correction in semiconductors. They highlight that despite the volatility, the long-term secular drivers for the technology sector remain strong, and valuations are becoming more attractive. The team emphasizes their valuation-disciplined investment style and focus on quality companies benefiting from powerful secular themes.
authors: Alison Porter|Richard Clode and Graeme Clark

title: Chinese court orders ban on old iPhone models in Qualcomm patent dispute
summary: A Chinese court has ordered a ban on the sale of several older Apple iPhone models in China following a patent dispute with chipmaker Qualcomm Inc. The ban affects models from iPhone 6S through iPhone X, although Apple states all its models remain on sale and has filed for reconsideration. This ruling is part of a larger global patent dispute between the two US companies and adds uncertainty to Apple's business in a key market.

title: China court bans older iPhone sales
summary: A Chinese court has banned the sale of several older Apple iPhone models in China following a patent infringement case brought by chipmaker Qualcomm Inc. Apple contested the decision and stated that all its phone models remain on sale, while Qualcomm affirmed the immediate ban. This ruling adds to the ongoing global patent dispute between the two US tech giants and creates uncertainty for Apple in a crucial market.

title: Apple to add to its New York presence, but it has really big Texas plans
summary: Apple is expanding its presence with "hundreds" of new jobs in New York, while planning a significantly larger expansion in Austin, Texas, with up to 15,000 new jobs and a new $1 billion campus. The company is also expanding in Southern California (San Diego and Culver City) and Pittsburgh. This indicates a broad strategic growth in several key U.S. locations.

title: These are the best tech companies to work for in India
summary: A new study by jobs site Indeed identifies the top tech companies to work for in India, revealing that multinational corporations are preferred over homegrown firms. Adobe, Nvidia, and Microsoft lead the list, emphasizing job seekers' desire for clear progression structures and positive workplace environments. The article details the top 15 companies, highlighting their employee ratings, key facts, and offices in India.

title: Austin: $1 Billion 2nd Apple Inc. Campus Planned, Williamson County to Present Incentives
summary: Apple Inc. is planning a second $1 billion campus in Austin, Texas, located near its existing facility and expected to accommodate up to 15,000 employees. Williamson County will present an incentive agreement, including a 65% tax abatement over 15 years, in exchange for an investment of at least $400 million and the creation of up to 4,000 full-time jobs over 12 years. This expansion is part of Apple's nationwide $30 billion growth strategy.

title: Apple to spend $1 billion on Austin campus
summary: Apple announced a $1 billion investment to build a new campus in North Austin, Texas. The facility will initially employ 5,000 workers, with potential growth to 15,000. This move signifies Apple's investment in a rapidly growing Texas city.

title: Qualcomm now wants China to ban sales of iPhone XS and XR
summary: Qualcomm Inc. is seeking a ban on sales of Apple's latest iPhone models, XS and XR, in China, following a preliminary injunction against older models. This move is part of an ongoing global patent dispute between the two US tech giants, with Apple stating that all its phone models remain on sale and it has requested a reconsideration from the court.

title: Working in shadows at Google
summary: Google heavily relies on a large contract workforce, known as "TVCs," who now outnumber direct employees. These contractors, despite often doing similar work, lack the benefits and opportunities afforded to full-time staff, leading to feelings of being "second-class citizens" and raising concerns among both contractors and full employees. The reliance on contractors helps Google keep official headcount low and manage costs, but Google distances itself from responsibility for their pay, benefits, and grievances, despite asserting authority on intellectual property matters.

title: Advertising: Why billboards and outdoor ads are booming in a smartphone age
summary: Despite the rise of digital advertising and smartphone addiction, billboards and outdoor ads are experiencing a resurgence. Major tech companies like Netflix, Apple, Google, and Amazon are investing heavily in out-of-home advertising, which offers relevance and bypasses ad-blocking software. The integration of technology, data, and programmatic buying is making these traditional ad formats more interactive, targeted, and effective in capturing consumer attention.

title: German court rules Apple infringed Qualcomm patent| Gulf Times
summary: A German court ruled that Apple infringed a Qualcomm hardware patent, potentially banning sales of some iPhone models (7 through X) in Germany. Qualcomm must post a €668.4mn bond to enforce the order, which targets iPhones containing a specific Qorvo component that violates Qualcomm's envelope tracking patent. This ruling is part of a broader global legal battle between the two tech giants over patent infringement and alleged anti-competitive practices.

title: Tesla names close Musk friend Larry Ellison to board
summary: Tesla appointed Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison and Kathleen Wilson-Thompson to its board, aiming to satisfy U.S. regulators' demands for independent oversight following Elon Musk's tweets about taking the company private. Ellison, a close friend and shareholder of Musk, and Wilson-Thompson, Walgreens Boots Alliance's global head of HR, join the board amidst investor concerns about corporate governance. This move follows an SEC settlement requiring Tesla to add independent directors and control Musk's communications.

title: Globalfoundries Launches Legal Battle Against Taiwan Semiconductor, Also Targets Manufacturers
summary: Globalfoundries has initiated a multi-front legal attack against its rival Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., including requests for a U.S. import ban that could affect devices like iPhones and Lenovo laptops. The Santa Clara, Calif.-based chip maker filed 25 complaints across courts in Germany, Texas, and Delaware, and before the U.S. International Trade Commission. Globalfoundries alleges that TSMC violated over a dozen patents related to chips and their manufacturing methods.

title: Netflix Just Made a Truly Radical Change Affecting Millions of New Subscribers, and It's Totally Brilliant. (Or Maybe Insane--Time Will Tell)
summary: Netflix has implemented a significant change by no longer allowing new subscribers to pay via iTunes, aiming to avoid the substantial "Apple tax" of 15% to 30% on subscription revenue. This strategic move, which could save Netflix hundreds of millions, shifts new users to subscribe directly through the Netflix app, cutting Apple out of its commission. While potentially risking some new subscribers, Netflix is betting that its strong demand across platforms will outweigh any losses and make the decision financially brilliant in the long run.

title: GaN market growing at CAGR of 55% to 2023, driven by power supply segement, or 93% if adopted for wireless charging in consumer electronics
summary: The market for Gallium Nitride (GaN) is projected to grow at a CAGR of 55% to 2023, primarily driven by power supply applications, according to Yole Développement. This growth could accelerate to 93% if GaN is widely adopted for wireless charging in consumer electronics, especially by major smartphone manufacturers. Key players like Infineon, STMicroelectronics, and various start-ups are actively developing GaN solutions, despite current cost challenges compared to silicon-based alternatives.

title: Is Apple Still a ‘forever’ Company? How About Facebook?
summary: This article questions the "forever company" thesis for tech giants like Apple and Facebook, highlighting recent market shifts and challenges. It discusses how Apple's plunging shares due to demand issues in China and Nokia's past trajectory suggest even dominant companies face significant hurdles. The piece also explores the concept of S-curves in technology, suggesting that while current tech giants are strong, a new S-curve, potentially augmented reality or AI, could reset the competitive landscape.

title: MD Anderson Cancer Center and Ascentage Pharma announce strategic alliance in cancer drug development
summary: MD Anderson Cancer Center and Ascentage Pharma Group, Inc. have announced a five-year strategic collaboration to accelerate the clinical development of five potential new cancer therapies.
