This alliance aims to develop novel cancer therapeutics targeting cancer cell apoptosis and other pathways for treating life-threatening hematologic malignancies. The collaboration leverages MD Anderson’s translational research and clinical expertise, led by Dr. Hagop Kantarjian, to bring new treatment options to patients worldwide.

title: Philips Cares digitizes the aging and caregiving experience
summary: Royal Philips launched Philips Cares in the U.S. at CES 2019, a new digital experience designed to support family caregivers and their aging loved ones. This service creates a care circle of trusted contacts, provides insights into a loved one's well-being, and allows for managing Philips Lifeline personal alert services. The aim is to ease the caregiving journey by leveraging digital technology, allowing caregivers to stay informed and connected.

title: Vuzix announces the launch of consumer version of its Blade augmented reality smart glasses
summary: Vuzix has launched the consumer version of its Blade augmented reality smart glasses for USD$999.99, featuring proprietary waveguide optics, a full-color display, and an HD camera. The glasses integrate with an Android-based OS, a companion app for customization and notifications, and are expected to ship in early February 2019. Vuzix is also partnering with Amazon for Alexa integration and AccuWeather for AR weather experiences, highlighting the Blade's potential for both prosumers and consumers as a step towards wearable computing.

title: Apple struggled in China. Abbott Labs isn't
summary: Abbott Laboratories, a healthcare conglomerate, predicts "solid" business in China, contrasting with Apple's recent struggles in the region. Abbott's CFO emphasized that healthcare is a "sticky" and recession-proof sector, especially compared to consumer brands. The company generates about 10% of its annual revenue from China through medical products and infant formula.

title: Apple considered Samsung, MediaTek to supply 5G modems for 2019 iPhones
summary: Apple Inc. considered Samsung Electronics Co Ltd and MediaTek Inc, in addition to existing vendor Intel Corp, to supply 5G modem chips for its 2019 iPhones, according to testimony from an Apple executive. Historically, Apple sourced these chips from Qualcomm before splitting business between Intel and Qualcomm, and later moving solely to Intel for its newest phones in 2018. This consideration came out during the trial between Qualcomm Inc. and the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

title: UnitedHealth Group to Launch Electronic Health Records Platform in 2019; Will It Guide Physicians to Preferred Clinical Laboratory Providers?
summary: UnitedHealth Group plans to launch an electronic health records (EHR) platform by the end of 2019, leveraging its existing Rally mobile wellness platform and targeting 50 million members and one million providers. The new Individual Health Record (IHR) aims to complement existing EHRs by connecting data from multiple sources, potentially positioning UnitedHealth as a central point in healthcare and influencing physician choices for clinical laboratories. This move highlights the increasing importance for clinical laboratories to develop informatics strategies that deliver actionable intelligence to stakeholders.

title: Apple considered Samsung, MediaTek to supply 5G modems for 2019 iPhones
summary: Apple Inc. considered Samsung Electronics Co Ltd and MediaTek Inc, alongside current vendor Intel Corp, to supply 5G modem chips for 2019 iPhones, according to testimony from an Apple executive at a Qualcomm trial. This move highlights Apple's long-standing effort to diversify its modem chip suppliers and reduce its reliance on a single vendor like Qualcomm. The testimony also revealed Apple's "Project Antique" to secure a second modem supplier and the negative impact of Apple's lawsuit against Qualcomm on their business relationship.

title: With Google project, Minnesota joins the boom in megasized data centers
summary: Minnesota is set to join the growing trend of megasized data centers with Google Inc.'s proposed project near the Sherco power generating station in Becker, Minn. This $600 million facility will consume significant electricity and contribute to the regional boom in "hyperscale" data centers being built by major tech companies. While these centers employ few people once operational, their construction phases provide hundreds of jobs, particularly for skilled electricians, and are critical for the infrastructure of cloud-based computing.

title: Apple’s COO Says Qualcomm Refused to Provide Chips for 2018 Phones
summary: Apple's COO Jeff Williams testified that Qualcomm refused to provide cellular modems for 2018 iPhones after Apple sued the chipmaker in 2017. This refusal forced Apple to exclusively use Intel-made modems in its latest iPhone models. Williams attempted to negotiate with Qualcomm, but the company declined to supply the chips.

title: BlackRock’s Fink looks to a ‘deep bench’ to pick heir
summary: Larry Fink, BlackRock's CEO, is looking to his firm's "deep bench" of executives to name a successor, with Mark Wiedman's recent promotion positioning him at the top of the list. Despite this, other senior figures including Mark McCombe, Mark Wiseman, Rob Goldstein, Rob Kapito, and Rich Kushel remain in contention. The article details the backgrounds, key accomplishments, and potential challenges for each of these possible heirs to the leadership of the $6.4tn asset management group.

title: Israeli Johny Srouji reportedly in running for Intel CEO
summary: Johny Srouji, an Arab Israeli and senior VP of hardware technologies at Apple, is reportedly a candidate for the CEO position at Intel Corp. Srouji previously worked for Intel and was instrumental in establishing Apple's R&D center in Israel, overseeing the development of silicon and hardware technologies. This comes after Intel's former CEO, Brian Krzanich, resigned, and the company is reportedly considering a "non-traditional" candidate from outside.

title: Google spends $40 million on Fossil smartwatch tech
summary: Google has agreed to acquire smartwatch technology and personnel from Fossil Group Inc. for US$40 million. This move aims to bolster Google's presence in the smartwatch market, where its Wear OS platform has lagged behind competitors like Apple. The acquisition includes intellectual property not yet released to the market and transfers an unspecified number of Fossil's R&D employees to Google, while Fossil retains 200 people in its smartwatch division.

title: New Cardiac Imaging Technologies Unveiled at RSNA 2018
summary: The RSNA 2018 conference highlighted several new cardiac imaging technologies, including software to streamline fetal heart exams from GE Healthcare and a new iPhone-based ultrasound system called Butterfly IQ. Spectrum Dynamics also introduced an innovative SPECT-CT scanner with robotic arms, while GE Healthcare showcased AI-enhanced virtual contrast angiography for interventional radiology. These innovations aim to improve accuracy, efficiency, and accessibility in cardiac diagnostics.

title: Google to pay $40 million for mystery Fossil smartwatch technology
summary: Google has paid Fossil Group $40 million for undisclosed smartwatch technology, which is described as an innovation not yet on the market. This acquisition suggests Google is moving forward with plans to develop its own smartwatch, possibly leveraging Fossil's technology from its Misfit acquisition. The technology is expected to be made available to other Wear OS partners and is hinted to involve health monitoring, potentially giving Wear OS a boost in competing with Apple Watch.

title: Sonos Amp Streaming Amplifier Review
summary: The Sonos Amp streaming amplifier is reviewed, highlighting its versatility and ease of integration into Sonos multiroom audio systems, despite some limitations in EQ and sound with certain speakers. This new device replaces the Connect:Amp, offering enhanced features and design improvements based on extensive feedback from custom integrators and DIY enthusiasts, effectively bridging the gap between consumer preferences and professional installation needs.

title: Apple concedes that Qualcomm was the only 4G ready chip source
summary: Apple Inc. has conceded in court that Qualcomm Inc. was its sole viable supplier for 4G-ready chips during the mobile industry's transition, according to testimony by Apple's director of cellular systems architecture, Matthias Sauer. This admission is crucial for Qualcomm's defense against antitrust allegations by the US Federal Trade Commission, which accuses Qualcomm of using its market dominance to enforce inflated patent licensing fees. Apple's COO Jeff Williams also testified that Qualcomm cut off their chip supply, forcing Apple to move all business to Intel.

title: The No. 1 Airline App Just Got Better - United Airlines Introduces Upgraded App
summary: United Airlines has launched a reimagined mobile app to enhance customer experience, building on its position as the top-downloaded airline app. The updated app features a dynamic design to provide relevant information throughout the travel journey, new navigation and "My Trips" tab, an inbox for notifications, and personalized content, all based on extensive customer and employee feedback. This initiative reinforces United's commitment to using technology to make travel easier and more intuitive for its customers.

title: January 2019 Newsletter: 4 Charts Every Investor Should Know
summary: This January 2019 newsletter by Lyn Alden discusses major market drivers for 2019, highlighting high asset valuations and global leverage. It presents four key charts investors should know: corporate debt, central bank balance sheets, net worth to income, and the U.S. federal deficit and debt to GDP, to help balance risk and reward. The article also provides detailed portfolio updates and investment strategy recommendations focusing on dividends and defensive positioning.

title: Caterpillar, Nvidia Warnings Send Wall Street Tumbling
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: U.S. stocks tumbled following warnings from Caterpillar and Nvidia, which highlighted concerns about a slowing Chinese economy and the impact of tariffs on corporate profits. Caterpillar missed profit estimates due to softening demand in China and rising costs, while Nvidia cut its revenue forecast due to weak demand and lower data center sales. This sent major indexes like the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite lower, despite overall positive earnings reports.

title: China weakness spreads far and wide, from Caterpillar to Nvidia
summary: Major US and European companies like Caterpillar, Nvidia, and Intel are reporting significant impacts from the slowing demand in China across various sectors, from industrial machinery and technology to automotive parts. While many industries are feeling the pinch, luxury goods and some consumer brands like Tiffany & Co. and Starbucks are, for now, experiencing continued growth, creating a mixed economic picture. The emerging trend suggests a broadening slowdown in China is affecting a diverse range of goods and services.

title: Harley-Davidson hits rough road, and now all eyes move to Apple
summary: Harley-Davidson Inc. is facing significant demand issues, evident from a substantial bottom-line miss, lower-than-expected revenue, and a bleak outlook for motorcycle sales in 2019. The company projects a slow start to the year and does not anticipate immediate improvement in its U.S. market position. This weak performance shifts market attention to upcoming earnings reports, particularly from Apple, in its shadow.

title: Taiwanese manufacturers are expecting an increase in OLED production and demand
summary: Taiwanese manufacturers like AU Optronics and Novatek Microelectronics are anticipating a massive surge in OLED display demand and are adjusting their production to meet it. The growth is leading established players to ramp up output for Android devices and iPhones, while newcomers like Novatek Microelectronics and Himax Technologies are entering the market focusing on OLED driver ICs. This trend is also observed with other Asian companies, highlighting a widespread belief in the continued expansion of the OLED market.

title: Silicon Valley's highest-paid CEOs may surprise you
summary: This article from the Silicon Valley Business Journal reveals the highest-paid CEOs in Silicon Valley for 2019, highlighting some lesser-known executives at the top of the list. John Donahoe of ServiceNow Inc. is identified as the highest-paid CEO with total compensation of $41.52 million. The piece suggests that the typical highest-paid CEOs might not be the most obvious names.

title: RTC Apple Store Eyes Neighboring Space Vacated by Pottery Barn
summary: The Apple Store in Reston Town Center has filed a building permit for the space recently vacated by Pottery Barn, which is directly next door. This move suggests Apple Inc. may be considering an expansion or relocation within the shopping center. Pottery Barn and Williams-Sonoma both closed their Reston Town Center locations on January 24th.

title: Scientists’ discovery could mean less-expensive smartphones, TVs
summary: USC chemists have developed a new copper-based compound that could replace rare and expensive iridium in smartphone and TV screens, potentially leading to cheaper devices without compromising visual quality. This discovery addresses the scarcity of iridium and solves the long-standing problem of short lifetimes for blue light emission in OLED technology. The new rigid copper compound exhibits efficient light emission comparable to iridium, opening new possibilities for display technology and energy-efficient lighting.

title: Apple patches FaceTime flaw, and two exploited zero days in new security update
summary: Apple released a security update, iOS 12.1.4, which fixes the widely publicized FaceTime bug that allowed spying through audio and video. The update also addresses two zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2019-7286 and CVE-2019-7287) that hackers had already exploited, allowing them to gain elevated privileges and execute arbitrary code. Users are urged to update their iPhones immediately to protect against these known threats.

title: A rare look inside the factory that makes iPhone's Gorilla Glass
summary: CNBC gained exclusive access to Corning's Harrodsburg, Kentucky factory, where Gorilla Glass is manufactured. This factory produces the durable glass used in over 6 billion smartphones, tablets, screens, and wearables globally for major brands like Apple, Samsung, and LG. The article provides a rare look inside the manufacturing process of this essential component.

title: 17 Stocks That Warren Buffett Just Bought, Trimmed or Dumped
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway made 17 buys and sells in its equity portfolio during the fourth quarter of 2018. Changes included taking new stakes in StoneCo, Suncor Energy, and Red Hat, significantly increasing positions in bank stocks like JPMorgan Chase and General Motors, and trimming holdings in Apple, Southwest Airlines, and Wells Fargo. Notably, Berkshire fully exited its position in Oracle just one quarter after initiating it.

title: Cognex expands presence in ASEAN market, targeting Việt Nam
summary: Cognex Corporation, a leading supplier of industrial barcode readers and machine vision, is expanding its presence in the ASEAN market, with a strong focus on Việt Nam due to its impressive emerging market status and rapid increase in foreign direct investment. The company has already established an office in Việt Nam in 2017 and is now consolidating its efforts from a new Singapore office to target the burgeoning manufacturing industry in Việt Nam, Indonesia, and Thailand. Cognex aims to serve both foreign manufacturers like Samsung and LG, and local firms such as Vingroup, across key sectors including electronics, automobile, and motorbike industries.

title: Nokia Bets it All on 5G
summary: Nokia is embarking on a bold strategy to bet its future on 5G technology, repositioning itself after divesting its mobile handset business and undergoing significant acquisitions. Despite a challenging competitive landscape and internal reorganization, CEO Rajeev Suri expresses confidence in 5G's potential to drive growth, especially in the enterprise market, anticipating a stronger performance in the latter half of 2019 and 2020. The article highlights Nokia's history of reinvention and the risks and opportunities associated with its current 5G-centric gambit amidst geopolitical headwinds and the evolving tech industry.

title: PayPal CEO Grapples With Fringe Groups
summary: PayPal CEO Dan Schulman addresses the complex issue of defining hate speech and its implications for payment processing services. The article discusses PayPal's decision to stop processing payments for Infowars and Gab.com due to their alleged promotion of hate speech or association with individuals who do. This highlights the ongoing challenge for tech companies in balancing free speech with content moderation.

title: Special report: Sophisticated ad fraud permeates the mobile app ecosystem
summary: This report details how sophisticated ad fraud has become an endemic problem within the mobile app ecosystem, affecting both Android and iOS platforms. It covers various fraudulent techniques such as unviewable ads, bad traffic, synthetic audiences, and click injection, highlighting the significant financial and personal costs to users, advertisers, and the industry. Case studies including apps from Cheetah Mobile, Kika Tech, and GOMO illustrate the pervasive nature of these fraudulent activities and the challenges in detection and regulation.

title: Activist-shareholders-push-TransDigm-on-greenhouse-gas
summary: Activist shareholders, including representatives of the New York City comptroller and five city retirement systems, are pressuring Cleveland-based aerospace component maker TransDigm Group Inc. to adopt a policy to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions in line with the Paris Agreement. While TransDigm's board is asking shareholders to vote against the proposal, arguing their decentralized business model makes a top-down approach impractical, experts note a growing trend of investors pushing for more specific environmental, social, and governance (ESG) initiatives. Companies in energy and transportation are particularly targeted, and law firms advise proactive goal-setting to avoid shareholder-driven policies.

title: A new billionaire emerges from S'pore gaming powerhouse Sea
summary: Forrest Li, the Chinese-born founder of Singapore-based gaming and e-commerce company Sea Ltd., has become a new billionaire due to the success of Sea's mobile game "Free Fire" and strong sales growth. His 13.8 percent stake in Sea is now worth approximately $1 billion following a 35 percent surge in the company's shares. Despite widening net losses, investors are optimistic about Sea's expansion into e-commerce and its potential to become a regional internet-services powerhouse, similar to its backer Tencent Holdings Ltd.

title: More customers are using Xcel Energy’s mobile app
summary: Xcel Energy's mobile app, launched in 2017, has been downloaded over 400,000 times by customers across its eight-state service area, indicating a growing adoption rate. The app offers features like outage reporting, billing information, energy usage tracking, and a secure payment portal, enhancing customer convenience and access to service details. David Hudson, president of Xcel Energy – New Mexico, Texas, highlights the company's commitment to improving customer experience by providing accessible information and tools.

title: Apple iPhone supplier Corning is developing flexible glass for foldable phones
summary: Corning Inc., a supplier for Apple iPhone, is developing flexible glass for foldable phones to address the durability issues of plastic screens currently used in such devices. The company is working on an ultrathin, bendable glass that is 0.1 millimeters thick and can bend to a 5-millimeter radius. While progress is being made, Corning faces the challenge of combining tight bend radii with increased damage resistance, and Apple is expected to wait for these issues to be resolved before adopting foldable technology.

title: Your U.S. Equity Fund Is More Global Than You Think
summary: This article examines how U.S. equity funds are more globally exposed than commonly perceived by analyzing revenue streams by region. Morningstar's new proprietary data reveals that many popular U.S. index-tracking ETFs have significant revenue exposure outside the United States, particularly in emerging Asian markets and the eurozone. The analysis highlights that relying on a company's headquarters or stock listing alone provides a flawed picture of a fund's true geographic allocation.

title: If you’re tracking the S&P 500 instead of this fund, you’re leaving money on the table
summary: This article suggests that investors tracking the S&P 500 might be missing out on better returns by overlooking the Invesco QQQ Trust. It highlights the Invesco QQQ Trust as a successful and diversified fund. The piece also addresses concerns about tech stock valuations, noting they are lower than before the dot-com bubble burst.

title: FIS to Buy Worldpay, an E-Commerce Payments Processor, for $34 Billion (Published 2019)
summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) announced its agreement to acquire Worldpay for approximately $34 billion, seeking to become a dominant force in the global payments sector. This acquisition adds Worldpay's significant e-commerce transaction processing capabilities to FIS's existing financial services software, marking one of the largest deals in the rapidly consolidating payments industry. The merged company will retain the FIS name and leadership, with the deal still subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

title: Why Netflix is diving deeper into interactive storytelling
summary: Netflix is expanding its interactive storytelling initiatives following the success of Black Mirror: Bandersnatch, with plans to launch "You vs. Wild" starring Bear Grylls and explore interactive content in other genres like romance and horror. The company sees this strategy as a way to differentiate itself from increasing competition from Apple and Disney and boost viewer engagement. Netflix is also analyzing viewer behavior with Bandersnatch to refine the implementation of interactive choices in future projects.

title: Apple rolls out first update to iMac desktop in almost two years
summary: Apple Inc. has introduced the first update to its iMac all-in-one desktop computer since mid-2017, ahead of a major event on March 25. The new iMacs feature speedier Intel processors and AMD graphics engines, with the 4K 21.5-inch models being up to 60 percent faster and the 5K 27-inch versions up to 2.4 times faster. This update follows the recent launch of new iPad Air and iPad mini models, as Apple continues its hardware refreshments.

title: New Era Dawns for Hollywood as Disney Completes $71.3B Takeover of 20th Century Fox
summary: The Walt Disney Co. completed its $71.3 billion acquisition of 20th Century Fox, marking a significant consolidation in Hollywood. This deal reduces the "Big Six" studios to five and provides Disney with extensive content for its upcoming streaming service, Disney+, as it competes with digital giants like Netflix. The acquisition is expected to lead to thousands of layoffs and raises questions about cinematic diversity and the future of risk-taking productions within the more conservative Disney structure.

title: Tesla sues Zoox, claims ex-workers stole trade secrets
summary: Tesla has filed lawsuits against former employees for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to its Autopilot system and sharing them with rival companies like Xpeng Motors and Zoox Inc. This comes amid a broader U.S. crackdown on Chinese corporate espionage, with Apple also having faced similar issues regarding employee theft of autonomous vehicle technology. Xpeng denies involvement in any wrongdoing and states it respects intellectual property rights, while also confirming an internal investigation into the allegations.

title: How Salesforce paved the way for the SaaS platform approach
summary: This article details how Salesforce pioneered the SaaS platform approach, starting from simple customization requests in its early CRM product to the development of APIs, the AppExchange, and eventually Force.com (now Salesforce Lightning Platform). It highlights the company's incremental steps in building an ecosystem where other companies could build applications on its infrastructure, effectively setting a new standard for enterprise SaaS.

title: Introducing Apple Card, a new kind of credit card created by Apple
summary: Apple announced Apple Card, a new credit card designed for iPhone, focusing on simplicity, transparency, and privacy, aiming to help customers achieve a healthier financial life. It integrates into the Apple Wallet app, offers Daily Cash rewards, eliminates fees, and provides real-time spending insights. Apple is partnering with Goldman Sachs and Mastercard for its launch in the US this summer.

title: Sally Beauty launches mobile-first site, adds Apple Pay, Instagram shopping
summary: Sally Beauty Holdings Inc. has launched a redesigned mobile-first website, integrated Apple Pay as a payment option, and is developing a mobile app. These initiatives are part of the company's strategy to transform its retail business for the e-commerce world, including offering a shop-by-solution experience and enabling direct purchases through Instagram.

title: Strengths in the SWOT analysis of Motorola Inc :
summary: This article provides a comprehensive SWOT analysis of Motorola Inc., a technology and communications company. It details the company's strengths, such as its diverse product portfolio and global presence, and discusses weaknesses like past failures to adapt to new technologies. The analysis also identifies opportunities in the growing demand for feature phones and emerging economies, as well as threats from intense competition and high investment requirements.

title: Apple Card vs. American Express Platinum: A High-End Credit Card Face-Off
summary: This article compares the new Apple Card with the American Express Platinum, highlighting their features, fees, interest rates, and perks. While Apple aims to disrupt the credit card industry with its fee-free model and integrated ecosystem, American Express Platinum offers a suite of luxury benefits. The comparison aims to help consumers understand where Apple Card fits into the high-end credit card market.

title: US Bank updates iPhone app to help millennials
summary: U.S. Bank updated its iPhone mobile app to include a "money mentorship" feature aimed at helping millennials analyze spending habits in real time and manage their finances. The app now allows Face ID for secure access and provides a comprehensive view of a user's financial portfolio. This update, which will also be released for Android, coincides with a national ad campaign highlighting the bank's role in customers' everyday financial lives.

title: Apple and Goldman Sachs Dive Head-First Into Crowded $175 Billion Credit Card Pool
summary: Apple and Goldman Sachs have launched a new credit card, entering the competitive $175 billion industry. While Apple aims to revolutionize the credit card experience, both companies are new to consumer credit and must quickly master customer service, dispute resolution, and statement management, areas where Apple typically maintains end-to-end control. They are implementing strategies like iMessage support and machine learning for transaction clarity to address these challenges.

title: Netflix’s Market Value Dropped $8 Billion After the Disney Plus Announcement
summary: Netflix Inc. experienced an $8 billion drop in market capitalization after Disney announced its new, cheaper streaming service, Disney+, slated to launch in November. Disney's stock, conversely, surged to a record high, adding $25 billion in market value, as the company detailed its content-rich streaming offering designed to compete directly with Netflix.

title: Why Apple was so upset with Qualcomm — and why it finally had to give in
summary: Apple and Qualcomm settled a bitter legal battle over royalties and licensing fees, leading Apple to once again buy Qualcomm chips. The dispute centered on Qualcomm's patent licensing model, which Apple viewed as abusive due to its "no license, no chips" policy and pricing based on the total device cost. Despite Apple's strong objections and Qualcomm's history of antitrust issues, Apple ultimately settled because Qualcomm remains the leader in wireless technology, especially with the rollout of 5G networks, leaving Apple with few alternatives after Intel's exit from the 5G chip market.

title: Sell in May, and Go Away? Maybe At Least Rotate? or Hedge…
summary: This article discusses the "Sell in May and go away" adage for traders, exploring alternatives like rotating into less affected sectors or using inverse/leveraged inverse ETFs. It highlights potential opportunities in healthcare and technology stocks, citing specific companies and Direxion ETFs. The piece emphasizes that traders have choices to navigate seasonal market trends, whether by shifting focus or hedging against potential downturns.

title: Apple expands global recycling programs
summary: Apple announced a significant expansion of its global recycling programs, quadrupling the number of locations where US customers can send iPhones to be disassembled by its recycling robot, Daisy. The company also introduced a Material Recovery Lab for future recycling process research and highlighted its use of recycled materials like cobalt, tin, and aluminum in new products. Apple is celebrating Earth Day with new initiatives and its annual environmental report, which includes commitments from 44 suppliers to use 100% renewable energy for Apple production.

title: ECONOMY: San Diego Is a Clear Winner as Qualcomm’s Stock Surges
summary: Qualcomm's stock surged by $26 billion in market cap following its settlement with Apple, signaling a major win for San Diego. The deal is expected to lead to increased hiring in the region, with the company's strong local presence and economic impact benefiting the entire economy. Qualcomm's prior economic impact included 10,030 employees directly and an additional 17,850 jobs supported indirectly.

title: eBay's Managed Payments Offering Expands
summary: eBay is expanding its managed payments offering to Germany, making it the second market after the U.S., pending regulatory approval. This expansion aims to provide buyers with more payment flexibility and choice, while also giving sellers access to new customers and simplifying business processes with lower costs. The company has already processed $363M in GMV and saved sellers $2.7M through its managed payments as of Q1 2019.

title: Exclusive: Apple supplier Jabil to close Silicon Valley site, impacting nearly 300 employees
summary: Jabil, a key supplier for Apple and other tech giants, is reportedly closing one of its Silicon Valley facilities, leading to the layoff or transfer of nearly 300 employees. This closure and workforce reduction are attributed to a slowdown in business operations for the company.

title: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) earnings Q3 2019
summary: Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) exceeded revenue and earnings estimates for Q3 2019, causing its shares to rise by 3% in aftermarket hours. The company reported strong growth across its segments, including Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing, driven by demand for cloud offerings and increased engagement in LinkedIn and Xbox Live. Microsoft's CFO highlighted that commercial cloud revenue reached $9.6 billion, marking a 41% year-over-year increase.

title: Qualcomm’s stock rallying like it’s 1999
summary: Qualcomm's stock has surged over 51% in five days following its settlement with Apple, reaching levels not seen since the internet bubble. The stock closed at $86.72, its highest since January 2000, after Morgan Stanley issued a bullish outlook and raised its price target. This rally indicates a significant positive market response to the Apple settlement.

title: Corning — how the glass giant went from Pyrex to Gorilla Glass
summary: This article chronicles the evolution of Corning, the glass giant, from its origins as a light bulb manufacturer in 1851 to its current status as a producer of high-tech glass products like Pyrex and Gorilla Glass. It highlights Corning's continuous reinvention to adapt to changing technologies and market demands, serving major companies such as Apple, Google, Verizon, and Samsung.

title: After years serving CEOs, Goldman's Ayco also wants other workers
summary: Goldman Sachs' Ayco unit, traditionally serving top executives, is expanding its financial advisory services to target millions of other affluent employees at major U.S. companies. This expansion, which includes offering high-yield savings accounts and personal loans through its Marcus online bank, is part of Goldman's strategy to reposition itself as a traditional bank and reach a wider customer base to meet its $5 billion new annual revenue target by 2020. Despite some investor concerns about entering new territory, Goldman views Ayco's growth into the mass affluent market as a cost-effective way to acquire new clients.

title: Five fund buyers reveal their favourite tech ETFs
summary: This article compiles the favorite tech ETFs of five different fund buyers, highlighting various approaches to gaining tech exposure, including robotics and automation, pure-play sector exposure, and broad Nasdaq 100 coverage that includes FAANG stocks. Each fund buyer explains their rationale for choosing particular ETFs, considering factors like methodology, diversification, liquidity, and how well the ETF captures the evolving definition of "tech."

title: Yext CEO: New HQ, snugged between Google and Apple, will bring 500 jobs
summary: Yext's CEO announced a new headquarters, strategically located between Google and Apple offices, which is expected to create 500 jobs. The company's investment in the new building is projected to exceed $150 million over 12 years, covering rent and build-out costs.

title: Ahead of Apple Earnings, Investors Rush Into Tech ETFs
summary: Ahead of a significant week for technology earnings, investors are increasingly pouring money into tech-focused ETFs like XLK, QQQ, and VGT. Data from Bank of America Merrill Lynch, citing EPFR Global, indicates that tech stocks saw $1.3 billion in inflows last week, contrasting with overall U.S. equities experiencing net outflows. This surge in interest is partly attributed to anticipated reports from major tech companies, including Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL), a top component in these funds.

title: Warren Buffett drops a $10-billion bomb into Anadarko bidding war
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway plans to invest $10 billion into Occidental Petroleum to support its bid to acquire Anadarko Petroleum, significantly impacting a decades-long bidding war. This substantial investment, contingent on the deal closing, provides Occidental with crucial financing and a seal of approval from Buffett, while raising questions about the high cost of his support. The move intensifies the competition between Occidental and Chevron, which has an existing agreement with Anadarko.

title: HPE opens new headquarters in north San Jose
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise officially unveiled its new headquarters in north San Jose, bringing 1,300 workers to the America Center complex. San Jose Mayor Sam Liccardo lauded HPE's commitment to the community. The company also announced new corporate initiatives, including enhanced parental leave benefits and flexible work options, aiming to attract and retain top talent.

title: Qualcomm Pegs Payment From Apple at $4.5 Billion to $4.7 Billion (Published 2019)
summary: Qualcomm announced it expects a one-time payment of $4.5 billion to $4.7 billion from its settlement with Apple in a patent dispute. This payment comes as Qualcomm reported its quarterly earnings, but the company's forecasts suggest that Apple's licensing fees will not substantially increase future revenue after the initial catch-up on unpaid royalties. The settlement, which includes a six-year patent license and a chip supply agreement, is anticipated to generate $2 per share in additional earnings for Qualcomm.

title: TurboTax maker, H&R Block sued for allegedly charging taxpayers for free service
source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
source_domain: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
summary: Federal lawsuits have been filed against H&R Block and TurboTax-maker Intuit by the Los Angeles City Attorney, alleging that the companies deliberately misled low-income taxpayers into paying for tax-filing services that should have been free. The lawsuits accuse the companies of reneging on a 2002 agreement with the IRS and hindering access to free services. Both companies have denied wrongdoing, stating their commitment to offering free tax filing options.

title: MVP revives plan to develop new PLDT headquarters
summary: PLDT Inc. chairman and CEO Manuel Pangilinan announced the revival of a plan to redevelop the company's headquarters in Makati City into a vertical office tower. The project involves redeveloping the Ramon Cojuangco Building (RCB) and Makati General Office (MGO) potentially into two towers, with one possibly being sold. This initiative, first conceived in 2014, aims to create an ideal work environment for employees and could be partly funded by the disposal of other company properties, potentially raising P3 billion to P4 billion.

title: ACI Acquires Western Union’s Walletron Mobile Bill-Presentment Business
summary: ACI Worldwide Inc. has acquired Walletron Inc., Western Union's mobile bill-presentment business, in a move to enhance its electronic bill-payment offerings. This acquisition follows ACI's earlier $750 million cash acquisition of Western Union's Speedpay business, emphasizing ACI's strategy to bolster its position in the bill-payment market. Walletron's technology, which enables bill viewing and payments via mobile wallets like Apple Wallet and Google Pay, is used by over 6 million consumers globally.

title: Supermicro moves production from China
summary: Supermicro is reportedly moving its production facilities out of China due to lingering customer concerns about embedded spy chips in its motherboards, despite being cleared by investigations. This decision also serves to mitigate the impact of the U.S.-China trade war. The company is expanding manufacturing in Taiwan and California to increase self-reliance and meet customer demand.

title: Supreme Court Deals Blow to Apple in Antitrust Case
summary: The Supreme Court ruled 5-4 that App Store customers have the standing to sue Apple for antitrust violations, rejecting Apple's argument that customers purchase apps from third-party developers directly. The decision allows a 2011 lawsuit alleging Apple drives up app prices through a 30 percent commission to proceed, potentially impacting other companies that operate as marketplaces. The ruling clarifies the "direct purchaser" principle under antitrust law but does not overturn the Illinois Brick doctrine.

title: Here's why Apple is so vulnerable to a trade war with China
summary: Apple is highly vulnerable to a U.S.-China trade war due to two main factors: its primary iPhone assembly takes place in China, making it subject to potential tariffs on Chinese exports, and it generates a significant portion of its revenue from Chinese consumers. Analysts predict substantial price increases or earnings per share decreases if tariffs are imposed, and a large-scale move of manufacturing out of China would be costly and time-consuming. The company's revenue and consumer confidence in China are notably sensitive to trade tensions.

title: Opinion analysis: Divided court allows antitrust lawsuit against Apple to continue
summary: The Supreme Court, in a 5-4 decision, allowed an antitrust lawsuit against Apple to proceed. iPhone users allege Apple violates federal laws by requiring app purchases exclusively through its App Store. The ruling, penned by Justice Kavanaugh, rejected Apple's argument that the lawsuit should be dismissed because app developers, not Apple, set prices, emphasizing that iPhone users are direct purchasers from Apple.

title: Vanguard ESG U.S. Stock ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:ESGV)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Vanguard ESG U.S. Stock ETF (ESGV), including its current price, key statistics, fund overview, expenses, and recent news headlines. It highlights ESGV's investment strategy, which focuses on U.S. all-cap companies screened for environmental, social, and corporate governance criteria, and lists its top holdings and sector exposure. The fund, managed by Vanguard, has assets under management of $11.58 billion and a net expense ratio of 0.09%.

title: World’s First 1TB MicroSD Card from SanDisk is Available
summary: SanDisk, a part of Western Digital, has released the world's first 1TB microSD card, certified as UHS-I V30 for video and A2 for smartphone use. This high-capacity memory card offers read speeds up to 160MB/s and write speeds up to 90MB/s, making it suitable for filmmakers, drones, and action cameras, despite its premium price of $449.99 US. The card is shockproof, temperature-proof, waterproof, and X-ray-proof, and comes with a full-size SD adapter.

title: Google, Qualcomm, Intel, and Broadcom all cut ties with Huawei
summary: Google revoked Huawei’s Android license, and American chipmakers like Intel, Qualcomm, and Broadcom announced they would stop selling components to the Chinese smartphone giant following a U.S. executive order banning American companies from supplying Huawei. This move is a major escalation in the U.S.-China trade war, potentially crippling Huawei's smartphone sales and delaying China's 5G network build. Analysts warn of potential retaliation from China against American tech companies operating within the country.

title: Toshiba Memory Corp to buy out Apple, Dell, Kingston and Seagate
summary: Toshiba Memory Corp. (TMC) is buying back shares from its US corporate shareholders Apple, Dell, Kingston, and Seagate, who had invested in TMC last year to help it stay independent. TMC will use ¥1.3 trillion ($11.8bn) in loans from Japanese banks to refinance the company and plans for a Tokyo Stock Exchange listing. The US companies are expected to receive a significant profit on their less than year-old investment.

title: China's rare earth supplies could be vital bargaining chip in U.S. trade war
summary: Amid rising U.S.-China trade tensions, Beijing's dominant position as a rare earth supplier could serve as a crucial bargaining chip. Rare earth elements are essential in various consumer products, military equipment, and advanced technologies. The article details their uses, primary suppliers, and potential U.S. strategies to reduce reliance on Chinese imports.

title: Huawei: ARM memo tells staff to stop working with China’s tech giant
summary: UK-based chip designer ARM has instructed its staff to suspend all business with Huawei and its subsidiaries following the US trade clampdown, as its designs contain "US origin technology." This move is considered an "insurmountable" blow to Huawei's ability to develop its own chips for smartphones, 5G base stations, and computer servers. Huawei acknowledges the pressure on its partners due to politically motivated decisions but remains confident the situation can be resolved.

title: Is it time to revive the rumors that Apple will buy Tesla? Here’s why one expert thinks so
summary: Marketing professor Scott Galloway believes that Tesla Inc. is "coming undone" and will be ripe for acquisition in the near future. He specifically suggests that Apple Inc. could be the acquiring company amidst Tesla's recent struggles. This revives long-standing rumors about a potential acquisition of the electric-car maker.

title: Exclusive: Amazon interested in buying Boost from T-Mobile, Sprint - sources
summary: Amazon.com Inc is reportedly interested in acquiring Boost Mobile from T-Mobile US Inc and Sprint Corp, according to sources familiar with the matter. The potential deal would allow Amazon to utilize the new T-Mobile's wireless network for at least six years and could also include the divestment of wireless spectrum. This move comes as T-Mobile and Sprint look to sell assets like Boost to gain regulatory approval for their planned $26 billion merger.
authors: Angela Moon|Sheila Dang and Diane Bartz

title: VGT ETF Down On Tech Stock Woes
summary: The Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT) experienced a downturn due to woes in the technology stock market, particularly with the Nasdaq being rattled. The article highlights Apple's decline due to regulatory concerns and mentions the broader risk associated with the concentration of VGT's portfolio in a few major tech companies. Analysts from D.A. Davidson noted increasing regulatory disruption potential for tech titans like Amazon, Apple, Facebook, and Google globally.

title: Stock Market Turns Lower; Google Shares Tank As U.S. Prepares Antitrust Probe
summary: The stock market, particularly the Nasdaq, turned lower on Monday, with Google-parent Alphabet's shares falling amid news of an impending antitrust probe by the U.S. Justice Department. Investors were also closely watching Apple stock ahead of its Worldwide Developers Conference.

title: tvOS 13 powers the most personal cinematic experience ever
summary: Apple today previewed tvOS 13, enhancing Apple TV 4K with a new Home screen, multi-user support, expanded game controller support for Apple Arcade, and new 4K HDR screen savers. The update aims to provide the easiest, most personalized, and complete entertainment experience for the home. tvOS 13 will be available this fall as a free software update for Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD.

title: Apple Design Awards celebrate best-in-class design for apps and games
summary: The 2019 Apple Design Awards recognized nine iOS app and game developers for their exceptional artistry, technical achievement, user interface, and application design. These winning apps showcase a variety of categories and leverage breakthrough Apple technologies, demonstrating the developers' creativity and ingenuity from around the world. Ron Okamoto, Apple’s vice president of Worldwide Developer Relations, congratulated the winners for raising the bar in app and game development, particularly in areas like health, fitness, creativity, and gameplay.

title: General Motors Competes With Google, Facebook Over Israeli Tech Workers, GM Exec Says
summary: General Motors is intensely competing with tech giants like Google, Facebook, and Apple for top engineering talent in Israel, according to Gil Golan, executive director of GM’s Advanced Technology Center in the country. GM's R&D center in Israel has grown significantly and plans further expansion, emphasizing the appeal of working on impactful autonomous vehicle technology. The article also highlights the growing presence of other major automakers like Ford, Renault, Nissan, Daimler AG, and Volkswagen Group in Israel, indicating the country's importance in autonomous vehicle R&D.

title: Broadcom results to provide clear insight into trade war impact
summary: Broadcom's upcoming earnings report is expected to offer crucial insights into the impact of the U.S.-China trade war on the semiconductor industry, especially given its significant ties to China and key customers like Huawei and Apple. Analysts are closely watching for commentary on second-half revenue recovery, cloud capital expenditures, and smartphone unit trends, as expectations for the company's performance have recently been lowered. Despite the trade tensions, some analysts view Broadcom's stock as attractive due to its strong long-term growth and profitability prospects.

title: Tech titans fuel a scramble to build large-scale offices
summary: Tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Netflix are driving a massive scramble for office space in Los Angeles, leading to billions of dollars in new construction and radical redevelopments. These "content creators" prefer large-scale, campus-like facilities with unique amenities over traditional high-rise offices, focusing their hubs in areas like Hollywood, Culver City, and Playa Vista. This trend is sparking an economic boom and attracting a multiplier effect of smaller service providers looking to be near these major players.

title: Amazon pulls back from original kids programming
summary: Amazon Studios is reportedly scaling back its investment in original animated shows for young children, shifting its focus to content for families to view together and for young adult audiences. This strategic change comes amidst executive changes and internal debates over the value of kids' programming in driving Prime memberships, especially as competitors like Netflix, Disney, and Apple continue to invest heavily in the children's content space. The company will continue to produce existing kids' animated series but will prioritize licensing kids' content and developing more mature animation, including young adult animated series.

title: Adobe’s Project Gemini will be called Adobe Fresco, is a new painting app for iPad
summary: Adobe has officially named its "Project Gemini" painting application for iPad as Adobe Fresco. The app, which was first revealed in October 2018, aims to replicate real-world painting effects like oil and watercolor using AI-powered Live Brushes. Adobe Fresco is designed for both professionals and art novices, offering tools like layers, masking, and export to Adobe Photoshop CC or Illustrator.

title: Progressive's Tricia Griffith shines in Glassdoor's ranking of the country's best CEOs
summary: Progressive Corp. CEO Tricia Griffith has been ranked No. 61 on Glassdoor's list of the 100 best CEOs in America, with a 93% approval rating from employees. The article highlights employee praise for the company's work environment and benefits, balanced with complaints about long hours. It also touches on a boom in mortgage refinancing due to low rates and other local news involving the Cleveland Orchestra and Cleveland State University research.

title: Best Buy to repair Apple products at all US stores
summary: Best Buy will now repair Apple products at all of its nearly 1,000 U.S. stores following an expanded partnership between the two companies. This agreement significantly increases Apple's authorized service network, making repairs more accessible for customers, with 7,600 Geek Squad members ready for same-day iPhone repairs or other Apple product services. The expansion means that eight out of ten Apple customers now live within 20 minutes of an authorized repair provider.

title: Apple partners with Best Buy for expanded repair service
summary: Apple announced a major expansion of its authorized service network through a partnership with Best Buy. Almost 1,000 Best Buy stores across the U.S. will now offer expert service and repairs for Apple products, making it more convenient for customers to access reliable repairs. This collaboration includes nearly 7,600 newly Apple-certified Geek Squad technicians ready to perform repairs using genuine Apple parts.

title: Apple warns US against imposing additional China tariffs
summary: Apple Inc. urged the Trump administration not to proceed with proposed tariffs of up to 25% on products imported from China, stating it would reduce the company's contribution to the U.S. economy. The tariffs would impact nearly all major Apple products, including iPhones, and Apple highlighted its role as a major U.S. job creator and taxpayer. The company also noted the potential negative impact on its global competitiveness and its relationship with China if production were to move.
authors: Mark Gurman and Mark Niquette and Bloomberg

title: 10 Things You Didn’t Know About Rob Glaser
summary: This article provides 10 interesting facts about Rob Glaser, the founder of RealNetworks and a partner at Accel Partners. It covers aspects of his professional career, including his time at Microsoft and investments, as well as personal details regarding his net worth, charitable work, political support, and family life.

title: Disappointing Exit for Machine Learning Company ParallelM
summary: Machine learning company ParallelM has been acquired by DataRobot Inc. for several tens of millions of dollars, a sum that reportedly did not yield a return on investment for its shareholders. Despite the founders' previous successful exits, ParallelM itself, after five years of operation and several business pivots, seemingly failed to meet its potential. As part of the merger, ParallelM's management will head a new MLOps and Governance business unit within DataRobot.

title: Blockchain Could Make You—Not Equifax—the Owner of Your Data
summary: This article discusses how blockchain technology can empower individuals to own and monetize their personal data, challenging traditional data brokers like Equifax. It explains how blockchain-based solutions use cryptography, such as keys, hashes, and smart contracts, to create a decentralized system for data exchange, giving users control over who accesses and profits from their information. The piece also addresses the challenges of scalability and storage within blockchain technology but highlights its potential to disrupt the current data economy.

title: Charlotte-based BofA rolls out digital debit card
summary: Bank of America is introducing a new digital debit card accessible through its mobile app, offering faster access to funds for customers awaiting a physical card. This initiative aims to improve customer convenience by providing immediate card functionality.

title: Can Adobe's Digital Experience Revenues Exceed Digital Media Revenues By 2025?
summary: This article examines whether Adobe's Digital Experience revenues can surpass Digital Media revenues by 2025, noting the former's faster growth rate. While Digital Experience is expanding rapidly, driven by market growth and strategic acquisitions, Digital Media is expected to remain the larger segment through 2025 due to its established market share and steady expansion. The analysis provides detailed forecasts and market share projections for both segments.

title: Expedia shows off $900M Seattle waterfront campus as thousands of employees prepare to move in
summary: Expedia showcased its new $900 million Seattle waterfront campus as thousands of employees prepare to move in. The campus, located in the Interbay neighborhood, is a sprawling space with stunning waterfront views, featuring biophilic design and a mix of renovated and new buildings. The move signifies a major shift from its former Bellevue headquarters, with the company implementing solutions to mitigate traffic impacts and aiming to house over 8,000 workers eventually.

title: Apple Leases 635 KSF Office Campus in Seattle
summary: Apple has signed a long-term lease with Kilroy Realty Corp. to fully occupy 333 Dexter, a 635,000-square-foot campus in Seattle’s South Lake Union. The two-tower project, Kilroy Realty's first ground-up development in the city, is scheduled for completion in late 2019. Apple plans to hire over 2,000 employees in the city within the next five years for this new location.

title: Should You Be Impressed By Apple Inc.'s (NASDAQ:AAPL) ROE?
summary: This article analyzes Apple Inc.'s Return on Equity (ROE), which stands at an impressive 54%. It explains the ROE calculation, compares Apple's ROE to the industry average, and discusses the role of debt in achieving this strong return. The article concludes by emphasizing that while Apple's ROE is high, investors should also consider debt levels and future profit growth.

title: Delta becomes first airline to use Apple's Messages app for customer service
summary: Delta Air Lines is rolling out a new customer service tool using Apple Business Chat, making it the first airline to adopt the Messages app for this purpose. The tool will be phased in this summer to test customer preferences and demand. This initiative aims to enhance customer interaction for Delta.

title: Jony Ive to form independent design company with Apple as client
summary: Sir Jony Ive, Apple's chief design officer, will leave the company later this year to establish his own independent design firm, with Apple becoming one of its primary clients. Ive will continue to collaborate closely with Apple on various projects, while his long-standing design team will report to COO Jeff Williams. This move allows Ive to pursue personal projects while maintaining a relationship with Apple.

title: Apple Moves Mac Pro Production to China
summary: Apple is shifting the production of its new $6,000 Mac Pro desktop computer to China, marking a significant change as it was the company's only major device previously assembled in the U.S. This move comes amid escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and China. The tech giant has partnered with Taiwanese contractor Quanta Computer Inc. and is ramping up production near Shanghai, benefiting from reduced shipping costs due to the proximity of suppliers.

title: iPhone designer Jony Ive to leave Apple and start own firm
summary: Jony Ive, the acclaimed designer behind iconic Apple products like the iPhone, iMac, and iPad, is leaving Apple later this year to establish his own independent design firm, LoveFrom. Ive, a close creative collaborator with Steve Jobs, will continue to work on Apple products through his new venture. Following the announcement, Apple's shares fell by 1.5 percent, wiping approximately $9bn from the company's value.

title: Report: Apple moving Mac Pro production out of Austin
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly moving the manufacturing of its new Mac Pro models from Austin, Texas, to China. This decision comes despite Apple's previous commitment to Austin for Mac Pro production, with reports indicating challenges at the Austin plant and declining demand. Local officials are not concerned about job losses from this move given Austin's tight job market and Apple's continued significant investments in new facilities and campus expansion in the city.

title: Apple ‘fundamental deterioration’ to last into 2020, analyst predicts
summary: Rosenblatt Securities analyst Jun Zhang downgraded Apple's stock to a sell rating, citing concerns that the company's iPhone business could underperform in the upcoming upgrade cycle. This downgrade comes after a disappointing performance by the iPhone XS and ahead of a potentially lackluster iPhone launch later in the fall. Zhang predicts this "fundamental deterioration" could extend into 2020.

title: Dow closes lower as Apple, Boeing shares slump, investors rethink Fed rate-cut chances
summary: U.S. stocks finished lower on Monday, with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all declining as investors adjusted their expectations for interest-rate cuts. This change in sentiment followed last week's strong June jobs report. Boeing also lost a nearly $6 billion deal due to ongoing 737 Max safety concerns.

title: Piper Jaffray announces $485M deal for Sandler O'Neill, new name
summary: Piper Jaffray Cos. announced it will acquire fellow investment bank Sandler O'Neill for $485 million. The combined entity will operate under the new name Piper Sandler Cos. Chad Abraham, CEO of Piper Jaffray, will lead the new firm.

title: Apple Updates MacBook Air and MacBook Pro
summary: Apple announced updates to its MacBook Air and MacBook Pro lines, adding True Tone display technology to the MacBook Air and upgrading the entry-level 13-inch MacBook Pro with faster processors and Touch Bar/Touch ID. These updates come with new pricing, including special discounts for university students. The company also announced its Back to School promotion and highlighted features coming with macOS Catalina.

title: Former Tesla employee admits uploading Autopilot source code to his iCloud
summary: A former Tesla employee, Guangzhi Cao, admitted in a court filing to uploading Autopilot source code to his personal iCloud account in late 2018. Tesla has sued Cao for allegedly stealing trade secrets and taking them to Chinese EV startup Xiaopeng Motors (XPeng), where he is now the "head of perception." While Cao denies stealing sensitive information, he admits to creating backup copies and zip files of Autopilot source code, and deleting files and browser history before leaving Tesla.

title: Cannabis Career Watch | New Hires and Promotions July 12, 2019
summary: This article details new hires and promotions within the cannabis industry as of July 12, 2019. Key movements include leadership changes and new appointments at cbdMD, ONE Cannabis, Caliva, Surterra Wellness, Canopy Growth Corporation, Cresco Labs, Leafly, and Kona Gold Solutions. Noteworthy items include Jay-Z partnering with Caliva and Bruce Linton's departure from Canopy Growth.

title: From Hot Sauce to Pop Gloss, US Products Seep Into Iran
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Despite heightened political tensions and sanctions, American brands and products like Coca-Cola, Pepsi, Heinz, and Tabasco are widely available and popular in Iran. Young Iranians particularly embrace U.S. lifestyle and products, although many items are bottled locally or enter the country through indirect channels. This prevalence highlights a cultural divide between official rhetoric and popular consumer choices in Iran.

title: Tesla to Apple: Help us nail robocar-secrets thief
summary: Tesla is seeking Apple's help in a lawsuit against a former engineer accused of stealing confidential Autopilot files before joining Chinese startup XMotors.ai, a subsidiary of Xpeng. Both Tesla and Apple suspect their former autonomous technology engineers defected to Xpeng with proprietary information. Tesla has subpoenaed Apple, suggesting a shared interest in prosecuting alleged intellectual property theft by individuals moving to Xpeng.

title: Netflix stock drops more than 10 percent after earnings shortfall
summary: Netflix Inc.'s shares plunged after reporting a loss of U.S. customers and significantly slower international growth, missing analyst expectations. This shortfall, the first since 2011, raises investor concerns about heavy program spending and low profitability amidst looming competition from new streaming services by Disney and Apple.

title: Netflix stock plummets after massive subscriber miss
summary: Netflix shares plunged after the company announced it missed its subscriber forecast for the second quarter of 2019, adding only 2.7 million new subscribers compared to analysts' expectations of 5 million. This miss comes as major competitors like Disney, Apple, NBCUniversal, and AT&T's WarnerMedia prepare to launch their own streaming services, some of which will reclaim popular licensed content from Netflix. Despite the competitive landscape, Netflix points to its content slate as the reason for lower-than-anticipated growth, while still boasting an enviable subscriber lead and strong original programming.

title: How Apple iOS 13 device management features benefit IT
summary: This article discusses the new device management features introduced in Apple iOS 13 and iPadOS, specifically focusing on user enrollment and Managed Apple IDs for Business. Zack Blum, CEO of Fleetsmith Inc., explains how these features improve BYOD policies, enhance security through better authentication, and create new opportunities for managing Apple devices in enterprise environments. The piece highlights Apple's continued focus on privacy and security while addressing the differing needs of various business sizes.

title: Tinder is the latest app to boycott Google Play by bypassing its payment system
summary: Tinder has joined a growing trend of apps bypassing Google Play's payment system to avoid the 30% revenue cut. The online dating site now forces users to enter credit card details directly into its app, removing the Google Play payment option for future purchases. This move could spark a domino effect among other app developers looking to reclaim more revenue from in-app transactions.

title: Apple was always going to build its own modem, and the Intel deal helps
summary: Apple has confirmed its acquisition of Intel's modem business for $1 billion, gaining 2,200 employees, patents, leases, and equipment. This move enables Apple to develop its own modems, a long-term goal given its preference for in-house chip design and its past disputes with Qualcomm. By owning its modem technology, Apple aims to optimize its future products and reduce dependence on external suppliers for critical intellectual property.

title: Apple Is Now the Most Valuable Brand Ever, Thanks to This 1 Thing. (The Good News Is You Can Totally Do It Too)
summary: Apple has become the most valuable brand globally, valued at $205 billion, largely due to its commitment to delighting customers beyond expectations. While the iPhone's success contributes, the core reason for its brand value lies in fostering positive feelings through every customer interaction. The article suggests that any business can achieve similar brand success by focusing on unexpected customer delight rather than just meeting expectations.

title: Apple pays $1 billion for Intel unit in push for chip independence
summary: Apple Inc. has acquired the majority of Intel Corp.'s modem business for $1 billion, a strategic move to internalize smartphone chip production. This deal includes the transfer of 2,200 Intel employees and intellectual property, significantly boosting Apple's wireless technology patent portfolio to 17,000. It positions Apple to reduce reliance on external suppliers like Qualcomm and strengthen its leverage in future 5G patent licensing negotiations.

title: SoftBank Group's $108 billion Vision Fund 2 draws in Microsoft, Apple
summary: SoftBank Group announced commitments of approximately $108 billion for its second Vision Fund, aimed at investing in technology firms, with Microsoft and Apple among the new investors. The Japanese conglomerate itself plans to invest $38 billion. Notably, the sovereign wealth funds of Saudi Arabia and Abu Dhabi, key investors in the first fund, were absent from the initial list, though discussions with them were ongoing.

title: Trump says Apple will face tariffs on Mac Pro parts made in China
summary: President Trump announced that Apple will face tariffs on Mac Pro parts made in China, rejecting the company's request for tariff exemptions. Trump suggested Apple should instead manufacture the components in the United States, although he later indicated they would "work it out" and hinted at a new plant in Texas. This comes as Apple plans to produce its new Mac Pro in China, despite previous models being made exclusively in the U.S.

title: Apple offers new augmented reality art sessions
summary: Apple is launching "[AR]T," a new series of augmented reality art experiences and sessions available at Apple Stores worldwide. These include an interactive AR art walk curated with the New Museum, an in-store lab for creating AR experiences using Swift Playgrounds, and a global AR art installation by Nick Cave. The initiative aims to provide a window into creative arts using Apple products, with sessions starting on August 10th.

title: Netflix is not a tech company
summary: Benedict Evans argues that Netflix, despite its reliance on technology, is fundamentally a TV company, much like Sky was in its time. He uses "technology as a crowbar" as a metaphor to explain how companies leverage tech to disrupt traditional industries, but their core business remains within that industry. The article emphasizes that for Netflix, the critical questions are "TV questions" related to content, rights, and industry dynamics, rather than "tech questions" typical of Silicon Valley.

title: Apple Just Announced Something Unthinkable About the iPhone. Here's Why It's Actually Great News
summary: Apple's recent quarterly results show that iPhone sales are cooling and now represent less than half of Apple's total sales for the first time in seven years. This shift highlights the growing importance of Apple's services and wearables divisions, which are becoming the company's most crucial product areas for future growth and profitability. The article argues that these areas are now more significant for Apple's future than the iPhone itself.

title: Health care giant Abbott Laboratories takes new building in Southwest Austin
summary: Abbott Laboratories, a health care giant, is leasing the first office building at the Seven Oaks project in Southwest Austin. This new location will place the company next to Apple offices. The Seven Oaks project, a two-building office park, is currently under construction on Bee Caves Road.

title: Fossil Group Launches Exclusive Smartwatch Capabilities within Wear OS by Google™ Platform - Multi-Day Battery Life and Tethered iPhone Calls
summary: Fossil Group announced new proprietary nextgen touchscreen smartwatch technology and an evolution in hybrid smartwatches within the Wear OS by Google platform. Key advancements include multi-day battery life through an exclusive app with four battery modes and the ability for iPhone users to make and receive calls on tethered smartwatches, thanks to a newly added speaker and proprietary app. These technological improvements, along with increased storage and memory, aim to enhance user experience and address critical customer demands for longer battery life and improved call functionality.

title: Marriott Stock Testing Support After Mixed Quarter
summary: Marriott International (MAR) stock is trading lower after the company met EPS estimates but missed revenue expectations for Q2, leading to a lowered fiscal year 2019 EPS guidance. The article discusses MAR's long-term and short-term chart performance, highlighting key support and resistance levels, and the potential impact of an economic downturn on the hotel giant. Technical indicators suggest a fresh sell cycle, with shares currently testing support between $128 and $130.

title: Apple, Goldman Sachs start issuing Apple Cards to consumers
summary: Apple and Goldman Sachs have begun issuing Apple Cards to a limited number of consumers. This virtual credit card, designed to work with the iPhone, offers 2% cashback, no fees, and strong privacy features, aiming to diversify Apple's revenue and expand Goldman Sachs' consumer banking brand. Analysts predict it will strengthen Apple's ecosystem loyalty, with potential high-margin revenue growth by 2023.

title: Lufthansa | IBM How iOS apps help Lufthansa to ease the flying experience
summary: Lufthansa partnered with Apple and IBM to develop a suite of iOS apps for its Ground Operations team. These apps aim to improve flight operations efficiency, speed up passenger boarding, and reduce delays by providing real-time data to staff on the go. The collaboration has resulted in faster aircraft turnarounds, a smoother flying experience for passengers, and higher productivity for Ground Ops staff.

title: New Wave Of Cisco Firings Sees 500 People Lose Jobs
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has initiated a second round of layoffs in 2019, impacting 488 employees in California, primarily in engineering roles at its San Jose headquarters and Milpitas. These firings are part of Cisco's ongoing shift from a hardware manufacturer to a software developer. The company has experienced continuous layoffs under CEO Chuck Robbins, with previous cuts in 2017 and 2016.

title: Apple’s US job footprint grows to 2.4 million
summary: Apple's US job footprint has expanded to 2.4 million, an increase of four times the number of American jobs attributable to the company eight years prior. The company is on track to directly contribute $350 billion to the US economy by 2023, while also supporting numerous American suppliers and fostering growth in the app economy. This growth includes significant expansion plans, such as creating 20,000 new jobs across the US by 2023 and opening new facilities in San Diego and Seattle.

title: Snap Inc to release Spectacles 3 video-recording sunglasses
summary: Snap Inc. is releasing Spectacles 3, an updated version of its video-recording sunglasses, in autumn for £330. These new Spectacles feature dual HD cameras for capturing 3D video and photos, which can then be uploaded to Snapchat. The Spectacles 3 are made with a stainless steel frame and are designed with new augmented reality creative tools, contrasting with the less successful, plastic predecessors.

title: Big health plans pledge to look beyond shareholders. Critics aren’t so sure
summary: Major health plans, including CVS Health, Anthem, and Cigna, have signed a Business Roundtable statement indicating a shift from prioritizing only shareholders to considering all stakeholders. However, critics and political figures like Sen. Elizabeth Warren remain skeptical, calling for concrete actions. Kaiser Permanente CEO Bernard Tyson declined to sign, stating that as a non-profit, Kaiser does not have shareholders to prioritize.

title: Mystery new Sonos Move could be company’s first portable Bluetooth speaker
summary: Sonos is expected to launch its first portable Bluetooth speaker, named the Move, designed to work both within a home Wi-Fi network and as a standalone Bluetooth device. The speaker will feature Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Apple AirPlay 2, and voice control via Amazon Alexa and Google Assistant, along with an Auto Trueplay feature for automatic sound tuning. It will be battery-powered with a charging dock and a USB-C port, marking a significant expansion for Sonos into portable audio.

title: Apple, JPMorgan CEOs Say Companies Must Serve More Than Shareholders
summary: 181 CEOs of major public companies, including Apple, JPMorgan Chase, and Walmart, have declared their companies' purpose extends beyond just shareholders to include all stakeholders like customers, employees, and communities. This new stance, articulated by the Business Roundtable, signifies a shift away from the "shareholder primacy" theory that has guided corporate governance for decades, reflecting growing public and generational expectations for corporate responsibility. The declaration suggests that capitalism, when adjusted, can be a force for broader societal good.

title: Kral sets up subsidiary in China
summary: Kral AG, an Austrian screw pump and flowmeter manufacturer, has established a new subsidiary in Wuxi, China, named Kral (Wuxi) Machinery Technology Co Ltd. This new location, headed by Ren Lu and Haichao Li, marks Kral's sixth global subsidiary, expanding its presence in key markets like marine, power generation, oil and gas, mechanical engineering, and chemicals. The company, headquartered in Austria, employs around 250 people and has a high export quota, indicating its global reach and continued expansion.

title: Globalfoundries Launches Legal Battle Against Taiwan Semiconductor, Also Targets Manufacturers
summary: Globalfoundries has initiated a multi-front legal attack against Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), seeking a U.S. import ban that could impact various electronic devices. The company filed 25 complaints in multiple courts and with the U.S. International Trade Commission, alleging TSMC violated over a dozen patents related to chip manufacturing. This legal action targets not only TSMC but also device manufacturers using their chips, like Apple and Lenovo.

title: PGE gives big customers a new green option, and they like it
summary: Portland General Electric (PGE) has launched "Green Future Impact," a new program that allows its large customers to directly purchase renewable energy certificates and energy from new, specific third-party renewable sources. This initiative aims to address the growing demand from big corporations for green energy options. The program connects the energy buy directly to new renewable sources, building on previous discussions and proposals for a "green tariff."
authors: Pete Danko – Staff Reporter|Portland Business Journal

title: 5G Technology Making Its Way Into Self-Driving Cars
summary: 5G technology, specifically C-V2X, is set to revolutionize self-driving cars by enabling vehicles to communicate wirelessly for improved functionality and safety. Investors can capitalize on this emerging trend through ETFs like the iShares Self-Driving EV and Tech ETF (IDRV), which provides exposure to companies involved in self-driving and electric vehicle innovation. The article highlights the disruptive potential of 5G in various industries beyond just autonomous driving.

title: Apple apologizes for Siri privacy issue and quits keeping audio recordings
summary: Apple Inc. has apologized for privacy mishaps with its Siri voice assistant and announced it will no longer retain audio recordings of Siri interactions. This change comes after criticism regarding human contractors listening to user recordings to improve the product, a practice Apple had temporarily suspended. The company plans to reinstate the program with new privacy controls for users and will only use its own employees, not contractors, for audio sample review.

title: Apple apologises, and makes changes to Siri’s privacy policy
summary: Apple Inc. announced changes to its Siri privacy policy, stating it will no longer retain audio recordings for human review by default and will limit human review to employees only. This decision comes after a human review program raised privacy concerns. Users will now have the option to opt-in if they want their audio reviewed to improve the service.

title: Apple Welcomes India's Easing of Foreign Investment Rules
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple Inc. has welcomed India's decision to relax foreign direct investment rules, specifically easing local sourcing requirements for single-brand retailing and permitting online sales. This change paves the way for Apple to open its first retail store in India and expand its online presence, despite currently facing tough competition from other smartphone makers. The Indian government liberalized these rules to attract capital and boost an economy that has been experiencing a slowdown.

title: Apple invites go out, 3 new iPhones expected at event
summary: Apple has sent out invitations for a special event on September 10th where it is expected to unveil three new iPhone models and updates to other products. This event follows a trade-war delay for Apple products and discussions between Apple CEO Tim Cook and President Trump regarding tariffs. The company recently reported strong second-quarter results driven by its services business, offsetting weaker iPhone sales.

title: Here are 2019’s biggest stock market winners and losers in the Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq
