summary: This article reviews the biggest stock market winners and losers across the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq for 2019 up to August 29th. It highlights top performers like Chipotle, AMD, Apple, and Visa, and notes disappointing results for Macy’s and Kraft Heinz. Despite ongoing media narratives, the U.S. stock market has shown strong returns year-to-date, with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite Index all posting significant gains.

title: Here are 2019’s biggest stock market winners and losers in the Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq
summary: This article identifies the top stock market performers and laggards in the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq for 2019 as of August 29. It highlights strong market performance despite negative headlines, with the Dow rising 13%, the S&P 500 climbing 16.8%, and the Nasdaq Composite jumping 20%. Apple is noted as a strong performer in the Dow.

title: How Adobe InDesign took over publishing with Steve Jobs' help
summary: This article details how Adobe InDesign, with key assistance from Steve Jobs and strategic decisions, rose to dominate the publishing industry. It chronicles InDesign's competition with QuarkXPress, its integration into the Adobe Creative Cloud, and current challenges related to forced software updates. The piece also reflects on the history of desktop publishing and the rivalries between Adobe, Apple, and Aldus.

title: Apple Inc. v. Pepper: A Case to Watch for Commissions-Based Retailers Selling Directly to Consumers
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in *Apple Inc. v. Pepper* that consumers could sue Apple for antitrust violations related to its App Store, specifically regarding the 30 percent commission imposed on developers. This decision allows direct purchaser suits against monopolistic retailers using commissions, impacting not just Apple but also other major online marketplaces like eBay, Etsy, Amazon, Jet, Walmart, and Newegg. The article highlights the significant implications for commissions-based retailers selling directly to consumers.

title: Apple Is Set to Unveil New iPhones on Tuesday. Here’s How and When to Watch
summary: Apple is expected to unveil new iPhones and potentially other devices like redesigned iPads and MacBook Pros during an event on Tuesday, September 10, at its Steve Jobs Theater. The article provides details on how to watch the event, including live streams on Apple.com and YouTube, and discusses rumored product announcements. This year marks the first time the iPhone event will be streamed on YouTube, making it accessible to a wider audience.

title: Apple, Foxconn admit breaking Chinese labour law at iPhone plant
summary: Apple and Foxconn confirmed they violated Chinese labor laws by employing too many temporary workers at the world’s largest iPhone factory, a situation brought to light by a China Labor Watch report. The report detailed that temporary staff, including student interns, comprised a significant portion of the workforce, far exceeding the legal limit of 10%. Both companies stated they are working to address the issues and ensure compliance with labor standards.

title: Apple Says Apple TV+ Is Coming Nov. 1. Here’s How Much It Will Cost
summary: Apple announced that its video streaming service, Apple TV+, will launch on November 1 in over 100 countries for $4.99 monthly. Customers who purchase a new Apple device will receive one free year of Apple TV+. This announcement led to a nearly 3% dip in Netflix's stock and positions Apple TV+ to debut before Disney+'s launch.

title: US Vegan Climate ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:VEGN)
summary: The US Vegan Climate ETF (VEGN) tracks a principles-based index of U.S. equities weighted by market-cap, focusing on vegan and climate-friendly investments. Launched on September 9, 2019, it has assets under management of $65.70 million and a net expense ratio of 0.60%. The ETF's holdings include major tech companies like Micron Technology, Alphabet, Cisco Systems, Apple, and NVIDIA, and its stock has seen a 2.0% decrease year-to-date as of February 27, 2026.

title: Apple announces three groundbreaking health studies
summary: Apple announced three new groundbreaking health studies in partnership with leading medical institutions, focusing on women's health, heart and movement, and hearing. These studies will utilize the new Research app to gather data from a large participant base, aiming to advance medical discoveries and contribute to innovative health products. The research will specifically address conditions like PCOS, cardiovascular health, and the impact of everyday sound exposure on hearing.

title: Opinion: Apple iPhone event reveals a dramatic change in strategy
summary: Apple's latest iPhone event showcased a significant shift in its business strategy, moving from a focus on high margins to competing on price. This change was evident with lower prices for its newest iPhones and an aggressively priced Apple TV+ streaming service at $4.99 a month, including a free year for new device buyers. This move suggests Apple is now prioritizing market share and accessibility, a departure from its traditional high-end positioning.

title: Why Markets Think Trump Tariffs Will Help Apple and Hurt Amazon
summary: This article explores why markets believe tariffs will impact Apple positively and Amazon negatively. It highlights Apple's strong embedded position within the Chinese economy, suggesting that China needs Apple as much, if not more, than Apple needs China, thus incentivizing protection from tariffs. Conversely, Amazon's business model, heavily reliant on Chinese-made goods without a similar indispensable role in China's economy, makes it vulnerable to tariff impacts.

title: Better battery, Night mode, 'slofies' and other cool things coming to Apple's new iPhones
summary: Apple unveiled new iPhones, Apple Watch, and services, focusing on improved battery life, camera features like Night mode and "slofies," and an always-on display for the Apple Watch Series 5. The article details five key announcements, including new gaming and video subscription services that are affordably priced.

title: Starbucks appoints execs from Dominos, Nike, Apple to board
summary: Starbucks has appointed new executives from diverse companies like Apple, Nike, and Domino's to its board of directors. The 13-member board is led by Myron Ullman III, former chairman and CEO of J.C. Penney Co. This move brings new experience and perspective to Starbucks' leadership.

title: 3 ETFs to Buy If You Want to Go Long Netflix Stock
summary: As Netflix faces increasing competition from new streaming services like Apple TV Plus and Disney+, investors are growing nervous about NFLX stock. For those who believe in Netflix's business model but want to mitigate volatility, three ETFs are recommended as safer alternatives: Invesco NASDAQ Internet ETF (PNQI), Fidelity MSCI Communication Services Index ETF (FCOM), and ERShares Entrepreneur 30 ETF (ENTR), all of which include Netflix among their holdings. This diversification strategy helps reduce direct exposure to Netflix's stock fluctuations while still allowing participation in its potential growth.

title: Disney CEO Bob Iger resigns from Apple’s board of directors
summary: Disney CEO Bob Iger has resigned from Apple's board of directors, according to an SEC filing. The resignation comes as both Disney and Apple are preparing to launch their respective video streaming services, Disney+ and Apple TV+, in November, suggesting a potential conflict of interest due to competitive overlap. Iger had been a member of Apple's board since November 2011.

title: Netflix vs Apple: Here's which stock would have made you richer if you invested $1,000 10 years ago
summary: This article compares the returns of a $1,000 investment made 10 years ago in Netflix versus Apple. Netflix significantly outperformed Apple, turning $1,000 into nearly $47,400 compared to Apple's $10,400. The article also discusses Apple TV+'s launch and its potential impact on Netflix, noting that while Netflix's past returns are impressive, its future growth depends on subscriber retention amidst new competition.

title: Walt Disney Co. CEO Bob Iger leaves Apple’s board
summary: Walt Disney Co. CEO Bob Iger has departed Apple's board of directors as both companies prepare to launch competing video streaming services. Iger's departure on September 10th signals increased competition between Disney and Apple in the video streaming market. The move comes as Apple TV+ and Disney+ are set to launch later this year at competitive price points.

title: Intel 5G Exec Cormac Conroy Departs Company
summary: Cormac Conroy, an Intel executive who led the company's 5G modem efforts, resigned from Intel just before the company announced the sale of most of its smartphone modem business to Apple. Despite this departure and the sale, Intel states that LTE and 5G connectivity remain important for its PC business and Project Athena innovation program. Conroy's resignation follows Intel's decision earlier in the year to exit the 5G smartphone modem business, a move that aligns with Apple's reported efforts to recruit talent from Intel's 5G team.

title: Apple awards an additional $250 million from Advanced Manufacturing Fund to Corning
summary: Apple has awarded an additional $250 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to Corning Incorporated, bringing the total investment to $450 million. This funding supports Corning's research and development for state-of-the-art glass processes for Apple products like iPhone, Apple Watch, and iPad. The investment reinforces the long-standing partnership between Apple and Corning, emphasizing American innovation and manufacturing in consumer technology.

title: Apple awards Kentucky company $250 million to fuel innovation for iPhone glass
summary: Apple has awarded $250 million to Corning Inc. to expand advanced manufacturing and technology for the scratch-resistant iPhone glass produced in Harrodsburg, Kentucky. This investment follows a previous $200 million award in 2017 and aims to further innovation in durable glass for evolving Apple devices, including the new iPhone 11 and iPhone 11 Pro models. The partnership between Apple and Corning, which dates back over a decade to the first iPhone, highlights their shared commitment to American innovation and manufacturing.

title: Apple awards iPhone supplier Corning $250 million from U.S. manufacturing fund
summary: Apple Inc. has awarded $250 million to iPhone glass supplier Corning Inc. from its $5 billion U.S. advanced manufacturing fund. This marks a total of $450 million Corning has received from Apple's fund, supporting 1,000 jobs. The investment highlights Apple's commitment to U.S. suppliers amidst ongoing trade tensions and its strategy of providing upfront cash to lock in supplies and better prices from its vendors.

title: Walt Disney Co. Bob Iger’s Memoir On Apple: “If Steve (Jobs) Were Still Alive, We’d Have Combined Our Companies”
summary: In his new memoir, former Walt Disney Co. CEO Bob Iger reveals that he believes a merger between Disney and Apple would have occurred if Steve Jobs were still alive. Iger detailed his close relationship with Jobs and the previous animosity between the two companies before his tenure. This revelation comes as Disney and Apple are now streaming media rivals, with Iger recently resigning from Apple's board.

title: Comcast gives away Xfinity Flex TV device, pounding shares of Roku
summary: Comcast announced it will offer its Xfinity Flex streaming media set-top box for free to internet customers, intensifying competition with Roku Inc. This move caused Roku's stock to tumble nearly 14% as investors reacted to the increased competition from a major player like Comcast, alongside existing rivals Amazon, Apple, and Google. The Xfinity Flex aims to simplify the streaming experience by offering access to various services and free content.

title: Netflix Stock Drops After CEO Acknowledges ‘Tough Competition’ Coming From Disney, Apple
summary: Netflix's stock dropped significantly after CEO Reed Hastings acknowledged the upcoming "tough competition" from Disney Plus and Apple TV Plus. Investors were unnerved by his comments, despite Netflix having previously addressed the increased competitive intensity in the streaming sector. An analyst, however, predicted a rebound for Netflix's stock, arguing that new entrants won't necessarily steal existing subscribers.

title: Avantis U.S. Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:AVUS)
summary: The Avantis U.S. Equity ETF (AVUS) is an exchange-traded fund that tracks the Russell 3000 index, launched on September 24, 2019, and managed by American Century Investments. It features a diversified portfolio of US equities with a bias towards smaller, more profitable, or value companies. As of January 9, 2026, AVUS was trading at $115.05, with $10.80 billion in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.15%.

title: U.S. Grants Apple 10 Exemptions from Tariffs on Chinese Imports
summary: The Trump administration has granted Apple Inc. exemptions from tariffs on 10 specific items imported from China, including a power supply and a logic board. These items were part of the $200 billion worth of Chinese imports first subjected to tariffs in September 2018. U.S. trade officials are currently evaluating additional requests from Apple for tariff relief.

title: Apple’s new iPhones reportedly selling better than their predecessors
summary: Analysts from Wedbush and Instinet indicate positive demand trends for Apple's new iPhone 11 lineup based on preorder data. Longer shipment wait times compared to last year's models suggest potentially better sales. This comes despite initial concerns about limited new features and lack of 5G compatibility.

title: Netflix Stock Breaks Down as Competition Heats Up
summary: Netflix stock fell following increased competition from Apple and Disney, causing CEO Reed Hastings to emphasize the company's core strategy of in-house content. Despite concerns about subscriber misses, an analyst suggests that the broader trend of shifting to streaming will outweigh competitive headwinds. Technically, the stock is showing bearish indicators and could see further decline, with its next earnings report due on October 15, 2019.

title: Apple’s new Mac Pro to be made in Texas
summary: Apple announced that its newly redesigned Mac Pro will be manufactured in Austin, Texas, at the same facility where previous Mac Pros have been made since 2013. This move is part of Apple's commitment to US economic growth, with the new Mac Pro incorporating 2.5 times more American-made components than its predecessor. CEO Tim Cook highlighted Apple's significant investments in the US economy, supporting 450,000 jobs with US suppliers and contributing to the goal of investing $350 billion by 2023.

title: Apple says Mac Pro manufacturing will remain in Austin
summary: Apple announced on Monday that it will continue manufacturing its Mac Pro computers in Austin, Texas, reversing earlier reports of a potential move to China. This decision comes after the company secured tariff exemptions from the U.S. government for key imported parts, allowing it to avoid additional costs. Apple (AAPL) emphasized its commitment to U.S. manufacturing and its significant investment in the American economy, including its operations and planned expansion in Austin.

title: Apple-launched China Clean Energy Fund invests in three wind farms
summary: The Apple-launched China Clean Energy Fund has made its first investments in three wind farms in Hunan and Hubei provinces, delivering a combined 134 megawatts to China's national power grid. These projects are part of a larger initiative by Apple and 10 of its suppliers to invest nearly $300 million by 2022 to develop 1 gigawatt of renewable energy. The fund aims to support China's renewable energy goals, benefit local economies, and provide greater purchasing power for suppliers transitioning to clean energy.

title: Netflix stock turns negative for 2019, but trader sees another big drop ahead
summary: Netflix stock has turned negative for 2019, with one trader predicting a further significant drop due to a lack of support on the charts until $231. This decline is attributed to increasing streaming competition and a recent decrease in subscriber numbers, leading analysts to advise investors to stay away from the stock for the near future.

title: McDonald’s uses Alexa and Google to help with hiring
summary: McDonald's is implementing a new voice-activated job application system using Amazon's Alexa and Google Assistant to streamline hiring. This initiative, dubbed McDonald’s Apply Thru, allows job seekers to start applications through voice commands and is part of a broader technology push by the company to attract employees in a tight job market. The feature is available in multiple countries and aims to help McDonald's fill constant staffing needs across its 38,000 restaurants worldwide.

title: BlackBerry CFO to become chief revenue officer
summary: Steven Capelli, BlackBerry's CFO, is transitioning to a newly created Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) position to boost revenue, with Deputy CFO Steve Rai stepping in as the new CFO. This strategic move aims to increase revenue and turn a profit for the struggling enterprise software and services company, especially following the acquisition of cybersecurity firm Cylance Inc. Despite a stock drop after the announcement, the company remains focused on execution and growth in the secure Internet of Things and auto markets.

title: Abbott nets FDA approval for 'low-dose' long-term neurostimulator
summary: Abbott has received FDA approval for its new Proclaim XR system, a "low-dose" spinal cord neurostimulation system designed to treat chronic pain for up to 10 years without recharging. This system uses mild, patterned bursts of electricity to alter pain signals, extending battery life while allowing users to manage their pain via Apple devices. Clinical studies demonstrated significant pain relief with minimal battery usage, offering a convenient, long-term solution for chronic pain sufferers.

title: Las acciones argentinas lograron sortear el derrumbe en Wall Street: cuál ganó más
summary: Argentine stocks defied a general downturn on Wall Street, which saw major U.S. indices fall by an average of 1.7%. While global economic recession fears impacted American companies like American Express, Apple, and Exxon Mobil, Argentine ADRs such as Banco Macro (+4%), Grupo Financiero Galicia (+2.7%), and Banco Supervielle (+2.6%) saw gains. The local S&P Merval index also rose by 2.3%, with Transportadora de Gas del Norte leading with a 7.2% increase.

title: DBS must bulk up or lose fintech turf in South-east Asia
summary: DBS, despite being South-east Asia's most tech-savvy bank, is losing out on prime digital banking deals to larger rivals like Citi in the rapidly growing South-east Asian internet economy. The article argues that DBS needs to significantly expand its presence and assets in the region, potentially through "bolt-on" acquisitions, to remain competitive against international players who are aggressively pursuing fintech opportunities. The future success of DBS in this market will also depend on political factors and regional economic integration.

title: This Fund Is Better Than It Looks
summary: Artisan Value Investor (ARTLX) has a Bronze rating from Morningstar, despite some recent underperformance, due to its experienced team and disciplined, valuation-sensitive approach. The fund's strategy often leads it to differ significantly from its index and peers, favoring economically sensitive sectors over traditionally defensive ones. While its performance has been mixed historically, its contrarian approach has paid off in value-favoring markets, and the firm's overall parent company strategy is highly effective.

title: 6 ETFs to Watch as Momentum Factor Favors Apple, Netflix
summary: This article discusses how momentum investing is currently favoring Apple and Netflix, highlighting the "momentum crowd's" interest in these stocks. It recommends six ETFs that have significant allocations to Apple and Netflix, providing details on their investment strategies and underlying indices. The piece categorizes the ETFs based on whether they prioritize Apple or Netflix holdings.

title: PayPal is launching a Venmo credit card to help monetize the payment app
summary: PayPal's Venmo is partnering with Synchrony Financial to launch a Venmo-branded credit card in 2020, aiming to monetize its popular peer-to-peer payments app which has grown to 40 million users but has yet to turn a profit. This move follows the launch of a Venmo debit card last year and is intended to round out its financial offerings, with Synchrony handling the banking side and a profit-sharing agreement in place. The credit card will allow users to manage bills directly through the Venmo app and will explore rewards and cash-back options.

title: Class Action Claims Apple Violated Illinois Privacy Law by Collecting Siri Users’ Voiceprints
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple in Illinois, alleging the company unlawfully collects and stores voiceprints of Siri users without consent, violating the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA). The lawsuit claims Apple failed to inform users about the collection, purpose, and retention schedule of their biometric data, which can be used to identify individuals and potentially access sensitive personal information. The suit seeks up to $5,000 for each violation and aims to cover all Illinois Siri users whose voiceprints were collected by Apple.

title: Visa, Mastercard Say They Just Made Mobile Shopping a Lot Easier
summary: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover have partnered to launch a one-click checkout option for online mobile shopping, aiming to simplify the purchasing process and reduce "online cart abandonment." This new service competes with existing offerings from PayPal and Apple Pay by allowing consumers to save their payment details and bypass the need to manually enter credit card information for each transaction. The initiative seeks to address the frustration associated with inputting credit card details on small screens, which currently costs retailers billions in lost sales.

title: Arlo Announces Apple® HomeKit™ Compatibility For Arlo Ultra
summary: Arlo Technologies announced that its flagship Arlo Ultra Security Camera Systems are now compatible with Apple HomeKit, enabling iPhone and iPad users to control cameras via the Apple Home app and Siri. This compatibility, rolled out via a free firmware update, allows for advanced smart home automations and notifications. The Arlo Ultra features 4K HDR video, color night vision, and a 180-degree field of view, starting at $399.99 for a one-camera system.

title: Can Apple Stock Hold New Highs?
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) has rallied to an all-time high ahead of its third-quarter earnings, driven by strong iPhone demand and the upcoming Apple TV+ streaming service. Despite this optimism, trade tensions with China and the reaction to earnings remain key uncertainties. Technical analysis suggests potential for new highs, but also highlights risks if negative catalysts emerge.

title: Jabil details costs of its deal with Johnson & Johnson
summary: Jabil Inc. has detailed the costs associated with its "transformational" deal to acquire Johnson & Johnson Medical Devices assets, spending $270.3 million in cash and an additional $52.7 million for onboarding and integration since February. The acquisition is part of Jabil's diversification strategy, aiming to reduce its reliance on Apple Inc. while expanding its medical device manufacturing footprint. The company reported a significant increase in revenue and net income for FY 2019, reflecting the initial impact of the Johnson & Johnson operations.

title: First year of Apple Entrepreneur Camp offers invaluable experiences to 100 innovators
summary: Apple Entrepreneur Camp has completed its first year, providing invaluable experiences to 100 innovators from 42 companies, all founded and led by women. Participants receive hands-on technology labs, code-level guidance from Apple experts, and mentorship, leading to significant improvements in their apps and businesses. The program aims to empower women leaders in tech, helping them gain confidence, build networks, and secure venture capital.

title: Tesla Stock Is on Fire and Shorts Are Feeling the Heat
summary: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) shares surged over 20% after the company reported better-than-expected Q3 earnings, returned to profitability, and had positive free cash flow. This positive news created significant heat for short sellers, wiping out a large portion of their year-to-date profits, as many had been betting against the company. Although short interest has been decreasing, the rally is described more as a "lingering hug" rather than a classic short squeeze due to the gradual nature of short covering.

title: Adaptable couch & chair furniture store comes to Boise mall
summary: Lovesac, the national furniture company specializing in reconfigurable couches and foam-filled chairs, is opening a new store at Boise Towne Square next to the Apple Store. This marks Lovesac's second attempt at a Boise Towne Square location, having previously operated on the upper level. The company, known for its Sactionals and Lovesacs, plans to open approximately 20 new stores annually following a successful IPO.

title: This ETF Suggests There May Be A Tidal Change Coming
summary: The article discusses a potential shift in market dominance from growth to value stocks, signaled by the SPDR S&P 500 Value ETF (SPYV) reaching a new high after a prolonged period. Experts suggest that as the global economy slows, valuation will become more critical, prompting investors to rotate from growth-heavy portfolios to value. This shift is compared to a "tidal change" where value could outperform growth after years of underperformance.

title: Apple, United in Early Discussions to Upgrade SFO Terminal
summary: Apple (AAPL) and United Airlines (UAL) are in early discussions regarding potential upgrades to United's terminal at San Francisco International Airport (SFO). While specific details remain vague, Apple's team has been assessing various areas within the terminal. The talks are still in their very initial stages.

title: Apple Revamping Smart Home Efforts After Falling Behind Amazon, Google
summary: Apple Inc. is increasing hiring for a team dedicated to developing new smart-home software and devices to compete with Google and Amazon.com Inc. This initiative aims to expand Apple's smart-home platform, encourage more third-party accessory integration with the iPhone and Siri, and explore new home device possibilities beyond the HomePod speaker.

title: Apple Debuts Higher-End AirPods With Noise-Cancellation and Water-Resistance
summary: Apple Inc. announced the AirPods Pro, a new higher-end version of its wireless headphones featuring noise-cancellation, water-resistance, and a new design, priced at $249. This new model will sell alongside the existing $159 version and includes features like interchangeable tips, Adaptive EQ, and transparency mode. The launch comes as Apple faces increased competition in the wireless earbud market and looks to further segment its successful AirPods product line.

title: Apple and United Airlines may partner to renovate SFO terminal
summary: Apple and United Airlines are reportedly in early talks to partner on a renovation of a terminal at San Francisco International Airport (SFO). Apple, known for its sleek designs and a major client of United, is being considered to help modernize parts of the airport terminal. The discussions are in their preliminary stages, with specific upgrade details yet to be decided.

title: Introducing the Global X Video Games & Esports ETF (HERO)
summary: Global X has launched the Video Games & Esports ETF (HERO) to capitalize on the rapidly expanding video game industry, which has grown into a global entertainment powerhouse exceeding revenues of movies and professional sports. The ETF focuses on companies involved in game development, streaming, esports leagues, and hardware production. The article outlines the industry's evolution from a niche market to a widespread cultural phenomenon with significant revenue generation through digital distribution, recurring revenue models, and the rise of streaming and esports.

title: Apple Lost Ground to Huawei, Samsung Before iPhone 11 Debut
summary: Apple Inc. saw its smartphone shipments decline by 7% in the third quarter of 2019, just before the iPhone 11 release contributing to it losing ground to competitors. Samsung Electronics Co. and Huawei Technologies Co. capitalized on this period, with Samsung growing sales by 11% through cheaper models and Huawei's shipments rising 29% due to a strong home market. This occurred during the first quarterly increase in global smartphone sales in two years.

title: Apple Stock Testing All-Time High After Strong Quarter
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter results, beating profit and revenue estimates, driven by aggressive growth in wearable items and solid American sales. Despite a decline in iPhone revenues and China sales, the company raised its first-quarter guidance. The stock is testing all-time highs but analysts suggest it might be fully valued, and technical indicators warn of potential short-term risks despite the strong performance.

title: Apple Names Five VPs, Including Return of Early IPhone Executive
summary: Apple Inc. has promoted several key executives to the role of vice president, a significant title within the company. These promotions include Paul Meade for hardware engineering, Jon Andrews for software engineering, Gary Geaves for acoustics, and Kaiann Drance for marketing. The new appointments follow recent departures of other high-profile executives in design, public relations, and retail.

title: Review: Bob Iger’s The Ride of A Lifetime: Lessons Learned From 15 Years as CEO of the Walt Disney Co.
summary: This review discusses Bob Iger's book, "The Ride of a Lifetime," highlighting its blend of personal and professional insights from his 15 years as CEO of The Walt Disney Company. The book delves into Iger's handling of major deals, including the acquisitions of Pixar, Marvel, Lucasfilm, and FOX, and his interactions with prominent figures like Steve Jobs and George Lucas. While praising the book's insights into Disney's growth, the review also notes omissions regarding certain controversies and personnel issues during Iger's tenure.

title: Early iPhone Marketing Executive Returns To Apple As VP
summary: Bob Borchers, an early iPhone marketing executive, has returned to Apple as Vice President of product marketing. This article also details several other significant marketing leadership changes, including new CMOs for FASTSIGNS International, Destination XL Group, Yasso Frozen Greek Yogurt, and Jos. A. Bank, as well as the departure of JUUL Labs' CMO Craig Brommers.

title: What Has Been Driving Akamai Technologies’ Performance?
summary: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ: AKAM) exceeded Q3 revenue and EPS expectations, primarily driven by strong growth in its security solutions business. This positive performance led to an upward revision of the company's guidance for Q4 and full-year 2019. The article highlights Akamai's shift from its core content delivery network (CDN) business, which faces competition from large internet players, towards leveraging its network reliability and insights to build a robust security business and become a key player in edge computing.

title: Apple Pledges $2.5 Billion for Affordable Housing in California
summary: Apple Inc. has committed $2.5 billion to combat California's housing crisis, funneling funds into new housing production, mortgage assistance for first-time homebuyers, and land for affordable housing development. The initiative aims to address the soaring costs of living that affect essential service providers and includes specific allocations for lower-income housing in the Bay Area and support for homelessness programs. This comprehensive plan involves a $1 billion affordable housing investment fund, a $1 billion mortgage assistance fund, $300 million in company-owned land, and $200 million for Bay Area housing, including a public-private fund and support for Destination: Home.

title: Expansion Planned at Culver City's Blackwelder Office Campus
summary: Kilroy Realty Corp. recently purchased the Blackwelder office complex in Culver City for $186 million with plans to significantly expand the existing 19-building, 158,000-square-foot campus. The property, currently fully leased with tenants paying below-market rates, is located in an area benefiting from the growth of content production companies like Amazon, Apple, and HBO.

title: Apple’s Gadgets Co-Star in Its New TV Drama
summary: Apple's flagship TV series "The Morning Show" not only aims to attract subscribers for its new streaming service but also serves as an extensive advertisement for Apple's various gadgets. iPhones, iPads, and Macs are prominently featured throughout the drama, appearing alongside stars Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, and Steve Carell. The devices are given significant screen time, highlighting their role in the show's visual narrative and reinforcing their presence in the tech giant's business strategy.

title: Netflix co-founder Marc Randolph says company’s ‘secret weapon’ sets itself apart from Disney, other streaming services
summary: Netflix co-founder Marc Randolph believes the company's "focus" on streaming and movies is its "secret weapon" against new competitors like Disney+ and Apple TV+. Despite growing competition and concerns about Netflix's revenue stream, Randolph emphasizes the company's startup mentality and willingness to adapt, assuring its continued presence in the evolving digital streaming landscape.

title: Here are the best analyst calls of the week on Wall Street including Nike, Sonos
summary: This article highlights top analyst calls of the week, with Morgan Stanley naming Nike a top pick and DA Davidson suggesting Sonos as an acquisition candidate for Apple. Stephens initiated coverage on The Trade Desk with an Overweight rating, while Morgan Stanley also identified Tal Education Group as a potential $100 billion company through online education. Oppenheimer maintained a positive long-term view on Wayfair despite recent stock struggles.

title: ProShares S&P Technology Dividend Aristocrats ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:TDV)
summary: This article provides details on the ProShares S&P Technology Dividend Aristocrats ETF (TDV), including its current price, holdings, and news. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, key statistics, and a list of its top ten holdings, such as Cognex, Avnet, and Apple. The fund aims to track an equal-weighted index of U.S. technology companies with at least seven years of dividend growth.

title: Viral Tweet About Apple Card Leads to Goldman Sachs Probe
summary: A Wall Street regulator has launched a probe into Goldman Sachs Group Inc.'s credit card practices following a viral tweet from a tech entrepreneur alleging gender discrimination in the Apple Card's credit limit algorithms. David Heinemeier Hansson claimed the Apple Card gave him a credit limit 20 times higher than his wife's, sparking online controversy and even attracting attention from Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak. The card is part of Goldman's new main street business lines.

title: Microsoft Net Income by Quarter: FY Q1 1996 – Q3 2021
summary: This article provides a quarterly breakdown of Microsoft's net income from fiscal Q1 1990 to Q3 2021, highlighting significant financial milestones and growth figures. It details key periods of high net profit, including a substantial 48.6% YoY increase in Q4 2019, and notes a significant net loss in FY Q2 2018 due to a tax payment on overseas profits. The article also includes a brief company profile of Microsoft Corporation, detailing its founding, key products, and major acquisitions.

title: Microsoft Revenue and Net Income by Year: FY 1990 – 2021
summary: This article provides an overview of Microsoft's annual revenue and net income from fiscal year 1990 to 2021, highlighting key milestones like record revenues and net profits. It details significant financial achievements and dips, along with a brief history of the company's formation, key product launches, IPO, and major acquisitions. The article focuses on financial performance and strategic company developments over the decades.

title: Microsoft Revenue by Year: FY 1990 – 2021
summary: This article details Microsoft's annual revenue from fiscal year 1990 to 2021, highlighting key milestones such as surpassing $50 billion in 2007 and $100 billion in 2018. It notes that Microsoft achieved its highest-ever annual revenue of $143,015 million in fiscal year 2020, driven by growth across all its segments. The article also provides a brief overview of Microsoft's history, major software products, and significant acquisitions.

title: Microsoft Net Income by Year: FY 1990 - 2021
summary: This article analyzes Microsoft's annual net income from fiscal year 1990 to 2021, highlighting key financial milestones and significant changes in its profitability. It details the company's highest-ever annual net profit in FY 2020 and discusses factors behind fluctuations in other fiscal years, such as tax payments and acquisitions. The article also provides a brief overview of Microsoft's history and major corporate moves.

title: Microsoft Annual Revenue by Geography: Fiscal 2002 – 2021
summary: This article details Microsoft's annual revenue by geography from fiscal year 2002 to 2021, highlighting significant milestones such as the $50 billion revenue mark in the US and international markets. It notes Microsoft's global revenue reaching $125.8 billion in fiscal 2019, with over half coming from the United States. The article also provides a brief overview of Microsoft Corporation's history, key products, major acquisitions, and its valuation.

title: Nike Places All Bets On ServiceNow CEO John Donahoe
summary: Nike has appointed John Donahoe, CEO of ServiceNow and former eBay CEO, as its new CEO, succeeding Mark Parker. This move signals Nike's strategic shift towards becoming a digitally-enabled consumer platform, leveraging Donahoe's expertise in enterprise technology and digital innovation. Industry analysts view this appointment as a strong step to enhance Nike's successful digital efforts and direct-to-consumer business model.

title: Apple Plans Standalone AR and VR Gaming Headset by 2022 and Glasses Later
summary: Apple is reportedly developing a range of augmented and virtual-reality devices, leveraging a new 3-D sensor system. The company plans to release a new iPad Pro in early 2020 with this 3-D system, followed by its integration into high-end iPhones later that year. Ultimately, Apple aims to launch a standalone AR and VR gaming headset by 2022 and AR glasses by 2023.

title: Historic Apple computer prototypes set to auction at Bonhams
summary: Three rare Apple computer prototypes, including an original Apple-1, an early Apple Macintosh prototype, and the unreleased Video Pad, are set to be auctioned at Bonhams. These devices represent significant milestones in Apple's history, from its early personal computing innovations to concepts that predated the iPhone. The auction offers a glimpse into the foundational and experimental stages of one of the world's largest companies.

title: Announcing the 2020 Rankings of America’s Most JUST Companies
summary: JUST Capital has released its 2020 Rankings of America's Most JUST Companies, with Microsoft taking the top spot for the second year. This initiative highlights companies that prioritize stakeholders beyond just shareholders, aligning with a recent Business Roundtable statement. The rankings analyze 922 companies on issues important to workers, customers, communities, the environment, and shareholders, and are used in the Goldman Sachs Asset Management's JUST U.S. Large Cap Equity ETF (JUST).

title: Microsoft tops list of the most ‘just’ US corporations
summary: Microsoft Corp. has topped the list of America's Most JUST Companies for the second consecutive year, recognized for its commitment to workers, communities, and the environment. The nonprofit JUST Capital's rankings highlight corporations that prioritize all stakeholders, not just shareholders, addressing concerns about wealth inequality and climate change. The study analyzed 922 companies on issues like fair wages, environmental protection, and ethical business practices, with tech companies like NVIDIA and Apple also ranking high.

title: AMD Radeon™ Pro 5000M Series Mobile GPUs Bring High-Performance AMD RDNA Architecture to Apple’s New 16-inch MacBook Pro
summary: AMD announced that its new Radeon Pro 5500M and 5300M mobile GPUs, based on the 7nm AMD RDNA architecture, will power Apple's new 16-inch MacBook Pro. These are the first 7nm discrete mobile GPUs for professional users, offering up to 4.0 TFLOPS of performance and up to 8GB of GDDR6 memory to enhance video editing, 3D content creation, and macOS game development. The GPUs aim to provide creative professionals with powerful visual fidelity for demanding applications.

title: Through Apple Heart Study, Stanford Medicine researchers show wearable technology can help detect atrial fibrillation
summary: A Stanford Medicine study conducted with Apple found that wearable technology like the Apple Watch can safely and effectively identify heart rate irregularities that indicate atrial fibrillation. The Apple Heart Study, the largest virtual study to date, demonstrated that only a small percentage of participants received irregular pulse notifications, alleviating concerns about over-notification. The findings suggest a significant role for wearable tech in early detection of this often-undiagnosed and dangerous condition.

title: Google teams with Citigroup bank to offer checking accounts from 2020
summary: Google is partnering with Citigroup bank and credit unions to launch checking accounts under the codename "Cache" starting in 2020. This venture aims to offer banking services in the US, with Google emphasizing a partnership approach where financial and compliance activities are handled by the banks and credit unions. Despite potential privacy concerns, Google believes this collaborative method will be more sustainable and beneficial for increasing digital financial activities.

title: ‘Not on Netflix’ and proud of it, Tubi boasts in ads
summary: Tubi, a free ad-supported streaming service, is launching a new advertising campaign that highlights its content not available on Netflix to attract viewers suffering from "subscription fatigue." The campaign, featuring actor Chris Noth, emphasizes Tubi's free model and its extensive library of movies and TV shows, including a "Not on Netflix" category. Despite some concerns about promoting competitors, Tubi aims to position itself as a complementary service to paid subscriptions.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff Runs a $143 Billion Company From an iPhone. Here's Why That Makes Perfect Sense
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff manages his $143 billion company entirely from his iPhone, claiming he no longer even owns a computer. This article explores why this approach makes perfect sense for a CEO, emphasizing the iPhone's role as a powerful communication tool and how its limitations can force a leader to focus on essential tasks. It highlights that modern business software, including Salesforce's own apps, are designed for mobile use, making a smartphone an effective tool for running a large enterprise.

title: Trump Considering Whether Apple Should be Exempt From China Tariffs
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: President Donald Trump stated on Wednesday he was considering whether to exempt Apple Inc. from tariffs on imports from China after touring an Apple manufacturing plant in Austin, Texas. Trump acknowledged the competitive landscape with companies like Samsung and the impact of existing trade deals. This consideration follows Apple's requests for tariff waivers and the company's commitment to boosting U.S. manufacturing.

title: Trump Says ‘Looking’ at Exemption for Apple on China Tariffs
summary: During a tour of an Apple Inc. assembly plant in Texas, President Donald Trump stated he is considering exempting the iPhone manufacturer from tariffs imposed on goods imported from China. This comes as trade tensions continue to impact global supply chains. The potential exemption could offer relief to Apple amidst ongoing trade disputes.

title: Four for Free? T-Mobile Has a Major iPhone “on Us” Deal for Magenta Friday
summary: T-Mobile announced new holiday offers starting Magenta Friday, November 22, allowing customers to get up to four iPhone 11 or iPhone XR devices "on us" after 24 months of bill credits, only paying the sales tax. The deal requires activating a new voice line on a qualifying rate plan and trading in a qualifying iPhone, with options also available for iPhone XS, iPhone 8, and Apple Watch models. T-Mobile's "Essentials" plan enables customers to combine four unlimited lines with the iPhone offer, alongside other benefits like international texting and robust network coverage.

title: If You Invested $1,000 in Berkshire’s IPO, This Is How Much Money You'd Have Now
summary: This article illustrates the immense power of long-term compounding by examining an investment in Berkshire Hathaway's IPO. It details how a hypothetical $1,000 investment in 1964, when Warren Buffett took over, would be worth millions today. The piece showcases Buffett's investment strategy, highlighting the significant growth achieved by focusing on high-quality businesses with strong management.

title: What to look for in a TV on Black Friday?
summary: This article provides comprehensive advice on what to consider when buying a TV during Black Friday sales. It covers key factors such as screen size, resolution (4K vs. 8K), HDR technology, connectivity (HDMI ports), panel type (OLED vs. LCD), and smart TV features. The author also advises on distinguishing between genuine bargains and lower-quality brands.

title: Rentokil Initial is ranked the best place to work in Britain in a survey by Indeed
summary: Pest control and hygiene firm Rentokil Initial has been ranked the best company to work for in the UK by Indeed, unseating last year's winner Apple. The company was praised for its job security, advancement opportunities, work-life balance, and positive culture, based on reviews from its employees. The survey highlighted that companies need to offer more than just attractive salaries to secure top talent in today's job market.

title: Fincrime Briefing: Westpac CEO, chair out amid laundering probe, OFAC dings Apple, Samsung pays $75 million on FCPA failings, and more
source: Association of Certified Financial Crime Specialists
source_domain: Association of Certified Financial Crime Specialists
summary: This fincrime briefing covers significant enforcement actions and compliance challenges. Westpac's CEO and chair are stepping down amid a money-laundering probe, Samsung Heavy Industries faces a $75 million penalty for FCPA violations, and Apple was fined nearly $500,000 by OFAC for sanctions breaches related to a steroid trafficking network. The article also highlights the slow progress in securing the integrity of the EU's financial system and emphasizes the need for a stronger culture of compliance across various industries.

title: Rentokil Initial tops league table of UK's most popular employers
summary: Rentokil Initial has been named the best company to work for in the UK, according to data from Indeed, surpassing 2018 winner Apple. The pest control and hygiene firm was praised for job security, advancement opportunities, work-life balance, and positive culture. British firms, including several high street brands and financial institutions, dominate the top 25 list of private sector employers, with employees valuing good work-life balance and positive work culture.

title: El lugar más árido del planeta está amenazado por culpa de la gran demanda de vehículos eléctricos y teléfonos inteligentes
summary: The Atacama Desert in Chile, the driest place on Earth, is facing severe threats due to the high demand for lithium used in electric vehicles and smartphones. Lithium extraction processes extensively exploit the desert's scarce water resources, causing irreparable damage to the fragile ecosystem and endangering indigenous communities and native wildlife. Despite scientific evidence and local community complaints about reduced water levels and environmental degradation, mining companies and the Chilean government have largely dismissed these concerns, prioritizing economic interests over environmental protection.

title: Apple worth more than US stock index’s energy sector
summary: Apple's market capitalization has surged to nearly $1.2 trillion, surpassing the entire S&P 500 energy sector. This growth is attributed to a strong share rally and the success of its wearables division, contrasting with the struggles of the energy sector due to stagnant oil prices and investor concerns over climate-unfriendly industries.

title: DarioHealth Launches Digital Diabetes Program on Walmart.com
summary: DarioHealth announced the expansion of its digital diabetes management program through Walmart.com, offering its MyDario solution to consumers. This initiative provides a comprehensive digital tool for managing diabetes, hypertension, nutrition, and exercise. The program will be available through Walmart's significant online distribution channel, leveraging the retailer's status as a major e-commerce player and innovator in healthcare.

title: CEOs of Microsoft, Apple, Google and more urge Trump administration to stay in Paris climate deal
summary: 73 leaders from major companies like Microsoft, Apple, Google, and Tesla, alongside labor unions, signed a letter urging the Trump administration to remain in the Paris Agreement on climate change. They argue that staying in the agreement will strengthen U.S. competitiveness, create jobs, and foster innovation. This appeal comes after the Trump administration initiated withdrawal from the accord, with notable absences from the signatory list including Amazon's Jeff Bezos and Facebook's Mark Zuckerberg.

title: Intel in talks to buy Israeli AI chip co Habana Labs
summary: Intel is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire Israeli AI chip startup Habana Labs for an estimated $1 billion. Habana Labs, founded in 2016, previously raised $120 million, with Intel Capital leading its latest financing round in November 2018. If the acquisition proceeds, it would significantly strengthen Intel's position in the AI chip market, where Habana Labs has developed specialized AI Training and Inference Processors.

title: Apple Has Biggest Drop Since August
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares experienced their biggest one-day drop since August due to escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and China. This followed President Trump's statement about being willing to wait another year for a trade agreement and Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross's warning of impending tariffs if no changes occur within two weeks.

title: Dash, Tidepool Loop, Horizon: the Future of Omnipod
summary: This article discusses the future of Omnipod insulin management systems, focusing on the Omnipod Dash, Tidepool Loop, and Omnipod Horizon. It highlights the increased availability of Omnipod Dash, the upcoming hybrid closed-loop systems expected in late 2020, and the shift towards Bluetooth technology for enhanced integration and patient flexibility. The piece also details key differences and functionalities of Tidepool Loop and Omnipod Horizon, including their algorithms and device compatibility.

title: Should You Like Apple Inc.’s (NASDAQ:AAPL) High Return On Capital Employed?
summary: This article examines Apple Inc.'s (NASDAQ:AAPL) high Return On Capital Employed (ROCE) and its implications for investors. It details the ROCE calculation, compares Apple's 27% ROCE to the Tech industry average of 9.2%, and discusses the impact of current liabilities on this metric. The piece concludes that Apple's strong ROCE suggests efficient capital utilization and makes it an attractive prospect for further research.

title: Apple buys first-ever carbon-free aluminum from Alcoa-Rio Tinto venture
summary: Apple Inc. has purchased the first commercial batch of carbon-free aluminum from Elysis, a joint venture between Alcoa Corp and Rio Tinto. This innovative aluminum, produced using a new smelting process that emits only oxygen, will be used in future Apple products. The move aligns with growing consumer and investor demand for more sustainable manufacturing practices from major companies.

title: PPG's price increase among many in the global coatings industry
summary: PPG has announced a price increase on its industrial coatings, joining several other companies in the global coatings industry that have recently implemented similar price hikes. This move by PPG aligns with the actions of its main competitors in the market. Each PPG Paints store offers a wide variety of PPG-branded paint products, serving both residential and commercial applications.

title: Opinion: These companies have low labor costs that can turn into a stock payday for you
summary: Goldman Sachs analysts suggest investors focus on companies with low labor costs to counter the impact of rising wages on corporate profits, especially in a slow-growth environment. They identified 50 companies across all 11 S&P 500 sectors, including Apple, Netflix, and Take-Two Interactive Software, that are relatively insulated from wage pressures. This strategy aims to help investors achieve better returns despite the tight labor market.

title: Which groups earn the most at Intel? Company offers peek at pay data other tech firms won’t show
summary: Intel released compensation data for its U.S. employees from 2017 and 2018, revealing that white and Asian males were the highest earners in most occupation categories. The company, which is transparent about its diversity efforts, aims to encourage other tech firms to share similar data. This disclosure comes as the EEOC for the first time required companies to include pay data.

title: Apple’s New Mac Pro Can Cost $52,000. That’s Without the $400 Wheels
summary: Apple Inc. has launched its new Mac Pro desktop computer, with pricing options that can push the cost above $50,000. While the base model starts at $5,999, a fully configured machine can exceed $52,000, not including optional $400 wheels. The computer is manufactured in Austin, Texas, following tariff relief from the Trump administration.

title: Why Facebook and Google Dropped Out of the Top 10 in Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work List
summary: Big tech companies like Facebook and Google have fallen out of the Top 10 in Glassdoor's annual "best places to work" rankings. This shift comes as both companies face increased criticism and internal employee dissent regarding various issues and executive decisions. HubSpot Inc. took the top spot, while Microsoft Corp. was one of the few big tech companies to improve its ranking.

title: 'Fully Loaded,' Apple’s New Desktop Mac Pro Costs Over $52,000 — and Don't Forget the $400 Wheels
summary: Apple has launched its new Mac Pro desktop computer with pricing options that can exceed $52,000 for a fully loaded version, excluding the optional $400 wheels. The base model starts at $5,999, but upgrades like increased RAM to 1.5 terabytes cost an additional $25,000, significantly raising the total price. This high cost, especially when factoring in additional purchases like the Pro Display XDR and its stand, has drawn comparisons to the price of a car.

title: XP Stock Price Soars on First Day, Outperforms Saudi Aramco IPO
summary: Brazilian finance firm XP skyrocketed 24% in its multibillion-dollar IPO, outperforming Saudi Aramco's debut. XP's shares surged as much as 26% on its first day of trading, raising nearly $2 billion and valuing the company at roughly $18 billion. This strong performance comes after a year of several disappointing IPOs, including Uber and Lyft.

title: Buy or Sell a Car via Mobile with the New eBay Motors App
summary: eBay Motors has launched an all-new, automotive-focused app for Apple iPhone and Android, designed to streamline the buying and selling of vehicles. The app leverages advanced technology, including AI and machine learning, to offer features like license plate scanning for automatic listing population and AI-powered image sorting, promising an elevated experience for both buyers and sellers. Users can now easily list a car in under five minutes, browse a vast inventory with personalized homepages, and utilize robust filters.

title: Apple IPO makes instant millionaires, December 12, 1980
summary: On December 12, 1980, Apple launched its IPO, selling 4.6 million shares at $22 each under the stock symbol “AAPL” on NASDAQ, generating more capital than any IPO since Ford Motor Company in 1956. The IPO instantly created about 300 millionaires, including many Apple employees, and propelled Steve Jobs' wealth to $217 million. Despite fluctuations, Apple's stock continued to grow, particularly with the introduction of consumer electronics like the iPod and iPhone.

title: Apple launches new “Ultimate Rivals” sports game franchise on Apple Arcade
summary: Apple has launched a new sports game franchise called "Ultimate Rivals" on Apple Arcade, developed by Bit Fry Game Studios. The first title, "Ultimate Rivals: The Rink," allows players to combine superstar athletes from multiple professional sports for two-on-two hockey matches. The franchise, which features licensed athletes from major sports leagues, aims to offer innovative gameplay and will expand with an NBA-licensed basketball game in spring 2020.

title: Your complete guide to investing with a conscience, a $30 trillion market just getting started
summary: ESG investing, which considers environmental, social, and governance factors, grew to over $30 trillion in 2018 and is projected to reach $50 trillion in the next two decades. Once considered niche and potentially detrimental to returns, ESG strategies have proven to be market-beating and are gaining significant momentum as investors demand greater transparency and companies face consequences for not adapting. The article explores various ESG strategies, highlighting their growing popularity, market performance, and the factors driving this surge, particularly among younger generations and institutions.

title: Intel buys Israeli AI start-up for $2 billion
summary: Intel has acquired Israeli artificial intelligence firm Habana Labs for approximately $2 billion. Habana Labs, which develops dedicated AI processors called Goya, will operate as an independent business unit reporting to Intel’s data platforms group. This acquisition marks one of the largest private exits in Israel and is particularly significant given Habana Labs had only raised $120 million prior to the deal.

title: Jabil narrows the CEO-to-worker pay ratio
summary: Jabil's CEO, Mark Mondello, maintained a flat compensation of $11.4 million in fiscal year 2019, but the CEO-to-worker pay ratio significantly narrowed to 1,605:1 from 2,238:1 in the previous year. This change occurred despite an increase in the median employee's total compensation. The article details executive compensation, financial performance, and upcoming shareholder votes on executive pay for the St. Petersburg-based manufacturing solutions company.

title: Aeva launches ‘4D’ LiDAR on chip for autonomous driving
summary: Aeva has announced the launch of its "4D" LiDAR-on-chip system, Aeries, for autonomous vehicles, which integrates all key LiDAR elements into a miniaturized photonics chip, significantly reducing size and cost while offering advanced capabilities like instant velocity measurement. This technology is expected to cost less than $500 at scale and has received a significant investment from Porsche SE, a majority voting shareholder of the Volkswagen Group, highlighting its potential to address a major roadblock for autonomous driving adoption. The Aeries system will be officially launched at CES 2020.

title: SP Funds S&P 500 Sharia Industry Exclusions ETF (SPUS) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: The SP Funds S&P 500 Sharia Industry Exclusions ETF (SPUS) tracks a market-cap weighted index of S&P 500 stocks adhering to Sharia-compliant principles. The fund's top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: SAP best tech workplace in India, followed by Adobe, VMware: Report
summary: SAP has been recognized as the top-rated technology workplace in India for 2019, based on employee ratings and reviews on job site Indeed. The survey also highlighted that a significant majority of job-seekers prioritize employer reputation and online company reviews when considering new job opportunities.

summary: This article discusses the investment opportunity in Saudi Aramco, the world's most highly-valued company following its debut on the Saudi stock exchange. title: Apple has secret team working on satellites to beam data to devices
summary: Apple has a secret team of engineers working on satellite technology that could be used to beam internet services directly to devices, bypassing traditional wireless networks. The project is still in its early stages and could potentially mitigate dependence on wireless carriers or enhance location tracking for Apple devices. While the clear direction and use for satellites haven't been finalized, CEO Tim Cook's interest indicates it's a company priority.

title: Apple wants to bypass carriers and beam internet data directly to iPhones via satellites
summary: Apple is reportedly working on a secret project to beam internet data directly to iPhones via its own constellation of satellites, aiming to bypass traditional wireless carriers. This initiative could offer a more uniform experience for Apple users and create a new subscription-based revenue stream. The company hopes to deploy these satellites within the next five years, potentially disrupting the business models of current mobile carriers.

title: Vanguard Information Technology ETF: Expensive, But Should Be Okay To Continue To Own
summary: The article analyzes the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), noting its portfolio of large-cap technology stocks with competitive positions. Despite its current premium valuation, the fund is deemed suitable for continued ownership due to the long-term growth outlook of its holdings and their "moaty" characteristics like recurring revenues and strong ecosystems.

title: Netflix was the best-performing stock of the decade, delivering a more than 4,000% return
summary: Netflix emerged as the top-performing stock of the decade, achieving an astounding return of over 4,000% for investors who bought in early. Despite perennial skepticism from analysts regarding its financial metrics and increasing competition, the company's strategic expansion into international markets and significant investment in original content have been key drivers of its success. Netflix also leverages its platform to offer creative autonomy to filmmakers, attracting top talent and high-quality productions.

title: Gone in a Flash: Adobe to discontinue the plugin by end of 2020
summary: Adobe Flash, a once-ubiquitous software platform for multimedia content, is set to be discontinued at the end of 2020. This decision comes after years of vulnerability to cyber-attacks and the rise of HTML 5, which incorporates multimedia display technology, rendering Flash obsolete. Microsoft will automatically remove Flash via Windows updates, eliminating the need for users to take action.

title: Apple Extends Rally, Solidifying 2019 as Best Year in a Decade
summary: Apple (AAPL) shares continued their rally, hitting a new record high and cementing 2019 as the company's best annual performance in a decade, with an 82% increase for the year. This growth was boosted by strong holiday shopping sales of Apple products like AirPods and iPhones, and growing optimism for 2020 due to the anticipated 5G iPhone launch. Piper Jaffray forecasts Apple's shares to reach $305.

title: Apple Sued by New York Doctor Over Watch’s Heart Technology
summary: A New York University cardiologist, Dr. Joseph Wiesel, has filed a patent-infringement lawsuit against Apple Inc., claiming the Apple Watch infringes on his patented method for detecting irregular heartbeats. Dr. Wiesel is seeking compensation, asserting that Apple's Watch utilizes his invention for heartbeat monitoring. The suit was filed in federal court in Brooklyn on Friday.

title: Here are the best-performing Nasdaq stocks of 2019
summary: This article highlights the top-performing Nasdaq stocks of 2019, following the Nasdaq Composite Index's rise past 9,000 points for the first time. AMD, Lam Research, and KLA are identified as leading the list of winners. It also notes that the five largest components—Apple, Microsoft, Amazon.com, Facebook, and Google holding company Alphabet—constitute 36% of the index's market value.

title: Here are the best and worst Dow and S&P 500 stocks of 2019
summary: The article highlights the best and worst performing stocks in the Dow Jones Industrial Average and S&P 500 for the year 2019. Apple led the Dow with an 86% return, while AMD was the top performer in the S&P 500. This strong market performance is attributed to the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts and foreign capital flowing into the U.S.

title: After its strongest year in a decade, Apple stock is historically expensive on a price-to-earnings basis
summary: Apple's stock concluded its strongest year in a decade with its price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio reaching a historic high of 24.7, up from just over 13 at the beginning of 2019. This elevated P/E ratio makes Apple historically expensive compared to its past performance, although it remains below the average for the S&P 500 information technology index and many other megacap tech stocks. Factors like improved iPhone 11 expectations, anticipation of the 5G 2020 iPhone cycle, and strong wearable device performance have contributed to the increased valuation, according to Deutsche Bank analyst Jeriel Ong.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | General Motors’ Credit Card, Brought to You by Goldman Sachs
summary: Goldman Sachs is acquiring General Motors' credit-card business for approximately $2.5 billion, marking its second co-branded credit card venture after the Apple card launch. This deal will transfer over a million GM cardholders and about $8.5 billion in annual spending to Goldman. The acquisition further solidifies Goldman's expansion into Main Street lending, even as it navigates economic uncertainties due to the coronavirus pandemic.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | General Motors’ Credit Card, Brought to You by Goldman Sachs
summary: Goldman Sachs is acquiring General Motors' credit card business for approximately $2.5 billion, marking a significant step in the investment bank's expansion into Main Street lending. This deal makes the GM card Goldman's second co-branded credit card, following its successful Apple Card launch. The acquisition will bring over a million GM cardholders and around $8.5 billion in annual spending to Goldman Sachs.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | General Motors’ Credit Card, Brought to You by Goldman Sachs
summary: Goldman Sachs is buying General Motors' credit-card business for approximately $2.5 billion, marking its second co-branded credit card venture after the Apple Card. This deal will give Goldman over a million new cardholders and their significant annual spending. The transaction is expected to finalize in the coming weeks, with Goldman aiming to cross-sell its own financial products to the new customer base.

title: The inside story of the ‘AppStore’, Salesforce’s Marc Benioff, and a gifted URL to Steve Jobs
summary: This article details how Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff came up with the "AppStore" concept and registered the domain AppStore.com years before Apple's App Store launched, following advice from Steve Jobs in 2003. Although Salesforce ultimately used "AppExchange" due to customer feedback, Benioff later gifted the AppStore.com domain to Steve Jobs when Apple unveiled its own App Store in 2008. The story highlights the shared foresight between the two tech leaders regarding a software ecosystem model.

title: Insight Partners in Negotiation for a Stake in Gaming Company Moon Active
summary: Insight Partners is in advanced negotiations to acquire an 8%-10% stake in Israeli gaming company Moon Active Ltd. The deal is based on a company valuation of approximately $1.2 billion, with Insight Partners expected to pay between $100 million and $120 million to existing shareholders. Moon Active, known for its popular mobile game Coin Master, generates significant revenue, with key investors including Gigi Levy-Weiss.

title: American Tower not ruffled by Apple’s reported satellite efforts
summary: American Tower's CEO, Jim Taiclet, dismisses Apple's rumored satellite initiative, stating that direct satellite-to-device connectivity is unlikely to be economically scalable outside of niche, low-density scenarios. He asserts that macro towers remain the optimal technical solution for mobile architecture in most common environments. Taiclet also discussed American Tower's exploration of edge computing opportunities by leveraging its tower infrastructure for future storage and processing needs.

title: SP Funds S&P 500 Sharia Industry Exclusions ETF
summary: This page provides a comprehensive profile of the SP Funds S&P 500 Sharia Industry Exclusions ETF (SPUS), detailing its stock profile, price, dividend, valuation, expenses, holdings, and ESG performance. title: The Mighty Apple, Google, Tesla, Dell and Microsoft in “the dock” - A Look At the Child Labour Lawsuit
summary: A landmark class-action lawsuit has been filed against Apple, Google, Tesla, Dell, and Microsoft, alleging their complicity in child labor practices within the cobalt mines of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). The International Rights Advocates (IRA) are seeking injunctive relief and damages on behalf of child victims, arguing that these tech giants knowingly benefit from and aid in the exploitation of young children. The lawsuit highlights the horrific conditions faced by child miners and seeks comprehensive relief, including medical care, monitoring, and environmental cleanup, while asserting U.S. jurisdictional basis given the companies' operations and alleged willful ignorance.

title: Jabil: Diversified Manufacturer Growing with Technology
summary: Jabil Inc. is a leading diversified manufacturing services company with nearly $26.3 billion in revenue, specializing in automated, continuous flow manufacturing for major tech companies like Apple and HP. The company is broken into EMS and DMS segments and is growing rapidly in areas like 5G, IoT, and 3D printing. Jabil's diversified portfolio and strategic acquisitions, such as medical device manufacturing facilities from Johnson & Johnson, position it for continued growth despite risks related to its footprint in China.

title: Capitalism 'will fundamentally be in jeopardy' if business does not act on climate change, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella asserts that capitalism is in jeopardy if businesses fail to address climate change, emphasizing that corporations must find profitable solutions to planetary problems. Microsoft has committed to being carbon negative by 2030, investing $1 billion in carbon-removal technology, and CFO Amy Hood views these initiatives as "good return investments." This stance aligns with a growing trend of companies and investors prioritizing sustainability alongside financial performance.

title: Now, Try Slicing the Stock Market Into Equal Pieces (Published 2020)
summary: This article explores an alternative approach to index fund investing: equal-weighted indexes, contrasting them with the more common capitalization-weighted indexes. It highlights that capitalization-weighted indexes, like the S&P 500, give more weight to larger companies, while equal-weighted funds allocate the same proportion to every company. The piece discusses the merits of both methods, noting that while equal-weighted funds can offer better diversification, capitalization-weighted funds have outperformed in recent years due to the continued rise of major companies.

title: This Salesforce CEO Felt Stuck. Here Is the Business Advice Steve Jobs Gave Him That Changed Everything
summary: Marc Benioff, founder and co-CEO of Salesforce, credits Steve Jobs with invaluable advice that shaped his company. Jobs advised Benioff to make Salesforce 10 times larger within 24 months, close a major customer like Avon, and build an application economy, emphasizing the importance of being mindful and projecting the future. This guidance led Benioff to open up the Salesforce platform to developers, fostering innovation and contributing to his current net worth of $7.6 billion.

title: Apple eyes 11 Penn Plaza for new office space
summary: Apple is reportedly negotiating to lease approximately 200,000 square feet of office space at 11 Penn Plaza, a building owned by Vornado. This move comes after Apple lost a bid for space in the Farley Building to Facebook and is driven by the iPhone maker's current offices in the Flatiron District being at capacity. The deal could also help Macy's sublease part of its space in the building.

title: Roku arrives in Brazil
summary: Roku, Inc. has announced its launch in Brazil, partnering with AOC to introduce AOC Roku TVs, making affordable streaming accessible to Brazilian consumers. The platform will feature a wide range of streaming channels, including local content through a partnership with Globoplay, providing users with a comprehensive entertainment experience. The 32-inch and 43-inch AOC Roku TV models will be available online and in stores starting January and February, respectively.

title: Teardown: Apple iPhone 11 Pro Max
summary: This article provides a detailed teardown analysis of the Apple iPhone 11 Pro Max, highlighting its key specifications, manufacturing cost, and major internal components. It also touches upon Apple's strategy regarding 5G modems, including its settlement with Qualcomm and acquisition of Intel's modem business for future product development. The teardown provides insights into the device's camera modules, RF components, and main PC board.

title: Apple, Broadcom Strike $15 Billion Worth of Chip-Supply Deals
summary: Broadcom Inc. has signed new multiyear agreements with Apple Inc. to supply high-performance wireless components and modules for Apple devices through mid-2023. These new pacts, combined with a similar agreement from 2019, are expected to generate approximately $15 billion in revenue for Broadcom. The chipmaker will supply these components for various Apple products.

title: Broadcom touts $15B in Apple deals as it seeks buyer for one of its businesses
summary: Broadcom has announced $15 billion in deals to supply Apple with chips, while simultaneously seeking a buyer for its radio frequency chip business. Companies like Qorvo and Skyworks are potential candidates to acquire Broadcom's RF chip operations. This strategic move highlights Broadcom's significant partnership with Apple alongside its efforts to divest certain business units.

title: Voting Gets Lazy; Cash-Craving Comcast; Broadcom Bites the Apple
summary: This article discusses several market topics, starting with the potential for smartphone voting and its implications for democracy. It then analyzes Comcast's strategy to offset video subscriber losses with price hikes and new streaming services, Intel's strong quarterly performance, and Broadcom's significant supply deal with Apple. Finally, it questions the market's enthusiastic reaction to American Express's latest earnings report and offers advice on navigating uncertain market conditions.

summary: The five largest tech companies (Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Facebook) now comprise 17.5% of the S&P 500, leading to potential overexposure for passive investors. Financial advisors are suggesting strategies like equal-weighted funds, specialized tech ETFs focusing on emerging areas, and ESG investments to diversify portfolios. While Big Tech's dominance has driven strong returns, advisors emphasize balancing diversification with access to growth, cautioning against timing the market with large active bets.

title: Which tech stock is best? Here’s how the ‘big five’ companies stack up as they report earnings
summary: This article examines how the "big five" U.S. tech companies are positioned as they prepare to report their quarterly earnings. title: Which tech stock is best? Here’s how the ‘big five’ companies stack up as they report earnings
summary: MarketWatch discusses how the "big five" U.S. tech companies compare as they prepare to report earnings. Amazon is noted as an analyst favorite, while Apple is deemed the "cheapest" despite its recent stock run-up. The article previews the upcoming earnings reports, with Apple kicking off the round.

title: Apple travel to China could be cut after United reduces flights
summary: Apple Inc.'s ability to send engineers to China could be affected by United Airlines' decision to reduce flights there due to the rising coronavirus death toll. Apple, which designs products in Cupertino but manufactures mostly in China, reportedly buys around 50 business-class seats daily on the San Francisco to Shanghai route. The CDC has advised against non-essential travel to China, and Apple has already reduced store hours in some Chinese locations.

title: Apple, Broadcom Told to Pay Caltech $1.1B in Patent Lawsuit
summary: A federal jury has ordered Apple Inc. and Broadcom Inc. to pay $1.1 billion to the California Institute of Technology for infringing on Wi-Fi data transmission patents. The lawsuit focused on Broadcom's computer chips, which were used in various Apple devices including iPhones and iPads, with both companies' headquarters located in California. Caltech expressed satisfaction with the decision, while Apple and Broadcom did not immediately comment.

title: Tesla Exceeds Expectations in Blowout Quarter
summary: Tesla Inc. reported blowout earnings for the second consecutive quarter and accelerated the Model Y crossover's introduction, leading to a significant surge in its stock price. Despite skepticism and challenges like reduced capital expenditures and potential impacts from the coronavirus, analysts note a strong "sentiment shift" towards Tesla as a profitable and investable company. The company projects delivering at least 500,000 vehicles this year, a more than 35% jump from 2019, and does not plan to raise capital.
