summary: Apple Inc. has sold $5.5 billion in bonds, joining a trend of tech giants leveraging low borrowing costs. This marks Apple's second bond issuance in a single calendar year since 2017, a strategy also pursued by companies like Amazon and Alphabet. The move highlights the current environment where cash-rich companies are taking advantage of historically low yields to raise capital.

title: Small Headphone Specialist Koss Corp. Attempts to Slay Giants Apple, Bose & Others in Court
summary: Koss Corporation has initiated significant litigation against major industry brands including Apple and Bose, alleging infringement on its wireless headphone and speaker patents. This move comes after Koss successfully recovered funds from a previous embezzlement scandal, suggesting a new focus on intellectual property enforcement following a failed "Striva" product line due to economic realities and market challenges. Apple has responded with a countersuit, claiming Koss violated a confidentiality agreement, which could impact the jurisdiction of the cases.

title: Warren Buffett’s Stock Portfolio Saw Massive Recovery, Big Bank and Gold Changes
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway filed its Q2 13F report, revealing significant shifts in its equity portfolio as of June 30, 2020. The portfolio saw a massive recovery in the second quarter, contributing $26.3 billion in earnings, primarily driven by investment gains. Notable changes include increased stakes in Apple and Bank of America, a new position in Barrick Gold, and reduced holdings in JPMorgan Chase, PNC, Wells Fargo, Visa, Mastercard, and a complete exit from Goldman Sachs and airline stocks.

title: Samsung includes NXP secure UWB chip in Galaxy Note20 Ultra
summary: Samsung's Galaxy Note20 Ultra is the first Android device to integrate an ultra-wideband (UWB) chip, specifically NXP's SR100T. This chipset combines UWB with NFC, a secure element, and an eSIM, enabling highly accurate location services and secure, hands-free access control for various applications like payments, ticketing, and digital keys. Both Samsung and NXP are co-founders of the FiRa Consortium, aiming to promote UWB interoperability and widespread adoption.

title: EY and Procter & Gamble team up on supply chain offering
summary: EY and Procter & Gamble (P&G) have broadened their alliance to offer supply chain expertise, combining their strengths to help businesses navigate challenges, particularly those exacerbated by the Covid-19 pandemic. This collaboration will address various supply chain processes, including strategy, planning, and distribution, leveraging advanced tools and joint teams. P&G, recognized for its leading supply chain operations, aims to share its successful methodologies with clients through EY to enhance responsiveness, productivity, and cost savings.

title: Colgate-Palmolive Launches hum by Colgate: The New Smart Electric Toothbrush that Guides Users to Brush Better and Get In Sync with Their Smile
summary: Colgate-Palmolive has launched hum by Colgate, a new smart electric toothbrush designed to help users improve their brushing habits. The toothbrush features smart sensors that track brushing frequency, duration, and coverage, providing personalized guidance and helping users achieve 50% better brushing efficacy. The accompanying app offers rewards through "Smile points" and integrates with the Apple Health app for a holistic wellness experience.

title: Barrick Gold's stock soars more than 10% after Buffett's Berkshire reveals stake
summary: Barrick Gold's stock surged over 10% after Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway disclosed a $562 million stake in the gold miner. This move is significant given Buffett's historical aversion to gold, though the purchase could have been made by one of his investment lieutenants. The investment comes as gold prices hit record highs amid economic worries and low bond yields due to the coronavirus pandemic.

title: BlackBerry partners with Texas startup to build 5G smartphone
summary: BlackBerry Ltd. is partnering with Texas-based startup OnwardMobility to launch a new 5G-enabled smartphone in the first half of 2021, targeting North American and European markets. This move marks BlackBerry's return to the hardware business through licensing, with OnwardMobility outsourcing manufacturing to a Foxconn subsidiary and aiming for significant North American production. The announcement follows BlackBerry's termination of a licensing agreement with TCL Communication.

title: How Warren Buffett spends his billions
summary: Warren Buffett, despite being one of the world's wealthiest individuals with a net worth over $100 billion, is known for his frugal lifestyle. He lives in the same modest home he bought in 1958, drives Cadillacs (whose parent company Berkshire Hathaway holds shares in), and considers his private jet one of his few luxuries. A significant portion of his wealth is dedicated to philanthropy, with over $37 billion donated since 2006, primarily through The Giving Pledge.

title: The Inside Story Of Robinhood’s Billionaire Founders, Option Kid Cowboys And The Wall Street Sharks That Feed On Them
summary: This article delves into Robinhood's rapid rise, driven by commission-free trading and a surge of novice investors during the pandemic, and reveals its controversial business model of selling customer order flow to high-frequency trading firms. It explores how this model, while minting billionaire founders, prioritizes profit from sophisticated market makers, potentially at the expense of inexperienced traders. The piece also highlights the platform's challenges, including outages and the tragic death of a user, leading to calls for better regulation and education.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) tops $2 trillion market capitalization
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) reached a market capitalization of over $2 trillion in intraday trading, marking a significant milestone for the tech giant. This achievement follows a strong rally in tech stocks amidst the pandemic, with Apple's stock more than doubling since March 2020. The article discusses Apple's valuation, its recent 4-for-1 stock split, and the broader market implications of such high valuations.

title: Apple becomes US' first $2T company
summary: Apple Inc. has become the first U.S. company to achieve a $2 trillion market capitalization, with its share price climbing above $467.77. This milestone follows its previous achievement of being the first U.S. company to reach a $1 trillion market cap just over two years prior. The tech giant's market cap soared by 59% this year, joining Saudi Aramco as the only other company globally to reach this valuation.

title: Apple's $2 trillion value is proof that Tim Cook's services plan worked
summary: Apple reached a $2 trillion valuation, largely symbolic but indicative of CEO Tim Cook's successful pivot towards a services-led business model. Investors are increasingly valuing Apple like a software company, reflected in its rising P-E ratio, as the company diversified its revenue streams beyond hardware. This shift helped Apple double its value in just over two years, despite challenges like App Store criticism and dependence on its existing device base.

title: Apple Becomes First U.S. Company Worth More Than $2 Trillion
summary: Apple achieved a new financial milestone, becoming the first publicly traded U.S. company to reach a market capitalization of over $2 trillion. This valuation marks a doubling in just two years, driven by a strong stock performance, especially during the pandemic due to work-from-home trends and robust online sales. The company's market cap now surpasses that of other U.S. tech giants and the GDP of several developed countries, with a four-for-one stock split planned to make shares more accessible.

title: Intel rolls out $10B stock buyback, CEO says stock is cheap
summary: Intel announced a new $10 billion stock buyback program through accelerated share repurchase agreements, with the CEO stating the stock is trading below its intrinsic valuation. This move is part of a larger $20 billion buyback plan initiated in October, which was suspended in March. The company will fund these repurchases using existing cash resources.

title: Apple is valued at $2 trillion as investor confidence grows
summary: Apple Inc. reached a market valuation of over $2 trillion in August 2020, just two years after hitting $1 trillion. This surge is attributed to growing investor confidence in Apple's shift toward services and surprisingly strong quarterly results despite the pandemic, with its stock up 57% year-to-date at the time. The company's market capitalization now rivals the combined value of the S&P 500's 200 smallest companies.

title: Apple says Fortnite maker wanted ‘Epic Games Store’ in App Store
summary: Apple has alleged that Epic Games' CEO sought a "side letter" to establish its own game store within the App Store, which Apple viewed as a move to undermine its commission-based model. This claim was made in a court filing as Apple sought to block Epic's request to remain in the App Store amidst their dispute over in-app payment rules. Apple categorized Fortnite's removal as a "self-inflicted wound" by Epic, stating that Epic knowingly breached their agreement.

title: Apple says Epic sought special ‘side’ deal on ‘Fortnite’ fee
summary: Apple Inc. claims that Epic Games Inc. sought a special "side" deal to bypass App Store fees for "Fortnite," an accusation that Epic denies. This dispute highlights a growing antitrust showdown over Apple's App Store policies and its 30% commission on app sales. Apple argues that Epic's actions, including offering direct in-app purchases, constitute a breach of contract and could undermine the entire App Store ecosystem.

title: Apple worth $1 trillion more since COVID-19 crashed the NASDAQ 100
summary: This article highlights how certain tech companies, particularly Apple and Tesla, have seen significant market value increases since the NASDAQ 100's low point in March 2020 following the COVID-19 crash. Apple's market capitalization grew by over $1 trillion to surpass $2 trillion, while Tesla also experienced a remarkable recovery. The article also notes other companies like Mercadolibre, PayPal, NVIDIA, eBay, Align Technology, and Lululemon that showed strong rebounds, contrasting these with a couple of pharma stocks that had not fully recovered.

title: Reliance Industries Successfully Became The Second Largest Brand In The World After Apple In 2020
summary: Reliance Industries, led by Mukesh Ambani, was ranked the second-largest brand globally after Apple in the FutureBrand Index 2020. The company was recognized for its ethical behavior, growth, innovative products, and excellent customer service due to its transformation into an advanced conglomerate catering to diverse consumer needs, spanning petrochemicals, telecommunications, and more. FutureBrand highlighted that Reliance's success is partly due to its expansion and investments from Google and Facebook, positioning it for potential future growth in the rankings.

title: Netgear unveils Meural Wi-Fi widescreen picture frames for art lovers
summary: Netgear has introduced a new line of Meural digital picture frames, the Meural WiFi Photo Frame, designed to appeal to art enthusiasts. Priced at $300, these frames feature a 15.6-inch by 7.5-inch display that can showcase personal photos, play short videos, and access curated art from Netgear's Meural Art Library. The frames offer smart features like automatic orientation adjustment, ambient light sensors, and remote management for family sharing.

title: TSMC details its future 5nm and 3nm manufacturing processes—here’s what it means for Apple silicon
summary: TSMC revealed details about its upcoming 5nm and 3nm chip manufacturing processes at its annual Technology Symposium. These advancements will significantly impact Apple's future A-series and Apple silicon processors, leading to increased performance, improved power efficiency, and higher transistor density. The 5nm enhancements will appear in products soon, while the 3nm process is expected to revolutionize Apple devices by 2022, enabling more powerful and efficient iPhones, iPads, Macs, and potentially AR headsets.

title: Amazon's Audible debuts cheaper, R134-a-month service
summary: Amazon's Audible is launching a more affordable service called Audible Plus for $7.95 (R134) a month, aiming to attract podcast listeners and expand beyond audiobooks. This new plan offers over 11,000 titles, including original series from various artists and actors, and is a strategic move to compete with platforms like Spotify in the non-music audio market. The initiative seeks to make Audible a daily destination for listeners with its extensive library, moving beyond its previous credit-based subscription model.

title: TSMC N3 (3nm) mass production scheduled for H2 2022 - Industry - News
summary: TSMC announced its N3 (3nm) mass production is scheduled for the second half of 2022, with risk production beginning in 2021. The N3 process, which is FinFET-based, is expected to offer significant performance and power improvements over the N5 process. Additionally, TSMC introduced its 3DFabric technology, an advanced packaging technique for chip stacking, to further enhance SoC capabilities.

title: Unity Software files for IPO on the New York Stock Exchange
summary: Unity Software, the developer of the Unity Engine, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “U”. The company reported approximately 40% revenue growth this year, despite disclosing net losses in both the current and previous fiscal years. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Credit Suisse Group AG are leading the IPO, with an initial deal size of $100 million.

title: Dave Inc. Faces Class Action Over Mobile App Data Breach
summary: Mobile software developer Dave Inc. is facing a proposed class action lawsuit on behalf of 7.5 million users following a June 2020 cyberattack. The breach exposed sensitive user data, including passwords and encrypted Social Security numbers. Plaintiffs allege that the company failed to implement adequate security measures, detect the breach promptly, and provide timely notification to affected users.

title: Exxon Mobil Exits: The Dow Drops Its Oldest Member
summary: Exxon Mobil, once the Dow Jones Industrial Average's longest-tenured member, is being removed from the influential index, effective August 31, 2020. This change is driven by Apple's stock split, which necessitates a rebalancing of the Dow. While symbolic, the move underscores Exxon's diminished stature compared to tech giants and highlights the struggles of oil companies amid climate concerns and shifting market dynamics.

title: Unity Software Prepares To Become Publicly Owned
summary: Unity Technologies is planning its initial public offering (IPO), having filed an S-1 statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, revealing significant revenue alongside ongoing losses. The Unity engine is widely used by over 1 million active users for games and animations, with its diverse application extending beyond gaming to architecture and filmmaking. Going public is expected to help Unity address financial losses and expand into new markets, improving its competitive position.

title: Apple v. Prepear : Does Apple Really Need All the Fruit?
summary: This article discusses the trademark dispute between Apple Inc. and Prepear, a small meal prep app company, over Prepear's pear-shaped logo. Apple filed an opposition with the USPTO, claiming likelihood of consumer confusion and brand dilution, sparking debate on whether Apple is protecting its brand or engaging in trademark bullying. The author questions Apple's argument that food and meal planning services are within its natural "zone of expansion."

title: Investor, Philanthropist Warren Buffett Turns 90
summary: The article celebrates Warren Buffett's 90th birthday, highlighting his career as a renowned investor and philanthropist. It delves into his early life, his journey to becoming CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, and his investment philosophy which emphasizes value investing, patience, and emotional control. The piece also acknowledges his substantial philanthropic contributions and shares several of his famous quotes on investing and life.

title: Insulin Pump Market Heats Up Among Rivals Medtronic, Insulet and Tandem
summary: The insulin pump market is experiencing increased competition among key players Medtronic, Insulet, and Tandem. This article highlights the intensifying rivalry and recent activities of these companies, including partnerships and technological advancements. It suggests a dynamic landscape in the diabetic care medical device sector.

title: Investing basics: what is a stock split?
summary: This article explains what a stock split is and how it impacts investors, using recent announcements from Apple and Tesla as examples. It clarifies that while a split increases the number of shares and reduces the per-share price, it does not change the company's fundamental value or an investor's total holdings. The piece also briefly covers the concept of a reverse stock split.

title: Uber Expands Self-Driving Safety Report After NTSB Slams Culture
summary: Uber has updated its voluntary safety assessment for self-driving cars, pledging to make more safety information public, including an open-sourced "Safety Case Framework." This move comes after the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) partially blamed Uber's policies for a fatal 2018 crash involving one of its autonomous vehicles. Uber aims to address criticisms regarding its autonomous driving program and demonstrate its commitment to safety.

title: Exclusive: -Facebook says Apple rejected its attempt to tell users about App Store fees
summary: Facebook stated that Apple rejected its attempt to inform users about the 30% App Store fee on sales from a new online events feature, citing a rule against "irrelevant" information. Facebook had sought a fee waiver from Apple for small businesses using the feature to recover COVID-19 related revenue losses, but Apple declined. This incident aligns Facebook with other developers, like Epic Games, who are criticizing Apple's App Store commissions and facing scrutiny over alleged anti-competitive behavior.

title: Tesla, Apple stock splits pave way for more gains
summary: Apple and Tesla's recent stock splits may lead to further share price gains by increasing affordability for smaller investors, despite splits not changing a company's overall market capitalization. Historically, major brands have seen an average 33% share price rally within 12 months post-split. While some experts caution about increased volatility and diluted earnings, others predict continued growth for mega-cap tech stocks.

title: Big Oil Faded. Will Big Tech?
summary: This article questions whether Big Tech companies, currently dominant, face a similar fate to Big Oil, specifically Exxon Mobil, which was once the world's most valuable company but has since declined. The author draws parallels between the economic and political influence of past oil superpowers and current tech giants, suggesting that while Big Tech seems invincible now, history shows that even dominant industries can lose relevance due to changing global dynamics and a failure to adapt.

title: Three things you need to know about Apple’s stock split
summary: Apple Inc. underwent a four-for-one stock split after the close of trading on Friday, August 28, 2020. This is the fifth split in the company's history and aims to make shares more accessible to a broader base of investors. As a result, Apple's component weighting in the price-weighted Dow Jones Industrial Average will decrease significantly.

title: Apple and Tesla shares spike after stock splits kick in
summary: Apple and Tesla shares surged on Monday after their stock splits took effect, attracting significant investor buying. Apple jumped over 4 percent after its 4-for-1 split, while Tesla rallied 10 percent following its 5-for-1 split, elevating its market capitalization to over $440 billion and showcasing the impact of increased affordability for individual investors. The success of these splits may encourage other companies with high-priced stocks to consider similar moves, as both Silicon Valley companies have strong individual investor followings.

title: Apple Preparing 75 Million 5G iPhones Alongside New Watches and iPad
summary: Apple has requested suppliers to build at least 75 million 5G iPhones for the end of the year, similar to last year's launch, indicating resilient demand despite the pandemic and recession. The company expects shipments of these next-generation iPhones to potentially reach up to 80 million units in 2020. Apple plans to introduce four new models in October, featuring 5G connectivity, a new design, and diverse screen sizes, alongside new Apple Watch models, an iPad Air, and a smaller HomePod.

title: Cybersquatting: Attackers Mimicking Domains of Major Brands Including Facebook, Apple, Amazon and Netflix to Scam Consumers
summary: This article discusses the widespread issue of cybersquatting, where attackers register domain names similar to major brands like Facebook, Apple, Amazon, and Netflix to scam consumers. Palo Alto Networks identified 13,857 squatting domains in December 2019 alone, with 18.59% being malicious and 36.57% posing high risks, engaging in activities such as phishing, malware distribution, and various scams. The report details different squatting techniques, common targets, and infrastructure preferences of attackers, emphasizing the need for continuous monitoring and user vigilance.

title: Sonos wireless headphones revealed in new patent filing
summary: A new patent filing reveals Sonos' plans to enter the wireless over-ear headphone market. The patent, filed in September 2019 and recently granted, details two possible designs and innovative integration features with existing Sonos speaker systems, allowing users to "swap" audio playback between headphones and speakers. While pricing and release dates are unknown, Sonos aims to leverage its customer base with features that enhance its current audio ecosystem.

title: What Enabled Adobe to Raise Its Digital Media ARR?
summary: Adobe's Digital Media ARR increased due to the continued migration of its Creative Suite and the addition of new Creative Cloud subscriptions. The company saw significant growth in its Digital Media segment, with Creative Cloud revenues rising 44% year-over-year, leading Adobe to raise its fiscal 2016 Digital Media ARR guidance to $4.0 billion. Fiscal 1Q16 was the last quarter Adobe provided specific Creative Cloud subscriber figures.

title: Bet on Wild Swings in the Market With the SPXL ETF
summary: The Direxion Daily S&P 500 Bull 3X Shares (SPXL) ETF is designed for traders to capitalize on short-term market upswings by delivering three times the gains of the S&P 500, using leverage and investing in fast-rising stocks. While it offers significant profit potential during bullish periods, its leveraged nature and daily resets mean it can also lead to rapid losses during downturns, making it suitable only for active trading rather than buy-and-hold strategies. Investors are cautioned about its high expense ratio and the need for close market monitoring due to its volatility.

title: Amid Covid’s Impact, Installment Pay Gets a Lift With New Offerings From Mastercard And Sezzle
summary: Mastercard and online installment startup Sezzle are expanding their installment payment offerings, capitalizing on increased consumer demand due to Covid-19's impact. Mastercard is integrating with TSYS and other technology providers to allow cardholders to elect installment payments before, during, or after a purchase. Sezzle is launching a virtual card compatible with Google Pay and Apple Pay, enabling its buy-now-pay-later service for in-store purchases for the first time.

title: RBC one of the top victims of cybersquatting
summary: A study by Palo Alto Networks reveals that Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) is among the top three brands most targeted by cybersquatters, ranking behind PayPal and Apple. Cybersquatting involves creating fake websites that mimic legitimate ones through similar logos, aesthetics, and deliberately misspelled domain names to trick users. Many of these fake sites are malicious, distributing malware or conducting phishing attacks, posing significant risks to individuals who might share sensitive information.

title: The 2020 Edition Zepp E Smartwatch Is Tested By Luxurious Magazine
summary: Luxurious Magazine reviewed the 2020 Zepp E smartwatch, highlighting its style, ease of use, and comprehensive features. The Zepp E, priced from £209, comes in Circle and Square variants and is compatible with both iOS and Android systems. The article praises its design, 5ATM water resistance, and various health tracking and sports modes accessible via its intuitive interface and dedicated app.

title: Pullback Brings Stocks Closer to Fair Value
summary: The market experienced its largest single-day decline since June 11, primarily driven by a sell-off in the technology sector, which was deemed overvalued. While the overall market remains overvalued, this pullback has brought stock valuations closer to fair value. The energy sector, in contrast, appears significantly undervalued, offering potential opportunities for long-term investors.

title: Which coding languages are used by the world’s top companies?
summary: This article examines the most popular and highest-paying coding languages used by major companies, highlighting an infographic from Flyaps that details the languages adopted by 25 Fortune 500 companies. It discusses how choices in programming languages depend on factors like speed, reliability, and scalability, and contrasts findings from other studies on popular and high-paying languages. Examples of specific companies and the languages they use are also provided.

title: Apple delays iPhone change that's expected to make it harder for Facebook and other apps to target ads
summary: Apple announced a delay until early 2021 for a planned change to the iPhone operating system that would impact how mobile advertisers track users. This privacy-focused update, previously set for iOS 14, gives developers more time to adapt to new rules requiring user permission to track their activity. The change is expected to significantly affect mobile advertising, with Facebook warning of potential revenue drops for publishers.

title: Publishers are getting a (brief) reprieve from Apple’s coming ad-pocalypse
summary: Apple has delayed a planned privacy change for iOS 14, requiring explicit user permission for ad tracking, until early 2021. This move provides a temporary reprieve for publishers and developers who rely on ad revenue, giving them more time to adapt. While larger publishers like The New York Times are better equipped to handle these changes with their own data stacks, smaller entities will face greater challenges and may resort to tighter registration walls.

title: Apple stock drop makes history with $179B loss
summary: Apple experienced the largest single-day market cap drop for a U.S. listed company, losing over $179.92 billion (8%) in value on Thursday. This significant decline followed a period where Apple had recently become the first U.S. company to reach a $2 trillion valuation. Despite the drop, its market cap remains at the $2 trillion level, and analysts largely maintain a "strong buy" rating as the company prepares to release a 5G iPhone.

title: -Broadcom signals delay in iPhone chip ramp-up, pointing to later release date
summary: Broadcom, a key Apple supplier, indicated a delay in its annual chip shipments, suggesting the next iPhone launch will be later than usual. This comes after Broadcom previously warned of lower revenues due to a delayed phone launch from its "large North American mobile phone customer," believed to be Apple. Analysts widely expect Apple's new 5G-enabled iPhone to launch in October due to coronavirus-related disruptions.

title: Apple’s ‘dark side’ led these brothers to launch the AppRising
summary: Ben and Dan Volach, Israeli brothers who deeply admire Apple products, are leading an "AppRising" against Apple Inc. and its App Store. They view Apple as a monopoly and have filed antitrust litigation, preceding similar actions by other companies like Epic Games.

title: Fossil Gen 5 Garrett HR review: Style and substance
summary: The Fossil Gen 5 Garrett HR smartwatch blends classic chronograph style with modern features like hands-free calls, activity tracking, and contactless payments. Its improved hardware provides a smooth user experience, addressing previous Wear OS sluggishness. While battery life is typical for smartwatches, and Google Fit has some limitations, the Garrett HR is praised for its design, vibrant display, and multi-functional capabilities, making it a strong lifestyle-focused smartwatch.

title: Apple Marina Bay Sands opens Thursday in Singapore
summary: Apple is opening its new Apple Marina Bay Sands store in Singapore on Thursday, September 10, the first Apple Store to sit directly on the water. The unique spherical, all-glass dome structure offers 360-degree panoramic views and will feature "Today at Apple" sessions and an underwater boardroom. The store continues Apple's 40-year commitment to Singapore and will implement rigorous health measures for visitors.

title: Is it worth upgrading from the original Peloton Bike to the new premium Bike+?
summary: This article evaluates whether upgrading from the original Peloton Bike to the new Bike+ is worthwhile, especially for existing owners. It details the new features of the Bike+, such as a rotating screen, improved sound system, Apple GymKit integration, and auto-follow resistance, comparing them to the original model. The author discusses the trade-in program, pricing, and personal relevance of these new features, concluding that while the Bike+ offers enhancements, the original model remains a strong option, particularly with its reduced price.

title: Using Foreign Funds to Correct for a Sliding Dollar
summary: The dollar has recently been in decline relative to other foreign currencies, primarily due to low interest rates and a shift in the Fed's monetary policy. This can have varying implications for investors' portfolios depending on their holdings. Foreign stock and bond funds, particularly unhedged ones, can benefit from a weaker dollar, while U.S. companies with significant foreign revenue may also see improved profits.

title: Precedential Opinion: Board weighs six factors in discretionary denial analysis
summary: The Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) has issued a precedential opinion outlining six factors it considers when deciding whether to discretionarily deny an IPR petition due to parallel litigation. These factors aim to address efficiency concerns and potential conflicts with ongoing district court proceedings, particularly when a trial date is set before the Board's final written decision. The guidance provides clarity for parties on arguments related to discretionary denials based on the stage of related litigation.

title: Hipgnosis becomes a fully-fledged publisher, buying Big Deal Music to form Hipgnosis Songs Group
summary: Hipgnosis Songs Fund has acquired Los Angeles-based Big Deal Music Group, rebranding it as Hipgnosis Songs Group, to become a traditional music publisher. This acquisition brings 4,400 copyrights and Big Deal's US-based publishing administration business, Words & Music, under Hipgnosis, enabling greater control over royalties, direct relationships with DSPs, and the ability to sign new songwriters. Big Deal's founders will take key leadership roles within the newly formed Hipgnosis Songs Group.

title: Ninth Circuit’s Ruling In Frlekin v. Apple, Inc. Is A Cautionary Tale For Employers
summary: The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals ruled in Frlekin v. Apple, Inc. that Apple must compensate California non-exempt employees for time spent waiting for and submitting to mandatory bag searches. This decision, influenced by a California Supreme Court ruling emphasizing employer control, highlights the need for employers to compensate employees for all time under their control and consider using arbitration agreements with class action waivers to mitigate exposure.

title: Apple’s First Major Product Launch Event of 2020: What to Watch
summary: Apple Inc. is set to unveil new products at a virtual event, including upgrades to its Apple Watch and iPad Air. The new Apple Watch Series 6 is expected to feature a faster processor and a blood oxygen meter, alongside a new low-end model. While headphones and a smaller HomePod are also anticipated, new iPhones will be introduced at a later date.

title: Nvidia jumps on $40 billion chip deal; analysts expect tough scrutiny
summary: Nvidia's shares surged after announcing a $40 billion deal to acquire UK-based chip designer Arm from SoftBank, marking a potential shift in the chip industry landscape. Analysts anticipate significant regulatory challenges for the acquisition, particularly from China, due to its scale and implications for competition. The deal is seen as a major move for Nvidia, positioning it among top chip suppliers, but also raising concerns among rivals and regulators.

title: -Nvidia to buy chip designer Arm for $40 bln as SoftBank exits
summary: Nvidia announced its acquisition of UK-based chip designer Arm from SoftBank Group for $40 billion, a deal set to significantly impact the global semiconductor sector. The acquisition poses questions regarding Arm's maintained neutrality under U.S. ownership and faces regulatory scrutiny, particularly in China. Nvidia plans to expand Arm's licensing model and integrate its own IP, while SoftBank exits Arm to raise cash.
authors: Sam Nussey|Stephen Nellis and Kate Holton

title: ARM: Can 'crown jewel' of UK technology be protected?
summary: The UK government is considering options to protect Cambridge-based ARM Holdings after its acquisition by Nvidia, a move that contrasts with their earlier stance during Softbank's purchase in 2016. ARM's co-founder has criticized the deal, fearing Nvidia will disrupt ARM's business model. While Nvidia promises UK investment, blocking the deal poses challenges given current laws and potential impacts on foreign investment.

title: Can digital devices help detect flu, COVID-19 infection?
summary: UW Medicine and Apple Inc. are collaborating on a study to investigate whether physiological data collected from Apple Watches, such as heart rate and blood oxygen, can detect early signs of acute respiratory infections like influenza and COVID-19. Participants will receive Apple Watches and home-testing kits to track biosignals and test for various respiratory conditions. The study aims to identify individuals who are becoming ill quickly so they can self-isolate and help break the chain of viral transmission.

title: Thanks to the Pandemic, Apple’s Big Event Was Just … an Event
summary: Due to pandemic-related disruptions, Apple's annual September event did not feature a new iPhone unveiling. Instead, the company introduced a new iPad Air, Watch Series 6, and a streaming workout platform called Fitness+. Apple is expected to reveal four new iPhone 12 models with 5G connectivity and OLED screens at a later event, likely in October.

title: Introducing New Logitech Folio Touch — Extremely Versatile and Flexible Keyboard Case Available for the New iPad Air
summary: Logitech has announced the new Folio Touch, a versatile keyboard case with an integrated trackpad for the 4th generation iPad Air. It connects via the Smart Connector, offers four use modes (typing, viewing, sketching, reading), and features an adjustable kickstand, backlit keys, and iPadOS shortcut keys. The Folio Touch also provides protection for the iPad Air and a secure magnetic latch, with availability expected in October 2020 for $159.99.

title: Why Nvidia is spending $40 billion on Arm
summary: Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm, if completed, would be the largest semiconductor deal ever and is primarily aimed at boosting Nvidia's data center business by integrating Arm's CPU technology with Nvidia's GPUs. The deal raises questions about Nvidia's ability to neutrally license Arm's technology to competitors like Apple and Samsung, but Nvidia plans to operate Arm as an independent, customer-neutral subsidiary. Investors reacted positively to the news, and Nvidia sees significant potential in infusing its AI and graphics chips into Arm's vast ecosystem.

title: Apple event: A new fitness service and updates to Watch and iPad
summary: Apple announced a new remote fitness service called Fitness+ along with updated versions of its Apple Watch and iPad products. The Fitness+ service, available at $9.99 a month, will offer online workout videos and integrate with the Apple Watch and music service. The new Apple Watch Series 6 will include blood oxygen measurement and an improved sleep tracker, while Apple also introduced a new service bundle called Apple One, starting at $14.95 a month.

title: Here is everything that was announced at today’s Apple event
summary: Apple held its annual hardware event on September 15, 2020, announcing several new products and services, though notably without a new iPhone. Key revelations included the Apple Watch Series 6, a budget-friendly Apple Watch SE, updated iPad Air and entry-level iPad models, and the new Apple One subscription service. The company also unveiled Apple Fitness+ and confirmed the release of iOS 14 and other software updates on September 16.
authors: Kevin Parrish and Christian de Looper

title: Why Investors Should Not Expect a NextEra Stock Split to Act Like Apple or Tesla
summary: NextEra Energy Inc. announced a 4-for-1 stock split and increased its financial expectations for 2021, 2022, and 2023. While stock splits often generate excitement, investors are cautioned not to expect a similar stock surge seen with Apple and Tesla, as NextEra is a utility stock with different market dynamics. Despite being the largest U.S. utility by market cap and a strong performer, its P/E ratios and low dividend yield reflect a different investment profile.

title: The 6 Most Interesting Things Apple Just Announced
summary: Apple unveiled several new products and services, including two new Apple Watches, a fitness subscription service called Fitness+, and updates to its iPad lineup. The company also introduced Apple One, a bundled subscription service for its various offerings, aiming to simplify subscriptions for users. Notable product improvements include a blood oxygen sensor in the Apple Watch Series 6 and a faster processor for the entry-level iPad and iPad Air.

title: The Apple Watch Won’t Come With a Charger Anymore. Are iPhones Next?
summary: Apple announced that its new Apple Watch models will no longer include a USB power adapter to reduce environmental harm, following rumors and a commitment to become carbon neutral by 2030. This decision sparked speculation that the upcoming iPhone 12 might also ship without a power adapter. The company states that removing adapters is equivalent to taking 50,000 automobiles off the streets annually, despite past criticism regarding its environmental impact.

title: What to Expect with the Release of Apple iOS14
summary: Apple's iOS14 update introduces significant privacy changes, requiring consumer opt-in for sharing personal data with third-party apps for ad targeting, primarily affecting the use of the IDFA. This shift presents challenges for app advertisers and platforms like Outbrain, which are adapting by participating in Apple’s SKAdNetwork framework, working with Mobile Measurement Partners, and leveraging their extensive network on the open web. The changes are expected to have a massive impact on in-app advertising, particularly for social platforms like Facebook, making it crucial for advertisers to diversify their media mix.

title: Stock ETFs Gain Amid Positive Economic Data And Tech Rebound
summary: Stock index ETFs and individual stocks rallied on Tuesday, September 15, 2020, driven by a rebound in tech stocks, positive economic data from China and the U.S., and optimism surrounding coronavirus vaccine trials. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all advanced, with tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, Netflix, and Facebook leading the gains, while the banking sector lagged. Analysts have also raised the year-end target for the S&P 500 due to tech mergers and sector excitement.

title: Global Audio and Video Editing Software Market- Featuring Acon AS, Adobe Inc., Animoto Inc., and Apple Inc. Among Others
summary: The global audio and video editing software market is projected to grow by USD 2.5 billion between 2020 and 2024, with a CAGR exceeding 11%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing rate of content generation and a shift towards cloud-based delivery models. The report provides a comprehensive analysis including market sizing, forecast, trends, growth drivers, challenges, and vendor analysis, covering companies such as Adobe Inc., Apple Inc., and Microsoft Corp.

title: Intel Revenue by Quarter: FY Q1 1993 – Q2 2020
summary: This article presents Intel Corporation's quarterly revenue data from Q1 1993 to Q2 2020. It highlights that Intel reported revenue of $19,728 million in Q2 2020, marking a 19.5% year-over-year growth. The article also notes key milestones such as crossing the $10 billion revenue mark in Q4 2005 and reaching an all-time high of $20,209 million in Q4 2019.

title: How Big a Slice is Apple in Your ETF?
summary: This article examines the significant allocation of Apple (AAPL) in various ETFs, particularly in technology and growth-focused funds, highlighting its impact on potential future returns. CFRA finds Apple attractively valued, citing new product unveilings and service bundles as key drivers for recurring revenue and future growth. The author emphasizes that understanding ETF holdings, especially major stakes like Apple, is crucial for investors.

title: Studio Dragon sets sights overseas, $3.4 bn market cap possible: analyst
summary: Studio Dragon, a leading South Korean drama producer, is poised for significant growth due to increasing global demand for Korean dramas and joint projects with international distributors like Netflix, HBO, and Apple. An analyst from Shinhan Investment Corp. projects Studio Dragon's operating profit to surge, potentially leading to a market capitalization of over 4 trillion won ($3.39 billion). The company's shares are currently in a correction phase, which a Shinhan analyst views as a rare buying opportunity for investors.

title: Anthem Announces Research Study with Apple Watch to Help Improve Asthma Management
summary: Anthem, Inc. announced a new two-year research study in collaboration with Apple, Inc. and University of California, Irvine, to investigate how Apple Watch and iPhone can help individuals self-manage asthma and improve clinical outcomes. The study will enroll participants from Anthem-affiliated health plans and explore the use of digital tools and data from everyday devices to predict asthma exacerbation and serve as potential digital biomarkers. Participants will receive an Apple Watch and Beddit™ Sleep Monitor, and the active group will have access to a digital asthma tool for symptom tracking, personalized nudges, and educational resources.

title: 3M Oral Care Launches 3M Tx Selector App
summary: 3M Oral Care has launched the 3M Tx Selector app and formed a strategic partnership with the Grin Remote Monitoring Platform, expanding its digital application offerings for orthodontists. The Tx Selector app allows patients to visualize different 3M orthodontic appliances on themselves, promoting engagement in treatment decisions. The collaboration with Grin enables virtual check-ins for patients, helping orthodontists adapt to remote care trends and enhance the patient experience.

title: Old Casio patent used by troll to try to block sales of Apple Watch
summary: A patent troll named Solas OLED Ltd, an Irish company, has sued Apple for patent infringement, claiming the Apple Watch Series 3, 4, and 5 infringe on an old Casio patent they acquired. Solas OLED Ltd, which focuses on OLED technology licensing, is seeking an injunction to stop Apple from selling these Apple Watch models and also financial damages. Apple typically fights such lawsuits to deter future attempts by patent trolls.

title: Apple to launch its first online store in India next week
summary: Apple announced it will launch its first online store in India on September 23, 2020, coinciding with the country's holiday season. This move aims to capture a larger share of the Indian smartphone market, where Apple currently holds only 1% of shipments, and will offer direct sales, customer assistance in English and Hindi, and personalization options. The company is also building its first physical retail store in Mumbai, slated for 2021.

title: IBM Remains America’s Worst Big Tech Company
summary: Despite a change in CEO, IBM continues to underperform significantly compared to other major tech companies, with its stock price declining while competitors surge. The company has experienced nearly six years of revenue decline and its cloud operations show minimal growth, placing it outside the top 50 American companies by market capitalization. IBM lacks strong consumer-focused products and its key brands like Watson and The Weather Channel fail to provide compelling growth engines, indicating a dim future for its return to the top tier of tech.

title: Oracle (ORCL) Trading Lower After TikTok Download Ban
summary: Oracle (ORCL) stock is trading lower after the U.S. Commerce Department banned TikTok and WeChat downloads, effective Sunday. The announcement creates uncertainty around Oracle's potential deal for TikTok's U.S. operations, with the stock needing to hold the $57 to $57.50 price zone to maintain a bullish technical outlook. Long-term patterns suggest only modest upside even if a deal is finalized, and a failed deal could trigger a bearish double top pattern.

title: ESG sees record inflows amid pandemic. One leading issuer on the theme's staying power
summary: ESG investing has seen record inflows in 2020, with over $19 billion flowing into ESG ETFs, bringing the total to over $40 billion. Luke Oliver of DWS Group attributes this growth to investors adopting ESG as a core portfolio strategy, noting that ESG companies are often better prepared for future challenges. The trend is fueled by increased awareness of environmental and social issues, along with a shift away from sectors like fossil fuels.

title: Trump Blesses Oracle’s TikTok Deal, Delaying Potential U.S. Ban
summary: President Trump approved Oracle Corp.'s bid for TikTok's American operations, delaying a potential U.S. ban on the video-sharing app. The deal involves the creation of TikTok Global, with Oracle taking a 12.5% stake and Walmart Inc. taking 7.5%, and includes a $5 billion contribution to a U.S. education fund as well as the creation of 25,000 jobs. This agreement aims to address U.S. national security concerns while allowing TikTok to continue operating in the country.
authors: Josh Wingrove|Jennifer Jacobs and Shelly Banjo / Bloomberg

title: Expedia Group Unveils New Widgets and App Clip for iOS 14
summary: Expedia Group has released new versions of its Brand Expedia, Hotwire, and Vrbo iOS apps, integrating new features available in iOS 14. These updates include widgets for easier access to travel details and deals, an App Clip for Hotwire, and improved transparency and planning tools aimed at simplifying travel bookings and experiences. The enhancements are designed to provide users with more information at a glance and help them access discounts for last-minute accommodations.

title: Peacock Now Available on the Roku Platform
summary: Peacock announced its app is now available nationwide on the Roku® platform, reaching an estimated 100 million people. This partnership expands Peacock's reach and provides Roku users with access to over 20,000 hours of on-demand movies, shows, live news, and sports programming, including a free tier offering 13,000 hours of premium content. Both companies expressed excitement about delivering high-quality content to engaged streamers.

title: Nvidia's ARM acquisition prompts Ceva upgrade
summary: Northland Capital Markets upgraded semiconductor IP company Ceva to "Outperform" and raised its price target to $48, citing Nvidia's ARM acquisition. Analyst Gus Richard highlights Ceva's strong IP revenue, immunity to the US-China trade war as an Israeli company, exposure to 5G, and potential integration into Apple's future iPhones. The increased valuation reflects the anticipated growth in the semiconductor IP market and a shift towards chiplet models.

title: Expedia Group unveils new widgets and app clip for iOS 14
summary: Expedia Group has updated its Brand Expedia, Hotwire, and Vrbo iOS apps to leverage iOS 14 features, introducing new widgets and an app clip to enhance user experience. These updates provide easier access to travel deals, upcoming reservation details, and trip information, aiming to make travel planning more accessible and less stressful. The new features help users view trip countdowns, weather, flight reminders, and last-minute deals, with the Hotwire app clip allowing for one-tap bookings via Apple Pay.

title: Nasdaq 100 Is Just Halfway Through Its Plunge, Says Morgan Stanley
summary: Morgan Stanley's chief US equity strategist, Michael Wilson, believes the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 is only halfway through its market correction despite a recent bounce. He points to continued overvaluation, strong call option interest, successful tech IPOs, and hedge fund commitment as signs that bullish sentiment for major tech companies like FAANGs and Microsoft remains too high, indicating further declines are likely. The Nasdaq 100 has already seen a significant drop this month, underperforming the S&P 500.

title: Prepear Stands Up To Apple
summary: Prepear, a small Provo-based food prep startup, is fighting Apple's formal opposition to its trademark, which Apple claims could cause consumer confusion due to a stylized apple and pear logo. Prepear's CEO views Apple's actions as bullying and an injustice, especially against a company helping small food bloggers secure sustainable revenue. Over 260,000 people have signed a Change.org petition supporting Prepear, and the two parties are currently in negotiations for a settlement.

title: Is the VGS ETF the best for passive investing?
summary: This article examines the Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (ASX: VGS) as a strong option for passive investing due to its broad diversification across over 1,500 businesses in major developed countries. It highlights VGS's sector allocation, top holdings including tech giants, and its average annual return of 9.65% over the past five years. The piece concludes that while VGS offers good diversification, investors should consider its management fee compared to other ETFs and explore various options for growth and dividends.

title: Honest Company Founder Jessica Alba: Ethics shouldn’t be a ‘marketing ploy’
summary: Jessica Alba, founder of The Honest Company, states that ethics should be an inherent part of a company's model rather than just a marketing ploy, and that businesses can achieve both social good and profitability. She highlights how The Honest Company's commitment to ethical practices and transparency gives it a competitive advantage, despite past challenges. The company has seen significant growth, partly due to new disinfectant products during the pandemic, and aims to continue positively influencing how other companies operate.

title: Vitality: Delivering a valuable experience for customers
summary: Vitality, founded in 2004, distinguishes itself in the insurance market by integrating wellness into its offerings, aiming to improve customers' lives and encouraging 100 million people to be 20% more active by 2025. Chief Digital Officer Dave Priestley explains how the company uses digital transformation, including an innovative member app, to deliver value and a superior customer experience. Despite initial challenges with legacy systems, Vitality has leveraged technology and partnerships, such as with TCS, to create a unified customer platform and adapt rapidly to new demands like the COVID-19 pandemic, all while focusing on customer-centric outcomes.

title: Spotify, Match Launch Coalition to Protest App Store Rules
summary: Spotify, Match Group, Epic Games, and 10 other organizations have formed the Coalition for App Fairness to challenge the app store rules of Apple and Google. The coalition introduced "App Store Principles," advocating against platform owners using developer data to compete and requiring exclusive use of their app stores or payment systems. This move aims to pressure Apple and Google into modifying their existing app store guidelines.

title: To Fight Apple and Google, Smaller App Rivals Organize a Coalition (Published 2020)
summary: Smaller app developers like Spotify, Match Group, and Epic Games have formed the "Coalition for App Fairness" to challenge the dominance and app store practices of Apple and Google. This move comes as scrutiny intensifies on major tech companies for alleged antitrust violations. The coalition aims to advocate for changes in app store policies and "protect the app economy."

title: 3 quick reasons I like the IOO ETF
summary: The iShares Global 100 ETF (ASX: IOO) is recommended for its three key features: diversification from the Australian market, exposure to global developed and emerging markets, and investment in strong, growing businesses with entrenched economic moats. The article highlights its holdings in major global companies like Apple and Microsoft, and its 10-year average annual return of 13.1%. The author suggests it's a good option for investors seeking to diversify beyond ASX shares.

title: Mediaweek Roundup: Simon Reeve, Netflix, Dean Jones, ABC + more
summary: This Mediaweek Roundup covers several key media industry stories, including a coalition of smaller app companies forming to challenge Apple and Google's app store policies, former Seven Network presenter Simon Reeve suing the network for over $500,000, and a call for Netflix to contribute more to Australian content production. It also touches on celebrity manager Sam Mangan's "virtual" LA office, Peter V'landys suing ABC, and the passing of cricket legend Dean Jones.

title: Apple grants CEO Tim Cook first major stock package since 2011
summary: Apple has granted CEO Tim Cook a new stock package, his first since 2011, consisting of 333,987 restricted stock units with the potential for more based on performance targets. This move aligns his compensation timeline with other executives and is in recognition of his leadership. The stock units, valued at $38.1 million at the time of the grant, will vest in tranches between 2023 and 2025.

title: The Most Popular ESG ETF is also a Five-Star Fund
summary: The iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA ETF (ESGU), a five-star rated fund by CFRA, is highlighted for its strong record and appealing holdings. Despite excluding some companies based on ESG criteria, ESGU has outperformed the S&P 500 Index over various periods and maintains a sector exposure similar to the broader market. CFRA recommends ESGU for investors seeking ESG-focused strategies, citing its low expense ratio and growing popularity.

title: All of 'Mad Men' Available on AMC Plus
summary: All seven seasons of the AMC hit drama "Mad Men" are now available commercial-free on AMC Plus, AMC Networks’ new streaming bundle. The streaming service also features original programming from AMC, IFC, BBC America, WE TV, and SundanceTV, including shows like "The Walking Dead" and "Killing Eve." AMC Plus is accessible to subscribers of Amazon Prime Video Channels, Apple TV Channels, Comcast Xfinity, Dish, and Sling TV, with pricing set at $8.99 monthly for some providers.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | General Motors’ Credit Card, Brought to You by Goldman Sachs
summary: Goldman Sachs is set to acquire General Motors' credit-card business for approximately $2.5 billion, marking its second co-branded credit card venture after the Apple card. This deal expands Goldman's presence in Main Street lending, giving it ownership of over a million GM cardholders and their $8.5 billion in annual spending. The acquisition takes place during a challenging economic period when banks are bracing for potential losses due to deferred payments linked to the coronavirus pandemic.

title: Greg “Joz” Joswiak
summary: Greg “Joz” Joswiak is the Senior Vice President of Worldwide Marketing at Apple, reporting to CEO Tim Cook. He has been with Apple since June 1986, playing a key role in the development and launch of major products like the iPod and iPhone. With over 30 years of marketing experience, he previously served as vice president of Apple Product Marketing, managing the product marketing and management teams across Apple's entire product lineup.

title: Phil Schiller
summary: Phil Schiller is an Apple Fellow, responsible for leading the App Store and Apple Events. He has guided Apple's products and marketing for 30 years, contributing to the development of the Mac, iPod, iTunes, iPhone, App Store, and iPad. Prior to Apple, Schiller held roles at Macromedia, FirePower Systems, Nolan, Norton & Company, and Massachusetts General Hospital, and he graduated from Boston College with a degree in Biology.

title: Birthday week: Cloudflare turns 10
summary: Cloudflare celebrated its 10th birthday with a "Birthday Week" featuring several significant announcements. Key updates included advancements in their Workers platform with Durable Objects and Cron Triggers, a new privacy-focused Web Analytics product, and the launch of Cloudflare Radar for real-time internet traffic insights. The company also introduced API enhancements like gRPC support and API Shield, and Automatic Platform Optimization for improved website performance, starting with WordPress.

title: Apple Announces “Hi, Speed” Event for 13 October 2020
summary: Apple announced a "Hi, Speed" product announcement event for October 13, 2020, to be held at Apple Park. Expectations are high for new iPhones, possibly with 5G capabilities, given the event's tagline, and potential updates regarding Apple Silicon Macs. Other anticipated announcements include refreshed HomePod and AirPods lines, and possibly updates to the Apple TV or iPad Pro.

title: Apple Stops Selling Third-Party Audio Gear. It’s a Setback for Sonos and Logitech.
summary: Apple has reportedly ceased selling third-party headphones and speakers in its stores and online, impacting companies like Sonos and Logitech. This move has led to a dip in the stock prices of both Sonos and Logitech. The decision signals Apple's potential focus on promoting its own audio products.

title: Apple pulls rival products from stores ahead of launch
summary: Apple Inc. has removed rival products from its retail and online stores ahead of the anticipated launch of its new iPhone, speakers, and headphones. This strategic move impacts brands like Bose Corp., Sonos Inc., and Logitech International SA, and is a recurring practice for Apple, previously seen when it launched the Apple Watch in 2014. The company stated this is part of its regular curation of third-party accessories to complement its own products.

title: Walmart tests electronic tech services similar to Best Buy's Geek Squad
summary: Walmart is testing electronic tech services, similar to Best Buy's Geek Squad, in partnership with True Network Solutions. The services are being piloted in a Springdale, Arkansas Supercenter and four Dallas-Fort Worth area stores, offering smartphone repairs and diagnostic data recovery among other tech support. This initiative positions Walmart to compete with Best Buy by offering lower prices on services like screen replacements.

title: QQQ’s Stellar ETF Rise
summary: The article discusses the enduring success of the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) ETF despite criticisms of its underlying Nasdaq-100 index structure. It highlights QQQ's significant asset growth, strong performance against major indices, and its appeal to investors seeking exposure to tech and growth stocks, particularly among younger generations. The piece also delves into the unique rules and rebalancing methodology of the Nasdaq-100 and QQQ's substantial marketing efforts.

title: 8 ways Alaska Airlines is committed to making travel more accessible
summary: Alaska Airlines is dedicated to making travel more accessible for all guests and employees, highlighting eight key initiatives. These include supporting the employee ACCESS business resource group, consulting with a Disability Advisory Board, and developing a "Fly for All" app to ease travel anxiety. The airline also offers accessible travel options, organizes airport tours for the autism community, collaborates with organizations for service animals, implements touch-free travel solutions, and actively seeks customer feedback.

title: Morgan Stanley’s Makeover (Published 2020)
summary: Morgan Stanley is continuing its strategic shift away from investment banking toward steadier, fee-based businesses, exemplified by its recent $7 billion acquisition of Eaton Vance. This move, coupled with the earlier E-Trade purchase, aims to bolster its wealth and investment management divisions, which already account for over half of its revenue. CEO James Gorman hopes this transformation will lead analysts to value Morgan Stanley more like Charles Schwab, which trades at a higher earnings multiple.

title: Flexjet, Sentient Jet boss sets SPAC to target $100 million + acquistions
summary: Kenn Ricci, the principal of Directional Aviation (which controls Flexjet and Sentient Jet), is launching Zanite Acquisition Corporation (ZAC), a Special Purpose Acquisition Company. ZAC aims to target next-generation aviation acquisitions valued above Directional Aviation's typical $150 million ceiling, focusing on new areas like electric and sustainable aviation. This move addresses a gap between Directional Aviation's usual investment range and the larger investments pursued by private equity firms.

title: U.S. judge rules Apple could bar Epic Games's 'Fortnite' from App Store
summary: A federal judge ruled that Apple Inc. could remove Epic Games's "Fortnite" from its App Store but must not impact Epic's developer tools business, which includes the "Unreal Engine" software. This decision partially granted and partially denied Epic Games' preliminary injunction request in their lawsuit against Apple regarding anticompetitive behavior concerning App Store commissions and controls. The judge acknowledged Epic's strong arguments but stated Epic had not sufficiently addressed Apple's counterarguments.

title: Peloton rolling out "Skip Intro" feature
summary: Peloton is introducing a "Skip Intro" feature for experienced riders, allowing them to bypass the minute-long class introductions. This feature will be available on Peloton hardware and the web, but not on their apps, and requires users to have completed at least five cycling classes to unlock it. The update aims to improve the workout experience for those already familiar with Peloton's interface and class structure.

title: Apple announces iPhone 12 and iPhone 12 mini: A new era for iPhone with 5G
summary: Apple has unveiled the iPhone 12 and iPhone 12 mini, introducing 5G technology, a new design with edge-to-edge Super Retina XDR displays and Ceramic Shield front cover, and the A14 Bionic chip. These new models feature an advanced dual-camera system, MagSafe for enhanced wireless charging and accessories, and include iOS 14. Apple also emphasized its commitment to the environment with these new devices, using 100% recycled rare earth elements in all magnets and removing power adapters and EarPods from packaging.

title: 'The next generation is here': Apple unveils its first-ever 5G iPhone
summary: Apple officially unveiled its first-ever 5G iPhone, the iPhone 12, marking a "new era" for the company. CEO Tim Cook highlighted the significance of the 5G technology, which industry analysts regard as a "once-in-a-decade product cycle." The new line includes the iPhone 12 Mini, iPhone 12, iPhone 12 Pro, and iPhone 12 Pro Max, with varying features and price points, and also introduced a revamped HomePod mini.

title: Corning Inc. glass technology used for new iPhone 12
summary: Corning Inc.'s new Ceramic Shield technology is being utilized in Apple’s newly unveiled iPhone 12, significantly improving its durability. This advanced glass-ceramic material, developed in collaboration with Apple, offers 4X better drop performance than previous iPhone models. Apple invested $250 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund into Corning Inc. to support research and development for next-generation consumer devices.

title: Buy HomePod mini
summary: This page on Apple's website facilitates the purchase of the HomePod mini, detailing its features and comparing it to the larger HomePod. It highlights the HomePod mini's surprising sound, smart home capabilities, and includes information about a free Apple Music trial. The page also provides extensive details regarding AppleCare One and AppleCare+ plans, outlining their terms, conditions, and coverage for various devices, including accidental damage, theft, and loss.

title: Apple introduces iPhone 12 Pro and iPhone 12 Pro Max with 5G
summary: Apple announced the iPhone 12 Pro and iPhone 12 Pro Max, featuring 5G capability, the A14 Bionic chip, a new design with Ceramic Shield, and advanced pro camera systems. These new iPhones also introduce a LiDAR Scanner for AR and MagSafe for enhanced wireless charging and accessories. Pre-orders for the iPhone 12 Pro begin October 16, and for the iPhone 12 Pro Max on November 6.

title: Apple Announces First 5G Smartphone, iPhone 12
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple has announced its first lineup of 5G-enabled smartphones, the iPhone 12 series, which includes four models: the iPhone 12, iPhone 12 Mini, iPhone 12 Pro, and iPhone 12 Pro Max. While these new phones support 5G technology for faster wireless speeds, the full benefits may not be immediately available to all users as 5G networks are still under construction in many regions. Aside from the phones, Apple also revealed a smaller, more affordable HomePod Mini smart speaker.

title: BBVA launches Aqua, the first card without numbers or a CVV
summary: BBVA has launched Aqua, a new line of credit cards in Spain that enhances security by featuring a dynamic CVV code and omitting printed card numbers or expiration dates. This innovation aims to prevent fraudulent use both online and physically. The associated BBVA app also provides renewed payment management, allowing customers to easily access card details and control expenses, with plans for a global rollout of this experience.

title: Free Apple 5G iPhones are offered by carriers to lure upgraders
summary: US carriers such as AT&T and Verizon are offering free Apple iPhone 12 devices to new and existing customers to attract subscribers during the 5G upgrade cycle. These promotions require qualifying trade-ins and unlimited plans. The carriers are gambling that by covering the phone cost, subscribers will sign up for higher-priced unlimited data plans, despite potential economic headwinds and initial limited 5G network speeds.

title: Chip machine maker ASML posts growth despite COVID-19
summary: Dutch microchip machine maker ASML Holding NV reported better-than-expected third-quarter sales, driven by sustained demand from the tech sector despite the COVID-19 pandemic. The company's sales rose 33% from the previous year, and net profit increased by 69%. ASML expects continued growth in the coming year, despite macroeconomic uncertainties, due to factors like the 5G network.

title: iPad Air 4 release date revealed by Best Buy, matches previous rumors
summary: Best Buy has listed the iPad Air 4 with an October 23 release date, corroborating earlier rumors from Jon Prosser. While Best Buy later removed the date, Apple's own website still indicates an October launch, suggesting the release is imminent. The new iPad Air features a redesigned look similar to the iPad Pro, an A14 processor, and Touch ID in the top button.

title: Taiwanese iPhone 12 Pre-Orders Sold Out in 45 Minutes
summary: Pre-orders for the iPhone 12 and iPhone 12 Pro in Taiwan sold out in just 45 minutes, with Chunghwa Telecom (CHT) reporting 65% of its pre-orders were for the iPhone 12. Far EasTone Telecommunications (FET) saw three times higher iPhone 12 pre-orders compared to the iPhone 11 lineup last year. Market sources believe the iPhone 12 series will significantly boost demand for 5G services in Taiwan.

title: The End of Anonymity? Ensuring Facial Recognition Is Used for Good
summary: Facial recognition technology is rapidly becoming commonplace, raising significant questions about privacy, ethical use, and regulation, especially in Canada. While it offers commercial benefits like personalized services and easier verification, its widespread application requires clear guidelines to prevent misuse and address concerns about surveillance and inherent biases in the technology. Canadian businesses are urged to approach this technology cautiously, educate themselves on risks, and advocate for federal regulatory guidance to ensure responsible adoption.

title: 5G iPhone 12 off to strong start as Taiwan pre-orders sell out in 45 minutes
summary: Pre-orders for the 5G iPhone 12 and iPhone 12 Pro in Taiwan saw exceptional demand, with one major telecom operator selling out its quota in under 45 minutes. While Taiwan is a small market for Apple, these strong initial figures provide a positive indication ahead of global pre-orders in key markets like the United States, Europe, and China. Apple is set to release two more iPhone 12 models in November, including the iPhone 12 mini and iPhone 12 Pro Max, which are expected to further impact sales.

title: Fisker closes deal for supplier Magna to build its SUV, take stake in startup
summary: Fisker Inc. announced a deal with Magna International Inc. for Magna to supply the vehicle platform and build its Ocean SUV, with Magna also receiving warrants for up to a 6% stake in Fisker. This partnership marks Magna's first entry into contract manufacturing for an EV startup and is seen as critical for Fisker's future, targeting significant sales volumes for the Ocean SUV starting in 2022. Fisker's merger with Spartan Energy Acquisition Corp. is expected to close on Oct. 30, with shares trading as "FSR" on the NYSE.

title: IYW ETF A Novel Look for Large Tech
summary: This article highlights the importance of large-cap tech exposure for financial advisors, especially given the sector's continued dominance during the COVID-19 pandemic. It discusses the Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) as a popular option and introduces the iShares U.S. Technology ETF (IYW) as an alternative for investors seeking exposure to top tech companies like Apple and Microsoft, emphasizing technology's vital role regardless of political changes.

title: New 5G iPhones kick off poaching season for carriers
summary: The introduction of Apple's new 5G-enabled iPhones is initiating a competitive battle among major cellular carriers like AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile. These providers are leveraging 5G technology in their marketing strategies to attract customers, understanding that new phone purchases are key moments for subscribers to switch carriers. While 5G promises faster speeds, some analysts and consumers believe its revolutionary impact on smartphone usage is yet to be fully realized.

title: Apple iPhone 12 Review: Superfast Speed, if You Can Find It
summary: This review of the Apple iPhone 12 highlights its advanced 5G capabilities but notes the significant challenge of finding locations with access to the fastest 5G networks. While the phone delivered impressive speeds in specific areas, the widespread availability of high-speed 5G is still limited, leading to a less consistent experience for users. The article suggests that while 5G is a promising feature, its practical benefits are currently hampered by the uneven rollout of the network infrastructure.

title: A lot more goes into good smartphone haptics than you'd think
summary: This article delves into the intricacies of smartphone haptics, explaining the technology behind the vibrations and tactile feedback users experience. It distinguishes between older eccentric rotating mass (ERM) motors and modern linear resonant actuators (LRAs), highlighting how LRAs provide crisper and more precise sensations. The piece also emphasizes the crucial role of software, like those developed by Cirrus Logic, in controlling and enhancing haptic performance, even suggesting that Apple's Taptic Engine sets the benchmark for superior haptic feedback in mobile devices.

title: South Korea's SK Hynix to buy Intel's NAND business for $9 bln
summary: SK Hynix is set to acquire Intel's NAND memory chip business for $9 billion in an all-cash deal, positioning the South Korean company as the world's second-largest NAND memory producer. This move allows Intel to divest its non-core businesses and focus on its more lucrative Optane memory technology, while SK Hynix aims to boost its NAND capacity and market share. The deal is expected to consolidate the memory chip industry, despite initial negative reaction from SK Hynix shareholders regarding the price.

title: Apple, Google Worked as ‘One Company’ on Search Deal, U.S. Says
summary: The U.S. Justice Department has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Alphabet Inc.'s Google, accusing it of abusing its monopoly in search through a multibillion-dollar deal with Apple Inc. This agreement makes Google the default search engine on iPhones and other Apple devices. Documents from Google reportedly describe the potential loss of this deal as a "Code Red."

title: Airbnb hires former Apple designer Jony Ive's firm for multi-year partnership
summary: Airbnb has announced a multi-year partnership with Jony Ive's design firm, LoveFrom, to work on new products and services and help develop Airbnb's internal design team. This collaboration comes as Airbnb prepares for one of the largest U.S. stock market listings of 2020 and follows the news that Airbnb's Chief Design Officer, Alex Schleifer, will move to a part-time role. Jony Ive, known for his nearly three decades at Apple designing iconic products like the iMac and iPhone, left the company last year to form LoveFrom.

title: Apple's iPhone 12 to propel S. Korea's 5G user growth
summary: Apple's new iPhone 12 is expected to significantly boost South Korea's 5G user base, potentially reaching over 10 million by year-end, according to local analysts. This surge is anticipated to help telecom operators struggling with slower-than-expected 5G adoption and network quality issues, despite the fact that Korean users will not have access to the fastest mmWave 5G speeds available in other markets like the US.

title: Apple expands 'Express' retail store format ahead of holiday season
summary: Apple is expanding its new "Express" retail store format to navigate holiday sales challenges and rising COVID-19 rates in the U.S. and Europe. These stores allow customers to pick up online orders or receive service by appointment, maintaining social distancing with plexiglass barriers. This strategic move helps Apple launch its iPhone 12 during the pandemic, although analysts predict a slight dip in sales due to the retail environment.

title: Babe Rainbow brighten their year during COVID, land syncs with Apple, Nissan and Netflix
summary: The psychedelic rock group Babe Rainbow, facing halted touring due to COVID-19, pivoted successfully to sync licensing. They secured placements for their music in advertisements for major companies like Apple, Nissan, and Allstate Insurance, as well as in Netflix productions. This strategic shift allowed the band to maintain momentum during the pandemic, capitalizing on the growing sync sector and their unique appeal.

title: Fix, or Toss? The ‘Right to Repair’ Movement Gains Ground (Published 2020)
summary: The "right to repair" movement is gaining traction, with increasing efforts across the U.S. and Europe to make it easier for consumers to fix their own products. Manufacturers have made repairs difficult, but new legislation and public pressure aim to change this for a wide range of items, from cellphones to medical devices. This initiative seeks to counter anticompetitive practices and empower consumers and independent repair shops.

title: SanDisk IXpand Wireless Charger Sync Can Also Backup Smartphone Data
summary: SanDisk, a Western Digital company, has introduced two new wireless charging accessories: the SanDisk Ixpand Wireless Charger Sync and the Ixpand Wireless Charger 15W. The "Sync" model offers integrated data backup for contacts, photos, and videos while charging, available in up to 256GB, ideal for users without microSD slots. The 15W model provides traditional fast wireless charging without the backup feature; both include safety features and come with a 2-year warranty, with the Sync starting at $99.99 and the 15W at $49.99.

title: Comparing AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon's unlimited 5G wireless plans for iPhone 12
summary: This article compares the unlimited 5G wireless plans offered by AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon for iPhone 12 users. It breaks down each carrier's various tiers, highlighting data throttling limits, hotspot allowances, video streaming quality, and bundled subscription services like HBO Max, Disney+, and Netflix. The author advises checking coverage maps and considering individual usage habits to find the best plan, noting that "unlimited" plans invariably come with caveats.

title: If you suddenly can't print to your HP Printer from your Mac, you're not alone: Code security cert snafu blamed
summary: Many Mac users are unable to print to their HP printers due to a code-signing certificate issue affecting macOS Catalina and Mojave. Apple, via an XProtect update, revoked the certificate for HP's printer driver software, causing Macs to reject it as potentially damaging. HP later confirmed they "unintentionally revoked credentials on some older versions of Mac drivers" and are working with Apple to restore them, recommending AirPrint as a temporary workaround.

title: ExxonMobil Deploys QR Codes And NFC Tags for Streamlined Mobile Payment at the Pump
summary: ExxonMobil is implementing QR codes and NFC tags at over 11,500 U.S. stations to enable streamlined mobile payments with Apple Pay and Google Pay. This system leverages Apple App Clips for iPhone users without the ExxonMobil app and provides a web-based solution for Android users, enhancing contactless payment options at the pump. The initiative is part of ExxonMobil's ongoing efforts to innovate and offer secure, convenient payment methods, building on prior contactless payment solutions like "Alexa, Pay for Gas."
