title: AT&T Has The Most Disappointing Big Company Stock This Year
summary: AT&T Inc. (NYSE: T) has recorded the worst stock performance among large-cap companies this year, dropping 27% while the S&P 500 gained 7%. This decline is significant compared to top companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Facebook, which have seen substantial gains. The company also reported a 5% fall in operating revenue and a 22% drop in EPS, coupled with massive long-term debt, despite an impressive dividend yield.

title: How to invest in the S&P 500 without betting hard on the ‘FAAMG’ stocks that are 20% of the index
summary: This article discusses how to invest in the S&P 500 without heavily relying on the "FAAMG" stocks (Facebook, Apple, Amazon.com, Microsoft, and Google/Alphabet), which represent a significant portion of the index's valuation. It suggests that when investing in a standard S&P 500 index fund, one is implicitly making a large bet on the continued success of these five tech giants. The article hints at an alternative strategy, specifically mentioning an "Equal-weight S&P 500 ETF," for investors looking to diversify away from the concentrated influence of FAAMG stocks within the S&P 500.

title: How to invest in the S&P 500 without betting hard on the ‘FAAMG’ stocks that are 20% of the index
summary: This article discusses a strategy for investing in the S&P 500 without being overly reliant on the performance of the dominant "FAAMG" stocks. It highlights that the FAAMG stocks (Facebook, Apple, Amazon.com, Microsoft, and Google/Alphabet) collectively represent about 20% of the S&P 500's valuation. The piece suggests that investing in an equal-weight S&P 500 ETF can mitigate the concentrated risk associated with these technology giants.

title: How to invest in the S&P 500 without betting hard on the ‘FAAMG’ stocks that are 20% of the index
summary: This article discusses a strategy to invest in the S&P 500 without being overly exposed to the "FAAMG" stocks (Facebook, Apple, Amazon.com, Microsoft, and Google/Alphabet) which collectively represent 20% of the index's valuation. It suggests that when investing in a typical S&P 500 index fund, one is inherently making a significant bet on the continued success of these five dominant companies. The piece broadly hints at an "equal-weight S&P 500 ETF" as a potential solution for investors seeking diversification away from these outsized positions.

title: Apple Cable Supplier Said to Start Mass Production in India Soon
summary: Cheng Uei Precision Industry Co., a cable and connector maker, is expected to begin mass production for Apple Inc. in India later this year from its new plant. This move further expands Apple's manufacturing presence in South Asia, following major assembly partners like Foxconn, Pegatron, and Wistron who have already established operations in India. The chairman of Cheng Uei is T.C. Gou, brother of Foxconn founder Terry Gou.

title: Pacific blue iPhone 12 Pro a hit on 1st day sale in Taiwan
summary: The Pacific blue iPhone 12 Pro was a major success on its first day of sales in Taiwan, with telecom providers reporting high demand and supply shortages for the new color. The iPhone 12 Pro, along with other models in the iPhone 12 series, supports 5G and is expected to drive a significant upgrade cycle. Taiwanese consumers showed a particular preference for the 256GB Pacific blue iPhone 12 Pro, which was the top seller for Chunghwa Telecom.

title: OBITUARY Samsung's Lee: tainted titan who built a global tech giant
summary: Lee Kun-hee, the late chairman of Samsung, transformed his father's noodle trading business into a global tech empire, instilling a "sense of crisis" in his leadership to drive relentless innovation and quality. Despite being a reclusive and sometimes abrasive leader, he was instrumental in Samsung's rise to dominance in electronics, beating out major international competitors. His legacy is also marked by controversy, including convictions for tax evasion and embezzlement, though he received a presidential pardon.

title: Apple, Google and a Deal That Controls the Internet (Published 2020)
summary: The Justice Department has filed a landmark antitrust lawsuit against Google, targeting a secretive partnership with Apple worth billions where Google's search engine is the preselected choice on Apple devices. This alliance, seen as "co-opetition" by former Apple counsel, is now under scrutiny for potentially stifling competition and preventing smaller companies from flourishing. The lawsuit highlights the complex relationship between the two tech giants and their role in controlling web search.

title: Etsy to offer installment payment plans via Klarna
summary: Etsy has partnered with Klarna to offer installment payment plans to its U.S. shoppers, allowing them to pay for purchases between $50 and $10,000 in interest-free installments or financed plans. This initiative aims to support small businesses by providing payment flexibility to customers, especially relevant given Etsy's significant growth during the COVID-19 pandemic. Klarna continues to expand its partnerships with retailers and brands, though it faces competition from other buy-now-pay-later services like Affirm and Afterpay.

title: Artificial intelligence helping review, change bad word choices used in the workplace
summary: Artificial intelligence is being used in workplaces, including the Valley Transportation Authority (VTA) and Intuit, to review and suggest changes to language, aiming to remove racially insensitive terms and promote respectful communication. This movement comes as a result of the social justice movement and growing attention to messaging, with software like San Francisco-based Writer incorporating AI to aid in this process. Experts emphasize that while AI can help, human oversight remains crucial for cultural sensitivity and historical context.

title: Microsoft Tunnel Makes Connecting to Corporate Apps Easier from Android and iOS
summary: Microsoft Tunnel is a new VPN gateway solution for Intune, announced at Ignite in September 2020, designed to make connecting to on-premises corporate resources easier from Android and iOS devices. Differentiating itself from existing VPN solutions, it integrates fully with Microsoft 365, supporting single sign-on and Azure Active Directory Conditional Access policies. The solution aims to be enterprise-ready with simple deployment, automatic updates, and an app for users, although always-on VPN is limited to Android due to iOS restrictions.

title: Forrester: Marketing will shift to localized, loyalty-based efforts in 2021
summary: Forrester Research predicts that business-to-consumer (B2C) marketers will increase spending on loyalty and retention marketing by 15% in 2021, while reducing product and performance-based marketing. This shift is driven by the ongoing impact of the pandemic, leading to a focus on customer retention, localized campaigns, and automation. Marketers will face challenges with increased consumer messaging, tracking difficulties, and reduced budgets, necessitating more contextually relevant communications and a reevaluation of traditional sponsorships.

title: T-Mobile Unveils ‘TVision’ Skinny Pay-TV Bundles Starting at $10 per Month in New Play for Cord-Cutters
summary: T-Mobile has launched new TVision internet packages, aiming to attract cord-cutters and existing pay-TV customers with bundles starting at $10 per month. The service includes various channel packages and an Android TV-based adapter, with availability expanding from T-Mobile postpaid users to other customer segments in the future. This move by T-Mobile directly challenges traditional pay-TV providers and rivals like AT&T, leveraging the technology from its 2017 acquisition of Layer3.

title: Election, Stimulus, or Rising Infections — What’s Driving Market Volatility?
summary: The ProShares Ultra VIX Short-Term Futures ETF (UVXY) spiked significantly on October 26th due to increased stock market volatility. This surge was attributed to concerns over rising COVID-19 infections, stalled stimulus talks, and upcoming U.S. elections and mega-cap company earnings reports. The article explains how the VIX Index and UVXY function in measuring and leveraging market volatility.

title: Apple shrinks, Amazon grows among Jabil’s largest customers
summary: Jabil's annual report shows a shift in its largest customer base, with Apple's share of revenue decreasing while Amazon's grows significantly. This diversification strategy aims to reduce Jabil's reliance on a single customer, a concern previously highlighted by analysts. The company also reported an increase in employees and manufacturing space, along with COVID-19 related costs.

title: Sands Retail – Bringing London to Macao
summary: The Londoner Macao is a new integrated resort set to recreate London's classic shopping districts and offer British-themed experiences. Developed by Las Vegas Sands Corporation, it will feature authentic recreations of British landmarks, luxurious interiors, new hotels, and an enhanced retail and F&B mix. The project also includes exclusive suites designed in collaboration with David Beckham, aiming to provide guests with a unique British hospitality experience in Macao.

title: ServiceNow Appoints Apple Executive Larry Jackson to Its Board of Directors
summary: ServiceNow announced the appointment of Apple executive Larry Jackson, Global Creative Director of Apple Music, to its board of directors, increasing the board to 11 members. CEO Bill McDermott highlighted Jackson's expertise in consumer experiences and innovative mindset as crucial for ServiceNow's growth. Jackson, instrumental in launching Apple Music and known for his work at Beats and Universal Music Group, expressed his enthusiasm for ServiceNow's mission to enhance the world of work.

title: Amazon bites into Apple's share of Jabil's revenues
summary: Amazon has significantly increased its business with St. Petersburg-based Jabil in 2020, becoming a larger customer. While Apple remains Jabil's biggest revenue source, Amazon's share of Jabil's revenues grew substantially this year. This indicates a diversification of Jabil's customer base.

title: Apple’s Q4 2020 Marks Record Revenues but Lower Profits as Mac and iPad Boom
summary: Apple reported record-setting revenues of $64.7 billion for Q4 2020, but net profits were down 7.4% to $12.67 billion due to a significant 20.7% drop in iPhone sales because of delayed iPhone 12 models. Despite this, Mac and iPad sales saw astronomical growth, rising 29.2% and 46% respectively, driven by increased remote work and schooling. The Services category also posted record growth of 16.3%, reaching $14.5 billion in revenue, while Wearables, Home, and Accessories were up 20.8%.

title: Apple’s micro-LED supply chain to disrupt display industry?
summary: Over $5 billion has been invested in micro-LED development, with companies like Apple and Samsung investing heavily. Apple's strategy involves using silicon CMOS micro-drivers and internally assembling components from foundry partners, reducing reliance on traditional display makers. Meanwhile, Samsung aims to use micro-LEDs to compete in the high-end TV market, and AUO sees it as critical for its survival in premium automotive and TV panels, avoiding massive OLED fab investments.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN), Apple (AAPL) Probed by Germany Over Online Sales Curbs
summary: Germany's Federal Cartel Office is investigating Amazon.com and Apple over a policy known as "brandgating." This policy allows brand product manufacturers, like Apple, to have independent sellers removed from Amazon's platform if Amazon itself sells the items, raising concerns about potential antitrust violations.

title: Apple's subscription bundle of news, music and services to launch Friday
summary: Apple Chief Executive Tim Cook announced that the Apple One bundle, which includes news, music, video, and cloud storage, would launch on Friday, October 30, 2020. This move increases Apple's competition with services like Spotify and Netflix, and Cook also noted that Apple Fitness+ would launch later in the year. Apple has already exceeded 585 million subscribers on its platforms and anticipates surpassing 600 million for the calendar year, with services revenue up by 16.3% to $14.5 billion.

title: Geojit Financial Services launches AI-powered global investment platform
summary: Geojit Financial Services has launched an AI-powered global investment platform in partnership with Stockal, a New York-based wealth management services platform. This new platform allows Indian investors to diversify their portfolios by investing in global equities, starting with US stocks and ETFs. The initiative aims to cater to the growing interest among domestic investors, high-net-worth individuals, and expatriates in global asset classes.

title: With robotaxis still a distant dream, lidar makes itself useful
summary: The article discusses how lidar technology, initially poised for robotaxis, is now finding more immediate applications in advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) for passenger cars due to delays in fully autonomous vehicles. Companies specializing in lidar are pivoting to make the sensors more affordable for semi-autonomous features, with the market for lidar sensors in light-duty vehicles projected to reach $46 billion by 2030. Despite skepticism from some, lidar is increasingly seen as a critical safety component for higher levels of autonomy, offering a more robust alternative or complement to camera and radar systems.

title: Apple's late iPhone launch temporarily wiped $100 billion off its stock value
summary: A delayed launch of new 5G iPhones led to Apple Inc.'s steepest quarterly drop in iPhone sales in two years, causing its stock value to temporarily fall by $100 billion. Despite strong sales in other product categories like Macs and AirPods, iPhone sales dropped significantly, particularly in China, but CEO Tim Cook expressed optimism for a recovery in the fiscal first quarter due to the new 5G devices. The company's dependence on iPhone sales remains a concern for investors, even as its services segment shows continued growth.

title: Hugh Regan Jr. ’82
summary: Hugh Regan Jr. ’82, CFO, treasurer, and secretary for inTEST Corporation, reflects on his successful career, which he attributes to hard work and his experiences at Rider University. A former president of the Student Entertainment Council and active in DAARSTOC, Regan quickly advanced in the finance world, becoming the youngest CFO of an NYSE-listed company at 34. He also dedicates significant time to the Girl Scouts, helping raise over $6 million, inspired by his mother's encouragement to give back.

title: Tesla short sellers have lost $27B betting against Elon Musk this year
summary: Tesla short sellers have experienced significant losses, totaling $27 billion in 2020, as the company's stock price has surged over 400%. Despite these losses, Tesla (TSLA) remains the most shorted US-listed stock, with $23.4 billion worth of shares currently shorted. While a major "short squeeze" is deemed unlikely due to the long-term nature of most short positions, the number of shorted shares has gradually decreased throughout the year as investors concede losses.

title: Apple loses $450b in value on iPhone woes
summary: Apple Inc. has lost $450 billion in market value since its peak last month, with a recent 5.6% drop wiping out over $120 billion. The decline follows disappointing iPhone sales that missed analysts' estimates and a lack of holiday quarter guidance. This slump, along with other tech giants' performance, is raising concerns about future growth in the sector.

title: Apple to Close Out 2020 with “One More Thing”
summary: Apple has announced its third and presumably final event of 2020 for November 10th, expected to focus on new Macs featuring Apple silicon and the release date for macOS 11 Big Sur. Rumors suggest new MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models, with potential updates for the Apple TV or iPad Pro also being considered. The article also touches on community speculation regarding the event's announcements.

title: Electric vehicle stocks come unplugged
summary: Henrik Fisker's electric car company, Fisker Inc., successfully merged with a SPAC, raising $1 billion and seeing its stock climb 13% on its first day of trading. Fisker aims to bring his Ocean SUV to market under $40,000, learning from past failures, by outsourcing hardware and focusing on design. However, the EV market faces challenges, including low consumer interest outside of Tesla, high prices, and recent plunges in other EV startup stocks like Hyliion and Lordstown, as well as established players like Nikola and Workhorse.

title: Apple to Launch MacBooks With Own Chips Next Week
summary: Apple is set to announce new Mac computers with its custom-designed processors next week, marking the beginning of the company's transition away from Intel chips. This "One more thing" event on November 10th will introduce the first Macs to feature Apple's in-house silicon, ending a 15-year partnership with Intel. The move is a significant strategic shift for Apple's computer line.

title: Austin’s Cirrus Logic announces CEO change
summary: Austin-based chipmaker Cirrus Logic announced that company president John Forsyth will become CEO next year, replacing longtime chief executive Jason Rhode. The announcement coincided with Cirrus Logic reporting strong quarterly earnings, surpassing Wall Street expectations, largely driven by smartphone component sales. The company's stock rose in after-hours trading following the news.

title: Comcast, Walmart in talks to develop and distribute smart TVs
summary: Comcast and Walmart are reportedly in discussions to develop and distribute smart TVs. This partnership aims to position Comcast as a major streaming hub, with Walmart promoting TVs running Comcast software and sharing in recurring revenue. The move would put them in direct competition with established tech giants like Apple, Amazon, and Roku in the smart TV market.

title: T-Mobile tops 100M customers, posts $1.3B in quarterly profit six months after Sprint merger
summary: T-Mobile announced strong third-quarter results, reaching over 100 million customers for the first time and posting $1.3 billion in quarterly profit. This growth occurred six months after its merger with Sprint, exceeding Wall Street expectations for both revenue and earnings per share. CEO Mike Sievert highlighted the "strongest financials in our history" despite a slower customer switching environment due to COVID-19, with further growth anticipated from 5G network expansion and the iPhone 12 launch.

title: Analyzing FRAND-Related Statements for Cellular Wireless SEPS (Part II)
summary: This article is the second in a series examining statements from entities in the cellular industry regarding the licensing of Standard Essential Patents (SEPs) on a Fair, Reasonable and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) basis. It focuses on unconditional offers to license, the arbitration of FRAND terms, specific FRAND rates and their application, and portfolio licensing. The authors discuss how various companies like Qualcomm, InterDigital, Nokia, and Apple articulate their FRAND commitments, often emphasizing flexibility, historical data, and the efficiency of portfolio licensing, while also noting differing views on arbitration and tying.

title: Apple must face shareholder lawsuit over CEO Cook's China sales comments
summary: A U.S. District Judge ruled that Apple Inc. must face a class-action lawsuit from shareholders accusing CEO Tim Cook of concealing declining iPhone demand in China, which led to significant investor losses. The lawsuit stems from Cook's 2018 comments assuring investors about China sales, shortly before Apple drastically cut its revenue forecast due to trade tensions impacting the Chinese economy. The judge found it plausible that Cook's statements were misleading and that he likely knew about the risks.

title: Carl Icahn boosts Xerox stake to over 14%
summary: Billionaire activist investor Carl Icahn increased his stake in Xerox Holdings Corp to approximately 14.4% as of November 5th. This move comes months after he supported Xerox's unsuccessful attempt to acquire HP Inc. Following the news of Icahn's increased stake, Xerox's shares rose 3.4% in extended trading.

title: Ink-Stained Wretches: The Battle for the Soul of Digital Freedom Taking Place Inside Your Printer
summary: This article criticizes HP's aggressive business practices in the printer industry, detailing how the company has shifted from innovation to exploiting customers through printer ink policies. It highlights HP's conversion of a "Free ink for life" plan to a paid subscription and its use of "security updates" to block third-party ink cartridges. The piece argues that HP's tactics reflect a broader trend among "smart" gadget manufacturers to redefine ownership and restrict user choice.

title: FAANG stands for five very successful tech companies that can move the stock market — here's what to know about investing in them
summary: The article defines FAANG as an acronym representing Facebook, Amazon, Apple, Netflix, and Google, five influential tech companies that significantly impact the stock market. It details their historical performance, their combined market capitalization, and how investors can invest in them, either individually or through various tech and index funds. The piece emphasizes their role as modern-day blue-chip stocks and their substantial impact on the S&P 500 and the broader US economy.

title: Converting Podcast Listeners Into Paying Customers Isn't Easy, but Taking Out the Ads Is a Good Start
summary: Converting podcast listeners into paying subscribers is a challenge due to the medium's historically free nature. However, Morning Consult data indicates potential demand for paid services, especially for ad-free content and bundled offerings, with frequent listeners showing the most willingness to subscribe. While advertising remains the primary revenue driver for many companies, platforms like Stitcher and Wondery have found success with premium content and exclusive deals, suggesting a gradual shift towards a more subscription-reliant model.

title: Apple's new Macs could revive the PC chip wars, analysts say
summary: Apple's expected unveiling of new Mac computers with in-house processor chips based on Arm Ltd. technology could re-ignite competition in the PC chip market, currently dominated by Intel and Advanced Micro Devices. This shift marks a departure from Apple's nearly 15-year relationship with Intel and could benefit companies like Qualcomm, which has been adapting Arm-based processors for Windows PCs. Analysts believe Apple's entry will accelerate the adoption of Arm-based PCs, although challenges remain for software compatibility.

title: Apple's new Macs could revive the PC chip wars, benefit Qualcomm: Analysts
summary: Apple is expected to unveil new Mac computers using its own in-house Arm-based processor chips, a move that could disrupt the PC chip market currently dominated by Intel and AMD. This shift could benefit companies like Qualcomm, which has been developing Arm-based processors for Windows PCs. Analysts suggest that Apple's entry into this market could accelerate the adoption of Arm-based PCs, despite challenges related to software compatibility.

title: Apple launches MacBook laptops powered by its own computing chips
summary: Apple has introduced new MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and Mac Mini models, marking a significant shift to its in-house designed M1 central processor, moving away from Intel technology. This move aims to technologically integrate Apple's computers and iPhones more closely, enhance battery life, and encourage developers to create universal apps for both platforms. The company anticipates cost savings and improved performance for its Mac lineup.

title: Introducing the next generation of Mac
summary: Apple announced a new generation of Mac computers, including the MacBook Air, 13-inch MacBook Pro, and Mac mini, all powered by Apple's M1 chip. This new chip offers significant performance improvements, enhanced machine learning capabilities, and extended battery life compared to previous generations. The updated Macs are also optimized for macOS Big Sur, providing a more seamless and powerful user experience with access to a wider range of applications.

title: Apple Launches Three New Macs to Kick Off Switch From Intel
summary: Apple announced a significant transition away from Intel chips by unveiling three new Mac computers featuring its in-house designed M1 processor: the MacBook Air, 13-inch MacBook Pro, and Mac mini. This marks the beginning of a two-year process to fully integrate Apple silicon across its Mac lineup. The announcement was made during Apple's virtual "One More Thing" event.

title: Apple unveils M1, the 1st in-house chip for Mac computers
summary: Apple unveiled its first in-house "Apple Silicon" chip, dubbed the M1, at its final event of 2020, powering new MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and Mac Mini models. This new chip aims to significantly improve performance, speed, and battery life for Macs, marking a major step for Apple to gain more control over its hardware development. The company plans to transition its entire computer lineup to Apple Silicon chips within a few years.

title: Apple’s New MacBooks (and Mac Mini) Are Powered By iPhone DNA
summary: Apple announced new Mac desktop and laptop computers powered by its homemade M1 chip, an ARM-based design similar to those found in iPhones and iPads. This marks a significant shift from Intel processors, promising benefits like improved battery life, graphics, and machine learning capabilities, along with compatibility for iOS and iPadOS apps. The initial models include new MacBook Air ($999), MacBook Pro ($1,299), and Mac Mini ($699) offerings.

title: How Apple built its own chip empire and gave Intel the boot
summary: Apple is transitioning its MacBooks from Intel chips to in-house silicon, marking a major milestone for the company's hardware and software integration. This move stems from a long-term strategy that began with the acquisition of P.A. Semi in 2008, enabling Apple to develop its own A-series chips for mobile devices. The decision was driven by improved performance of Apple's chips, better chip economics by outsourcing production, and greater control over hardware-software integration.

title: Apple unleashes M1
summary: Apple announced the M1 chip, its first chip designed specifically for the Mac, delivering industry-leading performance, powerful features, and incredible efficiency. The M1 chip integrates numerous technologies into a single SoC, featuring an 8-core CPU, the world's fastest integrated graphics, and a 16-core Neural Engine for accelerated machine learning. This launch marks the beginning of a two-year transition for the Mac to Apple silicon, with macOS Big Sur optimized to leverage the M1's capabilities.

title: TSMC Approves $3.5 Billion Capital Investment For Subsidiary In Arizona
summary: TSMC has approved a $3.5 billion capital investment to establish a subsidiary in Arizona, following earlier plans for a new semiconductor fabrication facility in the state. This move aims to expand production capacity for advanced process nodes and addresses U.S. concerns about semiconductor supply chain integrity. The investment also includes $15.1 billion for increased production capacity and R&D, complementing Apple's shift to in-house CPUs and GPUs likely manufactured by TSMC.

title: Apple’s iPhone 12 Pro Max and iPhone 12 mini land in stores and homes worldwide
summary: Apple announced the availability of its iPhone 12 Pro Max and iPhone 12 mini in stores and online starting November 13. The iPhone 12 Pro Max features Apple's most advanced pro camera system and the largest display, while the iPhone 12 mini is positioned as the world's smallest, thinnest, and lightest 5G smartphone. Apple emphasized its commitment to customer service, offering support both in-person at Apple Store locations and through online specialists for setup and shopping assistance.

title: Garmin quatix 6 review
summary: This article reviews the Garmin quatix 6 smartwatch, highlighting its extensive capabilities both on and off the water. It details how the watch integrates multisport features from the fenix 6, alongside advanced marine functionalities like boat data streaming, remote waypoint marking, and autopilot control, making it suitable for a wide range of outdoor and water-based activities. The review also touches upon its smart features, health monitoring, music storage, and robust design, positioning it as a versatile and sophisticated device for outdoor enthusiasts.

title: Sonos Radio HD subscription service launches with high-definition audio, curated radio
summary: Sonos has launched Sonos Radio HD, a new premium subscription service offering high-definition, lossless CD-quality audio without advertisements. It includes exclusive curated radio stations, original content, and new "soundtracks for home" categories, including sleep stations. The service costs $7.99 a month in the U.S. and U.K. with a one-month free trial.

title: What Stocks Did Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Buy in the 3rd Quarter? We Will Soon Find Out.
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway was active in the stock market during the third quarter, with movements in major holdings. The company bought stock in Bank of America, sold shares of Wells Fargo, and seemingly reduced its significant stake in Apple. More details on these transactions are expected to be revealed soon.

title: Apple, Google, and LG Start Working on 6G Technology
summary: Apple, Google, and LG have joined the Next G Alliance, a group focused on developing sixth-generation (6G) communication technology. Established in October by ATIS, the Alliance aims to position North America at the forefront of 6G innovation, even as 5G technology has yet to become mainstream. The group is working towards making 6G a reality potentially ahead of Samsung's 2028 estimate.

title: TSMC Will Open $3.5 Billion Semiconductor Fab in Arizona
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) announced plans to build a new $3.5 billion semiconductor fabrication plant in Arizona, with a total investment of $12 billion between 2021 and 2029. The facility is expected to begin production of 5nm chips in 2024, employing around 1,600 people, and aims to give TSMC a foothold in the US for potential government contracts. While the Arizona fab will produce 5nm chips, TSMC plans to keep its most advanced 3nm production in Taiwan to serve its high-volume customers.

title: Heads Up Health Closes $1.35M Seed Round Lead by Innosphere Ventures
summary: Heads Up Health, a digital health analytics company, has secured a $1.35 million seed round led by Innosphere Ventures. The company's HIPAA-compliant SaaS platform integrates data from wearables, genomics, labs, and EHRs to help users and healthcare providers improve health outcomes, with over 30,000 individual users globally. The investment will be used for accelerated growth, including adding enterprise capabilities, enhancing remote patient monitoring features, and expanding the sales team.

title: 115 Amazon lobbyists. 1 works on climate
summary: Despite Amazon's public stance on climate action and its "Climate Pledge," an analysis reveals that only one out of its 115 lobbyists is focused on environmental issues. This raises questions about the company's commitment, with critics suggesting it may be "greenwashing." Other tech giants like Facebook also show similar discrepancies, while Microsoft, Apple, and Google appear to align their lobbying efforts more closely with their climate concerns.

title: American Express launches support for adding new cards to Apple Pay instantly after approval
summary: American Express has launched a new feature allowing customers to instantly add newly approved credit cards to Apple Pay. This development allows American Express to compete with Apple Card's instant access feature, offering immediate use of new cards in retail stores, online, and in-app for U.S. consumer credit card customers.

title: Infographic: Gaming industry revenues grow 8.3% worldwide, pandemic accelerating upward trend
summary: The gaming industry is experiencing significant growth, with global revenues forecast to increase by 8.3% to $178.2 billion in 2021. This upward trend has been accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to increased spending by gamers, especially in mobile gaming. The industry is also proving to be crisis-resistant, with gaming shares performing well on the stock exchange.

title: American Express members can add card to Apple Pay after approval
summary: American Express has introduced a new feature that allows approved card members to add their new card to Apple Wallet immediately after application, streamlining the process for using Apple Pay. This integration builds on Amex's existing Instant Card Number system but offers automated provisioning and immediate access to membership benefits. The feature is currently available to U.S. customers applying for new consumer cards via AmericanExpress.com.

title: Microsoft reveals Pluton, a custom security chip built into Intel, AMD and Qualcomm processors
summary: Microsoft has announced Pluton, a new security chip developed in partnership with Intel, AMD, and Qualcomm. This chip aims to enhance security by acting as a hardware root-of-trust, protecting devices from tampering and hardware attacks by integrating directly into future CPUs. Drawing lessons from its use in Xbox One and Azure Sphere, Pluton is designed to make it significantly harder for hackers to exploit physical access to extract sensitive data and will also help keep device firmware up-to-date.

title: BlackBerry looks to sell majority of its patents as it refocuses
summary: BlackBerry Ltd. is reportedly looking to sell the majority of its 38,000 patents, a move that could generate over US$450-million but also raise concerns about Canadian intellectual property leaving the country. This comes as the company continues to pivot away from its smartphone business towards cybersecurity and automotive operating systems, relying on patent licensing for a significant portion of its revenue. Industry experts are debating the implications for Canada's innovation strategy and standing in the global patent market.

title: Garmin Pilot adds graphical airspace & obstacle NOTAMS
summary: Garmin International Inc. has introduced new features to its Garmin Pilot application for Apple mobile devices, enhancing pre-flight planning and in-flight situational awareness with graphical airspace and obstacle NOTAMs. The update also includes an improved user interface for Profile View, offering quicker access to critical flight information like weather and PIREPs. This release, version 10.3, is available immediately for Apple mobile devices, with subscription options after a 30-day free trial.

title: BIPA Lawsuit Proceeds Against Apple in Federal Court
summary: A federal court has allowed a class action lawsuit against Apple Inc. to proceed, alleging violations of Illinois' Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA) due to its facial recognition software in the Photo app. While some claims were remanded to state court for lack of federal standing, the central allegation that Apple collected biometric information without informed consent is permitted to continue in federal court. The judge found that facial geometries are clearly defined as biometric information under BIPA and that the alleged violation would constitute a concrete injury.

title: Nevada gets $1.6 million in iPhone ‘batterygate’ settlement
summary: Nevada will receive nearly $1.6 million from Apple as part of a multistate settlement over iPhone throttling. Apple is required to pay $113 million to over 30 states for violating consumer protection laws by concealing malfunctions and misrepresenting iPhone products. The settlement mandates that Apple provide accurate battery information and offer battery servicing to customers, while Nevada's share will fund the Attorney General's office’s legal fees and future consumer protection investigations.

title: Apple to Pay $113 Million Settlement Involving iPhone Batteries
summary: Apple has agreed to pay $113 million to settle allegations that it misled consumers about software updates that intentionally slowed down iPhone performance to mask battery issues. California will receive $24.6 million of this settlement, which also includes terms requiring clear disclosures to consumers about how updates affect performance and battery management. This resolution aims to protect consumers and ensure transparency from technology companies regarding their products.

title: Garmin adds pregnancy tracking feature to its smart devices and Connect platform
summary: Garmin has introduced a new pregnancy tracking feature to its smart devices and the Connect platform, making it the first brand to offer such comprehensive tracking. This allows pregnant users to log symptoms, track baby movements, create reminders, and receive tips, while also giving them the option to pause training plans on their devices. The feature is available on various Garmin models including the Forerunner, Fenix, Venu, and Vivoactive 4 series.

title: WWE SuperCard Season 7 Now Available
summary: 2K Games has released Season 7 of WWE SuperCard, introducing new features, card tiers, and gameplay mechanics. The update includes Styles & Techniques, Super Moves, three new card tiers (Bio-Mechanical, Behemoth, Swarm), and an art refresh. Players can also earn rewards through dynamic Collections and participate in a new WarGames event.

title: Who’s a Very Good Pandemic Business? Chewy Is. Oh, Yes It Is
summary: Chewy (CHWY) has experienced phenomenal growth in 2020, becoming a top pandemic business as pet care surged. CEO Sumit Singh's 2019 compensation of $108.2 million placed him among the highest-paid executives, despite the company not yet achieving profitability in its nine years of existence. This success highlights the booming online pet supply market.

title: Looking Closer At T. Rowe Price's Dividend Growth ETF, 'TDVG'
summary: The T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth ETF (TDVG), launched in August 2020, is an actively managed ETF that seeks dividend income and long-term capital growth by investing in dividend-paying companies expected to increase their dividends over time. Managed by Tom Huber, this ETF typically holds 100-125 stocks like Microsoft, Apple, and Visa, focusing on business model durability and financial health with a "growth-at-a-discount" approach. It's designed for investors seeking a mix of income and capital appreciation from an experienced management team.

title: Apple (AAPL) to Cut App Store Fees in Half to 15% for Most Developers
summary: Apple Inc. is set to cut App Store fees in half, to 15% from 30%, for developers who generate up to $1 million in annual revenue from their apps, effective January 1. This change, part of the App Store Small Business Program, marks the biggest alteration to the store’s revenue model since its 2008 launch and will apply to the vast majority of developers, though major apps like Netflix and Spotify will not be affected. Some developers, such as Spotify, have called the change "window dressing."

title: Apple to lower App Store fees for small developers, but some critics not impressed
summary: Apple announced a new program to lower its App Store commission to 15% for software developers who make $1 million US or less in proceeds annually. While some hail this as a "huge win" for smaller developers, critics like Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney and Spotify Technology SA dismissed it as "window dressing" and a calculated move to preserve Apple's monopoly and divide app creators. The change affects a large percentage of developers but a small portion of Apple's overall App Store revenue.

title: Apple Agrees To Pay $113 Million To Settle 'Batterygate' Case Over iPhone Slowdowns
summary: Apple has agreed to pay $113 million to settle consumer fraud lawsuits with more than 30 states over its "batterygate" controversy, where it secretly slowed down older iPhones. The settlement also requires Apple to be more transparent about software updates affecting battery life. This follows a previous agreement in March where Apple agreed to pay up to $500 million directly to consumers.

title: Apple, U.S. states reach $113 million settlement on iPhone throttling
summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to pay $113 million to settle allegations from 33 U.S. states and the District of Columbia that it intentionally slowed down iPhones to mask battery issues and prompt users to buy new devices. The settlement, which includes an agreement for Apple to provide truthful information about iPhone power management, is separate from a previous $500 million class-action settlement. States contended Apple acted deceptively by not disclosing the throttling or offering battery replacements.

title: Attorney General Josh Stein Announces $113 Million Settlement with Apple over iPhone Throttling
summary: Attorney General Josh Stein announced a $113 million multistate settlement with Apple Inc. regarding its 2016 decision to throttle iPhone speeds. North Carolina will receive over $2.7 million from the settlement. The agreement requires Apple to provide truthful information to consumers about iPhone battery health, performance, and power management.

title: AG Yost Announces $113 Million Settlement with Apple Over iPhone Throttling
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, along with over 30 other attorneys general, announced a $113 million settlement with Apple, Inc. regarding Apple’s 2016 decision to throttle iPhone speeds. The attorneys general alleged that Apple concealed battery issues leading to unexpected shutdowns, profiting by selling new iPhones after purposefully slowing older devices. As part of the settlement, Apple will pay Ohio $4.3 million and must provide truthful information to consumers about iPhone battery health and performance.

title: Atlantic City educators reimagine after-school learning to lift a community
summary: In Atlantic City, educators are transforming after-school programs through new technology labs at the Boys & Girls Club, focusing on STEAM, design, and career training. These labs, equipped with Apple products, aim to provide students with critical 21st-century skills and diverse career pathways beyond hospitality. Apple is supporting this initiative with device donations and curriculum integration to clubs nationally, fostering coding, creativity, and career development.

title: Exclusive: Apple hires Intel’s Barbara Whye as its new head of diversity
summary: Apple has announced that Barbara Whye, Intel's former head of diversity and inclusion, will join the company in early 2021 as its new vice president of inclusion and diversity. Whye is recognized for achieving significant representation goals at Intel two years ahead of schedule. Her appointment follows a $100 million racial justice initiative announced by Apple CEO Tim Cook, as the tech giant continues its efforts to diversify its workforce.

title: Nvidia, Epic to bring 'Fortnite' back to iPhones in 'near future'
summary: Nvidia and Epic Games are collaborating to bring "Fortnite" back to Apple iPhones through Nvidia's GeForce NOW streaming game service in the near future. The game was removed from Apple's App Store in August due to Epic's billing system, which violated Apple's rules. This return route bypasses Apple's App Store rules by utilizing the iPhone's Safari web browser.

title: Apple to Pay $113 Million Settlement Over ‘Batterygate’ Scandal
source: Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project | OCCRP
source_domain: Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project | OCCRP
summary: Apple has agreed to pay a $113 million settlement to 33 U.S. states over "batterygate" allegations, where the company was accused of deliberately slowing down older iPhones to encourage purchases of newer models. In addition to this, Apple is also liable for up to $500 million in a separate class-action lawsuit. The company had previously apologized for the performance issues, attributing them to a power management feature, and had offered discounted battery replacements.

title: Universal Electronics Debuts Remote for MVPDs Offering Apple TV
summary: Universal Electronics has introduced a new remote control designed for MVPDs offering Apple TV 4K to their subscribers. This remote is optimized for live TV, featuring quick access to electronic program guides and channel control buttons, unlike Apple's minimalist remote. It combines Bluetooth Low Energy and Infrared connectivity and offers features like voice control, automated backlit keys, and options for MVPD branding.

title: Why IBM’s Global Business Services Failed to Grow
summary: IBM's Global Business Services (GBS) segment experienced a decline in revenue in fiscal 1Q16, despite strong growth in its Strategic Imperatives and Cloud sectors. To counter this, IBM acquired several companies in the digital agency and marketing space and partnered with Apple and Twitter to strengthen its presence in software, cloud, and big data.

title: Applied Materials’s position in the semiconductor space
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) generates significant revenue from leading semiconductor companies like Samsung, Intel, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. The company's revenue growth is driven by orders from smaller customers and its diverse product lines, though it faces geographical risk due to a substantial portion of its revenue coming from Taiwan. The article highlights AMAT's ability to increase revenue and backlog despite chip capital expenditure forecasts remaining constant.

title: Why Teradyne’s offerings provide value to end-user markets
summary: This article examines Teradyne's (TER) diverse product line and how it delivers value to its customer base across various end-user markets. It details Teradyne's semiconductor test platforms like FLEX, J750, and Magnum, and how they cater to industries such as mobile, automotive, and data storage. The article also highlights Teradyne's key customers and its presence in semiconductor-focused ETFs.

title: The Apple TV remote has yet another alternate option that includes buttons
summary: Universal Electronics (UEI) has introduced a new Apple TV remote alternative designed for cable companies, addressing user frustration with the standard Apple TV remote's touchpad controls. This new remote features physical buttons, Siri voice command capability, backlit keys, and universal control for other entertainment devices. It will be available through cable, satellite, and MVPD customers starting next year, rather than directly to consumers.

title: Universal Electronics Offers Alternative Apple TV Remote to Cable Companies
summary: Universal Electronics has unveiled a new remote for Apple TV 4K, designed for cable, satellite, and MVPD customers. This alternative remote uses Bluetooth Low Energy and Infrared for universal control, includes Siri integration, a program guide button, and traditional media controls. It will be offered directly to cable companies rather than consumers, targeting the growing number of MVPDs providing Apple TV 4K to subscribers.

title: Hasbro makes some noise with Peppa Pig
summary: Hasbro is expanding Peppa Pig's presence into the audio streaming market with a new series of 10 audio stories to be released globally on platforms like Amazon Prime, Apple Music, and Spotify. This move follows the success of Peppa Pig's first digital music album, which garnered 24 million streams worldwide. The new audio content will feature stories inspired by popular episodes of the show, joining a growing number of kids' audio offerings.

title: T-Mobile destroys Verizon's 5G network claims by sliding in its coverage DMs
summary: T-Mobile has criticized Verizon's "nationwide" 5G network claims, asserting that Verizon's use of Dynamic Spectrum Sharing (DSS) makes its 5G speeds similar to 4G. T-Mobile highlights its wider 5G coverage, reaching 270 million people, and its faster average speeds, especially with its mid-band 5G deployment. The article discusses the confusion surrounding US carriers' 5G strategies and T-Mobile's counter-claims against Verizon.

title: Apple’s global security chief and two members of Sheriff’s office indicted for alleged bribery
summary: Apple’s global head of security, Thomas Moyer, and two members of the Santa Clara County Sheriff’s office have been indicted for an alleged bribery scheme. Moyer is accused of promising to donate 200 iPads to the sheriff's office in exchange for concealed weapons licenses for Apple employees. The district attorney's office claims Undersheriff Rick Sung and Captain James Jensen withheld the licenses until they received a valuable offer.

title: AMC Plus Launches on Roku Channel
summary: AMC Networks announced that its premium streaming service, AMC Plus, is now available on The Roku Channel. The service, priced at $8.99 a month, offers original programming from AMC's cable networks and streaming services, including "The Walking Dead Universe" and "Mad Men." This launch expands AMC Plus's distribution, following recent additions to Prime Video Channels, Apple TV channels, Comcast, Dish, and Sling TV.

title: Should you invest in technology sector funds?
summary: This article discusses the performance and prospects of technology sector funds in India, highlighting their strong returns during the pandemic and the factors influencing their growth. It explores investment options including domestic and international tech funds, the outlook for Indian IT firms, and crucial risks and investment strategies investors should consider, such as staggering investments via SIPs.

title: Developers say App Store Small Business Program is good for business
summary: Developers are expressing positive reactions to Apple's new App Store Small Business Program, set to launch on January 1, 2021. This program will reduce the commission for the vast majority of developers selling digital goods and services on the App Store to 15 percent. Many developers anticipate that this change will boost their revenue, allow for team expansion, and foster further innovation and creativity within the App Store ecosystem.

title: Salesforce invests in Israel's AppsFlyer at $2 billion valuation
summary: Salesforce Ventures has invested in Israeli startup AppsFlyer as part of its $210 million Series D round, valuing the company at $2 billion. AppsFlyer, which develops tools for analyzing and measuring digital campaigns, plans to recruit 350 additional employees after doubling its annual recurring revenue to over $200 million. Co-founder Oren Kaniel stated the company intends to remain private for now, focusing on long-term growth and leveraging Salesforce's operational knowledge.

title: Ozon IPO Is First U.S. Listing Of A Russian-Based Firm In 18 Months
summary: Russian e-commerce company Ozon Holdings (OZON) successfully raised $990 million in its initial public offering, marking the first U.S. listing by a Russian-based firm in 18 months. The IPO began trading on Tuesday afternoon. Ozon's listing on U.S. domestic exchanges breaks a dry spell for Russian IPOs since May 2019.

title: New CEO of the Year: Christy Wyatt’s focus on customers turned Absolute Software’s fortunes around
summary: Christy Wyatt's leadership as CEO of Absolute Software Corp. has led to a significant turnaround for the cybersecurity company. By focusing on customer needs and emphasizing the company's unique "self-healing" technology, she improved financial performance, customer acquisition, and market perception. Wyatt's strategic approach, stemming from a diverse background in tech, has positioned Absolute Software for continued growth, especially with the increased reliance on remote work.

title: Apple Takes Additional 116K SF at 11 Penn Plaza
summary: Apple has expanded its presence at 11 Penn Plaza, signing a six-year sublease with Macy’s for an additional 116,000 square feet. This new deal brings Apple's total footprint in the building to 336,000 square feet. The move comes after Apple lost out on space at the nearby Farley Post Office to Facebook and as Macy’s consolidates its offices to lower costs.

title: E-commerce wars: Which buy now, pay later service will become the next PayPal?
summary: The "buy now, pay later" (BNPL) market has surged during the pandemic, with apps like Afterpay, Affirm, Quadpay, and Klarna gaining significant traction as consumers seek flexible payment options. While PayPal currently dominates the BNPL space, backed by its "Pay in 4" installment option, a battle is brewing among global competitors for market share. Companies like Klarna, Afterpay, Affirm, and the rapidly growing Sezzle are vying to become the next dominant player, with each showcasing unique strengths and expansion strategies.

title: COVID turns Houston's Black Friday rush into slow trickle of in-person shoppers
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly reduced in-person Black Friday shopping in Houston, turning the usually chaotic day into a quiet affair with sparse crowds and empty parking lots. This shift was largely due to concerns about the virus and a massive increase in online shopping, accelerating a pre-existing trend of Black Friday's declining importance. Retailers observed that consumer habits may have permanently shifted towards online purchasing and extended discount periods, impacting the traditional holiday shopping season.

title: Do These 5 AI Chip Startups Pose a Threat to Nvidia?
summary: This article examines five prominent AI chip startups—Graphcore, SambaNova, Cerebras Systems, Groq, and Tenstorrent—that are developing hardware to challenge Nvidia's dominance in the AI chip market. These companies aim to offer superior performance, lower power consumption, and reduced costs in data centers and edge computing. While Nvidia remains the market leader, these startups' rapid commercialization and technological advancements could lead to acquisitions or fiercer competition in the coming years.

title: Apple Hires Prominent Venture Capitalist for App Store Role
summary: Apple Inc. has hired Josh Elman, a former venture capitalist from Greylock Partners, for a role rumored to be focused on the App Store. Elman, known for early investments in Discord and the precursor to TikTok, will concentrate on app discovery for customers and step back from his investing and corporate board positions. This move suggests Apple's continued focus on enhancing the App Store experience.

title: Cirrus Logic enters Apple’s Smart Watch world
summary: Cirrus Logic has entered Apple's Smart Watch supply chain, supplying the multichannel amplifier for the new Apple Watch Series 6, a role previously held by Dialog Semiconductor. The latest Apple Watch Series 6 features an elaborate pulse oximeter and heart rate sensor, alongside a UWB module with Apple's U1 chip, but sees the elimination of Force Touch technology. These findings come from a teardown report by System Plus Consulting, highlighting significant component changes in the new smartwatch.

title: Apple expands partnership with (RED) to combat HIV/AIDS and COVID-19
summary: Apple is expanding its 14-year partnership with (RED) to fight both HIV/AIDS and COVID-19. Through June 30, 2021, 100 percent of eligible proceeds from (PRODUCT)RED purchases will go to the Global Fund’s COVID-19 Response, assisting vulnerable communities and ensuring the continuity of HIV/AIDS services disrupted by the pandemic. The partnership has already raised almost $250 million, providing care, testing, and life-saving treatments to millions in sub-Saharan Africa.

title: COVID Tech: Silicon Valley Exodus? Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Moving From San Jose To Houston
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is relocating its company headquarters from San Jose, California, to the Houston area in Texas, a move announced by Texas Gov. Greg Abbott. This relocation, set for early 2022, is part of a trend of tech companies expanding operations outside Silicon Valley, driven by factors like lower housing costs and talent recruitment opportunities in Texas. HPE emphasized that it will maintain a significant presence in Silicon Valley, particularly for innovation and talent, and that no layoffs are associated with the move.

title: Amazon and Apple Are Powering a Shift Away From Intel’s Chips (Published 2020)
summary: Apple and Amazon are accelerating a shift away from Intel's chip technology by integrating their own Arm-based chips into their products. Apple recently launched Macs with its proprietary chips, while Amazon's cloud computing division is offering services powered by its Arm-based chips, boasting improved speed and cost-efficiency. This move signifies a potential redistribution of power within the computer industry, with major tech companies increasingly relying on their in-house chip designs.

title: Companies that support LGBTQ-inclusive policies outperform: Credit Suisse
summary: A Credit Suisse analysis highlights that companies supporting LGBTQ-inclusive policies have historically outperformed the broader market. These firms, including Apple and Tesla, attract and retain talent more easily, and show stronger revenue growth and cash flow. While not proving causation, the report indicates a strong correlation between LGBTQ inclusivity and better financial performance.

title: NBA® 2K Inks First of its Kind Partnership with Kevin Durant
summary: 2K has announced a unique partnership with NBA superstar Kevin Durant and Thirty Five Ventures, making Durant the icon athlete for Season 3 of NBA® 2K Mobile. This long-term collaboration will involve Durant and his team in future game development, exclusive content, and in-game merchandise. Additionally, Durant's charitable organization, the Kevin Durant Charity Foundation, will work with 2K Foundations on court renovations and programming for underprivileged youth.

title: Why Dimensional Fund Advisors is converting six of its mutual funds to the ETF format
summary: Dimensional Fund Advisors is converting six mutual funds to ETFs as part of an industry-first move, building on the success of its newly launched transparent, actively managed ETFs like DFAU and DFAI. The conversion aims to offer investors the benefits of indexing combined with active management, as requested by financial professionals, and capitalizes on the strong performance of value stocks.

title: Qualcomm taps Samsung to make new flagship 5G smartphone chips
summary: Qualcomm announced its new flagship 5G smartphone chip, the Snapdragon 888, which will be manufactured by Samsung Electronics. This chip features enhanced gaming and photo abilities and is expected to be in high-end Android phones from brands like Xiaomi and LG in Q1 next year. Samsung secured the manufacturing contract on its 5-nanometer process, a first for Qualcomm's flagship chip.

title: Judge throws out Trump rules limiting skilled-worker visas
summary: A federal judge in California has struck down two Trump administration rules that aimed to significantly reduce the number of H-1B visas issued to skilled foreign workers. The judge ruled that the government failed to follow proper transparency procedures and that its argument of an emergency response to COVID-19 job losses was unconvincing. This decision is seen as a significant relief for companies and current H-1B visa holders.

title: WWE® SuperCard Season 7 Introduces Three-Ring Chaos with New WarGames Event
summary: WWE SuperCard Season 7 is set to introduce a new WarGames event on December 3rd, bringing a unique three-ring, three-round solo battling experience. Players will strategize their card decks to control rings, earning points to win, and can play at their own pace for various rewards. To celebrate the launch, players can claim a free pack containing an Adam Cole "Cataclysm" card and other in-game items from December 3rd to December 10th.

title: FedEx’s ShopRunner acquisition provides access to e-commerce marketplace data
summary: FedEx announced its acquisition of ShopRunner, an e-commerce platform, to enhance its digital and logistics capabilities and gain access to valuable e-commerce marketplace data. The deal, subject to regulatory approval, is expected to close by the end of 2020. This acquisition will help FedEx better understand and serve the e-commerce market, especially important with the surge in online shopping due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Adobe Stock Looks Like a Buy Before Its Earnings Release
summary: Adobe stock has gained over 47% YTD and is considered a potential buy before its Q4 earnings release on Dec. 9. Despite a recent cool-down from its 52-week highs, the company's consistent revenue growth and attractive PEG ratio suggest it could be a long-term investment, especially if positive earnings and guidance are provided. Analysts anticipate a median target price of $560, indicating a 15.2% premium over current prices.

title: Exxon faces proxy fight launched by new activist firm Engine No. 1
summary: A new activist investment firm, Engine No. 1, backed by the California State Teachers' Retirement System (CalSTRS), is challenging energy giant Exxon Mobil Corp to pivot towards clean energy, improve financial performance, and refresh its board. Engine No. 1, launched by hedge fund veterans, plans to nominate four directors with clean energy expertise to Exxon's board, citing the company's underperformance and governance issues. This proxy fight is seen as a significant move that could unite traditional and climate activists, especially given Exxon's recent financial struggles and its decision not to raise its dividend.

title: Festive Beauty Brand Albums
summary: e.l.f. Cosmetics has released a holiday album titled "e.l.f. the Hauls" on Spotify, Apple Music, and Triller, featuring remixed holiday songs by up-and-coming artists. This initiative makes e.l.f. the first beauty brand to launch on Triller, with each track accompanied by a mini music video showcasing their products. The campaign leverages music and multiple social media platforms to engage audiences and promote its beauty line uniquely during the holiday season.

title: Qualcomm (Allegedly) Ships 176 Million 5G Modems To Apple – Becomes TSMC’s Largest 7nm Customer
summary: Qualcomm is reported to have become TSMC's largest 7nm customer due to massive orders from Apple for 5G modems. Estimates suggest that Qualcomm might have shipped as many as 176 million Snapdragon X55 modems to Apple for its 5G iPhones, significantly impacting both companies' market positions. This influx of orders positions Qualcomm as a key supplier for Apple's high-volume demand, especially during the holiday season and into the next year.

title: Apple announces over-the-ear AirPods Max headphones for $549
summary: Apple Inc. has announced its new over-the-ear headphones, named AirPods Max, priced at $549. These headphones are set to start shipping on December 15th, marking Apple's first entry into branded over-the-ear headphones, building on the success of its popular AirPods earbuds. This move follows Apple's acquisition of the Beats brand in 2014, through which it previously sold headphones.

title: Peloton shares briefly fall as Apple announces cheaper rival fitness service launches Dec. 14
summary: Apple announced its new digital fitness subscription service, Fitness+, will launch on December 14th at $9.99 per month, a lower price point than Peloton's base subscription. Following this news, Peloton's shares initially dropped by 2% in pre-market trading but recovered and saw a slight increase after markets opened. Apple's service integrates with its ecosystem, using Apple Watches for metrics and offering personalized recommendations, while Peloton emphasizes its hardware products like bikes and treadmills.

title: Victoria’s Secret Names Martha Pease Chief Marketing Officer
summary: Victoria’s Secret has appointed Martha Pease as its new chief marketing officer, effective January 2021. This move is part of parent company L Brands' ongoing efforts to revitalize the lingerie and beauty brand, which has seen a decline in market share. Pease's role will focus on repositioning the brand for "relevance with modern women" and accelerating its digital performance through customer insight and data analytics.

title: Apple Launches $550 Over-Ear Headphones Into Crowded Market
summary: Apple has launched its first over-ear headphones, named AirPods Max, priced at $550. These headphones feature wireless pairing, noise cancellation, and a 20-hour battery life, entering a competitive market currently dominated by Bose and Sony. This move represents Apple's continued expansion into accessories to boost revenue beyond its core iPhone sales.

title: Adobe Lightroom CC Adds Native Support For Apple M1 And Windows 10 On Arm Devices
summary: Adobe has released native Arm64 versions of Lightroom CC (version 4.1) for systems running Apple M1 (macOS Big Sur) and Qualcomm Snapdragon 8cx (Windows 10 on Arm) processors. This update aims to deliver optimized performance on these platforms, as Adobe continues to retool its Creative Suite for Arm-based architecture. Existing x86-64 versions of Lightroom will still function via Rosetta 2 emulation on Apple M1 Macs.

title: Samsung Electronics buys land in Austin, eyes foundry expansion
summary: Samsung Electronics has purchased a large plot of land in Austin, Texas, near its existing chip plant, fueling speculation about an expansion of its foundry business. This move comes as its rival TSMC also plans a significant expansion in the US. Industry observers believe Samsung aims to boost its contract-manufacturing operations to compete more effectively with TSMC in the global chipmaking sector.

title: North Phoenix land auctioned Wednesday will become massive international semiconductor company factory
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has successfully bid $89 million for 1,129 acres of undeveloped state land in north Phoenix, where it plans to build a multi-billion dollar semiconductor factory. This acquisition makes public the exact location for the advanced chip factory, which is expected to open by 2024, employ 1,600 workers, and significantly boost the region's economy and advanced manufacturing industry. The city of Phoenix is committing $205 million for infrastructure development to support the site, while federal assistance programs, like Chips for America, are still under consideration but eyed as crucial for the project's long-term success.
authors: Jen Fifield|Russ Wiles|Lorraine Longhi|Uriel J. Garcia

title: Zimmer Biomet and Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS) Partner to Advance Innovation on mymobility® with Apple Watch® Remote Care Management System
summary: Zimmer Biomet and Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS) have announced an exclusive partnership to integrate HSS Orthopedic Care Pathways into Zimmer Biomet's mymobility® with Apple Watch® remote care management platform. This collaboration will allow healthcare systems and clinicians worldwide to access HSS's orthopedic expertise, leveraging Apple Watch's sensors for patient activity monitoring and virtual connections between patients and doctors. The goal is to enhance remote patient care, optimize pre- and post-surgical protocols, and lower overall care costs by making HSS's validated protocols globally accessible.

title: Adobe Flash Player Receives Final Software Update As It Walks Death Row
summary: Adobe has released its final software update for Flash Player, marking the end of its support after December 31, 2020. All Flash content will be blocked from running starting January 12, 2021, and Adobe strongly recommends users uninstall the player due to its long history of security vulnerabilities. This phase-out comes over a decade after Steve Jobs famously criticized Flash for its proprietary nature, security flaws, and performance issues.

title: Apple, TSMC rumored to be working on chips for a Tesla-like 'Apple Car'
summary: Apple is reportedly collaborating with TSMC to develop chips for its rumored "Apple Car," which is said to be similar to a Tesla. The companies are purportedly planning a U.S. factory for these chips, though DigiTimes, the source of this information, has a mixed track record. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo previously forecast an "Apple Car" debut between 2023 and 2025, possibly sourcing chips from TSMC.

title: Hyatt CEO Mark Hoplamazian named Board of Directors Chair at American Hotel & Lodging Association (AHLA)
summary: Mark Hoplamazian, President and CEO of Hyatt Hotels Corporation, has been named Chair of the Board of Directors at the American Hotel & Lodging Association (AHLA). The AHLA also announced its 2021 officers and executive committee, highlighting a diverse group of industry leaders. Justin Knight was named vice chair, Leslie D. Hale as secretary/treasurer, and Greg Juceam continues as chair of the AHLA Foundation Board of Trustees.

title: Sonos One SL review
summary: The Sonos One SL delivers impressive sonic performance, streaming options, and app support, making it a strong contender in the wireless speaker market. It allows for stereo pairing with a Sonos One, providing a cost-effective solution for rooms needing voice control without buying two smart speakers. Despite lacking Bluetooth, its sound quality, Trueplay optimization, and AirPlay 2 integration make it a compelling choice for multi-room audio or standalone use.

title: Broadcom names Kirsten Spears as CFO in leadership shakeup
summary: Broadcom Inc. has appointed Kirsten Spears as its new Chief Financial Officer, replacing Tom Krause, who will now lead the Infrastructure Software Group. The company also forecast first-quarter revenue above estimates, driven by strong demand for 5G devices from key client Apple, despite warnings of soft demand from enterprise clients due to COVID-19 related supply disruptions. Broadcom's shares dipped initially in extended trading following the announcement.

title: Canada Gets Overhauled Apple Maps Data
summary: Apple has completed its overhaul of Maps data for Canada, following a similar update in the US earlier this year. Canadian Apple Maps users will now experience more detailed maps, improved directions, electric car routing, speed camera warnings, and guides. The "Look Around" feature has also been implemented in several major Canadian cities and regions.

title: Broadcom names Kirsten Spears as CFO
summary: Broadcom Inc. announced the appointment of Kirsten Spears as its new chief financial officer, effective immediately, replacing Tom Krause. The company also forecast first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, driven by anticipated higher demand from Apple Inc. due to the rollout of 5G technology. Shares of Broadcom were down nearly 1% in extended trading following the announcement.

title: Oracle is the latest Silicon Valley company to head for the hills — of Austin
summary: Oracle Corp. announced its decision to move its corporate headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas, following a trend of companies and executives relocating from Silicon Valley. This move aligns with a new flexible employee work location policy and positions Oracle for growth in a business-friendly state. Governor Greg Abbott lauded the relocation, emphasizing Texas's appeal for business, jobs, and opportunity, with other tech giants like Tesla and HP Enterprise also making similar moves or expanding in the state.

title: Life Time Announces It will be the First Health & Fitness Club Company to Offer Apple Fitness+ to Its Members
summary: Life Time, a premier health and wellness brand, announced it would be the first U.S. health & fitness club company to offer Apple Fitness+ as part of its memberships. This integration, starting later in December 2020, complements Life Time's existing training programs and expands its digital and omnichannel wellness offerings. Apple Fitness+ provides studio-style workouts via Apple devices, intelligently incorporating metrics from Apple Watch.

title: Apple probes supplier after workers at Wistron plant in India rampage
summary: Apple is investigating its supplier Wistron Corp after workers at an iPhone manufacturing plant in India rampaged over unpaid wages, causing an estimated $60 million in damages. Thousands of contract workers protested, leading to violence where equipment and vehicles were vandalized. Apple has sent teams to investigate and Wistron, a major Apple supplier, stated it abides by the law and is cooperating with authorities.

title: I tried out Fitness+, Apple’s polished new exercise subscription
summary: The author reviews Apple's new Fitness+ connected workout service, highlighting its polished design, rich content library, and seamless integration with Apple Watch. Despite being impressed with its technical and design aspects, the author notes that some "For Beginners" workouts might not fully cater to complete novices in certain exercise types. Overall, Fitness+ is presented as a valuable option for at-home exercise, especially for existing Apple Watch users.

title: UnitedHealthcare Introduces Enhancements for Wearable Device Well-being Program, Including Access to Apple Fitness+ at No Additional Cost
summary: UnitedHealthcare has enhanced its Motion wearable device well-being program, offering eligible members at least six months of Apple Fitness+ at no additional cost starting in 2021. The program also introduces new ways to earn financial rewards for various physical activities beyond walking, including cycling and elliptical use, with the goal of making activity goals more accessible. These enhancements aim to provide digital resources and financial incentives to help members improve their health and well-being.

title: Washington Post Team Wins Hinrich Trade Reporting Award
summary: A team from The Washington Post—David J. Lynch, Carol Leonnig, Josh Dawsey, and Jeff Stein—has been awarded the 2020 Hinrich Foundation Award for Distinguished Reporting on Trade. Their winning submissions illuminated the intricacies of doing business with China amidst the coronavirus pandemic and detailed the impact of key U.S. trade figures on policy. The stories included an in-depth look at Flex Ltd.'s supply chain adaptation, a profile of trade adviser Peter Navarro, and a scoop on delayed trade decisions affecting Ukraine.

title: FedEx, UPS are managing surge in packages with strict limits on shippers
summary: FedEx and UPS are implementing strict package limits on shippers to manage the record surge in holiday package volumes, leading to backlogs for retailers like Costco. This strategy helps the carriers maintain strong on-time delivery scores despite the high demand and pandemic-related challenges, though it frustrates businesses unable to ship all their inventory. Small and medium-sized businesses have been particularly affected, with some experiencing sudden cutoffs and significant delays.

title: Apple (AAPL) iPhone Subcontractor Rocked by Worker Riot
summary: A worker riot occurred at an iPhone assembly plant in India owned by Apple subcontractor Wistron, sparked by grievances over unpaid wages and working hours. The incident caused significant damage and led to arrests, impacting Apple's supply chain and potential production targets for 2021. This event highlights the risks and challenges of outsourcing production for cost-cutting to subcontractors.

title: Apple Inc. stock rises Thursday, still underperforms market
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock increased by 0.70% on Thursday to $128.70, during a generally favorable trading session for the market where the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also rose. Despite this, Apple's stock closed below its 52-week high but outperformed some competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet. Trading volume was lower than its 50-day average.

title: Yext CEO Howard Lerman returns to the OMR Podcast
summary: Yext CEO Howard Lerman returned to the OMR Podcast to discuss the current search landscape and the importance for companies to provide search alternatives to Google. Lerman emphasizes that brands need to optimize their websites for search to keep customers from going to Google and finding competitors, highlighting Yext's mission to revolutionize company search functions. He also discusses the company's $2 billion valuation.

title: Here Are Barron’s 10 Top Stocks for the New Year
summary: Barron's has released its list of 10 top stock picks for the upcoming year, as the S&P 500 expects a 17% return by the end of a remarkable year. Investors are optimistic about 2021, anticipating the end of the pandemic and significant improvements in corporate earnings. The article highlights key CEOs whose companies are included in these picks.

title: Chocolate Pepsi? Brand Announces ‘Cocoa' Cola Flavor
summary: Pepsi has announced a new "Cocoa" Cola flavor that blends the taste of cola with cocoa and a hint of marshmallow, available as a limited edition. To unlock the flavor's release, Pepsi requires its announcement tweet to reach 2,021 retweets. This innovative flavor aims to bring unexpected joy during an atypical year.

title: How to find photos on your phone? Here's how I do it.
summary: This article provides practical advice on organizing and finding photos on a smartphone, addressing a common issue for many users. The author shares his personal method, which involves using professional tools like Adobe Lightroom and various cloud storage services including Amazon Photos and SmugMug. It also highlights how others, like Kristin Metcalfe, leverage built-in features such as Apple's "Places" tool, and discusses the evolving landscape of photo storage services with Google Photos' upcoming changes.

title: Tesla shares jump to record high ahead of S&P 500 debut
summary: Shares of Tesla Inc. surged to a record high on Friday, driven by anticipation of its inclusion in the S&P 500 index. The electric carmaker, led by Elon Musk, will become the most valuable company ever admitted to the benchmark, accounting for over 1% of its value. This event prompted index-tracking funds to buy an estimated $85 billion worth of Tesla shares, influencing significant trading volume and price movements.

title: Apple to Suspend New Orders to Wistron After India Workers Riot
summary: Apple Inc. has placed its Indian supplier Wistron Corp. on probation and will suspend new orders following labor practice lapses that led to worker riots. Preliminary investigations revealed that Wistron, the first Apple supplier to produce iPhones in India, failed to properly manage working hours, resulting in payment delays for some employees in October and November. Apple will withhold new orders until these issues are resolved.

title: Now in Beta: Premiere Pro, Premiere Rush, and Audition for Apple M1 Systems
summary: Adobe has released public Beta versions of Premiere Pro, Premiere Rush, and Audition with native support for Apple's new M1 chipsets, aiming for improved performance and energy efficiency. These initial Beta builds include core editing functions and support for widely used codecs, with a phased approach planned for full M1 integration due to the large codebase.
