Full native support is expected in the first half of 2021, while users can currently run existing versions via Rosetta 2 emulation.

title: Tesla stock slides on first day in S&P 500
summary: Tesla's shares fell over 6% on its first day in the S&P 500, influenced by concerns about a new Covid-19 variant, disappointment over a stimulus deal, and news of Apple's self-driving car plans. The drop followed a significant pre-inclusion surge, with analysts noting similar post-inclusion declines for other high-profile S&P 500 additions. Analysts also voiced concerns about the stock's valuation and increasing competition in the EV market.

title: Rare-Earth Elements: Will China Continue Its Market Dominance?
summary: China currently dominates the global market for rare-earth elements, essential for various high-tech and military applications, leveraging decades of strategic investment. The article explores whether recent US efforts and alliances with countries like Japan and Australia can challenge China's market dominance, particularly given China's "Made in China 2025" initiative and temporary pandemic-induced price fluctuations. Despite challenges, rare-earth prices are expected to recover, and investment opportunities exist through ETFs like VanEck Vectors Rare Earth/Strategic Metals ETF (NYSEArca: REMX).

title: Buyer or Seller? How Each Film Studio Tackled Dealmaking Amid the Pandemic
summary: This article examines how various film studios adapted their dealmaking strategies during the 2020 pandemic, which caused significant disruptions like production halts, theatrical window changes, and a shift towards streaming. It details key moves made by major studios such as Amazon, Apple, Disney, Lionsgate, MGM, Netflix, Paramount, Sony, Universal, and Warner Bros., including acquisitions, production adjustments, and executive changes. The report highlights each studio's unique approach to navigating the unprecedented challenges and opportunities presented by the altered entertainment landscape.

title: Exclusive: Apple targets car production by 2024 and eyes 'next level' battery technology - sources
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly advancing its self-driving car technology with a target to produce a passenger vehicle by 2024, featuring its own groundbreaking battery design. This initiative, known as Project Titan, aims for a mass-market personal vehicle, contrasting with rivals' focus on robo-taxis. The company's innovative "monocell" battery design and use of LFP chemistry are expected to significantly reduce costs and increase range, though challenges in manufacturing and potential pandemic-related delays could push production to 2025 or later.
authors: Stephen Nellis|Norihiko Shirouzu and Paul Lienert

title: Walmart Sued, Apple Car, Riot Blockchain Jumps 32%
summary: The Justice Department is suing Walmart for allegedly contributing to the opioid crisis, causing a slight dip in Walmart's stock. Meanwhile, Riot Blockchain's stock surged over 32% after announcing a massive purchase of new mining machines, and Apple's stock rose on renewed speculation about its potential entry into the automotive industry with an "Apple Car." The stock market overall experienced a slight downturn despite the signing of a new pandemic relief package.

title: Apple en route to face-off with Tesla
summary: Apple is reportedly accelerating its plans for an Apple-branded electric vehicle, potentially launching as early as next September or with a 2024 timeframe. This move positions Apple to directly challenge Tesla, leveraging its expertise in supply chain management and manufacturing. The article suggests that while Tesla excels in design, Apple's strength lies in consistent product delivery and manufacturing, indicating that the competition will ultimately be decided on the factory floor.

title: Two Electrical and Computer Engineering students land big jobs with giant companies
summary: Two Oklahoma State University Electrical and Computer Engineering Ph.D. students, Hisham Abuella and Amit Kachroo, have secured significant jobs with major companies after graduating in spring 2021. Abuella will join Qualcomm Inc. as a senior engineer on the Modem System team, while Kachroo will be an Applied Scientist II at Amazon Lab126. Both students conducted their research in the OSU Wireless Lab under Dr. Sabit Ekin and had prior summer internships with their respective companies.

title: Elon Musk says had once reached out to Apple for acquiring Tesla
summary: Elon Musk revealed he tried to discuss a potential acquisition of Tesla by Apple during Tesla's "darkest days of the Model 3 program," but Apple CEO Tim Cook refused the meeting. This happened as Apple is reportedly looking to produce a passenger vehicle by 2024 with new battery technology, which Musk commented on. Tesla has since overcome its production issues and recently joined the S&P 500.

title: Alibaba's Only Stock Split Was Out of the Ordinary
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. executed an eight-for-one stock split on July 15, 2019, to increase the number of available shares and raise capital for growth. This split coincided with a secondary listing on a Hong Kong exchange, which diluted shares but helped Alibaba secure the largest IPO listing of 2019. Despite its significance, the secondary listing caused the stock split to go largely unnoticed by American investors.

title: Apple Inc's Contract Manufacturer Salcomp Bets Big On India: Acquires Fourth Factory In Nokia SEZ In Chennai
summary: Apple Inc.'s contract manufacturer Salcomp has acquired its fourth factory in Chennai's Nokia SEZ, a 30,000 square meter facility from Jabil that had been idle for a decade. This expansion will allow Salcomp to manufacture electronic and electromechanical components for smartphones and other gadgets, including new green energy products. The company, which has invested significantly in Tamil Nadu since 2019, expects to increase its workforce from over 8,000 to 12,000-13,000 by mid-next year.

title: Time Magazine to be sold to Salesforce Billionaire Marc Benioff
summary: Marc Benioff, co-founder of Salesforce, and his wife Lynne, are purchasing Time magazine from Meredith Corp. for $190 million in cash. Benioff becomes the latest tech billionaire to acquire an iconic media brand, emphasizing journalistic integrity and a desire to preserve the publication's legacy. The Benioffs intend to keep Time's editorial independence and help it transition into the digital world while maintaining its print foundation.

title: Tim Cook's Response to Facebook's Attack Is the Best Example of Emotional Intelligence I've Ever Seen
summary: This article praises Tim Cook's response to Facebook's public attack on Apple's privacy changes in iOS 14, highlighting it as an exemplary demonstration of emotional intelligence. Cook's concise and measured 47-word statement effectively clarified Apple's position without engaging in emotional or argumentative language, contrasting sharply with Facebook's extensive advertising campaign. The author argues that Cook's approach serves as a perfect model for leaders facing public criticism.

title: These married former Apple execs used lessons learned from Steve Jobs to pivot their small business during Covid
summary: Former Apple executives Jim Bean and Christine O'Sullivan, owners of Brand winery in Napa Valley, successfully pivoted their business during the Covid-19 pandemic by drawing on lessons learned from Steve Jobs. They launched virtual wine tastings for executives and a direct-to-consumer sales program, allowing them to retain employees and build customer momentum despite the challenging year. Their strategy emphasized focus and innovation, inspired by Apple's approach during past economic downturns.

title: Intel shares rise after Third Point urges chipmaker to explore strategic alternatives as market share deteriorates
summary: Hedge fund Third Point, which recently acquired a significant stake in Intel, is urging the chipmaker's board to explore strategic alternatives due to deteriorating market share against rivals like TSMC, Samsung, and AMD. Third Point highlighted concerns about Intel's loss of manufacturing leadership and its impact on U.S. national security. Intel has stated it welcomes input from investors to enhance shareholder value.

title: Exclusive: Hedge fund Third Point urges Intel to explore deal options
summary: Activist hedge fund Third Point LLC, which has amassed a nearly $1 billion stake in Intel Corp, is urging the U.S. chipmaker to explore strategic alternatives, including whether it should keep chip design and production under one roof. Third Point CEO Daniel Loeb sent a letter to Intel Chairman Omar Ishrak, calling for immediate action to boost the company's position, address "human capital management issue," and consider divesting failed acquisitions. This push comes as Intel has lost market share to competitors like TSMC, Samsung, and AMD, and its stock has underperformed the Nasdaq Composite Index.

title: With or Without Apple Partnership, Nio Is Still a New Year Buy
summary: Despite missing out on Nio's earlier rally in 2020, the author remains bullish on NIO stock for 2021, expecting it to be a long-term bargain even at $48 per share. The article discusses a potential partnership between Apple and Nio for an upcoming EV, but argues that Nio's significant production growth and diversification of models mean it will thrive with or without Apple. Nio is projected to increase its vehicle offerings, launch new batteries, and achieve substantial annual production goals, solidifying its position in the premium EV market.

title: Apple loses copyright claims in lawsuit against U.S. security bug startup
summary: A federal judge in Florida dismissed Apple Inc.'s copyright infringement claims against Corellium LLC, a startup whose software helps security researchers find vulnerabilities in Apple products. The judge ruled Corellium's software, which emulates iOS, amounted to "fair use" as it was "transformative" and served a public benefit by aiding in the discovery of security flaws. Apple may still pursue a separate federal law claim regarding circumvention of security measures.

title: Today Adobe Flash Player reached the end of life (EOL)
summary: Adobe Flash Player officially reached its end of life (EOL) on January 1, 2021, ceasing to receive updates and support. Adobe strongly recommended users uninstall the software due to past vulnerabilities and the company blocking Flash content from running after January 12, 2021. The move was a culmination of a 2017 announcement by major tech companies to phase out Flash in favor of more secure and performant alternatives like HTML5.

title: Aeva, Inc. and InterPrivate Acquisition Corp. Announce $200 Million Investment from Sylebra Capital in Support of Business Combination
summary: Aeva, Inc. and InterPrivate Acquisition Corp. announced a $200 million investment from Sylebra Capital to support their business combination. This follow-on PIPE investment significantly exceeds the minimum cash requirement, paving the way for the transaction's completion in Q1 2021. The funding is expected to accelerate Aeva's expansion in automotive, consumer, and industrial markets with its 4D LiDAR sensing technology.

title: Lidar Startup Aeva Adds $200 Million to SPAC Deal
summary: Aeva, a lidar sensor startup, is set to increase its financing by $200 million through an investment from Hong Kong-based hedge fund Sylebra Capital Management, boosting its total proceeds to $563 million ahead of its public listing via a reverse merger with InterPrivate Acquisition Corp. This investment highlights growing investor interest in next-generation auto-related companies focusing on electric and driverless technology. Aeva's technology, which is applicable beyond automotive to consumer devices and industrial robots, is valued at approximately $3 billion and aims to use the additional funding to meet demand and accelerate technological advancements.

title: Google employees form union in rare move for a US tech giant
summary: Employees of Google and its parent company, Alphabet, have formed a new union called the Alphabet Workers Union, in a rare move for a major US technology company. The union, supported by the Communications Workers of America (CWA), aims to address issues like compensation, employee classification, and the types of work Google undertakes. This initiative follows years of workplace confrontations and dismissals of employee concerns by executives.

title: Apple rumored to adopt GaN for smaller charger designs
summary: Apple is rumored to be planning smaller and lighter USB-C wall chargers using gallium nitride (GaN) technology, with Navitas Semiconductor expected to receive orders. This adoption would allow Apple to produce chargers similar in size and power to those offered by third-party manufacturers, streamlining its charging lineup and potentially offering features like dual-port designs and faster performance. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo previously predicted new chargers from Apple in 2021, suggesting these could incorporate GaN technology.

title: Discovery Plus Launches on Multiple Platforms, Including Roku and Amazon Fire TV
summary: Discovery Plus has officially launched in the U.S. and is now available on a wide array of platforms, including Roku, Amazon Fire TV, Apple devices, Google devices, Microsoft Xbox, and Samsung Smart TVs. This broad availability is part of Discovery's strategy to make its real-life entertainment content accessible everywhere, leveraging strong partnerships with leading distributors. The launch follows deals with Vodafone, Verizon, and Sky, expanding its reach across the U.S., U.K., and European territories.

title: Why Apple became a $2 trillion company in 2020, and what could come next
summary: Apple's market valuation soared to over $2 trillion in 2020, largely due to a significant re-rating of its services business by investors, which now accounts for roughly half of its total value. While the iPhone remains crucial, the company faces challenges with its streaming content, risks to its Google search licensing revenue, and an undefined strategy for the EV market. Analysts are optimistic about continued growth, but some caution about the stock's valuation, given the service's growth rate and potential threats to key revenue streams like the Google licensing deal and App Store commissions.

title: Apple Veterans’ Lidar Startup Adds $200 Million to SPAC Deal
summary: Aeva, a lidar startup founded by former Apple engineers, is set to receive an additional $200 million investment from Sylebra Capital Management, boosting its total proceeds from a SPAC deal to $563 million. This funding increases Aeva's war chest ahead of its public listing via a reverse merger with InterPrivate Acquisition Corp., highlighting strong investor interest in next-generation auto-related technologies.

title: The TDVG ETF Is a Stellar Choice for Dividend Growth
summary: The TDVG ETF is highlighted as a strong option for investors seeking dividend growth in 2021, given forecasts for increased S&P 500 payouts. This actively managed ETF focuses on companies with a history and expectation of increasing dividends, offering a compelling strategy in a low-interest-rate environment. Companies are showing renewed confidence in dividend growth, further supporting the investment thesis for TDVG.

title: Google's iOS apps haven't been updated in weeks. Could Apple's privacy labels be the reason?
summary: Not a single one of Google's iOS apps has been updated in almost a month, a period considered unusually long for a major tech company. This lack of updates coincides with Apple's new mandate, which started on December 8, requiring developers to publish privacy labels for any new apps or updates submitted to the App Store. It appears that by not updating its apps since December 7, Google has avoided providing these privacy labels for its iOS applications, as indicated by the "No Details Provided" message on their App Store listings.

title: Apple car: Who will build it? Magna Steyr possible candidate
summary: This article speculates on potential manufacturers for the rumored Apple Car, with Magna Steyr identified as a possible candidate due to its contract manufacturing expertise. It also briefly mentions other automotive industry news.

title: Adobe Flash Player is officially dead. Here’s how to uninstall it
summary: Adobe Flash Player has officially reached its "end-of-life" on December 31, after years of declining usage due to security vulnerabilities and the rise of open web standards like HTML5. Adobe is urging users to uninstall the software immediately to protect their systems, as it will no longer receive security updates. The article provides brief instructions on how to remove Flash Player from both Windows and Mac operating systems.

title: Chip giant Qualcomm names President Amon as new CEO
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has named its president and chip division head, Cristiano Amon, as its new chief executive, replacing Steven Mollenkopf, effective June 30. Amon has been with the company since 1995 and became president in 2018, overseeing the chip division and guiding the company's expansion into new areas like 5G infrastructure, automotive computers, and personal computers. Mollenkopf, a 26-year veteran, successfully navigated Qualcomm through significant crises including a hostile takeover attempt, an antitrust challenge, and a legal battle with Apple.

title: Cable One Preps IP-Based Sparklight TV
summary: Sparklight, a subsidiary of Cable One, is set to launch Sparklight TV, an IP-based TV service, in early 2021. This transition to IPTV aims to reclaim spectrum, freeing up network capacity to enhance Gigabit offerings and establish a 10G technology platform. The service will be accessible via streaming apps on platforms like Amazon Fire TV, Android TV, and Apple TV.

title: Qualcomm Chief Executive Steve Mollenkopf to Step Down
summary: Qualcomm announced that CEO Steve Mollenkopf will step down in June, after leading the company through a period of significant growth in smartphone sales despite legal battles with Apple. Cristiano Amon, the current president, will succeed Mollenkopf and aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for 5G phones. Mollenkopf will remain an adviser for a period after the transition.

title: Nvidia takeover of chip designer Arm investigated
summary: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has launched an investigation into Nvidia's proposed $40bn takeover of British chip designer Arm Holdings. The CMA will examine the merger's potential impact on competition, particularly concerning Arm's intellectual property licensing services to Nvidia's rivals. This marks a significant move by the CMA, now handling large competition cases post-Brexit, and aligns with broader international regulatory scrutiny of the deal.

title: Interested In Apple But Not Its Volatility? 2 ETFs To Preserve Stability
summary: This article suggests two ETFs for investors interested in Apple's potential returns but wary of its volatility. It highlights the iShares Russell Top 200 Growth ETF (IWY) and the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC) as ways to gain exposure to Apple and other growth/tech companies while potentially mitigating short-term price swings. The article details the holdings, performance, and financial metrics of both ETFs.

title: Qualcomm CEO Steve Mollenkopf to retire; Cristiano Amon will replace him
summary: Qualcomm CEO Steve Mollenkopf will retire in June 2021 after 26 years with the company, with company President Cristiano Amon set to succeed him. Mollenkopf led Qualcomm through significant challenges, including legal battles and a hostile takeover attempt, while Amon is tasked with leading the company through the 5G transition and expanding into new markets beyond smartphones. Amon, who has been with Qualcomm since 1995, is expected to continue the company's focus on product roadmap and leverage 5G opportunities.

title: Rock star chip designer Jim Keller joins Tenstorrent
summary: Legendary computer chip designer Jim Keller has joined Toronto startup Tenstorrent Inc. as its new president and chief technology officer. Keller, known for his work at AMD, Apple, and Tesla, will guide Tenstorrent in its mission to create a machine learning training chip that utilizes software to optimize computing power and increase efficiency, challenging established players like Intel, AMD, and Nvidia.

title: Stock Option Volume ‘Exploded’ in 2020. What Comes Next.
summary: In 2020, stock option volume saw a significant surge, largely driven by individual investors entering the market. These investors showed a strong preference for popular stocks such as Apple, Tesla, and NIO, a Chinese electric vehicle company. The article discusses this explosive growth in option trading and anticipates future trends.

title: Device-Based Hybrids Solution for Mobile Network Operators in Canada
summary: Comtech discusses its perspective on the Emergency Services Working Group's (ESWG) recommendations for implementing device-based hybrid location solutions for Canadian Mobile Network Operators (MNOs). The article highlights the importance of combining handset-based and network-based location data to provide the most accurate locations for 911 calls, especially in indoor environments. Comtech's solution involves AML End Point functionality and enhanced Location Platforms to integrate and compare various location results for optimal accuracy and cybersecurity.

title: The Walt Disney Company
summary: This page from Newsweek compiles various news articles related to The Walt Disney Company. It covers topics ranging from leadership changes, theme park updates, streaming content, financial performance, and political disputes primarily with Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, showcasing a wide array of company-related news.

title: This gesture-sensing band could make the Apple Watch more accessible
summary: The Mudra Band is a CES 2021 Innovation Award honoree that uses gesture-sensing technology to make the Apple Watch more accessible, particularly for individuals with physical and cognitive disabilities. It detects electrical signals from the brain to the fingers, mapping them to corresponding movements, and allows users to control the Apple Watch without needing to touch the screen. The company, Wearable Devices, is also exploring accessibility use cases with universities, such as enabling quick typing for ALS patients and helping an IDF veteran learn to play the piano.

title: Development Site Near Culver City Sells for $92 Million
summary: A 3.5-acre mixed-use development site near Culver City was sold by Public Storage for $92 million to Australia-based companies Lendlease Corp. and Aware Super. The site is intended for a 500,000-square-foot project, including multifamily units, creative office space, and retail, with an estimated value of $600 million upon completion. The location benefits from its proximity to major tech and entertainment companies and its designation as an opportunity zone provides tax benefits.

title: Biogen to Launch Pioneering Study to Develop Digital Biomarkers of Cognitive Health Using Apple Watch and iPhone
summary: Biogen announced a multi-year virtual research study in collaboration with Apple to investigate the use of Apple Watch and iPhone in monitoring cognitive performance and screening for decline, including mild cognitive impairment (MCI). The study aims to develop digital biomarkers to help detect early signs of MCI, which affects 15-20% of adults over 65. This initiative leverages Apple's technology and Biogen's neuroscience expertise to accelerate patient diagnoses, empower individuals, and potentially lower healthcare costs.

title: Going Rogue- a Mastermind behind Android Malware Returns with a New RAT
summary: Check Point Research (CPR) has uncovered the activities of a prolific Android malware developer named "Triangulum," who, in collaboration with "HeXaGoN Dev," has released a new Remote Access Trojan (RAT) called "Rogue." Rogue is a sophisticated MRAT that combines elements of previously sold malware, DarkShades and Hawkshaw, and uses Firebase for its command and control infrastructure to exfiltrate vast amounts of sensitive user data and control infected devices. Despite a leak of the Rogue package and previous marketing failures in the Russian darknet, Triangulum continues to operate and expand his customer network, highlighting the persistent threat posed by agile mobile malware development and sales on the dark web.
authors: Aviran Hazum|Alex Shamshur|Raman Ladutska|Ohad Mana|Israel Wernik

title: IBM received the most patents in 2020. Here’s the rest of the top 20
summary: IBM maintained its leading position as the top recipient of U.S. patents in 2020 with 9,130 inventions, continuing a trend despite a slight national decrease in issued patents. The article highlights the rise of patents in machine learning, quantum computing, and surprisingly, "electronic smoking devices," along with "computer systems based on biological models." It also touches upon the ongoing controversy surrounding patent validity and "patent trolls."

title: Explainer: What is Parler and why has it been pulled offline?
summary: Parler, a social media network founded in 2018 as a "free speech-driven" space, has been largely adopted by U.S. conservatives. It was taken offline after Amazon suspended its web hosting service, following similar bans by Apple and Google. These companies cited Parler's failure to prevent the spread of posts inciting violence, particularly after the U.S. Capitol unrest, leading Parler to sue Amazon.

title: Apple to open developer school in Detroit, the first program of its kind in the US
summary: Apple Inc. will open its first U.S. "Apple Developer Academy" in Detroit later this year as part of its $100 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. The program, a collaboration with Michigan State University, aims to train Black entrepreneurs and coders for free. This initiative follows social justice movements after the killings of Breonna Taylor and George Floyd.

title: Qualcomm to acquire NUVIA for $1.4 billion – A major shift for the CPU market
summary: Qualcomm has announced its acquisition of NUVIA for $1.4 billion, a move that will allow Qualcomm to re-enter the custom CPU market with a highly experienced design team. NUVIA was founded by veterans from companies like Apple and AMD and aims to develop high-performance ARM CPU cores for various devices, including laptops and servers, potentially challenging Intel and AMD. This acquisition signals a significant strategic shift for Qualcomm, though the impact of NUVIA's technology on consumer products is still years away.

title: Qualcomm scoops up Apple vets' Santa Clara chip startup Nuvia for $1.4B
summary: Qualcomm has acquired Santa Clara-based chip startup Nuvia, founded by former Apple veterans John Bruno, Gerard Williams III, and Manu Gulati, for $1.4 billion. This acquisition price represents a 159% premium over Nuvia's valuation during its September funding round. The deal highlights Qualcomm's strategic move in chip technology.

title: Southern Co., Apple launching 'Propel Center' incubator in Atlanta for HBCU students
summary: Southern Company and Apple are partnering to launch the "Propel Center," a new HBCU incubator located in Atlanta's Atlanta University Center. Southern Company will be the energy partner, focusing on sustainable and environmentally-friendly construction. The initiative aims to support students from Clark Atlanta University, Morehouse College, Morehouse School of Medicine, Morris Brown, and Spelman College.

title: Alabama A&M Partners With Apple's Innovative Propel Center
summary: Alabama A&M University is partnering with the Propel Center, a new global campus in Atlanta supported by Apple, Southern Company, and Ed Farm. This initiative, part of Apple's $100 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative, aims to provide HBCU students and faculty with access to innovative curricula, technology, career opportunities, and fellowship programs. The Propel Center will offer educational tracks in AI, machine learning, agricultural technologies, social justice, app development, and creative arts, with expert mentorship and internship opportunities from Apple.

title: Intel CEO Bob Swan Is Replaced by VMware’s Pat Gelsinger
summary: Intel CEO Bob Swan is being replaced by VMware CEO Pat Gelsinger, who is returning to Intel to address the company's challenges in the chip industry. Gelsinger's appointment is seen as a move to bring back technical leadership and address manufacturing delays that have plagued Intel, causing it to lag behind competitors. The change in leadership was welcomed by investors and analysts who believe Gelsinger is the best option to lead Intel through its critical transformation period.

title: Southern Company joins Apple in announcing launch of new digital learning hub, global headquarters for innovation for HBCUs
summary: Southern Company and Apple have partnered to launch the Propel Center, a digital learning hub and innovation headquarters for Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in Atlanta, Georgia. Both companies are contributing $25 million each to establish the center, which aims to foster Black entrepreneurship, provide technology resources, and prepare HBCU students for future careers. The initiative builds on previous collaborations to advance educational equity and address underrepresentation in STEM fields.

title: Apple announces latest efforts in its $100 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative
summary: Apple has announced a new set of major donations as part of its $100 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. This includes a $25 million donation to the Propel Center, a learning hub for Historically Black Colleges and Universities, and the launch of the Apple Developer Academy in Detroit. The company will also provide venture capital funding for Black and Brown founders with investments in Harlem Capital and Siebert Williams Shank's Clear Vision Impact Fund.

title: Qualcomm eyes challenge to Apple, Intel with $1.4 billion deal for chip startup
summary: Qualcomm Inc announced its acquisition of Nuvia Inc, a chip startup founded by former Apple executives, for $1.4 billion. This move aims to enhance Qualcomm's chip performance in smartphones, laptops, and automotive processors, challenging rivals like Apple and Intel. The deal also helps Qualcomm reduce its reliance on Arm Ltd's licensed technology by incorporating Nuvia's custom core designs.

title: Qualcomm eyes challenge to Apple, Intel with $1.4 billion deal for chip startup
summary: Qualcomm Inc. announced its acquisition of Nuvia Inc., a chip startup founded by former Apple Inc. executives, for $1.4 billion. This strategic move aims to integrate Nuvia's technology into Qualcomm's smartphone, laptop, and automotive processors, challenging Apple and Intel in chip performance. The deal also represents Qualcomm's effort to lessen its reliance on Arm Ltd. and enhance its custom core designs for various applications.

title: Disney+ Streaming App: Supported Devices, Bundles & Content
summary: Disney+ launched on November 12, 2019, offering a vast library of content from Disney, Pixar, Marvel, Star Wars, and National Geographic, alongside original series like "The Mandalorian." The service provides a premium, commercial-free viewing experience with multiple streams and profiles, available on various devices. It also offers a bundle option with Hulu and ESPN+ for U.S. subscribers and plans for a global rollout.

title: New Google Union Triples in Size in First Week, But Faces Formidable Challenges
summary: The Alphabet Workers Union, a new Google union, has tripled its membership to over 700 workers in its first week, reflecting growing activism in Silicon Valley. The union aims to give employees more influence over company operations but faces significant challenges, including Google's historical resistance to unions and strategies to "fissure" its workforce through diverse employment statuses. Critics question its partnership with the Communications Workers of America and its status as a minority union, not yet recognized by the NLRB or Google.

title: These Companies Will Make Your Electric Car For You
summary: Several new joint ventures are emerging in the automotive industry to facilitate the production of electric vehicles (EVs) and components for other companies. Notable partnerships include Magna International with LG Electronics for EV powertrains, and Foxconn with Zhejiang Geely Holding Group for vehicle manufacturing and technical consulting. These collaborations aim to accelerate the shift to EVs by offering contract manufacturing solutions, improving scalability, and fostering quicker market entry for both established automakers and new entrants.

title: Samsung debuts new flagship Galaxy S21 phones, Bluetooth tracker and earbuds
summary: Samsung has unveiled its new Galaxy S21 smartphone series, featuring three 5G-enabled models with varying screen sizes and price points, starting from $799. The phones incorporate Qualcomm's Snapdragon 888 system-on-chip (or Samsung's Exynos 2100 internationally) and boast a redesigned camera housing and improved displays. Alongside the new phones, Samsung also introduced the Galaxy SmartTag, a Bluetooth location tracker, and the high-end Galaxy Buds Pro wireless earbuds.

title: TSMC to Spend Up to Record $28 Billion on Advanced Chips, Capacity
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a major chip supplier, announced plans to significantly increase its capital expenditures to between $25 billion and $28 billion in 2021. This substantial investment, representing at least a 47% year-over-year increase, aims to develop advanced chips and expand plant capacity. The move comes in response to a global surge in semiconductor demand that has resulted in supply shortages.

title: Intel CEO Bob Swan to Be Replaced by VMware Chief
summary: Intel Corp. Chief Executive Officer Bob Swan will be replaced by VMware Inc. CEO Pat Gelsinger, who is returning to Intel more than a decade after leaving. This leadership change comes as Intel faces manufacturing delays, increased competition, and customers designing their own chips. Gelsinger's appointment is seen as a move to bring back technical and home-grown leadership to address the company's challenges.

title: Tech giants pledge millions for tech hub housed at Atlanta University Center
summary: Apple and Southern Company Foundation are each pledging $25 million to establish the Propel Center, a digital tech hub at the Atlanta University Center. This initiative aims to bridge the gap between minorities and the tech industry, serving several historically Black colleges and universities. The center will offer a curriculum focused on entrepreneurship, AI, and social justice, building a pipeline for young people into tech careers.

title: Conservative ETF Reshuffles, ‘QQQ’ Sibs Hit $1B
summary: The American Conservative Values ETF (ACVF) has ceased new purchases of Apple, Amazon, and Google stock due to their perceived censorship of free speech, following their blocking of social media platform Parler. Meanwhile, Invesco's NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) and Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF (QQQJ) have jointly surpassed $1 billion in assets under management in just three months, marking Invesco's most successful launch ever.

title: Neuromod portfolios advance at Medtronic, Boston Scientific, Abbott
summary: Medtronic, Boston Scientific, and Abbott have all announced advancements in their neuromodulation portfolios, signaling a renewed focus on innovation in a sector that was criticized for stagnation prior to 2020. Despite the disruption caused by COVID-19 in 2020, these companies are launching new products and initiatives, including Abbott's digital health app for pain management, Boston Scientific's new SCS systems with Fast Acting Sub-perception Therapy, and Medtronic's clinical trial for adaptive deep brain stimulation in Parkinson's patients. These efforts aim to drive growth and improve patient outcomes in 2021 and beyond.

title: GM, Goldman Sachs, Mastercard join forces on credit card venture
summary: General Motors, Goldman Sachs, and Mastercard are collaborating to introduce co-branded, rewards-based credit cards in 2021. Goldman Sachs will serve as the issuing bank for GM's credit card programs starting in September, with Mastercard continuing as the network provider. This follows Goldman's acquisition of GM's credit card business for approximately $2.5 billion, aiming to enhance the credit card experience for GM customers.

title: Burberry, Entain and blue-chip miners among the big names in coming week as trading updates continue
summary: The upcoming week will feature continued trading updates from major companies, including luxury brand Burberry, betting firm Entain, and several blue-chip miners like Rio Tinto and BHP. Investors will also be watching for macro data such as UK inflation, retail sales, and flash PMIs from both the UK and US, alongside the inauguration of the new US President and the accelerating US earnings season. Companies like Experian, WH Smith, Dixons Carphone, JD Wetherspoon, and EMIS Group are also set to release updates, with focus on pandemic impacts, recovery prospects, liquidity, and digital performance.

title: Goldman Sachs to issue credit cards for General Motors
summary: Goldman Sachs announced it will become the issuing bank for General Motors' credit card business starting September 2021, part of its strategy to expand its consumer banking division. This move follows previous media reports of Goldman Sachs winning the bid over Barclays in a $2.5 billion deal. Mastercard will remain the network for the card, and this marks Goldman's second significant credit card partnership after its collaboration with Apple in 2019.

title: Apple Plans First iMac Desktop Redesign in Nearly a Decade
summary: Apple Inc. is planning the first redesign of its iMac all-in-one desktop computer since 2012, moving away from Intel processors to its own silicon. The new models will feature a slimmer design, similar to Apple's Pro Display XDR monitor, with a flat back and reduced bezels. Apple is expected to launch two versions, codenamed J456 and J457, later this year to replace the current 21.5-inch and 27-inch models.

title: Apple's new executive bonus formula is designed for the fast-changing world we live in
summary: Apple is introducing an ESG (environmental, social, and governance) bonus modifier for its executives in 2021, linking up to 10% of their cash incentives to ESG performance. This change comes after years of pressure from activist shareholders and reflects a growing trend towards incorporating social responsibility into executive pay, accelerated by recent global events. While some experts question the 10% impact, it positions Apple among a minority of companies tying compensation to ESG, potentially influencing other major corporations.

title: Online TV Streaming Service Market: Strong Growth Continues
summary: The online TV streaming service market is experiencing strong growth, driven by technological advancements, urbanization, increased internet/mobile subscriptions, and rising disposable incomes. Key players like Netflix have significantly expanded their subscriber base. The COVID-19 pandemic further accelerated demand, leading to a surge in subscriptions for major streaming platforms.

title: World's top 10 most valuable companies
summary: Apple is the most valuable company globally, with a valuation of $2.12 trillion, followed by Microsoft and Amazon. The 2020 Hurun Global 500 report also highlighted that 51 Chinese companies made the list, with their total value increasing significantly. Electric carmakers Nio and Xpeng Motors, along with Beike Zhaofang, were among the top gainers in percentage growth.

title: Netflix will consider buybacks as it returns to positive cash flow after 2021
summary: Netflix announced it expects to be cash-flow positive after 2021 and will no longer require external financing, ending a decade of significant debt. The company also stated it would consider share buybacks, a practice not seen since 2011. This shift validates Netflix's long-term content spending strategy and comes as its market capitalization has grown significantly.

title: Apple, Southern Company to invest $25 million for AUC’s Propel Center
summary: Apple and Southern Company are investing $25 million each to establish the Propel Center, a global innovation and learning hub for Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), at the Atlanta University Center. This initiative, part of Apple's Racial Equity and Justice Initiative, aims to provide innovative curricula, technological support, career opportunities, and fellowship programs to promote diverse leaders and address systemic racism. The center will focus on various fields, including agricultural technologies, social justice, entertainment arts, app development, and augmented reality, to help minority students excel in the tech industry.

title: The Crown Jewels of UK patent cases
summary: This article reviews the significant UK patent cases decided in 2020 despite the pandemic, highlighting landmark decisions from the UK Supreme Court, High Court, and Court of Appeal, especially concerning mobile phone patents, pharmaceuticals, and AI inventorship. It also previews key cases anticipated for 2021, including disputes in e-cigarette technology and further FRAND trials, and discusses the leading law firms involved in these high-profile intellectual property battles.

title: Chinese hacking group ‘Chimera’ targets Taiwanese chip and airline companies
summary: A Chinese advanced threat protection group named "Chimera" is reportedly targeting Taiwanese semiconductor companies to steal intelligence such as IC chip documents, SDKs, IC designs, and source code. Additionally, the group is accused of stealing passenger data from the airline industry, abusing cloud services from Google and Microsoft. Experts suggest these activities, whether state-sponsored or for competitive advantage, highlight a significant cybersecurity threat requiring robust government action.

title: Netgear warns users that iOS 14's MAC address randomization could cause 'Unknown Devices' to appear on their networks
summary: Netgear has issued a warning to users of its Circle parental control feature that Apple's iOS 14 MAC address randomization could cause "Unknown Devices" to appear on their network. This security feature in iOS 14 (and Android 10) randomizes MAC addresses to prevent tracking, but it can confuse Netgear's Circle, which relies on MAC addresses to identify and manage devices. Netgear advises users to disable the Random MAC / Private MAC Feature for their home WiFi Network to ensure proper functioning of Circle for managing kids' devices.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor suppliers announce plans to come to Arizona
summary: Three Asian companies, Chang Chun Petrochemical Co. Ltd., United Integrated Services Co. Ltd., and Mirle Automation Corp., have reportedly announced plans to establish facilities in Arizona. These companies intend to supply Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd.'s new $12 billion factory in Phoenix, which began construction in 2021 and is expected to create 1,900 jobs. This move positions Phoenix as a future hub for semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Apple, Marriott, Johnson Controls CEOs take lead on Biden initiatives
summary: CEOs from Apple, Marriott, and Johnson Controls are taking an active role in supporting President Biden's early initiatives, including immigration reform, rejoining the Paris climate agreement and WHO, and mask mandates. Tim Cook chairs the Business Roundtable Immigration Committee, George Oliver heads the Energy and Environment Committee, and Arne Sorenson leads the Health and Retirement Committee, all aligning with Biden's policy reversals. These business leaders emphasize the importance of these policies for American values, community strength, and public health.

title: Dan Riccio begins a new chapter at Apple
summary: Apple announced that Dan Riccio will transition to a new role, focusing on a new project and reporting directly to CEO Tim Cook, after over two decades of significant contributions to the company's hardware engineering. His previous role as the head of Hardware Engineering will be taken over by John Ternus, who will also join Apple’s executive team. Both Cook and Riccio expressed excitement for the future, highlighting Riccio's continued involvement in shaping Apple's products and Ternus's leadership in upcoming innovations.

title: Clarivate Launches Web of Science My Research Assistant Mobile Application
summary: Clarivate Plc launched Web of Science My Research Assistant, a new mobile application designed to help scientific researchers search, save, and share publication records from the Web of Science citation index. The app offers features like personalized feeds and reading lists, with both free and unlimited functionality available depending on the user's institutional affiliation. This initiative aims to improve the researcher experience by providing a more personalized service and fostering collaboration within the science community.

title: Bloomberg: Senior Peloton instructors earn $500,000 annually in total compensation
summary: A recent Bloomberg article revealed that senior Peloton instructors can earn upwards of $500,000 annually in total compensation, excluding external sponsorships. Unlike most fitness companies, Peloton hires instructors as full-time employees with fixed salaries and incentive compensation, even offering stock options. This business model highlights Peloton's focus on retaining its key talent, who are central to the company's community feel and scalable growth.

title: The SNPE ETF Has Outpaced the S&P 500 Since Its Inception
summary: The Xtrackers S&P 500 ESG ETF (SNPE) has outperformed the S&P 500 since its inception, boasting a 35% gain compared to the S&P 500's 30%. The fund tracks the S&P 500 ESG Index, focusing on companies with strong environmental, social, and governance performance. Analysts are split on whether the ESG trend will continue its momentum, particularly with a new administration taking office.

title: Sony Music Entertainment & Gospel Artist Kirk Franklin Team Up For Podcast Series
summary: Sony Music Entertainment and 16-time Grammy-winning gospel artist Kirk Franklin are launching a new podcast series titled "Good Words with Kirk Franklin" on February 9. The podcast will feature Franklin engaging in intimate conversations with artists and activists like H.E.R, Glennon Doyle, Kelly Rowland, and Pharrell Williams, exploring themes of faith, redemption, race, religion, politics, music, and self-expression, with original music provided by Franklin. This initiative is part of Sony Music Entertainment's expanding podcast ventures.

title: Qualcomm to supply General Motors with 'cockpit' chips for next generation vehicles
summary: Qualcomm Inc announced a deal to supply General Motors Co with a range of chips for its next generation of vehicles. The agreement includes Qualcomm's "cockpit" chips for in-dashboard systems and automated driver assistance system chips. Qualcomm's Snapdragon Ride driver-assistance computer is also expected to be in a production vehicle in 2022.

title: Smartphone audio is important, and here’s what it takes to build the best
summary: This article discusses the importance of audio quality in smartphones and the technology behind building superior smartphone speakers. It highlights the role of high-quality amplifier chips, particularly Class H amplifiers, in enabling clear, powerful, and distortion-free audio. The piece also mentions companies like Cirrus Logic and their CS35L40 and CS35L41 amplifier ICs, which are used in many high-profile smartphones to achieve top audio performance.

title: Micron's 1-alpha process node offers impressive improvements in DRAM density and performance
summary: Micron has begun manufacturing DRAM using its new 1α (1-alpha) process, which offers a 40 percent improvement in memory density and a 15 percent drop in power consumption compared to its 1Z nm technology. This new node supports densities from 8Gb to 16Gb and is being used for DDR4 and LPDDR4 RAM, with plans for broader integration. The company anticipates this will lead to decreased manufacturing costs for DRAM, subsequently resulting in better prices for consumers, although the long-term strategy for scaling DRAM with or without EUV technology remains to be seen against competitors.

title: AT&T takes $15.5 billion charge on DirecTV, adds more phone customers
summary: AT&T announced a $15.5 billion writedown on its premium TV business, including DirecTV, due to cord-cutting and a $780 million charge for its WarnerMedia unit. Despite these setbacks, the company added 800,000 new phone subscribers in the fourth quarter, surpassing analyst expectations. AT&T's full-year outlook projects 1% revenue growth and flat adjusted earnings per share.

title: Start Diesel Pump and Pay for Fuel Directly With TA App
summary: TravelCenters of America (TA) has introduced a new "PumpSmart" feature to its TruckSmart mobile app, allowing professional drivers to activate diesel pumps and pay for fuel directly from their cabs. This feature is available for UltraONE loyalty members using commercial payment cards and securely stores payment information while automatically applying loyalty points. The PumpSmart addition is part of TA's broader effort to enhance the guest experience and upgrade its TruckSmart app,

title: Apple posts first $100B quarter in company history after strong iPhone 12 holiday demand
summary: Apple reported its first-ever $100 billion quarter, with revenues reaching $111.4 billion, driven by robust demand for the iPhone 12 and growth in its digital services. This performance exceeded Wall Street expectations despite the pandemic, with iPhone 12 sales alone generating $65.6 billion. The company also saw increased revenues from Mac, iPad, and wearables, and returned over $30 billion to shareholders.

title: The Tech Sector Today: Not the Bubble of the 1990s
summary: The article argues that the current tech sector is not in a bubble similar to the 1990s, highlighting the differences in market characteristics and momentum volatility. It points to the Invesco DWA Technology Momentum ETF (PTF) as an attractive investment, noting its focus on relative strength (momentum) and its potential diversification benefits for portfolios. The author emphasizes that today's tech sector and momentum strategies are less volatile than during the dot-com era.

title: Intel invests additional $475 million in Vietnam plant
summary: Intel Corp has announced an additional $475 million investment in its Vietnam plant, bringing its total investment in the country to about $1.5 billion. This new funding aims to enhance technologies and boost the production of its 5G products and core processors. The move highlights Vietnam's growing importance in the global technology supply chain, as companies like Apple and Samsung diversify production to mitigate trade war impacts and the country's effective handling of the coronavirus pandemic.

title: The Tech Sector Today: Not the Bubble of the 1990s
summary: The technology sector, while performing strongly, is fundamentally different from the 1990s bubble. The Invesco DWA Technology Momentum ETF (PTF) is highlighted as an attractive option, leveraging relative strength investing. Momentum strategies such as PTF are less volatile now compared to the 2000 tech bubble and can offer diversification benefits to portfolios.

title: AI fund that's beating S&P 500 has shifted into tech stocks
summary: A machine-guided fund, the AI Powered Equity ETF (AIEQ), has significantly shifted its holdings into technology stocks, now comprising over 37% of its portfolio. This move comes as the fund continues to outperform the S&P 500 Index, having risen 12% this year compared to the S&P 500's nearly 3% gain. The fund has also halved its industrial exposure and doubled its position in Tesla Inc., while significantly reducing its Apple Inc. holdings.

title: STMicroelectronics, Tesla and Apple: what happens when eMobility & 5G collide
summary: This article examines how STMicroelectronics' Q4 2020 results were bolstered by its key relationships with Tesla in e-mobility and Apple in 5G mobile and consumer markets. It highlights STMicroelectronics' dominance in Silicon Carbide (SiC) for electric vehicles, largely driven by Tesla's adoption, and its significant role in CMOS image sensors for Apple's 3D imaging and sensing applications. The article also discusses the company's strategies to address capacity constraints and maintain its market position against competitors.

title: Apple sees revenue growth accelerating after setting record for iPhone sales, China strength
summary: Apple reported holiday quarter sales and profits that exceeded Wall Street's expectations, driven by strong sales of its new 5G iPhones and a significant 57% increase in China sales. The company's revenue rose 21% to $111.44 billion, with iPhone sales hitting a new record of $65.60 billion. Chief Financial Officer Luca Maestri indicated that revenue growth is expected to accelerate in the current fiscal second quarter.

title: Google to stop using Apple tool to track iPhone users, avoiding new pop-up warning
summary: Google has announced that its iPhone apps, including Maps and YouTube, will cease using Apple's IDFA tool for personalizing ads. This move comes as Apple prepares to enforce new tracking transparency rules that would require apps to obtain explicit user consent via a pop-up warning, which Google aims to avoid. While Google's core ad business may not be significantly impacted due to its logged-in user base, the company warns that publishers and advertisers relying on its mobile ad software will likely see weaker results without IDFA access.

title: Apple Just Got a Little Greener. The EFIV ETF Is All Ears
summary: Apple recently had its largest revenue quarter ever and is now incorporating environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics into its executive compensation. This move makes Apple a greener investment, reinforcing its position as the top holding in the SPDR S&P 500 ESG ETF (EFIV). The EFIV ETF offers investors exposure to S&P 500 firms that meet specific sustainability criteria while maintaining similar overall industry group weights.

title: UVXY ETF Is a Good Buy as Earnings and WSB Drive Volatility
summary: The article discusses why the ProShares Ultra VIX Short-Term Futures ETF (UVXY) is a good buy amidst rising market volatility. It explains that the VIX Index, which UVXY tracks, is rising due to the earnings season, fears of a stock market bubble, and the impact of the WallStreetBets Reddit community on certain stocks. The article suggests UVXY as a way to bet on continued volatility given its asset size, liquidity, and expense ratio compared to competitors.

title: Apple Just Got a Little Greener. The EFIV ETF Is All Ears
summary: Apple is incorporating environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics into its executive compensation, aligning with responsible investing trends. This move is significant for the SPDR S&P 500 ESG ETF (EFIV), which holds Apple as its top investment and focuses on companies meeting sustainability criteria. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance, having achieved its largest quarterly revenue, while also detailing how the EFIV ETF provides exposure to ESG-compliant S&P 500 firms.

title: Apple Supplier Skyworks Is Soaring. What It Means for Other Chip Makers.
summary: Skyworks Solutions, an iPhone component supplier, saw its stock rise significantly after reporting earnings that exceeded expectations. The strong performance of Skyworks suggests potential positive implications for other companies that supply chips to Apple. This article explores the company's recent success and its broader market impact.

title: “Hometown,” shot on iPhone 12 Pro
summary: Apple commissioned over 30 Black photographers to capture their hometowns using iPhone 12 Pro for Black History Month. The project, titled "Hometown," showcases various Black experiences across the US, focusing on themes like diversity, excellence, resilience, joy, pain, and community. Photographers like Julien James, Lawrence Agyei, Gabriella Angotti-Jones, Brian Day, and Lelanie Foster share their unique perspectives and how the iPhone camera helped them tell their stories.

title: Nestle, Nvidia, and the Changing Global Equity Markets
summary: The article analyzes the shifting dynamics of global equity markets over the past decade, characterized by the dominance of U.S. technology stocks like Nvidia, in contrast to traditional companies like Nestle. It highlights the increased concentration of U.S. market indexes in technology and consumer cyclical sectors, and discusses current valuation opportunities in non-U.S. markets and value sectors like energy.

title: Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Disney (DIS) Are Top Admired Companies
summary: Apple, Microsoft, and Disney have been recognized as the top admired companies in Fortune Magazine's 2021 list, securing the first, third, and fourth spots respectively. This ranking, compiled in partnership with Korn Ferry, surveyed executives, directors, and analysts on various criteria. While direct financial impact is minimal, such accolades are valuable as recruiting tools to attract top talent.

title: Bei Zhong, General Manager Asia–Pacific of LORD Corporation
summary: Bei Zhong, General Manager Asia–Pacific of LORD Corporation (now part of Parker Hannifin Corporation), emphasizes a leadership philosophy centered on driving culture, empowering teams, and fostering innovation. She highlights the importance of changing mindsets, understanding employee motivations, and building trust to achieve outstanding results. Zhong also discusses the significant growth potential in the electric vehicle market, where LORD Corporation is a key supplier, and addresses challenges like supply chain resilience and the accelerating pace of technological innovation.

title: Climb On Board the ESG Megatrend with 'EFIV'
summary: The article highlights ESG investing as a "megatrend" and introduces the SPDR S&P 500 ESG ETF (EFIV) as a way for investors to access this market. It notes that President Joe Biden's administration is expected to provide tailwinds for ESG through its focus on clean energy and potential SEC reforms. EFIV invests in S&P 500 firms meeting sustainability criteria, maintaining similar industry group weights to the S&P 500, and has shown a 17% gain over the past year with a 0.10% expense ratio.

title: Taiwan’s MediaTek launches 5G chip aimed at US
summary: MediaTek, a rival to Qualcomm and Samsung, has launched a new 5G chip, the M80, designed to capture market share in the United States. This new chip supports millimetre-wave technology, which is crucial for US carriers like Verizon, unlike its previous 5G chips that only supported the "sub-6" variant. The company aims to secure more contracts for US models, building on its progress with handset makers like Samsung and LG.

title: Apple, Its Control Over the iPhone, and The Internet
summary: This article examines Apple's extensive control over the iPhone ecosystem and its implications for the internet, competition, and future digital economies. It argues that Apple's policies, while leading to success, inhibit innovation and create anti-competitive barriers for developers and users through its App Store, payment systems, and technology decisions. The author suggests regulatory intervention to force competition in app distribution and payments, allowing users to download apps from any source and developers to use alternative payment solutions.

title: Here’s how the FAANG companies stack up this earnings season
summary: The article discusses the strong performance of FAANG companies during the earnings season, specifically highlighting their impressive revenue growth and pricing power. This trend continued into the fourth quarter of the previous year and throughout the entire year, even amidst the pandemic. The author notes that these companies consistently deliver significant increases in revenue despite their large size.

title: Here’s how the FAANG companies stack up this earnings season
summary: This article examines the performance of FAANG companies during the latest earnings season, highlighting their continued impressive growth in revenue and display of pricing power. Despite their large size and the challenges posed by the pandemic, these companies maintained successful growth strategies in the fourth quarter and throughout the previous year. The piece underscores the dominance of FAANG stocks in earnings reports.

title: Apple, Hyundai-Kia close to finalizing deal for Apple Car - report
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly nearing a deal with Hyundai-Kia to produce the autonomous Apple Car, with a potential rollout in 2024. While no agreement has been finalized, the report suggests manufacturing could occur at Kia's assembly plant in Georgia, though Apple may still consider other automakers. Uncertainty remains regarding the final partner and launch timeline.

title: Tiger Brokers To Be The One-Stop Online Trading Solution For Investors; Adds Two New Trading Services
summary: Tiger Brokers Singapore has launched two new trading services: Daily Leveraged Certificates (DLCs) and selected US-listed over-the-counter (OTC) stocks, aiming to be a one-stop solution for investors. These additions expand their investment offerings beyond six global exchanges and mutual funds, driven by increasing user demand for diverse investment instruments. The platform also announced strategic partnerships and supports multi-currency trading to reduce costs for investors.

title: Netflix raises prices for Japanese customers
summary: Netflix has increased its monthly subscription prices in Japan for its standard and basic plans. This move comes as the streaming giant invests heavily in original content to compete with a growing number of rivals. The price of the premium plan in Japan remains unchanged, while earlier price hikes were also implemented in the United States.

title: Chris Lyons: Where Tech Meets Culture
summary: Chris Lyons, head of Andreessen Horowitz's Cultural Leadership Fund, discusses how the fund connects Black cultural leaders with tech companies and elevates African-American creators in the tech industry. He explains the rationale behind the fund, its unique all-African-American LP structure, and its mission to foster entrepreneurship and generational wealth within the Black community by increasing access and exposure to technology and venture capital. Lyons also shares his personal journey from audio engineer to Silicon Valley investor, emphasizing the importance of networking and service.

title: T-Mobile’s new CEO answers the call
summary: Mike Sievert, T-Mobile's new CEO, has taken the helm after the impactful tenure of John Legere, who transformed the company into a dominant 5G player. Sievert, known for his marketing prowess and long history with the company, aims to capitalize on T-Mobile's 5G network advantage, secured through the Sprint merger, to continue subscriber growth and expand into the cable market. Analysts believe T-Mobile has a strong recipe for continued success under Sievert's leadership.

title: Robinhood’s Reckoning: Facing Life After GameStop
summary: Robinhood, a firm that aimed to democratize investing, is grappling with the aftermath of the GameStop trading frenzy. Its CEO, Vlad Tenev, found the app's top ranking in Apple's store to be a "nightmare" as the company faced intense scrutiny and regulatory challenges. This period marks a critical turning point for the company as it confronts the complexities of Wall Street regulations.
authors: Peter Rudegeair|Kirsten Grind and Maureen Farrell

title: The Q's Take It: Why This Pair of 'QQQ' ETFs Will Keep Performing
summary: This article discusses the continued strong performance of the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM), particularly in the wake of the Covid-19 pandemic. It highlights how the Nasdaq 100's heavy allocation to technology, consumer services, and healthcare has driven outperformance compared to the S&P 500. The article also differentiates QQQ for traders and QQQM for buy-and-hold investors, emphasizing the growth component of the Nasdaq 100 for long-term strategies.

title: Bank of Hawaii begins issuing contactless debit cards
summary: Bank of Hawaii has started a mass reissue of contactless debit cards, becoming the only local bank in Hawaii to do so. This initiative was announced on Monday, February 8, 2021. The move follows similar actions by other financial institutions, including First Hawaiian Bank.

title: Hyundai, Kia say Apple car deal now off, see $8.5 billion wiped off market value
summary: Hyundai Motor Co and its affiliate Kia Corp announced that they are no longer in talks with Apple Inc regarding the development of autonomous electric cars. This news led to a combined $8.5 billion drop in their market value, with Hyundai's stock sliding 6.2% and Kia's tumbling 15%. The decision follows internal divisions within Hyundai Motor Group, as some executives were concerned about becoming a mere contract manufacturer for Apple.

title: A Look At 6 ETFs That Cover The Electric Vehicles Trend
summary: This article discusses the accelerating growth of the global electric vehicle (EV) market, highlighting record sales in late 2020 and forecasts for exponential growth from 2022 onwards. It recommends six electric vehicle-related ETFs, four of which cover EVs plus autonomous vehicles (AVs) and two that focus on EVs plus the EV supply chain, including battery makers and miners. The author emphasizes that the real EV boom has just begun and provides sales figures and market share percentages to support this trend.

title: The Infosys Technologies IPO was undersubscribed - 10 facts of the financial world you will have a hard time believing
summary: This article lists 10 surprising facts about the financial world, covering topics like historical income tax rates, the undersubscribed Infosys IPO, the past duration of trade settlements, and Warren Buffett's wealth accumulation. It also highlights Apple's market capitalization, changes in tax deductions for investments, the past rivalry between Anil and Mukesh Ambani, and records in public equity fundraising and mutual fund launches.

title: Twitter Shares and Social Media ETFs Balloon on Strong User Growth
summary: Twitter (NasdaqGS: TWTR) shares and social media ETFs, particularly the Global X Social Media ETF (SOCL), surged after Twitter reported strong user growth during the holiday quarter and into January, with revenue exceeding $1 billion. Despite this success, the company warned of potential user growth slowdowns due to post-pandemic normalization and possible impacts from Apple's privacy changes. Twitter also addressed the suspension of former President Donald Trump, noting it was well-received by advertisers.

title: Ball Corp. sets retail launch of Rome, Ga.-made recyclable aluminum cups for early 2021
summary: Ball Corp. is set to launch its recyclable aluminum cups, manufactured in Rome, Ga., for retail in the first half of 2021. These cups, which are infinitely recyclable, have already been utilized at major sporting events like Super Bowl LIV and LV. This move expands their availability beyond limited pilot programs.

title: Twitter Shares and Social Media ETFs Balloon on Strong User Growth
summary: Twitter shares surged 12.2% after the company reported strong user growth and its second-ever $1 billion quarter, leading to a 3.2% rise in the Global X Social Media ETF (SOCL). Despite a positive financial outlook, Twitter cautioned about potential user growth slowdowns post-pandemic and the impact of Apple's privacy changes on advertising. The company also addressed the suspension of former President Donald Trump, noting it did not negatively affect advertiser sentiment.

title: SuperMicro server spy chip story returns, with no more proof than before
summary: Bloomberg has revisited its 2018 report alleging Chinese spy chips in Super Micro server hardware used by Apple, Amazon, and the US government, but the new story offers no additional corroborating evidence. Apple, Amazon, SuperMicro, and several US federal agencies previously denied the original claims, with Apple CEO Tim Cook criticizing Bloomberg's reporting. Despite the continued denials and lack of proof, Bloomberg stands by its controversial story.

title: Years later, Bloomberg doubles down on disputed Supermicro supply chain hack story
summary: Bloomberg has published a new article, years after its initial disputed report, to double down on its claims that the Chinese state infiltrated Supermicro to plant secret chips on its motherboards. While Supermicro and other companies involved continue to deny these allegations, the new report offers additional context and quotes, alleging wider historical tampering and government investigations. Despite the ongoing denials and lack of official government confirmation, Bloomberg stands by its investigative journalism.

title: With new CEO Pat Gelsinger, Intel looks to its past in hopes of securing the future
summary: Pat Gelsinger, a veteran engineer, returns as Intel's CEO to revitalize its manufacturing and engineering, a move seen as a risky but essential gamble to restore the company's pole position in semiconductor production. Despite investor skepticism and past manufacturing failures that allowed rivals to leap ahead, Gelsinger is committed to internal production rather than outsourcing, aiming to bring Intel back to its former glory by drawing on its historical strengths and leadership principles, especially those of former CEO Andy Grove. This strategy is an attempt to address Intel's existential crisis stemming from production delays and increased competition from companies like Apple and AMD.

title: How Does Apple’s M1 Chip Compare to Current Intel and AMD Chips?
summary: This article compares Apple's M1 chip to current Intel and AMD chips, highlighting the M1's Arm-based architecture, 5nm manufacturing process, unified RAM, and superior performance per watt. It discusses benchmark tests showing the M1's strong performance in 3D rendering and some single-threaded tasks, while also noting Intel's counter-claims and the M1's current limitations in gaming. The article concludes by suggesting that while the M1 is a potent threat to x86 dominance, early adopters might face challenges as software developers adapt.

title: Spotify set for uphill battle in South Korea
summary: Spotify recently launched its service in South Korea, a market already dominated by local music streaming services like Melon, Genie Music, and Flo. Despite its global presence, Spotify faces challenges including a lack of content from major Korean artists due to an unconfirmed deal with Kakao M, and increasing competition from both established local players bolstering their services and new contenders like Clubhouse. The company hopes to succeed by offering tailored playlists, a vast catalog, and launching podcast services, but its success is not guaranteed in this competitive landscape.

title: Clubhouse says reviewing data protection practices after report points to flaws
summary: Clubhouse is reviewing its data protection practices following a report by the Stanford Internet Observatory that identified security flaws potentially exposing user data to the Chinese government. The audio app plans to implement stronger encryption and blocks to prevent data transmission to Chinese servers within 72 hours, spurred by the discovery that conversations by users in China could be transmitted via Chinese servers despite the app not being officially available there.

title: Sonos One vs Apple HomePod: Which smart speaker should you buy?
summary: This article compares the Sonos One and Apple HomePod smart speakers to help consumers decide which one to purchase. It evaluates them based on price, multi-room system compatibility, music service support, control methods, and loudness. The Sonos One typically comes out ahead for versatility and value, while the HomePod is preferred for Apple users with larger rooms and deeper pockets.

title: Apple wins victory as North Dakota votes down bill that would regulate app stores
summary: The North Dakota state senate has voted down a bill that would have required app stores like Apple's and Google's to allow developers to use their own payment processing software, thereby avoiding the up to 30% fees charged. This decision is a win for Apple, which argued that its tight control over the App Store is essential for user safety and product integrity. The proposed legislation, despite being defeated, highlights ongoing debates at state and federal levels regarding the commercial power of tech giants and their app store policies, fueled by efforts from groups like the Coalition for App Fairness.

title: Business Acronyms You Should Know: The Master Guide
summary: This master guide clarifies essential business acronyms, acting as a "Rosetta Stone" for common terms in finance, U.S. agencies, international organizations, and general business. It aims to make business news more accessible by explaining terms like IPO, M&A, S&P, CDC, EU, OPEC, GDP, ESG, and FAMGA. The article categorizes these acronyms for easy reference, helping readers navigate complex business discussions.

title: 2021 Polaris Slingshot R First Drive Review: Shot in the Dark
summary: The 2021 Polaris Slingshot R receives key upgrades, primarily software-related, making it the best version yet. Improvements include recalibrated "Autodrive" transmission for smoother shifting, paddle shifters for manual control, a new hill-hold feature, and optional Apple CarPlay and heated/cooled seats. While the overall look and drive remain similar to the 2020 model, these enhancements aim to broaden its appeal, especially with a high percentage of new buyers opting for the automatic transmission.

title: Morgan Stanley creates bot that does junior analysts' work — faster
summary: Morgan Stanley has developed an AI-powered virtual assistant called AskResearch that processes its 50,000 annual research reports to help employees find specific information faster. The bot uses natural language processing and machine learning to answer questions, acting as a "junior analyst" to free up human analysts for more complex tasks. While initially for internal use, Morgan Stanley plans to roll out AskResearch to clients, making information more accessible and strengthening client relationships.

title: Corning and Schott battle over flexible glass handset displays
summary: Schott has successfully overturned two patents for flexible glass owned by Corning at the European Patent Office, intensifying their dispute over ultra-flexible glass for mobile devices. The outcome of this ongoing legal battle could significantly impact the production of flexible displays in the mobile industry. Corning continues to defend a third patent while Schott, a key supplier for Samsung Display's foldable phones, appears to be gaining ground.

title: Buffett’s Berkshire reveals major stakes in Verizon and Chevron
summary: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Inc. announced significant new investments, including an US$8.6-billion stake in Verizon Communications Inc. and a US$4.1-billion stake in Chevron Corp., as disclosed in a regulatory filing. To fund these and other new investments, Berkshire pared back holdings in companies like Apple Inc., while also reducing stakes in several banking rivals and increasing investments in pharmaceutical companies. These moves signal where Buffett and his portfolio managers see value and demonstrate efforts to deploy Berkshire's substantial cash hoard.

title: Epic Games Files EU Antitrust Complaint Against Apple
summary: Epic Games has filed an antitrust complaint against Apple in the European Union, accusing the tech giant of anti-competitive practices regarding app distribution and payment processes. The company alleges that Apple's control over the iOS ecosystem harms competition, leading to inflated prices for consumers and stifling developers. Epic Games is seeking fair access and increased competition, not damages, and this complaint aligns with ongoing legal battles in the US, Australia, and the UK.

title: Amazon documents reveal company’s secret strategy to dodge India’s regulators
summary: Internal Amazon documents reveal the company's strategy to navigate and "test the boundaries" of India's foreign direct investment regulations, which aim to protect small retailers. The documents show Amazon favored a small group of sellers, some with indirect equity ties to Amazon, accounting for a disproportionately large share of sales, and helped them secure exclusive deals and subsidized fees. This practice allowed Amazon to circumvent rules prohibiting direct sales and inventory ownership by e-commerce platforms, contradicting its public statements about fair competition and compliance with Indian law.

title: News Corp signs news partnership deal with Google
summary: News Corp has finalized a global news partnership with Google, encompassing a subscription platform, advertising revenue sharing, and the development of audio and video journalism. This extensive deal concludes years of tension between Rupert Murdoch's media empire and the tech giant, especially in Australia, where Google had threatened to cease operations due to proposed content laws. The agreement follows similar deals News Corp struck with Apple and Facebook, marking a significant win for media companies seeking compensation for their premium content from large digital platforms.

title: Design of first Sonos headphones detailed in new patent drawings
summary: New patent drawings reveal details of Sonos' first headphones, expected to launch later in 2021. These headphones are anticipated to compete with premium models from Bose, Sony, Apple, and others, potentially offering features like Wi-Fi connectivity and seamless hand-off with Sonos speakers, with an estimated price of $400-$450. The latest design shows a simpler headband attachment and the Sonos name on the ear cups.

title: How ST is driving Tesla and Apple e-mobility and 5G ambitions
summary: STMicroelectronics' success is heavily reliant on its key customers, Tesla and Apple. For Tesla, STMicroelectronics is a leading supplier of Silicon Carbide (SiC) power devices for e-mobility, particularly traction inverters, despite capacity constraints which ST is addressing through acquisitions and R&D. For Apple, STMicroelectronics makes significant contributions to 3D imaging and sensing, including NIR image sensors and Time of Flight detectors for iPhones, though Sony's increasing presence in Apple devices poses a potential challenge for STMicroelectronics' future in the CMOS image sensor market.

title: Apple Hiring Engineers to Develop 6G Wireless
