summary: Apple is actively recruiting wireless system research engineers to develop 6G cellular technology, aiming to reduce its reliance on other companies for next-generation connectivity. The job postings are for positions in Apple's Silicon Valley and San Diego offices, indicating a strategic move to position itself as a leader in future wireless innovations, shortly after launching its first 5G-enabled iPhones.

title: Apple Is Working on Magnetic Battery Pack Attachment for iPhones
summary: Apple is reportedly developing a magnetically attached battery pack for the iPhone 12, which would wirelessly charge the device and serve as a new accessory. The company has been working on this product for at least a year. Despite facing development issues, particularly with software, the accessory is expected to be a lucrative add-on.

title: Futu Holdings To Officially Launch Its Mobile Brokerage App – moomoo. Here’s What Singapore Investors Can Expect
summary: Futu Holdings is launching its mobile brokerage app, moomoo, in Singapore, offering low-cost access to U.S., Hong Kong, and Singapore stock markets. The app provides a fully digital investment experience with competitive commission fees, free Level 2 market data for US stocks, and tools for charting and analysis. New customers can receive a S$180 welcome bundle by signing up and depositing funds before March 8, 2021.

title: Apple’s Advanced Manufacturing Fund helps COPAN Diagnostics ship millions of COVID-19 test kits
summary: Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund helped COPAN Diagnostics ship over 15 million COVID-19 test kits by investing $10 million and collaborating with COPAN and other US companies. This partnership led to a nearly 4,000 percent increase in test-kit production, created jobs, and established a state-of-the-art medical supply chain, playing a critical role in supporting healthcare professionals during the pandemic.

title: The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation Sells Its Alibaba Shares
summary: The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation sold its entire stake in Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba, earning $21.13 million from the sale. Simultaneously, the foundation also divested its shares in Uber and reduced its holdings in Apple and Amazon, while significantly increasing its investment in the software company Schrodinger. These changes reflect a notable shift in the foundation's investment portfolio.

title: Traeger grills and smokers can now be controlled directly from Apple Watch
summary: Traeger has released an update to its app, allowing users to control their grills and smokers directly from their Apple Watch. This new functionality utilizes Traeger's WiFIRE technology and enables users to monitor temperatures, set timers, and manage various grill modes remotely through their Apple Watch. The update, version 2.1.0, is now available on the App Store.

title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway bought these 3 stocks while sold Apple, bank shares
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway added three new stocks to its portfolio in late 2020: Chevron Corp., Verizon Communications, and Marsh & McLennan Companies. Concurrently, Buffett significantly trimmed his stake in Apple and completely exited positions in JPMorgan Chase, PNC Financial Services, M&T Bank, and reduced holdings in Wells Fargo. These moves reflect a shift in investment strategy, adding to energy and telecom while reducing exposure to tech and traditional banking.

title: Apple buys a company every three to four weeks
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook revealed that the company has acquired approximately 100 companies over the past six years, averaging one acquisition every three to four weeks. These acquisitions are primarily focused on gaining technology and talent, helping Apple to integrate innovations into its products and expand into areas like AI, self-driving technology, and payments. Despite its rapid acquisition pace, Apple tends to buy smaller firms and has been more financially restrained in its purchases compared to other big tech companies.

title: Qualcomm launches AR smart viewer reference design specifications
summary: Qualcomm has released its Snapdragon XR1 AR Smart Viewer reference design, aiming to guide hardware manufacturers in creating powerful and affordable AR smart glasses for consumer and enterprise use. This design utilizes the Snapdragon XR1 platform, offloading significant processing to a tethered host device to enhance performance and reduce power consumption. The reference design supports 2D app frameworks for smartphone applications in 3D environments, is compatible with Windows 10, and includes features like 6-DoF head tracking, hand tracking, and remote assistance capabilities, with contributions from partners like Microsoft, Lenovo, and Nreal.

title: Review: SanDisk Ultra Dual Drive Luxe USB-C Flash Drive is a stylish way to offload iPad Pro files
summary: The SanDisk Ultra Dual Drive Luxe USB-C Flash Drive offers both USB-C and USB-A connections in a stylish, all-metal design, making it suitable for transferring files between modern and older devices, including the iPad Pro. While it boasts decent read speeds up to 150MB/s, its write speeds are considerably slower, around 60MB/s. Despite this, its compact size and dual connectivity make it a good option for occasional file transfers and offloading data from devices like the iPad Pro, earning it a 3.5 out of 5 rating.

title: American Express rolls out new Digital Receipts feature
summary: American Express has launched its new Digital Receipts feature, allowing customers to easily track and identify online purchases from merchants such as Google, Microsoft, Square, and Apple. This initiative aims to enhance transparency, reduce confusion over transactions, and provide greater account protection against fraudulent charges, especially given the surge in e-commerce. The service provides detailed purchase information directly through the Amex App or website.

title: Apple Watch Can Now Control Your Traeger Grill With Sizzling New App
summary: Traeger grill owners with WiFIRE-enabled models can now control their grills directly from their Apple Watch. This new app functionality allows users to adjust temperatures, start smoke cycles, monitor probe temperatures, set timers, and enable "Keep Warm" mode, making the grilling experience even more convenient. The WiFIRE technology, first introduced in 2017, is available across several Traeger models.

title: Target to open mini Apple stores in select locations
summary: Target announced plans to open "mini Apple stores" within 17 of its locations initially, with more to follow by the end of the fall. These dedicated spaces will offer an expanded assortment of Apple products and accessories, and feature "Target Tech Consultants" trained by Apple. This collaboration aims to enhance the customer experience and builds on Target's positive sales performance, including a 102% increase in digital sales during the holiday season.

title: Hacker drains Baden woman's bank account after intercepting call, taking over iPhone
summary: A Baden woman, Donna Francis, was scammed out of nearly $1,500 after a hacker allegedly intercepted her call to Apple Support, convinced her to install remote access software, and drained her bank account. This incident follows a similar phone call interception scam in Allegheny County involving COVID-19 vaccine appointments. The FBI advises extreme caution and verification when a support person requests remote access to a device.

title: Target to open 'mini Apple shops' in select stores, offer more products online. Will your location get an upgrade?
summary: Target is introducing "mini Apple shops" within 17 select stores across the country, expanding Apple's physical footprint and offering an enhanced shopping experience. These dedicated spaces will showcase iPhones, iPads, Apple Watches, AirPods, and accessories, with Target tech employees receiving specialized training from Apple. The initiative aims to capitalize on the popularity of Apple products among Target shoppers and builds on Target's strategy of partnering with national brands to elevate its in-store experience.

title: Apple is Berkshire Hathaway's biggest equity investment, so why did Warren Buffett sell shares?
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway sold 9.81 million shares of Apple stock in Q4 2020 for nearly $1.1 billion, matching the initial purchase quantity from 2016. Despite the sale, Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway's largest equity investment, valued at around $120 billion. The sale provided funds to invest in new stakes in companies like Verizon, Marsh & McLennan, and Chevron, diversifying Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio while reducing concentration in Apple.

title: Deals: Apple AirPods Pro $189, $100 off SanDisk Portable SSD
summary: Amazon-owned Woot has reissued a flash deal on Apple AirPods Pro, bringing their price down to $189 (a $60 discount) for one day only. Separately, a 1TB SanDisk Extreme Portable SSD is also on sale for $149.99, saving buyers $100 off the MSRP. Various other Apple products, such as Magic Keyboards, MacBook Pros, and iMacs, are also available at discounted prices through AppleInsider and authorized resellers.

title: Goldman consumer head is leaving to run Walmart fintech
summary: Omer Ismail, who was instrumental in building Goldman Sachs' consumer-banking business, is departing to lead Walmart's new financial-technology startup. Ismail's move comes as Walmart seeks to expand its financial services offerings, with David Stark also joining from Goldman's team. This leadership change highlights both Goldman's ongoing adjustments in its consumer division and Walmart's strategic push into fintech.

title: MicroLED, mini LED, OLED - understanding Apple's future iPhone, iPad, and Mac displays
summary: This article explains the differences and applications of TFT LCD, OLED, mini-LED, and microLED display technologies, focusing on Apple's current and future use cases for its iPhone, iPad, and Mac products. It details how mini LED enhances traditional LCDs with localized dimming, while OLED offers self-illuminating pixels, and microLED represents the most radical shift with inorganic, ultra-small, self-emissive pixels. The piece highlights Apple's investment in these technologies and their potential impact on product design and user experience.

title: Goldman loses 4 executives in 3 days as Walmart fintech poaches Marcus chief
summary: Goldman Sachs is experiencing a significant executive exodus, with four executives departing in three days, including Marcus chief Omer Ismail and deputy David Stark who are joining Walmart's new fintech venture. Karen Seymour, Goldman's general counsel, is also leaving, and Eric Lane, co-head of asset management, is moving to Tiger Global Management. These departures highlight Walmart's serious entry into the fintech space and pose a challenge for Goldman's consumer banking division.

title: Vizio Aims for Some of That Roku-like Magic with IPO
summary: Vizio is pursuing an initial public offering, positioning itself as a technology company akin to Roku by leveraging its SmartCast operating system and "Platform+" business. The company reported a 20% increase in smart TV shipments and 11% revenue growth in 2020, with its SmartCast OS gathering 12.2 million active users. Vizio aims for its Platform+ segment, focused on advertising and data sales, to drive future margin growth and financial performance, similar to how Roku's platform revenues have outperformed hardware sales.

title: Gatorade Debuts Wearable Patch To Measure Hydration Needs
summary: Gatorade has introduced the Gx Sweat Patch and a companion Gx App, a new wearable technology designed to measure an individual's unique sweat profile for personalized hydration recommendations. The patch is used during a single workout, and its data, scanned by the app, provides tailored advice for hydration before, during, and after exercise. This new product is part of Gatorade's expanding Gx platform, though the company recently faced an intellectual property challenge regarding its Gatorlyte product.

title: KT and LG Uplus Invest in SKT’s Subsidiary One Store to Unite in Building Korea’s Leading App Store
summary: KT and LG Uplus have invested a total of KRW 26 billion in SKT's subsidiary, One Store, acquiring a 3.8% stake to strengthen Korea's ICT ecosystem and enhance local app store competitiveness. This investment aims to deepen collaboration among the three mobile operators (SKT, KT, LG Uplus) and further increase One Store's market presence by expanding content offerings and user base. One Store, established in 2016, achieved its first net income surplus last year and has seen consistent gross merchandise value growth, proactively implementing policies to support content providers.

title: Arizona House passes bill that threatens Apple and Google's app stores
summary: The Arizona House of Representatives passed a bill (HB 2005) that would compel app stores, specifically targeting Apple and Google, to allow app makers to use their own payment processing software to bypass the 15-30% fees charged. The legislation, which passed 31-29, is a setback for Apple and Google but a victory for groups like the Coalition for App Fairness. While the bill still requires Senate approval and the governor's signature, it highlights ongoing legislative efforts to regulate app store policies following criticism of their alleged anticompetitive practices.

title: KT and LG U+ buy into OneStore, the Google Play killer
summary: South Korean mobile carriers KT and LG U+ have acquired stakes in One Store, a local app market, joining SK Telecom to challenge the dominance of Google Play and Apple App Store. This strategic investment aims to bolster the domestic IT ecosystem and comes as foreign app markets face increasing criticism over their in-app payment policies. One Store saw a significant increase in transaction volume and turned a profit for the first time in 2020, benefiting from the controversy surrounding Google's 30 percent commission policy.

title: Brent Potts joined Airbnb, Inc. as Vice President of Community Support
summary: Brent Potts has joined Airbnb, Inc. as Vice President of Community Support, bringing extensive experience from Apple and Hewlett-Packard. His role will focus on enhancing support for Airbnb's global community of hosts and guests, aiming to further elevate the company's customer service standards. Potts previously led AppleCare divisions, streamlining customer support journeys for billions of customers.

title: The Parent of All Parents: Here's What Berkshire Hathaway Actually Does
summary: Berkshire Hathaway is a holding company led by Warren Buffett that owns about 70 private companies and minority stakes in 41 public stocks across various sectors. While it doesn't engage in manufacturing, it controls company shares and investment interests. Buffett is the top shareholder, and notable holdings include significant stakes in Apple and Coca-Cola, with a clear disinterest in investing in Tesla.

title: Four Investments of Nancy Pelosi and Her Husband Reveal Much
summary: The article discusses four significant investments made by House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and her husband, Paul Pelosi, on December 22. These investments include shares in AllianceBernstein (AB) and call options in Apple (AAPL), Tesla (TSLA), and Walt Disney Co (DIS), totaling between $1,751,003 and $3,500,000. Experts weigh in on the implications of these bullish trades, noting that while the Pelosis are wealthy, their investment choices align with market opportunities, although the full scope and reasoning behind the trades are not publicly known.

title: Tech-heavy Nasdaq hits correction with 10.5% fall from peak
summary: The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite has entered correction territory, falling 10.5% from its February peak, as technology shares experienced a significant sell-off. This downturn was driven by concerns over rising bond yields and inflation fears stemming from a new $1.9 trillion COVID-19 relief bill. While financial, restaurant, and travel stocks rose on reopening hopes, they couldn't offset the weight of declining big tech shares like Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) shares fell 5.34% on Monday, closing at $253.10, despite a mixed trading session for the broader market. The stock underperformed against key competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL), Adobe Inc. (ADBE), and Synopsys Inc. (SNPS), all of which also experienced declines. Trading volume for Autodesk surpassed its 50-day average.

title: CDW Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Shares of CDW Corp. (CDW) rose 0.43% to $155.14 on Monday, outperforming the S&P 500 Index despite a mixed trading session. The stock closed below its 52-week high but showed a mixed performance against competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and HP Inc. (HPQ). Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

title: Tesla loses a third of its value for the third time in a year
summary: Tesla Inc.'s stock experienced its third significant drop in a year, losing a third of its value from its January record high, primarily due to investor concerns about rising interest rates affecting high-valuation stocks. The selloff wiped off nearly $300 billion from its market capitalization, moving it behind Facebook. This decline is more pronounced than other major tech companies, and its shares have steadily fallen since announcing a bitcoin purchase, even as bitcoin’s price climbed.

title: The Sonos Roam is a small, portable smart speaker for $169
summary: Sonos has unveiled its smallest and most portable smart speaker to date, the Sonos Roam, priced at $169. This lightweight device, weighing under a pound, is designed for on-the-go use and features an IP67 rating for water and dust resistance, as well as Qi wireless charging compatibility. It boasts enhanced sound quality with Automatic TruePlay Tuning, connects via Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, and supports over 100 streaming services and multiple voice assistants.

title: WWE® SuperCard Celebrates 25 Years of “Stone Cold” Steve Austin with New Content Available Now
summary: 2K has announced new content for WWE SuperCard to celebrate the 25th anniversary of "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's WWE debut. Throughout March, players can enjoy new items and events, including a "Stone Cold" Steve Austin cardback, his iconic jean shorts, and a "Stone Cold" Fusion event starting March 9th to create Special Edition cards.

title: The 10 most innovative companies in consumer electronics
summary: This article highlights the top 10 most innovative companies in consumer electronics, showcasing how they adapted and thrived during the pandemic by releasing useful new products. It features companies like Corning, Apple, and Samsung alongside newer players such as Raspberry Pi, Tempo, and Aira. The piece details Corning's advancements in Gorilla Glass and Ceramic Shield, emphasizing their impact on device durability.

title: Video Games & Esports: Building on 2020’s Rapid Growth
summary: The video game industry experienced significant growth in 2020, driven by the pandemic, new console releases, and the rise of social gaming, with total revenue reaching $175 billion. This article explores key trends poised to continue this expansion, including the increasing impact of digital distribution, the monetization strategies of free-to-play games, the evolution of social gaming into metaverse-like experiences, and the growing prominence of mobile gaming. It also highlights the role of game engines in facilitating development for smaller studios and concludes that the industry is set for continued strong performance and investor opportunities.

title: Sonos Announces the AirPlay 2-Compatible Roam Portable Speaker
summary: Sonos has unveiled the Roam, a new portable, battery-powered speaker that supports AirPlay 2 and is designed for both indoor and outdoor use. Priced at $169, the speaker features IP67 water resistance, 10 hours of playback, and automatic switching between WiFi and Bluetooth. It also incorporates Sonos' Trueplay technology for adaptive audio and supports Sound Swap, Amazon Alexa, and Google Assistant.

title: Patent Filings Roundup: Fintiv Dooms CBMs; Canadian Process Ends IPR; New and Old NPE Campaigns Filed
summary: This article summarizes recent patent filings, noting a decrease in PTAB filings compared to district court patent litigation. It highlights key rulings, including the termination of CBM proceedings against Boom! Payments due to a Federal Circuit 101 ruling and the PTAB's denial of a petition based on Canadian service-of-process precedent. The piece also details new and continuing patent enforcement campaigns by various entities, including a significant increase in DynaIP subsidiary litigation.

title: Why a Growth Stock Fund Is Betting on Align Technology, DocuSign, and Apple
summary: The Lord Abbett Growth Leaders fund, managing $8.1 billion, differentiates itself by integrating technical analysis into its investment process, a less common approach for large mutual funds. Thomas O'Halloran, one of the fund's managers, oversees this strategy, which includes investments in companies like Align Technology, DocuSign, and Apple. The article highlights this unique methodology in growth stock investing.

title: Apple starts iPhone 12 assembly in India
summary: Apple Inc. has begun assembling its iPhone 12 in India, marking a significant ramp-up of its manufacturing operations in the country. This move, undertaken with contract manufacturer Foxconn, aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify production away from China due to ongoing trade tensions and India's efforts to boost its smartphone manufacturing sector. India's technology minister, Ravi Shankar Prasad, welcomed the development, highlighting its potential to create numerous jobs.

title: Netflix password sharing crackdown underway with new test and warning
summary: Netflix is implementing a new test to curb password sharing, requesting users to verify account ownership to ensure they are authorized to use the service. This move comes as the streaming market has become increasingly competitive, with password sharing costing providers billions in lost revenue, an amount expected to rise to $12.5 billion by 2024. Users who do not verify their account will be asked to create a new one, as Netflix aims to enforce its terms of service that limit sharing to individuals within the same household.

title: Target, The 'Apple Experience' And Training
summary: This article discusses Target's strategy for staffing its new in-store Apple kiosks with specially trained employees, emphasizing the importance of knowledgeable staff for customer satisfaction and sales. It highlights how co-branding extends to employee training, crucial for retail partnerships and reducing employee turnover, while warning against exclusive training that could undermine collaborative culture.

title: Richest people in the world: Warren Buffett is now worth $100 billion
summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has seen his net worth climb to $100 billion, making him the sixth richest person globally. His company's Class A shares are up nearly 15% this year, and his net worth has increased by approximately $13 billion. Buffett, at 90, has stated he has no plans to retire, and Berkshire Hathaway recently added Verizon, Chevron, Marsh & McLennan, and EW Scripps to its portfolio while selling off stakes in JPMorgan Chase and Pfizer.

title: Apple signs order for 'The Jet' about iconic 90s Pepsi Points Harrier promo
summary: Apple TV+ has ordered a docuseries titled "The Jet" which will delve into the notorious 1996 Pepsi Points Harrier Jet promotion. The series will explore the events surrounding John Leonard's lawsuit against PepsiCo after he attempted to redeem 7 million Pepsi Points for a Harrier Jump Jet. Directed by James Lee Hernandes and Brian Lazarte, the docuseries promises a deep dive into 90s pop culture and the legal battle that ensued.

title: The New Possible Podcast: Vortic Watch Co: Not Your Grandpa’s Pocket Watch
summary: This article introduces an episode of The New Possible podcast featuring Vortic Watch Co., a company that repurposes WWII-era American pocket watches into modern wristwatches. Founders RT Custer and Tyler Wolfe discuss their business model, which involves restoring old pocket watch mechanisms and manufacturing new cases and components using Autodesk Fusion 360, focusing on transparent and U.S.-based production. The podcast highlights their journey, including pivoting ideas, crowdfunding success, and bringing manufacturing in-house.

title: Semiconductors as a shield for Taiwan?
source: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site
source_domain: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site
summary: Taiwan's independence is significantly bolstered by its semiconductor industry, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). TSMC's critical role in the global supply chain for microprocessors provides Taiwan with a strategic advantage, deterring China from invasion due to potential economic repercussions and compelling the United States to protect its access to these vital technologies. The company's capabilities and importance make a military conflict over Taiwan highly costly for both China and the US, thus acting as a powerful "silicon shield."

title: Qualcomm Completes Acquisition of NUVIA
summary: Qualcomm Incorporated announced the completion of its acquisition of NUVIA for $1.4 billion. This acquisition aims to enhance Qualcomm's CPU roadmap, integrating next-generation CPUs across various products including smartphones, laptops, and automotive systems. The first Qualcomm Snapdragon platforms featuring these new internally designed CPUs are expected to sample in the second half of 2022.

title: Nokia to cut up to 10,000 jobs worldwide
summary: Nokia plans to cut between 5,000 and 10,000 jobs globally over the next two years to reduce costs by €600m (£518m). The telecoms giant aims to invest in 5G, cloud computing, and digital infrastructure research to improve its performance against rivals. Job cuts are expected across various regions, with about 96 roles in the UK and 300 in Finland under threat.

title: Apple v. Iancu: Oral Argument on Motion to Dismiss and Plaintiff Summary Judgment Motion, PTAB Litigation Blog
summary: This article discusses the oral argument in the case of Apple v. Iancu, focusing on motions to dismiss and for summary judgment. The lawsuit, filed by major tech companies, challenges the PTAB's "Fintiv" rule, which allows for discretionary denial of patent institution petitions when parallel litigation exists. The plaintiffs argue the rule exceeds statutory authority, is arbitrary and capricious, and was adopted without proper rulemaking procedures.

title: Laptop Bench Test: Dell Latitude 5410 – An upgrade in mediocrity?
summary: This article reviews the Dell Latitude 5410 laptop, evaluating if it offers significant upgrades over its predecessor or remains a mediocre device. The review details specifications, performance benchmarks (Cinebench, GeekBench), and its suitability for corporate use, concluding it's a solid "fleet laptop" for business tasks rather than a high-performance personal device.

title: Apple’s $4.7 billion Green Bond spend is helping to create 1.2 gigawatts of clean power
summary: Apple has invested $4.7 billion from its Green Bonds to fund 17 projects that will generate 1.2 gigawatts of clean energy globally and avoid 921,000 metric tons of carbon emissions annually. These projects include solar farms in Nevada and Virginia, a wind farm in Illinois, and large onshore wind turbines in Denmark. Apple aims to be carbon neutral across its entire business, manufacturing supply chain, and product life cycle by 2030, building on its already carbon-neutral global corporate operations.

title: At $2.08 Trillion, Apple Is Bigger Than These Things
summary: As of March 15, 2021, Apple Inc. (AAPL) boasts a market capitalization of $2.08 trillion, making it the world's largest public company. This staggering figure surpasses the GDP of most countries, the total market value of many global stock exchanges, the U.S. budget deficit, and the inflation-adjusted costs of multiple major U.S. wars. It also exceeds the combined net worth of the world's 18 richest people.

title: “Think twice about whose money you take”: 21 tips for aspiring unicorns
summary: Lior Handelsman, co-founder of SolarEdge and now with Grove Ventures, shares 21 essential tips for aspiring entrepreneurs, drawn from his experiences building SolarEdge into a billion-dollar company and investing in startups. His advice covers critical aspects like aiming big, securing funding, managing valuations, team dynamics, and the psychological demands of entrepreneurship.

title: Clarivate celebrates BD’s culture of innovation
summary: Clarivate has named BD to its Top 100 Global Innovators 2021 list for the sixth time, recognizing the company's significant investment in innovation and its commitment to protecting intellectual property. BD invests over a billion dollars annually in innovation, contributing to advancements in medical technology, and holds approximately 27,000 active, issued patents worldwide. The recognition highlights BD's ongoing efforts to advance global health through pioneering solutions and a robust intellectual property portfolio.

title: Coral South FLNG Project, Rovuma Basin, Mozambique
summary: The Coral South FLNG Project involves the development of the Coral Sul floating liquefied natural gas (FLNG) facility in the Coral South field, located in Area 4 of the Rovuma Basin off Mozambique. Operated by Eni, the project aims to monetize gas reserves and is expected to start production in 2022. The article details the project's financing, contractors, and the technical specifications of the FLNG unit.

title: How Texas is luring big businesses and billionaires away from California
summary: Texas is experiencing a significant economic boom, attracting numerous companies and wealthy individuals from California, including major tech firms like Oracle, Tesla, and Apple. This migration is fueled by factors prompting a "tremendous increase" in relocations and expansions, making Texas a burgeoning business hub. The article explores the reasons behind this trend and its potential implications for the state's future.

title: 3 Mid-Cap Global Battery Manufacturer Stocks
summary: This article examines three mid-cap global battery manufacturers: Varta AG (Germany), EnerSys (USA), and Simplo Technology (Taiwan), as potential investment opportunities given the growing demand for lithium-ion batteries. It details each company's business model, revenue streams, and market position, highlighting both their strengths and potential risks. The piece concludes by suggesting that foreign battery stocks can offer diversification for investors looking beyond domestic options.

title: Scammers trick Android users into installing fake, malware-laden Clubhouse app
summary: Scammers are exploiting the demand for the audio-based social app Clubhouse among Android users by distributing a fake, malware-laden version of the app. This fraudulent Android app, discovered by ESET researchers, contains a Trojan virus called "BlackRock" that can steal login information from over 458 online services, including financial apps, social media, and cryptocurrency exchanges, and can also intercept text messages, bypassing two-factor authentication. Experts warn that the absence of an official Android version of Clubhouse creates a significant vulnerability that cybercriminals are eager to exploit, making an official Android app the ultimate solution.

title: Intel to Spend $20 Billion on 2 New Chip Factories in Arizona. (Published 2021)
summary: Intel's new CEO, Patrick Gelsinger, announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip factories in Arizona. This move signifies Intel's commitment to doubling down on chip manufacturing in the U.S. and Europe, aiming to become a major manufacturer for other companies, addressing concerns about component shortages and reliance on Asian factories. The initiative also seeks to regain Intel's footing in the competitive chip production market, where it had previously fallen behind competitors like TSMC and Samsung.

title: Lucid Motors Deepens Expertise with Executive Team Additions and Promotions
summary: Lucid Motors has strengthened its executive team with significant additions and promotions as it prepares for customer deliveries of its Lucid Air EV in the second half of 2021. The company appointed Michael Bell as Senior VP of Digital, Achim Pantfoerder as VP of Program Management, and Nicolas Minbiole as VP of Global Quality. Additionally, Eric Bach was promoted to Senior VP – Product and Chief Engineer, and Derek Jenkins to Senior VP – Design and Brand, demonstrating Lucid's commitment to quality and innovation.

title: Lumentum raises Coherent buyout offer to $7 bln
summary: Optical fiber firm Lumentum Holdings Inc (LITE) has increased its buyout offer for Coherent Inc (COHR) to approximately $7 billion, intensifying a three-way bidding war for the laser manufacturer. This new offer surpasses a previous bid from II-VI Inc (IIVI) and involves a combination of cash and Lumentum shares for Coherent shareholders. The battle for Coherent began after Lumentum initially offered $5.7 billion, drawing rival bids from MKS Instruments and II-VI.

title: SEC is investigating Japanese investment giant SoftBank for market manipulation
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is investigating SoftBank Group Corp. for alleged market manipulation. The investigation reportedly concerns SoftBank's role in stoking a tech stock rally in August through massive call option purchases, which generated a reported $4 billion profit. While the exact grounds for the investigation are not fully disclosed, it is tied to allegations of stock manipulation.

title: Intel to spend $20 billion on U.S. chip plants as CEO challenges Asia dominance
summary: Intel announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip manufacturing plants in Arizona, aiming to expand its advanced chip production and open its factories to outside customers. This move by CEO Pat Gelsinger directly challenges the dominance of TSMC and Samsung in the advanced chip market and seeks to shift the technological balance of power back to the U.S. and Europe. The company expects $72 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings per share of $4.55 for 2021, and the new strategy is intended to restore Intel's reputation and create 3,000 permanent jobs.

title: In $20bn push, Intel hopes to make US chip-making great again
summary: Intel announced a significant expansion of its advanced chip manufacturing capacity with a $20bn investment to build two factories in Arizona, aiming to regain its market leadership and challenge rivals TSMC and Samsung. CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy includes opening Intel's plants to outside customers in a "foundry" business model and focusing on cutting-edge computing chip manufacturing. This move is also a response to US and European concerns about the concentration of chipmaking in Asia and seeks to boost domestic production.

title: Newfound Popularity of Thematic ETFs
summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in baskets of stocks benefiting from emerging trends, technology, or evolving economic landscapes, have seen a significant surge in popularity since October 2020, with AUM growing by 132%. Despite this growth, the article highlights potential risks associated with these ETFs, including the reversal of investor enthusiasm impacting fund flow and performance, the concentrated holdings of individual stocks within the ETFs, and the potential for misleading diversification that may dilute thematic exposure and justify higher fees. Investors are advised against chasing "hot" ETFs without understanding their underlying holdings and potential risks.

title: Stryker, Zimmer make data and analytics a top priority as robotic procedures ramp up
summary: Stryker and Zimmer Biomet are highly prioritizing data and analytics to enhance their robotic surgical procedures and improve patient outcomes. Stryker's Mako system uses data from past procedures to inform implant positioning, while Zimmer Biomet partners with Apple to collect post-operative patient data via the mymobility app and Apple Watch. Both companies are also investing in smart implants and predictive analytics to further refine surgical approaches and patient care.

title: Toyota VS Apple: The Age of EV Battles Unlatches the Door to Smart Cars
summary: The electric vehicle (EV) market is seeing an influx of traditional automakers and tech giants like Apple and Baidu, creating a new battleground for smart cars. Toyota, the world's leading automaker, is positioning itself with AI-based software development and strategic partnerships to compete against these tech companies, focusing on a "making friends" approach similar to Google's Android strategy. While Apple boasts significant financial and brand power, Toyota believes its collaborative ecosystem for automotive technology gives it an edge in the complex and responsibility-heavy auto industry.

title: Invesco unveils Nasdaq ‘next generation’ ETF
summary: Invesco has launched two new ETFs, the Invesco NASDAQ Next Generation 100 UCITS ETF (EQJS) and the Invesco Nasdaq-100 Swap UCITS ETF (EQQS), expanding its tech ETF range. EQJS tracks the Nasdaq Next Generation 100 index, mirroring a US-listed strategy, while EQQS provides synthetic exposure to the Nasdaq 100, designed to mitigate withholding tax on US equity dividends. These new offerings are listed on the LSE with competitive total expense ratios.

title: The China challenge: Why Intel is too strategic to fail
summary: This article analyzes Intel's strategic shift to IDM 2.0 under CEO Pat Gelsinger, focusing on the company's plan to leverage internal manufacturing, external foundries, and a new Intel Foundry Services division. It examines the market changes impacting Intel, the critical threat from China's semiconductor ambitions, and the need for significant partnerships, including with IBM and the U.S. government, for Intel to regain its leadership and combat the China challenge. The authors argue that despite positive steps, Intel needs even more substantial changes, possibly a joint venture with a volume leader like Samsung, to succeed.

title: Apple Encourages Staff to Get Vaccinated, Offers Paid Time Off
summary: Apple is encouraging its employees to get vaccinated against Covid-19 by offering paid time off for vaccine appointments and paid sick leave for any side effects experienced. However, the company is not providing vaccination shots itself and does not have direct access to vaccines for its staff. This initiative aims to support employee health and safety while acknowledging vaccine availability is outside their direct control.

title: Arm’s v9 Architecture Explains Why Nvidia Needs To Buy It
summary: This article explores why Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings, initially questioned, makes strategic sense following Arm's v9 architecture announcement. The v9 architecture's focus on integrating vector math, matrix math, and digital signal processing into CPUs, alongside new security features and a "Realms" memory partitioning system, suggests a future where specialized, highly integrated compute on-device is paramount. Nvidia's move to acquire Arm is positioned as a bet on this evolving compute landscape, where a single, malleable architecture can support a vast array of devices from IoT to datacenters, potentially limiting the expansion of hybrid CPU-GPU models for certain workloads.

title: TSMC says plans to invest $100 billion over next 3 years to meet chip demand
summary: TSMC announced plans to invest $100 billion over the next three years to increase chipmaking capacity, responding to surging demand fueled by 5G, high-performance computing, and pandemic-accelerated digitalization. This strategic move follows Intel's recent $20 billion expansion plan and aims to address a global semiconductor shortage impacting various industries from automotive to consumer electronics. The company's shares rose following the announcement, highlighting investor confidence in its growth trajectory.

title: moomoo – 1 Free Apple share and commission-free trading
summary: The article introduces Futu Singapore's moomoo trading platform, highlighting its features like in-app announcement alerts, detailed shareholder information, and access to multiple markets with low fees. It also details a promotional offer for new users, including a free Apple share and commission-free trading, alongside emphasizing the benefits of discount brokers for Singaporean investors.

title: Apple powers ahead in new renewable energy solutions with over 110 suppliers
summary: Apple announced that over 110 of its manufacturing partners are transitioning to 100 percent renewable energy for their Apple production, aiming for nearly 8 gigawatts of clean energy. These commitments are projected to prevent over 15 million metric tons of CO2e annually. Apple is also investing in novel energy storage solutions and working towards becoming carbon neutral across its entire business, manufacturing supply chain, and product life cycle by 2030.

title: Apple plans battery-based solar storage project in South County.
summary: Apple Inc. announced plans to build a large battery-based solar energy storage facility in South County, Monterey County, near its existing California Flats solar installation. This project aims to store 240 megawatt-hours of energy, enough to power over 7,000 homes for a day, addressing the intermittency of solar power and supporting grid stabilization. Apple's vice president of environment, Lisa Jackson, highlighted the project's potential as a model for other companies in transitioning to clean energy.

title: Sony undergoes 1st name change in 6 decades to Sony Group
summary: Sony Corp. has rebranded itself as Sony Group Corp., marking its first name change in over six decades. This change reflects the company's shift towards functioning as a conglomerate, focusing on diverse areas like financial services, gaming, and entertainment, rather than solely electronics. The move aims to maximize synergies among its various group companies and foster growth in new sectors.

title: MicroVision (MVIS) Stock Forecast: Will Microsoft Buy the Company?
summary: MicroVision (MVIS) stock surged over 50 percent on March 31, 2021, driven by rumors of potential acquisition interest from tech giants like Apple and Microsoft, especially after Microsoft secured a $22 billion contract with the U.S. Army for augmented reality headsets, in which MicroVision is believed to be a supplier. Despite this growth, MVIS remains a speculative investment due to its low revenue, lack of analyst coverage, and high volatility, with its CEO acknowledging the company is "up for sale."

title: Deluxe Acquires Sony Electronics’ New Media Solutions Division
summary: Deluxe has acquired Sony New Media Solutions, a division of Sony Electronics, to enhance its digital media supply chain capabilities for studios and streaming services. The acquisition is expected to strengthen Deluxe's technology and workflows, providing NMS customers with full access to Deluxe's integrated supply chain solutions, including localization and distribution. This deal follows Deluxe's earlier acquisition of Sundog Media Toolkit, further expanding its cloud automation resources.

title: Gartner Forecasts Global Devices Installed Base to Reach 6.2 Billion Units in 2021
summary: Gartner forecasts the global installed base of devices (PCs, tablets, and mobile phones) will reach 6.2 billion units in 2021, driven by permanent changes in device usage patterns due to the pandemic, including remote and hybrid work. The number of laptops and tablets is expected to increase significantly, while smartphone market confidence returns with a projected 1% increase in installed base in 2021. The article highlights the increasing complexity for IT services due to a mix of company-owned and personal devices and a rising demand for connected 4G/5G devices.

title: Bilibili’s $123 million investment in TapTap plans to revolutionize game distribution in China
summary: Chinese video-sharing website Bilibili has invested $123 million in TapTap, aiming to disrupt traditional game distribution channels in China. TapTap stands out by not charging a commission on in-app purchases, a direct challenge to the commission models of major app stores like Huawei, Xiaomi, Google, and Apple. This move allows Bilibili to diversify its revenue and directly compete with giants like Tencent, Huawei, and Xiaomi in the highly competitive Chinese gaming market.

title: Apple adding 240MWh of Tesla battery storage at California solar PV facility
summary: Apple is building a 240MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) using Tesla Megapacks at its existing 280MW California Flats solar PV plant in Monterey County. This system will support Apple's 130MW portion of the solar facility, storing excess daytime energy for later use. The initiative is part of Apple's broader commitment to power its operations with 100% renewable energy and achieve carbon neutrality by 2030, a goal also adopted by over 110 of its supply chain partners.

title: Micron and Western Digital Each Exploring Ways to Buy Kioxia
summary: Micron Technology and Western Digital are reportedly exploring separate deals to acquire Kioxia, a move that could significantly reshape the NAND memory market. Kioxia, majority-owned by Bain Capital, was valued at $16 billion before postponing its IPO due to market uncertainties. A successful acquisition by either company could lead to significant market consolidation, with Western Digital potentially becoming the largest flash memory maker.

title: OLB Group Adds QR Code Acceptance to Its Gateway
summary: OLB Group has integrated dynamic QR code acceptance into its cloud-based OmniSoft platform and SecurePay gateway to meet the demand for contactless payment options. This feature allows merchants, especially small and mobile businesses like tradesmen, to accept various digital payments including Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cryptocurrency, using just their phone. The company continues to expand its payment processing capabilities, having recently added social media app payments and cryptocurrency, with plans for PayPal wallet support.

title: Judge dismisses Koss Corp. patent suit against Skullcandy. Cases vs. Apple, Bose continue.
summary: A judge has dismissed Koss Corp.'s patent-infringement lawsuit against Skullcandy Inc. due to a geographic-venue issue. However, Koss Corp. is continuing its patent cases against four other companies, including Apple Inc. This development comes as Koss Corp. seeks to protect its intellectual property in the headphones market.

title: Delivery service startup Enjoy to go public via SPAC in a $1.6B deal
summary: Enjoy Technology Inc., a delivery service startup, is set to go public through a merger with the SPAC Marquee Raine Acquisition Corp. in a deal valued at $1.6 billion. Founded by former Apple executive Ron Johnson, Enjoy differentiates itself by having employees hand-deliver and set up electronic devices for consumers, providing an hour of installation and advice. The company has raised $230 million in venture capital and partners with notable firms like Apple, AT&T, and BT Group.

title: Asian-American business leaders rally against wave of hatred: ‘We don’t deserve to live in fear in our own country’
summary: Asian-American business leaders are speaking out against increased violence and animosity towards their communities during the COVID-19 pandemic. Many Asian Americans, who form a significant portion of Silicon Valley's tech industry, are using their platforms to voice their concerns. The article highlights an incident where Debby Soo, CEO of OpenTable, experienced discrimination in a grocery store, illustrating the pervasive nature of this prejudice.

title: Caledonia Mining ups dividend again, says Central Shaft is fully operational
summary: Caledonia Mining Corporation PLC increased its quarterly dividend for the fifth time in 18 months, raising it to 12 US cents. This decision stems from the anticipated combination of rising production and declining capital investment as their six-year investment program at Blanket mine concludes. Furthermore, the company announced that its new Central Shaft at the mine, the deepest gold mine shaft in Zimbabwe, is now fully operational, which is expected to resolve hoisting constraints and facilitate the expansion of mine capacity to target 80,000 ounces of gold production per year.

title: Is This the Future of Face Masks? (Published 2021)
summary: Will.i.am and Honeywell have collaborated to launch the "Xupermask," a smart face mask priced at $299. This device combines aspects of a traditional mask with noise-canceling headphones, LED lights, and Bluetooth, aiming to create a new category of wearable technology. The design, by Hollywood costume designer Jose Fernandez, is described as futuristic and will be sold through direct-to-consumer drops.

title: Lovesac opening a showroom at Shops of Saddle Creek in Germantown: Here's what to expect
summary: Lovesac, known for its adaptable and eco-friendly couches called "Sactionals," is opening a 1,989-square-foot showroom at the Shops of Saddle Creek South in Germantown by the end of 2021. This will be the company's first location in the Memphis area and its second in Tennessee. The showroom aims to allow shoppers to experience Lovesac’s reconfigurable furniture and highlight its commitment to reducing furniture waste through durable products.

title: Meet the highest paid CEO in S&P 500, Paycom’s $211M man
summary: Chad Richison, the founder and CEO of Paycom Software Inc., was awarded compensation valued at $211 million in 2020, making him the highest-paid CEO in the S&P 500 based on disclosures so far. This compensation package, centered on restricted shares, could be worth over $2 billion if Paycom's stock price significantly increases, demonstrating a performance-based alignment with shareholder interests. Richison's equity award is contingent on the company's share price more than doubling and reaching specific targets within the next decade.

title: Targeting Overseas Tax Shelters (Published 2021)
summary: This article discusses how Bristol Myers Squibb used offshore tax shelters in Ireland and other locations to avoid paying an estimated $1.4 billion in U.S. federal income taxes. The Biden administration plans to crack down on these practices by proposing a global minimum corporate tax rate of 21% to fund infrastructure projects. The article explores the history of such tax avoidance schemes, the motivations behind them, and the challenges the Biden administration faces in implementing its new tax policies.

title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon: Fintech is an 'enormous competitive' threat to banks
summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has identified fintech companies, including Big Tech firms like Amazon and Apple, as a significant competitive threat to traditional banks. He notes that fintechs are rapidly developing user-friendly digital banking products across loans, payments, and investing, leading to banks playing a smaller role in the financial system. Dimon warns of emerging issues like shadow banking and cryptocurrency regulation, advocating for government oversight to create a level playing field for all financial institutions.

title: JPMorgan’s Chief Sees a Boom Coming (Published 2021)
summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon predicts a potential economic boom extending into 2023 driven by excess savings, deficit spending, infrastructure plans, and vaccine-induced euphoria, though he warns of potential inflation and aggressive interest rate hikes. He also addresses concerns about the growing influence of shadow banking and big tech in finance, China's perception of American decline, and the complexities of addressing climate change. Dimon's annual letter also touched on remote work reducing office space needs, cybersecurity spending, and specific tax loopholes he believes should be closed.
authors: Andrew Ross Sorkin Jason Karaian Sarah Kessler Michael J. de la Merced Lauren Hirsch and Ephrat Livni

title: Israeli digital forensics startup Cellebrite to go public via SPAC in $2.4B deal
summary: Cellebrite, an Israeli digital forensics startup known for phone hacking, is set to go public through a SPAC merger with TWC Tech Holdings II Corp. The deal values the company at approximately $2.4 billion and will provide $580 million in cash, including a $300 million private investment. The combined company will be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "CLBT."

title: Paycom boss Chad Richison's net worth: Inside highest paid CEO's $211M package and philanthropy work
summary: Chad Richison, Paycom's founder and CEO, became the highest-paid CEO in the S&P 500 in 2020 with a compensation package worth over $200 million. This package includes 1.61 million restricted shares tied to aggressive performance goals, potentially adding billions to his net worth if Paycom's share price significantly increases. Richison, with a net worth of around $2.9 billion, is also known for his philanthropic efforts, having signed the Giving Pledge and founded the Green Shoe Foundation.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | P&G Worked With China Trade Group on Tech to Sidestep Apple Privacy Rules
summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) collaborated with a Chinese trade group on a new technology, called CAID, to gather iPhone data for targeted ads. This effort aims to bypass Apple's new iOS 14.5 privacy features, which require user permission for app tracking. The technique, using device fingerprinting, would allow advertisers to continue tracking users despite Apple's policies, which explicitly prohibit such methods.
authors: Sharon Terlep|Tim Higgins and Patience Haggin

title: T-Mobile ups 5G ante with home broadband, free phone upgrades
summary: T-Mobile has launched its "5G For All" campaign, which includes free 5G phone upgrades and the country's first widely available wireless broadband home internet service. The phone upgrades allow customers to trade in any working smartphone for a new Samsung A32 5G or receive discounts on an iPhone 12 with a 24-month postpaid service agreement. Additionally, T-Mobile's new Home Internet service offers speeds averaging 100 Mbps for $60/month and is expected to be available to 30 million US households, including many in rural areas, providing a crucial alternative to traditional wired internet.

title: Netflix (NFLX) Gets Exclusive Deal to Stream Sony Picture Movies
summary: Netflix has secured an exclusive five-year licensing deal with Sony Pictures Entertainment, granting it the right to stream Sony's theatrical releases starting in 2022. This agreement will bring major upcoming films and popular franchises like *Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse* and *Jumanji* to Netflix, along with a first-look agreement for Sony's direct-to-streaming original movies. The move aims to bolster Netflix's content library amidst increasing competition in the streaming market.

title: P&G reportedly testing Chinese workaround to Apple’s privacy changes
summary: Procter & Gamble is participating in a test in China of a method to collect iPhone data for ad targeting, as a potential way to circumvent Apple's new privacy features. This initiative, developed with the China Advertising Association, uses "device fingerprinting" technology (CAID) to track online consumers, which violates Apple's app rules. As a major advertiser, P&G is seeking alternative audience tracking methods to maintain ad efficiency given that many consumers are expected to opt out of device tracking.

title: State Street SPDR道瓊全球龍頭ETF-DGT-ETF相關分析
summary: This article provides an analysis of the State Street SPDR Dow Jones Global Titans ETF (DGT). It lists various ETFs that have a negative correlation with DGT, detailing their tracking indices and correlation coefficients. The data presented aims to help investors understand the related performance and risk factors associated with this global ETF.

title: Tech Innovation vs. Legislative Reform: Apple’s Response to the TCJA
summary: This paper examines the impact of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) on global corporate structures, using Apple as a case study. It concludes with an exploration of Apple’s use of assets repatriated under the TCJA. The paper details how Apple and similar multinational enterprises historically structured their overseas operations to minimize tax liability and how the TCJA aimed to incentivize repatriation of these offshore profits.

title: Eli Lilly and Company hires Apple's retail information technology chief
summary: Eli Lilly and Company has hired Diogo Rau, Apple's former chief of information technology for online and in-store retail, as its new senior vice president and chief information and digital officer. Rau will lead Lilly's initiatives in data use, analytics, and machine learning, succeeding Aarti Shah. His previous experience at Apple included developing technologies for the Apple Online Store and retail stores.

title: Eli Lilly, riding pharma's rising digital wave, drafts Apple exec to replace Shah as CDO
summary: Eli Lilly has appointed Diogo Rau, a former Apple executive, as its new chief digital and information officer, replacing the retiring Aarti Shah. This move reflects a growing trend in the pharmaceutical industry to recruit consumer-sector digital leaders, particularly as the pandemic has accelerated digital adoption. The article highlights similar hires by other major pharma companies like Sanofi, GlaxoSmithKline, and Novartis, while also noting some instances where such executives have returned to the consumer digital space.

title: Manager Question of the Month: How Active Fund Managers Cope With Index Concentration
summary: This article discusses the challenges active fund managers face due to increasing index concentration, particularly in U.S. large-cap growth benchmarks dominated by a few technology companies. It explores how managers adapt their strategies, including considering a switch from diversified to nondiversified funds, and the trade-offs involved in maintaining performance against highly concentrated indexes while adhering to SEC diversification rules. The piece highlights various approaches taken by investment firms like T. Rowe Price and Wellington Management.

title: For Two Vanguard ETFs, the Growth vs. Value Battle Roars On
summary: The article highlights the ongoing "growth vs. value" debate by comparing two Vanguard ETFs: VUG (Growth) and VTV (Value). While VTV has shown stronger short-term performance with a significant year-to-date gain, VUG has historically outperformed VTV over longer periods, driven by its heavy tech holdings. The piece discusses the investment approaches and expense ratios of both funds.

title: Taiwan's Pegatron to build Tesla parts plant in Texas - report
summary: Taiwanese manufacturer Pegatron, a key supplier for Apple Inc., has reportedly selected a site in El Paso, Texas, to construct a plant that will produce components for Tesla Inc. The company stated that specific details regarding its overseas plant investments are still in the planning stages, though it had previously announced intentions to build a U.S. factory. Pegatron views electric vehicles as a significant growth area for its business.

title: Google, Adobe and Samsung top best company culture list for 2021
summary: Google, Adobe, and Samsung have topped Comparably's 2021 Best Global Company Culture list. Google led with an 85% employee approval rating, while Adobe followed closely with 96%, and Samsung was the only Asian company in the top 50. The rankings were based on anonymous employee feedback across 20 workplace culture categories, factoring in companies' handling of the pandemic and employee well-being.

title: Apple and partners launch first-ever $200 million Restore Fund to accelerate natural solutions to climate change
summary: Apple has launched a $200 million Restore Fund with Conservation International and Goldman Sachs, aimed at investing in forestry projects to remove carbon from the atmosphere while generating financial returns. This initiative is part of Apple's broader goal to become carbon neutral across its entire value chain by 2030, specifically targeting the remaining 25% of its emissions. The fund will prioritize projects that meet strict environmental and social standards, build on Apple's existing forestry conservation efforts, and involve partnerships in areas like Kenya and Colombia to restore degraded savannas and mangrove forests.

title: Fueling the connected car economy
summary: This article by J.P. Morgan explores how connected cars are transforming into mobile payment sources and data hubs, creating new business models. It highlights five key areas: digital showrooms, cars as mobile wallets, developing marketplaces, data monetization, and enabling shared mobility services. The piece emphasizes the need for companies across various industries to develop holistic strategies to adapt to this evolving connected car economy.

title: The pandemic may have permanently altered Apple’s path
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly impacted Apple's business by reigniting interest in its less prominent product categories, such as iPads and Macs. This shift away from sole reliance on iPhone revenue is expected to continue. The company is now well-positioned to benefit from increased consumer spending as the economy recovers.

title: Adobe co-founder, Los Altos resident Charles ‘Chuck’ Geschke dies
summary: Charles M. “Chuck” Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Systems and Los Altos resident, has died at age 81. Geschke co-founded Adobe with John Warnock in 1982, creating PostScript which revolutionized desktop publishing, and the company later expanded significantly into various software areas. He was also notable for his kidnapping in 1992 and his extensive philanthropic work and awards recognizing his contributions to technology and education.

title: An activist investor calls for a slate of new directors at apparel retailer Genesco
summary: Activist investor Legion Partners is calling for a slate of seven new directors to be appointed to Genesco's eight-person board, aiming to enhance shareholder value through strategies like de-conglomeratizing and cutting overhead. This move marks Legion's second attempt at activism with Genesco, after a previous campaign in 2018 where the investor ultimately sold its position. Despite a strong plan, the author questions Legion's long-term commitment given their past divestment and the absence of a Legion principal on the proposed board slate, suggesting that a smaller board change might be more appropriate.

title: AutoX Selects Arbe's 4D Imaging Radar Platform for Level 4 Autonomous Vehicles
summary: Arbe announced that AutoX has selected its 4D Imaging Radar Platform for integration into 400,000 Level 4 autonomous vehicles, including RoboTaxis, over the next five years. This partnership aims to enhance safety and performance by utilizing Arbe's ultra-high resolution radar systems, which offer 100 times more detail than other market radars. The collaboration emphasizes an effort to elevate safety standards for L4 autonomous vehicles, particularly in dense urban environments like those in China.

title: Michele Romanow’s Clearbanc hits unicorn status, raising US$100-million at nearly US$2-billion valuation
summary: Michele Romanow's company, Clear Finance Technology Corp., has achieved unicorn status, raising US$100-million in equity at a valuation of close to US$2-billion. The company, rebranding as Clearco, provides cash advances to online sellers and has seen significant growth during the pandemic, benefiting from the surge in online orders. It plans to tap larger sources of off-balance sheet debt financing to fuel future expansion, including a new US$250-million debt facility from Credigy Ltd.

title: Chuck Geschke, Father of Desktop Publishing, Dies at 81 (Published 2021)
summary: Chuck Geschke, co-founder of Adobe and a pioneer in desktop publishing, has died at 81. He and John Warnock developed PostScript, a crucial technology that enabled digital documents to be sent to printers, revolutionizing how businesses and individuals could print their own materials. Their innovation at Adobe, including the PDF, significantly influenced the development of personal computing and digital document sharing.

title: Logitech Combo Touch Available for New iPad Pro 12.9” (5th generation) and iPad Pro 11” (3rd generation)
summary: Logitech announced the availability of its Combo Touch for the new iPad Pro 11” (3rd generation) and soon for the iPad Pro 12.9” (5th generation). This versatile case features a detachable keyboard and an integrated, large click-anywhere trackpad, connecting via the Smart Connector. It supports four use modes—typing, viewing, sketching, and reading—and offers protection for the iPad.

title: Netflix’s Dominance Starts to Slow as Rivals Gain (Published 2021)
summary: Netflix reported a slowdown in subscriber growth for the first quarter of 2021, adding only four million new customers compared to a forecast of six million. This modest growth, coupled with a decline in its share of global demand for original programming, indicates that competitors like Disney+, HBO Max, and Paramount+ are beginning to challenge Netflix's long-standing market dominance. The company anticipates adding even fewer subscribers in the current quarter.

title: iMac features all-new design in vibrant colors, M1 chip, and 4.5K Retina display
summary: Apple has introduced an all-new iMac with a compact, thin design enabled by the M1 chip, featuring vibrant colors, a 24-inch 4.5K Retina display, and the best camera, mics, and speakers ever in a Mac. It also brings Touch ID to the iMac for the first time, enhancing security and user convenience. The new iMac, powered by M1 and macOS Big Sur, promises significantly faster performance for everyday tasks and demanding workloads, building on Apple's transition to Apple silicon.

title: Apple supplier Quanta says no business impact from ransomware attack
summary: Apple Inc. supplier Quanta Computer Inc. announced that a recent ransomware attack would not significantly impact its business operations. This statement comes after reports that a Russian hacker group, REvil, claimed to have accessed confidential design files, possibly related to an Apple MacBook, and demanded a $50 million ransom from Quanta, also asking Apple to pay. Quanta is currently collaborating with law enforcement and data protection authorities regarding the incident.

title: Apple Launches Powerful New iPad, ‘Air Tags’ to Track Lost Items
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: Apple announced new products including a powerful iPad Pro with the M1 chip, new iMac computers, and AirTags for tracking lost personal items. The new iPad Pro aims to meet increased demand for work-from-home solutions, while AirTags offer a secure way to locate misplaced objects using the "Find My" network. Apple also unveiled a new podcast subscription service.

title: Is Apple a Growth Stock, a Value Stock, or Both?
summary: This article discusses whether Apple (AAPL) is a growth stock, a value stock, or both, examining its classification within Morningstar's Style Box and its historical journey. It highlights how Apple's style assignment impacts investor portfolios and fund performance, especially for index funds and active managers. The authors explain Morningstar's multi-factor style model, which places Apple in the "large core" segment due to its mixed characteristics, and contrast this with other index providers.

title: Charles Geschke (1939–2021), co-founder of Adobe
summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe, passed away on April 16, 2021, at the age of 81. He was instrumental in developing PostScript, which led to the first desktop publishing software and revolutionized the printing industry. Geschke, who served as Adobe's COO and president, was also known for his focus on the creativity of individuals within the company.

title: The Federal Circuit Provides New Guidance for Patent Licensees Wishing to Challenge the Licensed Patent’s Validity
summary: The Federal Circuit's decision in Apple Inc. v. Qualcomm Incorporated provides new guidance for patent licensees challenging patent validity, emphasizing the need to prove a specific, ascertainable injury directly impacting payment obligations. The court ruled that ongoing payment obligations under a broad licensing agreement are insufficient for standing without evidence that invalidating the challenged patent would change those obligations. Apple’s arguments for standing were rejected, clarifying that a direct contractual impact, not speculative future threats or estoppel effects, is required for Article III standing.
authors: Brad M. Scheller|Peter J. Cuomo|Monique Winters Macek|Mark D. Hammond

title: Encrypted chat app Signal alleges flaws in Cellebrite equipment
summary: Encrypted messaging app Signal has claimed that products sold by Israeli surveillance provider Cellebrite, used by law enforcement, contain security flaws that could be exploited. Signal's creator, Moxie Marlinspike, alleged that Cellebrite's software security is lacking and that he found snippets of Apple Inc. code within Cellebrite's software, potentially posing legal risks. These allegations come as Cellebrite prepares to go public through a merger, valuing the combined company at approximately $2.4 billion.

title: Signal messaging app reveals flaws in Israeli firm Cellebrite's equipment
summary: Encrypted chat app Signal has alleged that equipment sold by Israeli surveillance provider Cellebrite to law enforcement can be easily sabotaged. Signal's founder, Moxie Marlinspike, claimed to have found security flaws in Cellebrite's software after examining their equipment, making it vulnerable to specially crafted files. These allegations come as Cellebrite prepares to go public through a SPAC merger, valuing the combined company at around $2.4 billion.

title: Here's how much money you'd have if you bought $1,000 worth of Netflix stock in 2011
summary: This article examines the significant return on investment for Netflix stock over the past decade. A $1,000 investment in Netflix in 2011 would have grown to over $15,000 by April 2021, far outpacing the S&P 500, Google, and Apple. Despite recent subscriber growth concerns, analysts believe Netflix is well-positioned for the long term due to its first-mover advantage and extensive content investments, even amidst increasing competition from other streaming services.

title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
summary: The article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a way for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000 Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S., performing well with a 60% increase in the past year, driven by major tech companies. It caters to investors seeking long-term growth opportunities and capital appreciation over dividends.

title: Double down on health and fitness with Garmin’s Venu 2 Series GPS smartwatch
summary: Garmin announced the Venu 2 and Venu 2S GPS smartwatches, designed to help users achieve a healthier lifestyle with new features like fitness age, advanced sleep tracking with sleep score, and numerous health monitoring tools. The Venu 2 series offers over 25 built-in sports apps, enhanced battery life, and smart features for daily convenience. Available in two sizes, these smartwatches are priced at $399.99 and aim to provide deeper insights into overall wellness.

title: The Glazers
summary: This article chronicles the 15-year ownership of Manchester United by the Glazer family, delving into the controversial leveraged buyout in 2005 and its impact on the club. It examines their commercial strategies, financial dealings including debt and dividends, and the persistent fan protests, while also exploring the family's approach to the business of sport, their personal involvement, and the challenges of a potential sale. The piece highlights the significant financial returns the Glazers have received and how their business model transformed Manchester United's commercial operations.
authors: Laurie Whitwell|Daniel Taylor|Matt Slater|Adam Crafton|Oliver Kay

title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
summary: This article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a means for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000® Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S. The ETF has shown significant growth over the past year, driven by prominent technology companies.

title: The five best arguments that Apple’s App Store is a monopoly
summary: During a Senate Judiciary Antitrust Subcommittee hearing, Apple and Google faced scrutiny over their app store practices, particularly Apple's App Store, which was accused of monopolistic behavior. Lawmakers and app developers like Spotify, Tile, and Match Group criticized the high commission fees and restrictive policies, such as mandating proprietary payment systems and prohibiting developers from offering discounts outside the app. Spotify's lawyer highlighted issues like the "Apple tax" and alleged retaliation from Apple, while Senator Amy Klobuchar raised concerns about "self-preferencing" by Apple Music.

title: Equal-Weight Still Holds Quality for Growth Investors
summary: The article discusses how equal-weighted Nasdaq-100 ETFs like QQQE and QQEW can be beneficial for growth investors, particularly those looking to mitigate concentration risk in the cap-weighted Nasdaq-100 Index. By equal weighting, these funds reduce reliance on mega-cap firms and add a value factor, offering a more balanced exposure to the underlying companies. This approach helps lessen the impact of poor performance from individual large constituents.

title: Artificial Intelligence as the Backbone for the ARKQ ETF
summary: The article discusses how artificial intelligence serves as a fundamental theme for disruptive technologies, specifically highlighting its importance for the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ). It emphasizes AI's intersection with other growth themes and its role as a backbone for various products and services, particularly in edge computing. The piece also details how advancements in AI, machine learning, and supercomputing are driving demand for data center solutions and influencing new architectural developments like ARM's v9.

title: Apple Investing in Hendricks County Distribution Center
summary: Apple Inc. is investing $100 million to build a new distribution center in Hendricks County, Indiana, which will create nearly 500 jobs by the end of 2024. The facility will be operated by XPO Logistics Supply Chain and aims to accelerate delivery times for customers. The Indiana Economic Development Corp. has offered XPO Logistics up to $5 million in conditional tax credits for the project.

title: Apple and Google Are Killing the (Ad) Cookie. Here’s Why
summary: Apple and Google are implementing separate changes that will effectively end the use of third-party advertising cookies, significantly altering how marketers track online activity and target ads. Apple is requiring app developers to gain user permission for tracking across apps and websites, while Google is developing a cookie alternative that allows for targeted advertising without relying on individual web history. Both tech giants cite privacy as the driving force behind these changes, though Google also emphasizes balancing privacy with the financial viability of web publishing.

title: Apple incentives are the largest in NC history. Here’s how the deal breaks down.
summary: North Carolina has landed a new Apple Inc. high-tech campus in Research Triangle Park, marking the largest incentive deal in the state's history. The state and local incentives for Apple could total nearly $1 billion over four decades, primarily sourced from new Apple employees' state income tax payments which will be directed back to the company. In return, Apple promises $1 billion in investment across North Carolina and 3,000 new jobs with an average annual salary of $187,000, projecting over $1 billion in annual economic benefits for the state.

title: SiriusXM acquires Roman Mars’ 99% Invisible and a bigger stake in the podcasting world
summary: SiriusXM has acquired Roman Mars' podcast company 99% Invisible Inc., integrating Mars and his team under its Stitcher brand. This acquisition allows Mars to focus on content creation while benefiting from SiriusXM's resources and distribution across platforms like Stitcher, Pandora, and SiriusXM apps. The move highlights the ongoing consolidation in the podcasting industry as larger tech companies vie for top talent and content.

title: AirTags review: The Bluetooth tracker only Apple would build
summary: This article reviews Apple's new AirTag Bluetooth tracker, highlighting its seamless integration with the Apple ecosystem, ease of setup, and leveraging of the vast "Find My" network. It contrasts AirTags with competitors like Tile, noting Apple's focus on privacy and superior finding capabilities through Ultra-Wideband connectivity, despite some minor drawbacks like accessory costs and sound loudness. The review concludes that AirTags are a genuinely Apple product, both in design and functionality, designed to enhance the iPhone user experience rather than being a standalone business.

title: What do Apple’s new privacy settings mean for you?
summary: Apple is rolling out new privacy settings requiring app developers to explicitly ask users for permission to track their browsing habits across other apps and websites. This change is expected to significantly impact the mobile advertising market as many users are anticipated to decline tracking, potentially reducing ad effectiveness and revenue for businesses and publishers. The move has created a rift with companies like Facebook, which argues it will harm small businesses.

title: Apple commits $430 billion in US investments over five years
summary: Apple announced an accelerated commitment of over $430 billion in US investments and the creation of 20,000 new jobs across the country over the next five years. This includes building a new campus in North Carolina, expanding operations in multiple states, and significant spending on silicon engineering and 5G technology. The company also emphasized its commitment to environmental responsibility and supporting US suppliers and the iOS app economy.

title: Apple opens South Lake Union towers, plans to double current workforce
summary: Apple has announced plans to significantly expand its presence in Seattle by opening new towers in South Lake Union and doubling its current workforce there.
