This expansion is part of a larger initiative to add 20,000 jobs across the U.S. over the next five years. The company has leased all office space in the 333 Dexter campus to accommodate this growth.

title: Golf balls, massage chairs, and pies: Cantaloupe lays out our cash-less future
summary: Cantaloupe, formerly USA Technologies, is a payments technology company for unattended devices like vending machines, golf ball dispensers, and massage chairs. CEO Sean Feeney is leading a multi-year turnaround effort, including a rebranding and a focus on expanding cashless and touchless commerce internationally. The company develops its own point-of-sale terminals and software, expecting subscription services and more complex, data-rich transactions to drive future growth.

title: Apple investing $448 million in Catawba County as part of NC expansion
summary: Apple Inc. announced a $1 billion investment in North Carolina, with a significant portion, $448 million, allocated for expansion in Catawba County. While the new 3,000 jobs will be in Wake County's Research Triangle Park, the Catawba County investment will focus on real property construction and improvements at Apple's existing data center over the next decade.

title: Google (GOOGL) stock hits all-time high on Q1 2021 earnings, announces $50 billion buyback
summary: Alphabet (Google's parent company) reported record Q1 2021 earnings, with revenues of $55.31 billion and EPS of $26.29, significantly beating analyst estimates. Google's stock hit an all-time high of over $2400/share in after-hours trading following the announcement. The company also announced a new $50 billion share repurchase program for its Class C capital stock.

title: What to know about Apple's new privacy update and why it's riling Facebook
summary: Apple has rolled out a new privacy update, iOS 14.5, which requires apps to get user permission before tracking their data. This update has sparked a feud with Facebook, which argues it will disrupt online advertising and harm small businesses that rely on targeted ads. Apple, however, maintains the feature gives users more control over their personal data.

title: Looking for an ETF to Hold for the Long-Term?
summary: The article suggests the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG) as a long-term investment option, highlighting its strong performance and holdings in major growth companies. It emphasizes the potential for significant long-term returns through consistent investing in such ETFs, despite the allure of instant gratification.

title: LG Display posts strong Q1 profit on panel price boost
summary: LG Display reported a strong Q1 operating profit of 523 billion won, rebounding from a loss in the previous year, driven by increased panel prices due to high demand for TVs and laptops. This exceeded analyst expectations, though a shortage of semiconductors poses a threat to future production. The company noted that IT devices and TVs were significant contributors to its revenue.

title: Form 8-K IDEX CORP /DE/ For: Apr 27
summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2021 financial results, with record orders and sales up 10% overall and 6% organically. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $6.05-$6.20 and announced the acquisition of AirTech Group, Inc. These results reflect improving market conditions and effective capital deployment strategies.

title: Spotify forecasts weak quarter as subscriber growth falters
summary: Spotify Technology SA has forecast second-quarter paid subscribers below Wall Street estimates, despite its first-quarter results beating expectations due to growth in countries like the United States and India. The company faces increasing competition from rivals such as Apple Music and Amazon Music, with Apple also launching a new paid subscription platform challenging Spotify's podcast ambitions. Spotify anticipates total premium subscribers between 162 million and 166 million for Q2, while analysts expected 166.1 million.

title: Apple sales smash expectations on surging demand for devices
summary: Apple Inc. reported fiscal second-quarter sales of $89.6 billion, significantly exceeding Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for the 5G iPhone 12 line, iPads, and Mac computers as people continued working and learning from home. The company's iPad and Mac sales nearly doubled, demonstrating its control over the supply chain and its profitability during the pandemic. Apple also increased its dividend by 7% and expanded its share buyback program by an additional $90 billion.

title: These 3 ETFs Sit Atop the Invesco Fund Flows Leaderboard
summary: This article highlights the top three Invesco ETFs by year-to-date fund flows as of April 2021. The ETFs include the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP), the Invesco PureBeta MSCI USA ETF (PBUS), and the Invesco Senior Loan ETF (BKLN). Each ETF offers a distinct strategy, ranging from equal-weighting and pure beta exposure to senior loan investments.

title: Steve Jobs Made a Brilliant Change When He Returned to Apple. It Changed the Company Forever
summary: Upon his return to Apple in 1997, Steve Jobs implemented a crucial change to the company's management structure, eliminating the product-specific "business units" that had led to internal competition and nearly bankrupt the company. This shift, rooted in his experiences with NeXT, fostered a more collaborative and unified approach. Jobs's management philosophy, emphasizing unified teams rather than siloed general managers, became a cornerstone of Apple's success and continues to influence its operations today.

title: Apple just recorded a 42.5% gross margin for the quarter, the highest in nine years
summary: Apple announced a gross margin of 42.5% for the recent quarter, its highest since 2012, driven by increased prices for iPhones and a significant boost from its services business. The services division saw its gross margin climb to 70%, up from 65% a year prior, as the company extracts more profit from its hardware buyers through offerings like iTunes and the App Store. Apple also recently launched a podcast subscription service, intensifying its competition with Spotify.

title: Tencent Music Boasts 100 Million Listeners for ‘Long-Form Audio’
summary: Tencent Music has announced that its long-form audio services, including audiobooks and comedy, now attract 100 million listeners, marking a 230% year-on-year growth. This surge positions Tencent Music as a significant player alongside Spotify and Apple in the rapidly expanding audio content market. The company has integrated non-music tabs into its streaming services and merged acquired audio platforms to enhance its long-form audio offerings.

title: Highwoods Properties Inc (HIW) Q1 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Highwoods Properties Inc. (HIW) reported Q1 2021 FFO of $0.91 per share and increased its full-year FFO outlook, driven by improved leasing activity and strong performance in its Sunbelt markets. The company also announced plans to acquire a portfolio of office assets from Preferred Apartment Communities for $769 million, funded by selling $500-600 million in non-core assets. Executives highlighted rising office utilization rates, significant job growth announcements in their key markets like Raleigh and Nashville, and continued optimism for long-term growth.

title: Apple v. Epic: What to expect from a trial that could change antitrust law and the mobile-app ecosystem
summary: The article previews the upcoming federal court case between Apple Inc. and Epic Games Inc., scheduled to begin on May 3. This high-profile trial is expected to significantly impact antitrust law and the mobile app ecosystem, with the livelihood of app developers and the definition of antitrust law at stake. Epic Games will challenge Apple's App Store rules, arguing they unfairly leverage the tech giant's market dominance.

title: Apple Finally Feels the Effects of the Global Chip Shortage
summary: Apple Inc. reported strong revenue and record sales in its latest quarter, but warned that the global chip shortage is beginning to impact its business. Executives stated that supply constraints would specifically affect iPad and Mac sales, potentially reducing third-quarter revenue by $3 billion to $4 billion. This marks the first significant impact of the chip shortage on Apple's previously resilient production.

title: Consumer spending, streaming propel Amazon to second straight quarter of more than $100B in revenue
summary: Amazon reported quarterly sales of $108.5 billion, marking its second consecutive quarter exceeding $100 billion in revenue, driven by strong consumer spending and increased streaming by its 200 million Prime members. The company's profit more than tripled, leading to an all-time high in stock price and plans for a Prime Day sale in June. Amazon also announced pay raises for over 500,000 workers, effective next month.

title: Microsoft plans to take a smaller cut from developers in its games store. (Published 2021)
summary: Microsoft announced that it will decrease the revenue cut it takes from independent developers publishing PC games on its online store, starting in August. The fee will drop from 30 percent to 12 percent, allowing developers to keep 88 percent of their revenue. This move aims to make Microsoft's store more competitive with platforms like Steam and Epic Games, and comes amid increased scrutiny over app store fees faced by companies like Apple and Google.

title: Car firms, tech giants feel squeeze of worsening chip shortage
summary: The global chip shortage is intensifying, forcing major automakers like Honda, BMW, and Ford, as well as tech giants Apple and Samsung, to cut production and lower sales forecasts. The crisis, driven by high demand for electronics during the pandemic, is expected to continue into 2022, costing automakers billions in lost sales and risking a slowdown in the smartphone market's rebound. Companies across industries are struggling to secure critical semiconductor supplies, facing significant financial impacts.

title: I'm Not Turning Off App Tracking On My iPhone, Because I’m A Total Cuck
summary: The author humorously explains her decision to not disable app tracking on her iPhone, despite Apple's new privacy feature allowing users to opt out. She describes herself as a "datacuck" who enjoys personalized ads and wants to see "how far this goes" when it comes to data collection, contrasting her personal choice with the recommended smart privacy practices. The article satirically delves into the perceived benefits of targeted advertising while acknowledging the serious implications of data privacy and Facebook's track record.

title: Cirrus Logic Shares Fall on Earnings Miss. Blame Apple.
summary: Cirrus Logic's shares dropped significantly after the chipmaker reported disappointing March quarter results and issued June quarter guidance that fell short of Wall Street's expectations. The article suggests that Apple is to blame for these outcomes.

title: Skyworks Beat Estimates. Why Its Stock Is Falling.
summary: Shares of Skyworks Solutions are falling despite the company reporting better-than-expected results for the March quarter and providing strong guidance for the June quarter. Investors may have been hoping for a larger earnings beat, especially given recent positive sales reports for the Apple iPhone, Skyworks' largest customer.

title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway rebounds from pandemic with $11.7 billion profit
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway announced a net profit of $11.7 billion in the first quarter of 2021, recovering from a significant loss in early 2020 due to the pandemic. The conglomerate, which owns diverse businesses, saw strong revenue growth as the economy reopened. Despite a massive cash pile of $145.4 billion, Berkshire Hathaway continued significant stock buybacks rather than pursuing large acquisitions, even as its stock performance has lagged the broader market in recent years.

title: Apple’s new M1 chip creates a competitive advantage
summary: Apple has introduced new iMacs and iPad Pros featuring its M1 chip, allowing the company to control its entire technology stack and gain a competitive advantage. This strategy insulates Apple from the global chip shortage affecting other manufacturers reliant on third-party semiconductors. Experts like Richard Barnett highlight that Apple's approach contrasts sharply with the challenges faced by automotive, consumer electronics, and medical device industries.

title: American LGA-ORD Oasis: unremarkable in a good way
summary: The author recounts his first flight after a 413-day grounding, taking an American Airlines flight from New York LaGuardia to Chicago O’Hare. He describes the experience of flying in American's dense Oasis cabin, highlighting the sufficient comfort of the Main Cabin seats, the useful in-seat power and device holders, and the ample IFE content, despite the lack of onboard food and beverage service due to COVID-19 policies. The overall experience was unremarkable in a positive sense, characterized by cleanliness, adherence to mask mandates, and an early arrival.

title: Is the ‘Apple’ juice worth the squeeze?
summary: This article analyzes whether the tax benefits offered to Apple Inc. for establishing a new campus in North Carolina are justified by the economic returns. It presents various scenarios demonstrating that North Carolina could recoup its investment within 4 to 29 years through increased tax revenues from new jobs, local investments, other taxes, and corporate taxes. The analysis suggests that even with conservative estimates, the state and local municipalities will significantly benefit from Apple’s presence.
authors: Nathan C. Goldman and Erynn Stainback

title: 3 Clicks to $4,000 a Month in Dividend Income
summary: This article proposes that Closed-End Funds (CEFs) offer a superior alternative to ETFs for generating significant retirement income, highlighting their higher dividend yields. It outlines a portfolio of three specific CEFs—Nuveen NASDAQ 100 Dynamic Overwrite Fund (QQQX), Guggenheim Strategic Opportunities Fund (GOF), and PIMCO Corporate & Income Opportunities Fund (PTY)—that collectively provide an 8.5% payout and strong diversification. The author argues that CEFs enable investors to achieve a substantial passive income stream with a much smaller initial investment compared to traditional S&P 500 dividend ETFs, ultimately leading to financial independence faster.

title: General Motors’ Fairfax Plant To Remain Idle Until July
summary: General Motors announced its Fairfax Assembly plant in Kansas, which produces the Chevy Malibu and Cadillac XT4, will remain shut down until the first week of July due to the ongoing semiconductor chip shortage. This decision follows similar production halts at other GM plants in Ontario, Mexico, and Missouri as the company prioritizes chip allocation for its most popular full-size trucks and SUVs. The chip shortage is expected to impact auto production significantly, with competitor Ford also cutting its production outlook and anticipating the shortage to last into early 2022.

title: Epic Games CEO cites Apple’s ‘total control’ over iPhones at first day of antitrust trial
summary: Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney testified on the first day of the antitrust trial against Apple, stating his deliberate violation of Apple's App Store rules to spotlight the company's "total control" over software on iPhones. The lawsuit challenges Apple's mandatory App Store distribution and in-app purchase system, which charges up to 30% commissions. Epic argues Apple's practices stifle competition, while Apple contends its rules ensure user safety and a thriving market for developers.

title: Best Buy savior Hubert Joly details a new approach to leadership
summary: Hubert Joly, former CEO of Best Buy, is highlighted as a business hero for his success in turning around the company by focusing on purpose over profit. His new book, "The Heart of Business," advocates for a "refoundation of business and capitalism around purpose," aligning with growing demands from employees, customers, shareholders, and society, which has been accelerated by recent crises. The article emphasizes that this new model of leadership requires an environment that unleashes "human magic" rather than top-down problem-solving.

title: Apple's conflict with Facebook goes back to the days of Steve Jobs, emails show
summary: Internal Apple emails from 2011 involving Steve Jobs, Scott Forstall, and Phil Schiller reveal that the conflict between Apple and Facebook over App Store rules dates back a decade. Jobs referred to Facebook as "Fecebooks" and believed it shouldn't host its own apps, contributing to the delayed release of Facebook's iPad app. This historical tension sheds light on the ongoing disputes between the two tech giants regarding App Store policies and user privacy.

title: Apple announces $430B expansion
summary: Apple plans to invest over $430 billion and create 20,000 new jobs in the U.S. over the next five years, including significant investments in North Carolina for a new campus and infrastructure improvements. This expansion follows robust second-quarter sales, boosted by increased demand during the pandemic for remote work and entertainment. The tech giant's projects span multiple states, involving construction, silicon engineering, 5G, and manufacturing sectors.

title: XPO Logistics: Companies demand outsourcing, warehouse automation as e-commerce surges
summary: XPO Logistics saw increased revenue driven by shipper demand for outsourcing and warehouse automation, accelerated by the pandemic's impact on e-commerce. The company secured $4 billion in new business, including a significant deal with Apple for a direct-to-consumer distribution center featuring advanced automation. These trends, including faster fulfillment times, are expected to continue driving demand for intelligent machines and specialized logistics.

title: How wearable technology is transforming wellbeing in the workplace
summary: Wearable technology, ranging from smartwatches to specialized helmets and goggles, is increasingly being adopted in workplaces to enhance employee wellbeing and performance. This article explores the growing market for wearables, strategic shifts in their application, and the challenges associated with their implementation in creating smarter workplaces. The integration of these devices aims to help employees stay healthier and more productive.

title: Adobe CEO: We’re in the ‘sweet spot’ of the world’s reliance on tech
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses how the company transitioned from Flash software to a successful Creative Cloud subscription service, benefiting from the global reliance on technology. He highlights Adobe's three businesses being in the "sweet spot" of technological need and his strategies for continued growth, along with addressing the future of women and people of color in the workplace post-pandemic with Catalyst's Lauren Pasquarella Daley.

title: Apple awards an additional $410 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to II-VI
summary: Apple has awarded an additional $410 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to II-VI, a leading optical technology manufacturer. This builds on an initial $390 million award in 2017 and is expected to create additional capacity and accelerate the delivery of future iPhone components, supporting over 700 jobs across various US states. II-VI manufactures VCSELs for Face ID and LiDAR Scanners, and this expanded partnership is part of Apple's broader commitment to invest $430 billion and create 20,000 new jobs in the US over the next five years.

title: Lucid Motors Appoints Sherry House as Chief Financial Officer
summary: Lucid Motors has appointed Sherry House as its new Chief Financial Officer, bringing over two decades of financial and technical expertise from Waymo and other companies. This key appointment, along with the addition of Margaret Burgraff, Sanjay Chandra, and Jeff Curry to the leadership team, aims to strengthen Lucid as it prepares for its public listing and the start of customer deliveries for the Lucid Air in 2021. The new hires bring diverse experience from leading companies like Apple, Intel, Jaguar, and Ferrari, enhancing Lucid's strategy to redefine luxury and performance in electric vehicles.

title: The Best Tracker and App to Find Your Lost Phone
summary: The article "The Best Tracker and App to Find Your Lost Phone" discusses tools for locating a misplaced or stolen phone. While the provided content is minimal, Life360 is highlighted as a family tracking app offering location sharing and safety features. This suggests it's a recommended solution for finding lost devices among family members.

title: Grizzlies owner buys Taipei unit
summary: Robert Pera, owner of the NBA’s Memphis Grizzlies, has reportedly purchased a luxury apartment in Taipei for NT$570 million (US$20.39 million) in an all-cash deal. The unit is located in the upscale 55Timeless Tower in Xinyi District, a building where pop singer turned livestreaming media owner Jeffrey Huang also owns a unit. Pera, a tech entrepreneur and one of the world's richest individuals, also has previous connections to Taiwanese cultural figures, having lent money for a film directed by Wei Te-sheng.

title: IBM unveils 2-nanometer chip technology for faster computing
summary: IBM has introduced what it claims is the world's first 2-nanometer chipmaking technology, a significant advancement promising up to 45% faster performance and 75% more power efficiency compared to current 7-nanometer chips. This breakthrough demonstrates that silicon chips still have generational improvements to offer, despite slowing progress in transistor miniaturization. While the technology will take several years to reach the market, it represents a crucial step in computing power and efficiency.

title: Self-driving startup Aeva says its sensor can detect vehicles over 500m away
summary: Self-driving sensor startup Aeva Technologies Inc. announced that its lidar sensor can detect other vehicles beyond 500 meters and pedestrians over 350 meters away. This advanced detection range is a crucial metric for autonomous vehicles, enabling them to make timely decisions at high speeds. Aeva, founded by former Apple Inc. engineers, has secured deals with automotive suppliers Denso Corp and ZF Friedrichshafen AG for mass production of its sensors.

title: Fox Factory Bolsters Management to Lay the Foundation for Next Phase of Growth
summary: Fox Factory Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: FOXF) announced key leadership additions to support its next phase of growth and strategic vision. Tom L. Fletcher has been promoted to President, PVG Business & Corporate Strategy, Toby D. Merchant joins as the first Chief Legal Officer & Secretary, and Jackie B. Martin has been promoted to Chief Purpose & Inclusion Officer. These appointments aim to strengthen the company's capabilities in various critical areas.

title: IBM's new 2-nanometer chip will be faster than the one in Apple's iPhone 12
summary: IBM has introduced what it claims is the world's first 2-nanometer chipmaking technology. This new technology promises to be up to 45% faster and 75% more power-efficient than current mainstream 7-nanometer chips. While it will take several years to reach the market, these chips are expected to outperform the 5-nanometer chips found in devices like Apple's iPhone 12.

title: Apple Has Hired Cisco's Stella Low As Its Head Of PR
summary: Apple has hired Stella Low, former communications chief at Cisco, as its new vice president of worldwide corporate communications. She will report directly to Apple CEO Tim Cook and fills a role that has been vacant for nearly two years. Low's appointment comes at a critical time for Apple, as the company faces high-profile legal battles, geopolitical tensions, and prepares for significant product launches.

title: Sanctions-as-a-Service: US Regulators Escalate Sanctions Enforcement Priorities Into the Cloud with SAP Settlement
summary: US regulators announced a global resolution with SAP SE following its voluntary disclosure of providing software and cloud-based services to users in Iran, violating export and sanctions regulations. This settlement, involving the Departments of Justice, Commerce, and Treasury, highlights the US government's increased focus on sanctions compliance in the tech sector, particularly regarding cloud-based services and the need for robust screening and access controls. SAP agreed to pay over $8 million in penalties and invested over $27 million in enhancing its compliance programs.
authors: John P. Barker|John B. Bellinger|III|Soo-Mi Rhee|Baruch Weiss|Nicholas L. Townsend|Tal R. Machnes|Alyssa T. Gerstner|Trevor G. Schmitt

title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
summary: Samsung Electronics is set to enter the laptop processor market in the second half of this year with a premium Exynos chip, targeting the rapidly growing global laptop market. The new Exynos chip will feature a GPU developed with AMD, focusing on improved computing power and battery efficiency with 5-nanometer processing technology. Samsung's move follows other tech giants like Apple and Qualcomm, who have also developed laptop-grade chipsets, as the company seeks to compete with market leader Intel.

title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
summary: Samsung Electronics plans to release a laptop version of its Exynos mobile application processor in the second half of this year, entering the growing laptop market. This premium Exynos chip, developed with AMD's GPU technology, aims to offer improved computing power and battery efficiency using 5-nanometer technology. Samsung will join other tech companies like Apple and Qualcomm in developing laptop-specific chipsets, capitalizing on the rapid growth in global laptop sales.

title: This KY factory makes the glass for every iPhone. Apple just gave it $45 million.
summary: Apple has awarded $45 million to Corning Incorporated, which manufactures the glass for every iPhone generation in its Harrodsburg, Kentucky factory. This investment aims to expand U.S. manufacturing capacity and fund research and development for new, durable glass technologies like Ceramic Shield used in the iPhone 12. The funding comes from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund and supports over 1,000 jobs in Kentucky, deepening the long-standing collaboration between the two companies.

title: Apple awards Corning $45 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund
summary: Apple has awarded Corning $45 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to expand manufacturing capacity in the US and drive research and development into innovative new technologies like Ceramic Shield. This investment builds on $450 million previously provided, supporting over 1,000 jobs across Corning's US operations and fostering continued collaboration on durable glass for Apple products. The partnership has been crucial in developing materials like Ceramic Shield for the iPhone 12 lineup, and Corning is also part of Apple's Clean Energy Program.

title: This Kentucky iPhone glass manufacturer just received a major investment from Apple
summary: Apple is investing an additional $45 million into Corning Inc.'s glass innovation center in Harrodsburg, Kentucky, which manufactures scratch-resistant glass for its iPhones and other products. This investment comes from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund and will be used for research and development to improve durability and product life. The Harrodsburg plant has previously received $450 million from this fund and supports hundreds of jobs.

title: Harrodsburg glass plant that supplies all iPhones getting $45 million from Apple
summary: Apple is investing an additional $45 million into Corning Inc.'s plant in Harrodsburg, Kentucky, which manufactures glass for iPhones and other Apple devices. This new investment from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund aims to expand Corning's manufacturing capacity and support research and development for new technologies focused on durability and product life. The Harrodsburg plant has historically supplied scratch-resistant glass for all iPhone generations since 2007, along with glass for iPads and Apple Watches, and has previously received $450 million in Apple funding.

title: China tech giants bet US$19 billion on global electric car frenzy
summary: Chinese tech giants like Huawei, Xiaomi, and Baidu are pouring nearly US$19 billion into electric and self-driving vehicle ventures, aiming to capitalize on the high-tech shift in the automotive industry. This aggressive push establishes China as a major battleground for EV innovation, with companies making strategic tie-ups and unveiling their own car-making plans. Despite the hyper-competitive market and significant capital requirements, these tech companies are focused on making intelligent vehicles, with their main revenue expected to come from post-sale services and software rather than just car sales.

title: Apple awards $45 million to the company that helps make tough iPhone screens
summary: Apple has awarded an additional $45 million to Corning, the company responsible for manufacturing the Gorilla Glass and Ceramic Shield used in iPhones, iPads, and Apple Watches. This investment, part of Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund, aims to expand Corning's manufacturing and R&D in the U.S. Corning has been a long-standing partner with Apple, contributing to the durability of Apple's devices since the original iPhone.

title: Apple Alocates $45 Million to iPhone’s Ceramic Screen, Gorilla Glass Supplier -- Is it a Hint for the Bendable iPhone?
summary: Apple has invested an additional $45 million into Corning Inc., the supplier of Gorilla Glass and the iPhone 12's Ceramic Shield, through its Advanced Manufacturing Fund. This investment, which follows previous funding totaling $450 million, aims to expand Corning's manufacturing capacity in the US and aligns with recent rumors of a foldable iPhone potentially launching in 2023. While Corning has developed ultra-thin, bendable glass, its durability is a challenge, suggesting Apple's funding might support the development of a strong yet flexible material for future devices.

title: Google Pay now lets U.S. users send money to India and Singapore
summary: Google Pay has launched a new feature allowing U.S. users to send money to India and Singapore by integrating with money transfer services Wise and Western Union. This move marks Google's entry into the significant remittances market, which was valued at $508 billion for low- and middle-income countries in 2020. The company plans to expand this service to more countries in the future, despite stating no intentions of becoming a bank itself.

title: Google Pay launches international money transfers with Wise and Western Union
summary: Google Pay has partnered with Wise and Western Union to enable international money transfers for its U.S. users, expanding its financial services offerings. The service initially supports transfers to India and Singapore, with plans to cover 80 countries via Wise and 200 via Western Union by year-end. This move escalates competition in the digital payments sector, leveraging the boom in online payments spurred by the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Samsung and AMD will reportedly take on Apple’s M1 SoC later this year
summary: Samsung is reportedly accelerating its Exynos SoC development with an AMD-powered GPU, aiming to compete with Apple's M1 chip in laptops by the second half of this year. While this partnership could boost Samsung's GPU capabilities for laptops, current Exynos smartphone chips are not expected to be dramatically changed, and Samsung still faces challenges in CPU design and the Windows-on-ARM ecosystem compared to Apple's integrated approach. The article also touches on Qualcomm's Nuvia acquisition and Google's "Whitechapel" ambitions, highlighting the broader race to develop competitive ARM-based processors.

title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's non-profit teams up with Procter & Gamble
summary: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's Archewell Foundation announced a multi-year partnership with consumer goods giant Procter & Gamble. The collaboration will focus on gender equality, fostering inclusive online communities, and promoting "resilience and impact" through sports, though financial details were not disclosed. This partnership adds to the couple's various ventures in social work and media content creation since stepping away from royal duties.

title: Powerley and DTE Energy Launch Industry-First Advanced Rate Management Solution
summary: Powerley and DTE Energy have launched an industry-first advanced rate management solution designed to help energy customers understand and control advanced energy rates, such as time-of-use. This solution combines customer-specific energy pricing with household energy data to provide real-time rate advice and personalized coaching, enabling customers to maximize savings and helping energy companies manage peak demand and drive decarbonization. DTE Energy is implementing this technology in a new Time-of-Use pilot program through its DTE Insight app.

title: Corning opens new manufacturing facility in Wuhan
summary: Corning Incorporated has opened a new Gen 10.5 liquid crystal display (LCD) glass substrate manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, co-located with a BOE Technology Group Co., Ltd. plant. This facility aims to strengthen Corning's presence in China and support the production of large-size display panels, specifically for 65- and 75-inch TVs. Despite pandemic challenges, Corning successfully ramped up production at the Wuhan facility, which is its sixth LCD glass plant on the Chinese mainland and second Gen 10.5 facility.

title: Cantaloupe expands with crypto, cashless payments partnerships
summary: Cantaloupe, a software and payments company for self-pay equipment, is partnering with Bakkt Holdings to accept cryptocurrencies and loyalty points at its machines, capitalizing on the shift towards cashless payments and digital assets. This move follows another partnership with Castles Technology to enhance cashless operations at unattended retail outlets. The company, which rebranded from USA Technologies, aims to drive revenue growth through these new payment features and planned international expansion.

title: Under the hood of Australia’s largest sustainable ETF
summary: This article examines the BetaShares Global Sustainability Leaders ETF (ASX:ETHI), Australia's largest sustainable fund, highlighting its strong performance and strict exclusion criteria while also noting the inclusion of companies accused of tax evasion. It delves into the fund's methodology, its holdings (dominated by tech and financial services), and its performance compared to other funds, discussing the complexities of defining "sustainable" in investing. The piece also raises concerns about animal testing and tax minimization among some of ETHI's holdings.

title: The New Ransomware Threat: Triple Extortion
summary: Ransomware attacks have significantly increased globally, with a 102% rise compared to early 2020. Industries such as healthcare and utilities are prime targets, with Asia Pacific experiencing the highest volume of attacks. Check Point Research details a new "Triple Extortion" model where attackers not only encrypt data and threaten publication but also launch DDoS attacks and contact third parties like business partners and media for increased pressure, as seen with the REvil group.

title: JPMorgan Is America’s Largest Company In 2021, But Amazon Is Rising In The Ranks
summary: Despite a pandemic, America's largest companies grew significantly in 2021, with JPMorgan Chase maintaining its position as the largest for the third consecutive year. Meanwhile, Amazon climbed into the top five, reflecting the growing importance of e-commerce, while traditional giants like AT&T and ExxonMobil saw significant drops in rankings. This year's Global 2000 ranking illustrates the resilience and shifts within the U.S. corporate landscape amidst economic volatility.

title: Aeva announces customer deal; shares soar even after results disappoint
summary: Aeva Technologies Inc. announced a deal to develop a sensor for an "undisclosed large company's" self-driving car program, causing its shares to surge 13% despite reporting disappointing financial results. The company's first-quarter revenue fell short of estimates, and its operating loss more than doubled. Aeva, founded by ex-Apple engineers, specializes in lidar technology for autonomous vehicles and recently went public through a reverse merger.

title: GPS Watch Tracker Market Rewriting It’s Growth Cycle | Garmin, Apple, Suunto
summary: A new report by HTF MI provides a comprehensive investigation of the Global GPS Watch Tracker market, including its geographical landscape, industry size, revenue estimation, and growth strategies of leading companies like Garmin, Apple, and Suunto. The study segments the market by product type (Basic GPS Watch & Smart GPS Watch) and end-users (Specialist Retailers, Factory outlets, Internet sales & Other), offering insights into market drivers, challenges, and opportunities across various regions. The report utilizes PESTLE analysis to evaluate political, economic, social, technological, legal, and environmental factors affecting the market.

title: Is TiVo done with streaming dongles? Not so fast
summary: Despite recent reports suggesting TiVo might be abandoning its streaming dongle business, a company spokesperson states that TiVo will continue to produce new Android TV dongles and improve the existing Stream 4K. The move comes as TiVo's parent company, Xperi, also explores embedding its software directly into smart TVs. The article highlights the potential of TiVo's streaming solution for cord-cutters and the competitive landscape of both streaming devices and smart TV platforms.

title: Epic Games vs. Apple Trial, Week Two: Everything You Need to Know - WSJ
summary: The second week of the Epic Games vs. Apple trial focused on expert witness testimony regarding whether smartphones are interchangeable with other video game platforms and the definition of a market in the digital age. Epic Games sued Apple for alleged antitrust violations after its game "Fortnite" was removed from the App Store, while Apple maintains its app marketplace policies are fair and that Epic breached a contract. Apple's stock market value hit a new record in 2020 amid these disputes with app developers.

title: Stock Index ETFs Accelerate Gains to Close Out Volatile Week
summary: Stocks and index ETFs rallied on Friday, driven by technology shares and economic recovery plays, despite earlier weekly losses stemming from inflation fears. While some analysts see the recent decline as a healthy correction, others remain concerned about inflation's long-term impact on monetary policy and market stability, even as a robust earnings season suggests upside potential.

title: Review: Apple Inc. iOS 7
summary: This article reviews Apple's iOS 7, highlighting its significant changes and user impact. While some new features like Control Center and iTunes Radio are praised, the review critically examines the controversial aesthetic overhaul of the Lock Screen and Home Screen, detailing issues with readability, icons, and animations. It also touches on improvements to Siri and third-party app compatibility, concluding that iOS 7 succeeds despite its divisive design choices.

title: Review: Electronic Arts The Game of Life Classic Edition
summary: This article reviews Electronic Arts' The Game of Life Classic Edition for iPhone/iPod Touch, an impressive 3-D port of the classic board game. While the game provides an engaging visual and auditory experience, limitations include a single rule set, local multiplayer only, and a repetitive soundtrack. Despite these minor drawbacks, the reviewer finds the $5 asking price justified, highlighting its educational value for understanding life's financial and social paths.

title: Review: Electronic Arts Sudoku (Num-pre)
summary: This article reviews Electronic Arts' Sudoku game for the iPod and later the iPhone/iPod touch. The iPod version, priced at $5, is praised for its approachable design, helpful features like Auto-Fill and Hint, and a "Newspaper Mode" for solving external puzzles. However, the iPhone version, priced at $8, is criticized for offering minimal changes and less customization compared to competitors.

title: Berkshire sheds nearly all of Wells Fargo, a holding since 1989
summary: Berkshire Hathaway has divested almost all of its Wells Fargo shares, ending a 31-year investment that was once highly successful but was tarnished by customer mistreatment scandals. The holding, which was worth $32 billion in 2018, is now down to $26.4 million. Warren Buffett cited the bank's "dumb" incentive system and slow response to problems as reasons for the divestment, though Berkshire maintains holdings in other banks like Bank of America.

title: International Relocation Is Not the Only Type of Work Mobility Needed to Attract Great Talent
summary: This article discusses how workplace mobility has evolved beyond traditional international relocation, driven by technology and new work models. Belgin Ertam of GE MENAT highlights that successful companies like GE are creating diverse opportunities for employees regarding their work "place" to attract and retain talent. It suggests that a broader understanding of mobility, beyond just physical relocation, is crucial in the modern workforce.

title: An FAQ on the Matter protocol
summary: This article provides an FAQ on the Matter protocol, which is a new smart home interoperability standard launched by major tech companies. It explains what Matter and Thread are, how they will affect existing smart home technologies like Zigbee and Z-Wave, and what the implications are for consumers, hardware manufacturers, and professional installers. The article also touches on the potential impact on open-source platforms like Home Assistant.

title: A Licensor’s FRAND Commitments Do Not Limit A Licensee’s Rights
summary: This article argues that FRAND commitments made by licensors do not impose obligations on potential licensees, contrary to views expressed in a previous series of articles. The author contends that FRAND commitments are unilateral decisions by SEP holders to limit their own rights in exchange for broader licensing opportunities, and that licensees retain the right to challenge the essentiality and validity of declared standard-essential patents (SEPs) without being compelled to accept a license. The article criticizes the idea that FRAND commitments create reciprocal obligations for licensees or shortcut the traditional burdens of proof within the patent system.

title: Apple Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock fell 1.12% on Tuesday, closing at $124.85, amidst a broader market downturn with the NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declining. This marked the stock's second consecutive day of losses, placing it $20.24 below its 52-week high. The article also notes Apple's performance compared to competitors like Microsoft, Alphabet, and IBM, all of which also experienced declines.

title: Chips with everything: how one Taiwanese company drives the world economy
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plays a critical role in the global economy due to its dominance in sophisticated semiconductor production, supplying major tech companies like Apple and Qualcomm. The current global chip shortage, exacerbated by the Covid-19 pandemic, highlights TSMC's pivotal position, impacting various industries, especially automotive, and fueling inflation. The article discusses strategic challenges, including Taiwan's geopolitical vulnerability and efforts by the US and China to reduce their reliance on TSMC.

title: Diageo bets on data-driven partnerships with new Collaboration Center
summary: Diageo has opened a 17,000 square-foot Diageo Collaboration Center in Stamford, Connecticut, aimed at fostering data-driven partnerships with customers, distributors, and agencies. The center will leverage Diageo's extensive data and analytics resources to explore marketing opportunities and address industry challenges, including the impending deprecation of third-party cookies. This initiative reflects Diageo's strategy to capitalize on recent growth in the alcohol industry while preparing for future data-centric marketing demands.

title: The Dangers of Double Extortion Ransomware Attacks
summary: This article explains "double extortion" ransomware attacks, a growing trend where cybercriminals not only encrypt data but also exfiltrate it, threatening to leak or sell it if the ransom isn't paid. It highlights the shift from traditional ransomware to this new tactic, making data backups less effective. The article also provides a real-world example of such an attack detected by Darktrace, emphasizing the need for proactive cybersecurity measures.

title: Discovery CEO David Zaslav Steps Down From Lionsgate Board
summary: David Zaslav, CEO of Discovery, has stepped down from the Lionsgate board of directors, effective immediately. This move comes the same week Discovery announced its merger with WarnerMedia, increasing the competitive overlap between the companies. Directors often resign from boards when their primary company becomes a direct competitor.

title: Pega Infinity patches authentication vulnerability
summary: Security researchers discovered a critical password reset vulnerability in Pega Infinity, a popular enterprise software suite. This flaw could allow attackers to bypass authentication and fully compromise a Pega instance. Pega has released patches for both cloud and on-premises deployments, urging users to update their systems to address CVE-2021-27651.

title: Where and how to buy Alphabet (GOOGL) stock
summary: This guide provides a comprehensive overview for newcomers interested in buying Alphabet (GOOGL) stock. It covers essential steps such as choosing a trading platform, funding an account, deciding investment amounts, and differentiating between buying shares or ETFs. The article also explains various order types and the importance of monitoring GOOGL's performance.

title: Netflix in search of executive to oversee gaming expansion - source
summary: Netflix is actively seeking an executive to lead its expansion into the video game industry, signaling an intensified effort to diversify beyond its core streaming business amidst growing competition. This move comes as the gaming sector has seen a surge in demand, and Netflix has previously experimented with interactive content and games based on its popular shows. The company is reportedly considering a game bundle similar to Apple Arcade, with an internal decision that these games will not include advertising.

title: Nvidia sets 4-for-1 stock split, shares rise
summary: Nvidia Corp announced a four-for-one stock split to make its shares more affordable for investors, causing its stock to rise by 3%. This move follows similar splits by Apple and Tesla last year, with Nvidia's split requiring stockholder approval in June and effective mid-July.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook faces sharp questioning from judge in Epic Games trial
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook faced sharp questioning from Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers regarding Apple's App Store business model during the Epic Games v. Apple trial. The judge pressed Cook on the lack of competition in in-app purchases and expressed skepticism about Apple's Small Business Program. Cook defended Apple's policies by citing fierce competition in the smartphone market and the need for security and privacy for users.

title: How Apple, Tim Cook give into China while pushing back against the US
summary: This article criticizes Apple and CEO Tim Cook for their perceived double standard in handling user data and human rights issues. While Apple resisted data access requests from the FBI in the US, it has reportedly ceded control of customer data to the Chinese government and removed apps from its App Store in China that are critical of the regime. The author argues that corporations and free-world leaders must choose between financial gain and supporting free speech and human rights when dealing with China.

title: P&G Earns Seventh Straight Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award
summary: P&G has received its seventh consecutive Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award, an achievement that highlights its long-term excellence in supply chain management. This recognition comes despite the extreme disruptions caused by the pandemic, which tested P&G's supply chain significantly. P&G is one of only five companies, including Apple, Amazon, McDonald’s, and Unilever, to be recognized in the elite "Master" category, which acknowledges consistent top performance over many years.

title: Expedia Group hires top execs from Apple and Verizon as part of leadership structure overhaul
summary: Expedia Group has hired Jon Gieselman from Apple and Rathi Murthy from Verizon Media as part of a significant leadership and brand overhaul aimed at simplifying its operations and boosting revenue. These appointments come amidst organizational disarray, declining revenues due to the pandemic, and a restructuring into four key pillars: Expedia Services, Expedia Brands, Expedia Marketplace, and Expedia for Business. The changes are intended to streamline the company after years of acquisitions and regain clarity in its mission.

title: Apple Card - Family
summary: Apple Card Family allows users to share their Apple Card with a Co-Owner and up to four Participants, enabling shared financial management and credit building. Co-Owners manage the account together, build credit as equals, and can set spending limits for Participants, while everyone receives Daily Cash back. Participants aged 13 and over can spend, with those 18 and older also building their own credit history.

title: May 2021 Product Update -What’s New
summary: The May 2021 Fusion 360 product update introduces several new features and improvements across usability, sketching & modeling, electronics, generative design, and manufacturing. Key enhancements include Augmented Reality support via USDz export, a revamped Learning Panel, and expanded learning content for Fusion 360 Extensions. The update also brings significant advancements in electronics design with Stop Mask support in 3D PCB, improved display dialogs, and new library parts, alongside new generative design capabilities like Transparent View and enhanced manufacturing support for Formlabs and SLM Solutions 3D printers, plus machine simulation as a preview feature.

title: Samsung, TSMC up ante in US foundry competition
summary: The world's two largest foundry chipmakers, TSMC and Samsung Electronics, are engaged in fierce competition to expand their presence in the US, driven by significant investment plans. Samsung announced a $17 billion investment for a US factory, following TSMC's $12 billion Arizona plant. This rivalry, coupled with Intel's entry into the foundry business, intensifies the competition in the advanced chip manufacturing market, with both companies risking substantial losses if they fail to secure enough clients for their costly new facilities.

title: Flex manufactures everything from hair dryers to the Mac Pro — what’s next?
summary: This article features an interview with Flex CEO Revathi Advaithi, discussing the company's diverse manufacturing operations from consumer electronics to autonomous driving systems, and the complex interplay of politics, automation, and global supply chains. Advaithi also addresses the ongoing chip shortage, the future of manufacturing in the US, and Flex's commitment to sustainability. She highlights the company's shift towards investing in healthcare and automotive sectors due to technological changes.

title: App Store Arguments
summary: This article analyzes the legal, pragmatic, duopoly, moral, and anti-steering arguments surrounding Apple's App Store, particularly in light of the Epic Games v. Apple trial. The author suggests that while Apple has a strong legal standing based on Supreme Court precedents, there are valid counterarguments regarding competition, developer relationships, and the overall health of the iOS ecosystem. The piece concludes by suggesting Apple should consider conceding some ground to developers to foster a more open and innovative environment, especially given global regulatory scrutiny.

title: Future Looks Bright for Cloud Technology Degree
summary: Wake Tech's Cloud Infrastructure Associate in Applied Science (AAS) degree program is seeing high demand due to the increasing adoption of cloud technology by companies. The program prepares students for IT jobs in the Raleigh area, offering skills and credentials that align with industry needs, including hands-on experience in a unique Student Data Center. Graduates can expect a median salary of $85,000 and benefit from transfer partnerships for further education.

title: One Illinois McDonald’s is giving away iPhones to new hires
summary: One McDonald's location in Altamont, Illinois, is offering free iPhones to new hires as a recruitment incentive. The sign in the restaurant's window advertises "Now Hiring. Free iPhone." New employees will receive the Apple device after six months of employment.

title: Apple Faces Criticism Over Its Cooperation With China
summary: Apple is facing increasing criticism for its cooperation with Chinese laws, which involve sharing consumer data and censoring content, amid a wider technological and economic competition between the U.S. and China. The article also discusses the US Senate's "Endless Frontier Act" aimed at countering China's tech advancements, China's efforts to influence Latin American and African telecom markets, and its crackdown on cryptocurrency and its own tech giants.

title: Bitcoin and Ethereum are now, decentralized apps are the future
summary: Olaf Carlson-Wee, founder of Polychain Capital, believes decentralized web applications (DAOs) will be the next major trend in crypto, following the success of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and DeFi. He envisions decentralized versions of popular social media and chat apps owned by communities via crypto tokens, starting with simpler applications like photo hosting or messaging apps before moving to more complex platforms. Carlson-Wee also notes the rapid growth of decentralized exchanges (DEXes) like Uniswap, highlighting their collision course with regulators due to potential money laundering concerns.

title: Stock of the week - NVIDIA (27.05.2021)
summary: NVIDIA (NVDA.US) reported record first-quarter fiscal 2022 results, exceeding revenue and EPS expectations, driven by strong performance in Gaming, Data Center, and Professional Visualization platforms. The company also announced an upbeat Q2 forecast and a 4-for-1 stock split, which is seen as a bullish signal for investors. The growth is significantly boosted by the gaming sector during the pandemic and the increasing demand for its graphics cards by cryptocurrency miners.

title: S&P 500 trading guide: constituents, market hours and h
summary: This guide provides an in-depth look at the S&P 500, a key benchmark for U.S. stock market performance. It covers the index's constituents, listing criteria, calculation methods, factors affecting its price, and average annual returns. The article also details various ways to trade the S&P 500, including CFDs, futures, options, and ETFs, and discusses strategies for shorting the index.

title: AMD's Zen 5 CPUs, APUs Will Likely Tap TSMC's 3nm Process Node
summary: Rumors suggest that AMD's upcoming Zen 5 processors, expected around 2023, will utilize TSMC's 3nm manufacturing process node. These chips are anticipated to fall under the Ryzen 8000 branding, with codenames like Granite Ridge for mainstream CPUs and Strix Point for APUs. Strix Point APUs might feature a hybrid architecture combining Zen 5 and Zen 4 cores, potentially using a multi-chip module design.

title: Despite What You’ve Heard, Not All Tech Products Are Manufactured Overseas
summary: While many tech products like phones and computers are built overseas, a significant number of tech items and components are manufactured in the United States, particularly in Texas. Companies like Apple, Ericsson, Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Starlink have production facilities in the state, involving semiconductors, computers, consumer electronics, home audio, and electric vehicles. Despite consumer preference for American-made goods, most are unwilling to pay more than a 10% premium for them.

title: Yes, You Can Invest in a Tech-Less Nasdaq-100
summary: The article discusses the First Trust NASDAQ-100 Ex-Technology Sector Index Fund (QQXT), an ETF that allows investors to gain exposure to the Nasdaq-100 without the heavy technology sector allocation. This fund offers diversification away from tech and communication services while still providing growth potential and has historically outperformed the S&P 500 with less volatility. QQXT is presented as a compelling option for investors seeking growth outside of traditional tech-heavy portfolios.

title: Commander One - Free File Manager Compatible With M1-Powered Macs
summary: Electronic Team, Inc. has announced that its file manager, Commander One, now fully supports Apple Silicon Macs. This free, multifunctional file manager, written in Swift 2.0, offers a dual-panel interface and new features including native support for M1 chips, multipart upload for Amazon S3, and improved work with Backblaze B2 storage. A PRO Pack is also available with extended functionalities like connecting to remote servers, mounting various cloud storages, and advanced archive support.

title: Samsung Display to expand small OLED line with $2.7 bn investment
summary: Samsung Display plans to invest up to 3 trillion won ($2.7 billion) to build a new organic light-emitting diode (OLED) production line for small-size panels in an effort to maintain its market leadership amidst increasing competition from Chinese rivals. This expansion will boost its monthly production capacity of smaller OLED panels to 195,000 units and is part of its strategic withdrawal from the liquid crystal display (LCD) business. While Samsung's market share in mobile OLED panels is projected to decline, the company aims to capitalize on rising demand for OLED panels in smartphones and laptops, and will temporarily continue some large LCD panel production due to strong market prices.

title: iPhone 13 Pro, Pro Max 120Hz OLED displays enter mass production, report says
summary: Samsung and LG have started mass production of OLED panels for the upcoming Apple iPhone 13 series, with Samsung expected to be the primary supplier. Samsung will produce LTPO OLED displays for the iPhone 13 Pro and Pro Max models, enabling 120Hz refresh rates, while LG and BOE will supply 60Hz panels for the standard iPhone 13 and Mini. Apple is anticipated to launch four new iPhone models in September, continuing its strategy of offering both high-end and standard versions.

title: Chip giants are making more money than ever as the semiconductor shortage rages
summary: The world's 10 largest chip manufacturing companies achieved record revenues in the first quarter of 2021, totaling $22.75 billion, amidst a global semiconductor shortage. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. (TSMC) was a major contributor, accounting for 57% of global chip foundry revenues. The shortage, expected to last until 2023, is driven by high demand across various industries and production disruptions, leading to increased wafer prices and projected continued revenue growth for foundries.

title: TSMC Throws The Ball In Intel’s Court By Showcasing Customized Chipmaking Technologies
summary: TSMC announced new customized semiconductor nodes, N5A for automotive and N6RF for communications, at its online technology symposium. This move aims to solidify TSMC's position as a contract chip manufacturer by offering specialized processes, directly challenging Intel's plans to enter the contract manufacturing space. TSMC emphasized its commitment to not competing with its customers, differentiating itself from other potential foundries.

title: How to View Your Fusion 360 Designs in AR with USDz File Format
summary: Fusion 360 has introduced support for the USDz file format, enabling users to view their 3D designs in augmented reality (AR) directly from iOS mobile devices. This feature allows designers to preview designs in real-life environments, assess scale and aesthetics, and communicate concepts more effectively to clients and stakeholders. The article explains what USDz is, how to export files from Fusion 360, and highlights the benefits of using AR for design visualization.

title: The Moomoo Trading App Launches New Referral Program - Moomoo Ambassador
summary: Moomoo Inc., a subsidiary of Futu Holdings Limited, has launched a new customer referral program called "Moomoo Ambassador" for its moomoo stock investment platform. This program allows users to earn free stocks by referring new customers, with rewards for both the referrer and the referee, valued between $8 and $1,000. As a launch bonus, Moomoo Ambassadors can receive a guaranteed share of Apple stock for referring two qualifying new customers between June 1st and July 1st, 2021.

title: LG Electronics expands overseas appliance plants as it exits phone business
summary: LG Electronics is expanding its overseas production facilities in key markets like the US, Brazil, Vietnam, and Saudi Arabia after exiting its mobile phone business. The company is shifting its focus to home appliances, automotive parts, and telematics, investing significantly to ramp up capacity and improve productivity. With these strategic changes, LG is projected to surpass Whirlpool as the top appliance maker this year.

title: The Moomoo Trading App Launches New Referral Program - Moomoo Ambassador
Microsoft’s Irish subsidiary posted £220bn profit in single year | Tax havens
summary: Microsoft's Irish subsidiary, Microsoft Round Island One, recorded a profit of $315 billion (£222 billion) in a single year, which is nearly three-quarters of Ireland's GDP, despite having no employees other than directors. The company is resident for tax purposes in Bermuda, which has no corporation tax, allowing it to pay no tax on income. This news emerged as G7 finance ministers discussed global minimum corporate tax rates to combat multinational tax avoidance.

title: Apple Prepares Office Staff for Hybrid Workweek
summary: Apple is preparing its office staff for a hybrid workweek, requiring employees to return to the office three days a week starting in September. CEO Tim Cook announced that office workers are expected to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays, with the flexibility to work remotely on Wednesdays and Fridays. This move places Apple among a growing number of companies adopting a hybrid work model.

title: June Deal for Futu’s Moomoo: Free Apple and NIO Share For New Users, $50 For Share Transfer-in For All Users, And Twitter Shares for Referring Your Friends!
summary: Futu SG's Moomoo trading app is offering promotional deals for new and existing users in June. New users can receive free Apple and NIO shares by signing up, depositing funds, and completing trades, while existing users can earn Twitter shares through referrals and a $50 cash coupon for transferring shares. The promotion highlights commission-free trading for 180 days and free market data, aiming to attract more clients to its regulated platform.

title: AT&T, Verizon Push Free iPhones for Long-Term Customers
summary: U.S. cellphone carriers like AT&T and Verizon are offering significant discounts on new smartphones, including free iPhones, to encourage long-term customer commitments. This strategy provides stability for carriers as they invest heavily in 5G network upgrades and reinforces their customer base. The promotions highlight a resurgence of wireless contracts in exchange for valuable device subsidies.

title: How Apple Lossless Audio Challenges Streaming Rivals
summary: Apple is entering the high-fidelity audio market with lossless, high-resolution, and spatial audio for Apple Music, aiming to differentiate itself from competitors like Spotify and Amazon Prime. This move challenges streaming rivals by offering superior sound quality, as lossless audio preserves all original sound data, unlike compressed formats such as MP3s. This strategy focuses on technological advancement to improve the listening experience.

title: Apple is turning privacy into a business advantage, not just a marketing slogan
summary: Apple is increasingly integrating privacy measures into its products and services, differentiating itself from competitors. Recent announcements include features like email tracking prevention, IP address obfuscation, and burner emails, all under the umbrella of iCloud+. The company leverages its self-designed chips and on-device processing capabilities to enhance privacy and introduce new functionalities in areas like health and digital identity, reinforcing user trust and opening new market opportunities.

title: iOS 15 brings powerful new features to stay connected, focus, explore, and more
summary: Apple announced iOS 15, introducing major updates like more natural FaceTime calls with SharePlay for shared experiences, new Focus tools to reduce distractions, and enhanced on-device intelligence for features like Live Text and Spotlight. The update also brings a redesigned Safari, advanced Apple Maps, and expanded Apple Wallet capabilities for keys and state IDs. Privacy controls are strengthened, and redesigned Weather and Notes apps offer richer experiences, along with numerous other new features across the system.

title: Apple advances its privacy leadership with iOS 15, iPadOS 15, macOS Monterey, and watchOS 8
summary: Apple has introduced significant new privacy features across its operating systems — iOS 15, iPadOS 15, macOS Monterey, and watchOS 8 — to enhance user control over personal data. Key innovations include Mail Privacy Protection, App Privacy Report, on-device Siri processing, and iCloud+ features like Private Relay and Hide My Email. These updates aim to protect users from tracking, provide transparency into app data access, and secure online activities.

title: Apple's new 'private relay' feature will not be available in China
summary: Apple announced that its new "private relay" feature, designed to enhance user privacy by obscuring web browsing behavior, will not be available in China due to regulatory reasons. This decision represents another compromise by Apple in a country that is a significant market for the company, following previous adjustments like moving iCloud data keys to China. The feature, which routes web traffic through multiple servers to prevent tracking, will also be withheld in several other countries.

title: Adobe: Creative Cloud app performance surges more than 80% on M1 MacBook Pro vs. Intel-based systems
summary: Adobe's Creative Cloud applications demonstrate over 80% faster performance on Macs equipped with Apple's M1 processor compared to Intel-based systems, according to a study commissioned by Adobe. Native M1 versions of several Creative Cloud apps have been released, with Premiere Pro currently in public beta. Key findings include significant performance gains for Adobe Sensei features and apps like Photoshop and Lightroom Classic on the M1 chip.

title: Here are Tuesday's biggest analyst calls of the day: Netflix, Apple, AMC, Dave & Buster's & more
summary: This article summarizes major analyst calls made on Tuesday, June 8, 2021, featuring companies like Netflix, Apple, AMC, and Dave & Buster's. KeyBanc upgraded Generac, Morgan Stanley reiterated overweight ratings for Tesla and Apple, and Jefferies upgraded Delta and Lamb Weston to buy. Notable reiterations include Bank of America on Amazon (buy) and MKM on AMC (sell), with analysts providing brief justifications for their ratings.

title: Apple in talks with Chinese suppliers for EV batteries: Report
summary: Apple is in early-stage discussions with Chinese battery suppliers CATL and BYD for its planned electric vehicle. A key condition from Apple is that potential suppliers build manufacturing facilities in the United States, but CATL is reportedly hesitant due to political tensions and cost concerns. The report also indicates Apple's interest in cheaper lithium iron phosphate batteries and targets a 2024 launch for its self-driving passenger vehicle.

title: Apple Music’s Zane Lowe explains how Spatial Audio will transform music
summary: Zane Lowe, Apple Music's co-head of Artist Relations, discusses the transformative potential of Spatial Audio for both artists and fans. He emphasizes how this new immersive technology, with sound moving in a three-dimensional space, will allow artists to create music in entirely new ways, moving beyond traditional two-channel stereo. Lowe believes Spatial Audio will change how music feels to listeners and foresees future artists growing up creating specifically for this format.

title: Premiere Pro Beta on Apple M1: The results are in
summary: The Premiere Pro Beta for Apple M1 has shown significant performance improvements, with testing by Pfeiffer Report revealing it is 77 percent faster on average than comparable Intel-based systems. Key areas like importing, playback, and exports, as well as specific effects and Adobe Sensei features, exhibit substantial speed enhancements. The optimizations also contribute to improved battery life due to efficient use of the M1’s Unified Memory.

title: How fast can stores fill curbside pickup orders?
summary: This article examines the varying speeds at which different retailers fulfill curbside pickup orders, highlighting Dick's Sporting Goods as a leader with over 90% of orders ready within 15 minutes. It discusses how retailers are balancing customer expectations for speed and convenience with operational challenges like staffing and inventory management. The piece concludes that curbside pickup remains a significant part of omnichannel retail, with speed and accuracy being critical differentiators.

title: Ferrari appoints STMicroelectronics' Benedetto Vigna as new CEO
summary: Ferrari has appointed Benedetto Vigna, a technology industry veteran from STMicroelectronics, as its new CEO. This move signals Ferrari's commitment to developing high-end electric vehicles and integrating cutting-edge technology, aligning with Chairman John Elkann's vision for an electrified future. Vigna will face challenges including leading the company into electric mobility, reviving its Formula One performance, and managing brand extension while maintaining exclusivity.

title: Apple's New Private Relay Feature Seen Tied to Fastly, Akamai, and Cloudflare
summary: Apple's new iCloud Private Relay feature, announced at their Worldwide Developers Conference, is believed to be connected to content delivery network providers like Fastly, Akamai, and Cloudflare. This privacy feature aims to mask users' online identities when browsing websites. The article also touches upon Fastly's stock surge on the day of an outage, suggesting it might be related to Apple's announcements.

title: XPO Says GXO Spinoff Should Be Completed by Q3
summary: XPO Logistics announced an accelerated timeline for the spinoff of its logistics operations into GXO Logistics, now expected to be completed in the third quarter of this year. GXO Logistics anticipates strong organic revenue growth of 8% to 12% in 2022 and increased EBITDA guidance, while XPO will continue to focus on transportation services. The separation aims to create two independent public companies, each with enhanced growth prospects in their respective markets.

title: Apple announces winners of the 2021 Apple Design Awards
summary: Apple announced the winners of its 2021 Apple Design Awards, recognizing 12 apps and games across six new categories: Inclusivity, Delight and Fun, Interaction, Social Impact, Visuals and Graphics, and Innovation. Developers from around the world were celebrated for their outstanding achievements in design, creativity, and technical excellence, with winners receiving a physical award and hardware package. The awards highlight the essential role these apps and games play in daily life and their innovative use of Apple technologies.

title: Tower Theatre - Apple Store
summary: The Apple Tower Theatre in Los Angeles is a landmark of design and innovation, originally built during Hollywood's Golden Age and famed for its technology and creative flair. The theater underwent an extensive renovation, preserving its historic character while transforming it into a modern Apple Store that hosts live events, musical performances, and creative learning sessions. It offers various Apple services including Genius Bar appointments, Today at Apple sessions, and options for purchasing and financing Apple products.

title: Start-up: From engineer to IoT entrepreneur
summary: Teng Kong Leong transitioned from an engineer at multinational companies to an IoT entrepreneur, founding Wondernica Research PLT in 2014 and later Wondernica Technologies Sdn Bhd in 2018. The company specializes in end-to-end IoT solutions for smart cities, focusing on smart agriculture and environmental monitoring, and is expanding into cold chain logistics. Despite slow IoT adoption in Malaysia, Wondernica is gaining traction internationally and through pilot projects, aiming to provide affordable solutions by designing both hardware and software.

title: Airbnb and Booking targets of G7 push for higher taxes for multinationals and EU focus on restraining gatekeepers
summary: The article discusses how Airbnb and Booking.com are becoming targets for higher taxation and stricter regulation due to evolving international policies. The G7 summit's proposal for a 15% global minimum corporate tax and a push for multinationals to pay taxes where they sell services directly challenges current practices of these platforms, which often pay lower taxes in countries like Ireland. Additionally, the EU's Digital Services Act aims to regulate large digital platforms like Airbnb and Booking.com as "gatekeepers," potentially leading to increased complexity for hosts and greater data sharing with municipalities.

title: Check out Target’s ‘before’ and ‘after’ store remodel photos
summary: Target Corp. is planning to remodel approximately 150 stores this year, which will include modern fixtures, updated lighting, enhanced experiences, and health and safety upgrades. These remodels are part of a larger initiative, with over 800 locations already upgraded in the past four years, and will feature improvements to guest services, order pickup, drive-up areas, and new "shop in shop" experiences with brands like Ulta Beauty, Apple, and Disney.

title: IKEA's new Sonos Symfonisk Picture Frame speaker launches for $199
summary: IKEA has announced the launch of its new Sonos Symfonisk Picture Frame speaker, priced at $199, as the latest collaboration with Sonos. This flat, wall-mountable speaker is designed to offer robust audio despite its slim profile, featuring a woofer and a tweeter with a wave guide for room-filling sound. It supports Wi-Fi and AirPlay 2, can be integrated into a Sonos multiroom audio setup, and will be available starting July 15.

title: Steve Wozniak Revealed as Keynote Speaker for Spiceworks Ziff Davis’ Annual IT Conference, SpiceWorld 2021
summary: Apple Co-founder Steve Wozniak has been announced as the keynote speaker for SpiceWorld 2021, Spiceworks Ziff Davis' annual IT conference. The virtual event, scheduled for September 27-29, 2021, will feature Wozniak in a fireside chat discussing his journey, technological innovation, and entrepreneurial mindset. Attendees can register for free to hear Wozniak's insights into the future of technology and his role in founding Apple.

title: Bernstein recommends Sea, says Singapore-based internet company is a 'super app in the making'
summary: Bernstein recommends Sea Ltd., calling the Singapore-based internet company a "super app in the making" and initiating coverage with an outperform rating and a $350 price target. The investment firm believes Sea's strong execution track record ensures its dominance in various verticals, particularly highlighting the potential of its e-commerce arm, Shopee, to reach breakeven by 2025. Key drivers for the stock are expected to be Shopee's expansion in Latin America and grocery in Southeast Asia, leading to increased profitability.

title: This is how Black women can be seen, heard and promoted at work
summary: Susan Somersille Johnson, CMO for Prudential Financial Inc., shares her journey from a shy electrical engineer to a marketing leader, emphasizing the importance of networking, stepping out of one's comfort zone, and intentionality in career growth. She advises Black women to advocate for themselves by actively communicating their successes to leaders to ensure they are seen, heard, and promoted. Somersille Johnson also discusses the impact of the past year on her perspective as a Black woman and the importance of integrating one's life to foster diversity and inclusion in the workplace.

title: Inside a factors-focused model portfolio
summary: Tim Courtney, CIO of Exencial Wealth Advisors, discusses his firm's all-equity model portfolio, highlighting its focus on Dimensional Fund Advisors (DFA) funds for their factor-based weighting towards value, small companies, and profitability, despite recent underperformance. He also addresses portfolio diversification, managing overlap, and specific international fund selections like the DFA International Small Cap Value fund and American Funds EuroPacific Growth fund.

title: Apple Still Has a Privacy Problem
summary: Apple, despite its public stance on privacy, still faces significant challenges in protecting user data, as highlighted by recent DOJ data seizures. The company has handed over user data thousands of times to law enforcement and still does not encrypt iCloud backups, leaving sensitive information vulnerable. To truly be a champion of privacy, Apple needs to adopt practices demonstrated by smaller, privacy-focused companies that design their products to gather and retain minimal user data.

title: Inland Northwest banks, credit unions keep branching out
summary: Despite the rise in electronic transactions, financial institutions in the Inland Northwest continue to invest in brick-and-mortar branches. Companies like Gesa Credit Union, Numerica Credit Union, and Banner Bank are expanding or renovating physical locations, recognizing that while digital banking is prevalent, clients still value face-to-face services and physical accessibility for relationship building and complex financial discussions. This trend suggests a hybrid approach where physical branches evolve to complement digital offerings, focusing on customer experience and trust.

title: Kopin Pancake Optics to ‘Reshape’ MR Headsets
