summary: Kopin Corp has developed fully-plastic Pancake optics designed to significantly improve virtual, augmented, and mixed reality headsets. These new optics offer magnified image quality with a thinner and lighter form factor, aiming to overcome the bulkiness that has hindered consumer adoption. Kopin has filed multiple patents for this technology, which promises to make MR headsets much lighter and more compact.

title: Yu v. Apple Settles It: The CAFC is Suffering from a Prolonged Version of Alice in Wonderland Syndrome
summary: This article criticizes the Federal Circuit's handling of patent eligibility cases, particularly Yu v. Apple, arguing that the court is suffering from a "prolonged version of Alice in Wonderland Syndrome." The author contends that the court's interpretations of patent eligibility under 35 U.S.C. 101 are flawed, leading to tangible inventions being deemed abstract, and that the court disregards proper claim construction and statutory guidelines. The piece calls for Congress to address the perceived issues with patent eligibility jurisprudence.

title: Will Intuitive Surgical Be a Trillion-Dollar Stock by 2031?
summary: This article analyzes whether Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) can achieve a trillion-dollar market capitalization by 2031. It compares Intuitive's current market cap and growth trajectory to trillion-dollar tech companies, noting its dominance in robotic surgery market but also potential challenges from rivals and revenue growth limitations. Although reaching $1 trillion is deemed unlikely, the author remains optimistic about Intuitive Surgical as a strong long-term investment for healthcare portfolios.

title: Wang: $ZH Remains My Top Pick
summary: Felix Wang, a China analyst at HedgeyeTech, reiterates Zhihu (ZH) as his top pick, highlighting its significant outperformance with a 40% gain since being designated a "best Long" three months prior. The article explains that ZH is focused on expanding its content e-commerce solutions (Zhi+) and establishing itself as a new content leader, particularly by embracing "videolization" and attracting diverse content creators. Wang draws a parallel between ZH and Bilibili (BILI), suggesting ZH has substantial growth potential if it can emulate BILI's success to reach a similar market capitalization.

title: William Kaufman, Travelers Cos. Seal $185M Loan for 437 Madison Avenue
summary: The William Kaufman Organization and The Travelers Companies have closed a $185 million refinance loan for 437 Madison Avenue, with Wells Fargo providing the new debt. This loan retires $145 million in existing debt from Bank of America and Apple Bank. Eastdil Secured brokered the deal on behalf of the owners.

title: App Tracking Transparency and the Future of App Measurement
summary: This whitepaper from Comscore discusses the implications of App Tracking Transparency (ATT) and other privacy changes, including iOS 14.5, on mobile app measurement for content publishers and advertisers. It explores how Comscore and similar providers can address measurement challenges while upholding user privacy. The paper highlights the evolving landscape of web browser environments and privacy regulations.

title: Berkshire Hathaway: Interesting Facts About The Company
summary: This article delves into interesting facts about Berkshire Hathaway, led by Warren Buffett, based on insights from its 2020 annual report. It highlights Berkshire's unique approach as a conglomerate, its diverse portfolio including 100% and less-than-100% owned companies, and its strategy of share buybacks to enhance shareholder value. The piece also discusses Buffett's candid acknowledgment of a past investment mistake, the company's two classes of shares (BRK.A and BRK.B), its no-dividend policy, and the succession plan for its leadership.

title: Amazon and Apple: A tale of two closed-loop visions
summary: This article contrasts the circular economy visions of Amazon and Apple, detailing their different approaches to sustainability despite their massive scale and technological leadership. It highlights Amazon's focus on customer education and packaging reduction, and Apple's emphasis on supply chain engagement, material recycling, and product design for circularity. Both companies integrate social justice into their sustainability efforts, supporting initiatives for underserved communities.

title: What to expect from Microsoft's next version of Windows
summary: Microsoft is preparing to unveil a significant update to Windows, which a leaked build suggests will be called Windows 11. This new version is expected to feature design changes, including a centered taskbar and rounded window corners, along with modern features like enhanced window snapping and better multi-monitor support. Analysts are also watching for potential business model shifts, such as a "Windows-as-a-Service" offering or stronger M365 integration.

title: Why Steven Spielberg’s Netflix deal is not as groundbreaking as Netflix wants you to think
summary: Steven Spielberg's production company, Amblin Partners, struck a deal with Netflix, leading to significant media attention. While Netflix aims to leverage Spielberg's name for content and subscribers, the article argues the deal is not as groundbreaking as it seems, as Amblin's existing theatrical commitments with Universal remain unaffected, and Spielberg's personal directing projects are not exclusive to Netflix. The financial details and number of films in the Netflix deal also remain unspecified, dampening its perceived impact.

title: S&P ETF Fortunes Reverse In 2021
summary: In 2021, S&P 500 ETFs with alternative weighting methodologies, such as reverse market cap (YPS) and equal weight (RSP), began outperforming the traditional market-cap-weighted SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY). This shift is attributed to the underperformance of large-cap tech stocks and the better performance of mid-cap and smaller companies benefiting from the U.S. economic reopening. While alternative strategies show short-term gains, SPY maintains long-term outperformance and significant cost advantages due to its size and liquidity.

title: Microsoft challenges Apple's business model with new Windows 11 operating system
summary: Microsoft unveiled Windows 11, its first major operating system update since 2015, which directly challenges Apple's lucrative App Store model. The new Windows Store will allow developers to use their own in-app payment systems without commissions and enable Android apps to run on Windows PCs. This move contrasts sharply with Apple's "walled garden" approach and also competes with Google Play Store's commission structure, signaling a significant shift in Microsoft's strategy to attract developers and consumers.

title: Passbook by Remitly Launches Suite of New Features
summary: Remitly's Passbook app has launched several new digital banking features to better serve immigrants, including a streamlined identity verification process, faster bank transfers, early payday access, person-to-person payments, and immediate activation of the Passbook Visa debit card. These enhancements aim to address common barriers immigrants face in accessing banking services, such as high fees and restrictive documentation. The company emphasizes its commitment to financial inclusion and responding to direct customer feedback to transform traditional financial processes.

title: Google delays Chrome's blocking of tracking cookies to late 2023
summary: Google has announced a delay in fully blocking tracking cookies in its Chrome web browser until late 2023, nearly two years later than initially planned. This decision comes amidst antitrust concerns from competitors and regulators, with Google citing the need for sufficient time for public discussion and for the advertising industry to adapt. The delay has positively impacted shares of companies reliant on cookies, while regulatory bodies like Britain's CMA and the EU's competition commission are actively involved in overseeing Google's changes.

title: Apple Explores Bigger iPads and Reshuffles Its Car Team
summary: Apple is reportedly exploring the development of larger iPad models and making significant changes to its secretive Apple Car project. Additionally, the company Peloton is positioning itself to compete with the Apple Watch in the wearable technology market, while Amazon is delving into augmented reality.

title: T-Mobile cleverly brings free 30-day Test Drive program to iPhone XS and later via eSIM
summary: T-Mobile has launched a new, seamless 30-day Test Drive program for iPhone XS users and later, utilizing the device's built-in eSIM. This allows users to trial T-Mobile's network, including 5G access for iPhone 12 owners, with 30 days or 30GB of high-speed data, unlimited talk and text, all without switching carriers, needing a hotspot device, or providing credit card information. The program is set up through a new iOS app and is designed for risk-free network evaluation alongside their current service.

title: Medtronic Launches App-Based Study to Better Understand Relationship Between Atrial Fibrillation Disease Burden and Impact on Patient Outcomes
summary: Medtronic has launched the DEFINE AFib study, its first app-based research initiative, to investigate the relationship between atrial fibrillation (AF) burden and its impact on patient outcomes, quality of life, and healthcare utilization. The study will leverage data from Medtronic insertable cardiac monitors and the Discovery App, aiming to enroll approximately 5,000 patients with AF. This innovative approach to clinical research is designed to personalize AF management strategies through comprehensive data insights.

title: Antitrust and Platform Monopoly
summary: This article argues that large digital platforms are not "winner-take-all" entities and require competition, making antitrust policy relevant. It suggests that antitrust's firm-specific approach is better suited than broad regulation to address threats to platform competition, particularly when platforms exert market power over other firms. The author proposes remedies beyond breaking up companies, such as restructuring management, requiring interoperability, or mandating information pooling to foster competition and leverage network effects, while also noting that existing merger enforcement tools are inadequate for platform acquisitions of nascent firms.

title: Ahead of the Apple iPhone 13 Launch, These Chip Makers Look Like Buys
summary: As the launch of Apple's iPhone 13 approaches in September, Barclays analyst Blayne Curtis has identified several mobile-chip makers as promising investment opportunities. Qorvo, Cirrus Logic, Skyworks, and Broadcom are highlighted as favored stocks that could benefit from the anticipated product release. The article suggests that the market sees significant upside not only for Apple but also for its component suppliers.

title: Target to remodel Franklin store that could include in-store shop by Ulta Beauty, Apple or Disney
summary: Target plans to remodel its Franklin, Wisconsin store as part of a larger initiative to renovate approximately 150 stores in 2021. The renovation aims to enhance the guest experience and could potentially include in-store shops featuring brands like Ulta Beauty, Apple, or Disney. This move reflects Target's ongoing investment in upgrading its physical retail locations.

title: Aon turns to Latham antitrust team for $30 bln DOJ merger battle
summary: Insurance broker Aon plc has engaged Latham & Watkins' antitrust team to defend its planned $30 billion acquisition of Willis Towers Watson against a U.S. Justice Department lawsuit. Six Latham partners, including attorneys with significant prior experience at the Justice Department and FTC, have filed notices of appearance in the D.C. federal court case. The Justice Department alleges the merger would reduce competition and lead to higher prices in the insurance market.

title: Euro Tax Flash from KPMG's EU Tax Centre
summary: KPMG's Euro Tax Flash reports on the historic agreement reached by 130 countries in the OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework on BEPS 2.0, aiming to reform international tax rules with a two-pillar approach. Pillar One reallocates taxing rights for large companies to market countries, while Pillar Two establishes a global minimum tax rate of at least 15%. The article also discusses potential EU implementation challenges and notes that some countries, like Ireland, have reservations about certain aspects of the agreement.
authors: Raluca Enache|Robert van der Jagt|Mr Vinod Kalloe

title: Qualcomm's new CEO eyes dominance in laptop markets
summary: Qualcomm's new CEO, Cristiano Amon, aims to dominate the laptop chip market by next year, challenging Apple's energy-efficient chips with custom-designed processors. The company plans to leverage its acquisition of Nuvia and a team of ex-Apple chip architects to create leading performance for battery-powered devices. Amon also anticipates significant revenue growth from China, despite political tensions, and is open to licensing Nuvia's designs for cloud computing data centers.

title: Focus: Qualcomm's new CEO eyes dominance in the laptop markets
summary: Qualcomm's new CEO, Cristiano Amon, plans to challenge Apple's dominance in the laptop market by developing energy-efficient chips with the help of former Apple chip architects. The company is also focusing on revenue growth from China for its smartphone chip business despite political tensions and aims to leverage its Nuvia acquisition to push 5G connectivity into laptops, providing custom-designed chips to compete with Apple. Amon also faces the challenge of potentially losing Apple as a modem chip customer and improving Qualcomm's brand recognition among consumers.

title: Apple, Intel become first to adopt TSMC's latest chip tech - Nikkei
summary: Apple and Intel are reportedly the first companies to adopt Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) next-generation 3-nanometer chip production technology. Both companies are currently testing their chip designs with TSMC's new tech, with commercial production anticipated to begin in the second half of the following year. This development signifies a significant advancement in chip manufacturing and potential future product innovations from Apple and Intel.

title: TSMC Wins Intel Order For Two 3nm CPUs To Regain Market Share Lost To AMD
summary: TSMC has secured an order from Intel to produce two 3nm CPUs, signaling Intel's strategy to regain market share lost to AMD, particularly in notebooks and data centers. This move marks a significant shift for Intel, which has historically relied on its own manufacturing facilities, and indicates a larger 3nm chip order for Intel than for Apple. Mass production for TSMC's 3nm process is expected to begin next year, with commercial output of Intel's chips following in 2023.

title: Take-Two Interactive Software acquires Dynamixyz
summary: Take-Two Interactive Software has acquired facial motion capture firm Dynamixyz, whose software Performer was used by VFX and game studios. Dynamixyz will now operate as a division of Take-Two, with its technology becoming exclusively available to Take-Two's publishing labels and studios, including Rockstar Games. This acquisition is part of a trend where game developers are buying out mocap tech firms.

title: Amazon stock nabs record after Bezos-Jassy handoff
summary: Amazon's stock reached an all-time high of $3,675.74 after Andy Jassy officially took over as CEO from Jeff Bezos. Investors appear confident in Jassy, who previously ran Amazon Web Services, as the company nears a $2 trillion market capitalization, aiming to join Apple and Microsoft. Jassy also received a substantial incentive package of 61,000 shares, valued at over $214 million, vesting over 10 years.

title: 'Abuse of dominance': Was this what Congress originally intended for Section 2?
summary: The article discusses a potential shift in U.S. antitrust enforcement, moving toward stricter policing of single-firm dominance, akin to the European "abuse of dominance" standard. This change is influenced by Senator Amy Klobuchar's proposed legislation and President Biden's appointment of Lina M. Khan to the FTC, reflecting concerns about the market power of technology giants. The author argues that this renewed approach to antitrust law may align more closely with the original intent of the Sherman Act, which historically sought to prevent concentration of power.

title: LG swings battle for premium TV supremacy
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated demand for premium TVs, with OLED TV shipments increasing significantly. LG Electronics has seen its share of the premium TV market rise to 28% by Q1, while Samsung's share declined. LG Display's OLED technology is driving this shift, with confidence in OLED's image quality, eye health benefits, and environmental advantages leading to continued market growth and diversification among TV manufacturers.

title: US teens: iPhones hot, Android phones — not
summary: A Piper Sandler survey indicates that 88% of US teens use an iPhone, and 90% expect their next phone to be an iPhone, marking all-time highs. This dominance is further supported by a Counterpoint report showing that Apple accounted for one in two smartphone sales in the US in 2020. The article suggests this trend, alongside LG's exit from the smartphone market, points to growing iPhone sales and an widening gap between Apple and Android.

title: Whatever Happened To European Corporate Competitors?
summary: This article examines the decline of European corporate giants on the global stage compared to American and Asian firms. It highlights how Apple's market cap dwarfs entire European stock indices and notes that no European firm has been in the global top ten since 2015. The author attributes this decline to Europe's risk-averse business culture, lack of innovation in digital tech, and less integrated market, despite similar GDP growth to the US.

title: Apple May Join Forces with LG to Keep Samsung in Check
summary: Apple and LG Group are reportedly partnering to counter Samsung Electronics' dominance in the global market. Apple is leveraging LG Electronics' exit from the mobile business by offering trade-in programs for LG smartphone users in North America and Korea. Additionally, LG is exploring new business ventures with Apple, including selling Apple products in LG Best Shops and potentially collaborating on the Apple Car project through LG Magna e-Powertrain.

title: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak is a right to repair advocate
summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak has publicly expressed strong support for the right to repair movement, contrasting it with Apple's current practices. He believes that an open-source mindset, reminiscent of early Apple, is crucial, and companies inhibit repair rights to consolidate power and profit. Wozniak's comments come as President Biden considers new rules to expand consumer repair options.

title: "Stratospheric" tech company valuations outstrip US GDP
summary: New research indicates that tech company valuations have reached over $35 trillion, surpassing the entire US GDP of $20.81 trillion in 2020. This boom is fueled by substantial investor interest, with over $40 billion invested in tech vendors in the first half of 2021 alone, and more than two "unicorns" (billion-dollar tech firms) being created daily. The tech sector is seen as a resilient and safe investment, especially post-Covid-19, intensified by global government support and central bank liquidity.

title: Employers offer stipends for babysitting and tutoring to sweeten child-care benefits and win over workers
summary: Companies are increasingly offering enhanced child-care benefits, including backup care, stipends for tutoring and babysitting, and even college counseling, to attract and retain employees, especially working parents. This trend emerged during the Covid-19 pandemic as employers recognized the need to support workers with caregiving responsibilities, aiming to bring women back into the workforce and improve overall employee well-being. These expanded benefits also extend to support for older children and elderly family members, reflecting a broader shift in employer-employee relationships.

title: Harley-Davidson's First LiveWire Brand Electric Motorcycle Launches
summary: Harley-Davidson has spun off its electric motorcycle business into a new sub-brand called LiveWire, launching its first model, the LiveWire One. The LiveWire One starts at $21,999, offers a 146-mile electric range, and can charge from zero to 100 percent in 60 minutes. It's essentially the same as the previous Harley-Davidson LiveWire but with new branding and a lower price point, making its physical debut on July 18th in Northern California.

title: Remote work flexibility key for employee retention
summary: The article argues that offering remote and hybrid work options is crucial for employee retention and satisfaction, contradicting some business leaders' fears about productivity. It highlights that employees desire flexibility for personal well-being and increased productivity, and companies failing to adapt risk losing top talent. The author emphasizes that technology supports virtual collaboration, making traditional work models less relevant.

title: Apple’s Co-Founder Has Harsh Words for the Company Over Its Control of Your Devices
summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak has harshly criticized the company's stance against the "right-to-repair" movement, arguing that Apple's current practices restrict consumer access to repair information and parts. Wozniak contends that the early success of Apple was built on an open technological environment where users could understand and fix their devices. His comments align with growing pressure from regulators like the FTC against company-imposed repair restrictions.

title: T-Mobile has fastest 5G speeds with widest availability in the US
summary: A new report indicates that T-Mobile leads other US carriers in 5G download speed and availability, with its sub-6GHz 5G service proving most impactful for average users. While Verizon and AT&T offer superior experiences in voice, video, and gaming, T-Mobile's 5G speeds reached 87.5 Mbps, and its merger with Sprint is helping it expand rapidly. The superior but less available mmWave 5G showed Verizon nearly doubling T-Mobile's speeds.

title: Broadcom in talks to buy software firm SAS Institute - WSJ
summary: Broadcom Inc is reportedly in talks to acquire software firm SAS Institute Inc for a deal valued between $15 billion and $20 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal. This potential acquisition would allow Broadcom to expand its infrastructure software solutions business. SAS Institute provides data and analytics solutions to a wide range of customers globally across various industries.

title: Apple escapes email app maker's antitrust claims over sign-on feature
summary: Apple Inc. has successfully defended against antitrust claims brought by email app maker Blix Inc. in a Delaware federal court. Blix alleged that Apple's "Sign In With Apple" feature stifles competition, but the court dismissed Blix's third complaint, ruling that Apple's policy does not restrict competition and actually expands consumer choice. This final ruling concludes the case after two previous decisions also favored Apple.

title: Large office development planned for 57 acres in Northwest Austin
summary: Dallas-based Trammell Crow and Los Angeles-based Karlin Real Estate LLC are planning a new office campus, named Duval, with over 1 million square feet of space on a 57-acre site in Northwest Austin that previously housed a 3M manufacturing plant. The first phase will include two buildings totaling 485,000 square feet, with the entire campus targeted for completion in the second quarter of 2023. This development is part of a surge in commercial activity in Northwest Austin, further fueled by Apple Inc.'s new campus in the area, despite some local residents' concerns about increased traffic.

title: Is Nvidia Stock A Buy Now Before Its Stock Split? (NASDAQ:NVDA)
summary: NVIDIA has announced a stock split effective July 20, which historically can lead to short-term price movements. While growth and valuation are long-term drivers, NVIDIA is well-positioned for future growth but its shares are currently historically expensive. The article explores whether NVIDIA is a buy before its upcoming stock split, considering both its growth prospects and current valuation.

title: Boosted by Earnings Reports, S&P and Nasdaq Hit New Heights
summary: The S&P and Nasdaq indices reached new record highs due to strong earnings reports and the performance of mega-cap stocks, particularly in the technology sector. The article highlights how declining Treasury yields bolstered the growth thesis for mega-cap stocks, and discusses the Principal U.S. Mega-Cap ETF (USMC) as a way to capture these market movements. USMC tracks a modified equal-weighted index of mega-cap companies, focusing on minimizing volatility while seeking optimal outcomes.

title: Spotify’s Top Lawyer Has Spent Years Making Apple the Bad Guy
summary: Horacio Gutierrez, Spotify’s chief legal officer, has been a key figure in the ongoing antitrust battle against Apple. Having previously defended Microsoft in an antitrust case, Gutierrez has now switched sides and led Spotify's campaign, aiming to portray Apple as an abusive monopolist. His efforts have contributed to a significant shift in public perception regarding Apple's business practices, making it a target for antitrust scrutiny alongside other tech giants.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares close at all-time high of $149.15/share
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares closed at an all-time high of $149.15 on July 14, 2021, marking a significant milestone for the company. This achievement comes shortly before Apple is set to report its quarterly earnings later the same month.

title: Apple plans a ‘buy now, pay later’ service for all purchases
summary: Apple is developing a "buy now, pay later" service, internally called Apple Pay Later, for all Apple Pay purchases, rivaling offerings from Affirm and PayPal. The service will offer interest-free payments over four installments or interest-bearing payments over several months, with Goldman Sachs Group Inc. acting as the lender. This initiative aims to increase Apple Pay adoption and revenue for Apple's services business.

title: Facebook Users Said No to Tracking. Now Advertisers are Panicking
summary: Facebook advertisers are facing a crisis due to Apple's new iOS privacy features. Users are largely opting out of app tracking, disrupting a key data source for targeted advertising. This shift is leading to decreased ad effectiveness and widespread concern among businesses relying on Facebook's ad platform.

title: Wealthsimple Trade becomes first broker to offer fractional share purchases in both Canadian and U.S. companies
summary: Wealthsimple Trade has introduced fractional share purchases for both Canadian and U.S. companies, making high-priced stocks more accessible to investors. This initiative allows clients to buy as little as one dollar's worth of shares in big names like Shopify, Apple, and Amazon, a first for a broker offering this across both markets. The feature aims to help new and younger investors diversify portfolios and take advantage of market growth with smaller investments.

title: SK Hynix wins big from Cirrus Logic’s acquisition of Lion Semiconductor
summary: SK Hynix is set to gain a significant profit—estimated at ten times its initial investment—from Cirrus Logic's acquisition of Lion Semiconductor. This deal is particularly noteworthy in South Korea's semiconductor industry due to the strong ties between Lion Semiconductor's CEO, Wonyoung Kim, and SK Hynix, including his father being a former Hynix CEO and SK Hynix functioning as a foundry for Lion. The acquisition highlights the success of a new generation of South Korean entrepreneurs with international educational backgrounds achieving successful exits.

title: New battery technology promises double the energy density
summary: A new startup called Enovix is promising to mass-produce a Li-ion battery with double the energy density of existing technology, which could have significant implications for electric cars, cameras, and lights. Unlike many speculative battery projects, Enovix is already shipping a product and plans to scale up manufacturing. Their technology uses a silicon anode and an encased design to overcome the challenges of silicon's swelling, offering improved capacity per unit volume, though details on weight and cost are less clear.

title: SK Telecom enters the metaverse with ifland app
summary: SK Telecom has launched ifland, a new metaverse platform, by upgrading its JUMP Virtual Meetup app. ifland aims to compete with Naver's Zepeto by focusing on communication for users in their 20s and 30s, offering free custom avatars, virtual rooms for voice chats, and various meeting themes. Future updates will include text chats, iOS availability, and online events like concerts.

title: A complete guide to Amazon stock
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to Amazon stock, discussing its performance, market position, and how investors can trade it. It highlights Amazon's primary revenue streams (retail, subscriptions, and AWS), the company's valuation, and key factors investors should consider before trading AMZN shares, such as its growth-over-profit strategy and the importance of AWS.

title: Dow drops nearly 300 points on Friday, snaps 3-week winning streak
summary: U.S. stocks, including the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite, fell on Friday, snapping three-week winning streaks, as inflation fears overshadowed strong retail sales and earnings. The Dow lost 299.17 points, or 0.86%, closing at 34,687.85 and ending the week down 0.52%. Consumer sentiment declined in early July, with inflation expectations rising to levels not seen since August 2008, contributing to the market sell-off.

title: Shop Mac
summary: This page describes purchasing Macs from Apple, including details on financing options like Apple Card Monthly Installments (ACMI), trade-in programs, and various discounts. It also outlines eligibility requirements and terms for services like Apple Card and iCloud, along with educational and business purchasing programs.

title: The Competitive Landscape in the BNPL Sector
summary: This article discusses the intensifying competitive landscape within the Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) sector, highlighted by Apple's recent entry. It traces the history of installment plans, analyzes how BNPL providers like Afterpay and Humm Group are navigating increasing competition from major financial institutions and tech giants, and examines their strategies for profitability and differentiation amidst growing regulatory scrutiny and declining valuations.

title: It's ‘very dangerous’ to invest in stocks and bitcoin right now, long-time bear David Tice warns
summary: Long-time bear David Tice, known for his bearish bets, warns that investing in stocks and bitcoin is "very dangerous" due to an overvalued market, rising debt, and strange treasury market behavior. He suggests investors avoid pursuing small gains that risk a significant pullback, especially in Big Tech and FAANG stocks. Tice, who turned bearish on bitcoin, now advocates for investing in gold and silver mining companies, believing they offer protection against fiat money and significant growth opportunities.

title: Raleigh tech firm Bandwidth is betting millions on a full return to the office
summary: Raleigh tech firm Bandwidth is committing millions to an in-person, five-day-a-week office model, a contrast to the prevailing hybrid work trend in the tech industry. CEO David Morken believes this is crucial for preserving company culture, backing this conviction with a new headquarters featuring a Montessori school for employees' children, and stating that productivity increased with remote work, but the company prioritizes "presence". The company is not mandating COVID-19 vaccines but is encouraging vaccination and will continue to hire over 1,000 workers.

title: Worried about Inflation? Consider Royalty Companies, 'INFL'
summary: The Horizon Kinetics Inflation Beneficiaries ETF (INFL) has rapidly gained popularity since its January launch, amassing $620 million due to increasing inflation fears. The fund invests in royalty companies, offering exposure to inflation-protective commodities without the complexities of futures markets. Horizon Kinetics emphasizes how royalty companies, with their asset-light business models and high gross margins, can benefit from inflation through ownership stakes in profitable resource extraction operations.

title: Top prevalent malware with a thousand campaigns migrates to macOS
summary: Check Point Research details the evolution of Formbook, a prevalent info-stealing malware, and its successor, XLoader. Formbook, initially a keylogger, gained unintended popularity for spam campaigns, leading its author to halt sales, only for it to be reborn as XLoader, now offering macOS compatibility and operating under a "Malware-as-a-Service" model. XLoader’s continued activity and broad global reach, with over 1400 campaigns and victims in 69 countries, demonstrate its persistent threat.

title: Metro by T-Mobile is First and Only in Prepaid to Offer iPhone 12 mini on Us
summary: Metro by T-Mobile is now the first and only prepaid provider to offer the iPhone 12 mini on Us, starting July 22nd. Customers can get the 5G-capable iPhone 12 mini free after three months of service with a qualifying trade-in and activation on Metro's unlimited plan. The offer comes with access to T-Mobile's nationwide 5G network and a plan that includes unlimited talk, text, 5G data, high-speed hotspot data, and an Amazon Prime membership.

title: Ulta to open first Target shop-in-shop locations in August
summary: Ulta Beauty and Target are set to launch their first shop-in-shop locations in August, with plans for 800 nationwide. These 1,000 square-foot Ulta at Target spaces will offer premium beauty brands and provide specialized training for Target employees. This partnership aims to expand both retailers' customer reach and enhance Target's presence in the beauty market.

title: Carter's Inc. Jeff Jenkins Shares How the Brand Is Bringing Childhood Dreams to Life
summary: Jeff Jenkins, EVP of Global Marketing at Carter's Inc., discusses his decision to join the brand due to a deep emotional connection to parenthood. He highlights Carter's latest campaign featuring younger versions of American icons, including Mariah Carey's daughter, Monroe. Jenkins also emphasizes the importance of a strong leadership team and shares insights on mentorship and brand history.

title: Apple’s Attack on Email Marketing: Is It Helping Push the Industry Forward?
summary: Apple's introduction of iOS 15 and macOS Monterey Mail Privacy Protection features, including "Mail Privacy Protection" and "Hide My Email," will significantly impact email marketing by limiting marketers' ability to track email opens and user data. These changes, driven by a global push for data privacy following regulations like GDPR and fines against companies like Google and Facebook, compel email marketers to reassess their strategies, move beyond open rates as a primary KPI, and adapt their attribution models. The article suggests that while these privacy enhancements pose challenges, they could ultimately push the email marketing industry toward more efficient and effective practices focused on deeper engagement metrics.

title: What does the Optis v. Apple case mean for SEP Litigation in the UK?
summary: The Optis v. Apple case in the UK High Court ruled that Apple infringed Optis's standard-essential patents, with the court willing to set global royalty rates despite Apple being deemed an "unwilling licensee." This decision strengthens the UK's position as a favorable jurisdiction for SEP holders, potentially increasing patent filings but also raising concerns about jurisdictional clashes and the setting of global royalty rates by national courts. The controversy highlights the complexities of territorial patents versus global FRAND commitments.
authors: Enrico Bonadio (City St. George’s|University of London)|Luke McDonagh (London School of Economics)|Aarushi Mittal (Student|West Bengal National University of Juridical Sciences)

title: ACO Tech to add Spotify app to Proton GKUI soon?
summary: ACO Tech, the company behind Proton's GKUI infotainment system, hinted at new entertainment app additions, with strong speculation pointing to Spotify. While Google and Waze apps are unlikely due to Google's collaboration policies, ACO Tech teased an upcoming app starting with 'S' and stated they are "working with other entertainment applications." The company also suggested its own system is moving beyond mobile phone ecosystems like Apple CarPlay and Android Auto for future entertainment and lifestyle experiences.

title: FTC changes course on prior approval for reattempted mergers
summary: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has voted to rescind a 1995 policy statement, allowing them to routinely require prior approval for reattempted mergers and prior notice for mergers below the Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR) threshold. This move, initiated by FTC Chairman Lina Khan and following President Biden's executive order, aims to bolster protections against unlawful mergers and acquisitions by removing the need for new, time-consuming investigations into deals previously deemed anticompetitive. While supported by a 3-2 vote, Commissioner Noah Phillips expressed concerns that ignoring market changes could hinder normal market operations.

title: Better Buy: Invesco QQQ or Fidelity MSCI IT Index ETF
summary: This article compares the Invesco QQQ and Fidelity MSCI IT Index ETF, both strong technology-focused ETFs. While QQQ tracks the Nasdaq 100 and holds diverse sectors, the Fidelity ETF is a pure-play IT fund with broader diversification within the tech sector. The author concludes that the Fidelity ETF is the better buy due to its higher historical returns, lower expense ratio, and more consistent performance.

title: Tobi Lutke becomes Canada’s wealthiest company founder as Shopify shares soar
summary: Tobi Lutke, founder and CEO of Shopify Inc., has become Canada's wealthiest company founder as his stake in the e-commerce software provider reached US$13.3-billion. This surge in wealth is attributed to Shopify's stock crossing the $2,000 mark on the Toronto Stock Exchange and increasing optimism surrounding the company's second-quarter earnings. Analysts have raised their price targets for Shopify, anticipating its revenue to surpass US$1-billion for the first time in the June 30 quarter.

title: Klarna’s paying for crowded market by buying Stocard for €110m – report
summary: Klarna is reportedly acquiring loyalty card startup Stocard for €110m, a move seen as diversification amidst a crowded Buy-Now-Pay-Later (BNPL) market. This acquisition follows Klarna's earlier purchase of HERO in July and allows them to integrate Stocard's mobile wallet and loyalty program into their offerings, potentially pushing their own card to Stocard's 60 million customers. Analysts suggest this strategy is driven by the booming but increasingly competitive BNPL sector and anticipated regulatory changes.

title: Sparkline Launches Intangible Value ETF, ‘ITAN’
summary: Sparkline Capital has launched the Sparkline Intangible Value ETF (ITAN), an actively managed fund that evaluates companies based on their "intangible value." This value is derived from four pillars: intellectual property, brand equity, human capital, and network effects, integrated with tangible assets using machine learning. The fund aims to provide a modern interpretation of value investing by focusing on high-quality companies with strong intangible drivers of growth.

title: Apple's iPhone expected to drive sales, but App Store faces regulatory risk
summary: Apple is projected to see significant growth driven by 5G iPhone sales and its services, particularly the App Store and Apple Music. However, the company faces substantial regulatory risks and lawsuits concerning its App Store practices, including potential legislation to reduce commissions and scrutiny over search engine payments. Analysts anticipate high iPhone sales for the fiscal third quarter, which currently overshadow these regulatory concerns, though the services sector's growth has contributed to Apple's higher valuation.

title: Goldman Sachs boosts transaction banking biz with Fiserv tie-up
summary: Goldman Sachs is expanding its transaction banking business by partnering with Fiserv to offer services to Fiserv's clients, starting with cross-border supplier payments. This collaboration will allow Fiserv clients to use Goldman Sachs' cloud-based payment suite for domestic and foreign currency payments, managing FX and delivery for suppliers in over 125 currencies. The partnership aims to bring automation, efficiency, and cost savings to accounts payable for businesses with a global presence.

title: Apple says chip shortage reaches iPhone, growth forecast slows
summary: Apple announced that the global chip shortage, which previously affected its Mac and iPad sales, will now impact iPhone production and lead to a slowdown in revenue growth. Despite beating analyst expectations for its third-quarter sales and profits, the company forecasted decelerating growth for the current fiscal fourth quarter and its services business. CEO Tim Cook noted that while the third-quarter impact was less severe, the fourth quarter will see the shortage extend to iPhone components, particularly older technology chips essential for the devices.

title: Spotify quarterly revenue beats on paid users, ad rebound
summary: Spotify Technology SA exceeded Wall Street's second-quarter revenue estimates, driven by a 20% increase in premium subscribers, particularly in Europe and North America. Despite a dip in monthly active users due to the COVID-19 pandemic in Asia, the company saw a significant rebound in its advertising business, growing 110%. Spotify also continues to invest heavily in its podcast segment, which has reached an all-time high in consumption hours.

title: ANNUAL REPORT 2021 Arm: Business Strategy
source: ソフトバンクグループ株式会社
source_domain: ソフトバンクグループ株式会社
summary: Arm's 2021 annual report highlights its business strategy as a leader in semiconductor technology, emphasizing its licensing model and significant investments in R&D since being acquired by SoftBank Group in 2016. The report details how these investments are leading to increasing revenue growth, particularly from 5G smartphone sales and the emerging Arm-based server chip market, despite challenges like the COVID-19 pandemic. It also touches on future growth drivers, including new markets like IoT and autonomous vehicles, and the planned sale of Arm to NVIDIA.

title: Google founders have sold $1 billion in stock since May, the most since 2017
summary: Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin have sold over $1 billion worth of stock combined since May 2021, marking their first sales since 2017. Their stock sales, totaling more than $1.07 billion, occurred after the company's share price nearly tripled and Alphabet reported strong second-quarter earnings. Despite stepping down from executive roles in 2019, Page and Brin remain board members and control a majority of Alphabet's voting shares.

title: Consumer’s Claims About His Rights to iTunes Purchases Survives Motion to Dismiss
summary: A consumer's lawsuit against Apple Inc. regarding the meaning of "Buy" for digital content on iTunes has survived a motion to dismiss. David Andino claims Apple's use of "Buy" is misleading, suggesting full ownership when users only receive a limited license, potentially impacting millions of consumers and setting a precedent for digital media rights. The court found Andino had alleged an injury and that consumers could reasonably be confused by Apple's terminology.
authors: Gary R. Greenstein|Christopher A. Paniewski|Angelina He|Morgan Dudkewitz|Brian Levy

title: Apple Is Now an Antifragile Company
summary: This article argues that Apple has transformed into an "antifragile" company under Tim Cook's leadership, meaning it improves under stress rather than just resisting it. The company's resilience stems from its robust supply chain management, significant cash reserves, and diversified product and service portfolio, which allowed it to thrive amidst the challenges of 2020. While potential threats like government regulation, future leadership changes, and geopolitical issues remain, Apple's recent performance suggests a strong footing for continued success.

title: Anti-Vax App Squares Off With Google, Apple Over Misinformation
summary: A new social networking app named Unjected, created for unvaccinated individuals, is challenging Google and Apple's content policies regarding COVID-19 vaccine misinformation. The app positions itself as a community for "medical autonomy." This situation highlights the ongoing struggle between tech platforms and apps that host content controversial to public health guidelines.

title: Stage is set in L.A. for its next studio
summary: Hudson Pacific Properties Inc. and Blackstone are developing Sunset Glenoaks Studios, a new $190 million facility with seven soundstages in Sun Valley, Los Angeles, to meet the surging demand for film and TV production facilities. The project aims to expand their existing "Sunset Studios" brand, which will total 42 soundstages upon completion, and other companies are also investing in new studio developments across L.A. in response to the booming entertainment industry and streaming services.

title: Ferrari Posts Steady Profit Before Outsider CEO Takes Helm
summary: Ferrari (RACE:US) reported stable profitability for the second quarter, with adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization rising to 386 million euros, surpassing analyst estimates. This performance comes as the supercar manufacturer prepares to welcome a new CEO from outside the auto industry, who will be tasked with advancing the company's efforts in electrification and software. Despite slightly lower-than-expected revenue, Ferrari also increased its annual free cash flow forecast.

title: Reese Witherspoon’s Hello Sunshine to be be sold to media company backed by Blackstone
summary: Reese Witherspoon's media company, Hello Sunshine, is being sold to a firm backed by Blackstone Group Inc. for approximately $900 million. This acquisition is part of a strategy to create an independent entertainment company for the streaming era, led by former Disney executives Kevin Mayer and Tom Staggs. Witherspoon will continue to operate Hello Sunshine and join the new company's board, aiming to further the mission of telling more women's stories.

title: Reese Witherspoon's Hello Sunshine to be sold to Blackstone-backed media company for $900 million
summary: Reese Witherspoon's media company, Hello Sunshine, is being sold to a Blackstone-backed media company for approximately $900 million. This new venture, spearheaded by former Disney executives Kevin Mayer and Tom Staggs, aims to build an independent entertainment company focused on content for the streaming era. Witherspoon will maintain an ownership stake and continue to oversee operations, with the goal of expanding content focused on women's stories.

title: Apple ranks as world's most profitable company: Report
summary: Apple has been named the world's most profitable company for the 2020 fiscal year, according to the Fortune Global 500 list, with profits increasing 3.9% to $57.41 billion. This marks a shift from the previous two years where Saudi Aramco held the top spot. While Apple leads in profitability, Walmart remains the top company in terms of revenue on the global list.

title: Jack Dorsey’s Square snaps up Australia’s Afterpay for $29bn
summary: Jack Dorsey's payments firm Square is set to acquire Australian buy now, pay later (BNPL) pioneer Afterpay for $29bn, marking the biggest buyout of an Australian firm. This acquisition highlights the growing popularity of the BNPL model and positions Square as a major global transactions giant. The deal will result in significant paydays for Afterpay's founders and validates the BNPL industry, creating a formidable competitor for existing players like Affirm, PayPal, and Klarna.

title: ¿Cuáles son las empresas españolas con más ingresos en 2020?
summary: The article discusses the "Fortune Global 500" list for 2021, highlighting Spanish companies with the highest revenues in 2020. Banco Santander, Telefónica, and ACS lead among Spanish firms but have fallen in global rankings due to the pandemic's impact. Inditex and Naturgy Energy notably dropped off the list, while Walmart topped global rankings for the eighth consecutive year.

title: Formula 1's Skyrocketing Popularity
summary: Formula 1 is experiencing a significant surge in popularity and revenue, with viewership increasing by 36% compared to the 2019 season and first-quarter revenue reaching $180 million in 2021. This growth is attributed in part to Netflix's "Drive to Survive" series and strategic partnerships. The article also touches on World Rugby's discussions with private equity firms, Clemson University taking its multimedia rights in-house, and NHL player Evander Kane denying gambling allegations.

title: XPO Logistics Completes Spin Off of GXO Logistics
summary: XPO Logistics has successfully completed the spin-off of its logistics division, forming two independent public companies: GXO Logistics, focused on contract logistics, and XPO Logistics, focused on freight transportation. GXO Logistics celebrated its first day of trading by ringing the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange, with CEO Malcolm Wilson highlighting the company's strong platform for growth driven by innovation, customer relationships, and an experienced leadership team. GXO aims to expand further in high-growth areas like e-commerce and leverage its leadership in logistics automation and AI.

title: AT&T's DirecTV to become standalone video business
summary: AT&T Inc.'s satellite television provider, DirecTV, will become a standalone video business as part of a deal with buyout firm TPG Capital. The new entity will launch DirecTv Stream, a platform providing access to various streaming services. AT&T will retain a 70% ownership stake, while TPG Capital will own 30% of the new company, which was valued significantly lower than AT&T's initial purchase price.

title: Reese Witherspoon's media firm to be sold to Blackstone-backed company
summary: Reese Witherspoon's media company, Hello Sunshine, is being sold to a new media firm backed by Blackstone Group Inc. The deal, which values Hello Sunshine at about $900 million, comes amid a video streaming war and strong demand for high-quality content. Witherspoon and Hello Sunshine's CEO, Sarah Harden, will maintain significant equity and continue to oversee operations.

title: HP Elite Dragonfly G2 review: Updated premium 2-in-1 gains performance, but not weight
summary: The HP Elite Dragonfly G2 is a premium 2-in-1 convertible laptop that offers updated 11th-generation Intel Core processors without significantly increasing its lightweight design. While it maintains its impressive portability and versatile features like a "sanitization mode" for cleaning, the 4K display option, though visually appealing, can notably reduce battery life. Despite its high price point and some minor display shortcomings, it remains a strong contender for users prioritizing an ultraportable and fully-featured device for general productivity.

title: Delta Air Lines Pilots Upgrading to AT&T 5G and iPad Pro
summary: Delta Air Lines is upgrading its pilots' electronic flight bags to new iPad Pros with Apple's M1 chip and 5G connectivity via AT&T Business. This initiative aims to enhance pre-flight management tasks by providing reliable, high-speed cellular access for critical flight data, ensuring pilots can efficiently download necessary information even when Wi-Fi is unavailable. The move follows Delta's earlier decision to equip all flight attendants with iPhone 12s and 5G, highlighting a broader commitment to leveraging advanced connectivity for airline operations.

title: The world’s richest company is not a tech firm says Fortune list
summary: Fortune's annual Global 500 list for fiscal year 2020 revealed that Walmart is the world's richest company by revenue, not a tech firm, despite significant tech industry profits during the pandemic. China continues to dominate the list with 143 firms, while the US follows with 122. Brookfield Asset Management was the top Canadian firm, with Apple being the most profitable company overall.

title: Federal Circuit Ruling Underscores Importance of Clear and Present Assignments of Patent Rights
summary: A Federal Circuit ruling in Omni Medsci, Inc. v. Apple Inc. highlighted the need for clear language in contracts concerning patent rights assignments. The court affirmed that the University of Michigan's employment agreement with an inventor did not automatically assign patent rights, allowing Omni Medsci to sue Apple. The decision emphasizes that general phrases like "shall be the property of" may not constitute an immediate transfer of rights, contrasting with more definitive language such as "hereby assign."

title: Samsung, TSMC in heated race for industry’s smallest 3 nm process node
summary: Samsung Electronics and TSMC are in an intense competition to lead the semiconductor industry with the 3-nanometer process node technology, aiming for mass production in 2022. This rivalry is further fueled by Intel's entry into the foundry business, pledging significant investments and aiming to become a top player by 2025, challenging both Samsung and TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and market share. The race is also extending to 2-nanometer technology, pushing these companies to invest heavily in expanding facilities and R&D.

title: QQQ Versus QQQJ: Who’s Winning the Battle?
summary: This article compares the performance of the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF (QQQJ). QQQ, which focuses on large-cap tech companies, has outperformed QQQJ, a mid-cap focused ETF, across various timeframes. Chad Morganlander of Washington Crossing Advisors favors QQQ due to its higher quality large-cap names and stronger balance sheets.

title: Qualcomm Bids $4.6 Billion for Veoneer as Its New CEO Looks Beyond iPhone
summary: Qualcomm has made a $4.6 billion offer to acquire auto-technology company Veoneer Inc., aiming to outbid Magna International Inc. This strategic move highlights Qualcomm's new CEO's objective to diversify the company's business beyond its established mobile-phone chip market. The acquisition is driven by the increasing integration of chips into modern vehicles for automated driving and driver-assistance features.

title: Apple to scan U.S. iPhones for images of child sexual abuse
summary: Apple announced plans to scan U.S. iPhones for child sexual abuse images, a move praised by child protection groups but criticized by security researchers concerned about potential misuse by governments and the undermining of user privacy. The system, called "NeuralHash," will scan images before they're uploaded to iCloud and will also blur sexually explicit content in messages on children's devices and warn parents. Privacy advocates, including the Electronic Frontier Foundation, argue this compromises Apple's long-standing commitment to end-to-end encryption.

title: Music is an extremely personal and emotional experience for people: Dolby Lab’s Ashim Mathur
summary: Ashim Mathur, Senior Regional Director, Emerging Markets at Dolby Laboratories, discusses the collaboration with Apple to bring Dolby Atmos support to Apple Music. He highlights how Dolby Atmos enhances the music listening experience by providing spatial audio, making it more immersive and emotionally engaging. Mathur emphasizes that this partnership aims to change how music is created, heard, and experienced, catering to listeners' desire for better quality and enhanced streaming.

title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recovers from coronavirus slowdown
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway reported a strong recovery in its businesses from the coronavirus pandemic, leading to increased profits and revenue. The company repurchased $6 billion of its own shares in Q2, signaling confidence, and saw significant improvements across its manufacturing, service, and retailing units, with some exceeding pre-pandemic levels. Operating profit rose 21% and net income increased 7%, bolstered by unrealized gains from investments in tech and banking giants.

title: Apple’s TV Box Is Now Mostly Pointless. Will That Change?
summary: The article discusses how Apple's TV box, despite recent upgrades like a faster processor and new remote, has become mostly pointless in 2021. It questions whether the $199 price tag is justified given that content is now accessible on many devices, competing with cheaper alternatives like Amazon Fire and Google Chromecast. The author suggests that Apple needs to re-evaluate the device's purpose to remain relevant in the streaming market.

title: Austria’s Nuki wants to replace physical keys with smartphones; raises €20M and plans to hire 100+ people
summary: Nuki, an Austrian company specializing in smart home access solutions, has raised €20M in a new funding round with new investor Cipio Partners and existing investors. The company aims to replace physical keys with smartphone-controlled access and plans to hire over 100 new employees. Funds will be used to develop new products and technologies, including the "Smart Door" for direct integration in new buildings, and to expand its market leadership in Europe.

title: Sonos Move vs. Apple HomePod: Which should you buy?
summary: This article compares the Sonos Move and Apple HomePod, helping consumers decide which smart speaker best fits their needs. It highlights the Sonos Move's flexibility, portability, and compatibility with multiple voice assistants and music services, contrasted with the HomePod's high-quality sound and deep integration with the Apple ecosystem, particularly Apple Music and Siri. The comparison delves into features like weather resistance, battery life, and integration with smart home platforms, concluding that the choice depends on user preference for portability, voice assistant, and commitment to the Apple ecosystem.

title: Unity Software stock jumps 10% as analysts credit it with dodging Apple's online-ad crackdown
summary: Unity Software's stock jumped 10% after analysts praised the company for navigating Apple's recent online advertisement policy changes. Unity found a workaround to help its customers continue effective advertising, leading to an upgraded outlook for the year. The company also announced the acquisition of Parsec for $320 million following a strong quarter.

title: New Samsung foldables: Z Flip 3 and Z Fold 3 make their debut at Unpacked 2021
summary: Samsung unveiled its new Galaxy Z Flip 3 and Galaxy Z Fold 3 foldable devices at its Unpacked 2021 event, emphasizing improved durability with new materials like Armor Aluminum and Gorilla Glass Victus. The Z Flip 3 is a flip phone-inspired model designed for portability, while the Z Fold 3 functions more like a tablet that folds into a smartphone. Both devices are powered by the Snapdragon 888 processor and feature enhanced resistance technologies, with the Z Flip 3 starting at $999 and the Z Fold 3 at $1,799.

title: U.S. lawmakers introduce bill to rein in Apple, Google app stores
summary: A bipartisan group of U.S. senators has introduced a bill aiming to curb the market control of app stores operated by Apple and Google. The proposed legislation would prevent these companies from forcing app developers to use their payment systems or penalizing those offering different prices elsewhere. This move comes amidst ongoing scrutiny and legal challenges regarding the practices and commission fees of Apple's App Store and Google's Play Store.

title: Domino’s Pizza, Pinterest, GoDaddy e AMC: confira os 33 BDRs que estreiam na B3 nesta 2ª feira
summary: On August 16th, 33 new BDRs, including Domino's Pizza, Pinterest, GoDaddy, and AMC Entertainment, began trading on the B3. These BDRs allow Brazilian investors to access global company stocks, with significant growth in investor numbers and trading volumes since regulatory changes in 2020. The article highlights top-performing BDRs and the increasing interest of Brazilian investors in this market segment.

title: Sonos ARC Review: An Upgradeable and Flexible Soundbar with Captivating Sound
summary: The review introduces the Sonos ARC as a high-end, modular soundbar system that delivers an immersive audio experience. It highlights the ARC's upgradeable hardware, seamless setup (especially for Apple users), and captivating sound quality, particularly when paired with a Sonos Sub. While praising its performance and modularity, the review also notes minor drawbacks like a hidden sleep timer, lack of HDMI expansion, and absence of DTS support.

title: Spotify Snags Copyright Office Legal Chief as Public Policy Head
summary: Spotify has hired Regan Smith, formerly the general counsel of the U.S. Copyright Office, as its new head of public policy and government affairs. This strategic move comes as Spotify intensifies its competition with Apple Inc. Smith’s appointment strengthens Spotify's legal and public policy team, which is led by chief legal officer Horacio Gutierrez.

title: John Deere Moves Into Autonomous Tractors With Robots Startup Acquisition
summary: John Deere has acquired Bear Flag Robotics, a Silicon Valley startup, marking a significant move into autonomous tractor technology. The acquisition aims to bring entirely autonomous tractors to market, addressing severe labor issues in the agricultural industry. Bear Flag Robotics already has autonomous tractors with a limited set of customers, though details on wider release and pricing are not yet available.

title: Project Chess: The Story Behind the Original IBM PC
summary: This article recounts the story of "Project Chess," the initiative behind the original IBM Personal Computer, which was introduced on August 12, 1981. It highlights IBM's unconventional decision to develop the PC outside its traditional bureaucracy, led by William C. "Bill" Lowe and later Philip D. "Don" Estridge, and its key choices like an open architecture, Intel processor, and Microsoft operating system, which established industry standards for decades. The project's rapid development, strategic retail partnerships, and immediate market success despite initial limitations are detailed, emphasizing its significant impact on personal computing.

title: Microsoft Partnership Puts the 'S' & 'G' Into the 'E' in ESG
summary: Microsoft has partnered with Volt Energy to bring carbon-neutral electricity to underserved communities, addressing both environmental and social justice issues within the ESG framework. This initiative, while small in scale regarding initial power purchase, is seen as a significant step in pushing the boundaries of corporate environmental and social leadership. The article also highlights the Putnam Sustainable Leaders ETF (PLDR), which invests in companies like Microsoft that demonstrate proactive leadership in ESG issues and includes Microsoft as a top holding.

title: Samsung Is Using AI to Design Its Next Exynos Chipset for Smartphones
summary: Samsung is reportedly leveraging artificial intelligence, specifically Synopsys' DSO.ai tool, to design its next Exynos chipsets for smartphones. This AI-driven approach aims to automate and accelerate the complex chip development process, potentially improving performance and power efficiency. While Samsung has not confirmed which future products, such as the rumored Exynos 2200, will utilize these AI-designed chips, industry experts suggest AI is well-suited for arranging billions of transistors on a chip.

title: Music distribution startup Distrokid hits unicorn status with Insight Partners funding
summary: Distrokid, an online music distribution startup, has achieved unicorn status after receiving new funding from Insight Partners, valuing the company at $1.3 billion. Founded in 2003, Distrokid allows musicians to upload unlimited music to various online stores and streaming services for a flat annual fee, while retaining 100% of their earnings. The company claims to distribute over a third of all new music globally.

title: Apple relying on frequent COVID testing for employees, no vaccine requirement yet
summary: Apple is implementing a strategy of frequent COVID-19 testing for its corporate and retail employees, starting with twice a week and increasing to three times, as it prepares for office reopenings. Unlike some other tech giants, Apple is not currently mandating COVID-19 vaccinations for employees but encourages them for those eligible. The company has also reconsidered restarting in-store classes and plans a hybrid remote work strategy for office staff.

title: Arch Capital appoint high flyers to board
summary: Bermuda-based Arch Capital Group Ltd has appointed Francis Ebong and Eileen Mallesch to its board of directors. Mr. Ebong brings extensive technology and innovation experience from Alphabet's X and Facebook, while Ms. Mallesch offers over 30 years of finance and risk expertise including senior leadership roles at Nationwide and other significant board positions. These appointments aim to enhance the board's capabilities in fintech transformation and broaden its talent and outlook for future growth.

title: Apple selects 15 Black- and Brown-owned businesses for first-of-its-kind Impact Accelerator
summary: Apple has selected 15 Black- and Brown-owned businesses for its inaugural Impact Accelerator, part of the company’s Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. These businesses, focused on green technology and clean energy, will receive customized training, access to Apple experts, and opportunities to become Apple suppliers as the company works toward its 2030 carbon-neutral goal. The program aims to combat systemic barriers and advance innovative environmental solutions for communities most affected by climate change.

title: Digital payments soared during the pandemic and are here to stay
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated the adoption of digital and contactless payment methods, a trend experts believe is permanent. Visa reported a 30% increase in tap-to-pay transactions year-over-year, and nearly half of consumers now avoid stores without contactless payment options. This shift is driven by health and safety concerns, as well as the convenience, speed, and security that digital payments offer.

title: XPO Logistics completes GXO Logistics spin-off
summary: XPO Logistics has finalized the spin-off of GXO Logistics, establishing GXO as the world's largest pure-play contract logistics provider. GXO launches with significant global operations, serving major blue-chip customers and high-growth sectors. Both XPO and GXO are now independent public companies, aimed at unlocking their full potential.

title: Bob Greenblatt Launches Production Company, Inks First-Look TV Deal With Lionsgate
summary: Bob Greenblatt, former Chairman of WarnerMedia Entertainment, has launched a new production company called The Green Room and signed a first-look deal with Lionsgate to develop and produce premium television projects. He has also appointed Jon Wu, previously of Hello Sunshine, as Producer and Head of Filmed Content for The Green Room. This move marks Greenblatt's return to producing after stepping down from WarnerMedia in August of the previous year.

title: The 75 per cent problem: aluminium’s carbon footprint
summary: Aluminium production accounts for a significant amount of global CO2 emissions, largely due to energy-intensive smelting processes. With demand for aluminium expected to increase, decarbonizing its production is critical, primarily through using renewable energy sources for smelting and developing technologies like carbon capture. The article also highlights the importance of improving recycling rates and exploring alternative production methods to reduce aluminium's environmental impact.

title: Zimmer Biomet to Unveil New Clinical Data and Portfolio of Innovative Technologies at AAOS 2021 Annual Meeting
summary: Zimmer Biomet announced it will present new clinical data and showcase innovative technologies at the AAOS 2021 Annual Meeting, taking place from August 31 to September 3 in San Diego. Key highlights include a podium presentation on its investigational Persona IQ® smart knee implant and three posters detailing the clinical utility of its mymobility® with Apple Watch® remote care management platform. The company will also feature interactive exhibits for its ROSA® Robotics and ZBEdge™ Connected Intelligence suite, aiming to transform patient experience and restore mobility.

title: MicroSectors™ Launches FANG & Innovation ETNs, 'BULZ' & 'BERZ'
summary: REX Shares, LLC announced the launch of two new MicroSectors Exchange Traded Notes (ETNs), BULZ and BERZ, issued by the Bank of Montreal. These ETNs are linked to the Solactive FANG Innovation Index, which tracks major U.S.-listed tech and internet/media companies. The products aim to offer sophisticated investors leveraged and inverse leveraged exposure to prominent technology and innovation stocks.

title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s 2nd Largest Customer – Apple Car Chip Production Rumored For China
summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer, behind Apple, due to strong growth across its chip segments, especially EPYC server products. This increased demand from AMD, Apple, and MediaTek is accelerating order flows for TSMC's 3nm process. Additionally, there are rumors that automotive chip production for an "Apple Car" might take place at TSMC's Nanjing facility using older 28nm process nodes.

title: This Crazy Cologne Comes in a Smartphone-Activated, Robot-Shaped Bottle
summary: Paco Rabanne has launched a new men's fragrance called 'Phantom' which comes in a unique robot-shaped bottle. This bottle features an NFC chip in its head, allowing users to connect their smartphones to access exclusive digital content like style guides, playlists, and games. The scent itself was developed using algorithms and expert perfumers, resulting in an "energizing green flash of lemon with ultra-creamy lavender."

title: Landsea Homes Closes on 80 Homesites For Glendale, Arizona, Community
summary: Landsea Homes has acquired 80 homesites in Glendale, Arizona, for a new community named Marlowe, with construction slated for 2023. The Marlowe community will offer two-story homes ranging from 1,800 to 2,500 square feet, featuring smart home automation powered by Apple HomeKit. Landsea Homes aims to provide high-performance, attainable housing choices in the West Valley of the Phoenix metro area.

title: TSMC overtakes Tencent to become Asia's most valuable company, as China crackdown takes a toll
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) has surpassed Chinese tech giant Tencent to become Asia's most valuable firm, largely due to Beijing's ongoing regulatory crackdown on its domestic tech sector. While Tencent and Alibaba have seen their market capitalizations plummet, TSMC has benefited from a global semiconductor shortage and increased demand from various industries. TSMC plans to invest $100 billion over the next three years to increase production capacity.

title: Universal Electronics' Apple TV remote now available through Deutsche Telekom
summary: Universal Electronics' (UEI) Apple TV remote, which features traditional buttons and Siri integration, is now being distributed by German telecommunications giant Deutsche Telekom to its MagentaTV subscribers. This remote, initially mistaken for an Apple-designed device, offers dedicated buttons for services like MagentaTV's program guide and utilizes Bluetooth Low Energy and infrared for control, along with Apple's Made for iPhone authentication chip for secure connectivity. It is currently available only through cable partners, not as a standalone purchase, distinguishing it from Apple's own revamped Siri Remote which lacks dedicated service buttons.

title: International Coalition Calls on Apple to Abandon Plan to Build Surveillance Capabilities into iPhones, iPads, and other Products
summary: An international coalition of over 90 civil society organizations has urged Apple to abandon its new plans to build surveillance capabilities into iPhones and other products. While Apple states these features are for child protection and reducing CSAM, the coalition argues they pose risks to children, could censor speech, and threaten privacy and security globally. They highlight concerns that the scanning could lead to misidentification, endanger vulnerable youth, and subject Apple to governmental pressure to scan for other "objectionable" content.

title: IBM Closes New York City Offices Amid Rising Covid-19 Cases
summary: IBM has temporarily closed its New York City offices to all but essential staff due to a rise in Covid-19 cases in the city, aligning with IBM's health and safety protocols. Despite this, the company is still planning to reopen other U.S. locations by September. This decision comes as the delta variant causes other companies, including Apple, to re-evaluate their return-to-work strategies.

title: For Long-Term Investors, a QQQ-to-QQQM Switch May Make Sense
summary: The article discusses how long-term investors might benefit from switching from the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) to the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) due to QQQM's lower expense ratio. While QQQM is newer and less liquid, its cost savings over time make it an attractive option for buy-and-hold investors seeking exposure to the Nasdaq-100 Index. The Nasdaq-100 Index has consistently outperformed other benchmarks, making both ETFs appealing for growth-oriented portfolios.

title: Apple’s Proposed Protections Against Child Predators — a Challenge to Privacy?
summary: Apple announced a new system to identify child predatory content in its photo cloud, sparking varied reactions from law enforcement, child protection groups, and privacy defenders. The system involves on-device scanning of iCloud Photos using an algorithm to detect known child abuse images, raising concerns among critics about potential privacy invasions and governmental pressure to expand surveillance capabilities. The article discusses the technical implementation, immediate reactions, and the broader implications for Apple's long-standing commitment to user privacy amidst new challenges.

title: Big Tech Is in a Perilous Moment. The Stocks Are Unstoppable.
summary: Despite significant regulatory scrutiny, major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Facebook have demonstrated unstoppable stock performance. During the April to June quarter, these five giants collectively generated $332 billion in revenue, a 36% increase year over year, with all reporting better-than-expected profits. Interestingly, most of their stocks sold off slightly after the positive earnings announcements.
authors: Eric J. Savitz and Max A. Cherney

title: ‘Fancy Like’ from Walker Hayes is now an Applebee’s ad
summary: The viral TikTok song "Fancy Like" by Walker Hayes has been integrated into an Applebee's advertisement, showcasing the influence of social media trends on brand marketing. This move highlights how companies are leveraging popular culture to promote their products. The ad follows the song's widespread success on platforms like TikTok.

title: REMS patient companion app enables faster, easier surveys
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has developed a new mobile app designed to simplify the survey process for patients taking medications that require Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) surveys. The app allows patients to complete these mandatory surveys on their smartphones at their convenience, reducing the burden associated with traditional methods like phone calls. This innovation, born from patient feedback, aims to enhance patient safety by ensuring compliance with FDA mandates while providing a more independent and discreet experience.

title: News Taiwan’s TSMC to charter boxship to deliver semiconductor components to US
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is reportedly in discussions with Wan Hai to charter a containership to transport components for its new Phoenix, Arizona plant from Kaohsiung to the US. This decision follows similar actions by Home Depot and Walmart, with unconfirmed reports suggesting TSMC's chairman has approved chartering an entire vessel for an estimated cost of at least $107 million. The Phoenix plant is expected to begin producing 5-nanometer chips in 2024, as TSMC invests $100 billion over three years to expand its semiconductor fabrication capacity.

title: White House to tackle cyber challenges with Apple, IBM, insurance CEOs
summary: The White House is hosting a cybersecurity event bringing together top executives from major tech companies like Apple, IBM, Microsoft, and Google, alongside financial and energy sector leaders and the CEO of Travelers Companies Inc. The discussion will focus on ransomware, critical infrastructure, supply chain security, cybersecurity education, and data breach insurance, aiming to secure public commitments for improved IT security measures and workforce training. The event highlights the unique inclusion of the insurance industry, whose policies are seen as crucial for widespread improvements in cyber defense.

title: Tim Cook Became CEO of Apple 10 Years Ago. Here's How Much Money You'd Have If You Invested $1,000 in the Tech Giant the Day He Took Over
summary: This article details the financial success of Apple under Tim Cook's 10-year tenure as CEO. An initial $1,000 investment in Apple when Cook took over would now be worth nearly $13,000, representing a 1,200% return, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. Cook's strategy has focused on leveraging Apple's user base for subscription services, growing the company's market cap from $364.4 billion to $2.45 trillion.

title: Blackstone is merging Liftoff and Vungle to create a Bay Area-based mobile advertising giant
summary: Blackstone is combining two of its portfolio companies, Liftoff and Vungle, to form a new mobile advertising giant based in the Bay Area. The merged entity will aim to provide a comprehensive suite of services for mobile app developers, covering user acquisition to revenue generation. This move comes as the adtech industry grapples with new privacy controls impacting mobile advertising.

title: South Korea set to curb Google, Apple commission dominance
summary: South Korea is poised to pass legislation, dubbed the "Anti-Google law," that would prevent Google and Apple from mandating their proprietary payment systems for in-app purchases, effectively stopping them from levying commissions.
