This move would make South Korea the first major economy to impose such restrictions on the tech giants, who currently charge up to 30% commission. The legislation has drawn criticism from Apple and industry groups representing U.S. tech companies, who argue it could risk users' privacy, reduce trust in app store purchases, and potentially violate trade commitments.

title: Women's Equality Day: How one ETF up nearly 20% this year promotes female empowerment
summary: Ahead of Women's Equality Day, two prominent female empowerment ETFs, WOMN and SHE, have achieved all-time highs. The Impact Shares YWCA Women's Empowerment ETF (WOMN) is up nearly 19% this year, while the SPDR SSGA Gender Diversity Index ETF (SHE) is up 17%. These thematic ETFs not only promote women's empowerment through their investment criteria but have also outperformed broader market indices like the Russell 1000.

title: A new type of healing, supported by Apple
summary: Apple's Racial Equity and Justice Initiative has funded VamosVentures, a VC firm that invests in diverse, technology-enabled companies, including SweetBio. SweetBio, a Latinx-founded biotech startup, is developing an advanced wound care product made from Manuka honey to address chronic health issues like diabetic ulcers, particularly impacting communities of color. The article highlights a successful case study and SweetBio's plans for national scaling, supported by Apple and VamosVentures.

title: As It Faces More Scrutiny, Apple Will Reduce App Store Fees If Publishers Provide Apple News Content
summary: Apple announced a new News Partner Program that will reduce its App Store commission from 30% to 15% for news publishers who provide content to Apple News. This initiative comes as Apple faces increasing scrutiny and criticism regarding its App Store fees from developers, competitors, and regulators. To qualify, publishers must supply all their content in Apple's preferred format, provide metadata, and maintain an active presence in markets where Apple News is available.

title: Apple settles antitrust case with developers, but it's far from an Epic resolution to App Store monopoly concerns
summary: Apple has settled an antitrust class-action lawsuit brought by developers, making concessions such as allowing developers to communicate alternative purchase options directly with customers outside of apps and increasing price points. While the settlement includes a $100 million fund for small developers and promises more transparency, it is limited to the USA and does not address the core issue of third-party payment systems, which remains central to the ongoing Epic Games lawsuit.

title: World's Biggest Stock Indices | Index Fundamentals
summary: This article explains the key factors that influence the prices of major global stock indices, including the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, FTSE 100, and DAX. It highlights how economic growth, dominant sectors, index weighting methodologies, and currency strength play significant roles in their performance. The piece offers a foundational understanding for traders looking to grasp index movements.

title: Amazon acquisitions: What does Amazon own?
summary: This article details Amazon's extensive acquisition history, highlighting how the company expanded from an online bookstore to one of the world's most valuable companies by acquiring over 100 businesses across various sectors. It lists Amazon's largest acquisitions, such as Whole Foods and MGM, and also touches upon Jeff Bezos's other ventures outside of Amazon, like The Washington Post and Blue Origin. The article emphasizes Amazon's "need-to-win mentality" in driving its acquisition strategy.

title: Tim Cook’s Run as Apple CEO Could End as Early as 2025. Who Will Replace Him?
summary: This article discusses the potential succession plan for Apple CEO Tim Cook, whose tenure could conclude as early as 2025 after a decade at the helm. It speculates on who might replace him and touches upon other recent Apple news, including a developer lawsuit settlement and plans for a larger Apple Watch.

title: Apple buys classical-music service Primephonic, will launch app
summary: Apple Inc. has acquired the classical-music streaming service Primephonic and plans to introduce a dedicated classical music app next year. Primephonic's content will be integrated into Apple Music, and its existing service will cease on September 7th, with subscribers receiving six months of free Apple Music. This acquisition is part of Apple's strategy to enhance Apple Music, which faces strong competition from Spotify.

title: Forecast for Globalstar (GSAT) Stock Amid Rumored Apple Deal
summary: Globalstar (GSAT) stock surged on August 30 due to rumors of a potential partnership with Apple to bring satellite communication to the iPhone 13. While the stock has seen significant gains this year and some analysts are bullish on its prospects, others remain cautious. The potential Apple deal is seen as a game-changer that could significantly boost Globalstar's demand.

title: What’s Up With Apple: Satellite-Capable iPhone, App Settlement and More
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly integrating low-earth orbit satellite connectivity into the upcoming iPhone 13, enabling calls and texts without cell service, with Globalstar being a potential partner. Separately, Apple reached a settlement with small app developers, allowing them to email customers about alternative payment methods outside of Apple Pay, a concession criticized by some as minor. Additionally, Australia is considering regulating digital wallet providers like Apple as payment providers.

title: Arlo Offers Comprehensive Third-Party Compatibility With Amazon, Apple, Google, Samsung, And Others For Seamless Smart Home Security Experience
summary: Arlo Technologies announced enhanced compatibility for its smart home security systems with major platforms like Amazon Alexa, Apple HomeKit, Google Home Assistant, IFTTT, and Samsung SmartThings. These integrations provide users with seamless control over their Arlo devices through voice commands, notifications, and live video feeds on various smart home devices, reflecting Arlo's commitment to a robust and integrated security experience. The updates aim to offer peace of mind regardless of the user's preferred smart home ecosystem.

title: Cleaning house: how Google and Yelp handle fake reviews; Tuesday’s daily brief
summary: This article discusses how Google and Yelp combat fake reviews, highlighting their differing approaches in automated systems, human moderation, and punitive measures. It also covers the shift in Google's title creation for SERPs and the tools SEOs can use to track these changes, as well as Google's substantial payments to Apple to remain the default search engine on Safari. Additionally, the piece touches on the persistent issue of gender stereotypes in advertising despite societal progress.

title: Tech Has Momentum Into Year-End. Time for QQQM.
summary: The Nasdaq Composite's consistent new highs are drawing attention to the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), but investors should also consider its more cost-effective counterpart, the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Both ETFs track the Nasdaq 100 Index, which has a significant allocation to tech stocks, and analysts are bullish on the sector for the remainder of 2021 following the Federal Reserve's recent messaging. QQQM offers a lower expense ratio of 0.15% compared to QQQ's 0.20%, positioning it well to benefit from renewed investor interest in growth stocks like Apple and Microsoft.

title: Zimmer Biomet Announces New Data Supporting Clinical Benefits of mymobility® with Apple Watch® at AAOS 2021
summary: Zimmer Biomet announced new data at the AAOS 2021 Annual Meeting supporting the clinical benefits of its mymobility® with Apple Watch® remote care management platform. Four studies highlighted that the platform can effectively support patients recovering from knee and hip replacement surgeries. The data suggest that mymobility with Apple Watch provides similar outcomes to traditional care, potentially reduces healthcare utilization, and offers valuable passively collected physical activity data for monitoring recovery.

title: Get Ready To Use Your iPhone As ID At The Airport
summary: Apple's digital ID initiative, allowing users to add their driver's license or state ID to the Wallet app, is launching soon. Arizona and Georgia will be the first states to accept these digital IDs at TSA checkpoints, with other states to follow. This privacy-focused feature aims to simplify identity verification for air travel and other transactions, using biometric authentication for security.

title: Financial services giant Charles Schwab to add 450 jobs in Austin
summary: Charles Schwab, a major financial services company, announced an expansion in Austin that will create approximately 450 new jobs across various functions, including software development, engineering, and client services. This expansion is part of the company's significant growth in 2021, which saw record net new assets and brokerage accounts, and follows a company-wide 5% pay increase for employees. Schwab joins several other large corporations like Oracle, Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Google in expanding their presence and job offerings in Austin, leveraging the city's tech talent pool.

title: Salt adds Universal remote to Apple TV service
summary: Swiss telco Salt has integrated a Universal Electronics-produced Apple TV remote into its service, designed specifically for Multichannel Video Program Distributors (MVPDs). The remote, launched by Salt in June, combines Bluetooth Low Energy and Infrared for universal control and is also available as a standalone accessory. This collaboration enhances the TV viewing experience for Salt's Apple TV 4K IPTV service customers.

title: Apple offers small concession in easing App Store rules for Netflix, others
summary: Apple announced a concession through a settlement with Japan's anti-trust regulator, allowing "reader" apps like Netflix and Spotify to link to their websites for user payments, bypassing Apple's 30% App Store fee. This change, effective early next year, aims to address some antitrust concerns but has been criticized by companies like Spotify and Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney as insufficient, particularly regarding game developers and broader app store policies. The move comes amid increasing global scrutiny and other recent challenges to Apple's App Store rules across various countries.

title: Broadcom forecasts upbeat current-quarter sales on 5G bet
summary: Broadcom Inc. forecasted fourth-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for semiconductors due to 5G adoption and hybrid work models. The company's wireless division, including Apple as a major customer, is also expected to benefit from new iPhone launches. Broadcom's diversification into software business has helped mitigate supply chain disruptions.

title: Wearable Devices Market Trend, Growth, Detailed Analysis
summary: The wearable devices market is projected to grow at an impressive CAGR of 18.78% from 2021 to 2028, driven by the increasing penetration of smartphones, internet connectivity, and rising consumer awareness toward physical fitness. Key players in this market include SAMSUNG, Apple Inc., Garmin Ltd., Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd., and Sony Corporation. The market analysis covers various segments such as wrist-wear, eyewear, footwear, and different types of smart textiles, with detailed country-level insights and a look at the impact of COVID-19.

title: Globalstar (GSAT) Stock Forecast 2025—Why It's a Good Investment
summary: Globalstar (GSAT) stock has surged over 500% in 2021 due to rumors of an Apple partnership for satellite communications in the new iPhone 13. While analysts have varying target prices, the company is seen as a good investment due to potential revenue increases from an Apple deal and the growing demand for satellite services, despite competition from Starlink and AST SpaceMobile.

title: Toll Brothers plans another high-end neighborhood in Austin area
summary: Toll Brothers, the nation's 11th-largest homebuilder, is planning another high-end neighborhood in Leander, north of Austin. The company is targeting 112 acres to build 330 luxury homes, with half-million-dollar homes expected to be the focus. This development continues the trend of rapid growth in the Leander area.

title: College debt crisis drives more students away from four-year schools
summary: The rising cost of college and the ongoing debt crisis are pushing more students away from traditional four-year universities towards more affordable alternatives like coding boot camps, community colleges, and trade schools. Companies like Apple and Google are also reducing their reliance on college degrees, making alternative education more appealing. Surveys show a significant drop in high school students planning to attend four-year schools, with many prioritizing career training and financial viability.

title: Garmin Venu 2S review: Stylish fitness watch packed with features
summary: The Garmin Venu 2S is reviewed as a stylish fitness watch, particularly appealing to women due to its elegant design and 40mm dial. While praised for its AMOLED display, extensive fitness tracking features (including GPS, pulse oximeter, and heart rate monitoring), solid battery life, and Spotify integration, the review notes some issues with inaccurate swim distance tracking and an at times confusing user interface with notifications. Despite these minor drawbacks and its premium price of Rs 37,990, the Venu 2S is recommended for hardcore fitness enthusiasts.

title: Alphabet, Tesla and Amazon are among the most favourite stocks held by US-focused mutual funds. Here are top 10 stocks
summary: This article identifies Alphabet, Tesla, and Amazon as top stock holdings among U.S.-focused mutual funds available to Indian investors. It details 10 such mutual funds, highlighting their investment strategies, underlying assets, and historical performance. The funds provide Indian investors exposure to leading US companies and geographical diversification.

title: Lennox announces new integration of its iComfort thermostat
summary: Lennox has expanded the compatibility of its iComfort thermostats to integrate with various smart home automation platforms, including premium systems like Control4 and popular virtual assistants such as Amazon Alexa, Apple HomeKit, Google Assistant, and IFTTT. This integration allows users to manage their Lennox heating and cooling systems through a wider range of smart home devices, offering enhanced convenience and control over their indoor environment. Control4 customers with Lennox HVAC systems and iComfort S30, E30, or M30 thermostats can request integration through their dealer, enabling centralized control from their smart home system.

title: S&P 500 ends down, Big Tech lifts Nasdaq to record
summary: The S&P 500 closed lower on Tuesday, while the Nasdaq reached a record high, driven by Big Tech stocks amidst investor concerns about a slowing economic recovery. The article highlights that while Apple and Netflix hit record highs, broader market sectors declined due to worries about the Delta variant and lukewarm employment data. Morgan Stanley also downgraded U.S. stocks, anticipating a bumpy period ahead.

title: Stryker Acquires Med Device Company Gauss Surgical
summary: Stryker announced its acquisition of Gauss Surgical, a medical device company known for its AI-enabled Triton platform that monitors blood loss during surgery. The Triton system utilizes artificial intelligence and mobile technology to provide real-time, accurate blood loss measurements, which has been shown to improve maternal and surgical care by enabling earlier recognition and intervention of hemorrhage. Financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

title: Stryker buys Gauss Surgical for its AI tech that tracks blood loss during childbirth
summary: Stryker has acquired Gauss Surgical for its AI-powered Triton system, which tracks blood loss during surgery and childbirth to help prevent postpartum hemorrhaging. The Triton system uses an iPhone app and computer vision to measure blood loss from sponges, towels, and fluid canisters, alerting caregivers to dangerous situations. Following the acquisition, Gauss's home diagnostic efforts, including a smartphone-paired rapid antigen test for COVID-19, will be spun off into a new separate company called Exa Health.

title: Focal Point Names Scott Pomerantz, formerly of Global Locate and Broadcom, as CEO
summary: Focal Point Positioning, a UK-based deep tech startup, has appointed Scott Pomerantz as its new CEO. Pomerantz, who founded Global Locate and later sold it to Broadcom, brings 30 years of experience in positioning and semiconductors. Focal Point's Supercorrelation technology aims to improve GPS chip performance in various devices, and Pomerantz will lead the company's efforts to scale up technical support, customer integration, and commercialization.

title: รุ่นพี่ปะทะรุ่นน้อง QQQ ETF กับ QQQJ ETF
summary: This article compares two Invesco ETFs, QQQ and QQQJ, which both track the NASDAQ-100 Index. QQQ invests in the top 100 large-cap technology companies on Nasdaq, while QQQJ (NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF) focuses on companies ranked 101-200. The article highlights differences in expense ratios, fund size, and investment diversification, noting that QQQJ has shown better one-year returns, while QQQ has performed better year-to-date, with QQQJ exhibiting higher volatility.

title: PayPal deepens its buy-now-pay-later push with acquisition of Japan’s Paidy for $2.7 billion
summary: PayPal has announced its acquisition of Japanese buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) startup Paidy for 300 billion yen ($2.7 billion) to strengthen its presence in the BNPL market, particularly in Japan. Paidy allows consumers to make online purchases and pay in installments at convenience stores, catering to Japan's cash-centric economy. This acquisition follows other significant BNPL deals in the industry, highlighting the growing global trend of flexible payment options.

title: Find Lost Items at Home with Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri
summary: This article provides guidance on how to locate lost items within a home using various smart assistants such as Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri. It covers different methods and tips for leveraging these technologies to find misplaced belongings.

title: Apple can’t force developers to use its in-app payment system, federal judge rules
summary: A federal judge ruled that Apple Inc. can no longer compel developers to use its in-app payment system, allowing them to bypass commission fees of 15% to 30%. This decision, stemming from the Epic Games Inc. case, could significantly alter how apps are distributed by dominant online platforms like Apple, Alphabet Inc. (Google), and Amazon.com Inc. However, the ruling stopped short of declaring Apple an antitrust monopolist.

title: The 10 Richest People on Wall Street and How to Invest in Them
summary: This article identifies the ten wealthiest individuals on Wall Street, based on Forbes' Real Time Billionaires list, and offers strategies for investors to potentially emulate their success. It suggests methods like investing in their publicly traded vehicles, analyzing their 13F SEC filings, or focusing on their core investment philosophies. The article details each billionaire's background, key holdings, and how individual investors might gain exposure to their strategies.

title: Epic Games court ruling unlikely to seriously damage Apple's services business
summary: A court ruling in the Epic Games case allows iPhone apps to link to external payment systems, bypassing Apple's in-app purchase cut. Despite Apple's stock drop, analysts believe the financial impact will be limited because developers cannot build alternative payment mechanisms directly into apps, and consumers may still prefer Apple's easier in-app payments. The ruling is seen as a victory for Apple as it did not challenge their right to control software on their store or declare them a monopoly, while preserving the significant profit engine of their services business.

title: Single Stocks & ETFs Do Mix
summary: This article discusses how single stocks and ETFs can both have a place in an investment portfolio. It highlights the similarities and differences, emphasizing the importance of considering overall portfolio concentration when adding individual stocks. The author also weighs the pros and cons, such as lower costs for single stocks versus the diversification and professional management benefits of ETFs.

title: Renate Nyborg becomes Tinder’s first female CEO
summary: Match Group Inc. has appointed Renate Nyborg as the new chief executive officer of Tinder, making her the dating app's first female CEO since its inception in 2012. Nyborg, who previously led Tinder's operations in Europe, Middle East, and Africa, will succeed Jim Lanzone. Her appointment is effective September 27 and she will report to Match CEO Shar Dubey.

title: Apple, Google, Amazon spying on you, lawsuits claim
summary: Lawsuits allege that Apple, Google, and Amazon are spying on users through their voice assistants, recording private conversations and sharing them with advertisers. A class-action lawsuit against Apple's Siri has been greenlit by a federal judge, with similar claims made against Google and Amazon. The companies deny these allegations, stating that recordings are only kept with user consent or to improve service, and offer options for users to manage their privacy settings.

title: Secret to Apple’s iPhone Sales Boom: Big 5G Deals From Wireless Carriers - WSJ
summary: Apple's recent surge in iPhone sales is largely attributed to aggressive 5G promotions by wireless carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile. These carriers have offered substantial subsidies to encourage customers to upgrade to 5G-capable iPhones and utilize their new high-speed networks. The article questions how long these costly carrier battles will continue to fuel iPhone sales as Apple prepares to unveil new 5G-enabled models.

title: Secret to Apple’s iPhone Sales Boom: Big 5G Deals From Wireless Carriers
summary: Apple's iPhone sales have seen a boom since the introduction of 5G-capable models, largely driven by aggressive promotional offers from wireless carriers. Major carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile have implemented substantial subsidies to retain customers and encourage upgrades to their 5G services. As Apple prepares to unveil new 5G iPhones, a key question remains about the sustainability of these carrier incentives in fueling iPhone sales.

title: How Navi's FoF on Vanguard US Total Stock Market ETF could add value to your portfolio
summary: Navi Mutual Fund has filed for a new Fund of Fund (FoF) that will invest in the Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund ETF (VTI), a passively managed US-based ETF with a very low expense ratio. This FoF will allow Indian investors to access the broad US equity market, including companies typically unavailable in India, potentially adding value due to low correlation with the Indian market. However, investors should be aware of debt fund taxation rules for international funds.

title: Apple fixes flaw exploited by Israeli firm’s Pegasus spyware
summary: Apple has released software updates to patch a critical security flaw in its Messages app that was reportedly exploited by NSO Group's Pegasus spyware. This "zero-click" vulnerability allowed hackers to access devices without user interaction, affecting iPhones, iPads, Macs, and Apple Watches. The flaw was discovered by Citizen Lab, which stated it was used to target a Saudi activist's device.
authors: Kartikay Mehrotra|Davide Scigliuzzo and William Turton and Bloomberg

title: Ford hires former Lowe's, Home Depot executive as chief digital and information officer
summary: Ford Motor Co. has appointed Mike Amend, formerly of Lowe's Companies Inc., as its new chief digital and information officer to lead the company's technology and software platforms. This strategic hire aligns with CEO Jim Farley's vision to transform Ford through electrification and advanced technologies, leveraging Amend's experience in e-commerce and digital growth from his previous roles at Lowe's, JCPenney, and Home Depot. Amend will work alongside Hau Thai-Tang and recent hire Doug Field to drive Ford's technological initiatives and develop new software solutions.

title: Apple loses (and wins) rulings in 'home button' patent dispute
summary: A U.S. court has affirmed Apple's win in invalidating parts of one fingerprint-authentication patent held by Korean company Firstface Co Ltd, while simultaneously upholding two other Firstface patents that Apple had challenged. The dispute centers on "home buttons" or "home keys" that wake devices and authenticate users via fingerprints. This mixed ruling comes after Firstface sued Apple and Samsung in 2018 for infringing these patents.

title: Redesigned Sonos Beam supports Dolby Atmos, eARC
summary: Sonos has redesigned its Beam soundbar, now in its second generation, to include Dolby Atmos support and HDMI eARC connectivity. The newer model retains the compact size of its predecessor but features a faster CPU for virtualized Dolby Atmos height effects and an updated polycarbonate grille. Shipping October 5 for $449, it will also support lossless and Dolby Atmos Music from Amazon Music.

title: Apple reveals iPhone 13 lineup, iPad Mini price increase and larger Apple Watch
summary: Apple Inc. unveiled its new lineup of devices for the holiday shopping season, including the iPhone 13, a more expensive iPad Mini, and a new Apple Watch with a larger screen. The announcement comes as the most valuable U.S. public company aims to focus on the busy holiday period rather than its ongoing regulatory challenges. These annual product reveals typically precede the holiday shopping frenzy.

title: Goldman Sachs appoints Coleman as new CFO, replacing Scherr
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. announced that Denis Coleman will replace Stephen Scherr as Chief Financial Officer at year-end. Coleman, currently co-head of the global financing group, will assume the deputy CFO role immediately. Scherr, who has been CFO since November 2018, will remain until the end of the year and formally retire at the end of January.

title: Apple’s Newest iPhone Comes With a $0 Price Tag Again at AT&T
summary: AT&T (T) is offering a new iPhone 13 Pro for free to new and existing customers with qualifying trade-ins and unlimited data plans, a deal valued at up to $1,000. This move marks a second year of giveaways for the carrier, aiming to extend subscriber growth and gain market share following the debut of Apple Inc.'s (AAPL) newest phones. T-Mobile US Inc. and Verizon Communications Inc. are also offering similar competitive deals, matching or providing substantial credits towards the new iPhone 13 models.

title: Here's everything Apple just announced at its iPhone 13 event
summary: Apple held its fall product event, unveiling the new iPhone 13 lineup, Apple Watch Series 7, and updated iPad and iPad mini models. Key improvements across the new iPhones include smaller notches, bigger batteries, and enhanced camera features, while the new Apple Watch boasts a larger display. The event also highlighted the international expansion of Apple Fitness+ and the upcoming iOS 15 release.

title: Apple offers iPhone 13 in four models
summary: Apple unveiled its new iPhone 13 lineup, offering four models with modest upgrades from previous versions, including an option for 1 terabyte of storage. Despite recent controversies for Apple, the new iPhones are expected to boost sales and continue the company's strong performance, although initial investor reaction was subdued. The company also previewed its next Apple Watch.

title: The rise of the customer innovation center
summary: Customer innovation centers (CICs) are evolving from traditional customer experience centers (CECs) to provide more experiential and memorable B2B engagements, addressing the historical lag in B2B customer experience compared to B2C. These new CICs focus on building brand identity, generating excitement through storytelling, reimagining the "new office" for hybrid workforces, and embedding constant change through accessible communication ecosystems. With the incorporation of hybrid compatibility and AI-experience curation, CICs are poised to become a critical component of experiential marketing, pushing creativity and connectivity for brands.

title: Doraville’s ‘Studio City’ developer gobbles up pioneering Third Rail Studios
summary: Gray Television, the developer behind Doraville's "Studio City" project, has acquired Third Rail Studios for $27.5 million. Third Rail Studios, a 70,000-square-foot film center that opened in 2015, was the first vertical component at the former Assembly Yards site and has hosted productions for Netflix, NBC, and Warner Bros. The acquisition is part of Gray Television's plan to transform the former General Motors plant into a 127-acre "Studio City," aiming to be the largest melding of TV/film production and mixed-use development in Georgia.

title: Apple reveals Apple Watch Series 7, featuring the largest, most advanced display
summary: Apple announced the Apple Watch Series 7, featuring a reengineered Always-On Retina display with significantly more screen area and thinner borders, making it the largest and most advanced display ever. The new series boasts enhanced durability, faster charging, new aluminum case colors, and runs on watchOS 8. It also introduces new workout types, improved fall detection for cycling, and expands Apple Fitness+ to more countries.

title: AT&T to offer all-new iPhone 13 Pro, iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 13, iPhone 13 mini, iPad, and iPad mini with Orders Starting on Friday, September 17
summary: AT&T announced it will offer the new iPhone 13 lineup, including the iPhone 13 Pro, Pro Max, 13, and 13 mini, alongside the 9th generation iPad and new iPad mini. Pre-orders begin September 17th, with availability on September 24th. The company highlights its 5G network and special offers, including deals for new and existing customers and services like AT&T Right To You for device setup.

title: A free iPhone 13? Wireless carriers’ subsidies could make it happen, with some catches
summary: Wireless carriers like AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile are once again offering significant discounts, potentially even a free iPhone 13, to attract new customers. This strategy, which resumed last year, aims to leverage the appeal of Apple’s new smartphones to shore up customer bases as 5G network access expands. While the iPhone 13 lineup offers few new features, the aggressive carrier subsidies are expected to drive sales.

title: Apple's new iPhone 13 touts faster 5G, sharper cameras to spur trade-ins
summary: Apple unveiled the iPhone 13 and a new iPad mini, featuring faster 5G, improved cameras, and a more powerful A15 Bionic chip, without increasing the phone's price. Though no revolutionary features were announced, analysts expect these upgrades, coupled with significant trade-in incentives from wireless carriers, will encourage customers to upgrade their older Apple devices. The company also updated its Apple Watch and base-model iPad, aiming to strengthen customer loyalty within its ecosystem despite recent regulatory scrutiny on its business practices.

title: Apple* and John Deere* shareholders file resolutions questioning their anti-repair stances
summary: Shareholders of Apple and John Deere have filed resolutions questioning the companies' anti-repair policies. Green Century Capital Management, an affiliated mutual fund company, cites concerns about anti-competitive practices, environmental sustainability, and the companies' stances creating risks for shareholders due to potential regulation and brand damage. The article highlights that despite lobbying efforts, pressure for Right to Repair reforms is growing from government and state legislatures.

title: An Extremely Simple 3 ETF Portfolio to Consider
summary: This article proposes a simple three-ETF portfolio for long-term investors who prefer not to engage in individual stock analysis. The suggested ETFs are the Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund (VTI), the Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US Index Fund ETF Shares (VEU), and the Vanguard Total Bond Market Index Fund ETF Shares (BND). The author highlights VTI's objective to track the US stock market and details its top holdings, current price, and expense ratio.

title: Goldman Sachs to acquire GreenSky for $2.24 billion
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has agreed to acquire GreenSky Inc. for approximately $2.24 billion, integrating the fintech company into its Marcus consumer-banking platform. This acquisition will add GreenSky's payment plan services for home-improvement and healthcare needs, along with its network of over 10,000 merchants. The deal aims to expand Goldman's consumer-banking offerings and reach new customers through merchant partnerships.

title: Shares of this obscure chip component supplier have surged by over 1200% in the past three years
summary: Shares of Nan Ya Printed Circuit Board Corp., an obscure Taiwanese company that supplies essential Ajinomoto build-up film (ABF) substrates for chipmaking, have surged over 1200% in the past three years. This surge is due to ABF substrate becoming a critical bottleneck in the semiconductor industry, with supply expected to remain constrained until at least 2025. Major tech companies are struggling to secure enough of this component, leading to increased profits and investment for substrate manufacturers.

title: Apple Pay: Usuarios logran enrolar tarjetas de crédito del Banco de Chile
summary: Clients of Banco de Chile successfully enrolled their credit cards with Apple Pay by changing their device's region to the United States. This unexpected integration suggests an imminent official launch of Apple Pay in Chile, despite the bank later blocking the enrollment possibility and declining to comment. The temporary success and subsequent notification indicate a direct, albeit unofficial, link between Banco de Chile and Apple's payment platform.

title: GUC supports TSMC's 3nm technology at 0.75V ...
summary: Taiwanese ASIC company GUC has developed a design flow for TSMC’s N3 3nm CMOS process, partnering with EDA vendors like Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA. GUC has also completed key N3 design elements, including a Computing Engine operating at 0.75V and customized low-power cells. The company is now working on N3 test chips and porting its IP, like its 5nm GUC-Link 3D interconnect, to 3nm.

title: Microsoft (MSFT) Board Approves $60B Share Repurchase Program
summary: Microsoft's board has approved a new $60 billion share repurchase program and increased its quarterly dividend by 11% to 62 cents per share. This move follows a series of similar buyback programs initiated by the company over the past decade, with the latest coming as a previous $40 billion program is nearing its completion. The announcement is particularly noteworthy given recent efforts by Democrats to impose a tax on corporate stock buybacks, a practice that critics argue can artificially inflate stock prices and EPS.

title: iPhone 13 T-Mobile Forever Upgrade Promo Allows You To Save Up To $800, But Here's the Catch
summary: T-Mobile has introduced its "Forever Upgrade" promotion for the new iPhone 13 lineup, offering savings of up to $800 on a new iPhone 13 and $800 off future iPhones every two years. However, customers should be aware of certain conditions, including a limit of four enrollments per account, incompatibility with Free Line promotions, trade-in restrictions, and the requirement of a premium plan like Magenta Plus, ONE Plus, or Magenta/Sprint MAX. Other carriers like AT&T and Verizon are also offering deals to reduce the price of new iPhones.

title: NC Fought Hard for Gilead’s New Site, Adding to Exploding Biotech Presence
summary: North Carolina aggressively vied with Atlanta to secure Gilead's new business services center, offering significant incentives like a nearly $10 million reimbursement. The new facility in the Research Triangle will create 275 high-paying HR, finance, and IT jobs, boosting the state's economy by an estimated $1.11 billion through tax revenue. This win further solidifies North Carolina's growing status as a major life sciences and tech hub, attracting other giants like Eli Lilly and Apple.

title: Kurt Preston Named Vice President of Sourcing for Sonos
summary: Sonos has appointed Kurt Preston as its new Vice President of Sourcing. Preston brings over 20 years of global experience in procurement and operations from companies like GE, Dell, Compaq, and Apple. ON Partners, specifically Heidi Hoffman and Jamie Ganner, facilitated this successful placement.

title: S&P 500 falls Friday, notches second straight week of losses in September slump
summary: Stocks, including the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite, experienced declines on Friday, marking the second consecutive week of losses for the S&P 500. This downturn is attributed to investor caution regarding the resurgent Covid virus, an upcoming Federal Reserve meeting, and September's historical tendency for market weakness. The market is also anticipating increased volatility due to seasonal patterns, continued virus uncertainty, and upcoming monetary and fiscal policy announcements from September to October.

title: Is a stock market correction on the horizon?
summary: The S&P 500 has seen a recent dip, leading analysts to narrow predictions for the U.S. equity market. While tech stocks may face challenges due to Fed policy and rising interest rates, other sectors like energy and small-caps are showing potential. Despite concerns, corrections are normal, and many analysts remain bullish on long-term market growth, suggesting current dips could be buying opportunities.

title: The computer chip industry has a dirty climate secret
summary: The computer chip industry, while crucial for green technologies, has a significant and growing carbon footprint due to its high energy and water consumption and hazardous waste production. Major players like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) are beginning to address this by committing to net-zero emissions and investing in renewable energy, driven by investor pressure and demand for greener supply chains. Decarbonizing the industry will require substantial innovation in manufacturing processes, energy efficiency, and the use of less polluting materials.

title: Apple’s Upgrades Were Modest, and Maybe That Was the Point
summary: This article discusses Apple's recent product announcements, including the iPhone 13, new iPads, and Apple Watch Series 7. The author suggests that the upgrades were modest, potentially by design. It also touches on the uncertainty surrounding Apple's App Store reforms.

title: Mattel, Warner Music Group's Arts Music Release He-Man, Hot Wheels, Thomas & Friends Albums
summary: Mattel has partnered with Warner Music Group's Arts Music division to release new albums based on popular franchises like He-Man and the Masters of the Universe, Hot Wheels, and Thomas & Friends. These releases build upon an existing multi-year global music licensing agreement. The new albums feature original soundtracks and theme songs from their respective series, with additional releases planned for the future across major digital music platforms.

title: Kontakt.io Launches Nano Tag, the World's Smallest Affordable, Disposable & Wearable BLE Tag
summary: Kontakt.io Inc. has launched the Nano Tag, hailed as the world's smallest affordable, disposable, and wearable BLE tag. This innovation aims to address worker safety and patient/visitor experience in healthcare, hospitality, and events by offering a cost-effective and environmentally friendly solution. The Nano Tag features a compact design, a non-toxic battery, Bluetooth 5.0 technology, and is available for purchase through various channels starting from $4.99 unit price.

title: รีวิวกองทุน SCBKEQTG: หุ้นเกาหลีกับศักยภาพการเป็นแถวหน้าของเอเชีย
summary: บทความนี้รีวิวกองทุน SCBKEQTG ซึ่งลงทุนในหุ้นเกาหลีใต้ โดยเน้นศักยภาพทางเศรษฐกิจของประเทศจากเทคโนโลยี การส่งออก และบริษัทชั้นนำ นอกจากนี้ยังอธิบายโครงสร้างของดัชนี MSCI Korea 25/50 ที่กองทุนใช้อ้างอิง และแนะนำ 10 อันดับแรกของหุ้นที่กองทุนลงทุน ซึ่งรวมถึง Samsung Electronics และ SK Hynix ที่เป็นผู้นำด้านเทคโนโลยีระดับโลก
authors: เด็กการเงิน DekFinance

title: SiriusXM Launches New Free Channel and Premium Podcast Subscription with Marvel Entertainment on Apple Podcasts
summary: SiriusXM and Marvel Entertainment have partnered to launch Marvel Podcasts Unlimited, a premium audio subscription available exclusively on Apple Podcasts. This service offers subscribers early and exclusive access to new scripted and unscripted Marvel podcast series, including "Marvel's Wastelanders" and the Spanish-language adaptation of "Marvel's Wolverine: The Long Night." Additionally, a free Marvel channel on Apple Podcasts will feature popular existing series.

title: BSX Begins Publishing MIAX Pearl Equities
summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX), a subsidiary of Miami International Holdings, Inc., has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for 10 actively traded U.S. companies to the Pyth Network. This marks BSX's first initiative to provide high-fidelity data to the Pyth Network, contributing U.S. equities exchange market data to support DeFi markets. The initial companies include Apple, Amazon, Google, Netflix, and Tesla.

title: BSX publishes market data to Pyth Network
summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for ten US companies to the Pyth Network, a decentralized financial market data distribution platform. This initiative marks BSX's first step in providing high-fidelity data to the blockchain industry via the Solana network, with plans to expand the data breadth. The included companies are major US firms like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla, marking a significant development for DeFi markets.

title: Apple bans Fortnite from App Store until appeals are exhausted
summary: Apple has informed Epic Games that Fortnite will remain banned from its App Store until all appeals in their legal dispute are exhausted, a process that Epic CEO Tim Sweeney estimates could take five years. This decision comes after a recent court ruling largely favored Apple, despite requiring the company to allow developers to direct users to alternative payment systems. Epic had sought reinstatement of its developer account, promising to adhere to Apple's guidelines, but Apple cited Epic's past "duplicitous conduct" and Sweeney's critical remarks post-ruling as reasons for refusal.

title: NetApp slims down to build itself back up
summary: NetApp has relocated its headquarters from Sunnyvale to Santana Row in San Jose, a move celebrated as the Deal of the Year. This strategic decision involves slimming down its physical footprint to build itself back up, moving into a 301,019-square-foot lease and selling its larger 21.5-acre Sunnyvale Campus. The article highlights the rationale behind this significant real estate transaction and its implications for NetApp.

title: Freshworks becomes first Indian SaaS company to list shares in US after stellar debut
summary: Freshworks Inc., an Indian-origin SaaS company, made a stellar debut on Nasdaq, becoming the first of its kind to list shares in the U.S. The company raised over $1.03 billion in its IPO, with its market capitalization surging to $13 billion on the first day of trading. This achievement not only marked a significant milestone for the company and its founders but also created 500 new millionaires among its employees.

title: Barry Diller's IAC Pursuing $2.5B Deal For Meredith Corp. - Report
summary: Barry Diller's InterActive Corp. (IAC) is reportedly in advanced discussions to acquire magazine publisher Meredith Corp. for over $2.5 billion. Meredith halted its print publication of People magazine, among others, and is focusing on its digital growth. The deal would significantly expand IAC's digital media presence by adding many of Meredith's brands.

title: Apple threatened Facebook ban over slavery posts on Instagram
summary: Apple threatened to remove Facebook's products from its App Store in 2019 after a BBC investigation revealed domestic "slaves" were being sold on apps, including Instagram. The Wall Street Journal's "Facebook Files" revealed Facebook took limited action until Apple's threat, despite internal reports showing knowledge of the online slave trade prior to the BBC's inquiry. Following the BBC report and Apple's intervention, Facebook banned hashtags, removed accounts, and developed technology to proactively address content related to domestic servitude.

title: Apple iPhone 13 tests whether deals, cameras will spur upgrades
summary: Apple released its iPhone 13 lineup, featuring new camera technology and enhanced battery life, to assess if these improvements combined with aggressive carrier deals will drive upgrades despite a lack of dramatic redesign. U.S. carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile are offering significant incentives, sometimes even making the iPhone 13 Pro free with trade-ins, which is already leading to backlogs in online orders for the new devices. This launch kicks off a busy holiday season for Apple, with other new products like a redesigned iPad mini and upcoming MacBook Pros and AirPods also in the pipeline.

title: Netflix VP Of Communications Richard Siklos Leaves The Company
summary: Richard Siklos, Netflix's VP of communications since 2017, has announced his departure from the streaming giant. His exit follows other executive changes at Netflix, which has seen significant growth in recent years and has maintained its leadership in the streaming market despite increased competition. Siklos previously held a senior communications role at Time Warner and had a notable career as a journalist specializing in the media business.

title: BlackBerry ditches Classic, adding to smartphone future doubts
summary: BlackBerry Ltd announced it will stop producing its Classic smartphone model, a move that contributed to a more than 4% drop in stock and intensified doubts about the future of its handset business. The decision reinforces the company's shift towards software, as the Classic and other BlackBerry 10 devices struggled to compete with the iPhone and Android-powered phones. Additionally, an internal U.S. Senate memo revealed that major carriers were informed that all BlackBerry 10 devices had been discontinued.

title: RIM delays new BlackBerry launch; sales crumble
summary: Research In Motion Ltd (RIM) has delayed the launch of its next-generation BlackBerry phones until early 2013, a significant setback for the company facing crumbling sales, a deeper-than-expected quarterly operating loss, and deep job cuts. The delay, coupled with a 30% workforce reduction and mounting financial pressure, signals a potential terminal decline for the once-dominant technology firm. Analysts are concerned about RIM's survival as it misses crucial shopping periods and struggles to compete with Apple and Android.

title: Apple claimed it had less than 20 million TV+ subscribers in July, showbiz union says
summary: A showbiz union, the International Alliance of Theatrical Stage Employees (IATSE), claims Apple stated it had under 20 million Apple TV+ subscribers in the U.S. and Canada as of July 1. This allowed Apple to pay lower rates to production crews under a contract provision for streaming services with fewer than 20 million subscribers. The union is currently in negotiations for a new contract and is considering a strike, arguing that streaming platforms, despite their profitability, are still leveraging outdated contract terms.

title: Apple’s power move to kneecap Facebook advertising is working
summary: Apple's iOS 14.5 privacy changes, which allow users to opt-out of app tracking, are significantly harming Facebook's advertising business. Advertisers, particularly performance marketers, are struggling to measure ad effectiveness and are consequently reducing their spending on Facebook and diversifying to other platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, and email. Critics suggest Apple's move is not solely privacy-driven but also a strategic play to build its own advertising platform, as it competes directly with Facebook's messaging apps.

title: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile to offer all-new iPhone 13 Pro, iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 13, iPhone 13 mini, iPad, and iPad mini with Orders Starting Today
summary: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile are now offering the full lineup of new Apple products, including the iPhone 13 series, iPad (9th generation), and iPad mini. Customers who port a new number to Xfinity Mobile will receive $300 off any iPhone, while existing customers upgrading their line will get a $300 Visa pre-paid card. The new devices feature advanced camera systems, the A15 Bionic chip, improved battery life, and enhanced 5G capabilities.

title: Which Game Making Tool Is Right for You?
summary: This article evaluates several popular game development tools—GameMaker Studio, RPG Maker, Unity, and Source Engine—to help aspiring indie developers choose the best one. It highlights GameMaker Studio as ideal for beginners due to its user-friendliness and gradual introduction to coding, while noting Unity's versatility for those willing to learn more complex systems. RPG Maker is recommended for those focusing on design over coding, and Source Engine is deemed better for experienced developers due to its age and complexity.

title: Some Apple, Tesla suppliers suspend production in China amid power pinch
summary: Several Apple and Tesla suppliers in China have suspended production due to stricter energy consumption policies and tight coal supplies, impacting their supply chains during the peak season for electronics. While some Taiwanese manufacturers temporarily halted operations, major chipmakers like UMC and TSMC reported no impact on their China facilities, and Foxconn experienced only a "very small" adjustment to its non-Apple production. This comes as China's heavy industry faces contraction, affecting the country's economic growth rate.

title: Apple Maps introduces new ways to explore major cities in 3D
summary: Apple Maps has received its biggest update ever with iOS 15, introducing a new 3D city experience in major cities like London, Los Angeles, New York City, and San Francisco. This update features highly detailed 3D maps, enhanced navigation for drivers, powerful transit updates, and immersive walking directions using augmented reality. Additionally, it offers curated guides for discovery and a new interactive 3D globe to explore the natural beauty of the Earth.

title: CfA Files Complaint Over Forced Labor in Apple’s Supply Chain
summary: The Campaign for Accountability (CfA) has filed a formal complaint with U.S. Customs and Border Protection regarding Apple's alleged use of forced labor in its supply chain. The Tech Transparency Project (TTP), a research initiative of CfA, has presented evidence linking Apple's products to forced labor in China, specifically involving minority Uighur laborers. CfA urges CBP to enforce the Tariff Act of 1930 and investigate Apple, potentially issuing a Withhold Release Order on Apple products tied to forced labor.

title: Everything you need to know about Apple Harvest Day 2021 as the festival returns to Dover
summary: Apple Harvest Day is returning to downtown Dover, New Hampshire on Saturday, October 2, 2021, after being canceled as an in-person event in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The 37th annual festival will feature over 250 vendors, food courts, live entertainment on two stages, kids' activities including a KidZone and Auto Alley, and the traditional Apple Harvest Day 5K race and apple pie contest. Shuttle parking will be available for attendees.

title: Match to sidestep Apple and Google Pay with imminent updates to Korean apps
summary: Match Group plans to update its Korean dating apps by the end of October to allow alternative payment systems, bypassing Apple and Google's in-app purchase fees. This move follows a new South Korean law targeting these fees and represents an initial test of the legislation. Match Group, which expects to pay $500 million in such fees globally this year, is preparing for potential resistance from Apple and Google.

title: Newmark Announces Sale of 200-Unit Multifamily Property Adjacent to Austin, Texas’ ‘Second Downtown’
summary: Newmark announced the sale of Northstar Apartments, a 200-unit multifamily property in Austin, Texas, located near "The Domain" and Apple's new campus. The property was sold by Nitya Capital to Langdon Street Capital, which plans further investment in the Austin market. The sale highlights the strong growth in Austin's multifamily capital markets, with significant sales volume driven by the tech employment cluster.

title: Specifications of Personal Computers Over Time
summary: This article traces the evolution of personal computer specifications over 50 years, from the 1971 Kenbak-1 to modern HP Pavilion models. It highlights significant advancements in RAM and processing speed, and discusses key PC specifications like processor speed and RAM, explaining their importance and upgradeability. An infographic details spec changes for popular computers through the years.

title: Jabil expects to hit over $8B in net revenue in Q1 of 2022
summary: Jabil Inc. (NYSE: JBL) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year, with net revenue of $7.4 billion and $29.3 billion respectively, despite supply chain challenges. Looking ahead to Q1 2022, the company projects net revenue between $8 billion and $8.6 billion, driven by its diversified business model and opportunities in vehicle electrification, connected healthcare, and infrastructure development. Jabil continues to work with major clients such as Amazon, Tesla, Apple, and Johnson & Johnson.

title: INSIGHTS | Conservative investors are pairing portfolio with principles
summary: Tom Carter, a mutual fund executive, has launched the American Conservative Values ETF to allow conservative investors to align their portfolios with their principles, avoiding "woke" companies. The fund, which began in October and already manages over $28 million, aims to provide an option for individuals who wish to invest in companies that share their conservative values rather than those that engage in social and racial justice advocacy. This initiative reflects a broader movement where conservatives are using economic choices to "fight back" against companies perceived as aligning with liberal politics.

title: Sonos Beam (2021, 2nd Gen) review: Compact soundbar delivers plenty of clout
summary: The Sonos Beam (2nd Gen) is presented as an excellent compact soundbar that, despite sharing much of its architecture with its predecessor, notably adds virtual Dolby Atmos support, a new processor, and an updated aesthetic with a plastic grille. While it doesn't offer true Dolby Atmos like larger, pricier alternatives, its virtual surround sound experience is highly effective for its size, especially when paired with additional Sonos speakers. The soundbar also boasts a sleek design, extensive smart features including voice assistants and AirPlay 2, and numerous music streaming options, making it a versatile choice for enhancing both TV audio and general music listening.

title: Online offers life-raft as local sports radio airwaves get rough
summary: Following Bell Media's abrupt closure of sports talk radio stations in several Canadian cities, including Vancouver, former hosts have successfully transitioned to online platforms. Shows like "Sekeres & Price" and "Donnie and Dhali – The Team" are thriving by offering independent, digital content, becoming a new business model for sports radio and demonstrating the strong demand for local sports talk.

title: Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, Disney Among Major Corporations Lying About Supporting Climate Action
summary: A new analysis by watchdog group Accountable.US reveals that major corporations like Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Disney are funding lobbying groups that actively work against significant climate legislation, despite publicly claiming to support climate action. These companies are reportedly supporting or directing organizations such as the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Business Roundtable in their efforts to defeat the $3.5 trillion Build Back Better Act. This behavior is labeled as "greenwashing," where companies project an environmentally responsible image while undermining climate initiatives behind the scenes.

title: Podcast Advertising
summary: TEGNA offers various podcast advertising solutions, including pre-roll, episode, and series sponsorships, through its premium podcast networks: VAULT Studios and Locked On Podcast Network. VAULT Studios focuses on true crime and cold cases, with several podcasts reaching the Top 10 on Apple's charts. Locked On provides daily local sports content for major professional and college teams.

title: GINN's Got the Goods of Quality Disruptive Companies
summary: The article highlights the Goldman Sachs Innovate Equity ETF (GINN) as a strong investment opportunity, particularly for those seeking exposure to high-quality, disruptive growth companies. Despite the S&P 500 Technology Index's flat performance in the third quarter, GINN focuses on larger, mature yet innovative companies with high return on equity (ROE), such as Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. The fund's significant allocation to technology and healthcare sectors, combined with its emphasis on quality traits and R&D-focused companies, positions it well for continued upside.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | Apple Pay Fees Vex Credit-Card Issuers
summary: Credit-card issuers are growing frustrated with the fees they pay to Apple for Apple Pay transactions. Banks want Visa to alter how it processes these transactions, particularly for recurring payments. This unhappiness has increased since Apple introduced its own credit card in 2019, adding to the banks' concerns about the costs associated with the mobile wallet.

title: The Beam (Gen 2) delivers in price, features and ease of use - WiFiHiFi
summary: The Sonos Beam (Gen 2) soundbar offers excellent 3D sound with Dolby Atmos support at an affordable price, but fully utilizing its advanced audio features requires a newer TV with HDMI eARC and premium streaming music subscriptions. The review highlights improvements over the original Beam, such as a more powerful processor, redesigned speaker grouping, and eco-friendly packaging. It concludes that while the Beam (Gen 2) is a strong entry point into the Sonos ecosystem, the larger Sonos Arc still provides a more immersive experience.

title: How DFW is getting more traction with semiconductors — and it's not just Texas Instruments
summary: The Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) area is seeing increased investment and activity in its semiconductor industry, extending beyond the historical dominance of Texas Instruments. This indicates a growing and diversifying semiconductor landscape in the region.

title: Ericsson sues Apple to clear proposed 5G patent licensing rates
summary: Ericsson has sued Apple in a Texas federal court, seeking a declaration that its proposed rates for licensing 5G wireless patents are fair and reasonable. The Swedish telecom giant accused Apple of employing tactics to lower royalty rates and refusing to license patents under terms other than its own. This lawsuit follows a previous patent licensing dispute between the two companies in 2015, which was eventually settled.

title: Ansys and Apple Develop the First Cloud-based RF Safety Testing Simulation Solution for MagSafe module MFi Developers
summary: Ansys and Apple have partnered to create the first cloud-based RF safety testing simulation solution for MagSafe module MFi developers. This new Ansys solution significantly reduces costs and accelerates the certification process for Made For iPhone (MFi) MagSafe module technology by eliminating the need for physical prototypes and expensive RF safety certification software. The platform uses Ansys' HFSS electromagnetic solver, offering a streamlined, intuitive, and automated process for SAR certification, helping developers bring safe and reliable products to market more quickly.

title: At a $2 Trillion Market Value, Saudi Aramco Races Apple for Most Valuable Company
summary: Saudi Aramco is nearing Apple Inc.'s market valuation, reaching $2 trillion due to surging energy prices, while Apple's value stands at $2.3 trillion. This shift reflects broader economic changes, with increasing oil demand benefiting Aramco and rising bond yields impacting tech stocks like Apple. Analysts suggest oil prices could climb further, potentially pushing Aramco's valuation even higher.

title: Roughly half of the Nasdaq 100 is below this critical longer-term trend line. Here's what's next
summary: A recent sell-off in high-growth stocks has caused roughly half of the Nasdaq 100 stocks to trade below their 200-day moving average, a critical longer-term trend line. This comes as bond yields rise due to higher inflation expectations, making valuations of growth stocks harder to justify. While some strategists believe tech might lag as the economy reopens, others anticipate a rotation back into growth stocks after the recent decline.

title: Nvidia offers EU concessions over $54 billion Arm deal
summary: Nvidia has offered concessions to the EU antitrust regulator regarding its $54 billion acquisition of British chip designer Arm. The deal has raised concerns within the semiconductor industry about Arm's neutrality as a technology supplier. The EU will now seek feedback before deciding whether to approve the deal, demand more concessions, or open a formal investigation.

title: Discover Equal Weight Benefits With RSP
summary: This article discusses the benefits of equal weight ETFs, using the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) as a prime example. It explains how equal weighting offers diversification beyond cap-weighted funds and benefits from mean-reversion, despite critics associating its outperformance with size and value factors. The article highlights RSP's long-term outperformance potential and its role in true diversification by preventing a few mega-cap stocks from dominating the index.

title: Erik Feig’s Mid-Budget Film Production Shop Picturestart Revs Up After Lockdown Pause
summary: Erik Feig's production company, Picturestart, which specializes in mid-budget films, overcame early challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic. Despite launching just months before the shutdown, the company focused on development and packaging, leading to six films entering production back-to-back in early 2021. Feig emphasizes a philosophy of "discovery of voice" and a flexible financial model to support unique stories and talent.

title: Paul Marshall and Ian Wace’s Stock Picks: Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL) and More
summary: This article details the top stock picks of hedge fund managers Paul Marshall and Ian Wace, based on their Q2 2021 holdings. It highlights a selection of companies including Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL), Zoetis Inc. (ZTS), ViacomCBS Inc. (VIAC), and Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO), providing details on their stake value, company background, and recent analyst ratings or news. The piece also explains the investment philosophy and background of Marshall Wace LLP.

title: A California investment firm went from near ruin to managing over $100 billion: Its turnaround may offer solutions to the ‘Great Resignation’
summary: WCM Investment Management, a Southern California firm, rebounded from near collapse in 2011 with just $833 million in assets to managing over $100 billion by September, a tenfold increase in a decade. The firm's success is attributed to its decision to retain employees and prioritize a positive corporate culture, rather than a "toxic" one, during challenging times. This unique approach, where top management absorbed losses instead of firing staff, is highlighted as a potential lesson for companies facing the "Great Resignation."

title: Chipmakers resist US bid for client data
summary: Chipmakers in Taiwan and South Korea are resisting a request from the US Department of Commerce for sensitive client and supply chain data, aimed at resolving global chip shortages. TSMC has stated it will not hand over client information, while the South Korean Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy has expressed concerns about the request's scope. This resistance highlights the complexity of the issue, with Taiwanese politicians and individual investors also voicing opposition.

title: TSMC and Sony considering joint chip factory, Japan gov't to help -Nikkei
summary: Taiwan's TSMC and Japan's Sony Group Corp are reportedly considering a joint venture to build a chip factory in Japan, with the Japanese government prepared to contribute to the estimated 800 billion yen investment. The facility in Kumamoto is expected to produce semiconductors for automobiles and camera image sensors to address the global chip shortage, aiming for operations by 2024. Japan's Denso is also looking to participate, as the country seeks to stabilize its industrial supply chains amid concerns about chipmaking concentration in Taiwan.
authors: Shinji Kitamura|Ben Blanchard|Ritsuko Ando|Kim Coghill|Kirsten Donovan|Lincoln Feast

title: It’s Time for the Apple Watch to Break Free From the iPhone
summary: This article argues that it is time for the Apple Watch to become more independent from the iPhone. It suggests Apple should allow the Apple Watch Series 7 and future models to function more autonomously. The piece also briefly mentions Apple's ambitions in car controls and an update on the Epic Games lawsuit.

title: T-Mobile 5G: all the phones, deals, coverage and pricing you need
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of T-Mobile's 5G rollout in the US, detailing its different 5G technologies (Extended Range and Ultra Capacity), coverage, and performance. It also lists compatible 5G phones available through T-Mobile, discusses various service plans, and highlights T-Mobile's initiatives for business and home internet services. The article emphasizes T-Mobile's leadership in 5G availability and reliability as per independent studies.

title: ClixTV Launches To Help Streamers Find Something To Watch
summary: ClixTV has launched as a new streaming service designed to help viewers find content across various top subscription services, offering its own short- and long-form programming, trailers, and e-commerce opportunities. Founded by industry veterans Edward Sullivan (CEO), Stacy Jolna (CMO), and Patricia Sullivan (CCO), ClixTV aims to simplify the content search experience for consumers and provide benefits for creators, distributors, and advertisers. The platform is available on multiple devices and services including Roku, Apple TV, Amazon Fire TV, and iOS/Android.

title: Understanding the Buzz Around the BUZZ ETF
summary: The article discusses the VanEck Vectors Social Sentiment ETF (BUZZ), which debuted in March 2021 and capitalizes on the "meme stock" phenomenon. It examines BUZZ's methodology, noting that its longer lookback period means it doesn't solely focus on highly speculative meme stocks like GameStop and AMC. Instead, BUZZ's portfolio includes quality names like AMD, Apple, Nvidia, and Pfizer, and exhibits low single-stock risk.

title: EU to investigate Nvidia's $54 bln ARM bid after remedies fall short - sources
summary: The EU is expected to launch an extended antitrust investigation into Nvidia's $54 billion bid for British chip designer ARM. This comes after remedies offered by Nvidia last week failed to address competition concerns, marking a second setback for the company after a similar warning from Britain's antitrust agency. The European Commission is scheduled to end its preliminary review on October 27, initiating a four-month investigation.

title: First Look at NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition Coming Exclusively to Apple Arcade on October 19
summary: 2K has released a trailer for NBA 2K22 Arcade Edition, a new version of the popular basketball game optimized for Apple devices, launching exclusively on Apple Arcade on October 19. The game will feature exclusive modes like "The Association" and "MyCOURT," along with online multiplayer, Blacktop mode, and MyCAREER mode, and will include the latest 2021-22 NBA rosters. It will be playable across iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple TV for a monthly subscription of $4.99.

title: Expedia CTO Rathi Murthy is helping the company simplify
summary: Rathi Murthy, who took over as Chief Technology Officer at Expedia Group in May 2021, is focusing on simplifying the company's technology infrastructure. Murthy brings experience from previous roles at Gap and Verizon to her new position at the Seattle-based travel company. This move is part of the company's efforts to streamline operations.

title: Apple May Cut iPhone 13 Production Goals Due to Chip Crunch
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to cut its iPhone 13 production targets for 2021 by up to 10 million units due to ongoing chip shortages impacting key suppliers like Broadcom and Texas Instruments. This reduction jeopardizes Apple's expected record-breaking holiday sales quarter, highlighting that even major tech giants are susceptible to global supply chain disruptions exacerbated by the pandemic. The shortages have already led to significant delays in iPhone 13 shipments and are affecting other Apple products as well.

title: Apple to slash iPhone 13 production on chip shortages
summary: Apple is expected to cut its projected iPhone 13 production targets for 2021 by up to 10 million units due to prolonged chip shortages affecting key suppliers like Broadcom Inc. and Texas Instruments Inc. This shortage is impacting Apple's ability to deliver new iPhone 13 Pro and Pro Max models, with current orders facing significant shipping delays. Despite Apple's strong buying power, the global chip shortage, exacerbated by the pandemic and an energy crisis in China, is affecting its production of other products as well, including the Apple Watch Series 7.

title: How ‘Squid Game’ reveals the flaw in Netflix’s products strategy
summary: Netflix's new partnership with Walmart for a digital storefront, the Netflix Hub, is criticized for its delayed inclusion of merchandise from the hugely popular show "Squid Game." While Netflix aims to diversify revenue through licensed products, the article highlights that fans are more interested in "real" items from the show, like Vans sneakers and tracksuits, which are experiencing massive sales spikes independently, revealing a flaw in Netflix's product strategy. The company's own official merchandise for "Squid Game" is described as limited and generic, not capitalizing on the immediate demand for authentic-looking items from the show.

title: Qualcomm Increases Share Buyback Plan by $10B
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) has approved a new $10 billion stock repurchase program, complementing an existing plan with $0.9 billion remaining. This move led to a 1.3% increase in shares during extended trading. Wells Fargo analyst Gary Mobley maintained a Hold rating but adjusted the price target to $138.

title: Apple likely to cut iPhone 13 production due to chip crunch – Bloomberg News
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly planning to slash its iPhone 13 production target by as many as 10 million units due to the ongoing global chip shortage, according to Bloomberg News. Chip suppliers like Broadcom Inc. and Texas Instruments are struggling to meet demand, impacting Apple's ability to produce the anticipated 90 million units by year-end. Despite this, some analysts believe Apple is handling the supply crunch better than competitors and maintain healthy sales forecasts for the iPhone 13.

title: TSMC announces chip plant in Japan, flags 'tight' capacity throughout 2022
summary: Taiwanese chip giant TSMC announced plans to build a new factory in Japan to address long-term chip demand, particularly for older chipmaking technology experiencing severe shortages. The company also indicated that tight chip supplies would likely persist through 2022 due to surging demand driven by the COVID-19 pandemic. TSMC reported higher-than-expected profits for Q3 and raised its 2021 revenue growth forecast to about 24%, highlighting strong demand across key growth platforms.

title: Zepp Health banks on new smartwatches for growth
summary: Zepp Health, formerly Huami Corp, is banking on its new smartwatch releases, the Amazfit GTR 3 and GTS 3 series, to drive growth. The company has seen significant global expansion, becoming the fourth-largest smartwatch vendor by shipments in Q1. Zepp Health is investing in R&D and incorporating advanced health-tracking features into its devices to compete in the growing smartwatch market.

title: Medtronic pledges R&D boost, brand refresh, amid rising Big Tech rivalry
summary: Medtronic is rebranding and increasing its R&D spending by 10% to adapt to the growing influence of consumers and tech giants in healthcare. CEO Geoff Martha emphasized moving "beyond devices" by investing in AI, data analytics, and robotics, acknowledging the company needs to build its direct-to-consumer presence. This strategic shift aims to speed up market entry and address chronic diseases, following a major reorganization implemented in October of the previous year.

title: Sustainability is an Integral Part of ST Value Proposition Says Jean-Louis CHAMPSEIX
summary: Jean-Louis CHAMPSEIX, Group VP, Head of Corporate Sustainability at STMicroelectronics, discusses the company's long-standing commitment to sustainability since its establishment in 1987. He emphasizes that sustainability is deeply embedded in ST's business model, culture, and value proposition, driving the creation of technology for a sustainable world in a sustainable way. The article highlights ST's goals for carbon neutrality by 2027, significant reductions in energy consumption, and efforts in responsible product development, aiming for one-third of its revenue from sustainable technology products by 2027.

title: Zepp Health banks on new smartwatches for growth
summary: Zepp Health, formerly Huami Corp, is launching new smartwatches to capitalize on growing consumer demand for smart devices. The company, which is already a top smartwatch vendor in several global markets, is focusing on enhancing user experience, battery life, and especially advanced health-tracking features to differentiate its products in a competitive market.

title: S.Korea targets Apple over new app store regulation
summary: South Korea is moving to investigate Apple Inc. after the company indicated it would not change its app store policy, despite a new law requiring app developers to be able to use alternative payment systems. This law, amended in August, aims to curb the market dominance of major app store operators like Apple and Google by preventing them from mandating their own payment systems and charging commissions. Google, however, has stated its intention to comply with the new regulation.

title: TSMC to Build Chip Fab in Japan
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced plans to build a semiconductor plant in Japan, with construction starting next year and production by 2024. The Japanese government will provide significant financial aid for the estimated $8.8 billion investment, which aims to produce chips using 22- and 28-nanometer processes for consumer products, sensors, IoT, and auto parts. This move comes amid a global chip shortage and aligns with efforts by the U.S., Europe, and Japan to reduce their reliance on foreign chip production.

title: Tile Compatibility With Android™ and Apple® iOS Devices | Tile
summary: This article discusses the compatibility of Tile trackers with both Android and Apple iOS devices. It highlights that Tile works seamlessly across both operating systems, allowing users to track their belongings regardless of their phone's brand. The article also touches upon the core functionalities of Tile, such as finding misplaced items, and emphasizes its cross-platform accessibility.

title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger hopes to win back Apple by outcompeting it
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger expressed his hope to regain Apple as a customer, acknowledging that Intel will need to outperform Apple to achieve this. Gelsinger stated that he doesn't blame Apple for choosing to produce its own chips over using Intel's. He emphasized the necessity of Intel's products being superior to Apple's.

title: Check out Cipher Mining Equity Warrant Exp 26th Aug 2026's stock price (CIFRW) in real time
summary: This article provides real-time stock information for Cipher Mining Equity Warrant Exp 26th Aug 2026 (CIFRW), including its current price, historical performance metrics, key financial stats, and recent news. It highlights the company's profile as an industrial-scale data center construction and operations firm focused on bitcoin mining and high-performance computing, along with insights from analyst calls.

title: Alibaba said to be launching a new home-grown chip this week
summary: Alibaba Group Holding is set to launch a server chip this week based on Arm Holdings technology, joining other Chinese tech giants in semiconductor development. The chip’s design process concluded mid-year and it will be minted using the 5-nanometre process, showcasing China's increasing investment in chip design due to lower capital requirements. Alibaba's previous chip endeavors include Hanguang 800 and XuanTie 910.

title: Apple unveils game-changing MacBook Pro
summary: Apple has unveiled a completely reimagined MacBook Pro powered by the new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, available in 14- and 16-inch models. These new laptops offer extraordinary performance, unprecedented battery life, and advanced features like a Liquid Retina XDR display, improved camera and audio systems, and extensive connectivity, marking a significant leap for professional users. The MacBooks are designed for various professionals, offer remarkable power efficiency, and feature an environmentally conscious design with recycled materials.

title: Apple launches faster chips, MacBook Pro laptops, Airpods at October event
summary: Apple Inc. unveiled new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, updated MacBook Pro laptops, and third-generation AirPods at its October event. The new chips are faster and more power-efficient, leading to improved performance and extended battery life for the MacBook Pros which also received a new design and 1080p camera. The new AirPods feature spatial audio, longer battery life, and a new design, with the base model receiving a price cut.

title: Megahit "Squid Game" puts focus squarely on Netflix's overseas growth
summary: The success of "Squid Game" is prompting investors to focus on Netflix's international content strategy as a key driver for subscriber growth, especially in emerging markets, after a period of slowing additions.
