Analysts anticipate the megahit will boost subscriber numbers beyond estimates for the third quarter and impact the next three months. The article also highlights increased competition in the streaming market and Netflix's financial fundamentals.

title: NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition Available Now on Apple Arcade
summary: 2K has launched NBA® 2K22 Arcade Edition exclusively on Apple Arcade, offering an authentic NBA 2K experience across Apple devices. The game includes various modes like The Association, MyCOURT, Quick Match, Online Multiplayer, and MyCAREER, featuring updated 2022 NBA rosters and top players. It is available for a monthly subscription with a free trial, playable on iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple TV.

title: On-device Panoptic Segmentation for Camera Using Transformers
summary: This article details Apple's HyperDETR, an on-device panoptic segmentation model for its Camera app. Built on Transformers, HyperDETR efficiently segments images for features like Portrait Mode and Photographic Styles without significant battery drain. The paper highlights the model's architecture, training, and compression techniques, making it suitable for resource-constrained mobile environments.

title: Warren Buffett, Berkshire Hit $9 Billion Gain on Amex Stock This Year
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has seen its investment in American Express (Amex) increase by $9 billion this year, bringing its total unrealized gain on the stock to $26 billion. Berkshire owns 19% of Amex, a position it has held since 1998, having only invested $1.3 billion initially. Amex is the third-largest holding in Berkshire's US stock portfolio, following Apple and Bank of America.

title: Are Cyber Actors Hiding in Your Pocket? Rooting Out Spyware on Mobile Devices
summary: Mobile device security features, while beneficial for privacy, create challenges for forensic investigators trying to detect sophisticated spyware like Pegasus, which can exploit "zero-click" vulnerabilities. A new methodology developed by FTI Consulting utilizes threat-detection tools and AI to analyze anonymized network data and device logs, allowing for more efficient identification of compromises and the creation of detailed attack timelines without the need for traditional, time-consuming forensic processes. This approach helps investigators keep pace with evolving threats and provides a clearer picture of cyber attacks on mobile devices.

title: A Look Inside The Apple iPhone 13 Pro
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Apple iPhone 13 Pro, model A2636, revealing its internal components, estimated build costs, and design wins. It highlights the A15 Bionic processor, image sensor specifications, and contributions from various suppliers like Qualcomm, KIOXIA, and STMicroelectronics. The report also compares the iPhone 13 Pro's cost and features to its predecessor, the iPhone 12 Pro, and a competitor, the Samsung Galaxy S21+.

title: Target is adding Apple mini-shops to 3 more Minnesota stores
summary: Target Corp. is expanding its partnership with Apple Inc., adding three new mini-shops within its Minnesota stores. This move more than doubles the number of Apple mini-shops already present in Target locations. This expansion is part of a broader strategy to integrate specialized retail experiences within Target stores.

title: Snap tumbles, drags social media giants on Apple privacy tweak worries
summary: Snap's shares plunged by nearly 20% after the company warned that Apple's new iOS privacy changes would have a prolonged negative impact on its advertising revenue, also dragging down other ad-dependent tech firms like Facebook, Google, and Twitter. Analysts cut Snap's price targets, expecting the impact to last into next year. The company cited global supply chain disruptions and labor shortages as additional factors compounding the advertising slowdown.

title: Phunware (PHUN) stock closes up 471% on Trump Truth Social network rumor
summary: Phunware (PHUN) stock surged 471% on an unsubstantiated rumor linking the company to Donald Trump's new Truth Social network. This followed a similar massive gain by Digital World Acquisition Corp (DWAC), which is merging with Trump Media & Technology Group. The article highlights the volatile and often irrational nature of the stock market, comparing these movements to meme stocks and other speculative investments in 2021.

title: Apple’s New AirPods Are Pandemic-Proof
summary: The review highlights the new Apple AirPods 3, emphasizing that their shorter stems make them "pandemic-proof" by not interfering with face mask loops, solving a common frustration. While also noting improved sound and battery life, the author positions the mask compatibility as the most significant enhancement. The article also discusses spatial audio, battery life, and sweat resistance, concluding with advice on which AirPods model to consider based on user needs.

title: PayPal says it is currently not pursuing Pinterest acquisition
summary: PayPal has announced it is not currently pursuing an acquisition of Pinterest, following media reports last week of a potential $45 billion deal. This news caused Pinterest's share price to drop over 12%, though analysts suggest Pinterest might still attract other bidders due to its valuation and recent share decline. The potential deal had faced skepticism from investors and analysts, who highlighted significant integration risks and PayPal's lack of experience in user engagement platforms.

title: How’s 5G standalone doing in the U.S.?
summary: Rogers Communications has launched Canada's first national standalone 5G core, highlighting a disparity with the U.S. where adoption has been slower. T-Mobile is currently the only major U.S. operator with a commercial nationwide standalone 5G network, while AT&T is still testing and Verizon is gradually integrating it. The benefits of standalone 5G, such as lower latency and network slicing, are primarily realized by enterprises and specific advanced devices, though wider consumer benefits are expected to develop over time.

title: Bradesco Cartões burla regra da App Store com pedido de rastreamento
summary: Bradesco Cartões is accused of violating Apple's App Store rules by restricting app functionalities if users deny tracking. The app requires tracking for full features, claiming it's for fraud prevention, despite Apple's guidelines prohibiting such demands. While Bradesco states its app passed Apple's verification, Apple has not yet commented on the situation.

title: These 10 Stocks Make Up 87% of Warren Buffett's Portfolio
summary: This article details the top 10 stock holdings in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, which collectively account for 87% of its $329.7 billion valuation. It highlights Buffett's belief that diversification isn't essential if one knows their investments well. The individual stocks, including Apple, Bank of America, and Coca-Cola, are discussed with brief explanations for their inclusion and performance.

title: Intel 12th Gen Core Alder Lake Details at Intel Innovation 2021
summary: Intel unveiled its 12th Gen Core Alder Lake architecture at Intel Innovation 2021, marking a significant architectural shift with a hybrid design combining performance (P-cores) and efficient (E-cores) cores. The platform also introduces support for DDR5 and PCIe Gen5, aiming to enhance multi-tasking performance and scalability across various form factors. This announcement sets the stage for a new generation of Intel processors focusing on diverse workloads and improved efficiency.

title: Apple charges forward to 2030 carbon neutral goal, adding 9 gigawatts of clean power and doubling supplier commitments
summary: Apple has significantly advanced its 2030 carbon neutral goal, doubling the number of suppliers committed to 100 percent clean energy to 175. This initiative is expected to bring online over 9 gigawatts of clean power and avoid more than 18 million metric tons of CO2e annually. Additionally, Apple is supporting 10 new "Power for Impact" projects globally to bring clean energy solutions to under-resourced communities and reduce its carbon footprint.

title: Apple And Comcast Set Two-Way Streaming Distribution Deal
summary: Comcast and Apple have announced a two-way streaming distribution deal. This agreement will bring Comcast's Xfinity Stream and Sky Go apps to Apple TV, and integrate Apple TV+ onto various Comcast platforms, including Xfinity X1, Flex, XClass smart TV, Sky Q, and Sky Glass. The deal aims to enhance content aggregation and user experience across both companies' ecosystems.

title: Target adding Apple display in Mandarin store
summary: Target is enhancing its Mandarin store at 10490 San Jose Blvd. with a dedicated "enhanced Apple experience" display. The city issued a permit for the $310,000 project to create a 2,700-square-foot Apple display unit within the store, featuring new lighting and displays for various Apple products and accessories. This initiative, which debuted online and in select locations earlier in the year, aims to provide customers with an interactive experience supported by specially trained Target Tech Consultants.

title: Apple and Amazon Stock Dives Set to Erase $200 Billion in Value
summary: Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN) are expected to see their combined market valuation drop by over $200 billion when markets open Friday after both companies reported disappointing earnings. Apple's revenue missed expectations due to supply constraints, while Amazon's revenue forecast was below consensus and it warned of high costs impacting holiday quarter profits.

title: Tesla's 'Sentry Mode' now offers live view of car to owner
summary: Tesla has released a new software update for its Sentry Mode, allowing owners to remotely view their car's surroundings via a live camera feed on iOS devices. This feature, called Sentry Mode Live Camera Access, requires an active Premium Connectivity subscription for full functionality. The company emphasizes that the live camera feed is end-to-end encrypted and inaccessible by Tesla.

title: Microsoft unseats Apple as most valuable company
summary: Microsoft has surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable company following Apple's rare quarterly sales miss due to chip shortages and supply chain issues. Microsoft currently holds a market capitalization of approximately $2.48 trillion, slightly exceeding Apple's $2.47 trillion. This shift comes after Apple reported fourth-quarter sales below Wall Street's projections, while Microsoft's first-quarter revenue significantly beat expectations, driven by strong growth in its Intelligent Cloud segment.

title: Move over Apple, Microsoft now the world’s most valuable company
summary: Microsoft Corp. surpassed Apple Inc. to become the world's most valuable public company after Apple's shares fell due to a $6 billion sales hit from global supply chain issues. Microsoft's stock has surged over 45% this year driven by cloud-based services demand, while Apple, despite robust share buybacks, saw its market capitalization slip below Microsoft's. Both companies face ongoing supply chain challenges, though Microsoft anticipates a strong end to the year for its cloud business.

title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger’s “insane” plan to restore the company’s glory days
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger is implementing an "insane" and ambitious plan to regain the company's market leadership. This involves massive capital expenditures between $25 billion and $28 billion annually, along with $15 billion in R&D, to improve technology and capacity, especially in foundry business. The strategy aims for performance parity by 2024 and industry leadership by 2025, a bold move given Intel's recent struggles against competitors like Apple and ARM-based chips.

title: Apple cuts iPad production to feed chips to iPhone 13 -Nikkei
summary: Apple has reduced its iPad production by 50% for the past two months to reallocate components to the iPhone 13, according to Nikkei. This move highlights the impact of the global chip supply crunch on Apple, despite its strong purchasing power and supplier agreements. The company is prioritizing iPhone 13 production due to forecasted stronger demand as Western markets recover from the pandemic and increased demand in China.

title: Apple adds suppliers to clean-energy pledge, including more chip firms
summary: Apple announced that 175 of its suppliers have committed to using clean energy for their work, bringing over 9 gigawatts of clean energy to power grids, as part of its goal to achieve a carbon-neutral supply chain by 2030. This expansion includes key chip manufacturers like SK Hynix Inc and STMicroelectronics, adding to previous commitments from major suppliers like Foxconn and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Apple is also funding 10 smaller renewable energy projects in under-resourced communities globally.

title: Another Apple Worker Says the Company Retaliated Against Her (Published 2021)
summary: Janneke Parrish, a former Apple Maps program manager and leader of the #AppleToo activist movement, has filed a complaint with the National Labor Relations Board, alleging Apple fired her in retaliation for her activism. This escalation marks a growing conflict between Apple and some of its employees who have been vocal about workplace concerns. The situation signals a notable shift for Apple, which has historically avoided the kind of worker unrest observed at other major tech companies.

title: Netflix Adds Mobile Games to Its App for Some Users
summary: Netflix has begun rolling out mobile games to its app for Android users, including titles based on its popular show "Stranger Things." This move is part of the streaming company's strategy to attract new subscribers and increase engagement among current users. Games are expected to become available for Apple iOS devices in the coming months.

title: The global chip industry has a colossal problem with carbon emissions
summary: The global semiconductor industry, particularly major players like TSMC, Samsung, and Intel, faces a significant challenge with carbon emissions due to the energy-intensive chip manufacturing process. While these companies have pledged to reduce their environmental impact, their emissions continue to rise with expansion, especially for TSMC, which consumes a substantial amount of Taiwan's power output. Shifting to renewable energy sources and improving energy efficiency are critical steps being taken, but turning these pledges into actual practice across complex global supply chains remains a difficult task.

title: Monthly Market Wrap - October 2021
summary: The October 2021 market wrap highlights a month of economic observations including rising inflation, the Federal Reserve's anticipated tapering, and varied performance across domestic and international equities. It also covers positive trends in alternative investments and the increasing integration of ESG factors into investment strategies. The report details specific market indices and economic indicators, providing a comprehensive overview of the financial landscape during the month.

title: Qualcomm forecast beats estimates as diversification efforts pay off
summary: Qualcomm Inc. reported better-than-expected profits and revenue forecasts, driven by strong demand for chips across various sectors including phones, cars, and IoT devices. The company's diversification strategy, which involves supplying chips for Android phones replacing Huawei units and utilizing multiple foundries, has paid off amidst global supply chain challenges. Qualcomm's CEO Cristiano Amon confirmed that securing additional chip supplies earlier in the year was successful, contributing to their record guidance.

title: Apple's software boss warns against draft EU policy on App Store
summary: Apple's software boss, Craig Federighi, cautioned against a draft EU policy that could mandate allowing third-party software installations outside its App Store. Federighi argued that such a move, known as "sideloading," would make iOS devices vulnerable to malware and cybercriminal attacks. Critics, including Spotify, view Apple's stance as an anti-competitive tactic to maintain control and high commission fees, while Apple emphasizes user safety and security through its current App Store policies.

title: PALCUS announces new mobile app, logo and brand identity
summary: The Portuguese-American Leadership Council of the United States (PALCUS) is launching a new mobile app designed to be a central hub for the Portuguese-American community. The app will facilitate networking, share information about businesses and events, and provide resources for elected officials and organizations. Concurrently, PALCUS is unveiling a redesigned logo and brand identity to reflect its renewed focus on advocacy, youth leadership, and modern digital engagement after 30 years of service.

title: How To Target Your Audience Without Third-Party Cookie Data
summary: This article explores the impending demise of third-party cookies by late 2023 and its implications for digital advertising. It discusses how advertisers will face challenges in targeting ads and measuring performance, and outlines three potential scenarios for digital ad targeting: walled gardens, a consent economy, and identity innovation. The article also provides strategies for marketers to adapt, focusing on first-party data, direct publisher relationships, and contextual advertising.

title: ‘Business needs a noble purpose and a human-centric approach — these core principles revived Best Buy’
summary: Hubert Joly, former CEO of Best Buy, discusses how a human-centric approach and noble purpose revived the company, leading its share price to rise 11-fold. He emphasizes that putting people at the center and embracing all stakeholders is crucial for businesses today, especially in navigating challenges like the pandemic and climate change. Joly argues that the "Milton Friedman doctrine" of profit-first is outdated and highlights companies like Microsoft, Ralph Lauren, and Unilever as examples of successful human-centric models.

title: How Intel plans to catch Samsung and TSMC and regain its dominance in the chip market
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has outlined an ambitious plan to regain dominance in the chip market by 2025, aiming to surpass competitors Samsung and TSMC. The strategy involves significant investment in new fabrication plants globally, totaling over $44 billion, and opening Intel's fabs to other companies through Intel Foundry Services. This move addresses past production missteps and aims to leverage government subsidies like the CHIPS Act to boost U.S. chip manufacturing.

title: Supply chain problems crimp profit at Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway; cash sets record
summary: Berkshire Hathaway reported that global supply chain disruptions significantly impacted its ability to generate profit in the third quarter of 2021, leading to an 18% rise in operating profit that still missed analyst forecasts. While net income fell 66% due to lower stock gains, the company's cash hoard reached a record $149.2 billion, reflecting reduced acquisitions and substantial stock buybacks. Supply chain issues also caused price increases for materials and freight, labor shortages, and reduced sales for various Berkshire businesses.

title: The Future of Netflix’s Gaming Service Rests With Apple’s App Store Rules
summary: Netflix's ambitious plans for its gaming service, aimed at customer retention and acquisition, face a significant hurdle: Apple's App Store rules. While Netflix's gaming offering is currently limited, particularly on Android, its expansion on iOS could be severely constrained by Apple's policies. This highlights a potential conflict between Netflix's strategic growth in gaming and Apple's control over its ecosystem.

title: Apple Music expands Chinese music reservoir via Tencent deal
summary: Apple Music is partnering with Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) to allow musicians participating in TME's "Music Cloud" program to distribute their works globally through Apple Music. This deal will give Apple Music users access to more Chinese artists. While details are vague, the "Music Cloud" program primarily supports independent musicians and comes amidst China's antitrust crackdowns on TME, pushing it to relinquish exclusive music rights.

title: Adobe issued a DMCA request to remove Clean Flash, an open source project that continued to support Flash
summary: Adobe has issued a DMCA request to shut down the "Clean Flash" open-source project, which aimed to continue supporting Adobe Flash Player worldwide after its official end-of-life on December 31, 2020. While Adobe officially stopped global distribution of Flash, it still supports the technology in China and for businesses, leading Clean Flash to provide a working and updated version independently. This move highlights ongoing debate within the developer community about the preservation of Flash content versus moving entirely to open standards like HTML5.

title: Google’s parent Alphabet breaches $2 trillion in market value
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., reached a $2 trillion market valuation for the first time, driven by strong growth in digital advertising and its cloud business. This puts Alphabet in an exclusive club with Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp., with analysts remaining optimistic about its future growth due to a cheaper valuation and increasing cloud adoption.
authors: Katrina Lewis and Nico Grant and Bloomberg

title: An original Apple-1 computer sells for $400,000
summary: An original Apple-1 computer, one of only 200 designed by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, was sold for $400,000 at auction. The computer, known as the "Chaffey College" Apple-1, was initially sold for $666.66 in 1976 and represents a significant piece of personal computing history. Its koa wood case is one of only six known to exist, making it a rare collector's item.

title: Salesforce CEO Benioff invests in search engine startup You.com
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has invested $20 million in You.com, a startup aiming to challenge Google's dominance in search with a more customizable and privacy-focused engine. Benioff transferred ownership of the You.com domain name, which he had owned since the 1990s, to the startup. You.com, founded by former Salesforce AI scientists, plans to differentiate itself by not tracking users or selling "privacy-invasive ads," instead focusing on revenue from transactions and helping publishers generate ad revenue.

title: Google-owner Alphabet breaches $2 trillion market cap, joins Apple and Microsoft
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., reached a $2 trillion market capitalization for the first time, driven by increased digital ad spending and growth in its cloud business. This achievement places Alphabet alongside Apple and Microsoft in the exclusive $2 trillion club. The company reported a record third quarter in 2021 with $65.1 billion in earnings.

title: Google loses challenge against EU antitrust ruling, $2.8-bln fine
summary: Alphabet's Google unit lost an appeal against a 2.42-billion-euro ($2.8-billion) antitrust decision by the EU related to its price comparison shopping service. This ruling marks a significant win for Europe's competition chief, Margrethe Vestager, and could strengthen the EU's position in ongoing investigations into other major tech companies like Amazon, Apple, and Facebook. Google stated it would review the judgment and has already complied with the Commission's order to ensure a level playing field.
authors: Francois Aulner and Foo Yun Chee

title: What Does iOS 14.5 Mean for Marketers? A Deep Dive into Apple’s ATT and PCM
summary: The iOS 14.5 update significantly impacts app advertisers due to Apple's App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework, which limits the use of Identifier for Advertisers (IDFA) by requiring explicit user consent for tracking. While web audiences are less affected, app-to-app tracking is heavily restricted, leading to fewer reported conversions. The article suggests strategies for marketers, such as separating Android and iOS traffic and optimizing for upper-funnel events, to navigate these changes.

title: Tencent Music brings catalog of Chinese tracks to Apple Music worldwide
summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) has partnered with Apple Music to make its catalog of Chinese music available worldwide through TME Music Cloud. This collaboration aims to introduce global audiences to China's unique music culture and genres, supporting the international development of Chinese musicians. TME reported strong growth in its independent musician platform and online music paying users in Q3 2021, despite a slight decline in overall mobile MAUs.

title: Apple's original computer fetches $400,000 at US auction
summary: An original Apple-1 computer, built by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs in 1976, was sold for $400,000 at a US auction. The rare, functioning computer with a Hawaiian koa wood case is one of only 200 made and had only two previous owners. This model is considered a "holy grail" for vintage electronics collectors, though it wasn't the highest-selling Apple-1 to date.

title: 5 Charts on General Electric's Fall From Grace
summary: This article details General Electric's significant decline in value over the past two decades, from once being the most valuable company globally to its current 75th position in the U.S. stock market. It attributes GE's fall to a deterioration of culture, poor capital allocation decisions, and an over-reliance on its financial unit. The article discusses GE's recent decision to split into three companies (aviation, healthcare, and energy), a move applauded by analysts and seen as a potential catalyst for closing the persistent price/value gap for the stock.

title: Cinemark Welcomes Moviegoers to New Roseville Galleria Theatre on Nov. 11
summary: Cinemark officially opened its new 14-screen Cinemark Roseville Galleria Mall and XD theatre in the Westfield Galleria at Roseville on November 11, 2021. This new location aims to provide an upgraded, immersive entertainment experience for the Sacramento area, featuring luxury loungers, XD auditoriums, laser projection, and expanded concession options. To celebrate the grand opening, Cinemark offered a one-month free trial of its Movie Club to the first 1,000 visitors.

title: Exclusive: First Sonos headphones could use this RHA design
summary: British audio brand RHA's cancelled "Atlas" headphones could provide the design blueprint for Sonos's first venture into the headphone market. Sonos recently acquired a majority stake in Origin North Ltd. (formerly RHA), suggesting they may leverage RHA's technology and designs. The Atlas headphones were sophisticated, featuring ANC, spatial audio, Hi-Res Bluetooth, and Google Assistant, with a projected price similar to Apple's AirPods Max.

title: Extended Warranty Revenue Trends, 11 November 2021
summary: This article analyzes extended warranty revenue trends through the financial data of four key companies: Assurant Inc., Frontdoor Inc., Lowe's Companies Inc., and Apple Inc. It suggests that 2020 and 2021 have been strong years for the product protection industry, despite initial pandemic lockdowns. The report details revenue and profit growth for these companies' respective extended warranty programs, concluding that the industry experienced no recession and is breaking records for growth and profitability.

title: Alibaba's Singles Day sales grow just 8.5%, slowest pace ever
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd reported an 8.5% growth in sales during its annual Singles' Day shopping event, marking the slowest pace ever for the e-commerce giant. This slowdown is attributed to strong regulatory and supply chain headwinds affecting Chinese tech firms. Alibaba also shifted its focus from sales figures to social welfare initiatives, toning down the traditional marketing hype amid ongoing regulatory tightening.

title: Moviegoers Can Now Use Bitcoin, Ether at AMC Theatres
summary: AMC Theatres now accepts Bitcoin, Ether, and several other cryptocurrencies and digital payment platforms for movie tickets, aiming to attract crypto-savvy customers. This move highlights the growing acceptance of digital currencies in mainstream business, creating new opportunities for exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that offer exposure to cryptocurrency or blockchain technology. Investors can gain exposure through ETFs like Amplify Transformational Data Sharing ETF (BLOK) for blockchain or by investing in companies like AMC via the SoFi Social 50 ETF (SFYF).

title: 6 Things You Didn't Know About Cisco Networking Systems, Inc.
summary: This article reveals six lesser-known facts about Cisco Networking Systems, Inc., highlighting its origins, challenges, past market dominance, ventures into consumer electronics, divestitures, and its fastest-growing cybersecurity business. It aims to provide investors with a broader understanding beyond Cisco's reputation as a mature growth company focused on networking products.

title: China’s property crackdown sinks economic growth to 90s levels
summary: China's economy is experiencing a significant slowdown, reaching levels last seen in the 1990s, largely due to President Xi Jinping's crackdown on the real estate sector. This policy aims to reduce the economy's reliance on property and redirect investment towards high-tech manufacturing, despite economists predicting a substantial drop in GDP growth for 2022. The crackdown, coupled with China's strict "zero-COVID" policy, is impacting consumer spending and causing concern among global financial institutions.

title: PayPal’s Legal Chief Louise Pentland to Depart at Year’s End (2)
summary: Louise Pentland, PayPal's chief business affairs and legal officer, is set to depart the company on December 31st after nearly seven years. She will be replaced by Bimal Patel as general counsel in January. Pentland entered a separation agreement with PayPal, which includes a cash payment of over $2.5 million.

title: Deal with Tencent Music brings Chinese song catalogue to Apple Music
summary: Tencent Music Entertainment Group has struck a deal to bring music from Chinese labels and artists, managed through its TME Music Cloud, to Apple Music globally. This collaboration aims to promote Chinese music culture and artists internationally, offering distribution, promotion, royalty settlements, and data insights. The move highlights Tencent Music's expanding international partnerships despite facing domestic regulatory pressure regarding its market dominance.

title: Qualcomm forecasts post-Apple sales growth, shares hit all-time high
summary: Qualcomm Inc. expects its chip sales to Apple Inc. to significantly decrease in the coming years as Apple develops its own modem chips. However, Qualcomm anticipates robust growth in other sectors like autonomous cars, the internet of things, and virtual reality headsets, which led to its shares hitting a record high. The company projects substantial revenue increases from automotive and IoT divisions, offsetting the expected decline from Apple.

title: Target Continues to Grow; Updates Holiday Guidance
summary: Target reported strong third-quarter earnings, exceeding expectations with robust growth across all five core categories, leading the company to raise its fourth-quarter holiday guidance. Despite these positive results and increased inventory for the holiday season, Target's shares fell due to investor concerns about ongoing supply chain issues and inflationary pressures. CEO Brian Cornell acknowledged these challenges, emphasizing the company's agility in addressing them and its long-term investment in meeting customer demand.

title: Apple announces Self Service Repair
summary: Apple announced Self Service Repair, allowing customers access to genuine Apple parts, tools, and manuals for self-repairs. Initially available for iPhone 12 and iPhone 13 lineups in the US in early 2022, with plans to expand to Mac computers and other countries. This program provides an additional option for customers comfortable with DIY repairs, alongside Apple's existing network of authorized service providers.

title: Exclusive first look inside Corning's new pharmaceutical vial factory
summary: CNBC got an exclusive look inside Corning's new pharmaceutical vial factory in Durham, North Carolina, which has the capacity to produce 500 million glass vials annually. This facility, built with partial funding from Operation Warp Speed, uses Gorilla Glass technology for enhanced durability and storage capabilities, crucial for Covid-19 vaccines. Corning CEO Wendell Weeks sees billions of dollars in opportunity for the company's pharmaceutical business.

title: Apple is one step closer to developing fully automated vehicles
summary: Apple is accelerating the development of its electric car project, codenamed "Project Titan," with a renewed focus on achieving full self-driving capabilities that require no human intervention. Kevin Lynch, the new leader of the car team, is pushing for a 2025 launch for a fully autonomous vehicle, equipped with an advanced internally designed chip and a hands-off driving interior. This initiative marks a significant shift in strategy for Apple's long-running and often turbulent car project, aiming to tackle a major technical challenge within the auto industry.

title: Apple’s First GaN Charger
summary: Apple has introduced its first gallium nitride (GaN) charger, a 140W model for the new 16-inch MacBook Pro, marking a significant shift in power transistor technology for the company. TechInsights research confirms that GaN Systems secured the design win for this charger, highlighting the growing adoption of GaN in power electronics. This move by Apple, while not the first in the market for GaN, is expected to accelerate its integration into various applications, especially given the charger's use of the new USB Power Delivery 3.1 standard.

title: Live Nation, Apple, Travis Scott Sued for $2 Billion Over Astroworld Disaster
summary: Live Nation, Apple Inc., and Travis Scott are being sued for $2 billion by 282 concertgoers who claim they were injured during the Astroworld festival. The lawsuit, filed in Houston, alleges that the crowd of 50,000 people was "incited into a frenzy," leading to 10 deaths and hundreds of injuries. The complaint is an amendment to an earlier lawsuit and is the largest one filed to date regarding the tragic event.

title: How the company that makes glass for your iPhone produces vials for Covid vaccines
summary: Corning, known for Gorilla Glass and cookware, has rapidly expanded its pharmaceuticals business. The company opened a new factory in Durham, North Carolina, capable of producing 500 million glass vials annually for vaccines and drugs, supported by $204 million in federal funding from Operation Warp Speed. Corning also unveiled Velocity Vials, engineered to speed up manufacturing, which are already being utilized by pharmaceutical manufacturer Catalent.

title: Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley Will Redesign AmEx Platinum Cards, and Other News
summary: Artists Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley are set to redesign the American Express U.S. Platinum Card, with the new designs launching next month during Art Basel Miami Beach. American Express also announced a $1 million donation to the Studio Museum in Harlem, supporting artists of African descent and aligning with its DEI initiatives. Other news includes Burberry's "Imagined Landscapes" pop-up in South Korea, the Preston Bus Station receiving the World Monuments Fund/Knoll Modernism Prize, and the renaming of Staples Center to Crypto.com Arena.

title: This company may overtake LG as OLED supplier for Apple iPhones
summary: Chinese display manufacturer BOE is reportedly set to overtake LG as an OLED panel supplier for Apple iPhones. BOE is increasing its production, aiming to supply 50 million OLED units by 2022, potentially surpassing LG's projected 70 million units in 2023. Samsung remains Apple's largest OLED supplier, expected to provide 150 million units by 2022.

title: Apple posts internal memo affirming employees' right to discuss pay
summary: Apple has issued an internal memo affirming employees' right to discuss their wages, hours, and working conditions, a significant shift for the company following months of employee advocacy. This move comes after workers pushed for greater transparency regarding pay equity and faced restrictions on discussing these topics internally. The memo acknowledges rights protected under the National Labor Relations Act and is seen as a victory for Apple organizers who have filed unfair labor practice charges against the company.

title: Amazon and Apple handed $225 million in Italian antitrust fines
summary: Italy's antitrust authority has fined Amazon and Apple a total of more than 200 million euros ($225 million) for alleged anti-competitive cooperation. The fines stem from a 2018 agreement that restricted the sale of Apple and Beats products on Amazon.it to only selected resellers, violating EU rules and impacting price competition. Both companies deny wrongdoing and plan to appeal the decision.

title: Apple sues company known for hacking iPhones on behalf of governments
summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against NSO Group, an Israeli firm that develops software to hack iPhones, alleging that the company weaponizes powerful state-sponsored spyware against innocent users. Apple seeks a permanent injunction to prevent NSO Group from using its software, services, or devices and is seeking damages exceeding $75,000. Additionally, Apple will donate $10 million and any lawsuit damages to organizations combating digital surveillance, while also pledging to inform iPhone users targeted by Pegasus malware.

title: Apple sues Israeli firm NSO Group over spyware
summary: Apple Inc. has filed a lawsuit against Israeli firm NSO Group, accusing it of using its Pegasus spyware to hack Apple devices and enable human rights abuses. This legal action follows previous revelations about Pegasus being used against journalists and activists, and comes after WhatsApp's successful lawsuit against NSO and US sanctions against the company. Apple is seeking financial compensation and a permanent ban on NSO from using its servers and devices, pledging to contribute damages from the lawsuit to groups fighting cyber-surveillance.

title: 20-Year Returns: Monster Eats Apple for Breakfast
summary: Over the past 20 years, Monster Beverage Corporation's stock has outperformed major tech companies like Apple and Amazon, increasing by over 100,000 percent. The company, an early entrant in the energy drink market, has grown significantly, recording $1.4 billion in profit on $4.6 billion in sales in 2020. Monster Beverage is now a dominant player in the energy drink market and has recently been in merger talks with Constellation Brands.

title: Apple files lawsuit against NSO Group, saying U.S. citizens were targets
summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against Israeli cyber firm NSO Group and its parent company OSY Technologies, alleging surveillance and targeting of U.S. Apple users with its Pegasus spyware. The iPhone maker is seeking to ban NSO Group from using any Apple software, services, or devices and will donate $10 million, plus any damages, to cybersurveillance research groups. NSO maintains that its tools are sold only to governments and law enforcement and have saved "thousands of lives."

title: Four Ways to Reduce Security Risks Using Software Updates and Deployment Resources
summary: This article outlines four key strategies for organizations to enhance their cybersecurity posture by effectively managing software updates and deployments. It emphasizes the importance of subscribing to security notifications, promptly installing updates, utilizing enterprise deployment tools, and replacing unsupported software. The guidance aligns with CISA's directive on reducing known exploited vulnerabilities and highlights Adobe's resources and support for these initiatives.

title: Smart tattoos and the future of wearable devices - Hardware Pioneers
summary: The wearable technology market is experiencing significant growth, projected to reach $57,653 million globally by 2022. The article discusses various types of modern wearables, including smartwatches, smart jewelry, and smart clothing, highlighting their capabilities in health tracking and lifestyle improvement. Looking ahead, the article points to the emergence of "smart tattoos" that will integrate flexible electronic sensors into the skin to monitor vital signs and bodily functions.

title: Apple warns Thai activists "state-sponsored attackers" may have targeted iPhones
summary: Apple Inc. issued alert messages to at least six Thai activists and researchers, warning them that their iPhones may have been targeted by "state-sponsored attackers." This comes after Apple filed a lawsuit against Israeli cyber firm NSO Group for alleged surveillance using its Pegasus spyware. The Thai individuals targeted are perceived as critical of the current Thai government.

title: Best Buy heist: Burglars cut hole in store roof, steal merchandise ahead of Black Friday
summary: In Spanish Fort, Alabama, burglars cut a hole in the roof of a Best Buy store and stole a significant amount of computer and electronics equipment, primarily Apple products, just days before Black Friday. Police described the incident as a professional operation, noting the thieves spent several hours inside the store and left a ladder and tools behind. Best Buy's CEO recently acknowledged an increase in theft affecting employees and company security.

title: FreeStyle Libre 2 Connected to Your Phone
summary: Abbott announced that the FDA has cleared its FreeStyle Libre 2 apps for iPhone and Android users, allowing compatible smartphones to connect to FreeStyle Libre 2 sensors. This update addresses the growing demand for digital and connected diabetes care, enabling users to discreetly monitor their glucose with a simple scan. The FreeStyle Libre system, which now includes the ability to connect to iOS and Android devices, continues to evolve with advanced features and accuracy, serving millions of users worldwide.

title: 'Spotify Wrapped' Recaps Your Listening History for 2021
summary: Spotify has launched its annual "Spotify Wrapped" feature for 2021, allowing users to review their top artists, genres, songs, and podcasts. This year's Wrapped includes new interactive elements like "2021: The Movie," "Your Audio Aura," and "Playing Cards," and can now be shared on TikTok. While Apple Music offers a "Replay 2021" playlist, it lacks the in-depth personalized data and social sharing features that make Spotify Wrapped popular.

title: Facebook parent grabs another 1M sf in Bay Area
summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) has leased an additional 1 million square feet of office space in the Bay Area, specifically in Sunnyvale and Burlingame, to reduce employee commute times and support its net-zero carbon emission goal. The lease of four buildings in Sunnyvale, totaling over 700,000 square feet, is considered the largest private-sector office lease in the U.S. this year. This expansion further solidifies Sunnyvale's position as a key office market in Silicon Valley.

title: Apple loses a top vehicle engineer to flying-taxi startup Archer Aviation
summary: Michael Schwekutsch, a top vehicle engineer previously with Apple's "Project Titan" electric vehicle initiative, has joined Archer Aviation, a startup focused on building flying taxis. His departure comes as Apple tries to accelerate its self-driving car project, sparking discussions among analysts about Apple Car's potential impact on the broader EV market, particularly on Tesla.

title: What Stocks Are in QQQ? Rebound Likely, Good Long-Term Pick
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq 100, is a tech-heavy fund that experienced a nosedive following the omicron variant news and inflation concerns in December 2021. Despite this slump, the article suggests that QQQ is likely to recover due to its diverse holdings and past performance, making it a potentially smart long-term investment. The article details the top 20 holdings and the industry breakdown of the ETF.

title: Apple warns suppliers of weak demand for iPhone 13 lineup - Bloomberg
summary: Apple Inc. has reportedly informed its parts suppliers that demand for the iPhone 13 lineup has slowed, indicating a decrease in consumer interest for the newest models. This comes after Apple had already cut production targets due to a global chip shortage, and now even those revised numbers appear unlikely to be met. The news led to a drop in Apple's shares and those of its suppliers, as consumers shift spending from new electronics to experiences like travel and outdoor activities post-pandemic.

title: U.S. sues to block Nvidia’s $40-billion chip takeover of Arm
summary: U.S. antitrust officials have sued to block Nvidia's proposed $40-billion acquisition of Arm Ltd., citing concerns that the deal would stifle innovation and undermine competition in the semiconductor industry. The Federal Trade Commission argues that the merger would grant Nvidia excessive control over chip designs widely used across various technologies. The acquisition, initially valued at $40 billion in September 2020, has faced regulatory hurdles globally due to Arm's critical role as a technology licensor to hundreds of companies including Nvidia's rivals.

title: Target's gift card sale returns this weekend with 10% off discount. Here's how to save.
summary: Target is bringing back its annual gift card sale this weekend, offering a 10% discount on purchases up to $500 for Target store gift cards. To access the discount, customers must be members of the free Target Circle loyalty program. The gift cards can be used for personal shopping or as gifts and are redeemable in-store or online.

title: U.S. FTC sues to block Nvidia deal to buy Arm
summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has sued to block Nvidia Corp's more than $80 billion acquisition of British chip technology provider Arm, citing concerns that the deal would give Nvidia undue control over crucial computing technology and designs. This move complicates an already challenging regulatory path for the acquisition, which has faced opposition from the chip industry and scrutiny from British and European Union regulators. The FTC argues the merger would harm competition, reduce innovation, and lead to higher prices by giving Nvidia the ability and incentive to undermine competitors who rely on Arm's technology.

title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
summary: An article discussing the importance of balancing tech investments with high-quality stocks. It highlights Mark Mahaney's "dislocated high-quality" (DHQ) investment tenet and introduces the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as a suitable option for investors seeking quality and reduced volatility.

title: Can Apple Take Down the World’s Most Notorious Spyware Company?
summary: Apple Inc. has filed a lawsuit against NSO Group Technologies and Q Cyber Technologies, the creators of the Pegasus spyware, which has been used to hack iPhones of journalists, human rights defenders, and government officials worldwide. The article details the widespread use and harmful impact of Pegasus, the background of previous legal actions against NSO Group, and analyzes Apple's legal strategy, highlighting its aggressive stance in protecting user security. The outcome of the lawsuit could significantly impact the future of malicious spyware and state-sponsored hacking.

title: Netflix, Disney Sue Streaming Sites for Copyright Infringement
summary: Netflix, Disney, and several other major entertainment companies have filed a lawsuit against two streaming sites, AllAccessTV (AATV) and Quality Restreams, alleging copyright infringement. The companies claim that these sites are illegally selling access to their copyrighted movies and television programs without permission. The lawsuit was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California and includes plaintiffs such as Universal Studios, Paramount Pictures, Amazon, Apple, and Warner Bros.

title: Samsung begins One UI 4 rollout to the Galaxy Note 10 and Galaxy Note 10 Plus with first beta build
summary: Samsung has initiated the One UI 4 beta rollout to the Galaxy Note 10 and Galaxy Note 10 Plus, making the new OS available in South Korea for unlocked devices and those from KT Corporation. While stable builds are anticipated in January 2022 for these models, following the Galaxy S21 series' update in November, the beta launch for the Note 10 series suggests a wider release will follow. This update brings Android 12 to the former flagship devices, with expectations for stable releases next month.

title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
summary: A market expert suggests investors prioritize high-quality stocks over solely focusing on big tech names. Mark Mahaney of Evercore ISI advocates for investing in "dislocated high-quality" companies with large total addressable markets and strong innovation, rather than just trading tech stocks. The article highlights the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as an example of an ETF that focuses on quality while managing volatility.

title: Nasdaq dives over 2% as tech stocks slide at end of volatile week
summary: Wall Street's major indexes, particularly the Nasdaq, experienced a significant decline as tech stocks slid at the end of a volatile week. This downturn was influenced by mixed jobs data, uncertainty surrounding the Omicron coronavirus variant, and the Federal Reserve's policy tightening signals. Major tech companies such as Apple, Meta Platforms, Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla all saw their shares fall, contributing to the broader market dip.

title: Nvidia metaverse future bright even as Arm may slip from grasp
summary: Nvidia's acquisition of British chip technology provider Arm faces significant hurdles, with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission suing to block the deal and analysts doubting its completion. Despite this potential setback, Nvidia's future prospects remain strong, driven by its focus on artificial intelligence (AI) and the metaverse, areas where it is a leading player. The company's data center business is thriving as internet companies invest heavily in augmented and virtual reality technologies.

title: The metaverse needs aggressive regulation
summary: The author, a pioneer in AR/VR technology, argues for aggressive regulation of the metaverse to prevent large corporations from extensive monitoring and manipulation of users. He emphasizes the need to restrict data collection, regulate real-time marketing tactics, and ensure transparency regarding AI-controlled agents and targeted placements. The article warns that without regulation, the metaverse risks becoming an environment where powerful entities exploit user data and emotions for commercial gain.

title: Market history says omicron volatility isn't a reason for investors to sell
summary: Market history suggests that volatility caused by the omicron variant should not prompt investors to sell, according to Keith Lerner of Truist. He notes that 18 out of 19 major VIX spikes since 1990 were followed by S&P 500 gains a year later, averaging 20%. While short-term volatility is expected, the long-term trend, supported by an expanding economy and adapting consumers and corporations, remains positive.

title: Apple iPhone 13 Rebates Fail to Deliver for Some Buyers
summary: Some customers are experiencing issues with wireless carriers denying rebates offered to promote new Apple Inc. iPhones, iPads, and Watches. This problem comes years after wireless carriers eliminated deep subsidies for phones, forcing buyers to contend with the full cost of devices. The article also briefly mentions the impact of the omicron variant on Apple and another executive departure.

title: Stellantis to Rely on Partners in $23 Billion Software Push
summary: Stellantis announced a strategy to generate approximately $23 billion in additional revenue by 2030 through software-driven features in its vehicles. The automaker plans to leverage partnerships with companies like Foxconn, Waymo, and BMW to develop three new technology platforms by 2024. This initiative aims to significantly increase the number of vehicles capable of generating software revenue to 34 million.

title: Shachar Scott Joins Meta Reality Labs as Director of Brand & International Marketing
summary: Shachar Scott has been appointed as the Director of Brand and International Marketing at Meta Reality Labs, joining on December 6th, 2021. In this role, she will oversee global marketing, creative, media, and production, reporting to Brian Irving. Scott brings extensive experience from previous positions at Bumble, Snapchat, and Apple, where she focused on building and scaling global brands.

title: Apple's alleged restriction on workers' Slack use sparks labor complaint
summary: A former Apple employee has filed a complaint with a U.S. labor regulator, alleging that Apple has restricted employees' use of the workplace messaging app Slack. The complaint claims these restrictions hinder workers' ability to engage in protected speech and organizing. This action comes amidst increasing employee activism at Apple regarding issues like pay equity and remote work, with the former employee claiming she was fired after leading such efforts.

title: How much control should Apple have over your iPhone and the App Store?
summary: Apple is facing increasing scrutiny over its tight control of the App Store, which critics call a monopoly due to its exclusive app distribution, mandatory commissions, and content restrictions. While Apple argues these measures ensure user security and privacy, lawmakers and regulators worldwide are pushing for changes to increase competition, potentially leading to more app choices and lower prices for consumers, but also raising concerns about security. Despite some minor concessions, Apple largely maintains its stance, suggesting significant shifts may only occur if forced by new legislation or legal challenges.

title: Apple Nears $3 Trillion in Market Value. Here’s Some Context
summary: Apple Inc. is close to reaching a $3 trillion market value, needing just a 6% increase in its stock price to hit this milestone. This achievement would make it the first company to reach such a valuation, less than four years after first surpassing $1 trillion. The company's size now rivals the entire German equity market or the U.K. economy.

title: B2C E-commerce Market May Set a New Epic Growth Story |
summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics titled "B2C E-commerce Market Insights, to 2026" forecasts significant growth for the B2C e-commerce market, driven by increasing disposable income, global per capita income, and expanding internet penetration. Key players in this evolving market include Alibaba, Amazon, eBay, and Apple. The report details market trends, opportunities, drivers, and challenges, segmenting the market by type, application, brand, and product across various regions.

title: Sonos now supports Dolby Atmos and 24-bit hi-res audio on Amazon Music
summary: Sonos has expanded its audio support to include Amazon Music Unlimited's hi-res and Dolby Atmos music. This allows Amazon Music subscribers to stream "Ultra HD" (24-bit) tracks and Dolby Atmos content on compatible Sonos devices. While Amazon offers up to 24-bit/192kHz, Sonos devices will stream up to 24-bit/48kHz, with broader compatibility for hi-res than for Dolby Atmos which is limited to the Beam Gen 2 and Arc.

title: Peterbilt Techs to Get Augmented Reality Tool
summary: Peterbilt Motors Co. plans to deploy 200 of its ARTech augmented reality tools to dealer locations by March 31, 2022, to enhance customer uptime. This tool transforms 2D technical information into 3D images, allowing technicians to quickly diagnose and repair trucks using an Apple iPad, leading to reported improvements of 15-20% in service repair times. The technology provides "X-ray vision" to improve diagnostic and repair times for Peterbilt trucks.

title: Ford Motor Co. beefs up tech strength with director known for global dominance
summary: Ford Motor Co. has elected John May, CEO of Deere & Co., to its board of directors, a move analysts say underscores Ford's commitment to technology and dominance in electric and autonomous vehicles. May's experience with Deere's technologically advanced agricultural equipment and successful resolution of a recent UAW strike are seen as valuable assets for Ford's own transformation and supply chain management. The appointment expands Ford's board to 15 members, though some observers noted the company's lack of progress in increasing female board representation compared to competitors like GM.

title: Apple Engineers Jumping to eVTOL
summary: Several engineers are leaving Apple's self-driving car project to join the electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) industry, particularly companies like Archer Aviation and Joby Aviation. This shift is driven by the growth potential and innovation opportunities within the eVTOL sector, which is backed by significant investor capital. These engineers are bringing critical expertise in propulsion, battery systems, and radar to help eVTOL companies develop and certify air taxi services.

title: Sandisk Professional G-Drive SSD review - Fast enough, secure, and durable
summary: The SanDisk Professional G-Drive SSD offers portable, fast, and durable external storage for Mac users. It features USB 3.2 Type-C connectivity, considerable physical protection with IP67 rating and drop resistance, and 256-bit AES-XTS hardware encryption. While not the fastest or cheapest option, its balance of speed, ruggedness, and Mac-centric formatting makes it a professional choice for expanding MacBook storage.

title: 4 Charts on Apple's $3 Trillion Market Cap
summary: Apple Inc. has become the first company to achieve a $3 trillion market capitalization, briefly hitting $182.88 per share. This article highlights the immense scale of Apple's market cap through various comparisons, such as its value exceeding the total market cap of all public companies in Canada. It also discusses Apple's dominance within the U.S. market, making up nearly 22% of all U.S.-domiciled technology companies' total market capitalization.

title: An industry first: Hyatt introduces room keys in Apple Wallet
summary: Hyatt Hotels Corporation has launched room keys in Apple Wallet, allowing World of Hyatt guests at select locations to use their iPhone or Apple Watch to unlock guest rooms and common areas. This digital innovation aims to provide greater control and flexibility, with features like Express Mode and Power Reserve, making access near-instantaneous and secure. The technology is currently available at six hotels in the US, with plans for wider adoption.

title: Polish regulator to investigate Apple's privacy policy
summary: Poland's antimonopoly watchdog, UOKiK, has launched an investigation into Apple's new privacy rules for iOS devices, suspecting they might violate competition law. The regulator is examining whether Apple's policy changes, which limit third-party apps from collecting personal data for personalized ads, are a move to eliminate competitors and boost Apple's own advertising services. This investigation follows similar warnings from EU tech chief Margrethe Vestager regarding Apple's App Store practices.

title: Samsung to produce STMicro's MCUs for use in iPhones
summary: Samsung Electronics has secured a foundry order from STMicroelectronics to produce 16-nanometer microcontroller units (MCUs) for Apple's next-generation iPhones. This marks the first time STMicroelectronics has outsourced MCU production for key clients, highlighting Samsung's growing presence in the foundry market. The deal is seen as a move by Samsung to challenge TSMC's dominance in outsourced MCU production.

title: Is Saudi Aramco Stock a Good Buy? • (TADAWUL: 2222)
summary: This article analyzes whether Saudi Aramco stock is a good buy, considering its status as one of the world's largest companies, financial performance, and future outlook. It provides guidance on how to gain exposure to the stock through ETFs, discusses its stock history and value, and presents both bull and bear cases from analysts. The article concludes with a "hold" recommendation due to uncertainties in the global economy and the stock's relatively muted performance despite strong oil prices.

title: Go With GINN as High-Margin Companies Remain Trendy
summary: Investors are expected to favor quality, high-margin companies in 2022, a trend supported by the Goldman Sachs Innovate Equity ETF (GINN). GINN tracks companies with robust profitability, strong earnings growth, and effective margin management, aligning with Goldman Sachs' recommendation for clients. Several of GINN's holdings, including Alphabet, Meta Platforms, Apple, Marvell Technology, Mastercard, PayPal, Autodesk, and Etsy, are featured on Goldman's list of high-margin companies.

title: Scotiabank and Cineplex Transform Loyalty Landscape in Canada with the Launch of Scene+
summary: Scotiabank and Cineplex have launched Scene+, an enhanced loyalty program designed to be one of Canada's largest and most flexible, offering more ways to earn and redeem points. This new program integrates SCENE and Scotia Rewards, introducing new partners like Rakuten and Scene+ Travel powered by Expedia Group, allowing members to earn and redeem points for travel, shopping, and banking, in addition to existing entertainment and dining options. Existing SCENE members and eligible Scotiabank cardholders are automatically enrolled, with their accumulated points transferred at a 1:1 value.

title: What Zoom’s $85m class action lawsuit means for data privacy
summary: Zoom has agreed to pay an $85 million settlement in a class action lawsuit alleging it shared user data without consent and falsely claimed end-to-end encryption. This case highlights a broader data privacy movement forcing companies to increase transparency and adjust to stricter regulations. Experts believe the lawsuit serves as a cautionary tale for the digital ecosystem and will prompt other companies to re-evaluate their data protection policies.

title: Live Nation sued by Goldenvoice over alleged infringement of Coachella trademark
summary: Coachella promoter Goldenvoice is suing Live Nation for alleged "contributory trademark infringement" of the Coachella name. The lawsuit targets an event called Coachella Day One 22, organized by the Twenty-Nine Palms Band of Mission Indians, whose ticketing is handled by Live Nation-owned Ticketmaster. Goldenvoice claims the event is intentionally trading on the Coachella festival's goodwill and has copied its advertising and design elements, and is seeking damages and profits from the alleged infringement.

title: The Invesco QQQ Stock Price Is Up 23.7% Since January. Could There Be More Gains?
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ) has seen its stock price rise 23.7% since January, driven by major tech companies like Apple and Google, despite recent pullbacks. Investors are closely watching the Federal Reserve's monetary policy meeting for potential hawkish policies that could further impact the Nasdaq index and the QQQ ETF. While the ETF is currently in a downtrend, its present stock price may be attractive for a future rally, though a clear upward trend based on EMA crossings is yet to materialize.

title: Intel to invest $7 billion in new plant in Malaysia, creating 9,000 jobs
summary: Intel Chief Executive Pat Gelsinger announced a $7 billion investment to build a new chip-packaging and testing factory in Malaysia, creating over 4,000 Intel jobs and more than 5,000 construction jobs. This expansion aims to address the global semiconductor shortage, which Gelsinger expects to persist into 2023. The new advanced packaging facility is projected to begin production in 2024.

title: Apple Builds New Team in Southern California to Bring More Wireless Chips In-House
summary: Apple is expanding its in-house chip development by hiring engineers for a new office in Irvine, Southern California. This team will focus on creating wireless chips, potentially replacing components currently supplied by companies like Broadcom and Skyworks Solutions. The move is part of Apple's broader strategy to reduce reliance on third-party suppliers and produce more of its own components, following similar efforts with modems and other chip technologies.

title: Southern Company Foundation Issues $10M in Grants to Foster HBCU Talent Through Collaboration Between INROADS, UNCF and Thurgood Marshall College Fund
summary: The Southern Company Foundation has announced $10 million in grants to support a new collaboration between INROADS, UNCF, and the Thurgood Marshall College Fund. This initiative aims to foster HBCU talent by providing scholarships, endowments, and paid internships, focusing on STEM, urban planning, and architecture programs. The grants are part of Southern Company's larger $50 million multi-year commitment to enhance career readiness for students at select HBCUs.

title: Sonos One review: still one of the best smart speakers, and an ideal multi-room starter
summary: The Sonos One remains a top-tier smart speaker, offering excellent sound quality and versatile smart assistant integration with both Alexa and Google Assistant years after its debut. Despite minor drawbacks like less powerful microphones and no Bluetooth, its multi-room capabilities and wide streaming support make it an ideal choice for a platform-neutral audio experience. It stands out in its price range as a stellar starting point for a multi-room speaker system.

title: |Product Liability & Mass Torts Digest
summary: |A Texas District Court has allowed a class-action lawsuit against Apple to proceed after a plaintiff, Robert Franklin, claimed his iPhone 6 exploded and injured him. The court ruled that Franklin had standing to represent a class of iPhone 6 owners who experienced similar issues, despite Apple's arguments against "substantially similar" products and lack of a safer alternative design. While the design defect claim was dismissed with leave to amend, claims for manufacturing defect, market defect, negligence, and certain Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act claims were allowed to go forward.

title: Cerner could be acquired by Oracle, says WSJ
summary: Oracle is reportedly in talks to acquire health IT giant Cerner Corp. for an estimated $30 billion, which would be Oracle's largest acquisition to date. This move would significantly bolster Oracle's presence in healthcare technology, particularly in cloud computing and AI, following a trend of major tech companies investing heavily in healthcare-focused firms. The potential acquisition comes amidst a challenging year for Cerner, despite its CEO expressing optimism about long-term growth.

title: Find Out Where Every ‘Spider-Man’ Movie is Streaming – Either for Free or Rental
summary: This article lists where to stream every 'Spider-Man' movie, including those starring Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland. It details which subscription services offer the films for free and where they can be rented, noting that "Spider-Man: No Way Home" was only in theaters at the time of publication. The piece also explains why Spider-Man movies are not all on Disney+ with the rest of the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

title: About IBS Bulgaria
summary: IBS Bulgaria is helping regional banks modernize their critical systems to comply with the PSD2 directive. They developed a new open banking platform powered by IBM API Connect and IBM Cloud Pak for Integration technology, resulting in reduced integration times and significant cost reductions for their clients. This solution allows banks to quickly adapt to new regulations and services, with approximately 90% of open banking in Bulgaria now relying on IBM integration technology.

title: Apple Should Make a Giant iPad as Its Smart Home Portal
summary: After testing Amazon's new Echo Show 15, the author suggests Apple Inc. (AAPL) should develop a larger iPad to serve as a smart home hub. This move would enhance Apple's presence in the smart home market and diversify the utility of its iPad line. The article also touches on other Apple-related news, including COVID-19 disruptions and internal company changes.

title: Amazon silenced criticism of Xi to do business in China: Report
summary: A Reuters report alleges that Amazon.com Inc. quieted criticism of President Xi Jinping's book on its Chinese platform to maintain business in China. This involved scrubbing negative ratings and reviews for "The Governance of China" following requests from Beijing. This situation mirrors cases of other US companies like Apple and contrasts with others like Yahoo! and LinkedIn that have exited the Chinese market due to a challenging business environment.

title: 5 things to know about Oracle after $28.3 billion acquisition of Kansas City’s Cerner
summary: Oracle has announced a $28.3 billion acquisition of Cerner Corp., Kansas City's largest private employer, marking one of the year's biggest deals. This move signifies Oracle's growing push into the healthcare industry, following similar expansions by other tech giants. The acquisition brings together Oracle, an $280 billion company with 133,000 employees globally, and Cerner, valued at $23 billion with 28,000 employees worldwide, notably 13,000 in Kansas City.

title: Avis Employee Used Apple Pay to Scam Customers
summary: An Avis Budget employee was arrested for scamming the company out of over $5,000 by accepting customer payments via Apple Pay for car rentals and keeping the money. Alicia Leon would then modify rental agreements to show extensions, causing Avis Budget to lose $7,479 in rental fees. She faces charges of larceny and fraud.

title: 15 Best Windows 10 Themes
summary: This article lists the 15 best Windows 10 themes, offering various options to personalize a PC's desktop. It provides detailed instructions on how to install themes through Microsoft Store or third-party sources and highlights diverse themes ranging from 3D designs and anime to classic operating system looks and pop culture references like Star Wars and NBA. The article emphasizes that changing themes is a simple yet effective way to customize one's device and reflect personal interests.

title: AT&T sells Xandr ad business to Microsoft for $1 billion
summary: AT&T has sold its Xandr digital advertising business to Microsoft for an estimated $1 billion. This divestiture is part of AT&T's broader strategy to scale back its media operations, following moves with DirecTV and WarnerMedia. The sale allows Microsoft to integrate Xandr's platform into its properties, enhancing its online advertising capabilities.

title: Apple’s long-term credit rating upgraded to AAA by Moody’s
summary: Moody’s Investors Service upgraded Apple Inc.’s long-term credit rating to Aaa, placing it in an exclusive group with Microsoft and Johnson & Johnson as the only U.S. S&P 500 corporations with the highest possible credit score. This upgrade reflects Apple's exceptional liquidity, robust earnings, and strong business profile, as the company continues to focus on a net cash-neutral position and expand into new markets like augmented reality and cars. Despite potential chip-related supply shortages impacting revenue, investors remain optimistic about Apple's long-term prospects.
authors: Jack Pitcher and Mark Gurman and Bloomberg

title: Moody's gives Apple top 'AAA' credit rating on growth promise
summary: Ratings agency Moody's upgraded Apple Inc.'s long-term credit rating to 'AAA' with a stable outlook, citing its exceptional liquidity and robust earnings. This places Apple in an elite group of companies with the highest rating. While Apple's financial performance was strong in fiscal 2021, the company faces execution risks from short product cycles, shifting consumer preferences, and supply chain management.

title: Market Breadth Showcases Equal-Weight Benefits
summary: The article discusses the benefits of equal-weight strategies in investing, particularly highlighted by current market breadth where a few large-cap tech stocks are dominating the S&P 500. It suggests that while cap-weighted indices show strong performance due to these few stocks, equal-weight ETFs like RSP offer better diversification and can outperform when market leadership narrows, as demonstrated by its recent performance against the S&P 500.

title: Apple Must Answer Shareholder Questions on Forced Labor, SEC Says
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission denied Apple Inc.'s request to block a shareholder proposal seeking greater transparency on its efforts to prevent forced labor in its supply chain. This means Apple will face a vote on the proposal at its next annual shareholder meeting. The request asks for details on identified risk suppliers and actions taken against them.

title: Ansys and AGI continue collaboration with NORAD to track Santa's annual trip
summary: Analytical Graphics Inc. (AGI), a sister company to Ansys Inc., has renewed its collaboration with NORAD's Operations Center for the annual Santa Tracker experience. This ongoing partnership leverages technological expertise to support the military unit's popular holiday program. The announcement was made as part of NORAD's yearly tradition of tracking Santa's journey.

title: Visa’s CFO started as an engineer but ended up in finance—and he has great advice for anyone looking to make a change
summary: Vasant Prabhu, Visa's CFO and Vice Chair, transitioned from an engineering background to a successful career in finance, holding CFO roles at major public companies for over two decades. He advises job seekers to build skills, relationships, and reputation while remaining flexible and considering four key factors: passion for the industry, company talent reputation, trustworthy colleagues, and potential for impact. Prabhu's career trajectory, including significant roles at PepsiCo, McGraw-Hill, Safeway, and Starwood Hotels, highlights his adaptability and willingness to take on new challenges, ultimately leading him to Visa.

title: Apple iPad mini, Fitbit Sense to Sonos Beam: Best gadgets of 2021
summary: This article compiles a list of the best gadgets released in 2021, categorized by smartphones, PCs, audio devices, tablets, fitness bands and watches, and other notable innovations. The selections are based on hands-on experiences, highlighting products that stood out for their performance, innovation, and value during the second year of the pandemic. Key mentions include the iPhone 13 Pro Max, Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 3 and Flip 3, Apple iMac M1, Sonos Beam, Apple iPad mini, and Fitbit Sense, showcasing technology's role in adapting to new needs and pushing boundaries.

title: Apple’s Next Step in Services Could Be New Bundles, Deeper Integration
summary: Apple's future services strategy is expected to focus on deeper integration among its existing offerings and the introduction of new bundles, building on the success of its services division. This push includes the hiring of Facebook's AR/VR communications chief, signaling potential new product categories like headsets. The article also notes the impact of rising Covid cases on Apple's retail operations and its plans for CES.

title: Apple's App Store broke competition laws, Dutch watchdog says
summary: The Netherlands' competition regulator, ACM, has found that Apple Inc. broke competition laws with its App Store payment policies, specifically concerning dating apps. Apple is ordered to allow alternative payment systems for dating apps and faces a fine of up to 50 million euros if it fails to comply by January 15. Apple has stated it disagrees with the order and has filed an appeal.

title: Israeli companies face Trojan horse dilemma
summary: Israeli cyberattack companies like Cognyte and Quadream are grappling with strategic decisions following the controversy surrounding NSO Group and its Pegasus spyware. These companies are weighing whether to fill the potential void left by NSO, scale back or sell off their own "Trojan horse" activities due to increasing scrutiny and diplomatic pressure, particularly from the US. The article highlights Cognyte's deliberations regarding its Trojan horse product and its declining share price, while also noting Quadream's similar considerations amidst opportunities to capture NSO's former clientele.

title: Could Movano's Smart Ring Help People With Chronic Illnesses?
summary: Movano is introducing a smart ring at CES 2022 designed to monitor health data in a less intrusive way than other wearables. The ring focuses on analyzing how different health metrics relate to each other, aiming to help prevent or manage chronic illnesses. Movano plans to seek Class II designation for its device and eventually incorporate cuffless blood pressure measurement.

title: The definitive ranking of streaming services as we head into 2022
summary: This article ranks the top streaming services as of late 2021, evaluating their performance and strategies during the "streaming wars." HBO Max emerged as a strong contender due to its day-and-date movie releases and popular original series, while Amazon Prime Video expanded its focus with major event films and Netflix saw a resurgence with global hits like "Squid Game." Apple TV+ demonstrated growth with acclaimed originals, though Disney Plus and Hulu faced challenges related to content strategy and corporate interests, respectively.

title: Allstate’s Arity auto insurance predictions for 2022
summary: Allstate's Arity has released three major predictions for auto insurance in 2022 based on extensive driving data. These predictions include rising car insurance rates due to riskier driving behaviors post-pandemic, the increased use of driving scores for new policy pricing, and more equitable and transparent pricing for consumers through greater reliance on individual driving data. The article highlights that these trends will lead to competitive advantages for insurers who leverage telematics data at the point of quote.

title: Dolby Survey: GenZ Could Save Music Sound Quality!
summary: A Dolby-sponsored OnePoll survey suggests that GenZ could be the generation that prioritizes and drives demand for high-quality audio, contradicting previous trends where convenience often overshadowed sound fidelity. The survey indicates GenZ's strong preference for lossless sound quality, high-end audio devices, and even older music, potentially signaling a significant shift in the consumer audio market.

title: Shed Concentration Risk With Equal-Weight ETFs
summary: This article discusses the increasing concentration risk in cap-weighted indexes like the S&P 500, primarily due to the large market capitalization of a few tech giants like Apple and Microsoft.
