The plaintiffs seek to compel the defendants to enforce policies to prevent such criminal conduct and comply with child abuse laws.

title: Korean Webtoon Companies that are giving the Audience Addictive Content
summary: The Korean webtoon industry is experiencing significant global growth, driven by the success of K-culture, despite challenges like increased competition and a decline in artists' average income in 2023. Key players like Lezhin Comics, Naver's Webtoon and Line Manga, KakaoPage, Ridi Corp's Manta, Toomics, and Tappytoon are expanding internationally, leveraging diverse genres, free-reading models, and innovative technologies like AI and NFTs. These companies are not only captivating worldwide audiences but also transforming the global entertainment landscape through partnerships and strategic investments.

title: Meta is opening its first store as VR headsets inch closer to mainstream reality
summary: Meta Platforms is opening its first physical store to sell VR headsets like the Oculus Quest 2, despite its Reality Labs division facing significant losses. The company is betting heavily on the metaverse, viewing it as the solution to boost virtual reality adoption beyond the current low engagement among teens. Experts suggest that widespread VR adoption could be triggered by one or two "killer applications" and that Meta's strategy is to build the foundational platform for this new computing paradigm, even if profitability remains years away.

title: 3 Surefire Stocks That Can Turn $200,000 Into $1 Million by 2030
summary: This article identifies three stocks — Nio, Upstart Holdings, and Pinterest — that have the potential to turn a $200,000 investment into $1 million by 2030, despite recent market downturns. The author emphasizes that while the stock market has experienced corrections, these companies possess strong catalysts and innovative business models that position them for significant long-term growth. The piece details how each company, through unique offerings and market positioning, is poised for substantial returns within the next eight years.

title: Vietnam 'darling' of global electronics giants
summary: Vietnam has emerged as a leading hub for electronics manufacturing in Southeast Asia, attracting significant investments from global giants like Samsung, LG, Intel, and Apple suppliers such as Foxconn, Pegatron, Goertek, and Luxshare. The country's electronics exports have become a key earner, with major manufacturers expanding operations and establishing R&D centers, contributing to substantial economic growth and technological advancement.

title: 15 Top Cities for Machine Learning Engineer Salaries
summary: This article identifies the top 15 U.S. cities offering the highest salaries for Machine Learning Engineers, based on data from Blind. It highlights that major tech hubs like Silicon Valley and Seattle lead due to high demand from companies like Meta and Apple. The piece also notes the increasing importance and compensation for AI and machine learning skills across industries.

title: Digital Health Literacy: Why It’s Important and How to Improve It
summary: As healthcare increasingly relies on technology, digital health literacy is crucial for ensuring health equity. This article defines digital health literacy, explains the consequences of its absence, and outlines strategies for healthcare providers to assess and improve patients' ability to use digital health tools and evaluate online health information. It emphasizes the need for comprehensive plans to ensure all patients can benefit from modern patient engagement technologies.

title: Former Senior Vice President of Autonomous Driving at VW Group Alexander Hitzinger Joins Arbe Board of Directors
summary: Alexander Hitzinger, former Senior Vice President of Autonomous Driving at VW Group, has joined Arbe's Board of Directors. Hitzinger brings extensive experience from top automotive and new mobility firms, including Volkswagen and Apple, to Arbe. His addition is expected to help Arbe revolutionize the radar industry and advance safe autonomous driving solutions.

title: Tinder owner Match sues Google in 'last resort' to avoid app-store booting
summary: Match Group Inc., owner of dating apps like Tinder, has sued Alphabet Inc.'s Google, describing it as a "last resort" to prevent its apps from being removed from the Google Play store. Match is contesting Google's policy of demanding up to 30% of sales from apps and threatening to block downloads unless Match uses Google's payment system exclusively. The lawsuit accuses Google of violating antitrust laws and seeks to protect Match from potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue payments to Google.

title: Telesat, Globalstar, Intelsat headline upheavals in satellite industry
summary: The satellite communications industry is undergoing significant changes, with major providers like Telesat, Globalstar, and Intelsat experiencing corporate upheavals, new leadership, and project adjustments. These developments underscore the evolving role of satellites in global telecommunications, moving from a relatively minor component to a potentially prominent one due to innovations from companies like OneWeb and SpaceX. These shifts also highlight a broader trend where various companies, including large telecom providers, are

title: How Citizens Financial used an iPhone plan to create a nationwide digital bank
summary: Citizens Financial Group Inc. launched its Citizens Pay iPhone upgrade plan in 2015, an early iteration of the buy-now-pay-later model. This initiative allowed Citizens to leverage its brand as a credit provider in conjunction with Apple's iPhone, helping customers bypass long waits for new iPhone releases. The article explores how this strategy contributes to Citizens' nationwide digital banking efforts.

title: Online dating giant Match Group sues Google over app store
summary: Match Group, the company behind popular dating apps like Tinder and OkCupid, has sued Google, alleging an illegal monopoly in the Google Play Store and anticompetitive in-app payment policies. Match Group claims Google's 30% cut of in-app purchases and its mandate to use Google's payment technology are harmful, especially as Google threatened to remove Match apps from the Play Store if they don't comply with new rules. Google, in response, argues Match Group is trying to avoid paying for services and that the majority of apps on its platform do not pay fees.

title: Grand Investment Opportunity Awaits in the Metaverse
summary: Despite the current downturn in growth and technology stocks, the metaverse offers a significant long-term investment opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) is presented as a viable option for investors who wish to capitalize on this emerging concept without individual stock picking, holding familiar large-cap tech companies. Experts believe the metaverse has the potential to transform social media, gaming, shopping, entertainment, and education.

title: The First Truly Personal Computer
summary: M.G. Siegler reflects on the discontinuance of the iPod Touch, marking the end of the entire iPod line. He reminisces about his first Apple product being an iPod in 2004 and the device's significance in Apple's growth towards becoming a multi-trillion dollar company. Siegler expresses a nostalgic wish for a new, simple, music-focused iPod, while acknowledging Apple's current ecosystem of iPhone, Apple Watch, and AirPods serves a similar purpose.

title: Unity Software loses $5 billion in market cap after Apple's changes lead to 'self-inflicted wound'
summary: Unity Software's market cap dropped by $5 billion after the company's attempts to circumvent Apple's IDFA changes backfired, leading to a "self-inflicted wound." Wall Street punished Unity U stock, slashing price targets by 43% as their workaround negatively impacted ad accuracy. Unity executives' strategy concerning Apple's mobile operating system changes proved ineffective, resulting in a significant market downturn for the company.

title: Saudi Aramco Becomes World’s Most Valuable Stock as Apple Drops
summary: Saudi Aramco has surpassed Apple Inc. to become the world's most valuable company for the first time since 2020. This shift is attributed to surging oil prices benefiting the crude producer, while inflation and technology stock weakness have led to a decline in Apple's valuation. Aramco's market capitalization reached approximately $2.43 trillion, exceeding Apple's $2.37 trillion.

title: Apple defeats new trial bid after overturning $506 mln patent verdict
summary: A U.S. federal court has rejected the University of Wisconsin's patent-licensing arm, WARF, bid for a new trial against Apple Inc. This decision follows the Supreme Court's annulment of a previous $506 million patent infringement award against Apple. WARF had sought to reopen the case with a new theory of infringement, but the court ruled against it, issuing a final judgment for Apple.

title: Home Audio Equipment Market size to Increase by USD 14.39 Bn | Featuring Top Vendors Including Apple Inc., Bose Corp., Koninklijke Philips NV, and Kripa Electronics India Pvt. Ltd. | Technavio
summary: The global home audio equipment market is projected to grow by USD 14.39 billion between 2020 and 2025, exhibiting a CAGR of 10.76%. This growth is primarily driven by rising disposable incomes and technological innovations, especially in the smart speakers segment. Key vendors include Apple Inc., Bose Corp., Koninklijke Philips NV, and others.

title: Breaking Bad's Giancarlo Esposito Is The Voice Of Sonos' New Alexa-Style Assistant
summary: Sonos has announced a new voice control feature called Sonos Voice Control, launching June 1 in the US. This Alexa-style assistant will be voiced by Giancarlo Esposito, known for his roles in "Breaking Bad" and "The Mandalorian." The company emphasizes that user requests are processed locally and not sent to the cloud, ensuring privacy.

title: A customer-centric approach: the key to the innovation paradox
summary: Kavitha Mariappan, a woman leader in tech, emphasizes that diversity and resilience are crucial for innovation. She highlights the innovation paradox, explaining that while customer centricity is vital, innovators must go beyond direct customer input to truly innovate, as customers often think incrementally. The key is to involve customers throughout the product development journey, focusing on their behaviors, use cases, and desired outcomes, and challenging them to envision future possibilities, which has been central to Zscaler's success.

title: Domino’s grants ‘Stranger Things’ fans ‘mind-ordering’ powers through new app
summary: Domino's has launched a "mind-ordering" app in promotion of season four of Netflix's "Stranger Things," allowing users to order pizza using facial recognition and eye-tracking technology. The app lets fans role-play as characters from the show and explore a virtual Hawkins National Laboratory. While Domino's says no personally identifiable facial data is stored, the initiative aims to experiment with nascent tech while engaging "Stranger Things" fans and boosting sales amid recent declines and inflationary pressures.

title: Starbucks to cover employees’ travel expenses for abortions; gender surgeries
summary: Starbucks announced an expansion of its health care benefits to cover travel expenses for employees seeking abortions or gender-affirming procedures if these services are not available within 100 miles of their homes. This decision comes in the wake of a leaked draft U.S. Supreme Court opinion potentially overturning Roe v. Wade, with the company affirming its commitment to ensuring employee access to quality healthcare. Starbucks joins other major companies in offering this benefit, consistent with its history of advocating for liberal causes.

title: SK Telecom's latest virtual assistant is more than just a voice
summary: SK Telecom has launched "A.", a new virtual assistant app that differentiates itself from others like Siri and Bixby by featuring a customizable character. This app aims to be a personalized daily companion for users, offering content curation, schedule management, and conversational capabilities, while integrating various services and utilizing the GPT-3 language model.

title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Acquires Small Stake in Paramount Global
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested approximately $2.6 billion in Paramount Global during the first quarter of 2022, acquiring nearly 15% of the company's Class B shares. This move signals Buffett's confidence in Paramount Global's valuation and its efforts in the competitive streaming market with Paramount+, alongside its other media properties like CBS and MTV. Berkshire Hathaway also made significant investments in other companies like Chevron and Apple, while reducing stakes in Kroger and Verizon, and selling off Abbvie and Bristol Myers Squibb.

title: Cashing in on the pandemic: the five highest-paid US executives in 2021
summary: A Wall Street Journal analysis revealed that the highest-paid S&P 500 executives saw record compensation in 2021, with a median of $14.7 million, largely due to stock-based pay capitalizing on the pandemic recovery. Expedia CEO Peter Kern topped the list, earning $296 million primarily in shares and options, followed by David Zaslav of Discovery, William McDermott of ServiceNow, Tim Cook of Apple, and Jamie Dimon of JP Morgan, whose compensations were also heavily tied to stock performance and incentives. This surge in executive pay reflects a strong benefit from the post-pandemic stock market rebound.

title: Competition Intensifying in LTPO OLED Display Market
summary: Competition in the mobile OLED panel market, particularly in the LTPO OLED segment, is intensifying as LG Display and BOE rapidly increase production. While Samsung Display currently leads, BOE is expanding its capacity to supply Apple's iPhone 15, and Apple is also partnering with LG Display for LTPO panels and plans to use them in future iPads and MacBooks. This has led to other companies like Visionox and CSOT also entering mass production of LTPO OLEDs.

title: Virtual Carriers Beat AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon on Customer Satisfaction
summary: A survey by the American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI) found that virtual wireless carriers like Consumer Cellular and Straight Talk received higher customer satisfaction scores than major carriers such as AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon. This finding aligns with PCMag's own Readers' Choice Awards, suggesting that customers are happier with Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs) that lease network access from the larger providers. Despite the big three performing similarly in sub-ratings, MVNOs consistently outrank them in overall customer satisfaction.

title: SEIV ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:SEIV
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the SEI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Value Factor ETF (SEIV), focusing on its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It highlights SEIV as an actively managed fund targeting US large-cap value stocks using a quantitative-based selection process. The analysis covers AUM, expense ratio, holdings breakdown by sector and region, and dividend history, including a 24.49% increase in its last reported dividend.

title: HP Envy 34 All-in-One Desktop review: A big-screen AIO for power users and creators
summary: HP has updated its Envy All-in-One desktop, featuring a 34-inch 5K wide-screen display, making it a strong alternative to the discontinued 27-inch iMac for creative users and power users. This review highlights its impressive design, versatile features including accessible RAM/SSD slots, and strong performance, especially in graphics, though it notes some bloatware and a lack of display swivel. The Envy 34 offers various configurations, from an entry-level model to a high-end option with a Core i9 processor and RTX 3080 graphics.

title: Apple introduces new professional training to support growing IT workforce
summary: Apple has launched updated professional training and certifications for IT support and management, delivered through new online, self-paced courses. These certifications, including Apple Certified Support Professional and Apple Certified IT Professional, aim to address the growing demand for IT professionals skilled in supporting Apple products within enterprises. Apple is also partnering with community colleges, universities, and the Mac Admins Foundation to provide scholarships and broad access to these new training programs.

title: US stocks suffer biggest daily drop in almost two years
summary: US stocks experienced their largest daily decline in nearly two years, as inflation concerns and the prospect of stricter monetary policy weighed on markets. The S&P 500 dropped 4%, with consumer shares plummeting over 6%, led by Target's steep fall after a reduced profit forecast. Growing hawkish sentiment from the Federal Reserve, global supply chain issues, and geopolitical tensions continue to fuel investor anxiety.

title: Why S&P booted Tesla from its ESG index
summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices removed Tesla from its S&P 500 ESG Index due to a decline in its ESG rank compared to global peers. Despite Tesla's role in the transition to electric vehicles, the decision was influenced by claims of racial discrimination, poor working conditions at its factory, and its handling of a federal investigation into autopilot-related deaths. Elon Musk called ESG an "outrageous scam" in response to the decision.

title: Robert Downey Jr. Transforms Classic Cars Into Eco-Friendly Green Machines in New Series Coming to discovery+
summary: Robert Downey Jr. is set to star in a new discovery+ original series titled "Downey's Dream Cars" (wt), launching later in 2022. In the show, Downey and a team of experts will restore six classic cars from his personal collection, updating them with eco-friendly technologies while preserving their original style. This project combines his passion for climate change solutions with classic car restoration, challenging viewers to find modern inspiration in vintage automobiles.

title: How to Send Money: The 9 Best Ways to Transfer Money
summary: This article details nine of the best ways to send money, catering to various needs from instant peer-to-peer transfers to large international sums. It compares options like Zelle, Venmo, Cash App, wire transfers, and money orders, highlighting their fees, transfer limits, and transfer times. The guide aims to help users choose the most suitable method for their specific financial transactions.

title: With multi-device FIDO credentials, you can now go all-in on passwordless | CXO Revolutionaries
summary: Apple, Google, and Microsoft's expanded support for multi-device FIDO credentials is set to revolutionize passwordless logins, addressing previous limitations of FIDO authentication like device-locked cryptographic keys and complex re-enrollment processes. This development, coupled with the Federal Zero Trust Strategy requiring phishing-resistant authentication, positions FIDO to become a prevalent, high-assurance authentication method for both consumers and federal use cases. The update promises seamless and secure access to FIDO credentials across multiple devices, even after device replacement.

title: GameStop Corp. Cl A stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: GameStop Corp. Cl A (GME) stock rallied 8.43% on Thursday, closing at $99.19, despite a generally dismal trading session for the broader stock market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw declines. GameStop outperformed competitors like Apple, AT&T, and Best Buy, though its trading volume remained below its 50-day average. The stock was $245.47 short of its 52-week high.

title: AC Hotels by Marriott developer buys land in St. Johns Town Center
summary: OTO Development LLC, the developer behind the AC Hotels by Marriott project in St. Johns Town Center, has purchased 2.725 acres of land for $1.18 million. The four-story, 118-room hotel, costing an estimated $18 million, is scheduled to start construction in the first quarter of 2022 and be completed in the second quarter of 2023. This project is a joint venture between OTO and Simon Property Group, with OTO serving as the managing partner.

title: Semiconductor Competition: Opportunities for Taiwan
summary: The article discusses how Taiwan, particularly TSMC, dominates the global semiconductor industry despite intensifying geopolitical competition and efforts by countries like the US, EU, and China to achieve self-sufficiency in chip manufacturing. It highlights Taiwan's current technological edge and suggests that rather than threats, these global shifts present opportunities for Taiwanese firms to expand into new markets, enhance R&D, and diversify their economic reliance on manufacturing through strategic international partnerships.

title: International Business Machines Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) stock fell 0.91% to $128.48 on Friday, May 20, 2022, underperforming the broader stock market, which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rise slightly. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for IBM, leaving its stock $17.51 below its 52-week high. While IBM declined, some competitors showed mixed performance, with Apple Inc. (AAPL) rising and Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Alphabet Inc. Cl A (GOOGL) falling.

title: Read This Before You Buy Any Udemy Course
summary: This article provides essential tips for purchasing courses on Udemy, emphasizing avoiding impulse buys due to frequent discounts and supporting instructors by using their direct links. It also advises against coupon sites and recommends buying courses via a desktop browser in incognito mode for the best pricing.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Announces New Fire 7 Tablets
summary: Amazon has announced its new Fire 7 and Fire 7 Kids tablets, which are available for pre-order and will start shipping on June 29, 2022. These tablets serve as budget-friendly alternatives to the Apple iPad mini, with prices starting at $59.99 and $109.99 respectively. The new Fire 7 boasts significant upgrades in battery life, RAM, and responsiveness compared to its 2019 predecessor, while only increasing in price by $10.

title: Cleveland-Cliffs and TravelCenters of America return to Fortune 500
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs and TravelCenters of America have rejoined the Fortune 500 list. Walmart topped the overall list, followed by Amazon and Apple. The article also mentions other related business news.

title: Broadcom Inc in talks to acquire VMware: sources
summary: Broadcom Inc. is reportedly in discussions to acquire cloud-computing company VMware Inc., a deal that would significantly expand the chipmaker's software presence. This potential acquisition aligns with CEO Hock Tan's strategy of diversifying Broadcom through software companies. VMware, valued at around US$40 billion, would provide Broadcom with greater access to the data center market, a critical area for growth.

title: Apple's Steve Jobs: An Extraordinary Career
summary: This article chronicles the extraordinary career of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, highlighting how his vision propelled the PC revolution and Apple's success. It covers his early life, his initial departure from Apple, his ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, where he spearheaded groundbreaking products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. The piece concludes with his health struggles, resignation, and passing, emphasizing his lasting legacy as a visionary leader.

title: Where Does the Supply Chain Crisis Stand Now?
summary: The article discusses the ongoing global supply chain crisis, examining its causes, current impact across various sectors like semiconductors, automobiles, industrials, retail, and restaurants, and potential timelines for recovery. It highlights that while some improvements are being seen, new bottlenecks, such as the war in Ukraine and lockdowns in China, continue to pose challenges, pushing companies to adapt strategies like diversifying supply chains and securing long-term contracts.

title: Newport plant critical to easing microchip shortage, says boss
summary: A factory in Newport, Wales, run by IQE, is highlighted as critical to addressing the global semiconductor shortage. New chief executive Americo Lemos is seeking government and private funding to expand the facility, which produces essential components for various electronic devices, including iPhones. The Welsh government supports the expansion, emphasizing the need for a coherent UK approach to the semiconductor industry.

title: 5 lessons I learned from helping to launch Tesla’s Model S
summary: The article shares five business lessons learned by Sukemasa Kabayama, the former President of Tesla Japan, during the challenging five-week launch of the Model S in Japan in 2014. These lessons emphasize creativity, holistic customer experience, laser focus on outcomes, the principle of "less is more," and clarity in problem-solving. Kabayama also draws parallels to his current startup, Uplift Labs.

title: Deere Models Apple to Drive Revenue with Autonomy
summary: Deere & Co. is adopting a strategy similar to Apple's, combining its machinery with software and subscription models to boost revenue, focusing on autonomy and AI in agriculture. The company plans to sell autonomy as a separate, likely subscription-based service for its $500,000 autonomous 8R tractor, with experts suggesting this recurring revenue model could significantly improve profit margins. Deere's move comes after a recent stock drop and aims to capitalize on market needs like labor shortages and high input costs by offering efficiency through data-driven precision agriculture.

title: Micron, Netflix, and 5 Other Beaten-Up Tech Stocks That Could Be Bargains
summary: This article identifies seven technology stocks that have experienced significant drops in value during the current year but could offer attractive investment opportunities. It notes that while the broader U.S. stock market has faced challenges, high-growth technology stocks have been particularly hard hit. The piece specifically mentions Micron and Netflix as examples of these beaten-up tech stocks.

title: How Chatbots are Revolutionizing Customer Experience
summary: Modern AI-powered chatbots are transforming customer experience by handling various tasks from ordering taxis to booking appointments, driven by advancements in AI, Machine Learning, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). Unlike traditional rule-based bots, NLP allows chatbots to understand complex conversations, retain context, and interpret human sentiment by integrating with external knowledge sources. Conversational AI, built on neural networks, enables chatbots to learn and improve continuously, process vast amounts of unstructured data to predict user behavior, and perform repetitive tasks, making them indispensable for businesses and freeing up human employees for more complex issues.

title: Apple can't dodge rival app store Cydia's antitrust lawsuit
summary: A California federal judge has allowed rival app store Cydia's antitrust lawsuit against Apple Inc. to proceed, rejecting Apple's argument that the claims were time-barred. The lawsuit accuses Apple of maintaining an unlawful monopoly over software distribution on its iOS operating system and that its technological updates between 2018 and 2021 harmed app distributors like Cydia. This case is similar to the antitrust suit brought by Epic Games against Apple.

title: Mobile Mondays: SanDisk iXpand Flash Drive Luxe provides ease of mind with convenient transfer and backup
summary: The SanDisk iXpand Flash Drive Luxe offers a convenient solution for transferring and backing up files between iOS devices, computers, and tablets thanks to its dual Lightning and USB-C connectors. While its read and write speeds are notably slow, it excels as a hassle-free backup device for iPhone photos, eliminating the need for cables or adapters. The review highlights its portability and ease of use, despite the slower performance, making it a practical tool for organizing and securing digital content.

title: Apple Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Apple Inc.'s stock (AAPL) fell 0.53% to $148.84 on Tuesday, underperforming the broader market as the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declined. This marked the end of a three-day winning streak for Apple, and the stock closed $34.10 below its 52-week high. Competitors showed mixed results, with Microsoft (MSFT) and IBM (IBM) also falling, while Alphabet (GOOGL) saw a gain.

title: Diablo Immortal Review
summary: This extensive review of Diablo Immortal dives into Blizzard's free-to-play mobile MMO action-RPG, highlighting its core Diablo elements despite its mobile platform and microtransaction systems. The author praises its depth, storyline, and optimization, while noting it might not be welcoming for new Diablo players. Despite initial skepticism about mobile games and monetization, the review concludes that Diablo Immortal is shaping up to be one of the best mobile offerings available.

title: Apple to improve working hours for retail staff after union push
summary: Apple is implementing more flexible work schedules for its retail staff in response to unionization efforts and employee frustrations. Changes include longer breaks between shifts, limits on late-night work, fewer consecutive workdays, and guaranteed weekend days off for full-time employees. These adjustments are part of broader efforts by Apple to improve working conditions and address labor market challenges.

title: Block to integrate Square payments with Apple iPhone's 'Tap to Pay' feature
summary: Block Inc (SQ.N) announced on Thursday that it will integrate Square payments with Apple's "Tap to Pay" feature on iPhones, planning to launch the service later this year. This integration will allow sellers to accept contactless payments using only their iPhone and the Square Point-of-Sales app, eliminating the need for additional hardware. Apple (AAPL.O) previously stated that its "Tap to Pay" feature would debut this year, with other companies like Stripe and Shopify also offering the service.

title: What Can a Multi-Factor Screening Methodology Do for Your Core Equity Exposure?
summary: This article discusses how applying a multi-factor screening methodology, focusing on value, quality, and momentum, can enhance core equity exposure for large-cap stock portfolios. It highlights research from FlexShares suggesting that this approach can deliver superior risk-adjusted returns compared to the broad large-cap market. The FlexShares US Quality Large Cap Index Fund (QLC) is presented as an example of an ETF that utilizes these factors.

title: What Can a Multi-Factor Screening Methodology Do for Your Core Equity Exposure?
summary: This article discusses how applying a multi-factor screening methodology can improve risk-adjusted returns for large-cap equity portfolios. It highlights factors such as value, quality, and momentum, and introduces the FlexShares US Quality Large Cap Index Fund (QLC) which integrates these factors. The fund aims to provide benefits of these factor exposures while maintaining a similar risk profile to the broader large-cap market.

title: Here are this year’s Fortune 500 companies by region
summary: The 2022 Fortune 500 companies collectively generated $16.1 trillion in revenue, reflecting a 19% increase from 2020. This article breaks down the geographical distribution of these companies across the U.S. and highlights dominant industries in the Western, Midwest, Northeast, and Southern regions. Insights into key companies and financial performances specific to each region are also provided.

title: Motley Fool: A logistics leader
summary: GXO Logistics, the world's largest pure-play contract logistics company, reported strong first-quarter results with 14% revenue growth and doubling net income, despite a general downturn in e-commerce stocks. The company, which operates high-tech warehouses for major multinational clients, is strategically positioned to gain market share even in a recession due to its "cost-plus" contracts and investments in areas like reverse logistics. The article also includes "Ask the Fool" sections addressing hyperinflation, investment strategies, and a "dumbest investment" anecdote regarding Black Elk Energy bonds.

title: Apple announces winners of the 2022 Apple Design Awards
summary: Apple announced the winners of its 2022 Apple Design Awards, celebrating 12 outstanding apps and games selected from 36 finalists from around the world. These winners were recognized across six categories: Inclusivity, Delight and Fun, Interaction, Social Impact, Visuals and Graphics, and Innovation, for their creativity, innovation, and exceptional design. The awards highlight how developers leverage Apple technologies to create transformative and engaging user experiences.

title: WWDC22 highlights
summary: Apple kicked off its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC22) with a special event at Apple Park, welcoming developers, students, and media. The event showcased the redesigned MacBook Air and updated 13-inch MacBook Pro with the M2 chip, alongside new features for iOS 16, iPadOS 16, macOS Ventura, and watchOS 9. The conference also unveiled the new Apple Developer Center and provided access to over 175 developer sessions.

title: watchOS 9 delivers new ways to stay connected, active, and healthy
summary: Apple previewed watchOS 9, introducing customizable watch faces, an enhanced Workout app with advanced metrics and new training experiences, and sleep stages. A first-of-its-kind FDA-cleared AFib History feature offers deeper health insights, and a new Medications app simplifies tracking and managing prescriptions. These updates aim to provide users with scientifically validated insights across fitness, sleep, and heart health, while enhancing personalization options for their Apple Watch.

title: iPadOS 16 takes the versatility of iPad even further with powerful new productivity and collaboration features
summary: Apple previewed iPadOS 16, introducing a new multitasking experience with Stage Manager and external display support, enhanced collaboration through Messages and a new Freeform app, and significant updates to Mail and Safari. The update also brings pro features like Reference Mode and Display Zoom, the Weather app, and improved Live Text, making the iPad even more versatile and powerful, especially for M1 chip-equipped devices. These features aim to boost productivity, collaboration, and introduce new ways to interact with content.

title: Apple's iOS 16 update will let you edit and unsend text messages
summary: Apple's iOS 16 update introduces the ability for users to edit and unsend text messages within a 15-minute window. This feature, along with the option to recover deleted messages and mark conversations as unread, was announced during Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference. The update also brings a personalized lock screen with customizable date and time, and the addition of widgets for quick access to information.

title: With shares of Kansas City’s Cerner turned over, sale to Oracle will close Wednesday
summary: Oracle's acquisition of Cerner Corporation for $28.3 billion is set to formally close on Wednesday, June 8th, following a successful stock tender offer. This acquisition marks Oracle's largest ever and signifies its entry into the healthcare market, with Cerner continuing as a dedicated subsidiary and Oracle pledging to grow jobs in Kansas City. The deal concludes Cerner's status as the largest private employer under local control in Kansas City, a company founded in 1979.

title: A wholly owned subsidiary of Apple will extend loans for its Pay Later service
summary: Apple's new Pay Later service will have a wholly owned subsidiary handle credit checks and short-term loans, signaling the company's deeper push into financial services. While Apple is partnering with Mastercard and Goldman Sachs, it will manage the lending decisions and balance sheet itself. This move aims to make the iPhone ecosystem more valuable to users and is part of Apple's strategy to bring fintech infrastructure in-house.

title: Baidu's electric vehicle firm Jidu unveils first 'robot' car
summary: Baidu's EV arm Jidu Auto has unveiled its first "robot" concept car, aiming for mass production in 2023. This vehicle, co-funded by Chinese automaker Geely, features Level 4 autonomous capabilities and voice recognition, and is designed to attract tech-savvy users. The move positions Baidu among global tech companies developing smart cars following Tesla's success.

title: Apple Will Handle Lending Itself With New Pay Later Service
summary: Apple Inc. is set to manage its own lending operations for a new "buy now, pay later" service called Apple Pay Later, rather than relying on third-party partners. A subsidiary, Apple Financing LLC, will conduct credit checks and make loan decisions, holding the necessary state lending licenses. This move signifies Apple's deeper push into the financial services sector.

title: New American Fortune 500 in 2022: The Largest American Companies and Their Immigrant Roots
summary: A report from the American Immigration Council finds that 43.8% of the 2022 Fortune 500 companies were founded by immigrants or their children. These "New American" firms generated over $7 trillion in revenue and employed 14.8 million people in fiscal year 2021, demonstrating a significant impact on the U.S. economy. The report highlights the critical role of immigrant entrepreneurs in job creation and economic growth.

title: Dow dives 800 points, S&P 500 posts worst week since January after inflation hits 40-year high
summary: U.S. stocks fell sharply on Friday, with the Dow dropping 880 points and the S&P 500 completing its worst week since January, following a higher-than-expected inflation report for May. The consumer price index reached its highest level since 1981, showing an 8.6% year-over-year increase, fueling concerns about a potential recession and prompting fears of a more aggressive Federal Reserve. Tech stocks, banks, and cyclical stocks were particularly impacted as the 2-year Treasury yield surged above 3%.

title: Buy Now Pay Later business model faces test as rates rise
summary: The Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) sector, which saw rapid growth during the pandemic, is now facing significant challenges due to reduced consumer spending, rising interest rates, and increased funding costs. This environment is expected to lead to consolidation within the industry, with smaller players struggling to compete against larger firms and new entrants like Apple. Regulatory scrutiny is also increasing globally, further impacting the business model.

title: Palo Alto Networks Appoints Channel Leader for EMEA, LATAM
summary: Palo Alto Networks has appointed Patricia Murphy as its new VP ecosystems, partners and strategic alliances for EMEA and LATAM. In this role, Murphy will lead the company’s channel across these regions and serve as a key member of CEO Helmut Reisinger’s executive team. She brings over 25 years of technology industry experience and will focus on driving engagement with partners, including MSSPs, to enhance Palo Alto Networks' market position.

title: Trudeau meets Google boss Sundar Pichai, but no word on whether the tech CEO chewed out the PM over Bill C-18
summary: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau met with Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet (Google's parent company), but it's unclear if Bill C-18, a controversial Canadian media compensation bill, was discussed. The bill, criticized by experts like Michael Geist as a "shakedown," aims to make platforms like Google pay for linking to news content, giving the CRTC greater oversight. Google's Canadian head of public policy, Colin McKay, has stated that while Google supports journalism funding, Bill C-18 "would fundamentally and negatively impact millions of Canadians."

title: Apple’s New CarPlay Is the Foreshock to Releasing Its Own Vehicle
summary: Apple's redesigned CarPlay interface, unveiled at WWDC 2022, is seen as a precursor to the company eventually releasing its own vehicle. The article also touches on other Apple developments from WWDC 2022, including its new multitasking system, the absence of tvOS, and hints about an upcoming AR/VR headset. This reflects a broader expectation that Apple is laying the groundwork for a future in automotive technology.

title: Google Engineer Claims AI System is Sentient; China's Tech Giants Flourish as Crackdown Eases
summary: A Google engineer has been suspended after claiming that the company's LaMDA chatbot system shows signs of sentience, which Google denies, citing confidentiality breaches. Meanwhile, China's major tech companies like Baidu, Tencent, and Alibaba are experiencing a surge in share value as Beijing eases its regulatory crackdown on the sector. Twitter has also introduced a new "people-first" content moderation process to improve reporting of offensive content, especially amid Elon Musk's previous concerns about fake accounts.

title: Grassroots mojo and 4 other reasons Starbucks workers have been so successful unionizing
summary: Starbucks Workers United has achieved significant success in unionizing stores across the U.S., winning over 100 elections since December 2021. This unexpected success is attributed to a shift in public sentiment towards unions, a replicable worker-driven organizing model, Starbucks' progressive brand attracting pro-union employees, a strong sense of community among baristas, and a decentralized, grassroots approach to organizing. This movement could signal a broader union revival in the service sector.

title: Newmark Completes Sale of Moffett Green, Fully-Leased Trophy Campus in Silicon Valley
summary: Newmark has successfully completed the sale of Moffett Green, a 719,037 square-foot, fully-leased trophy office campus in Sunnyvale, California, to CommonWealth Partners, LLC. The transaction, which represents the largest single-asset investment office sale in Silicon Valley history, involved Meta Platforms, Inc. as the long-term tenant. Steven Golubchik and his team at Newmark facilitated the deal, highlighting continued investor demand for prime assets in high-barrier-to-entry markets and the strong performance of Moffett Park.

title: China COVID controls makes Apple supplier Pegatron "emphasise" expansion elsewhere
summary: Apple supplier Pegatron Corp is emphasizing expansion in countries other than China due to recent COVID-19 lockdowns in China, which caused plant suspensions and continue to lead to labor shortages. The company, an iPhone assembler, plans to expand its presence in Vietnam, India, Indonesia, and North America to diversify its production capacity and address staffing issues. This move also reflects a broader industry trend among customers to reduce concentration in Chinese manufacturing hubs like Shanghai and Suzhou.

title: Police surveillance company seeks to acquire notorious Israeli NSO Group
summary: L3 Harris, an American defense contractor previously known for its Stingray surveillance technology, is reportedly seeking to acquire the Israeli NSO Group, creator of the powerful and controversial Pegasus spyware. Privacy experts and civil liberties advocates express significant concern that this acquisition could lead to the proliferation of NSO Group's technology, which has been linked to human rights abuses globally, into local U.S. police departments. Critics highlight NSO Group's blacklisting by the U.S. government and the potential for increased misuse of surveillance tools against citizens, journalists, and activists.

title: Far From a Couch Potato: How Shawn Nelson is Revolutionizing Your Living Room
summary: Shawn Nelson, founder of Lovesac, aims to revolutionize consumer buying habits by designing durable, reconfigurable furniture like the Sactional, which he insists customers should keep for decades rather than frequently replacing. Despite leading a billion-dollar company, Nelson's personal "point" has evolved to prioritize his family and inspire consumers to buy less and buy better, challenging the norms of capitalism. He plans to expand this "Design for Life" ethos beyond furniture to other household items.

title: Sonos tips: how to stereo pair, connect to a TV, and more
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to getting the most out of a Sonos system, covering frequently asked questions and offering 25 tips and tricks. It details aspects like Bluetooth connectivity, using Sonos without Wi-Fi, connecting to a TV, app usage, waterproofing, stereo pairing, and enhancing sound quality with Trueplay and external DACs. The guide also touches on streaming services, voice control, and integrating vinyl with Sonos, demonstrating the system's versatility.

title: L3Harris in talks to buy Israeli spyware firm NSO - reports
summary: U.S. defence contractor L3Harris is reportedly in talks to acquire Israeli spyware firm NSO Group, a deal that would require approvals from U.S. and Israeli authorities and L3Harris's board. The potential acquisition raises concerns in the White House due to NSO's controversial Pegasus software, which has been used by governments to spy on individuals, leading to the company being blacklisted by the U.S. Commerce Department. If approved, the deal could potentially remove NSO from the banned list and allow its assets to be exclusively used with the United States and its allies.

title: How HP went from takeover bait to one of Warren Buffett’s top tech bets
summary: Once considered takeover bait after its 2015 split, HP Inc. has seen a remarkable turnaround, becoming one of Warren Buffett's top tech investments. CEO Enrique Lores is driving this revival by diversifying into new high-growth markets like gaming and hybrid work and emphasizing sustainability, which has contributed significantly to sales. Despite the company's strong performance, Lores acknowledges that pandemic-driven growth is not sustainable and aims for steady growth from a higher baseline.

title: Qualcomm’s Antitrust Fine for Payments to Apple Is Annulled by European Court
summary: The European Union's second-highest court has annulled a €997 million ($1.04 billion) antitrust fine previously imposed on Qualcomm Inc. The fine was related to payments Qualcomm made to Apple Inc. for the exclusive use of Qualcomm chips in iPhones. The court's decision cited procedural irregularities by the European Commission in its case against Qualcomm, rather than substantive competition law violations.

title: Ariana Grande Debuts Chapter 3 of R.E.M. Beauty with a Bang(s)
summary: Ariana Grande's R.E.M. Beauty launched its third collection, 'Chapter 3: on your collar,' focusing on lip products. This collection, which includes five new formulas like lip oil, balm, liner, and lipsticks, follows the brand's 'drops' model and continues its extraterrestrial aesthetic. R.E.M. Beauty's initial collections, Chapters 1 and 2, are also available at Ulta stores.

title: Garmin native running power on Forerunner 955 & 255 explained
summary: Garmin has introduced native running power support for its new Forerunner 955 and 255 models, a move long awaited by users. While not a standalone solution, it integrates the existing Garmin Running Power app functionality more seamlessly, requiring an accessory like the HRM Pro/Run/Tri or Running Dynamics Pod. The article explains running power, how to set it up on compatible Garmin watches, and notes that Garmin's calculations are proprietary and not comparable to other brands like Stryd.

title: Opinion | Semiconductor Dependency Imperils American Security
summary: The article argues that the U.S.'s heavy reliance on Taiwan for advanced semiconductor manufacturing poses a significant national security risk, particularly in light of potential Chinese dominance. While some question the effectiveness of U.S. subsidies to foster domestic chip leadership, the authors emphasize that reducing dependence on TSMC is crucial to protect America's technology sector and military capabilities. A disruption in Taiwan's chip production could severely impact the U.S.

title: BITI Chart | ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF
summary: This page provides a live, interactive chart for the ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI), allowing users to analyze its performance. The chart offers various customization options and technical indicators to help investors understand price movements. It also includes current market data for BITI, such as its price, daily range, and 52-week range.

title: Report: EU plans to investigate Broadcom’s proposed acquisition of VMware
summary: The European Union is preparing to launch a lengthy "phase 2" antitrust investigation into Broadcom's proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware. This investigation could last over a year and potentially derail the deal, as multiple organizations, including VMware customer trade groups, have expressed concerns about potential price increases or forced bundling of Broadcom services. Broadcom is reportedly in preliminary discussions with EU officials to argue that the acquisition will not reduce competition or harm customers.

title: Toronto FC ink ten-year BMO shirt sponsorship and naming rights renewal
summary: Toronto FC has secured a ten-year extension of its foundational partnership with the Bank of Montreal (BMO), ensuring BMO remains the main shirt sponsor and retains naming rights for BMO Field. This renewal emphasizes a continued commitment to grassroots soccer initiatives, including expanding the Kickstart program to include girls for the first time. The agreement reinforces the long-standing collaboration between the MLS club and the financial group, especially timely after BMO Field's recent selection as a host venue for the 2026 Fifa World Cup.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Ties Exec Pay to Climate Targets
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced that a portion of its top executives' bonus pay will be linked to the management of value chain emissions, alongside mandatory climate training for leadership. This move is part of HPE's broader ESG initiatives, including accelerating its net-zero goal to 2040 and setting ambitious 2030 emissions reduction targets approved by the Science-based Target Initiative (SBTi). HPE is joining a growing trend of companies connecting executive compensation to ESG performance to drive sustainability.

title: Roe v. Wade overturned: Here are companies paying for abortion travel costs for employees
summary: Following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, numerous major U.S. companies announced expanded support and financial assistance for employees seeking abortions in states with new restrictions. This support often includes covering travel expenses for medical care unavailable in their home states. Corporations are responding to pressure from investors, customers, and employees, and are concerned about the impact on talent attraction and retention.

title: Disney, other U.S. companies offer abortion travel benefit after Roe decision
summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, numerous U.S. companies, including Walt Disney Co. and Meta Platforms Inc., announced they would cover travel expenses for employees seeking abortion services. This move comes as many states are expected to restrict or ban abortions, making it necessary for employees to travel to states where the procedure remains legal. Companies like Dick's Sporting Goods, Lyft, Yelp, Microsoft, Tesla, Apple, Alaska Air Group, Johnson & Johnson, OkCupid, Bumble, Netflix, and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are also offering similar benefits or legal protections.

title: Apple Readies iPhone 14 and HomePod Upgrade in Flood of New Products
summary: Apple is preparing for a busy period in late 2022 and early 2023, with plans to release new products including the iPhone 14 and an updated HomePod. This follows the WWDC software releases, ongoing developments like a US Apple store unionizing, and new hires. The company's revamped CarPlay is also signaling future advancements.

title: T-Mobile is now selling data on which apps you open
summary: T-Mobile has launched its new "App Insights" program, which sells app usage data to advertisers, allowing them to target users based on the apps installed and engagement patterns. While the program focuses on Android devices, Apple's strict privacy guidelines largely protect iPhone users from data collection, though T-Mobile notes that it is "kicking the tires" on incorporating iOS data in the future. Android users can opt out of the program through the "Magenta Marketing Platform Choices" app.

title: Abortion assistance: Kroger to offer up to $4,000 of out-of-state travel costs for employees seeking abortion
summary: Kroger announced it will offer up to $4,000 to cover out-of-state travel costs for employees seeking abortion and fertility treatments, following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade. The company, headquartered in Cincinnati, operates stores in states like Ohio and Kentucky where abortion is now banned or mostly banned. This move aligns Kroger with nearly two dozen other major U.S. companies providing similar assistance.

title: Target completes 1,000th store remodel — here’s what it means
summary: Target Corp. has completed its 1,000th store remodel, a key milestone in its "stores-first" strategy, which has shown a 2% to 4% sales bump in updated stores. The company's 2022 remodel program is its most ambitious yet, including full-store remodels, fulfillment remodels to boost same-day services, and the addition of Drive Up canopies. These renovations incorporate welcoming elements, consistent layouts, and shop-in-shop experiences, while also focusing on sustainability and improving customer loyalty.

title: Rashida Jones and Will McCormack Sign First Look Deal at Lionsgate TV, Develop ‘Lovesick’ Adaptation
summary: Rashida Jones and Will McCormack's production company, Le Train Train, has signed a first-look deal with Lionsgate Television Group to produce scripted projects across various platforms. Their first project under the deal is an adaptation of the UK romantic comedy series "Lovesick." Jones and McCormack will executive produce the adaptation, and a writer is currently being sought.

title: Watch: When Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer laughed at iPhone
summary: This article revisits a famous incident where then-Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer publicly dismissed the original Apple iPhone upon its launch. He criticized its high price and lack of a physical keyboard, believing it wouldn't appeal to business users. Years later, Ballmer admitted his mistake, acknowledging Apple's innovative business model and regretting Microsoft's slow entry into the hardware business for mobile devices.

title: BP, Shell To Pay Employee Travel Costs After Abortion Ruling
summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's reversal of Roe v. Wade, BP Plc and Shell Plc announced they would cover travel expenses for U.S. employees needing to access health services not available near their homes. This makes them the first major oil companies to implement such policies. Both companies maintain significant operations in Texas, a state expected to ban or severely restrict abortions, joining other corporate giants in expanding employee health benefits.

title: Apple hikes Japan price of iPhone by nearly a fifth
summary: Apple Inc. has increased the price of its flagship iPhone in Japan by nearly 20% due to the weakening yen currency and rising inflation. The entry-level iPhone 13 now costs 117,800 yen ($870), up from 99,800 yen previously. This price hike comes as Japanese consumers are already facing increased costs for daily necessities.

title: Xfinity Stream app Launches on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD
summary: Comcast has launched the Xfinity Stream app on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD, providing Xfinity TV customers with a new way to access their content within their homes. The app introduces a redesigned, intuitive user interface focused on content discovery, including editorial recommendations and personalization. This initiative follows a previous collaboration between Comcast and Apple to bring the Apple TV+ app to Comcast's entertainment platforms.

title: Apple Ex-Corporate Law Chief Admits Years of Insider Trading
summary: Gene Levoff, Apple's former director of corporate law and co-chairman of its disclosure committee, pleaded guilty to six counts of securities fraud. He admitted to using his access to unreleased financial information from 2011 to 2016 to personally profit through insider trading. Levoff was ironically responsible for policing insider trading at the company.
authors: Chris Dolmetsch and Bob Van Voris

title: The Apple-1 Computer, a Vintage Tech Grail That Ignited the World of Personal Computing
summary: The article discusses the Apple-1 computer, a rare vintage tech item that played a pivotal role in the inception of personal computing. Only about 82 of the original 200 units are believed to still exist, with one recently selling for approximately $340,000 on eBay. The device, which required users to provide a power supply, keyboard, storage, and display, was groundbreaking for offering a complete circuit board during a time when most computers were sold as DIY kits.

title: What are FAANG stocks?
summary: FAANG is an acronym for five leading technology companies: Facebook (now Meta), Amazon, Apple, Netflix, and Google (now Alphabet). These companies are known for their consistent growth, innovation, and significant impact on the American stock market, particularly the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500 indices. While they have shown strong performance and market capitalization, there's an ongoing debate among investors about their valuation.

title: Delta reportedly paid passengers $10K to leave oversold flight
summary: Delta Air Lines reportedly offered passengers up to $10,000 each to volunteer to leave an overbooked flight from Michigan to Minnesota. An Inc. magazine tech columnist and another passenger confirmed the offer, which started at $5,000 before increasing. A Delta spokesperson did not confirm the specific incident but stated that compensation is provided to ground staff for managing such situations to ensure flights depart on time.

title: Rio Tinto markets low-emissions aluminum can
summary: Rio Tinto, in partnership with Corona Canada and Ball Corp., has introduced "Canada's first specially-marked, low carbon beverage can." These 1.2 million cans, available in Ontario, utilize aluminum from Rio Tinto and incorporate Elysis technology, which produces aluminum without direct carbon dioxide emissions during smelting. This initiative, part of a collaboration with Anheuser-Busch InBev, combines renewable hydropower and recycled content, reducing carbon emissions by over 30 percent compared to conventional cans.

title: Top 15 Affirm Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article identifies and details the top 15 competitors and alternatives to Affirm, a leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) service provider. It highlights various companies like Sezzle, PayPal, Klarna, Afterpay, and Zip, outlining their services, financial performance, and unique features. The piece also discusses alternatives that cater to specific needs, such as travel payments (Uplift) or those for consumers with poor credit (Perpay).

title: Apple Pay: The ultimate guide
summary: This comprehensive guide details Apple Pay, Apple's digital payment system designed to replace physical cards and cash with enhanced security and privacy. It covers setting up Apple Pay, utilizing the Wallet app, and explores related features such as Apple Card, Apple Cash, Apple Pay Express Transit, Apple Tap to Pay, and Apple Pay Later. The article highlights Apple Pay's evolution beyond contactless payments and its aim to simplify financial transactions.

title: Deezer stock listing: Why did the Spotify rival crash on its first day of trading?
summary: Deezer's stock crashed on its first day of trading in the French markets, despite initial high hopes for its public offering through a SPAC. The company's valuation significantly dropped from an anticipated €1.1 billion to €677 million. This poor performance is attributed to the intense competition from larger tech giants like Apple and Amazon, which have diverse revenue streams, and a general cooling of investor interest in tech stocks amidst rising inflation and recession fears in 2022.

title: This Texas town is making itself into a semiconductor epicenter
summary: Sherman, Texas, is transforming into a major semiconductor manufacturing hub, with significant investments from Texas Instruments and GlobalWafers totaling over $35 billion. This growth is driven by local leadership, existing infrastructure, and a pro-business tradition, positioning the city to play a critical role in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain. The expansion is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and population growth, though it also presents challenges related to rising housing costs and infrastructure demands.

title: QQ Music introduces Dolby Atmos support
summary: QQ Music, a leading Chinese music streaming service, has introduced Dolby Atmos support through a strategic partnership between Tencent Music Entertainment and Dolby. This integration brings spatial audio to the platform, making QQ Music the first domestic streaming service in China to offer Dolby Atmos, initially available to SVIP subscribers on compatible Android devices and featuring a growing catalog of international and Chinese artists. The collaboration aims to enhance the music listening experience for users in China.

title: Gurus foresee 'virtual humans' enhancing many industries
summary: Experts predict that virtual humans, fueled by AI advancements, will revolutionize various industries such as retail, finance, and education. Production costs and development times for digital avatars are expected to decrease significantly, leading to widespread adoption. Companies like Baidu are already investing heavily in this technology, with the market in China projected to reach 10.24 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) by 2026.

title: GameStop Corp. Cl A stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Shares of GameStop Corp. Cl A (GME) fell 4.87% on Friday, closing at $128.54, an underperformance compared to the broader market and some competitors. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw slight declines, but GME's drop was more significant. The stock was $127.15 short of its 52-week high, and trading volume was below its 50-day average.

title: What the Shopify stock split means for investors
summary: This article discusses the implications of Shopify Inc.'s 10-to-1 stock split on June 29, 2022, following a significant drop in its share price. It explores the strategic benefits of such a split, including making shares more attractive to retail investors, improving liquidity, and potentially boosting the share price. The article also examines the initial impact on Shopify's stock performance and trading activity.

title: The Unstoppable Rise of Formula 1
summary: Formula 1 has experienced a remarkable resurgence, transitioning from declining viewership in 2016 to a global phenomenon by 2022. This growth is attributed to strategic marketing efforts by Liberty Media, particularly the Netflix series "Drive to Survive," and a new media rights deal with ESPN. The sport's increasing popularity is also reflected in increased viewership, significant revenue growth, and high-profile entertainment projects, making it a case study in effective content marketing for sports leagues.

title: Apple Watch ‘Pro’ aims to be Garmin competitor – Here’s how much it could cost
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to launch an expensive 'Pro' model of the Apple Watch this year, alongside the standard Series 8 and a new Apple Watch SE. This "rugged" Pro model is designed to compete with high-end Garmin sports watches, targeting extreme sports athletes with features like a larger screen, bigger battery, S8 chip, body temperature sensor, and a durable non-aluminum metal case. The price is estimated to be around $900.

title: ChromeLoader: New Stubborn Malware Campaign
summary: This article details the ChromeLoader malware campaign, a browser hijacker and adware that surfaced in January 2022, spreading through malicious advertisements and primarily targeting users via compromised ISO/DMG files. The malware, also known as Choziosi Loader and ChromeBack, uses obfuscated browser extensions to steal search queries and display ads. The research identifies multiple evolving variants, including one targeting macOS, and emphasizes the attackers' rapid adaptation and sophisticated obfuscation techniques while highlighting protections offered by Palo Alto Networks.

title: All-New IHG One Rewards app powers loyalty, gives members more choice with Milestone Rewards
summary: IHG Hotels & Resorts has launched an all-new IHG One Rewards app, significantly redesigning its mobile experience to enhance loyalty benefits and user control. The app introduces "Milestone Rewards" which allows members to choose personalized benefits like points or suite upgrades as they accumulate stays. It also features faster booking, exclusive offers, an account dashboard for tracking progress, and a digital concierge for improved customer service.

title: Tesla alumni behind all-electric RV startup Lightship raise $23M
summary: Lightship Energy, an all-electric RV startup founded by former Tesla employees Ben Parker and Toby Kraus, has raised $23 million in Series A funding. The company aims to revolutionize the RV industry by developing an electric travel trailer that eliminates propane tanks and generators, and, crucially, addresses the significant range reduction experienced by electric towing vehicles. Their design includes an underlying EV skateboard architecture and a battery pack capable of propelling the trailer, effectively making it a 300-mile trailer for a 300-mile truck.

title: Unity to Buy IronSource in $4.4 Billion Deal to Boost Ad Tech
summary: Unity Software Inc. is set to acquire app monetization company IronSource in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $4.4 billion to enhance its advertising technology. This move comes as Unity's ad tech has been affected by Apple's recent data-privacy changes, leading Unity to also lower its annual revenue forecast, which caused its shares to plunge 18%.

title: General Mills, Fetch Rewards Roll Out Brand Loyalty Program
summary: General Mills and Fetch Rewards have launched "Good Rewards," a new loyalty program offering exclusive deals and loyalty benefits on over 45 General Mills brands. Available through the Fetch app, this initiative aims to provide personalized, digital-first experiences and savings to consumers. Fetch Rewards allows shoppers to earn points by submitting receipts, which can then be redeemed for gift cards and merchandise.

title: Prime Day 2022 was the biggest Prime Day event ever
summary: Prime Day 2022 proved to be the largest Prime Day event in Amazon's history, with members purchasing over 300 million items worldwide and saving more than $1.7 billion. This year also marked the biggest Prime Day for Amazon's small and medium-sized selling partners, whose sales growth outpaced Amazon's retail business. Customers spent over $3 billion on small business items, highlighting a significant focus on supporting these businesses during the event.

title: Chip Shortage Turns into Surplus
source: VOA - Voice of America English News
source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News
summary: The global chip shortage, exacerbated by the coronavirus pandemic and high demand for electronics, has unexpectedly turned into a surplus due to high inflation, China's COVID restrictions, and the war in Ukraine, which reduced consumer spending. This shift has severely impacted semiconductor stocks, with companies like Micron Technology reducing production and manufacturers, especially in computing and smartphones, now facing an oversupply. The situation is worsened by hoarding practices, though some sectors like Apple suppliers and, for now, automobile chipmakers, are less affected.

title: Taiwan accuses Chinese Apple supplier of stealing secrets, charges 14
summary: Taiwanese prosecutors have accused Chinese Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co Ltd of stealing commercial secrets and poaching staff from Taiwanese competitor Catcher Technology Co Ltd. The investigation, which lasted a year and a half, found that Luxshare specifically targeted Catcher's R&D team to gain orders from Apple. Consequently, 14 individuals have been charged with breach of trust and taking commercial secrets for overseas use.

title: Verizon Communications Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Verizon Communications Inc. stock increased by 1.05% on Friday to $51.02, despite a strong day for the broader market indexes. The stock's rise ended a two-day losing streak, yet it still closed short of its 52-week high and underperformed key competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and AT&T. Trading volume for Verizon was lower than its 50-day average.

title: Sonos Ray review: Finally a capable budget soundbar
summary: The Sonos Ray is a new budget-friendly soundbar priced under $300, offering entry into the Sonos ecosystem. It provides excellent sound quality for its price, particularly with dialogue clarity, and supports Apple's AirPlay 2 and HomeKit. While compact and capable, its reliance on optical audio and limited remote support are notable drawbacks, especially in comparison to higher-end Sonos models like the Beam and Arc.

title: +25년간 쉼없이 배당준 배당귀족주 ETF ; NOBL ETF 투자에 대해 공부해보기
summary: This article discusses investing in the NOBL ETF, which focuses on Dividend Aristocrats—S&P 500 companies that have consistently increased dividends for at least 25 years. It explains the NOBL ETF's characteristics, including its investment strategy, current portfolio of 64 companies, and expense ratio, while noting its exclusion of major tech stocks like Apple and Microsoft. The author suggests it as a "peace-of-mind" investment during volatile market conditions.

title: Daily deals July 17: $130 Apple TV 4K, $700 off 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, $27 Netgear router, more
summary: This article lists the best daily deals for July 17, including significant discounts on an Apple TV 4K, a 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, and a Netgear router. It compiles offers from various online stores like Amazon and Walmart, advising readers to act quickly as deals can be short-lived. The piece also mentions ongoing deals from the previous day.

title: Apple (AAPL) Plans to Slow Hiring and Spending for Some Teams Next Year
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) plans to decelerate its hiring and spending growth in certain divisions next year due to a potential economic downturn. This strategic decision is part of an effort to exercise caution during uncertain economic periods, though it does not apply universally across all teams. Despite these adjustments, Apple intends to maintain an ambitious product launch schedule for 2023, including the introduction of a mixed-reality headset.

title: Lawsuit accuses Apple of antitrust violations over Apple Pay
summary: Apple Inc. is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit filed by payment card issuers, accusing the company of antitrust violations related to its Apple Pay mobile wallet. The lawsuit alleges that Apple uses its market dominance in mobile devices to coerce consumers into using Apple Pay, imposing excess fees on banks and credit unions, unlike Android-based devices that allow users to choose other payment wallets. This legal action follows a previous warning from EU regulators regarding Apple's practices in mobile wallets.

title: LinkedIn Still Number One Brand to be Faked in Phishing Attempts while Microsoft Surges up the Rankings to Number Two Spot in Q2 Report
summary: Check Point Research's Q2 Brand Phishing Report reveals that LinkedIn remains the most imitated brand in phishing attempts, accounting for 45% of all such attacks. Microsoft has significantly risen to the second spot with 13%, surpassing DHL, while new brands like Adidas, Adobe, and HSBC have entered the top 10. The report highlights the dangers of these scams, with examples provided for LinkedIn, DHL, Outlook, and Amazon phishing attempts.

title: Apple reaches $50 mln settlement over defective MacBook keyboards
summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to a $50 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit concerning defective "butterfly" keyboards in its MacBook laptops. Customers claimed the keyboards were prone to failure and that Apple's repair program was inadequate. The settlement covers MacBooks, MacBook Airs, and MacBook Pros purchased between 2015 and 2019 in seven U.S. states, with individual payouts up to $395.

title: Google faces $1 bln UK trial over app store pricing
summary: Google is set to face a London trial over a £920 million ($1.1 billion) class action lawsuit alleging it overcharged 19.5 million customers for app store purchases. The lawsuit claims Google abused its dominant position by charging up to 30% commission on apps in its Play Store since October 2015, breaching European and British competition laws. This action is part of broader global efforts by regulators and consumer groups to curb alleged anti-competitive behavior by Big Tech companies like Google and Apple.

title: Staple x Fossil Nate Sundial wristwatch
summary: The article discusses how Fossil Group Inc. has been significantly impacted by the Apple Watch's release, leading to a substantial drop in its stock price. It highlights the launch of a unique limited-edition Fossil x Staple Nate Sundial watch, questioning whether this collaboration could mark a turnaround for the brand. Fossil's challenges are contrasted with the success of other traditional watch brands that have adapted to the market.

title: JPMorgan adds former J&J chief Gorsky to board
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co has elected Alex Gorsky, the former chief executive officer of Johnson & Johnson, as a director of the bank. Gorsky, who is currently an executive chairman at J&J, previously led the development of J&J's COVID-19 vaccine and steered the company through significant crises. His appointment to JPMorgan's board is effective immediately.

title: Who Will Build The Apple Car? Here Are The Possible Candidates
summary: Apple's long-rumored "Project Titan" car initiative is inching closer to reality, but the company lacks automotive manufacturing experience. This article explores potential partners or strategies for building the Apple Car, including past talks with Hyundai-Kia, the LG/Magna joint venture, existing relationships with Volkswagen, and Foxconn's entry into EVs. It also considers the possibility of Apple manufacturing the car in-house, given its recruitment of top automotive talent and acquisition of self-driving technology.

title: 3 Reasons Why Investing in the Nasdaq-100 Is Effective
summary: This article highlights three core reasons why investing in the Nasdaq-100 is effective for long-term asset management. It explains how index investing, particularly in the Nasdaq-100, offers exposure to rapidly growing, innovative global companies with strong brand power and historically high growth rates. The article details various investment products available, such as mutual funds and ETFs, and discusses strategies like currency hedging and leverage.

title: Semiconductor suppliers see oversupply of chips as smartphone demand falls
summary: Semiconductor manufacturers are experiencing an oversupply of chips due to declining demand for smartphones and PCs, as indicated by executives from TSMC and Micron Technology. While TSMC anticipates a rebalancing of the supply chain in a few quarters, Micron expects a 10% year-over-year decline in PC and smartphone sales. Despite the current oversupply in the consumer sector, TSMC still foresees long-term demand exceeding supply, especially for automotive and data center customers, driven by the need for high-performance computing.

title: Apple Argues It’s Now a Major Force in the Health-Care World
summary: Apple has published a nearly 60-page report asserting its role as a pioneer in health technology and a significant force in the healthcare sector. The company highlights its existing health features, from sleep monitoring to atrial fibrillation detection, and plans to further innovate in science-based technology. This move indicates Apple's strategic focus on health as a future growth driver.

title: Netflix Lost Nearly 1 Million Subscribers Last Quarter. Here’s What That Means for Its Future
summary: Netflix reported a loss of nearly 1 million subscribers in the second quarter of 2022, compounding its 200,000 subscriber loss from the previous quarter. Despite this, the total loss was less than projected, leading to a temporary stock surge, though shares remain significantly down for the year. In response, Netflix is pursuing efforts like an ad-supported tier and focusing on high-quality original content to drive future growth and revenue.

title: FaZe Clan Has Gone Public
summary: Gaming and lifestyle media platform FaZe Clan has officially gone public, completing a business combination with B. Riley Principal 150 Merger Corp. Shares began trading under "FAZE" and "FAZEW" at a $725 million valuation. With this move, FaZe Clan aims to access capital for growth and allow fans to own part of its future, leveraging its influence among Gen Z.

title: T-Mobile And Apple To Sell Small-business Connectivity Plan
summary: T-Mobile US Inc. announced a partnership with Apple Inc. to offer a new mobile phone plan targeting small-business owners. This plan includes subsidized iPhone 13s, T-Mobile wireless service, and Apple's Business Essentials service, with pricing as low as $50 per month for businesses with six or more lines. The initiative aims to streamline device management and connectivity for businesses with limited IT resources.

title: 7 Reasons Why People Are Moving to Texas
summary: Texas has become a popular relocation destination, drawing nearly 4 million new residents between 2010 and 2020. This article explores the key factors attracting people to the Lone Star State, including a lower cost of living, more affordable housing, no state income tax, and a thriving job market. Additionally, Texas boasts great schools, a warm climate, and rich cultural diversity, making it an appealing option for many.

title: iRhythm Technologies receives another FDA 510(k) for AFib monitoring system
summary: iRhythm Technologies has received another FDA 510(k) clearance for its ZEUS System, developed in partnership with Verily, which monitors atrial fibrillation. The latest clearance is for an algorithm that characterizes the amount of AFib a patient experiences over time using PPG-based bio-signals. The ZEUS System is not yet commercially available but is expected to have a limited market release in 2023.

title: GM trails far behind Tesla in EV sales — CEO Mary Barra bet the company that will change
