summary: GM CEO Mary Barra is betting the company's future on electric vehicles, despite currently trailing far behind Tesla in sales. GM plans to invest $30 billion in EVs by 2025, launch 30 electric models globally, and believes its Ultium platform will enable it to surpass Tesla by mid-decade or 2029. Barra's legacy is tied to transforming GM into an electric leader, building on past efforts to make the company leaner and more agile.

title: SCHB ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:SCHB
summary: This article provides an analysis of the Schwab U.S. Broad Market ETF (SCHB), detailing its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It covers essential information such as assets under management, expense ratio, inception date, and the index it tracks. The article also breaks down the fund's holdings by sector and region, and answers frequently asked questions about SCHB's investments, dividends, and performance.

title: SCHX ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:SCHX
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap ETF (SCHX), covering its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It details the fund's asset allocation, top holdings, expense ratio, and replication strategy. The analysis also includes recent performance metrics, fund flows, and answers frequently asked questions about SCHX.

title: Tuya Announces Matter-compatible Solutions in Close Collaboration with Amazon Alexa
summary: Tuya Smart announced its collaboration with Amazon Alexa to implement Frustration-Free Setup (FFS) based on the Matter connectivity protocol, aiming to enhance the IoT device setup experience and allow direct Wi-Fi connections to Amazon Echo devices. Tuya, an initial FFS partner for Matter, is participating in interoperability tests with Amazon, Google, and Apple and plans to launch Matter-compatible devices by the end of 2022. The company is committed to promoting universal open standards in IoT and supporting its ecosystem partners in adopting Matter, enabling a wider variety of smart home solutions for consumers.

title: iRhythm, Alphabet unit Verily get FDA clearances for Zio Watch and software system
summary: iRhythm Technologies and Alphabet's Verily have received FDA 510(k) clearance for their new AI-powered Zio Watch and Zio ECG Utilization Software System (Zeus). This system is designed for continuous monitoring of cardiac arrhythmia, particularly atrial fibrillation, and aims to assist clinicians in diagnosis by characterizing the amount of AFib over time. While not yet commercially available, the collaboration aims to compete with existing wearables like the Apple Watch in the cardiac monitoring market.

title: Workers For Lululemon DC Store File To Form Union
summary: Employees at a Lululemon store in Washington, D.C., have filed a petition to unionize, forming a group called the "Association of Concerted Educators." This move is part of a growing trend of unionization efforts at major U.S. retail companies, with employees seeking demands such as increased pay transparency and equitable pay structures. Lululemon has acknowledged the petition, stating its respect for the process and commitment to ongoing dialogue with its teams.

title: Wafers and layers: Fab advancements at China’s YMTC
summary: China's Yangtze Memory Technology Corp (YMTC) is rapidly advancing its NAND technology, reportedly developing 196 and 232-layer NAND products and building a second fabrication plant to significantly increase production capacity. The company aims to expand its market share beyond 5 percent and is even in discussions with Apple. These developments underscore China's push for technological self-sufficiency in the memory sector.

title: Gainesville SWAT team accused of subjecting people to unconstitutional search and seizure
summary: Three individuals have filed a federal lawsuit against the City of Gainesville, Gainesville Police, and members of its SWAT team, alleging unconstitutional search and seizure during a 2021 raid. The lawsuit claims that despite not being named on the search warrant or accused of a crime, they were held at gunpoint, zip-tied, detained for hours, and had personal property seized, which was only returned months later after legal action. The plaintiffs argue the search warrant was based on flawed information and that the GPD's policy makes it too easy to deploy the SWAT team for search warrants, leading to civil rights violations.

title: Top stock reports to watch this week (25.07.2022)
summary: The article highlights the key earnings reports scheduled for the week of July 25, 2022, focusing on major US tech companies like Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Amazon, and Apple. It notes that the Wall Street earnings season is in full swing, with several Dow Jones members also reporting their financial results. This week is expected to be significant for traders due to the high-profile companies announcing their earnings.

title: Apple, Koss Reach Deal in AirPods Patent Case on Eve of Trial
summary: Apple Inc. and headphone maker Koss Corp. have reached a settlement in their patent-infringement dispute over Apple's AirPods. The agreement, whose terms were not disclosed, led to the cancellation of a trial that was scheduled to begin in a Texas federal court. Koss had sued Apple, alleging infringement of five technology patents.

title: Metaverse ETF Full of Long-Term Potential
summary: Despite recent underperformance by growth stocks, the metaverse investment thesis is still in its early stages, presenting a long-term opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET A-) is highlighted as a potentially well-timed investment, given forecasts predicting the metaverse market could grow from $40-60 billion in 2021 to $8-13 trillion by 2030. FMET offers broad exposure to companies like Alphabet, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Tencent, which are integral to the metaverse's growth across various technologies.

title: Metaverse ETF Full of Long-Term Potential
summary: The article discusses the long-term potential of the metaverse as an investment opportunity, particularly through the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET). Despite a challenging year for growth stocks, the metaverse market is projected to grow significantly, reaching trillions by 2030, according to major investment banks. FMET offers a diversified approach to capitalize on this growth, including holdings in major tech companies like Alphabet, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Tencent.

title: Snap Plunges, Drags Down Social Media ETF
summary: Snap Inc. shares significantly dropped after reporting weak quarterly results due to slowing advertisement revenue, impacting the entire social media sector and the Global X Social Media ETF (SOCL). This downturn follows increased competition for ad dollars, particularly from TikTok, leading analysts to lower their recommendations and price targets for Snap. The company's struggles may reflect broader challenges within the tech and social media industries, with Snap planning reduced hiring, mirroring other major tech companies.

title: Intellectual property enforcement nets Koss Corp. up to $14 million
summary: Milwaukee-based Koss Corp. is set to generate $10 million to $14 million in net proceeds during Q4 from its intellectual property enforcement program, significantly surpassing its typical quarterly revenue. This windfall comes after settling a patent infringement lawsuit with Apple Inc., part of ongoing litigation against several major audio companies over wireless audio technology. Other lawsuits filed in 2020 against competitors like Bose and Skullcandy are still pending.

title: Apple's earnings will give a peek into the mindset of the high-end consumer
summary: Apple is set to report its third-quarter earnings, which analysts and investors will closely scrutinize for insights into the impact of macroeconomic trends like inflation and rising interest rates on high-end consumer spending. While Apple's sales have historically been robust due to its affluent customer base, any indication of slowing demand for iPhones and Macs could signal broader economic concerns. The company's performance will also reflect its ability to manage supply chain issues and COVID-19 lockdowns in China, as well as the growth trajectory of its services business.

title: Tencent tops Apple, Sony to take gaming market crown
summary: Tencent Holdings has overtaken industry giants like Sony and Apple to become the leading company in the gaming market, primarily through over 180 strategic, smaller investments worldwide. Despite regulatory challenges in China and increased competition from large acquisitions by Microsoft and Sony, Tencent is intensifying its focus on overseas markets, planning a global release for its top game "Honor of Kings," as its value-added services segment remains highly profitable. The Chinese tech giant's diverse global portfolio has allowed it to achieve significant revenue figures even as it navigates a changing domestic and international landscape.

title: SK Hynix Profit Jumps 56% on Resilient Chip Demand, Weak Won
summary: SK Hynix reported a 56% surge in second-quarter profit due to strong chip demand and a weak Korean won. However, the company, like other tech giants, warned of an impending slowdown in demand for its key products like PCs, smartphones, and servers, leading to a cautious outlook and plans to adjust 2023 capital expenditure.

title: Tiny Koss Scores Big Win over Giant Apple in Patent Dispute Settlement
summary: Koss Corporation has settled its patent infringement dispute with Apple Inc., securing an estimated $10-$14 million in licensing fees. This significant win for the smaller audio company, which had filed suit in 2020 alleging infringement of its wireless audio patents, underscores the growing trend of smaller innovators successfully challenging tech giants. The settlement not only provides a financial windfall for Koss but also strengthens the legitimacy of its patent portfolio for future legal actions.

title: Why Spotify's 'Car Thing' was destined for the hardware graveyard
summary: Spotify has discontinued its "Car Thing" player, a device designed to stream music and podcasts from a user's phone in the car. The company cited an inability to achieve an "attractive economical profile" due to low sales, high component costs, and chip shortages. This move highlights the difficulties software companies face when venturing into hardware, especially when competing with established ecosystems like Apple CarPlay and Android Auto.

title: Apple Outperforms in Q3 2022 Despite a Rocky Road
summary: Apple announced its Q3 2022 financial results, showing a 2% increase in revenue to $83.0 billion but a 10% decrease in profits year-over-year. Despite a challenging global economic climate, the iPhone and Services sectors saw growth, while iPad, Mac, and Wearables experienced declines largely due to supply constraints. Apple still managed to beat analyst expectations, and CEO Tim Cook expressed optimism about the company's resilience.

title: Apple forecasts faster sales growth, strong iPhone demand despite glum economy
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL.O) reported stronger-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results, driven by robust iPhone demand, despite a global economic slowdown and supply chain constraints. The company's shares rose 3.5% after hours, as it forecasted faster sales growth for the current quarter and noted easing parts shortages. While macroeconomic indicators are turning negative, Apple's Chief Financial Officer Luca Maestri stated that iPhone demand remains strong, showcasing the resilience of its affluent customer base.

title: Apple Narrowly Tops Estimates as iPhone Fares Better Than Feared
summary: Apple Inc. narrowly beat analyst estimates for its fiscal third-quarter revenue and profit, driven by better-than-expected iPhone sales. Despite a "cocktail of headwinds," CEO Tim Cook anticipates sales will rebound in the coming months, offering reassurance to investors worried about an economic slowdown. This performance gives Apple time to prepare for new product launches.

title: Apple Supplier TSMC's Arizona Plant Nears Completion, Expected to Mass Produce 5nm Chips
summary: TSMC, a key Apple chip supplier, recently held a topping-out ceremony for its new $12 billion fabrication plant in Phoenix, Arizona. This factory is set to mass-produce 5nm chips starting in the first quarter of 2024, which will be the first time Apple's custom silicon chips of this size could be produced in the U.S. Despite Apple's rumored shift to 3nm processes, the iPhone 14 Pro's A16 chip is expected to use 5nm technology, ensuring continued demand for TSMC's Arizona output.

title: Walmart Hawks Refurbished Goods to Inflation-Squeezed Shoppers
summary: Walmart is launching a "Walmart Restored" program to sell refurbished goods from brands like Apple, Samsung, and KitchenAid, both online and in some stores this fall. This initiative aims to attract cost-conscious shoppers amidst high inflation by offering inspected, tested, and cleaned products with 90-day free returns. The move comes after Walmart cut its profit forecast, noting that rising food and fuel prices are causing customers to reduce spending on general merchandise.

title: Apple’s Hot iPhone Quarter Masks a Behind-the-Scenes Slowdown
summary: Apple experienced a strong quarter for iPhone sales, however, the company is facing an unapparent slowdown. Analyst Mark Gurman also notes Apple's acquisition of a top Lamborghini executive and Meta's interest in an eye-tracking startup. This article follows Gurman's previous assessment of Apple's vulnerability during an economic downturn.

title: Webull Launches Expansion into Singapore
summary: Webull Corporation has officially launched its expansion into Singapore and the Asia-Pacific region. Through Webull Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd., residents can now trade U.S. and Hong Kong-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares with low commissions. This move follows Webull's success in the U.S. and Hong Kong, aiming to capture the growing mobile-first brokerage market in Asia-Pacific.

title: Pontifications: Two books examine GE’s fall from grace
summary: This article reviews two books about General Electric and its former CEO Jack Welch: "Lights Out: Pride, Delusion, and the Fall of General Electric" and "The Man Who Broke Capitalism." The author, Scott Hamilton, suggests that "Lights Out" provides a more detailed corporate history, while "The Man Who Broke Capitalism" offers a personal biography of Welch and discusses his influence on leaders at other companies like Boeing. The review highlights how Welch's management style and GE's culture, focused on short-term shareholder value and cost-cutting, adversely affected other companies, including Boeing, leading to a "damning picture" of negative impact on American industry.

title: Apple buys HP’s 67-acre San Diego campus as it expands footprint and works on custom modem
summary: Apple has purchased HP's former 67-acre campus in San Diego for $445 million, marking a significant investment in its engineering efforts in Southern California. The acquisition is intended to support Apple's expanding work on an in-house modem and other wireless technologies, with plans to increase local job creation from 1,200 to 5,000 by 2026. This move reinforces Apple's commitment to bringing more hardware components in-house, despite potential delays in its own modem deployment.

title: Music Stocks List 2026 - Best Publicly Traded Companies
summary: This article identifies top publicly traded music companies for 2026, including Spotify, Apple, Sirius XM, Google, Tencent Music Entertainment, and Warner Music Group. It discusses their market positions, growth opportunities, and business models, highlighting shifts towards streaming, podcasts, and diverse media portfolios within the evolving music industry. The author emphasizes that investing in the music industry offers various choices, from streaming giants to production houses, with significant future growth potential.

title: Alibaba Cloud and XPeng Motors build autonomous vehicle cloud data center
summary: Alibaba Cloud and XPeng Motors have partnered to establish an "intelligent computing center for automated driving" in Ulanqab, Inner Mongolia, aiming to accelerate the development and testing of autonomous vehicle models. The facility boasts a claimed computing power of 600 petaflops and uses efficient air-cooling technology. This initiative is part of China's "Eastern Data Western Calculation" strategy, leveraging the region's favorable conditions for data centers.

title: YC and a16z back virtual reality basketball app Gym Class
summary: Virtual reality basketball app Gym Class has secured an $8 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz, even as the broader VR startup ecosystem faces challenges. The app, which is a VR pure-play and currently has nearly 1 million downloads on Meta’s App Lab, is viewed as a social hub for basketball enthusiasts. Its upcoming launch on the official Quest Store is a significant step, highlighting the critical role Meta's platform plays for VR developers.

title: Microstrategy Outperforms Every Asset Class and Big Tech Stock Since Adopting Bitcoin Strategy, Says CEO
summary: Microstrategy CEO Michael Saylor highlights that the company's bitcoin strategy has led to significant outperformance against all asset classes and major tech stocks since its adoption. Saylor attributes this success to bitcoin's digital property characteristics and its appeal as a safe haven asset. The company's unique approach involved acquiring substantial bitcoin holdings and integrating it into their corporate strategy.

title: Pelosi’s Taiwan trip puts the world's biggest chipmaker back in the spotlight of U.S.-China rivalry
summary: Nancy Pelosi's visit to Taiwan brought renewed attention to the island's critical role in the global chip supply chain, specifically highlighting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a key manufacturer for tech giants like Apple and Nvidia. The visit underscored the strategic importance of semiconductors to U.S. national security and its rivalry with China. TSMC's dominance in the foundry market and the ongoing U.S. efforts to boost domestic chip manufacturing and secure supply chains were central themes, with concerns about China's potential actions towards Taiwan posing a significant risk to the global chip market.

title: Apple's HR department comes under fire
summary: A Financial Times report reveals that Apple's HR department is facing accusations from over a dozen current and former female employees of mishandling misconduct allegations and retaliating against those who complain. Many claim that voicing concerns led to them being labeled "bad team members" and ultimately resulted in their departure or a severance offer in exchange for releasing Apple from liability. This comes amidst ongoing employee activism, including the #AppleToo movement, which has seen hundreds of accounts of misconduct reported by current and former Apple staff.

title: Nio sets up mobile technology company, first phone coming next year - ArenaEV
summary: Nio has officially established "Nio Mobile Technologies Co Ltd" with a registered capital of $100 million, signaling its entry into the smartphone market. The company aims to develop and sell electronic products, mobile devices, and wearable devices, with a self-developed phone expected next year. Nio's CEO confirmed plans for an annual phone release, designed to integrate seamlessly with Nio vehicles and offered at a competitive price compared to Apple products.

title: Jamf Holding Corp. (JAMF) Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Jamf Holding Corp. reported strong Q2 2022 earnings, exceeding expectations with 40% ARR growth and 34% revenue growth. The company highlighted significant expansion in commercial and education markets, driven by product diversification and a growing demand for Apple device management and security solutions. Key wins included Red Bull consolidating Apple devices with Jamf Pro and a large education program in Taiwan adopting Jamf for 80% of its schools, despite an uncertain macroeconomic environment and hardware supply issues.

title: The African “Silicon Valley” Underway
summary: South Africa, particularly Cape Town and the Western Cape, is emerging as a strong contender for "the next Silicon Valley" due to its robust tech ecosystem, high foreign direct investment, and a significant number of tech startups and venture capital firms. The region boasts a high concentration of developer talent, world-class universities, and an environment conducive to doing business, mirroring key factors that contributed to the success and sustained innovation of the original Silicon Valley. These attributes, combined with a focus on cultural diversity and strong educational institutions, position South Africa as an innovation hub on the African continent.

title: Apple M1 Macs: What you need to know about buying an Apple silicon Mac
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to Apple's M1, M1 Pro, M1 Max, and M1 Ultra chips, detailing their specifications, the Mac models they power, and their impact on software compatibility and accessory usage. It also addresses common questions about RAM limitations, Intel Mac support, and the future of Apple silicon. The piece emphasizes the performance and battery life benefits of M1 Macs while advising potential buyers on compatibility checks and future market trends.

title: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins speak out about the state's new restrictive abortion law
summary: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins have voiced strong opposition to the state's newly passed restrictive abortion law. Eli Lilly stated the law would hinder its ability to attract diverse talent and force it to plan for employment growth outside Indiana, while Cummins expressed concerns about its impact on talent attraction and retention. Both companies emphasized that the law conflicts with ensuring women's full participation in the workforce and the need for inclusive environments.

title: Apple’s iPad Software Delay Keeps Focus on Its Main Attraction: iPhone 14
summary: Apple has postponed the release of iPadOS 16 until October, a move that is considered a proper decision but could lead to new challenges for users and developers. This delay shifts the spotlight to the upcoming iPhone 14, as Apple concurrently prepares for its September keynote event. The article also notes potential unionization efforts in more Apple stores.

title: JPMorgan Active Growth ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:JGRO) $JGRO
summary: The JPMorgan Active Growth ETF (JGRO) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in U.S. large-cap companies with strong earnings growth potential. Launched on August 8, 2022, the ETF has $8.27 billion in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.44%. As of March 13, 2026, JGRO was trading at $85.77, a 7.6% decrease from its Jan 1, 2026 price of $92.80, and its top holdings include technology giants like NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet.

title: Direxion Launches Apple & Tesla Single-Stock ETFs
summary: Direxion has launched the first four of its single-stock leveraged and inverse ETFs, offering sophisticated traders magnified or inverse daily exposure to Apple and Tesla stock. These new ETFs are designed for short-term tactical trading in volatile markets and are not intended as buy-and-hold investments due to their high-risk nature and lack of diversification. They aim to provide active traders with tools to respond to company events and market sentiment.

title: PhonePe Enables Seamless Purchase of App Store Codes
summary: PhonePe, India's leading digital payments platform, has enabled the purchase of Apple's App Store Codes through its app. Users can buy these codes, ranging from Rs 100 to Rs 10,000, to pay for digital content like apps, games, music, and more on the App Store without needing a debit/credit card. This service was made possible through a partnership with epay, a segment of Euronet Worldwide, Inc., Apple's distributor for App Store Codes in India.

summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Direxion Daily AAPL Bull 2X ETF (AAPU), a short-term tactical tool designed to provide 2x the daily price return of Apple stock. The article also includes performance data, technical analysis, and answers to frequently asked questions about the ETF.

The article includes news, technical analysis summaries, and answers to frequently asked questions about the ETF.

title: How Microsoft secures its spot among the world's most valuable brands
summary: Microsoft has become one of the world's most valuable brands, with its brand value reaching $661 billion in 2022, securing the number four spot in Kantar BrandZ Most Valuable Global Brands. The company's success is attributed to its meaningful, different, and salient offerings, including superior cloud, software, and hardware products, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has focused on specific market segments, leading to a tenfold increase in brand value and solidifying its position as the world's most valuable business services brand, further boosted by the continued digital transformation and remote work trends.

title: Invisalign Photo Uploader by Align Technology, Inc.
summary: The Invisalign Photo Uploader application, developed by Align Technology, Inc., allows Invisalign Providers and their staff to easily create patient profiles, take high-quality photos, and submit them to the Invisalign Doctor Site. The app offers features like guided photo-taking, auto-cropping, quality analysis, and direct upload, alongside an option to export photos for doctor records. It is intended exclusively for Invisalign providers.

title: Desktop CPU Sales Lowest in 30 Years, AMD Gains Market Share Anyway (Updated)
summary: Desktop CPU shipments hit a nearly 30-year low in Q2 2022, marking the largest year-over-year decline in Mercury Research's records. Despite the overall market slump, AMD significantly increased its market share and revenue in desktop, mobile, and server segments, setting new records for x86 processor share and mobile unit share. Intel, in contrast, reported a rare loss and delayed key products, indicating a challenging period for the long-standing leader.

title: Download and Play Spellslingers Now!
summary: Wizards of the Coast has soft-launched Spellslingers, a new digital collectible card game set in the Magic: The Gathering multiverse. The game features fast-paced, hero-centric combat, allowing players to choose from sixteen unique Spellslinger characters, each with their own playstyle and starter deck. Spellslingers is available for download on Android, iOS, and PC, inviting players to customize their decks, form teams, and trade materials.

title: This weekend, I watched a hacker jailbreak a John Deere tractor live on stage
summary: The author describes watching a hacker named Sickcodes jailbreak a John Deere tractor's control unit at Defcon 30. This event highlighted John Deere's monopolistic practices regarding tractor repair and its flawed information security, despite the company's claims that locking down tractors protects farmers and copyright. Sickcodes's hack revealed the company's use of outdated software and poor security protocols, demonstrating that their "security through obscurity" approach makes them vulnerable, not secure.

title: Intel hands over nearly 5,000 patents in deal with IP management outfit
summary: Intel has transferred a portfolio of nearly 5,000 patents to Tahoe Research Limited, a new company within IPValue Management Group, which will license them to third parties. This deal aims to maximize revenue from Intel's older, non-core patents by leveraging IPValue's expertise in intellectual property management and licensing. This move follows Intel's past sales of its smartphone modem business to Apple and NAND flash business to SK hynix, indicating a strategy to streamline its IP portfolio.

title: Apple Sets Return-to-Office Deadline of Sept. 5 After Covid Delays
summary: Apple Inc. has set a September 5 deadline for corporate employees to return to the office for at least three days a week. This marks another attempt to implement its hybrid work model after previous delays due to COVID-19 surges. Employees will be required to work in-person on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and a third day that individual teams will decide, a change from the initial Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday plan.

title: Buffett's Berkshire boosts Ally, Activision holdings; sheds Verizon
summary: Berkshire Hathaway significantly increased its stakes in Ally Financial and Activision Blizzard while completely exiting its investment in Verizon Communications by June 30. The conglomerate, run by Warren Buffett, also slowed its overall stock buying in the second quarter, making fewer new purchases and sales compared to the first quarter. Berkshire's portfolio continues to be dominated by Apple, with substantial investments in oil companies Chevron and Occidental Petroleum.

title: Apple Lays Off Recruiters as Part of Its Slowdown in Hiring
summary: Apple Inc. laid off approximately 100 contract-based recruiters, a move reflecting the tech giant's effort to slow hiring and reduce spending. This action, although rare for Apple, signals a broader slowdown within the company. The affected recruiters were responsible for bringing in new employees, underscoring a shift in Apple's staffing strategy.

title: Google Strikes Back at Sonos; SmartSpeaker War Gets Ugly
summary: Google has sued Sonos for patent infringement, escalating an ongoing battle over smart home audio technology. This comes after Sonos previously won a ruling against Google regarding similar claims. The conflict highlights broader issues of antitrust concerns and data privacy in the smart speaker market, with Sonos launching its own privacy-focused voice assistant.

title: The Blue Light blues
summary: The article discusses the pervasive issue of blue light exposure from screens and its detrimental effects on human health, including disrupted sleep, eyestrain, headaches, and accelerated skin aging. It explains what blue light is, how it impacts circadian rhythms, and the potential benefits of blue light-blocking glasses. The piece also highlights a study on fruit flies linking blue light to neurological damage and offers advice on minimizing exposure.

title: Qualcomm planning return to server market with new chip - Bloomberg News
summary: Qualcomm Inc. is reportedly planning a return to the server market with a new chip, aiming to reduce its dependence on smartphones. The company is seeking customers for a product based on its Nuvia Inc. acquisition, with Amazon Web Services (AWS) agreeing to evaluate the offerings. This move comes as Qualcomm faces a slowdown in smartphone demand, impacting its core handset chip business.

title: Charles Schwab and Pinnacle Lose Options Strategy Arbitration Case
summary: Charles Schwab and Pinnacle Associates, an RIA firm, have been ordered to pay a former customer approximately $800,000. This award stems from an arbitration case where the customer alleged misrepresentation regarding an options strategy, leading to unexpected sales of her Apple shares. The case highlights risks associated with certain investment strategies and the importance of clear communication between advisors and clients.

title: Paramount renews U.S. rights to Champions League valued at $1.5 billion
summary: Paramount Global has renewed its U.S. media rights for the UEFA Champions League in a six-year deal valued at over $1.5 billion, more than double its previous contract. This agreement, running from 2024 to 2030, reflects the growing popularity of football in the U.S. and further emphasizes the rising cost of sports broadcasting rights. Paramount will pay approximately $250 million annually to air the matches on its CBS network and Paramount+ streaming service.

title: Why the ESG/Tech Combination Is Important
summary: Broad market environmental, social, and governance (ESG) exchange-traded funds (ETFs) frequently have significant allocations to technology stocks. For example, the Invesco ESG Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQMG) has a technology weight of almost 62%. While some critics view this as over-reliance on tech, experts argue that technology companies often embody sustainable development principles, making them suitable for ESG funds despite facing scrutiny on issues like data privacy and energy consumption.

title: Apple expands Self Service Repair to Mac notebooks
summary: Apple has expanded its Self Service Repair program to include MacBook Air and MacBook Pro notebooks with M1 family chips, making genuine parts, tools, and manuals available to US customers. This initiative allows individuals experienced with electronic device repair to fix their own MacBooks, with options to rent tool kits and receive credit for returning replaced parts. The program, which launched earlier for iPhone, is part of Apple's broader effort to increase access to repairs, including supporting a growing network of authorized service providers and independent repair shops.

title: Ex-Apple engineer admits stealing automotive trade secrets
summary: A former Apple engineer named Xiaolang Zhang pleaded guilty to stealing confidential automotive trade secrets from Apple. Zhang was indicted in 2018 for obtaining a 25-page document detailing a circuit board for Apple's autonomous vehicle project, shortly after announcing his departure to work for Chinese EV maker Xpeng. Xpeng has denied involvement in the case, while also being a competitor in the autonomous driving space.

title: SanDisk Professional G-Drive range overhauled with 10Gbit USB 3.2 Type-C
summary: SanDisk Professional has updated its G-Drive range with a new hard disk-based external drive featuring a 10Gbit USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C interface and a refreshed design. The stackable, anodized aluminum enclosure includes an Ultrastar enterprise-class hard drive for reliability, offering up to 22TB of storage. Preformatted for Mac and compatible with Windows after reformatting, the drives boast speeds up to 250MB/s and are available for preorder starting at $209.99 for a 4TB model.

title: Tinder-owner Match Group files antitrust case against Apple with India’s competition regulator
summary: Match Group, owner of Tinder, has filed an antitrust case against Apple with India's competition regulator, accusing the tech giant of monopolistic conduct by forcing developers to use its proprietary in-app purchase system and charging excessive commissions. This action adds to existing challenges against Apple in India and globally regarding its App Store policies. Match argues Apple's practices restrict innovation and unfairly differentiate between app types, especially concerning payment methods favored in India.

title: CVNets: High Performance Library for Computer Vision
summary: Apple Machine Learning Research introduces CVNets, an open-source library designed for high-performance training of deep neural networks in visual recognition. It supports various tasks like classification, detection, and segmentation, offering tools for image and video understanding, including data loading and transformations. The library also features implementations of standard networks with competitive or superior performance, aiming to streamline computer vision research and development.

title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s Second Largest 5nm Customer Courtesy Of Ryzen 7000
summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer for 5nm technology, primarily driven by its upcoming Ryzen 7000 series processors. These new CPUs, including desktop, notebook, and datacenter variants, will extensively utilize TSMC's 5nm process node. This increased demand will solidify AMD's position as a major client for the Taiwanese chip manufacturer, second only to Apple.

title: Lucid Appoints Derrick Carty as Vice President of Platform Software Engineering
summary: Lucid Group, Inc. announced the appointment of Derrick Carty as Vice President of Platform Software Engineering, reporting to Michael Bell, Senior Vice President of Digital. In this newly created role, Mr. Carty will lead software engineering for the Lucid Air, encompassing ADAS, Audio, CoreOS/Android Framework, Embedded Software, Controls Platform, AUTOSAR, and Systems Architecture. Carty brings 23 years of experience from Apple, Inc. and will focus on delivering new Lucid Air features via over-the-air software updates.

title: Smartwatch Market Size Will Achieve USD 156.3 Billion by 2030 growing at 20.1% CAGR - Exclusive Report by Acumen Research and Consulting
summary: The global smartwatch market is projected to reach USD 156.3 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 20.1% from 2022 to 2030, according to a report by Acumen Research and Consulting. Key drivers include rising demand for wearable devices with advanced features, increasing consumer spending on healthcare, and the impact of COVID-19 which highlighted the health monitoring capabilities of smartwatches. North America is expected to hold the largest market share, while Asia-Pacific is anticipated to show the fastest growth.

title: Apple’s Chief Privacy Officer Set to Leave Company for Law Firm
summary: Apple Inc.'s Chief Privacy Officer, Jane Horvath, is set to leave the company to join the law firm Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP. Horvath, who started at Apple in 2011, held a significant role, particularly as privacy became a key selling point for the iPhone maker. Her departure was recently communicated internally to Apple's legal department.

title: Google Says Trump’s Truth Social Must Scrub Violent Content to Join Play Store (Published 2022)
summary: Google stated it would not distribute Truth Social on its Play Store until the platform removed content inciting violence. Google identified physical threats in user content from Truth Social's Android app submission, while Truth Social's parent company, Trump Media & Technology Group, said it is working with Google to comply with policies without compromising free speech. The social media app is available on Apple's App Store and could bypass Google by offering direct downloads or using other app stores like Samsung's Galaxy Store.

title: Trump's Truth Social barred from Google Play store over content moderation concerns
summary: Google has barred Donald Trump's Truth Social app from its Play Store due to concerns over its content moderation policies, meaning 44% of U.S. smartphone users cannot download it. While Truth Social acknowledges and plans to address these issues, Trump Media CEO Devin Nunes claimed the approval was solely up to Google. The app is already available on Apple's App Store, and Android users can access it via the web, but its unavailability on Google Play adds to the challenges faced by the platform, which was created as a "free-speech" alternative to Twitter.

title: iPhone 14 could support satellite communication if Apple can settle on a partner
summary: Apple's upcoming iPhone 14 models have completed hardware testing for satellite communication, according to analyst Ming-Chi Kuo. While the technology is ready, Apple has yet to secure a business partner for the service. Kuo suggests Globalstar could be Apple's initial partner, as the need for satellite communication is growing due to natural disasters and geopolitical conflicts.

title: Songwriters and Music Publishers to Be Paid More by Streaming Services
summary: Music publishers and streaming services have reached an agreement to increase the rates paid to songwriters for their music played on digital platforms like Spotify. This deal, struck between the National Music Publishers Association and the Digital Media Association, means publishers will receive up to 15.35% of music streamers’ revenue, which will be phased in over a five-year period. The agreement was finalized just before a trial was set to begin in front of the Copyright Royalty Board.

title: Truth Social: content moderation issue keeps Trump app off Android phones | Donald Trump
summary: Donald Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, has not been approved for distribution on the Google Play Store due to insufficient content moderation, a Google spokesperson announced. Google cited concerns about violations of its policies, including those prohibiting physical threats and incitement to violence. This delay prevents Truth Social from accessing approximately 40% of the US smartphone market, a setback for the platform.

title: SpaceX, T-Mobile race to put an end to cell phone dead zones with help from outer space
summary: T-Mobile and SpaceX are collaborating to launch "Coverage Above and Beyond" service by late 2023, aiming to eliminate cellular dead zones across the continental U.S., Hawaii, parts of Alaska, and surrounding waters using SpaceX's Starlink satellites. The service will initially offer basic text and voice communications through existing smartphones, leveraging T-Mobile's 1900 MHz frequency. This initiative marks a significant step toward ubiquitous cellular coverage, with other companies like Apple and AST SpaceMobile also developing similar satellite-based phone connectivity solutions.

title: Snap CEO to restructure company, cut 20% of workforce
summary: Snap Inc. is restructuring its operations and laying off 20% of its workforce after experiencing its slowest-ever revenue growth in Q2 and a significant net loss. CEO Evan Spiegel stated the company will refocus on revenue and user growth, and augmented reality (AR) to ensure long-term success. This strategic shift includes discontinuing non-core projects like Snap Originals, Minis, Games, Pixy, Zenly, and Voisey, while also authorizing a $500 million stock buyback program.

title: DAX-ETF statt MSCI-World-ETF? Was der Fokus auf deutsche Aktien wirklich bringt
summary: This article examines whether a DAX-ETF is a viable alternative or supplement to an MSCI World-ETF for investors. The author argues that while German companies in the DAX have a long history, focusing solely on a DAX-ETF has historically yielded poorer returns compared to the more diversified MSCI World. Key reasons cited against a DAX-ETF include its historical underperformance, regional concentration, lack of tech companies, and limited number of constituent stocks.

title: Arm sues Qualcomm, wants it to “destroy” Nuvia’s pre-acquisition CPU designs
summary: Arm is suing Qualcomm over its $1.4 billion acquisition of Nuvia, claiming the purchase caused Nuvia to breach its Arm licenses. Arm alleges that Qualcomm and Nuvia continued to develop Arm-based technology under the terminated licenses without consent, demanding that they stop using and destroy the relevant Nuvia technology. Qualcomm denies these claims, asserting it has broad licensing rights covering its custom-designed CPUs.

title: T-Mobile US makes bold eSIM push to poach new customers
summary: T-Mobile US is leveraging eSIM technology to attract new customers by offering a free three-month trial of its network and an "Easy Switch" feature. This strategy allows non-customers with unlocked eSIM-compatible smartphones to experience T-Mobile's service, including 5G, with minimal effort. Analysts view this as an innovative move that prepares carriers for an eSIM-only future and highlights T-Mobile's confidence in its network performance.

title: Arm sues Qualcomm, aiming to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 bln Nuvia purchase
summary: Arm Ltd. has sued Qualcomm Inc. and its recently acquired chip design firm Nuvia Inc. for breach of license agreements and trademark infringement, seeking to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 billion Nuvia purchase. Arm is requesting an injunction that would compel Qualcomm to destroy designs developed under Nuvia’s license agreements with Arm, alleging its approval was needed before the transfer to Qualcomm. This lawsuit signifies a major rift between two key technology partners, as Qualcomm had acquired Nuvia to boost its custom computing core designs and challenge competitors in the PC market.

title: Friday Q&A: iRhythm CTO Mark Day talks Zio Watch, Verily partnership, Big Tech in healthcare
summary: iRhythm Technologies CTO Mark Day discusses the new Zio Watch, a diagnostic smartwatch for cardiac monitoring developed in partnership with Alphabet's Verily. Day emphasizes that the Zio Watch is a prescribed diagnostic tool, distinguishing it from consumer smartwatches like the Apple Watch, and addresses the strategy for clinical evidence, reimbursement, and market evaluation. He also shares his views on Big Tech's complementary role in raising awareness for conditions like atrial fibrillation.

title: Tencent Music to work with Billboard in bringing Chinese artists to the global stage
summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) has partnered with Billboard to launch the "Chinese Music Gravity Project," aimed at promoting Chinese music and artists globally. This collaboration will integrate Billboard's global content onto TME's platforms and use TME's insights with Billboard's presence to enhance Chinese music's international impact. The partnership follows Billboard's recent launch of Billboard China and aligns with TME's ongoing efforts to bring Chinese music to a global audience, despite regulatory challenges in China's music streaming industry.

title: Ryder System acquires Baton developing proprietary logistics technology
summary: Ryder System, Inc. has acquired Baton, a San Francisco-based logistics technology startup, following an earlier investment through its venture capital fund, RyderVentures. This acquisition aims to enhance Ryder's supply chain and transportation solutions by integrating Baton's proprietary technology and talented development team. Baton's cofounders, Andrew Berberick and Nate Robert, will now lead innovation in customer-facing technologies for Ryder's supply chain and dedicated transportation businesses.

title: Self-driving tech firm Aurora mulls sale to Apple or Microsoft - Bloomberg News
summary: Aurora Innovation Inc CEO Chris Urmson is exploring strategic options for the self-driving tech company, including a potential sale to Apple or Microsoft, to navigate challenging market conditions. Other considerations include cost cuts, going private, spinning off assets, and acquiring smaller companies. The company's shares rose 15% on the news but have suffered significantly this year, and it recently delayed the delivery of its autonomous freight trucks.

title: Herbalife Scores With Galaxy Deal
summary: Herbalife Nutrition Ltd. has renewed its long-term partnership with the LA Galaxy in a multimillion-dollar deal, highlighting the growing value of sports sponsorships. This extension makes it the longest partnership in Major League Soccer history, with Herbalife continuing as the official jersey sponsor, presenting sponsor, and exclusive nutrition partner. The deal reflects the increasing recognition of sports sponsorships as an effective advertising strategy, especially with the rising popularity of MLS.

title: 7 Growth Stocks That Could 10X by 2027
summary: This article identifies seven growth stocks that have the potential to appreciate tenfold by 2027, despite some experiencing declines in 2022. The author selected these stocks based on strong analyst ratings and their performance in previous years, aiming to provide a diversified list from various MarketWatch categories. Each stock, including Nvidia, Dick's Sporting Goods, Perficient, Moderna, Apple, O'Reilly Automotive, and Cross Country Healthcare, is analyzed for its growth prospects, financial health, and analyst sentiment.

title: Donald Trump’s Media Company Faces Setback As SPAC Seeks Approval To Extend Time To Complete Deal
summary: Digital World Acquisition Corp. (DWAC), the SPAC set to merge with Donald Trump's TMTG, is struggling to gain enough shareholder support to extend its deadline to complete the deal. If 65% of shareholders don't approve the extension by Thursday, the SPAC may be forced to liquidate, hindering Trump Media and Technology Group's financial plans. The deal has been complicated by a federal probe and Truth Social's recent ban from the Google Play store due to content moderation violations.

title: News | Back to the Office: Employers Push for a Post-Labor Day Return
summary: Following Labor Day, employers across various sectors, including tech and finance, are intensifying efforts to bring employees back to the office, moving past the pandemic's impact on workplace norms. Companies are employing diverse strategies, from strict mandates to appealing new office amenities, to encourage hybrid or full-time in-person work. This shift occurs despite a strengthened employee preference for remote work and a tight labor market, highlighting a complex balancing act between company requirements and employee flexibility.

title: Apple to appeal Brazil sales ban of iPhone without charger
summary: Apple Inc. plans to appeal a Brazilian order that bans the sale of iPhones without a battery charger, issued after the Justice Ministry fined Apple and deemed the product incomplete. The ministry rejected Apple's environmental carbon reduction claims, stating there was no evidence to support them. Apple maintains customer awareness of charging options and has previously won court rulings on this issue in Brazil.

title: Apple Unveils New iPhones & Watches
summary: This article from the National Review announces Apple's fall product event, where the company unveiled new models of its iPhone and Apple Watch lineup. The content provided is minimal, serving primarily as a placeholder or headline for a more detailed report.

title: Sales Per Square Foot: Retail Calculations & Metrics (2024)
summary: This article explains the retail metric "sales per square foot," detailing its calculation and importance for brick-and-mortar stores. It covers how this metric helps measure store performance, inform merchandising decisions, analyze staff productivity, and identify sales trends over time. The article also discusses the limitations of sales per square foot, particularly in the age of omnichannel retail, and provides strategies to increase it, such as improving store layout, building loyalty programs, and leveraging online audiences.

title: Sony Music Fully Abandons Operations in Russia Amid Ongoing Ukraine War
summary: Sony Music has announced its complete withdrawal from Russia, six months after first suspending operations due to the invasion of Ukraine. The company cited the devastating humanitarian impact of the war and increasing sanctions as reasons for transferring its Russian entity and local roster to local management. Other major labels like Warner Music Group and Universal Music Group, along with various music-related organizations, have also suspended or ceased operations in Russia.

title: Apple’s iPhone 14 launch sets off competing wireless promotions
summary: The launch of Apple's iPhone 14 has ignited fierce promotional competition among major U.S. wireless carriers, including AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon. These promotions involve free phones, discounts, and bundled services like Apple One to attract and retain customers. The competitive landscape is impacting carrier subscriber growth and profit margins as they vie for market share.

title: Union groups, investors seek worker rights review at Apple
summary: Labor groups and investors have filed a shareholder proposal requesting Apple Inc. to review its worker rights practices, citing a potential "misalignment" between Apple's public commitments and alleged anti-union activities. This proposal comes amidst growing unionization efforts at Apple retail stores and builds on previous successful shareholder resolutions concerning civil rights audits and non-disclosure agreements.

title: Vinci Brands Announces kate spade new york and Coach Branded Protective Cases for iPhone 14 Devices
summary: Vinci Brands has launched new protective cases for the iPhone 14 series under their licensed brands, kate spade new york and Coach. These fashion-forward cases emphasize sustainability, crafted from recycled plastic with features like drop protection, scratch resistance, and antimicrobial properties. The collections offer a variety of designs, including MagSafe compatible options, appealing to consumers seeking both style and device protection.

title: Netflix looks to control cloud computing costs with AWS - WSJ
summary: Netflix is reportedly working to control its rising cloud computing costs with Amazon Web Services as part of a broader effort to reduce expenses. The streaming giant is also hiring more junior staff, reducing its real-estate footprint, and minimizing data storage globally following subscriber losses and increased competition. These measures aim to help Netflix manage costs and achieve its goal of 500 million global subscribers in the next three years.

title: How to Buy NASDAQ Stocks
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy NASDAQ stocks, detailing what the NASDAQ is, who owns its shares, and the pros and cons of investing in them. It covers the steps to purchase NASDAQ stocks, from fixing a budget and choosing a broker to placing an order, and includes a brief history of the exchange. The article also mentions specific index funds and prominent companies listed on the NASDAQ.

title: Apple lovers in some Asian countries to pay more for iPhone 14
summary: Apple Inc. has maintained iPhone 14 prices in the United States but increased them in select Asian countries, such as Japan, due to local currency depreciation against the dollar. While Japan saw a significant price hike reflecting a 24% yen slump, China maintained its iPhone 14 pricing despite a 7% currency drop, although analysts anticipate weakening demand there due to COVID-19 lockdowns affecting consumer spending. The dollar's strength is identified as a major risk to Apple's earnings.

title: CORSAIR K100 AIR wireless mechanical keyboard is shockingly thin
summary: The CORSAIR K100 AIR is a new, shockingly thin wireless mechanical keyboard that combines premium mechanical keyswitches with a super slim design, measuring just 11mm at its slimmest point. It offers versatile connectivity options including Bluetooth, Slipstream Wireless, and wired USB-C with 8,000Hz hyper-polling for responsive gaming. The keyboard also features RGB backlighting, 8MB of onboard storage, and a long-lasting battery, and is expected to be available on October 8, 2022.

title: Vodafone launches the Phone Buy-Back Guarantee with the release of the brand new Apple iPhone 14
summary: Vodafone has introduced a new Phone Buy-Back Guarantee scheme alongside the launch of the Apple iPhone 14 series. This unique UK offer allows customers to sell their iPhone 14 back to Vodafone for a guaranteed price next year, with amounts up to £871 depending on the model, facilitating earlier upgrades. The scheme also complements Vodafone's trade-in program and aims to provide an affordable way for customers to access the latest technology.

title: Apple to be largest user of Globalstar’s satellite network for iPhone messaging
summary: Apple announced a partnership with Globalstar to provide satellite messaging services for its new iPhone 14 series, making Apple Globalstar’s largest customer. The "Emergency SOS" feature will allow iPhone users to send messages via satellite when out of terrestrial network coverage, with services starting in November in the U.S. and Canada. Globalstar revealed that Apple will fund 95% of the costs for a new generation of satellites and will allocate 85% of its network capacity to Apple's service.

title: Apple asks appeals court to overturn $502 mln verdict in VPN patent case
summary: Apple is asking a U.S. appeals court to overturn a $502 million jury verdict in a patent infringement case brought by VirnetX Inc concerning VPN technology. The dispute has lasted 12 years and involved multiple trials and appeals. Both companies acknowledge that the outcome could hinge on whether the Patent Trial and Appeal Board's rulings invalidating VirnetX's patents are upheld.

title: Musk says SpaceX discussed iPhone satellite service with Apple
summary: Elon Musk revealed that SpaceX has engaged in discussions with Apple Inc. regarding the integration of Starlink's satellite connectivity into iPhones. This announcement follows Apple's recent partnership with Globalstar Inc. for satellite services on its latest iPhone model, indicating a growing trend in offering satellite-based communication for smartphones. Musk emphasized that dedicated technology is crucial for optimal satellite-to-cell phone service.

title: Here are the iPhone 14 deals from AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon as preorders begin
summary: Preorders for Apple's new iPhone 14 lineup are set to begin on Friday, September 9th, at 8 a.m. Eastern time. The article announces the start of preorders for the newly released iPhone 14 models from Apple, highlighting camera improvements across all four new phones and the faster A16 processor in the Pro models, along with a redesigned top area. It serves as an announcement for consumers looking to purchase the latest iPhone models from carriers like AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon.

title: Google, Apple facing anti-competitive complaint in Mexico
summary: Apple and Google are facing an anti-competitive complaint in Mexico filed by former telecommunications chief Mony de Swaan Addati. The complaint alleges that both companies exploit their app store monopolies by forcing the use of their proprietary payment systems for in-app purchases, charging commissions of 15%-20%, and thereby inflating prices. De Swaan Addati hopes Mexico's telecommunications regulator (IFT) will investigate and address these practices.

title: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games Announce unVEIL the world, a Tactical Hero Summoning RPG for iOS and Android
summary: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games announced "unVEIL the world," a new tactical hero summoning RPG for iOS and Android, at Tokyo Game Show 2022. The game features an epic story centered around a gigantic tower, with character designs by Posuka Demizu, known for "The Promised Neverland." This collaboration aims to deliver an unparalleled gaming experience by combining Shueisha's content creation expertise with NetEase Games' development strengths.

title: The Scholastic Art & Writing Awards Open Entries as the Program Celebrates 100 Years of Recognizing The Nation's Creative Teens
summary: The Scholastic Art & Writing Awards have opened entries for their 100th annual program, inviting students in grades 7-12 from the U.S. and Canada to submit original works. In celebration of its centennial, the program, administered by the Alliance for Young Artists & Writers, announced nearly half a million dollars in scholarships, including increased portfolio awards and the inaugural Dav Pilkey Awards. These awards will recognize 12 high school seniors and their educators for outstanding portfolios, continuing the program's legacy of fostering young creative talent.

title: Paychex Time by Paychex, Inc
summary: Paychex Time is a mobile application for previously registered Paychex Flex users, allowing them to punch in and out quickly from their mobile devices. The app integrates with Paychex Flex Time or Flex Time and Labor Online subscriptions, offering features like a "Stay Logged In" option for quick access. It also includes an Apple Watch app for added convenience.

title: Tractor Giant John Deere Eyes 10% of Revenue from Software Fees
summary: John Deere aims for software fees to constitute 10% of its annual revenue by 2030, leveraging smart technology in its farm equipment. The company has invested in AI and autonomous capabilities through acquisitions, connecting 1.5 million machines to its cloud-based Operations Center for data collection. This strategic shift is driven by the high gross margins of farming software compared to equipment sales, though it faces concerns from farmers regarding fiscal independence and repair restrictions.

title: Apple’s Latest Products and Services Are About Loyalty—to Apple
summary: Apple recently unveiled its new iPhone 14 line, Apple Watch Ultra, and AirPods, with the overarching theme being to further integrate users into the Apple ecosystem. The company aims to enhance user loyalty through these product and service updates. The article also mentions a notable past debate between CEO Tim Cook and Steve Jobs.

title: Can the West wean itself off Taiwan’s semiconductors?
summary: The article discusses the West's reliance on Taiwan, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), for semiconductors and the efforts by the US and EU to onshore chip production due to geopolitical tensions with China. Despite significant investments like the US Chips Act and the European Chips Act, experts believe completely weaning off Taiwanese semiconductors would be costly and time-consuming, possibly creating new supply chain problems. The piece also highlights the complex nature of the global semiconductor supply chain, as US-made chips have been found in Russian weaponry despite sanctions.

title: Plenty of Robinhood Stocks Found in These ETFs
summary: The article discusses how many popular stocks among Robinhood users are also found in prominent ETFs like the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Robinhood's newly introduced Robinhood Investor Index, which tracks the top 100 stocks in client portfolios, reveals that a significant portion consists of large-cap names and stocks in the communication services, consumer discretionary, and technology sectors, aligning with the holdings of QQQ and QQQM. This suggests Robinhood users may be more practical in their investment choices than their "meme stock" reputation implies.

title: Graphjet Technology, the State-of-the-Art Graphene and Graphite Producer, Announces Membership Agreement with the MIT Industrial Liaison Program
summary: Graphjet Technology Sdn. Bhd., a Malaysian graphene and graphite producer, has joined the Massachusetts Institute of Technology's Industrial Liaison Program (MIT ILP), becoming the first Malaysian company to do so. This membership will provide Graphjet with access to MIT's expertise, research, and educational opportunities, aiming to advance its technology development and strengthen its position in the industry. Through this collaboration, Graphjet seeks to connect with leading companies and talents to apply new technologies, particularly in areas like big data, automobiles, manufacturing, and healthcare.

title: Tech stocks crushed in market selloff
summary: The six largest U.S. tech companies collectively lost over $500 billion in value following an unexpectedly high August inflation report. The Nasdaq Composite experienced its steepest drop since June 2020, sinking 5.16%. Major companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Nvidia saw significant declines, with the report potentially influencing further aggressive interest rate hikes from the Fed.

title: Company created by two South Africans tops S&P 500 30-year performance
summary: Monster Beverage Corporation, co-founded by two South African WITS graduates, has ranked as the top-performing stock in the S&P 500 over the last 30 years, achieving a staggering 213,088% total return. The company, initially Hansen Natural Corporation, was acquired in 1990 by Rodney Sacks and Hilton Schlosberg and saw significant growth after launching Monster Energy in 2002. This success story highlights the remarkable financial performance of the energy drink giant, even outperforming tech titans like Amazon, Apple, and Nvidia.

title: The Only Stock Warren Buffett Has Bought in Each of the Last 2 Bear Markets
summary: During recent bear markets, Warren Buffett has strategically purchased shares of numerous companies, primarily focusing on energy stocks like Chevron and Occidental Petroleum, and technology giants such as Apple and HP. However, the one constant investment across both the March 2020 coronavirus crash and the current bear market is Berkshire Hathaway itself. Buffett has overseen significant share repurchases of his own company, indicating his strong belief in its intrinsic value and long-term prospects.

title: Microsoft patches a new zero-day affecting all versions of Windows
summary: Microsoft has issued security patches for a zero-day vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2022-37969, impacting all supported Windows versions, including Windows 11 and earlier, and Windows Server. This elevation of privilege flaw, which allows attackers to gain system privileges, has been exploited in real-world attacks. Additionally, Microsoft addressed another zero-day, CVE-2022-23960, a cache speculation vulnerability affecting Windows 11 for ARM-based systems, as part of its monthly Patch Tuesday release.

title: Racing Archives
summary: The Racing Archives features recent NASCAR news, including Sheldon Creed's first career O'Reilly Series win and Kyle Busch's third consecutive Atlanta victory in the Craftsman Truck Series. It also highlights Colin Braun's return to NASCAR after 15 years and other updates from the racing world.

title: Apple Watch Ultra vs Garmin Fenix 7: which rugged wearable is best?
summary: This article compares the Apple Watch Ultra and Garmin Fenix 7, two rugged smartwatches designed for outdoor enthusiasts. It delves into their differences in pricing, tech specs, design, display, health and fitness tracking, software, and battery life, aiming to determine which device is superior for adventurers. While the Apple Watch Ultra offers a smarter experience with a vibrant OLED screen and dive capabilities, the Garmin Fenix 7 excels in battery life and extensive activity-specific metrics, making the choice dependent on individual priorities.

title: Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF
summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF (EMPW), noting it is no longer active. It covers key financial data, including its expense ratio, inception date, AUM, and historical trading data. The profile also lists its top holdings, concentration analysis, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposures, and technical indicators, offering a comprehensive overview for investors.

title: OTT Platform DISNEY+ HOTSTAR now supports Dolby Atmos on compatible TVs, AVRs, soundbars, Android smartphones and iOS devices
summary: Disney+ Hotstar has expanded its audio support to include Dolby Atmos for its users, delivering a multidimensional, spatial sound experience on compatible devices. This new feature, complementing existing Dolby Vision support, aims to immerse users more deeply in their favorite movies and shows. A wide range of titles, including Hotstar Specials and Multiplex content, are now available with Dolby Atmos.

title: Strive 500 ETF (STRV) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Strive 500 ETF (STRV), including its current price, holdings, and news. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, expense ratio, top holdings like NVIDIA and Apple, and recent news headlines related to its performance and capital attraction. The piece serves as a comprehensive resource for potential investors interested in the STRV ETF.

title: The Business of Formula 1
summary: Formula 1 is a global multi-billion dollar sport, attracting sponsorships from major brands and boasting a vast audience. This article explores F1's business model, including its ownership by Liberty Media, key revenue streams from hosting fees, broadcasting, and sponsorships, and highlights its highest-earning drivers and team owners. It delves into the financial powerhouses and individual wealth within the world of F1.

title: First It Was Health Monitoring Devices on Wrists, Now It’s Health Monitoring on Fingers
summary: The article discusses the emergence of smart rings for health monitoring, highlighting Movano, Inc.'s new Movano Ring and the existing Oura Ring. These devices offer miniaturized diagnostic capabilities, tracking various health metrics like heart rate, temperature, and sleep. Movano is seeking FDA clearance and aims to eventually add cuffless blood pressure and blood glucose monitoring.

title: New iPhones have Qualcomm satellite modem, new Apple radio chips
summary: Apple's new iPhone 14 models incorporate a Qualcomm X65 modem chip, which enables satellite connectivity for emergency messaging. While the Qualcomm chip provides the core satellite communication capability, Apple clarified that the iPhone 14 also contains proprietary custom-designed radio frequency components and software specifically developed by Apple for its Emergency SOS via satellite feature. This new feature allows users to send emergency messages when outside of WiFi or cellular service, utilizing Globalstar's satellite network capacity.

title: Why EVs need a 12V battery (Advance Auto Parts interview)
summary: Electric vehicles (EVs) require a 12V battery in addition to their larger lithium-ion battery, similar to internal combustion engine vehicles. This article explores the critical role of the 12V battery in EVs, discussing its function and how it differs from 12V batteries in traditional cars. Allison Lentz Bubar, SVP of marketing & DIT strategy at Advance Auto Parts, provides insights into these aspects in an interview.

title: Apple Will Hike App Store Prices from Europe to Asia Next Month
summary: Apple Inc. is increasing App Store prices for apps and in-app purchases across Europe and Asia, effective October 5. This move is primarily to protect its margins due to the strong US dollar against other major currencies. The price hikes will affect countries using the euro, as well as Sweden, Japan, South Korea, Chile, Egypt, Malaysia, Pakistan, and Vietnam, with Vietnam's increase also tied to new local tax regulations.

title: S&P 500 Buybacks Decline 21.8% as Financials Pull Back; 12-Months Buybacks Pass $1 Trillion for the First Time
summary: S&P 500 stock buybacks decreased by 21.8% in Q2 2022 to $219.6 billion from Q1's record $281.0 billion, primarily due to a pullback from Financials and Health Care sectors. Despite the quarterly decline, the 12-month expenditure ending June 2022 surpassed $1 trillion for the first time, reaching $1.005 trillion, a 65.0% increase from the prior year. An anticipated 1% excise tax on net buybacks in 2023 is not expected to significantly impact overall buyback activity in 2023, though some 2023 purchases may be accelerated into Q4 2022.

title: Spotify Offers Audiobook Service With 300,000 Titles
summary: Spotify is expanding its services beyond music and podcasts by launching an audiobook offering with over 300,000 titles. Users will be able to purchase audiobooks on a pay-per-download basis, similar to other major audiobook providers. Initially, recommendations will be editorially curated, with algorithmic recommendations based on user listening habits planned for the future.
authors: Anne Steele and Jeffrey A. Trachtenberg

title: Strive launches Strive 500 ETF
summary: Strive Asset Management has introduced its second index fund, the Strive 500 ETF (NYSE: STRV), designed to mirror the Solactive GBS United States 500 Index. The ETF aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure to US corporations and seeks to unlock investor value through pro-fiduciary shareholder engagement, moving companies to prioritize value maximization over other agendas. Strive emphasizes its commitment to clients' financial interests, independent of social or political objectives.

title: Apple Pay llegaría a fines de octubre a Chile
summary: Apple Pay is expected to launch in Chile by the end of October, according to financial industry executives and various indications. Several banks, including Banco de Chile, Bci, Banco Falabella, Itaú, BICE, Scotiabank, and Santander, are preparing for its arrival. The delay has been attributed to commercial differences, particularly Apple's demand for a share of transaction commissions, and technical implementation challenges related to Chile's evolving payment system and POS terminal requirements.

title: Lumentum and Coherent carve up VCSEL market
summary: Lumentum and Coherent now dominate the market for VCSELs, collectively holding 80% market share according to a Yole Intelligence report. Their dominance is largely attributed to close ties with Apple and strategic acquisitions, as VCSEL technology, initially expected to proliferate widely, has primarily seen sustained adoption in Apple iPhones. The VCSEL market is projected to reach $4 billion by 2027, driven by datacom and growing automotive applications.

title: Western Union’s transformation: From traditional wires to digital wallets in Latin America
summary: Western Union is expanding its digital wallet services into Latin America, focusing initially on Brazil, a strategically important market. The company acquired Brazilian digital wallet provider Te Enviei to accelerate its market penetration and offer an integrated digital banking and money transfer solution. This move is part of Western Union's broader strategy to modernize its operations and shift from a transactional business to a customer-centric, relationship-based approach in response to global digital financial trends.

title: Herbalife Nutrition Survey Reveals Top Ten Modern Day Entrepreneurs and What Traits are Needed to Join the List
summary: A Herbalife Nutrition survey identified Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, and Steve Jobs as the top three most influential modern entrepreneurs, followed by Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg. The study, involving 2,750 respondents across the U.S., Mexico, and Israel, also explored traits essential for entrepreneurial success, highlighting creativity and intelligence as most important. Herbalife Nutrition emphasized the ongoing strength of the entrepreneurial spirit and the company's commitment to fostering it.

title: Apple Dominates Global Revenue for ‘Earwear’, Thanks to AirPods
summary: Apple has become the global leader in "earwear" revenue, largely due to its AirPods, despite not reporting specific sales figures for the product. AirPods contribute significantly to Apple's "Wearables, Home and Accessories" category, which has seen substantial growth. This success is attributed to the seamless integration of AirPods with Apple devices and features like Find My, making them a popular choice for iPhone users.

title: Apple Music to Replace Pepsi as Sponsor of NFL’s Super Bowl Halftime Show
summary: Apple Music has signed a multiyear deal with the NFL to become the new sponsor of the Super Bowl halftime show, replacing PepsiCo Inc. The NFL announced the deal on Friday. The halftime show is a highly-watched, elaborate performance featuring popular musical acts, and Pepsi had been the sponsor for a decade, with its deal estimated at $2.3 billion in 2011.

title: Shrugging off crypto winter, Galaxy Digital’s Mike Novogratz calls inaugural NFT launch ‘building for the future’
summary: Despite the ongoing "crypto winter" and declining NFT sales, Galaxy Digital CEO Mike Novogratz remains bullish on NFTs, launching the company's inaugural collection of 3,210 digital collectibles. The NFTs, created in partnership with Time's Web3 initiative TIMEPieces, will be airdropped for free to employees and partners, with Novogratz emphasizing it as "building for the future" and a way to foster community spirit rather than a financial endeavor. He points to corporate adoption of NFTs by companies like Nike and the NBA as evidence of their long-term viability.

title: Apple Begins Making iPhone 14 in India Weeks Ahead of Schedule
summary: Apple has started manufacturing its new iPhone 14 in India earlier than expected, significantly reducing the production lag between China and India from months to mere weeks. This move aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify its supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions and disruptions in China, while also supporting India's ambition to become a major technology manufacturing hub. The accelerated timeline is a result of resolving supply chain issues and collaboration with manufacturing partner Foxconn.

title: Peloton losing global marketing head to AutoDesk
summary: Peloton's senior vice president and global head of marketing, communications, and membership, Dara Treseder, is leaving the company to become the chief marketing officer of AutoDesk, effective October 14. This departure follows a series of other executive resignations at Peloton and comes as the company is undergoing an $800 million restructuring plan to address significant financial losses and declining revenue. Treseder was instrumental in growing Peloton's membership base during her tenure.

title: Peloton marketing, comms chief Dara Treseder joins Autodesk
summary: Dara Treseder, formerly Peloton's SVP and global head of marketing, comms, and membership, has been appointed Chief Marketing Officer at Autodesk, effective October 14. She will be responsible for Autodesk's global marketing strategy, overseeing worldwide marketing, brand and communications, global demand generation, and education business teams. Treseder's appointment comes as Autodesk reported a 17% increase in revenue to $1.2 billion in Q2 of its 2023 fiscal year.

title: Assurant (AIZ) Boosts Mobile Device Protection With T-Mobile
summary: Assurant (AIZ) has renewed its multiyear partnership with T-Mobile, aiming to enhance mobile device protection solutions through its Global Lifestyle segment. This agreement is expected to expand service and support options, including integrating Assurant's Cell Phone Repair (CPR) network for T-Mobile's device repair program starting in October. The extension reinforces Assurant's strategy to grow its Connected Living platform and follows similar partnerships with other major brands.

title: 삼성자산운용, 월배당 'DIVO ETF' 한국판 출시
summary: 삼성자산운용이 미국 운용사 앰플리파이의 월배당 커버드콜 ETF인 'DIVO'의 한국판 상품을 출시한다. 이 상품은 'KODEX 미국 배당 프리미엄 액티브 ETF'로, 미국 우량 배당 성장주와 대표 성장주에 투자하며 주식 배당과 콜옵션 매도를 통해 월 배당 재원을 마련한다. 특히 개별 주식에 대한 전략적인 콜옵션 매도와 자산의 주가 상승에 따른 자본 이익 추구하는 액티브 전략이 특징이다.

title: Apple and Globalstar Deal May Signal Future of Personal Satellite Communications
summary: Apple's new iPhone 14 features satellite connectivity through a deal with Globalstar, initially for emergency distress messages. This partnership highlights a burgeoning market for smartphone-to-satellite communications, with companies like Lynk, AST SpaceMobile, and Starlink (with T-Mobile) also developing similar services. The article explores how this technology, while currently limited to basic functions like emergency SOS or text, represents a significant shift in personal communications and creates new opportunities for the space industry, including Canadian firms like MDA.

title: Vornado Realty Trust, Ironworkers, Turner, Skanska and partners celebrate topping out of 88,000 s/f The Bustle at PENN 2
summary: Vornado Realty Trust and its partners, including Ironworkers, Turner, and Skanska, celebrated the topping out of "The Bustle" at PENN 2. This 88,000 s/f space will serve as a reimagined entrance to Penn Station, featuring a triple-height lobby, public art, and direct access to the station. The project emphasizes sustainability, with facade replacements reducing thermal transmission and carbon emissions, and will relocate PENN 2's entrance to the new 33rd St. Plaza.
