The article highlights Truth Social's struggles with user attraction, advertising, and internal issues, alongside two federal investigations that jeopardize its future funding.

title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

title: Moody's closing its consulting business in China, laying off staff: Report
summary: Moody's Corp is reportedly shutting down its consulting business, Moody's Analytics, in China and laying off over 100 employees across its Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen offices. This move comes as China's zero-Covid policy and subsequent lockdowns are causing many multinational firms to reconsider their operations in the country. However, Moody's credit rating business will continue to operate in China.

title: Opinion | How One of the Country’s Most Storied C.E.O.s Destroyed His Legacy (Published 2022)
summary: The article discusses Jack Welch's regret over his choice of successor at General Electric, highlighting his belief that he prioritized charm over competence. Author William D. Cohan explores Welch's complex legacy, acknowledging his leadership while also criticizing his succession process. The piece delves into the debate surrounding Welch's impact on GE, questioning whether his focus on short-term profits ultimately harmed the company.

title: Walmart's $228 50-inch Smart TV sold out in minutes, but you can go bigger
summary: Walmart's popular 50-inch 4K Roku Smart TV, originally priced at $228, quickly sold out during a Black Friday sale. However, shoppers can still find a deal on a larger 70-inch VIZIO TV for $448. The article highlights the benefits of 4K resolution and the Roku Smart TV's features, encouraging consumers to upgrade their viewing experience.

title: Taiwan’s TSMC eyeing advanced chips base in Arizona, founder says
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC is planning to produce chips with advanced 3-nanometre technology at its new Arizona factory, according to company founder Morris Chang. This second phase of development would follow the initial 5-nanometre production, reflecting a broader global awakening to the importance of advanced chip manufacturing. Both the US and Europe are offering incentives to attract chip production to their countries.

title: T-Mobile rolling out 4 lines, 4 iPhones, $100 a month plan
summary: T-Mobile is reintroducing a popular holiday deal offering four lines of unlimited talk, text, and 5G data on their Essentials plan for $100 per month. Additionally, customers can get four iPhone 14 smartphones "on Us" by adding a line and trading in an eligible device. This offer represents a significant saving compared to similar plans from competitors.

title: Sound United Parent Masimo Wins Big as Judge Finds Former Employee Stole Its Trade Secrets
summary: Masimo, parent company of Sound United, won a significant legal battle against a former employee, Dr. Marcelo Lamego, who was found to have stolen trade secrets and used them to develop a competitive product and for patent applications. This ruling strengthens Masimo's ongoing lawsuit against Apple, Inc., which involves similar allegations of stolen technology and patent infringement related to Apple Watch features. The same judge presides over both cases, putting Apple in a difficult position.

title: IPhone Pro models scarce in stores this holiday - Best Buy
summary: Best Buy announced that higher-end iPhone 14 Pro models will be in short supply this holiday season due to production issues caused by China's zero-COVID policy. This scarcity is expected to negatively impact sales for both Best Buy and Apple during the crucial Black Friday and holiday shopping period, potentially pushing some sales into the next quarter. Despite strong demand for premium smartphones, analysts estimate a significant drop in iPhone sales compared to the previous year.

title: Apple’s main iPhone plant boils over in violent protests against China’s tough COVID restrictions
summary: Hundreds of workers at Apple's main iPhone manufacturing plant in Zhengzhou, China, clashed with security personnel over unpaid wages and fears of spreading COVID infection, stemming from tough restrictions in place for nearly a month. The violence, which saw workers protesting and clashing with white-clad guards, highlights the increasing economic and supply chain pressures caused by China's strict Covid Zero policy. Foxconn, the plant operator, acknowledged complaints about "work subsidies" and is communicating with workers and the government to prevent further incidents, as the situation underscores Apple's risks with centralized production in China.

title: Samsung Secures 3-nm Advanced Chip Orders From NVIDIA, Qualcomm – Report
summary: Samsung Foundry has reportedly secured orders from major firms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, IBM, and Baidu to manufacture advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move, leveraging Samsung's gate-all-around (GAA) transistor technology developed with IBM, aims to position Samsung ahead of TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and diversify the semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions. Deliveries in large quantities are expected to begin in early 2024.

title: Berkshire Hathaway says Warren Buffett donates shares to family charities
summary: Warren Buffett has donated an additional 2.4 million Class B shares of Berkshire Hathaway, valued at approximately $759 million, to four family charities. This donation includes 1.5 million shares to the Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation and 900,000 shares split among foundations run by his children. Buffett has now donated over half of his Berkshire shares since 2006, with a significant portion previously going to the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, though no new Gates Foundation donation was disclosed with this filing.

title: HP Inc. stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
summary: HP Inc. stock (HPQ) rose 1.80% to $29.91 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which also saw gains in the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed $11.56 below its 52-week high and outperformed competitors like Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp., though International Business Machines Corp. saw a slight decline. Trading volume for HP Inc. was significantly higher than its 50-day average.

title: NASDAQ Trading - How to invest in NASDAQ (US100) index?
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on investing in the NASDAQ (US100) index, detailing its structure, how it works, and reasons for its high volatility. It explains different trading positions (long and short) and identifies opportune times for trading, such as during periods of market panic or significant company announcements. The article also touches upon the use of CFDs and ETFs for investing in NASDAQ.

title: What does Google’s ₹2,274 crore fine mean for Big Tech?
summary: The article discusses the implications of the Competition Commission of India (CCI) fining Google ₹2,274 crore for anti-competitive practices, specifically regarding its Play Store and payment systems. It argues that such fines globally could signal the end of the unchecked dominance of major tech companies like Google, Apple, and Amazon, forcing them to re-evaluate their impact on innovation and society. The author suggests these regulations might ultimately save these companies from their own harmful habits.

title: Foxconn apologizes for pay dispute that sparked factory protests in China
summary: Foxconn, the iPhone assembler, issued an apology for a pay dispute that led to employee protests at its Zhengzhou factory. The company attributed the issue to a "technical error" in its hiring system and promised to honor the agreed-upon wages. Apple is also reviewing the situation to ensure employee concerns are addressed amidst China's "zero-COVID" strategy and related factory disruptions.

title: Black Friday on track to top $9 billion in online sales for first time
summary: Online sales for Black Friday were expected to reach a record $9 billion, potentially hitting $9.2 billion, despite recession worries and inflation. Adobe Analytics reported $7.28 billion in sales by 6 p.m. Eastern, with popular items including Apple smartwatches, AirPods, Funko Pop! figurines, and Pokémon videogames. This marks the first time Black Friday online sales were projected to exceed $9 billion.

title: Apple's interest in Manchester United would mark a major escalation in the ‘streaming war’
summary: Reports that Apple is interested in buying Manchester United could signal a significant shift in the streaming wars. Apple TV, currently a minor player, could leverage football's massive global audience to become a major streaming power. Acquiring a club like Manchester United would give Apple a crucial negotiating position for future broadcast rights, combining commercial opportunities with strategic content acquisition in the highly competitive streaming market.

title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF DFAC
summary: This article provides details on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC), including its current price, key metrics, and how to invest in it through eToro. DFAC aims to replicate the performance of the Russell 3000 Index and holds investments in over 2,500 US companies across various sectors, with major holdings like Apple and Microsoft. The ETF, established in 2007 by Dimensional Fund Advisors, had net assets of $14.7 billion as of September 2022.

title: Baxter overpaid for Hillrom • Sale of Weiss Memorial, West Suburban moves forward
summary: A recently issued report confirms that Baxter International overpaid for its Hillrom acquisition, leading to a $3.1 billion impairment charge. Meanwhile, the sale of Weiss Memorial and West Suburban Medical Center is progressing, with Resilience Healthcare expected to take over operations. Additionally, the article highlights a rare lung-liver transplant performed at Northwestern Medicine and Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul's call for Apple to enhance reproductive health data privacy.

title: iPhone 15 might feature "no buttons" - AppleMagazine
summary: Apple is rumored to be planning to remove physical buttons from its iPhone 15 by 2023, possibly using solid-state haptic buttons instead. This move, supported by reports from Cirrus Logic and analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, could lead to cost savings, improved water resistance, and a sleeker design for future iPhones. The technology would function similarly to the Touch ID home button on older iPhone models.

title: Hawley Demands Apple Condemn CCP COVID Crackdown, Reduce Reliance on Foreign Labor
summary: U.S. Senator Josh Hawley sent a letter to Apple CEO Tim Cook urging the company to reduce its dependency on foreign labor in China and reshore production to the United States. Hawley also pressed Apple to explain its decision to limit the iPhone AirDrop feature in China, which has been used by anti-CCP protestors, and to address reports about potentially removing Twitter from the App Store. The senator questioned Apple's silence on the treatment of workers in China and sought details on the company's risk assessment and supply chain diversification plans.

title: Fubo Sports Network Now Streaming on Amazon Freevee
summary: Fubo Sports Network, the ad-supported TV network from FuboTV, has launched on Amazon Freevee, expanding its reach to over 155 million devices across various streaming platforms. The network, which reported its highest viewership in September 2022, now features original programming like “Airing It Out” with Housh and Scandrick and “The Young Person Basketball Podcast” with R.J. Hampton. This launch aims to provide more athlete-driven content to a broader audience at no additional cost.

title: Lithium company CEO is ‘accelerating’ his U.S. investment plans, thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act
summary: Kent Masters, CEO of Albemarle Corp., a leading lithium provider, is accelerating the company's U.S. investment plans due to incentives in the Inflation Reduction Act. The act's provisions, which favor North American manufacturing and materials, are expected to drive volume and profitability for Albemarle. This move highlights how new policies are creating significant opportunities for companies at the forefront of evolving economies.

title: Android users in 12 US states cleared to sue Google Play
summary: A California judge has approved a class-action lawsuit against Google regarding alleged anticompetitive practices in its Play Store. The suit, involving Android users in 12 US states and territories who made app or in-app purchases since August 2016, claims Google maintains a monopoly and charges a 30 percent commission, passing inflated costs onto consumers. This case echoes previous antitrust litigation against both Google and Apple, with the same law firm, Hagens Berman, involved, suggesting significant financial implications for Google.

title: Apple TV's Lloyd Sam talks money — from a $50 dare to when he won’t pay extra
summary: Lloyd Sam, an Apple TV MLS commentator, shares his financial perspective in an interview with Ally's "Money Clips" series. Sam discusses his early savings habits which led to accidental real estate development, his current savings goals for a Range Rover, and his refusal to pay extra for first-class travel. He also reflects on financial lessons learned from his accountant father, emphasizing value and hard work.

title: President Biden coming to Arizona for semiconductor manufacturing plant visit
summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plant under construction in Phoenix, Arizona, on December 6. This visit follows Arizona securing the chip plant and aims to highlight how Biden's economic plan fosters a manufacturing boom, rebuilds supply chains, and creates jobs in the region. The TSMC plant is expected to create 1,600 direct jobs and thousands more among suppliers.

title: President Joe Biden to visit TSMC plant being built in north Phoenix
summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit Phoenix on December 6 to commemorate a key milestone, the "first tool-in," at the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) plant in north Phoenix. This marks his first presidential visit to Arizona and highlights the administration's focus on boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing through initiatives like the CHIPS and Science Act. The TSMC facility represents a significant $12 billion investment in Arizona, aiming to reduce the country's reliance on foreign microchip production.

title: Spotify, Epic Games get ally in Musk in fight against Apple fees
summary: Elon Musk has joined Spotify and Epic Games in criticizing Apple's 30% App Store fees, even suggesting Apple threatened to remove Twitter from its store. This stance strengthens the position of companies previously involved in antitrust complaints and lawsuits against Apple over its in-app purchase policies. Analysts are concerned that Musk's battle with Apple could deter Twitter users, as people are unlikely to abandon their iPhones.

title: Spotify CEO renews attack on Apple after Musk's salvo
summary: Spotify's CEO Daniel Ek criticized Apple in a series of tweets, accusing the iPhone maker of stifling innovation and hurting consumers by giving itself every advantage. This attack follows similar criticism from Elon Musk regarding Apple's in-app purchase fees. Spotify has previously filed antitrust complaints against Apple over its 30% charge, which it claims artificially inflates its prices.

title: Vodafone Picks Dubber for UK and European Business Strategy
summary: Vodafone Group PLC has partnered with Dubber, a Melbourne-based conversational intelligence and unified recording company, to deliver direct recording and AI services across its mobile networks for business customers in the UK and Europe, starting with Germany. This collaboration will leverage Dubber's platform to provide enhanced compliance and data insights across mobile and UC services, including RingCentral, Cisco Webex, and Microsoft Teams. The initiative aims to expand Vodafone's existing recording subscriber base and generate accretive revenue for Dubber by offering automated, self-service next-generation recordings and personalized customer experiences.

title: TSMC to make higher-end chips in US: sources
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) plans to produce advanced 4-nanometer chips at its new Arizona plant when it opens in 2024, an upgrade from initial plans, following pressure from US customers like Apple. The company is also expected to commit to a second plant for even more advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move aligns with US efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though Taiwan intends to keep its latest technology at home.

title: Samsung Galaxy S23 likely to adopt unbreakable smartphone screen glass
summary: Corning Inc. has introduced "Gorilla Glass Victus 2," a new reinforced smartphone cover glass designed to withstand drops from one meter onto concrete. This new ultra-durable glass addresses the growing demand for stronger screens as smartphones become heavier and larger. It is highly anticipated that this technology will be featured in Samsung Electronics' upcoming Galaxy S23 series.

title: Casetify teams up with Barbie on tech collection
summary: Casetify has announced its first collaboration with Mattel's Barbie brand, launching on December 15. The limited-edition collection will feature a variety of customizable tech accessories, including phone cases for iPhones, Samsung, and Google Pixel, as well as accessories for AirPods, MagSafe wallets, and Apple Watch bands. Unique items include a Pin’It Case for Barbie charms and a Silicone Purse Case mimicking a Barbie doll bag, with prices ranging from 33 to 90 pounds / 38 to 98 US dollars.

title: 40+ Spotify statistics 2026: SPOT stock, revenue and performance
summary: This article compiles over 40 statistics about Spotify (NYSE: SPOT) as of late 2022, detailing its market dominance, user growth, financial performance, and stock behavior. It highlights Spotify's leading market share in music streaming, its revenue model primarily driven by premium subscriptions, and its significant user base despite competition from tech giants. The report also touches upon Spotify's stock performance, including its all-time high and low, alongside insights into artist earnings and global user demographics.

title: After 18 years at Corning, an inventor of Gorilla Glass brought his expertise to Penn State
summary: John Mauro, co-inventor of Gorilla Glass, joined Penn State six years ago after 18 years at Corning Inc., drawn by the university's research prowess and industrial collaborations. He aims to inspire students in materials science and is leading research on LionGlass, a sustainable alternative to traditional soda-lime silicate glass, which significantly reduces carbon emissions during production. Mauro is seeking industry partnerships to scale up and commercialize LionGlass to benefit both companies and the environment.

title: How suite of dividend growth ETFs brings quality to the fore
summary: In an environment of market volatility and economic uncertainty, CI WisdomTree ETFs offer a quality-focused core portfolio solution that has outperformed benchmarks. These dividend growth ETFs, managed by CI Global Asset Management, employ a unique methodology to identify companies with strong fundamentals, sustainable earnings, and resilient business models, screening for quality and growth metrics. This strategy aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure and long-term stability by avoiding overvalued companies and focusing on profitable dividend payers, proving particularly effective during periods of economic contraction.

title: Apple sues AliveCor over patents as Apple Watch import ban deadline looms
summary: Apple Inc. has sued medical-device maker AliveCor Inc., alleging that AliveCor's wearable heart monitors infringe on Apple's patent rights. This lawsuit comes as the U.S. International Trade Commission is poised to rule on whether to ban Apple Watch imports based on AliveCor's claims that Apple infringed its heart-monitoring patents. Apple contends that AliveCor's lawsuits are a response to its market failures and that Apple is the true pioneer of the underlying technology.

title: MercadoLibre files complaints against Apple for 'anti-competitive practices'
summary: MercadoLibre has filed antitrust complaints against Apple in Brazil and Mexico, accusing the tech giant of anti-competitive practices related to its app store. The e-commerce company alleges that Apple abuses its monopoly by imposing restrictions on the distribution of digital goods and in-app purchases, including requiring the use of Apple's own payment system and preventing redirection to third-party websites. MercadoLibre attempted to negotiate with Apple but ultimately decided to pursue legal action, while also noting that Google's app store policies are less restrictive.

title: Check out the Polaris Slingshot 2023 Lineup
summary: Polaris Slingshot has officially unveiled its 2023 lineup, emphasizing personalization and customization with new vibrant colorways and a host of accessory options. The release allows drivers to design their perfect ride with features like the RAM® X-Grip Phone Mount, an Excursion Rearview Mirror, and over 50 new color-matched accessories, alongside an enhanced infotainment system with Apple CarPlay®. The 2023 Slingshot models, including the S, S with Technology Package, SL, SLR, and R, offer various styling, performance, and comfort upgrades, with pricing detailed for each model.

title: How TSMC’s AZ semiconductor plants will benefit U.S.
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is investing $40 billion in Arizona for two semiconductor plants, with the second expected to open in 2026. This move is aimed at addressing national security concerns regarding the U.S.'s reliance on foreign-made advanced chips and is expected to create 4,500 direct jobs. The facilities will also incorporate environmentally friendly practices, including water recycling and sulfuric acid reclamation.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Plans to Build Second Fab at Phoenix Site
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has begun construction on a second semiconductor chip plant in Phoenix, investing a total of $40 billion across both facilities. The first fab is expected to be operational in 2024 with 4-nanometer technology, while the second will feature 3-nanometer technology by 2026. President Biden highlighted these projects as a significant step in bringing manufacturing back to the U.S. and strengthening the supply chain, supported by initiatives like the Chips and Science Act.

title: Apple Scales Back Self-Driving Car and Delays Debut Until 2026
summary: Apple has reportedly scaled back its ambitious self-driving car project, codenamed "Titan," and delayed its target launch date to 2026. The company is now aiming for a less ambitious design, moving away from a fully autonomous vehicle without a steering wheel or pedals, which executives deemed unfeasible with current technology. The goal is to sell a consumer model by 2026 for under $100,000.

title: TSMC triples Arizona chip plant investment, Biden hails project
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced it would more than triple its investment in its new Arizona plant to $40 billion, marking one of the largest foreign investments in U.S. history. President Joe Biden praised the project as a move to bring American manufacturing back and strengthen the U.S. supply chain for semiconductors. The expanded investment includes building a second facility for advanced 3-nanometer chips by 2026, aiming to create 13,000 high-tech jobs and address semiconductor shortages.

title: Sherman a contender for Finisar’s new $3 billion semiconductor project
summary: Sherman, Texas, is under consideration for a new $3 billion semiconductor wafer fabrication plant by Finisar, a key Apple supplier. The project would create 700 jobs and produce silicon wafers for electric vehicles. Sherman is competing with other company-owned sites globally, and its bid for tax breaks through a Chapter 313 agreement is crucial for its competitiveness, despite the program's impending expiration.

title: Apple is sued by women who say AirTag lets stalkers track victims
summary: Two women have filed a class-action lawsuit against Apple, alleging that its AirTag devices facilitate stalking. They claim Apple has failed to protect individuals from unwanted tracking, despite marketing the device as "stalker proof." The lawsuit seeks unspecified damages for iOS and Android users tracked by AirTags or at risk due to Apple's alleged negligence, citing cases where AirTags were linked to tracking and even murders.

title: Uber Launches Robotaxis but Driverless Fleet Is ‘Long Time’ Away
summary: Uber has launched its first robotaxi service in Las Vegas, partnering with Motional, an autonomous driving joint venture between Hyundai Motor Co. and Aptiv Plc. This move reaffirms Uber's commitment to self-driving technology despite a fading hype around autonomous vehicles and other companies scaling back their efforts. Uber's global head of autonomous mobility, Noah Zych, stated that a hybrid network of driverless cars and human drivers is expected for a very long time.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) shares rose by 0.24% to $88.46 on a day when the broader market saw declines, ending a four-day losing streak. The stock's performance outpaced major competitors like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet. Amazon closed significantly below its 52-week high, with trading volume also below its 50-day average.

title: Apple AirTags Have Become Weapon of Choice for Stalkers, Class Action Alleges
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple, alleging that its AirTags have become a "weapon of choice" for stalkers and abusers due to their "unparalleled accuracy," ease of use, and affordability. The lawsuit claims that despite Apple's assertion that the devices were "stalker proof," the company "heedlessly forged ahead" with their release, leading to numerous reports of people finding AirTags placed on their belongings. It highlights two instances of murder where AirTags were allegedly used to track victims and features accounts from two plaintiffs who were stalked using the devices.

title: Apple to be TSMC US fab top customer
summary: Apple Inc. is set to become the largest customer of TSMC's new factories in Arizona, as announced by Apple CEO Tim Cook. TSMC will invest $40 billion in the US fabs, including a second plant for more advanced 3-nanometer chips, with production slated to start in 2024 for 4-nanometer chips and 2026 for 3-nanometer chips. Other major chip companies like AMD and Qualcomm also plan to source chips from these new US facilities, highlighting the growing importance of domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Broadcom gives upbeat revenue forecast as server, data center demand booms
summary: Broadcom Inc. projected first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, indicating strong demand for its chips used in data centers and networking equipment. The company's focus on infrastructure for hybrid work models gives it an advantage over competitors. Broadcom also expects to benefit from the global 5G rollout and its venture into software.

title: Dexcom G7 Receives FDA Clearance: The Most Accurate Continuous Glucose Monitoring System Cleared in the U.S.
summary: Dexcom G7, the next-generation Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) System, has received FDA clearance for individuals with all types of diabetes aged two years and older. This new system offers enhanced accuracy, a smaller all-in-one wearable, and quicker sensor warmup time, building on the success of prior Dexcom CGM technology. Dexcom anticipates launching the G7 in the U.S. in early 2023, aiming to provide a more convenient and effective diabetes management solution.

title: TSMC Revenue Rises 50% in November Despite Covid Challenges
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported a 50% increase in revenue for November, reaching NT$222.7 billion ($7.3 billion). This growth occurred despite a challenging environment marked by declining consumer electronics demand and COVID-19 disruptions in China. TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker and exclusive supplier of Apple's Silicon chips, continues a strong sales streak amplified by pandemic-driven demand.

title: Corning Confronts R&D Hurdles
summary: Corning Incorporated is facing significant challenges due to a recent tax policy change requiring the amortization of R&D expenses over five years, making innovation more costly. This shift from full deductibility poses a serious financial burden, potentially costing Corning an additional $150 million in cash taxes in 2022 alone. The company is urging Congress to either reinstate full R&D deductibility or at least make it retroactive to 2022 and extend it through 2025 to preserve its ability to invest in growth and innovation.

title: Will TNT Walk Away From The NBA?
summary: Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav's recent comments questioning the necessity of the NBA for TNT have sparked industry speculation about the future of their 33-year partnership. As the NBA seeks significantly increased media rights—potentially $50 billion to $75 billion—from incumbents TNT and ESPN, the possibility of tech giants like Amazon entering the bidding process looms. While an NBA-TNT divorce seems unlikely due to their deep integration and the network's commitment to "Inside the NBA," the evolving media landscape and WBD's cost-cutting measures could lead to a reevaluation of the relationship, possibly involving a split package with streaming services.

title: Disqo names CFO
summary: Disqo, a customer experience intelligence platform, has appointed Scott Henderson as its new chief financial officer. Henderson will report to co-founder and chief executive Armen Adjemian and will oversee finance work, strategy, sales enablement, revenue operations, and business systems. He brings over 20 years of experience from Salesforce and other leadership roles at companies like Apple.

title: Consoles and Competition
summary: This article traces the tumultuous history of the video game console market, highlighting the shifting dynamics between hardware manufacturers and third-party developers, from Atari's integrated system to Sony's third-party focus and later emphasis on exclusives. It critically examines the FTC's attempt to block Microsoft's acquisition of Activision, arguing that the FTC's concerns fail to understand the industry's dynamic evolution and Microsoft's innovative subscription-based business model, which offers a competitive alternative to Sony's platform-locking exclusive strategy. The author suggests that the FTC is trying to preserve an outdated model of competition and is missing the real antitrust issues concerning dominant storefronts.

title: Music now has over 616 million paying streaming subscribers globally
summary: Global music streaming services had 616.2 million subscribers by mid-2022, an increase of 17.6% year-over-year, according to Midia Research. While growth slowed slightly compared to the previous year, notably due to macroeconomic pressures and the maturation of Western markets, the industry remains resilient with significant contributions from emerging markets like China. Spotify continues to lead the market, though its share has gradually declined as competitors like Tencent Music Entertainment, Amazon Music, and YouTube Music gain ground.

title: As Market Dynamics Pivot, Alpha Opportunities Are Set to Improve
summary: The U.S. equity market is shifting from an environment favoring passive strategies to one that is more conducive for active managers. Historically, during periods of extreme market concentration, low volatility, and low dispersion of returns, active managers often underperformed. However, as these extremes unwind—driven by factors like slowing economic growth, inflation, and rising interest rates—opportunities for active stock picking and alpha generation are expected to improve significantly.

title: IBM Remains Big Tech’s Disaster
summary: The article portrays IBM as a struggling legacy technology company that has failed to keep pace with its Big Tech counterparts. Despite its historical dominance, IBM missed critical opportunities in personal computing and cloud computing, leading to a significantly smaller market capitalization and lower revenue compared to industry leaders like Microsoft and Apple. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Hold" rating, with price targets below its current trading price, suggesting a bleak outlook for the company's future performance.

title: Sonos Trueplay Now Works With iPhone 14 Models
summary: Sonos has updated its Trueplay feature to support iPhone 14 models, including the iPhone 14, 14 Plus, 14 Pro, and 14 Pro Max. Trueplay uses the iPhone's microphone to analyze a room's acoustics and optimizes the sound output of Sonos speakers, a feature that was previously unavailable for the newest iPhone users since their launch. This over-the-air update now allows all Sonos products, except the Port, Connect, and Roam SL, to be fine-tuned using iPhone 14 devices.

title: ElectraMeccanica Celebrates Commissioning Of New Manufacturing Facility And Corporate Headquarters In Mesa
summary: ElectraMeccanica Vehicles Corp. celebrated the commissioning of its new manufacturing facility and corporate headquarters in Mesa, Arizona, marking its official establishment as an American OEM. The facility is expected to produce up to 20,000 vehicles annually and create 200-500 new jobs, bolstering Arizona's manufacturing industry. This move supports the production of their flagship all-electric SOLO and SOLO Cargo vehicles, with the company emphasizing the importance of partnerships with local and state governments for economic development.

title: SPDR Gender Diversity ETF Officially Changes Index, Name
summary: The SPDR MSCI USA Gender Diversity ETF (SHE) has officially changed its name and underlying index to the MSCI USA Gender Diversity Select Index, effective December 8. This ETF, a pioneer in gender-focused investing with $208.65 million in assets, now holds 281 stocks. The new index emphasizes companies committed to gender diversity across all organizational levels and rebalances quarterly.

title: System in Package Market Size, Industry Share, Forecast to 2034
summary: The System in Package (SiP) market is projected to grow significantly due to increasing demand for compact, internet-based electronics, IoT devices, 5G-connected devices, and the miniaturization of electronic components. Key drivers include the adoption of SiP technology in smart wearables, smartphones, gaming processors, and graphic cards. The Asia Pacific region is expected to lead this growth, primarily fueled by the consumer electronics industry.

title: SPDR Gender Diversity ETF Officially Changes Index, Name
summary: The SPDR MSCI USA Gender Diversity ETF (SHE) has officially changed its underlying index and name to the MSCI USA Gender Diversity Select Index. This ETF, a pioneer in gender-focused funds with $208.65 million in assets, now holds 281 stocks and seeks to identify companies committed to gender diversity across all organizational levels. The index is sector-agnostic, with holdings weighted by market capitalization and gender diversity scores.

title: Discovering diamonds in the rough
summary: The insurance industry is facing a significant talent shortage, with a large percentage of the current workforce set to retire and a lack of interest from younger generations. To address this, MetLife organized the Hack4Job 2022 Virtual Hackathon, a global initiative to identify and recruit tech talent. The event, supported by various national digital players, focused on critical tech areas and resulted in job offers for successful candidates, with plans to expand the hackathon globally.

title: Apple planning huge change as a result of European legal push, report says
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to allow alternative app stores on its iPhones and iPads in Europe, a significant shift in response to pending EU regulations aimed at promoting competition. This change would challenge Apple's long-standing proprietary App Store model, which currently takes a 30% commission on digital purchases. The move comes as governments globally push for alterations to the company's business practices.

title: Why Kodak Died and Fujifilm Thrived: A Tale of Two Film Companies
summary: This article analyzes why Kodak, once a giant in the film industry, ultimately failed, while its competitor Fujifilm managed to thrive despite the digital revolution. It attributes Kodak's downfall to its inability to diversify beyond the declining photo industry and its misjudgment of the digital market's profitability. In contrast, Fujifilm successfully leveraged its existing technologies and pursued aggressive diversification into new markets like pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and highly functional materials, demonstrating a more adventurous and adaptable corporate culture.

title: Z-Wave hopes open-source will matter to the IoT
summary: The Z-Wave Alliance has open-sourced its protocol and moved beyond a single silicon vendor to stay relevant in the IoT landscape, particularly against the growing momentum of Matter and Thread. While this could foster competition and reduce costs for Z-Wave chips, the challenge remains its ability to attract new chipmakers and adoption compared to newer, widely supported standards. Z-Wave-LR also presents a notable opportunity for growth in Industrial IoT and larger properties, offering long-range connectivity where Matter and Thread might fall short.

title: Australia takes aim at Apple, Microsoft over child protection online
summary: Australia's e-Safety Commissioner has accused Apple and Microsoft of not doing enough to combat child exploitation on their platforms. Following legal demands for information, the regulator found that neither company proactively screens for child abuse material in their cloud storage services, iCloud and OneDrive, nor do they detect live-streaming of child sexual abuse on video services like Skype, Microsoft Teams, and FaceTime. The Commissioner deemed their responses "alarming" and highlighted a "major step backwards" after Apple announced it would stop scanning iCloud accounts for child abuse.

title: Masimo links passive health monitors to home stereos amid legal row with Apple
summary: Masimo is expanding its passive health monitoring technology by integrating it with home entertainment systems via a software upgrade to Denon’s HEOS system and Masimo’s Home Health Hubs. This initiative allows personal health information to be shared with clinicians and comes amidst a legal dispute between Masimo and Apple over alleged theft of monitoring technology. The move also clarifies Masimo's recent acquisition of audio brands, as it aims to provide continuous health data (like oxygen saturation, pulse rate, and temperature) to users and facilitate telehealth sessions through existing home TV and audio setups.

title: Want to Attract Top Tech Talent? Prioritize Their Needs
summary: This article emphasizes that attracting and retaining top tech talent requires more than just competitive pay; companies must prioritize technologists' needs for growth, modern tools, and an inclusive work environment. It suggests strategies like clear communication during recruiting, investing in up-to-date technologies, and fostering a culture of acceptance and work-life quality. Ultimately, understanding and acting on these priorities will help businesses thrive in the competitive tech talent market.

title: Warner Bros Discovery Global Brands and Experiences Announces New Global Consumer Products Leadership Team
summary: Warner Bros Discovery Global Brands and Experiences (GBE) has announced a new global leadership team for its Consumer Products business, integrating the legacy Warner Bros. Consumer Products and Discovery Consumer Products divisions. This realignment aims to maximize long-term consumer engagement with the company’s major franchises and streamline global relationships. Pam Lifford, President of Warner Bros. Discovery Global Brands, Franchises, and Experiences, will oversee the new team, which includes Julian Moon, Robert Oberschelp, Preston Lewis, Samantha Bushy, and Peter van Roden in key leadership roles overseeing different regions and categories.

title: WisdomTree Launches Quality Growth ETF, QGRW
summary: WisdomTree Investments has launched a new quality growth ETF, the WisdomTree U.S. Quality Growth Fund (QGRW), which tracks a market-cap-weighted index focusing on growth and quality factors. This new offering aims to avoid speculative growth names while including firms with strong growth signals, similar to their dividend growth fund DGRW. QGRW has a 28 basis point fee and includes major companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon in its top holdings, starting with $1.3 million in AUM.

title: What Is HP Smart Friend a Complete Overview | HP® Tech Takes
summary: HP SmartFriend is a subscription-based tech support service offering 24/7 access to experts for troubleshooting, maintenance, and setup of tech devices from any brand. It provides various plans, including individual, family, and a one-time fee option, covering services like software installation, network troubleshooting, virus removal, and PC migration. The service aims to save users time and money compared to traditional repair shops by offering affordable, reliable, and accessible remote assistance.

title: Apple Scales Back High-End Mac Pro Plans, Weighs Production Move to Asia
summary: Apple is reportedly scaling back its plans for the high-end Mac Pro, which features Apple silicon, due to changes in its chip and manufacturing strategies. The new Mac Pro is behind schedule, and the company is considering moving its production to Asia. This news comes alongside updates on Apple potentially allowing third-party app stores and sideloading, the release of iOS 16.2, and Apple Music integration in Tesla cars.

title: As markets shift, big office campus near Apple won't expand until anchor tenant is landed
summary: A large office campus in North Austin, 7700 Parmer, which has housed major tech companies, will not expand its current 1 million square feet of office space until an anchor tenant is secured. The owner plans for a mixed-use future with additional office buildings, residential, and retail, but is waiting for market conditions to stabilize. This strategy avoids speculative development in the current economic environment.

title: NSO Pegasus iPhone Spyware Vulnerabilities Fixed by Apple - Detect & Prioritize Using VMDR for Mobile Devices
summary: Apple has released iOS and iPadOS 12.5.5 and 15.0 to address critical zero-day vulnerabilities, some actively exploited by NSO Pegasus iPhone spyware. Qualys recommends immediate updates and highlights four key vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution flaws in CoreGraphics, WebKit, XNU, and ImageIO. Qualys VMDR for Mobile Devices offers tools to discover unpatched assets and issue remote response actions to ensure timely updates.

title: Apple CIO steps down from Rackspace board citing new job duties
summary: Rackspace Technology announced that Apple CIO Tim Campos resigned from its board of directors, effective December 12, due to new job duties and potential conflicts of interest. This change comes amidst a series of management restructurings at Rackspace and their ongoing recovery from a recent ransomware attack on their Hosted Exchange business, which impacted thousands of small- and medium-sized businesses. The company is actively focusing on recovering impacted email data and transitioning affected customers to Microsoft 365, while also making other executive changes.

title: Webull Expands into Australia
summary: Webull has expanded its retail brokerage services into Australia, offering U.S.-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares through its Webull Securities (Australia) Pty. Ltd. subsidiary. This marks Webull's third market in the Asia-Pacific region, following launches in Hong Kong and Singapore. The company plans to add local Australian securities and Hong Kong-listed securities in 2023.

title: Sonos Roam Review: A Portable Speaker That Is More Than Meets the Eye
summary: The Sonos Roam is a highly portable and robust wireless speaker designed to integrate into the Sonos ecosystem, offering impressive sound quality for its small size. It features both Wi-Fi with AirPlay 2 and Bluetooth connectivity, seamless transition between the two, and automatic TruePlay tuning. While it excels as part of a Sonos system, it's noted that dedicated Bluetooth speakers might offer more value for those not within the Sonos ecosystem.

title: Why I can't stop paying the 'Sonos tax'
summary: The author, a long-time Sonos user, explains why he considers the money spent on Sonos products a "tax" he doesn't mind paying due to the exceptional product quality and multi-room audio experience. He details the Sonos system and its various components, showcasing how it caters to different audio needs and integrates seamlessly into daily life. Despite its cost and a few minor drawbacks like drunk friends hijacking playlists or occasional update quirks, the author firmly believes Sonos enhances the listening experience and is an essential part of his home.

title: Purpose pushes through with yield-focused single-stock ETFs
summary: Purpose Investments has launched its initial suite of Yield Shares ETFs, which are single-stock, yield-focused ETFs. These world-first ETFs allow Canadian investors to gain exposure to individual stocks like Tesla and Apple while receiving enhanced monthly distributions through a covered-call options strategy and moderate leverage. The ETFs also address an advisor pain point by simplifying the process of generating augmented income from individual stocks and offer Canadian dollar-denominated exposure to U.S. stocks with currency hedging.

title: Masimo’s W1: A Game Changer in Watch Industry
summary: The article reviews the Masimo W1 watch, highlighting its advanced medical technology from Irvine-based Masimo Corp. The author, Peter J. Brennan, emphasizes the watch's accurate health data, such as oxygen levels, heart rates, and a unique Hydration Index, positioning it as a competitor to tech giants like Apple and Samsung in the wearable technology market. Despite Wall Street's reservations about Masimo's pivot to consumer devices, the W1 aims to bring hospital-grade monitoring to everyday users for $499, with optional monthly subscription plans.

title: Ken Griffin among world’s richest after stellar year for Citadel
summary: Ken Griffin, CEO of Citadel, has joined the ranks of the world's top 40 wealthiest individuals following an exceptional year for his hedge fund, with its flagship Wellington fund returning 32%. This success comes despite a challenging market for other financial institutions and amid accusations of market manipulation ("spoofing") by biotech firm Northwest Biotherapeutics, which Citadel firmly denies. The firm's performance was reportedly boosted by strategic trading of S&P 500 options.

title: Ad tech firms focus on layoffs as ad recession fears build
summary: Ad tech companies are facing significant economic contraction, leading to widespread layoffs as a consequence of slower growth forecasts and investor pressure. The industry is grappling with declining ad dollars, extended payment terms, and rising interest rates, indicating a prolonged period of difficulty before potential recovery.

title: Garmin Venu Sq 2 Music review: Stylish design, focused on fitness
summary: The Garmin Venu Sq 2 Music watch, priced at Rs 33,490, offers a stylish design and strong fitness tracking capabilities, boasting accurate heart rate and calorie data comparable to the Apple Watch. While it excels in sports tracking and battery life (up to 5-6 days with active use), its user interface is criticized for being clunky and less intuitive than competitors. The review suggests it's best suited for fitness enthusiasts who prioritize tracking over extensive smartwatch features, despite its premium price.

title: Does Life360 (ASX: 360) Have A Good Business Model?
summary: This article examines Life360's business model, highlighting its growth in users and revenue but questioning its path to profitability. The author details the company's reliance on subscription plans, recent acquisitions of Tile and Jiobit, and the impact of these acquisitions on financial metrics like gross margin and customer acquisition costs. Despite its user base, the article expresses skepticism about Life360's long-term viability due to competition, privacy concerns, and an unproven ability to convert loyalty into strong cash flows.

title: Is BEAST Still a Threat?
summary: The article discusses the BEAST attack, a serious flaw in TLS 1.0 and earlier protocols, and its current threat level. While client-side mitigations have largely addressed the vulnerability, particularly for most browsers, Apple's platforms (Safari and iOS) were slower to adopt the 1/n-1 split technique, leaving some users potentially vulnerable. The author concludes that although BEAST is not easily exploitable today due to changes in applet security and Java's TLS stack, a healthy security margin is still needed, and Safari should fully implement the 1/n-1 split.

title: How to use Sonos speakers with Apple TV
summary: This article explains how to connect Sonos speakers to an Apple TV. It differentiates between using Sonos soundbars, which connect directly to the TV via HDMI ARC or optical audio, and other Sonos speakers, which utilize AirPlay 2 and require re-selecting the output each time. The article notes that only Apple's HomePod can serve as a permanent AirPlay speaker with Apple TV.

title: 'WHAT… HAPPENED'? A look back on crypto's 'biggest year yet' (for fraud, hacks, implosions…)
summary: This article provides a comprehensive look back at the tumultuous year 2022 for the cryptocurrency market, detailing significant events such as major frauds, hacks, and implosions of key platforms and stablecoins. It covers a month-by-month breakdown of happenings, including initial high hopes for adoption, the Terra/LUNA collapse, the "Great Crypto Contagion" of June, the Ethereum Merge, and the eventual downfall of FTX and Sam Bankman-Fried. The author questions the common prediction of a "builders" year ahead, suggesting a contrarian outcome may still be possible.

title: 7 of the Best Meme Stocks to Double Down on in 2023
summary: Despite a challenging year for speculative investments in 2022, certain meme stocks with strong underlying businesses are poised for significant growth in 2023. This article highlights seven such companies, including Apple, Netflix, Walt Disney, SoFi Technologies, Taiwan Semiconductor, PayPal, and General Motors. Each company is discussed with details on its resilience, strategic moves, and potential for a strong rebound.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It details the fund's portfolio as of July 31, 2025, highlighting major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple among its top constituents. The report also includes performance metrics and historical data for ACWI.

title: Dominion Energy (NYSE:D), Meta (NASD: META) and Air Lease (NYSE:AL): Money Managers Invest Their Own Wealth: 2023 Stock Picks
summary: Three money managers share their top stock picks for 2023, highlighting opportunities in aerospace, technology, and renewable energy. Thomas E. Browne Jr. sees value in aircraft lessor Air Lease due to an anticipated plane shortage. Daniel L. Kane believes Meta's core business is undervalued despite challenges from Apple's IDFA changes, suggesting the metaverse investment will eventually be resolved. John G. Ullman favors Dominion Energy for its strategic shift to renewable energy, supported by tax credits and semi-automatic rate increases, viewing it as a stable investment in the sector.
authors: Thomas E. Browne|Jr.|CFA; Daniel L. Kane|CFA; John G. Ullman

title: Economic Slowdown: What it means for the IT industry?
summary: The article discusses the potential impact of a global economic slowdown, exacerbated by the COVID crisis and the Ukraine war, on the IT industry, particularly in India, the US, and the UK. It highlights that while major economies are predicted to slow down, India appears to be in a relatively stable position with a more manageable cost of living. The piece also touches on how this slowdown might positively affect attrition rates in the Indian IT sector, making it easier for employers to retain talent.

title: Garmin Ltd. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Garmin Ltd. (GRMN) stock saw a 0.57% increase to $91.78, outperforming the mixed market on Tuesday. This marks its second consecutive day of gains, despite being significantly below its 52-week high. The company's stock also outperformed key competitors like Apple Inc. and Alphabet Inc.

title: Stock Market Outlook 2023 for the Worst Performing S&P 500 Sectors
summary: This article provides an outlook for the worst-performing S&P 500 sectors in 2022, including Technology, Real Estate, Consumer Discretionary, and Communication Services. It analyzes the performance of key holdings within each sector, their outlook for 2023, and analyst sentiment with options market probabilities for recovery. The forecast indicates that a full recovery to previous highs for these sectors and their major constituents is unlikely in the short term.

title: Should John Hancock Multifactor Large Cap ETF (JHML) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: The John Hancock Multifactor Large Cap ETF (JHML) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Blend segment of the US equity market, launched in 2015 with over $722 million in assets. It has an expense ratio of 0.29% and a 1.46% dividend yield. The ETF's portfolio is heavily allocated to Information Technology, with top holdings including Microsoft, Apple, and Amazon.

title: As supply chain issues improve in China, Apple faces sales slump with some iPhone models
summary: Apple is experiencing a sales slump with its lower-end iPhone models despite improvements in its supply chain in China. The company's main iPhone plant, Foxconn in Zhengzhou, is trying to recover full capacity after COVID-related disruptions and protests, offering significant bonuses to workers. While supply issues for Pro models are easing, demand for non-Pro iPhone 14 models, particularly the iPhone 14 Plus, is declining, leading Apple to consider strategies like price reductions for future models.

title: TSMC starts mass-producing three-nanometer chips
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (TSMC) has begun mass-producing processors using its advanced three-nanometer chip technology. This new technology offers a 15% improvement in chip performance and up to 35% less power consumption compared to its earlier five-nanometer process. TSMC, a major contract chip manufacturer for companies like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia, plans to develop multiple versions of this technology and is building new fabrication plants in Arizona.

title: Evie’s smart ring is "designed for women" and aimed at challenging Oura
summary: Movano Health is launching Evie, a smart ring specifically designed for women, which aims to compete with Oura. The ring will offer health insights including heart and menstrual health data for less than $300, without a monthly subscription. Movano plans to seek FDA clearance for Evie, positioning it as a medical device to provide more specific health guidance.

title: TSMC starts volume production of most advanced chips in Taiwan
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) has begun mass production of its most advanced 3-nanometre chips in southern Taiwan, with chairman Mark Liu emphasizing continued investment and capacity expansion on the island. Despite increasing foreign investments, including a significant expansion in Arizona, TSMC assures that the bulk of its manufacturing will remain in Taiwan, demonstrating its commitment to its home base. The company also announced plans for next-generation 2-nanometre chip factories in northern and central Taiwan.

title: TSMC ramps up 3nm chip production in Taiwan
summary: TSMC has officially begun mass production of 3nm chips at its facilities in southern Taiwan, a process expected to offer substantial improvements in density, performance, and power efficiency compared to its 5nm node. This move comes as TSMC balances global expansion, including new fabs in the US, with a continued commitment to developing its most advanced technology in Taiwan, navigating complex geopolitical trade relations.

title: 2022, Year of Face-Ripping Bear-Market Rallies that Got Crushed
summary: The article describes 2022 as a year of "face-ripping bear-market rallies that got crushed," affecting indices like the Nasdaq and S&P 500. It highlights significant plunges in major tech stocks and compares the current market downturn to the Dotcom Bust, noting that the Nasdaq is not yet halfway to the 78% decline seen then. The author uses terms like "consensual hallucination" to describe the over-hyped market environment fueled by "free-money."

title: WisdomTree U.S. Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the WisdomTree U.S. Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW), including its stock price, holdings, and dividend yield. As of the latest N-PORT filing on September 30, 2025, the fund holds 301 stocks with a total value of $16.30 billion, with top holdings being Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, Exxon Mobil, and Chevron. The article also presents key statistics, performance data, and historical breakdowns of the fund's portfolio.

title: GM tells remote workers to ‘pivot’ in return-to-office mandate
summary: General Motors has mandated that its corporate employees return to the office three days a week, requiring remote workers to "pivot to a more regular in-person work cycle." This decision aligns GM with other major corporations like Apple and Peloton, which have also implemented return-to-office policies since Labor Day. The move contrasts with more flexible policies from companies such as Ford and Mitsubishi, as well as Tesla's CEO Elon Musk's strict in-office requirement.

title: Garmin celebrates Global Running Day with the introduction of the Forerunner 255 series
summary: Garmin announced the Forerunner 255 series of GPS running smartwatches, coinciding with Global Running Day. These new models include triathlon support, advanced training metrics like Morning Report and HRV status, and enhanced smartwatch features such as Garmin Pay and multi-band GPS. Available in different sizes and with music options, the watches offer extended battery life and personalized training insights to help athletes improve their performance.

title: HP Posts Steady Q2, Despite Notebook Slump
summary: HP Inc. reported modest gains in Q2 with $16.5 billion in revenue, up 3.9 percent year-over-year, despite a significant slump in notebook sales and overall consumer PC and printer revenue. Strong commercial sales, particularly in personal systems, peripherals, and gaming, buoyed the results. CEO Enrique Lores acknowledged impacts from China's COVID lockdowns but stated HP's impact was smaller than competitors, and a partner expressed concerns about future economic slowdowns and shifted focus to diversification.

title: What the Stock Split by Google’s Parent, Alphabet, Means - WSJ
summary: Alphabet announced a 20-for-1 stock split, its first since 2014, making its shares more accessible to individual investors. This move follows similar actions taken by Apple and Tesla since the pandemic, signaling a renewed interest in stock splits among major tech companies. The split will apply to all three classes of Alphabet stock.

title: What is TSMC?
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is the world's largest semiconductor manufacturer, producing chips for major tech companies like Apple, Amazon, and Google. It focuses solely on manufacturing for third parties, unlike competitors, and boasts significant market dominance and advanced process technology. The article also discusses the potential impact of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan on TSMC and its global operations, as well as the company's expansion plans in the US.

title: Apple’s market value drops below $2 trillion
summary: Apple Inc.'s market capitalization fell below $2 trillion for the first time since March 2021, driven by concerns over high inflation and a slowing global economy impacting demand for its products. This significant drop follows a year after the company briefly reached a $3 trillion valuation. Analysts have downgraded Apple due to anticipated supply chain issues and reduced consumer spending on high-end phones, forecasting a potential revenue decline.

title: If You Invested $100 in Berkshire Hathaway in 1965, This Is How Much You Would Have Today
summary: If an investor had put $100 into Berkshire Hathaway in 1965, that investment would be valued at approximately $2.42 million today, representing a 2,419,900% return. This significantly outperforms a similar investment in the S&P 500, which would be worth about $22,400. The article highlights Berkshire's history under Warren Buffett, its strategic diversification into various sectors including insurance and tech (like Apple), and its consistent market-beating performance.

title: If You Invested $100 in Berkshire Hathaway in 1965, This Is How Much You Would Have Today
summary: This article illustrates the phenomenal returns of investing in Berkshire Hathaway since Warren Buffett took over in 1965. A $100 investment made then would be worth approximately $2.42 million today, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. The article details Berkshire's strategic shift from textiles to insurance, its acquisitions like GEICO and Burlington Northern Santa Fe, and its current diverse portfolio including a significant stake in Apple.

title: Foxconn to use Nvidia chips to build self-driving vehicle platforms
summary: Electronics manufacturer Foxconn and chipmaker Nvidia Corp have partnered to develop autonomous vehicle platforms. Foxconn will manufacture electronic control units (ECUs) based on Nvidia's DRIVE Orin chip for self-driving cars, aiming to address challenges in the global automotive market. Nvidia views this collaboration as a way to scale its efforts and meet the increasing demand for chips in autonomous and connected vehicles, projecting a $300 billion market opportunity in the automotive sector.

title: Tuesday's ETF with Unusual Volume: IWL
summary: The iShares Russell Top 200 ETF (IWL) experienced unusually high trading volume on Tuesday, with shares down about 0.8%. Tesla and Apple, major components of the ETF, saw significant trading volume and share price drops, while PayPal Holdings performed best, rising 4.3%.

title: Salesforce to lay off 10% of workforce to cut costs amid economic downturn
summary: Salesforce announced it will lay off 10% of its workforce and reduce office space to cut operating costs amid a challenging economic environment. CEO Marc Benioff admitted the company "hired too many people" during the pandemic's revenue surge, and the company is now facing the consequences. This move follows similar cost-cutting measures by other tech giants like Amazon, Apple, and Meta Platforms.

title: Apple to sign Luxshare for iPhone production in China - FT
summary: Apple is reportedly set to partner with Chinese contract manufacturer Luxshare Precision Industry Co Ltd for the production of premium iPhone models. Luxshare has already been producing a small number of iPhone 14 Pro Max models to address production shortfalls at Foxconn's Zhengzhou factory last year. This move is seen as Apple's strategy to diversify its supplier base following production disruptions caused by employee unrest and COVID-19 lockdowns in China.

title: Apple brings AI narration to audiobooks
summary: Apple Inc. has introduced AI-narrated audiobooks through Apple Books, utilizing text-to-speech technology to make audiobook production more accessible and affordable for publishers and authors. While the digital voices aim to reduce costs and complexity, some critics note an "artificial edge" compared to human narrators. Apple plans to improve the AI voices and currently offers them for select English-language romance and fiction books, with more voices and nonfiction categories in development.

title: Android has a response to iPhone’s satellite connection, thanks to Qualcomm
summary: Qualcomm is introducing Snapdragon Satellite service, a new emergency satellite phone service for Android smartphones. This service aims to compete with Apple's existing satellite connectivity, with specific product announcements expected by mid-year. The service requires at least one ground gateway to connect smartphones to satellites.

title: Nordstrom appoints Atticus Tysen to its board of directors
summary: Nordstrom, Inc. has announced the appointment of Atticus Tysen to its board of directors, effective January 3, 2023. Tysen brings over three decades of engineering and information security experience, currently serving as senior vice president of product development, chief information security, and fraud prevention officer for Intuit. His expertise is expected to enhance the board's focus on technology and cybersecurity within the retail industry.

title: Qualcomm partners with Iridium to develop satellite connectivity
summary: Qualcomm has announced a partnership with Iridium to launch Snapdragon Satellite, bringing satellite connectivity to next-generation premium Android phones and other devices. This service will enable global mobile messaging and emergency services in areas without traditional coverage. Other companies like Apple, Bullitt Group, T-Mobile with SpaceX, BICS with Lynk, AST SpaceMobile, and Huawei are also actively developing their own satellite connectivity solutions, highlighting a growing race for space-based mobile communication.

title: Apple Analyst Says BOE Will Become Largest Display Supplier for iPhone 15
summary: An Apple analyst in Taiwan predicts that China's BOE will become the largest display supplier for Apple's iPhone 15 series, significantly surpassing Samsung Display. BOE is expected to supply around 70% of OLED panels for the iPhone 15 and 15 Plus, and potentially 20-30% of LTPO panels for the iPhone 16. This shift indicates a growing challenge from Chinese companies to Korean dominance in the OLED display market.

title: Kartoon Channel! Expands Global Reach
summary: Kartoon Channel!, owned by Genius Brands International, has expanded its global distribution through new partnerships. The channel is now available on Vizio smart TVs in North America and via in-flight entertainment systems on several major airlines, including British Airways and Qatar Airways. This expansion aligns with Kartoon Channel!'s strategy to diversify its business model and reach more viewers worldwide.

title: Garmin Venu Sq2 Review | A fitness lifestyle companion
summary: The Garmin Venu Sq2 is reviewed as a robust fitness lifestyle companion that boasts impressive battery life, lasting over a week on a single charge. The review highlights its advanced health tracking features, accurate GPS, AMOLED display, and subscription-free breathing exercises, making it suitable for those pursuing new fitness goals despite its cost. The article details its performance across various activities including running, HIIT, Pilates, swimming, and even scuba diving, affirming its versatility and improvement over its predecessor.

title: You might actually want a TiVo TV someday
summary: Xperi, the company that owns the TiVo brand, is launching its own TiVo OS smart TV platform in Europe in the first half of 2023, with U.S. availability planned for the future. The platform focuses on content discovery over apps, offering recommendations across various streaming services and an intuitive voice search. While it currently lacks a robust app store compared to competitors, its neutral approach and potential for ad revenue sharing aim to attract TV manufacturers.

title: U.S. Supreme Court lets Meta's WhatsApp pursue 'Pegasus' spyware suit
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has allowed Meta Platforms Inc's WhatsApp to proceed with a lawsuit against Israel's NSO Group. WhatsApp accuses NSO Group of exploiting a bug in its messaging app to install "Pegasus" spyware, enabling the surveillance of 1,400 individuals, including journalists and human rights activists. NSO Group had argued for immunity, claiming it acted as an agent for foreign governments, but the Supreme Court rejected their appeal.

title: Inari’s share price down over 4% as Apple plans to drop Broadcom chip from devices
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd's share price dropped over 4% after reports that Apple plans to replace Broadcom's Wi-Fi and Bluetooth chips with in-house designs by 2025. Although Inari's exposure to Broadcom is mainly on complex RF chips, which are harder to replace in the short term, the news still impacted the company's stock given its significant reliance on Broadcom. Apple is Broadcom's largest customer, accounting for about 20% of its revenue, and Inari is heavily dependent on Broadcom.

title: Illumina, the DNA sequencing giant, gives Wall Street bad news
summary: Illumina, the world's largest maker of DNA sequencing technology, has delivered disappointing news to investors, announcing a lower profit outlook for 2023. The company, which has successfully driven down the cost of sequencing a human genome, is now facing challenges due to its own success and uncertainties in funding for genetic research. Consequently, Illumina's stock price has significantly dropped from its peak in 2021.

title: Pepsi, Coke soda pricing targeted in new federal probe
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has launched a preliminary investigation into Coca-Cola and PepsiCo's pricing strategies for potential price discrimination in the soft drink market. This probe revives the largely dormant Robinson-Patman Act of 1936, which prohibits suppliers from offering better prices to large retailers over smaller competitors. The investigation signals the Biden administration's broader effort to apply antitrust scrutiny beyond the tech sector to traditional large companies.

title: Apple to start using in-house screens from 2024 - Bloomberg News
summary: Apple plans to begin using its own custom displays in mobile devices starting in 2024, aiming to reduce reliance on external suppliers like Samsung and LG. The transition will start with high-end Apple Watches, followed by other devices including the iPhone, and will involve upgrading to microLED technology. This move is part of a broader strategy by Apple to bring more component production in-house, following similar shifts with chips from Broadcom and Intel.

title: Inari pares loss as analysts see limited impact from Apple's plan to drop Broadcom chip
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd shares recovered some losses after analysts indicated that Apple's plan to replace Broadcom chips with in-house designs would have a limited impact on Inari's outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT) business. Analysts noted Inari's direct exposure to Apple's revenue through Broadcom is relatively small (17-18%) and that Inari's involvement in complex RF filters is unlikely to be immediately affected. Despite a maintained "market perform" rating with a slightly reduced target price, some analysts suggest the negative news is already priced in, presenting a potential buying opportunity.

title: Apple Watch patent infringement finding; Masimo seeks import ban
summary: A US court has ruled that Apple infringed on one of Masimo's pulse oximeter patents with its Apple Watch Series 6 and up, a significant setback in their ongoing legal battle. Masimo is now seeking a US import ban on all current Apple Watches, which could halt sales in the country. The United States International Trade Commission (USITC) will determine whether to implement this import ban.

title: Apple debuts free tool to help businesses get noticed
summary: Apple has launched a new free online tool called Apple Business Connect, designed to help businesses customize their information cards in Apple Maps and other services. This allows businesses to update details like hours, add photos, promote limited-time offers, and access analytics. The initiative aims to provide useful local information and potentially attract more engagement from merchants and consumers.

title: Kuo doubles down on iPhone 15 Pro having no physical buttons
summary: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo has reiterated his claim that the iPhone 15 Pro will feature solid-state buttons instead of physical ones, similar to the iPhone SE 2's home button. His latest survey indicates that Cirrus Logic will be the exclusive supplier of the Taptic Engine's controller IC for this change. Kuo also predicts that if users respond positively, this technology may be adopted in other high-end models in the future, alongside rumors of a rounded, titanium chassis for the iPhone 15 range.

title: Back Again At Comparing Skyworks Vs. Qorvo (NASDAQ:SWKS)
summary: This article compares Skyworks Solutions and Qorvo, both RF suppliers currently trading at low P/E ratios. It highlights Qorvo's transparency regarding market details and Skyworks' stable growth due to its strong ties with Apple. The author also suggests Qorvo has a significant opportunity with Apple's business, especially given Qualcomm's apparent withdrawal.

title: IDC, Gartner: 2022 PC Demand Dropped To Its ‘Lowest Level In Years’
summary: PC demand in 2022 dropped significantly to its lowest level in years, according to reports from IDC and Gartner. This decline was attributed to global recession, inflation, higher interest rates, and consumers already owning newer PCs purchased during the pandemic. The business market too saw a decline as enterprise buyers extended PC life cycles and delayed purchases, with growth not expected until 2024.

title: Meta faces a future of more legal woes and falling revenues
summary: Meta, formerly Facebook, is experiencing significant challenges including plummeting stock value, falling revenues, and increasing regulatory scrutiny, a situation that worsened after its rebrand in October 2021. Despite investor concerns and substantial losses from its metaverse division, Reality Labs, CEO Mark Zuckerberg remains committed to heavy investment in the metaverse. The company also faces ongoing legal battles, hefty fines for data privacy breaches, and a class-action lawsuit settlement, exacerbating doubts about its future prospects.

title: Starbucks tells corporate employees to return to the office
summary: Starbucks is requiring its corporate employees to return to the office for a minimum of three days a week, with specific days designated for in-person collaboration. Interim CEO Howard Schultz stated in a memo that the policy, effective January 30, aims to rebuild company culture, foster human connection, and ensure fairness among employees, especially considering store and plant workers who could not work remotely. This move aligns Starbucks with other companies like Disney and Apple that have also implemented stricter return-to-office mandates.

title: Buffett-Backed Taiwan Semiconductor Braces for Chip-Sector Downturn
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) reported record profits for its fourth quarter, exceeding analyst expectations, but also issued a warning about a potential downturn in demand for 2023. This news comes as the company, backed by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway, indicates higher costs for building facilities in the U.S. compared to Taiwan. TSMC is preparing for a challenging period in the chip sector despite its strong recent financial performance.

title: Starbucks tells corporate employees to return to office 3 days a week
summary: Starbucks is requiring corporate employees to return to the office three days a week by the end of January, a move announced by interim CEO Howard Schultz. This decision comes as other major companies like Disney, Apple, and Snapchat's parent company are also enforcing stricter return-to-office policies, with some citing low compliance with previous hybrid work arrangements. The shift reflects a broader trend of companies clamping down on remote work that became common during the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to take more than 40% pay cut
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook will receive a target total compensation of $49 million in 2023, marking a pay cut of over 40% from the previous year. This adjustment comes after Apple's compensation committee considered shareholder feedback on executive pay and a recommendation from Cook himself. While his base salary remains $3 million, equity awards have been restructured to align his compensation between the 80th and 90th percentiles relative to Apple's peer group.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to take a more than 40 percent pay cut
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is set to take a more than 40 percent pay cut in 2023, reducing his target compensation to $49 million, down from nearly $100 million in previous years. This adjustment was made due to shareholder feedback, the company's performance, and Cook's own recommendation. The compensation committee aimed to align his pay between the 80th and 90th percentiles relative to peer groups for future years.

title: Oracle jumps into payments for retailers
summary: Oracle has expanded its services for retailers to include in-store payment acceptance, offering tap-to-pay capabilities for various payment methods like debit, credit, Apple, Google, and Samsung digital wallets. This new feature, which became available in December, will be sold at a fixed fee without long-term contracts or minimum monthly payment volumes. Oracle aims to provide a more stable and cost-effective "payment to processing" solution, integrating it with their existing hardware and cloud-based software for inventory and staff management.

title: How Both Taiwan and the US Can Benefit from TSMC's Investment
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is increasing its investment in the U.S. to $40 billion, building a second factory in Phoenix, Arizona.
