This move, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to strengthen the U.S. domestic semiconductor industry and diversify global supply chains. Experts suggest that while complete self-sufficiency for the U.S. might be unrealistic, this investment helps address critical dependencies, enhances national security, and fosters innovation in chip manufacturing.

title: Apple Takes Major Steps Toward ‘Owning’ Its Key Technology
summary: Apple (AAPL) is reportedly taking significant steps to assert greater control over its proprietary technology. This initiative involves increasingly developing its own in-house chips and custom displays. Such moves signify a strategic shift towards self-reliance and vertical integration within the company's product development.

title: From Apple To Amazon And Toyota, Here Are The Top 10 Biggest Companies In The World
summary: This article lists the top 10 biggest companies in the world based on revenue. Walmart, State Grid Corporation Of China, and Amazon lead the list, primarily highlighting a competition between US and Chinese firms. The article also includes companies from Japan and Germany, detailing their revenue, founding year, and number of employees.

title: Biometric Sensors Market Size to Touch USD 3.5829 million by 2030 According to Acumen Research and Consulting
summary: The global biometric sensors market is projected to grow from USD 1.3195 million in 2021 to USD 3.5829 million by 2030, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.9% from 2022 to 2030. This growth is driven by increasing demand for secure authentication, advancements in technology, and wider adoption in various sectors like healthcare, banking, and government. Despite its growth, the market faces challenges due to lack of standardization, high implementation costs, and privacy concerns.

title: Goldman Sachs Vs. Jefferies: Which Is the Better Investment?
summary: This article compares Goldman Sachs (GS) and Jefferies Financial Group (JEF) as investment opportunities, especially in light of Berkshire Hathaway's decision to exit its Goldman Sachs position and initiate a new, albeit small, position in Jefferies. The author highlights Jefferies' smaller size, focused strategy in investment banking, higher dividend yield, and lower valuation as potential advantages over the larger and more diversified Goldman Sachs, suggesting a rationale for Berkshire Hathaway's move. Though both firms are performing well, the piece implies Jefferies may have greater long-term growth potential.

title: TSMC expects revenue to fall amid declining chip demand
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) anticipates a decline in revenue for 2023 due to softening demand for chips, particularly from consumer device and data center clients. Despite a 26.7% year-over-year revenue increase in Q4, the company expects a more than 14% drop in Q1 revenue. TSMC forecasts the semiconductor market to bottom out in the first half of 2023, with a recovery expected in the second half, and is adjusting its spending plans accordingly while still investing in new facilities like the Arizona fab.

title: Temu Review: What Is Temu and Why Is It Suddenly America’s Most Popular App?
summary: Temu, the American sister brand of China's Pinduoduo, has rapidly become America's most popular shopping app since its U.S. launch last fall. Its success is primarily attributed to extremely low prices, free shipping, and aggressive advertising. While the author found good deals on certain electronics and kitchen items, he advises caution on other categories and notes the app's business model involves cutting out middlemen for direct sales.

title: Netflix’s Reed Hastings steps aside as co-CEO, stays on as executive chairman
summary: Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings has stepped down as co-CEO, transitioning to executive chairman, with COO Greg Peters joining Ted Sarandos as co-CEO. This leadership change comes as Netflix aims to reaccelerate growth amid increased competition and a slowdown in subscriber numbers. The company reported slower fourth-quarter revenue but exceeded subscriber expectations, attributing success to popular shows and new initiatives like an ad-supported plan and paid password sharing.

title: Global X S&p 500 Covered Call Etf Share Price
summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the Global X S&P 500 Covered Call ETF (XYLD), including its current share price, performance metrics, historical returns, and fundamental data like expense ratio and AUM. It also lists the top holdings, sector breakdown, market cap distribution, and offers insights on its performance against peers and market cap movements. The article concludes with an FAQ section on investing in XYLD from India.

title: Apple’s Reliance On China Poses A Problem For The Company
summary: This article discusses Apple's significant reliance on its supply chain in China and the challenges this poses for the company amidst growing geopolitical tensions and trade barriers between the US and China. It highlights how Apple's deep integration with Chinese manufacturing, including specialized labor and R&D, makes disentanglement difficult, even as the US government increases pressure through tariffs, forced labor prevention acts, and technology export controls. The article suggests that while Apple is starting to move some operations, achieving true independence from China will be a slow and arduous process.

title: 予算3万円台の「スマートウォッチ」おすすめ5選　人気の「Apple Watch SE」から、コスパが良い高機能モデルまで【2023年1月版】 - Fav-Log by ITmedia
summary: This article recommends five smartwatches under 30,000 yen, including popular models like the Apple Watch SE and high-performance, cost-effective alternatives. It caters to users looking for smartwatches equipped with functions like health tracking, mobile payments, and sports activity monitoring. The selection includes devices suitable for various needs, from outdoor enthusiasts to those prioritizing design.

title: Silicon Valley 2.0? 5 US cities that could be the new tech start-up central
summary: Several southern US cities are emerging as new tech hubs, drawing companies like Tesla, Apple, Oracle, Microsoft, and Google away from California's Silicon Valley. High tax rates, labor costs, regulations, and a declining quality of life in Silicon Valley are pushing these companies to look for more affordable and business-friendly environments in states such as Texas, Arizona, Georgia, and Florida. These new tech centers offer incentives, lower costs of living, and new luxury residential developments to accommodate the influx of workers.

title: 7 Growth Stocks That Will Make You Rich in 10 Years
summary: This article identifies seven growth stocks across diverse high-demand sectors like technology, cybersecurity, nanotechnology, healthcare, and electric vehicles that are positioned to generate significant wealth for investors within the next decade. The selected companies, including Apple, Palo Alto Networks, Applied Materials, Intuitive Surgical, Welltower, ChargePoint, and Marathon Digital, are highlighted for their potential to capitalize on future market trends and innovation. Despite current market hesitations, the author emphasizes the long-term potential of these companies based on their strategic positioning and projected growth.

title: How Apple’s Upcoming Mixed-Reality Headset Will Work
summary: Apple's upcoming mixed-reality headset, likely named Reality Pro, is an ambitious attempt to create a 3D version of iOS with advanced eye and hand-tracking. Priced around $3,000, it aims to revolutionize virtual meetings and immersive video, challenging Meta Platforms Inc.'s dominance in the VR industry. This launch marks Apple's first major new product category since the Apple Watch in 2015, representing a significant strategic move for the company.

title: Nokia Corp. ADR rises Monday, still underperforms market
summary: Nokia Corp. ADR (NOK) saw a slight increase of 0.43% on Monday, closing at $4.63. Despite this rise, the stock underperformed the broader market, as both the NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average rose more significantly. The gain broke a four-day losing streak for Nokia ADR, though it remained below its 52-week high and lagged behind competitors like BlackBerry Ltd., Apple Inc., and Motorola Solutions Inc.

title: Apple builds on privacy commitment by unveiling new education and awareness efforts on Data Privacy Day
summary: In celebration of Data Privacy Day, Apple has launched new educational resources to help users manage their data. These efforts include a "Today at Apple" session focusing on iPhone privacy features and a partnership with Nick Mohammed for a short film titled "A Day in the Life of an Average Person’s Data" to raise awareness. Apple emphasizes that privacy is a fundamental human right, built into all its products and services.

title: Nokia Corp. ADR underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
summary: Nokia Corp. ADR (NOK) dropped 1.73% to $4.55 on Tuesday, performing worse than many of its competitors, despite a mixed day for the broader stock market. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose while the NASDAQ Composite Index fell. Nokia's ADR closed significantly below its 52-week high, and its trading volume was higher than its 50-day average.

title: Choice Hotels Opens Cambria Hotel Austin Uptown Near the Domain
summary: Choice Hotels International has opened the Cambria Hotel Austin Uptown Near the Domain, marking its second property in Austin, Texas, with a third on the way. The 135-room hotel is strategically located in Austin's high-tech corridor, providing convenient access to major tech companies and local attractions. It offers modern amenities and design-forward accommodations aimed at both business and leisure travelers.

title: Goldman Sachs signals partial retreat from consumer banking
summary: Goldman Sachs is partially retreating from its consumer banking efforts after accumulating $3 billion in losses since 2020. The firm is scrapping plans for a checking account and winding down its personal loan business, Marcus, to refocus on its core investment banking and wealth management services. While a popular savings account and credit card business will continue, the losses in consumer lending have attracted regulatory scrutiny.

title: Big Tech to Congress: Listen to Taylor Swift and Go After Ticketmaster, Not Us
summary: Lobbyists for major technology companies capitalized on public outrage over Ticketmaster's handling of Taylor Swift concert tickets to deflect congressional antitrust scrutiny away from Big Tech. They argued that Congress should focus on clear monopolies like Ticketmaster, implying that existing antitrust laws are sufficient and that efforts to reform them to target tech giants are misguided. This strategy led to a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing focusing on Live Nation/Ticketmaster's monopolistic practices instead of Big Tech.

title: AST SpaceMobile raises $75 million, partners with TIM Brasil
summary: AST SpaceMobile has raised $75 million through an offering of Class A Common Stock and formed a partnership with Brazilian telco TIM Brasil. The funds will support the production and launch of the company's commercial satellites, while the partnership will enable TIM Brasil to test AST SpaceMobile's satellite-based 4G services in remote Brazilian locations. This move highlights AST SpaceMobile's mission to connect unserved areas and positions it alongside other companies in the growing satellite-to-phone connectivity market.

title: IBM Is Tech’s Most Beaten-Down Company
summary: This article argues that IBM has significantly fallen from its former prominence, ranking 49th on the Fortune 500 compared to 8th in 1980. Its recent financial results show flat revenue and modest net income, trailing far behind other tech giants like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The author criticizes IBM for missing key technological advancements such as cloud computing, search, e-commerce, and operating systems despite its past R&D capabilities.

title: Is Augmented Reality safe?
summary: Augmented Reality (AR) blends digital information with the real world, raising significant safety concerns despite its benefits. The article highlights potential dangers in privacy, security, and mental health, including data collection, cyber-attacks, and the addictive nature of AR. It emphasizes the need for careful consideration and control as AR technology becomes more integrated into daily life.

title: Save $200 on the Sonos Arc Soundbar, now $699 (lowest price ever)
summary: The Sonos Arc Soundbar is currently available for $699, a $200 discount that marks its lowest recorded price, even beating Black Friday deals. This offer from Adorama applies to the white model and is for a limited time. The soundbar was rated 4.5 out of 5 in a hands-on review for its rich sound, substantial bass, and Dolby Atmos support.

title: Google continued to ramp up federal lobbying spending before DOJ filed second antitrust lawsuit
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet, significantly increased its federal lobbying expenditures, spending over $13 million in 2022, prior to the Department of Justice filing its second antitrust lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges Google monopolized the digital advertising technology sector by acquiring rivals and manipulating the auction process to stifle competition. Despite these lobbying efforts and substantial political contributions, particularly to Democratic candidates, Alphabet also announced mass layoffs of 12,000 employees in January 2023.

title: Apple takes first steps towards 'Made in India' AirPods
summary: Apple has begun manufacturing components for its AirPods in India, marking a significant step towards the "Made in India" initiative for its products beyond the iPhone. Jabil Inc, an Apple supplier, is producing AirPods enclosures in Pune, India, and shipping them to China and Vietnam for assembly. This move is part of Apple's broader strategy to reduce its reliance on Chinese manufacturing and diversify its supply chain.

title: ‘Acapulco’ Renewed for Season 3 at Apple
summary: Apple has renewed its series "Acapulco" for a third season, which will consist of 10 episodes and begin production in the spring. The show stars Eugenio Derbez and is inspired by the film "How to Be A Latin Lover." The cast includes Enrique Arrizon, Fernando Carsa, Damián Alcázar, and others, continuing the story of Máximo Gallardo at a resort in 1985 and his older self in the present day.

title: Get Your Game on With Build-a-Bear's New 'Ted Lasso' Collection
summary: Build-A-Bear has launched a new collection of plush toys inspired by Apple TV+'s hit series, "Ted Lasso." The collection features a Coach Lasso bear, complete with team kit and mustache, along with accessories like aviator sunglasses and a Union Jack mug. Fans can also find AFC Richmond accessories to dress up other cuddly toys as they eagerly await the third season of the Emmy-winning show.

title: Exxon Vaults to Record Annual Profit of $55.7 Billion
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. achieved a record annual profit of $55.7 billion in 2022, marking a significant recovery after suffering losses during the pandemic and a historic proxy fight in 2021. This surge was primarily driven by soaring oil and gas prices globally, allowing Exxon to surpass most major companies in profitability, except for Apple and Microsoft. The company's resurgence reflects a turnaround in its financial performance and market standing.

title: Apple workplace rules violate U.S. labor law, agency finds
summary: A U.S. labor agency has found that Apple Inc. maintains workplace policies that unlawfully discourage employees from discussing working conditions. The National Labor Relations Board plans to issue a complaint unless Apple settles, citing policies around confidentiality and surveillance, and past comments from CEO Tim Cook that allegedly stymied worker organizing. Former Apple senior engineering manager Ashley Gjovik filed the initial complaints in 2021, and the NLRB investigates such charges.

title: Netflix Inc. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market
summary: Netflix Inc. stock saw a modest increase of 0.21% on Tuesday, closing at $353.86, despite a strong day for the broader market with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also rising. The stock's performance underperformed compared to competitors like Apple, Walt Disney, and Comcast, and it remained significantly below its 52-week high. Trading volume was also notably lower than its 50-day average.

title: Spotify Reports Q4 2022 Earnings: Strong User Growth and Widened Losses
summary: Spotify reported strong Q4 2022 earnings, with revenue up 18% year-over-year to 3.17 billion euros ($3.43 billion) and monthly active users increasing by 20% to 489 million. However, the company's losses widened significantly due to higher personnel and advertising costs, leading to a planned 6% reduction in headcount. For Q1 2023, Spotify expects continued revenue growth and user base expansion, aiming for 500 million monthly active users, but anticipates an operating loss.

title: Apple Is Dropping Industrial Design Chief Role in Post-Jony Ive Era
summary: Apple Inc. will not replace its departing industrial design chief, Evans Hankey, signifying a major shift in the company's approach to product design after the departure of Jony Ive. Instead, the core industrial design team will report directly to Chief Operating Officer Jeff Williams, and veteran designers will be given expanded roles. This decision centralizes design under operations and elevates Williams, seen as a potential successor to CEO Tim Cook, while also acknowledging the challenge of finding a suitable replacement given recent departures from the design team.

title: McDonald’s new strategy to bring renewable energy to its supply chain
summary: McDonald's has launched a new strategy to decarbonize its supply chain by signing a 189-megawatt virtual power purchase agreement (VPPA) with its five logistics partners. This deal marks the first time a corporation has acted as an anchor buyer for an aggregation deal explicitly for its suppliers, simplifying access to renewable energy for smaller companies. The initiative targets Scope 3 emissions, specifically the electricity load of its logistics supply chain in the U.S., aligning with a growing trend of corporations providing resources to help suppliers transition to clean energy.

title: Samsung’s next frontier: XR in partnership with Qualcomm, Google
summary: Samsung Electronics is partnering with Qualcomm Technologies and Google LLC to enter the extended reality (XR) device market, which is projected to reach $1.39 trillion by 2030. This collaboration aims to challenge other Big Tech firms like Meta and Apple in the rapidly growing XR space. Although specific product details were not revealed, industry watchers expect Samsung to develop XR headsets utilizing Qualcomm's chipsets and Google's Android operating system, leveraging their existing smartphone collaboration.

title: Apple sales drop 5% in largest quarterly revenue decline since 2016
summary: Apple recorded its first year-over-year sales decline since 2019, with overall sales for the holiday quarter down 5%. CEO Tim Cook attributed this miss to a strong dollar, production issues in China affecting the iPhone 14 Pro and Pro Max, and the challenging macroeconomic environment. While iPhone and Mac sales decreased, the company saw a significant increase in iPad revenue and a 6% growth in its services business.

title: Apple Q1 2023 Revenues Slowed by Exchange Rates, COVID, and Inflation
summary: Apple's Q1 2023 financial results showed a revenue decrease of 5% and a profit decrease of 13.3% compared to the previous year, marking only the second such decline in a decade. CEO Tim Cook attributed this to foreign exchange headwinds, COVID-19 related production constraints impacting iPhone 14 Pro models, and a challenging macroeconomic environment. Despite these challenges, Apple reported strong performance in Services and iPad sales, reached 2 billion active devices globally, and avoided major layoffs seen in other tech giants.

title: Meta从虚拟元宇宙重回现实
summary: Meta Platforms reported a mixed Q4 2022 earnings, with revenue declining year-over-year but exceeding market expectations, driven by its traditional advertising business. While net income continued to decrease, particularly due to metaverse investments in Reality Labs, the company saw growth in its user base across its platforms. Meta is now signaling a return to prioritizing its core app business and cost-cutting measures, shifting focus from its aggressive metaverse push.

title: Peloton Rival Zwift Bets on Hardcore Racing In Your Living Room
summary: Zwift Inc., an exercise app with backing from KKR, specializes in virtual cycling races, gaining popularity among avid cyclists and investors. During the pandemic, it notably provided a virtual version of the Tour de France. The platform allows users to connect their bikes to virtual worlds for training or competition from home.

title: Corning Inc. recently cut about 1,500 jobs worldwide
summary: Corning Inc. recently announced a workforce reduction of approximately 1,500 jobs worldwide in mid-January due to macroeconomic concerns. This cut impacts less than 2.5% of its global employees. The announcement came as the company released its fourth-quarter and full-year 2022 financial results, reporting core sales growth but anticipating a sales decline in the first quarter of 2023 partly due to COVID-19 disruptions in China.

title: NLRB: Apple illegally interfered with employees’ protected activity
summary: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has found merit in five charges against Apple, alleging the company violated the National Labor Relations Act by interfering with employees' protected activities. The charges include violations related to work rules, handbook rules, confidentiality policies, and specific statements and conduct by high-level executives that "reasonably tend to interfere with, restrain, or coerce employees in the exercise of their right to protected concerted activity." If Apple and the charging parties do not settle, the NLRB's regional director will issue a complaint and pursue the charges before an administrative law judge.

title: Purifying the ‘miracle metal’: How to decarbonize aluminium
summary: Aluminium, despite its versatility and recyclability, is incredibly energy-intensive to produce, contributing 2% of global man-made emissions. This article explores how a combination of renewable energy, new technologies like inert anodes and carbon capture, and extensive recycling can significantly reduce the carbon footprint of aluminium production. It also highlights the crucial role of coalitions like the First Movers Coalition and government support to overcome economic and supply chain challenges in achieving near-zero emissions.

title: Sonos Is Having a Secret Sale Ahead of President's Day
summary: Sonos is holding a "secret sale" on its popular speakers and soundbars ahead of President's Day and the Super Bowl, offering significant discounts. Highlighting the rarity of Sonos sales and their premium status, the article details deals on products like the Sonos Arc and Sonos Beam Gen 2, recommending them for home theater upgrades. The sale includes individual speakers and immersive sets, but the duration and inventory are limited.

title: Calamos Investments Launches New Sustainable Equities ETF SROI
summary: Calamos Investments has launched the Calamos Antetokounmpo Global Sustainable Equities ETF (SROI) on NYSE ARCA, in collaboration with NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo. This actively managed ETF aims for long-term capital appreciation by investing in quality growth companies that integrate ESG principles, utilizing both traditional financial and nonfinancial indicators. The fund will also donate a portion of its profits to charity, reflecting a commitment to community impact.

title: 元宇宙烧了 137 亿美元，还得 Facebook 救 Meta
summary: Meta Platforms reported a Q4 2022 earnings beat, with its stock surging 23% despite a $13.7 billion loss in its metaverse division Reality Labs for the full year. The unexpected positive market reaction is attributed to lower investor expectations, positive signals from management regarding social advertising, and a $40 billion share buyback program. While the metaverse remains a long-term goal, Meta's immediate focus is on optimizing its core Facebook platform, pursuing short-video growth, and leveraging AI for advertising, coupled with significant cost-cutting measures including layoffs.

title: BioSig Appoints Steve Buhaly as CFO
summary: BioSig Technologies, Inc. has announced the retirement of its long-serving CFO, Steve Chaussy, after 12 years of service. Steve Buhaly, with 15 years of public company CFO experience, including a significant tenure at Qorvo, has been appointed as his successor. Buhaly's expertise in financial leadership, corporate expansion, and value creation is expected to support BioSig's commercial rollout of its PURE EP™ System and national scaling efforts.

title: Mobile App Development market, it is estimated to increase at the rate of more than 23.80% from 2020 to the end of the year 2030
summary: The global mobile app development market is projected to reach approximately USD 366.93 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 23.80% from 2022 to 2030. This growth is driven by the increasing use of smartphones and other gadgets, the leveraging of mobile applications by e-commerce and social media platforms, and the declining cost of data services coupled with rising internet penetration. North America is expected to generate the highest revenues, while the Asia Pacific region is anticipated to show the highest CAGR.

title: Apple Stock Versus Microsoft Stock: Which Is the Better Buy Now?
summary: This article analyzes whether Apple or Microsoft is the better stock to buy based on Morningstar's metrics, including price/fair value, uncertainty, economic moat, and capital allocation. While both companies are giants facing economic headwinds, Morningstar finds Microsoft stock to be more attractive due to it being 17% undervalued with a lower uncertainty rating, earning it a 4-star rating compared to Apple's 3-star rating. Both companies have wide economic moats and exemplary capital allocation.

title: Exclusive: Hearing startup Tuned adds more employers with fresh funds
summary: Hearing startup Tuned has successfully raised $3.5 million in a seed extension round, bringing their total seed funding to $6 million. This new investment, led by Distributed Ventures, will fuel the expansion of Tuned's commercial team and its goal to position hearing support as a vital component of employee health benefits. The company also announced new leadership additions and plans to launch a pediatric vertical while continuing to target employers with its tech-agnostic hearing solutions.

title: AMD wins nearly a third of processor market, Arm's climb slows: analyst report
summary: AMD has significantly increased its market share in central processor units, capturing nearly a third of the x86 processor market from Intel, according to a recent analyst report from Mercury Research. This growth occurred during the worst PC chip market downturn since the 1980s. Meanwhile, Arm's rise in the PC market, primarily driven by Apple's Mac computers, slowed in the fourth quarter of 2022.

title: Cartões Bradesco seguem pontuando em compras com Apple Pay
summary: Bradesco credit card holders were concerned about a recent change in the bank's loyalty program regulations indicating that transactions made through digital wallets would no longer accrue points. However, MacMagazine clarified with Bradesco that NFC payment services like Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Samsung Pay, as well as PayPal, will continue to be eligible for point accumulation. The new rules, which exclude app-based payment platforms like PicPay and Mercado Pago, will take effect on March 6th.

title: Navigate 2023 With Recession-Resistant ETF ZECP
summary: The Zacks Earnings Consistent Portfolio ETF (ZECP) is presented as a recession-resistant strategy for investors concerned about a potential economic downturn in 2023. ZECP, Zacks Investment Management's first ETF, identifies companies with strong balance sheets and consistent earnings, having demonstrated resilience through past recessions. The fund seeks to offer an active approach to navigate market volatility, focusing on quality companies with stable historic and forecasted earnings per share.

title: Apple hires its first Chief People Officer in executive reshuffle
summary: Apple has appointed Carol Surface as its first chief people officer, a move that shifts human resources responsibilities from retail chief Deirdre O'Brien. Surface, previously with Medtronic Plc, will report directly to CEO Tim Cook and oversee Apple’s 164,000 employees. This reshuffle aims to give more prominence to the HR role and allow O'Brien to focus more fully on Apple's expanding and increasingly challenging retail operations.

title: Wall Street ends sharply higher as investors eye inflation data
summary: Wall Street closed sharply higher as investors awaited key inflation data, which is expected to influence the Federal Reserve's future interest rate decisions. Tech-related heavyweights, including Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon, significantly contributed to the S&P 500's gains, with Meta jumping after news of potential further layoffs. Fidelity National Information Services slumped after announcing a spinoff of its merchant payments business.

title: T-Mobile suffers major network outage across US
summary: T-Mobile experienced a significant network outage across the United States on Monday night, affecting thousands of customers who reported issues with calls, texts, and data. The disruption, which coincided with network chief Neville Ray's retirement announcement, was attributed to a "third-party fiber interruption." Although the carrier seemed to be resolving the issue by late evening, customers were frustrated by the service interruption.

title: These Stocks Are Leading the Growth Rebound
summary: Growth stocks, particularly in the tech sector, have seen a significant rebound in early 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming value stocks. This surge is likely driven by investor expectations that the Federal Reserve may soon pause interest rate hikes. Despite strong recent gains, the Morningstar US Growth Index still needs a substantial rally to recover fully from last year's losses.

title: Qualcomm introduces new Snapdragon X75 mobile modem chip
summary: Qualcomm has unveiled its new Snapdragon X75 5G modem chip, expected to power future flagship smartphones. This new chip features an enhanced AI processor that is 2.5 times faster than its predecessor, designed to optimize 5G connections and consider sensory data for improved performance. The Snapdragon X75 also supports 5G Advanced and Qualcomm Satellite technology, aiming to reduce hardware costs, improve internet coverage, and enable satellite connectivity for handsets.

title: Microsoft Now Supporting Windows Virtualization on Mac M1, M2, via Parallels Desktop 18
summary: Microsoft has officially announced full support for running Windows 11 virtually on Apple Mac Silicon-powered computers (M1, M2, Pro, Ultra chips) via Alludo's Parallels Desktop 18. This integration offers Mac users a legitimate and hassle-free way to operate Windows 11 without requiring hard drive partitioning. Users can purchase or subscribe to Parallels Desktop for Mac to enable this functionality.

title: Forget Milk and Eggs: Supermarkets Are Having a Fire Sale on Data About You
summary: Supermarkets like Kroger are making significant profits by collecting and selling extensive data on their customers' purchasing habits and personal information to brands and advertisers. This data, which includes demographics, financial information, and even in-store movements, is enriched with third-party data and used for targeted advertising. The proposed merger between Kroger and Albertsons would significantly expand this data pool, raising privacy concerns among regulators and lawmakers.

title: Exclusive: SoftBank's Arm China profit drops over 90% in 2022
summary: Arm China, a joint venture of SoftBank's Arm Ltd, experienced a more than 90% drop in net profit in 2022, falling to $3.2 million despite a significant revenue increase to nearly $890 million. This decline occurred during the first year of new management appointed by SoftBank, following a two-year battle to oust the previous CEO. The profit slump is attributed in part to a $37 million loss in foreign exchange, though it reportedly will not impact Arm Ltd's overall financial performance as its royalty and licensing fees come before profit calculation.

title: UBS recommends its clients grab a full serving of Buffett's incredible, UNDERVALUED Berkshire Hathaway
summary: UBS is recommending its clients buy shares of Berkshire Hathaway (BRK), considering it undervalued by over 20% compared to its intrinsic value. The article highlights BRK's strong performance during past recessions, its diverse business mix, robust balance sheet, and substantial liquidity as reasons for its potential to outperform in an uncertain economic outlook. It also notes Buffett's historically aggressive share buybacks when the stock trades at such discounts and details recent portfolio moves, including the sale of Taiwan Semi shares and the success of BYD investments.

title: Groupon Outsources Legal Executive Role to Winston & Strawn (1)
summary: Groupon's general counsel, Dane Drobny, is stepping down next week to rejoin Winston & Strawn as a partner. Drobny will continue to serve as Groupon's outside general counsel through the end of the year, as the company undergoes restructuring and workforce reductions. His departure follows two rounds of layoffs at the e-commerce marketplace.

title: Garmin Ltd. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Garmin Ltd. (GRMN) stock declined by 0.14% to $97.01 on Friday, marking its second consecutive day of losses. Despite the overall market's mixed performance and Garmin's losses, the stock outperformed competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and Alphabet Inc. (GOOG, GOOGL) which saw larger drops. The trading volume for Garmin exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Familiar Faces Powering Growth Stock Resurgence
summary: Growth stocks are experiencing a resurgence in early 2023, primarily driven by large- and mega-cap names like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla, according to Morningstar research. This trend is significantly boosting ETFs such as the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). The rally is further supported by the strong performance of tech sectors like software infrastructure, software applications, and semiconductors, alongside investor optimism that the Federal Reserve may soon conclude its interest rate hikes, potentially averting a recession.

title: Trump’s Truth Social Dilemma
summary: This article examines Donald Trump's dilemma regarding his social media platform, Truth Social, one year after its launch. Despite having his Twitter and Meta accounts reinstated, Trump primarily uses Truth Social, but faces pressure to return to mainstream platforms for his 2024 presidential campaign's reach and fundraising, potentially impacting Truth Social's viability and his exclusivity deal.

title: Major League Soccer and RBC Wealth Management Announce Multi-Year Partnership
summary: Major League Soccer (MLS) and RBC Wealth Management have partnered in a multi-year deal aimed at engaging MLS's diverse fanbase and raising the profile of soccer in the lead-up to the 2026 World Cup. The partnership will include RBC Wealth Management's presence at MLS Player Engagement events, brand integration across various platforms, and a joint initiative with MLS WORKS to support the U.S. Soccer Foundation’s Soccer for Success program for approximately 500 children annually. This collaboration seeks to help RBC Wealth Management expand its business and connect with a new generation of investors while growing the game and strengthening community impact.

title: Apple HomePod 2 vs Sonos One: is it worth paying more?
summary: This article compares the Apple HomePod 2 and the Sonos One smart speakers, evaluating their price, design, sound quality, and smart features. While the HomePod 2 is significantly more expensive, the review concludes it offers superior performance and a more premium experience, especially for users integrated into the Apple ecosystem. The Sonos One remains a strong contender for those outside the Apple ecosystem or looking for a more affordable option.

title: Microsoft teams up with Nvidia and Nintendo in 10-year deal to stream Xbox games
summary: Microsoft has secured 10-year agreements with Nvidia and Nintendo to stream Xbox games as part of its strategy to address antitrust concerns regarding its proposed acquisition of Activision Blizzard. This partnership aims to make Xbox and potential Activision titles available on Nvidia's GeForce Now cloud gaming service and Nintendo platforms, covering various devices. The move is intended to placate regulators scrutinizing the $69 billion deal.

title: AMD Puts Hopes on Packaging, Memory on Logic, Optical Comms for Decade Ahead
summary: AMD outlined its decade-long strategy for continued performance and efficiency gains in computing at ISSCC 2023. The company aims to overcome tapering process technology benefits through advanced architecture, packaging technologies like 3D CPU & GPU silicon, and tighter integration of compute and memory, including processing-in-memory solutions. Additionally, AMD plans to leverage optical communications integrated with compute dies for efficiency boosts, continuing its fierce competition in the semiconductor industry with ever faster and more efficient chips.

title: Dexcom G6 Continuous Glucose Monitoring System Launches in Singapore
summary: Dexcom, Inc. has officially launched its G6 Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) System in Singapore for individuals aged two and older, including pregnant women. This system eliminates the need for routine fingersticks by continuously measuring and wirelessly sending glucose levels to a receiver or compatible smart device, offering real-time data and customizable alerts. The launch marks Dexcom's first CGM availability in Southeast Asia, with DKSH Singapore Pte Ltd appointed as the sales, marketing, and distribution service provider.

title: 2023 Clean200
summary: The 2023 Clean200 report identifies the top 200 public companies globally leading in sustainable practices and clean energy transition. These companies, from 35 countries, demonstrate that prioritizing sustainability can lead to strong financial performance, outperforming broad market and fossil fuel indices. The report highlights the growing importance of clean energy investment and the need for significant financial shifts to meet climate goals.

title: All 47 Warren Buffett Portfolio Stocks Now | Current 2023 Holdings List
summary: This article details Warren Buffett's 2023 portfolio, highlighting the top 20 holdings from Berkshire Hathaway's equity investments, valued at over $300 billion. It provides an overview of how investors can use Buffett's 13F filings to identify quality dividend stocks, listing key metrics and insights for each of the top companies. The article also categorizes all 47 stocks by GICS sector, offering analysis on their recent financial performance and competitive advantages.

title: Mercedes-Benz’s Superscreen Will Bring TikTok and Zoom to E-Class EVs
summary: Mercedes-Benz is integrating TikTok and Zoom into the entertainment system of its 2024 E-Class EVs via its new Superscreen display. The company has implemented safety measures to prevent distracted driving, such as disabling video transmission while the car is in motion and a visual shield to prevent the driver from seeing the passenger's screen. While the integration of productivity apps like Zoom caters to professionals, the inclusion of TikTok and games like Angry Birds also allows for entertainment during stationary periods.

title: 7 Super Speculative Stocks That Could Make You Very Rich
summary: This article identifies seven highly speculative stocks—Materion (MTRN), Magnite (MGNI), Lithium America (LAC), Sunrun (SUN), Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM), iCAD (ICAD), and Stem (STEM)—that have the potential for significant long-term gains. The author argues that despite their high risk, these stocks are well-positioned to capitalize on strong, ongoing market trends such as the growth of EVs, renewable energy, and digital advertising, especially as institutional investors increasingly re-enter growth sectors.

title: Unity Software stock slides nearly 20% as mobile-ad spending dries up and annual forecast weighs
summary: Unity Software Inc. shares fell nearly 20% after the company released its full-year forecast, which Wall Street found skeptical due to a currently weak quarter and declining mobile ad spending. This drop marked the worst decline for Unity stock since May 2022, when an advertising tool flaw was revealed. The company's struggles are attributed to a difficult annual forecast and the drying up of mobile ad spending.

title: Apple shareholder moves to boot Al Gore from board of directors
summary: An activist shareholder, the National Legal and Policy Center (NLPC), is calling for the removal of Al Gore from Apple Inc.'s board of directors, citing his political activism and perceived lack of qualifications. The NLPC argues that Gore's two-decade tenure is unwarranted and that his climate change advocacy poses a reputational risk to Apple. They also filed a separate exempt solicitation to oppose CEO Tim Cook's re-upping to the board and are sponsoring a shareholder proposal regarding Apple's business risks in China.

title: How does Spotify’s new AI powered DJ work?
summary: Spotify has launched a new AI-powered DJ feature designed to personalize the listening experience by curating music alongside commentary in a realistic voice. This new tool, currently in beta and using OpenAI technology, analyzes users' past listening habits to suggest music and rediscover old tracks, functioning similarly to "Discover Weekly" but with greater specificity. The feature is rolling out first in the US and Canada, aiming to enhance user engagement through tailored music selection and spoken insights.

title: This Week in the Metaverse: BlackRock's new ETF, Titanic artifacts as NFTs, and a Snow Crash Sotheby’s auction
summary: BlackRock has launched a new metaverse ETF, allowing investors to engage with companies contributing to virtual platforms, gaming, and AR/VR. In other news, never-before-seen photos of rapper Tupac Shakur are being released as NFTs, while artifacts from the Titanic will also be preserved and sold as NFTs. Additionally, Sotheby's is holding an auction to celebrate 30 years of Neal Stephenson's "Snow Crash," featuring physical and digital items tied to the seminal metaverse novel.

title: Step-By-Step Guide To Opening A moomoo Account
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to opening a moomoo securities account, highlighting the ease and speed of the process. It covers downloading the app, filling in details, waiting for confirmation, and funding the account, while also mentioning moomoo's value-added services like paper trading and a worldwide investment community. The guide aims to encourage both new and experienced Singaporean investors to try the moomoo platform due to its low trading fees and useful features.

title: How Reuters used AirTags to reveal Dow’s false recycling promises
summary: Reuters conducted an investigation into Dow Inc.'s recycling claims by embedding Apple AirTags into donated shoes. The investigation revealed that none of the shoes were recycled as promised by Dow, instead being tracked to an Indonesian flea market and other unexpected destinations. This highlights the clever use of AirTags to expose inconsistencies in corporate recycling initiatives.

title: If I'd invested $1,000 in Berkshire Hathaway shares 25 years ago, here's how much I'd have now!
summary: This article explores the hypothetical returns of investing $1,000 in Berkshire Hathaway B shares 25 years ago. The investment would have grown to $8,208.54, an increase of 713%, outperforming the S&P 500. The author also discusses Berkshire Hathaway's current portfolio, Warren Buffett's views on share buybacks, and the company's succession plans as key considerations for potential investors.

title: How Have the Top U.S. Asset Managers Voted in 2022?
source: The Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance
source_domain: The Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance
summary: This article analyzes how the top U.S. asset managers voted on key environmental and social shareholder resolutions in 2022, comparing trends across 2020-2022. It highlights a significant increase in social and environmental resolutions and varying support levels among major asset managers, especially the "Big Three" (Vanguard, BlackRock, State Street). The report also examines how sustainable funds within these managers voted compared to their wider fund ranges.

title: Apple pays $12.1 mln fine for alleged app market abuse in Russia - Antimonopoly Service
summary: Apple has paid a 906 million rouble ($12.12 million) fine to Russia's Federal Antimonopoly Service (FAS) over alleged abuse of its dominant position in the mobile apps market. The FAS had ruled in August 2020 that Apple's control over app distribution via its iOS operating system gave its own products a competitive advantage. This settlement follows a previous complaint from cybersecurity company Kaspersky Lab and comes after Apple paused product sales in Russia a year ago due to the conflict in Ukraine.

title: Apple iPhone's Clean Energy Charging causes stir on social media over battery charging times, 'shaming'
summary: Apple's Clean Energy Charging feature, introduced in iOS 16.1, is causing a stir on social media among iPhone users. The feature aims to reduce carbon footprint by only charging devices when lower carbon-emission electricity is available, leading to some users experiencing slower charging times. Some users are critical, questioning the feature's environmental benefits and claiming Apple "shames" them when it's deactivated.

title: PRF Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco RAFI US 1000 ETF)
summary: This article provides a detailed breakdown of the Invesco RAFI US 1000 ETF (PRF) holdings. It lists the top 25 stock holdings, including Alphabet, Apple, and Microsoft, which collectively account for 34.92% of the fund. The report also outlines PRF's geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures, with a primary focus on the United States and the Technology and Financials sectors.

title: BBLU Top Holdings List & Exposure (EA Bridgeway Blue Chip ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure breakdown for the EA Bridgeway Blue Chip ETF (BBLU). It lists the top 25 stock holdings by weight, including Broadcom, NVIDIA, and JPMorgan Chase & Co., which collectively comprise over 73% of the fund. Additionally, the article provides a comprehensive analysis of BBLU's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposure, revealing a 100% focus on the United States and USD, with significant allocations to Technology and Financials.

title: DGRW Top Holdings List & Exposure (WisdomTree U.S. Quality Dividend Growth Fund)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the WisdomTree U.S. Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW), an equity fund focusing on high dividend yield strategies within North America. It lists the top 25 stock holdings by weight, including NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, which collectively account for over 62% of the fund. Additionally, the article breaks down DGRW's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures, showing a predominant focus on the United States and the Technology sector.

title: Lightsource bp Places Multi-Year Order for 4GW of First Solar Modules
summary: Lightsource bp has placed a multi-year order for 4 gigawatts (GW) DC of advanced thin film solar modules from First Solar, to be delivered between 2026 and 2028 for projects in the US. This marks a significant commitment, building on a previous 4.3 GW order, and highlights the companies' collaboration in advancing US solar manufacturing and sustainability efforts. The deal includes First Solar’s Series 6 Plus and next-generation Series 7 modules, with Lightsource bp also committing to First Solar’s advanced high-value recycling program.

title: FTEC Top Holdings List & Exposure (Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC). FTEC's portfolio is heavily concentrated in major technology companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, with its top 25 holdings accounting for over 72% of the fund. The ETF shows significant exposure to the United States geographically, and primarily to the Technology sector, particularly Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment.

title: JGRO Top Holdings List & Exposure (JPMorgan Active Growth ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the JPMorgan Active Growth ETF (JGRO). It provides a list of the top 25 stock holdings by weight, including major technology companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet. The article also breaks down JGRO's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures, highlighting its significant allocation to the United States and the technology sector.

title: Why Target says 2022 was rougher than 2020
summary: Target Coo John Mulligan stated that 2022 was the most challenging operating environment of his career, even more so than 2020 during the height of the pandemic. Soaring inflation, supply chain issues, and changing consumer patterns led to significant drops in operating income and gross margins. In response, Target plans to cut $2 billion to $3 billion in costs, invest in customer-facing upgrades, expand owned brands and retail media, focus on price points and loyalty programs, and enhance convenience with expanded drive-up returns and store remodels.

title: IWV Top Holdings List & Exposure (iShares Russell 3000 ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings of the iShares Russell 3000 ETF (IWV), an equity fund investing in publicly traded companies primarily in North America. The top 25 holdings are listed by weight, with NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft leading. It also provides a breakdown of IWV's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures, showing a significant concentration in the United States and the Technology sector.

title: The 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality of 2023
summary: Fast Company has released its list of the 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality for 2023, highlighting AR and VR advancements despite a decrease in consumer headset sales. The article discusses how companies like Unity, Meta, Google, Niantic, and Varjo are pushing the boundaries of spatial computing for both consumer and enterprise applications. It also notes Apple's secretive efforts in mixed reality and the overall steady improvement in hardware and software for spatial computing experiences.

title: PNC Bank ends business relationship with Donald Trump Jr's MxM News app company 'without cause'
summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. has terminated its business relationship with MxM News, Donald Trump Jr.'s news aggregation app company, without cause. MxM News co-founder Taylor Budowich stated that PNC cited terms and conditions allowing them to re-evaluate business relationships at any time. Trump Jr. criticized the move, calling it another example of "woke corporations" targeting conservatives, but confirmed the issue was quickly resolved for MxM News.

title: VT vs ACWI ETF: Which is the Best World ETF?
summary: This article compares two popular World ETFs, Vanguard's Total World Stock Index Fund ETF (VT) and iShares' MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI), to help investors decide which is best for their portfolio. It delves into their differences in expense ratios, underlying indices, geographical breakdown, number of constituents, dividend yields, and fund managers, as well as their similarities in strategy, purpose, returns, liquidity, and sector breakdown. The author concludes that VT offers a lower expense ratio and exposure to smaller-cap companies, which might appeal to investors seeking that longer-tail exposure.

title: IBM Stock: What Is the Highest Price It Has Ever Been?
summary: IBM stock reached its all-time high of $148.74 on December 13, 2022, primarily driven by its acquisition of Red Hat and expansion into AI technology. Despite a recent decline and traditionally underperforming compared to some tech giants, its investments in hybrid cloud services and AI suggest potential for future growth, making it a possible long-term investment due to being undervalued.

title: WEBTOON and LINE Studio Announce March 14 Launch Date for the True Beauty Mobile Puzzle Game
summary: WEBTOON and LINE Studio have announced that the "True Beauty" mobile puzzle game will launch globally on March 14, available on Google Play and Apple App Store. The game is based on the popular webcomic "True Beauty," which has accumulated over six billion views, and will feature puzzle-style gameplay, fan-favorite characters, and customizable upgrades. Pre-registration for the game is currently open, offering limited edition in-game rewards.

title: IOO: Some Reasons To Stay Faithful Despite The Long Fall Ahead (NYSEARCA:IOO)
summary: The iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO) is an international equity ETF with a diversified portfolio, including major tech companies like Apple and Microsoft. Although the ETF may face a challenging medium term, the author believes it has long-term profit potential. Investors should consider its alternatives as economic recovery in the US could benefit IOO.

title: British chip giant Arm chooses New York listing in a blow to London
summary: British chip designer Arm, owned by Japan's SoftBank, and building materials group CRH have opted for U.S. stock market listings, delivering a blow to London's efforts to attract major companies. Arm stated that a U.S.-only listing in 2023 is the "best path forward," while CRH aims for increased commercial and acquisition opportunities in New York. This decision reinforces concerns that the U.K. stock market is struggling to compete with international rivals despite the British government's lobbying efforts and reforms to capital market rules.

title: Apple Readies Its Next Range of Macs, Including — Finally — a New iMac
summary: Apple is preparing to release a new lineup of Mac computers, notably including an updated iMac model. In addition to hardware news, the company is also facing changes in its executive team with the departure of its cloud chief, progressing with iOS and iPadOS updates, and recently secured an antitrust victory.

title: TSMC customer billed NT$529bn
summary: TSMC's largest customer, widely believed to be Apple Inc., generated NT$529.65 billion (US$17.32 billion) in revenue for the company last year, marking the first time a single customer contributed over NT$500 billion. Despite this record revenue, the customer's share of TSMC's total sales decreased from 26% in 2021 to 23% in 2022. This shift is attributed to significant growth in TSMC's automotive chip sales, which outperformed those for high-performance computing and smartphones.

title: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence on right to repair, spatial audio and those never-ending lawsuits
summary: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence discusses the company's new Era 100 and Era 300 speakers, focusing on the emergence of spatial audio and its mainstream adoption challenges. He also addresses Sonos' commitment to right-to-repair principles, its premium pricing strategy, the future of the smart home market, and ongoing lawsuits with Google.

title: Virtual try-on is being hit by class actions. Should brands worry?
summary: Fashion and beauty brands like Dior, Louis Vuitton, and Estée Lauder are facing class-action lawsuits over their use of biometric data collected through virtual try-on technology under the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA). These lawsuits highlight the need for brands to prioritize clear disclosure and consent regarding customer data, particularly sensitive biometric information, even as virtual try-on continues to grow in popularity and benefits. While legal challenges may represent "teething problems" for the industry, experts emphasize the importance of robust security and transparency practices to navigate evolving privacy regulations.

title: Apple and Foxconn efforts win labour reforms to advance Indian production plans - FT
summary: Apple and its supplier Foxconn successfully lobbied for significant labor law reforms in India's southern state of Karnataka. These new laws permit 12-hour shifts and night work for women, aligning with practices in China. This move is part of Apple's broader strategy to diversify its production away from China due to COVID-19 disruptions and geopolitical tensions, with new iPhone assembly plants planned for Karnataka.

title: Sonos Bets on Spatial Audio as a Key Brand Differentiator
summary: Sonos is positioning spatial audio as a critical differentiator with its new Era 100 and Era 300 speakers. The Era 300 is the first Sonos speaker to support spatial audio, delivering an immersive 3D audio experience tailored for movie and TV content, and is anticipated to be a definitive way to listen to spatial audio mixes. The company believes embracing spatial audio will solidify its leadership in the premium audio market, even as wider streaming support for the technology is still developing.

title: Apple Inc. to Launch Apple Music Classical App
summary: Apple Inc. is launching a new standalone app called Apple Music Classical, specifically designed for classical music enthusiasts. This app will feature over 5 million tracks, immersive spatial audio, curated playlists, composer biographies, and complete metadata for accurate displays. It will be available for pre-order and officially launch on March 28, 2023, at no extra cost for existing Apple Music subscribers.

title: Four Ways to Cluster based on Correlation
summary: This article explores four methods to cluster 20 companies based on the correlation of their stock prices. It aims to see if these methods can accurately classify companies into categories like tech or oil and gas, as an alternative to clustering based on raw stock prices. The objective is to validate whether correlation-based clustering reveals meaningful industry groupings.

title: Super-investor Mohnish Pabrai’s value investing principles
summary: Mohnish Pabrai, founder and CEO of Pabrai Investment Funds, is a value investor inspired by Warren Buffett. He has amassed a significant net worth by focusing on undervalued stocks for the long term, despite only holding a few select companies. The article outlines five of Pabrai's key investment strategies, including minimizing downside risk, avoiding shorting, approaching retail investments cautiously, buying at "maximum pessimism," and understanding a company's "DNA structure."

title: From Apple to Boeing, India is being put to the test as China manufacturing alternative
summary: India is emerging as a potential manufacturing alternative to China, attracting major companies like Boeing and Apple. While significant deals, such as Boeing's record-breaking aircraft order, signal growing ties, India faces substantial hurdles including bureaucracy, lagging infrastructure, and red tape in its ambition to become a global manufacturing hub. Despite these challenges, India's economic growth projections and strategic partnerships indicate a strong push towards self-reliance and increased integration into global supply chains.

title: Federal Circuit Allows Apple’s Fintiv Challenge To Proceed
summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals has revived Apple's challenge to the PTAB's Fintiv practice of discretionary denials, allowing it to proceed to the district court. This decision questions the USPTO's ability to issue rules through precedential decisions rather than traditional notice-and-comment rulemaking. The resolution of this case could significantly impact how the USPTO operates regarding Inter Partes Review practices.

title: Fall Detection Systems Market Current Status and Future
summary: The Fall Detection Systems market is experiencing significant growth driven by an aging global population and increased chronic diseases. These systems are crucial for quickly alerting caregivers or emergency services in the event of a fall, providing peace of mind for seniors and their families, and helping to maintain independence. The market is segmented by type, technology, and end-user, with key players like Koninklijke Philips N.V., ADT Security Services, and Apple Inc. involved in developing these essential health monitoring solutions.

title: MLBN and Apple TV+ national broadcasts to show Zoom Replay Operations Center live under new deal
summary: Major League Baseball (MLB) and Zoom Video Communications (NASDAQ: ZM) have announced a new partnership that will enhance the fan viewership experience by showing the Zoom Replay Operations Center live on MLB Network and Apple TV+ national broadcasts. This collaboration aims to create a more transparent replay review process, allowing fans to see the decision-making in action and hear from expert rules analysts. Beyond broadcasts, the deal also includes standardizing replay reviews with Zoom technology for on-field umpires and broader league-wide Zoom integration for secure information transmission, including the MLB Draft.

title: Los avisos de consumo que implementó el BBVA en la app (incluye débito)
summary: BBVA has implemented an immediate consumption alert system in its banking app, including for debit card transactions, which was not previously available through traditional card services like Visa Home or MasterConsultas. This new feature provides real-time notifications for purchases, improving security and enabling better tracking of expenses, especially for travelers. The author discusses the system's effectiveness, contrasting it with the outdated notifications from card networks, and hopes other banks will adopt similar immediate alert systems.

title: Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway speeds up stock buybacks
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. has significantly increased its stock buybacks, repurchasing over $1.8 billion worth of its shares this year. This move suggests that Buffett and Charlie Munger believe Berkshire's stock is undervalued, following nearly $60 billion in buybacks between 2020 and 2022. The company sees repurchases as a prudent use of its substantial cash reserves, despite a proposed quadrupling of the 1% excise tax on buybacks.

title: Raja Koduri, Main Driver Of Intel’s GPU Efforts, To Leave Company
summary: Raja Koduri, Intel's chief architect and the main driver behind its discrete GPU efforts, is leaving the company at the end of March to start a new software company focused on generative AI for gaming, media, and entertainment. Koduri had been with Intel for over five years, leading the buildout of its graphics capabilities and serving as chief architect for most of his tenure. His departure comes as Intel announced cuts to its data center GPU roadmap and delays to Falcon Shores.

title: For Broad-Based Large-Cap ESG Exposure, See CVLC
summary: The article highlights the Calvert US Large-Cap Core Responsible Index ETF (CVLC) as a significant option for investors seeking broad-based large-cap ESG exposure, particularly focusing on climate change and net-zero goals. It emphasizes CVLC's ability to identify companies taking actual steps towards net-zero objectives, citing examples like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla. The ETF offers a tangible way for investors to align their portfolios with values related to environmental sustainability and responsible investing.

title: Consider CVLC for Broad-Based, Large-Cap ESG Exposure
summary: The Calvert US Large-Cap Core Responsible Index ETF (CVLC) offers investors broad exposure to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles, particularly focusing on climate change and net-zero trends. Launched in February by Morgan Stanley Investment Management, CVLC includes companies like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla that are actively pursuing net-zero goals. The ETF helps investors identify companies genuinely committed to ESG, making it a relevant option as net-zero becomes an increasingly important investment criterion.

title: AB US Low Volatility Equity ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the AB US Low Volatility Equity ETF (LOWV), which was established on March 22, 2023. The ETF focuses on large- and mid-cap US stocks, actively managed to achieve lower volatility than the broader market. Key holdings include major technology companies like Alphabet, Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, and Amazon, alongside diversified investments across various sectors.

title: Friday Q&A: Movano takes on the women’s wearables market with a smart ring
summary: Movano Health is launching the Evie ring, a smart ring specifically designed for women's health, focusing on metrics like heart rate, blood oxygen, menstrual symptoms, and sleep patterns. The company aims for FDA clearance of its heart rate and blood oxygen monitoring features and is developing a radio frequency-enabled sensor for blood pressure and glucose monitoring. Movano's unique approach to accuracy, especially in glucose monitoring, utilizes millimeter wave technology to overcome limitations of light-based methods.

title: Taiwan PCB makers moving production to Southeast Asia
summary: Taiwanese Printed Circuit Board (PCB) manufacturers are relocating production from China to Southeast Asian countries like Thailand, Malaysia, and Vietnam. This move is driven by factors such as investment incentives and lower manufacturing costs in these regions, despite challenges including wastewater management and anti-Chinese sentiment in some areas. Companies like Unimicron, Kinsus Interconnect Technology, and Tripod Technology are establishing or expanding facilities, capitalizing on the growing semiconductor supply chain in Malaysia and Vietnam.

title: Beloved clothing store closes - leaving customers scrambling for new options
summary: The clothing retailer Buckle is closing one of its North Carolina stores at Northlake Mall on March 26, following Apple's recent departure from the same location. This closure is linked to a period of scrutiny for the mall due to a series of shootings, which council members say has led to 25% vacancy rates. Despite not citing the shootings as a reason, Buckle will continue operating in nearby malls.

title: Skip Big Tech And Buy Bank of America (NYSE:BAC)
summary: The article suggests investors consider Bank of America (BAC) over large tech stocks, particularly in light of recent banking sector pressures. It highlights the significant opportunities created by these pressures for both regional and large banks. Bank of America is presented as an attractive investment due to its strong dividend growth record, with a five-year DGR of 15% and nine consecutive years of dividend increases.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook meets with China's commerce minister on supply chain
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook met with China's minister of commerce, Wang Wentao, to discuss industrial and supply chain stabilization. This meeting took place while Cook was in China to attend the China Development Forum, and follows a period of strict lockdowns and supply chain disruptions in the country. The discussion also occurred as Apple is reportedly working to diversify its production away from a heavy reliance on China, with major supplier Foxconn planning a new factory in India.

title: Apple introduces Apple Pay Later to allow consumers to pay for purchases over time
summary: Apple has introduced Apple Pay Later in the U.S., allowing users to split purchases between $50 and $1,000 into four payments over six weeks with no interest or fees. Designed with financial health in mind, the service integrates into Apple Wallet for easy tracking and management of loans, with repayment via linked debit cards. Apple Financing LLC, a subsidiary of Apple Inc., is responsible for credit assessment and lending, and will report loans to U.S. credit bureaus starting in the fall, while Mastercard and Goldman Sachs facilitate the program.

title: AutoCAD for Mac 2024: New Collaboration Enhancements and Improved Performance With M-Series Chips
summary: AutoCAD for Mac 2024 and AutoCAD LT for Mac 2024 have been released, introducing significant performance enhancements, particularly for Apple Silicon M-series chips, which can double performance compared to the previous version. The new release focuses on improving collaboration and automation with features like Markup Import and Markup Assist, Smart Blocks for automatic block placement, and updates to the Trace environment. Additionally, it includes 3D Color Background customization, 3D Connexion device support, and new language support for Italian and Spanish.

title: Apple launches 'buy now, pay later' service in US
summary: Apple Inc. has launched its "buy now, pay later" (BNPL) service, Apple Pay Later, in the United States, posing a threat to established fintech firms like Affirm and Klarna. The service allows users to split purchases into four interest-free payments over six weeks for amounts between $50 and $1,000 on Apple Pay-enabled online and in-app purchases. This move has already impacted competitors' stock, with Apple directly underwriting, funding, and collecting on the loans.

title: Apple Introduces Buy Now, Pay Later Service. Here's How It Will Work.
summary: Apple is launching a "buy now, pay later" service, integrated with Apple Pay and Mastercard, which will require debit card and bank account payments rather than credit cards, and will not charge late fees. The loans will be interest-free, but missed payments could lead to loss of access to the service and be reported to credit bureaus. This service aims to provide a flexible payment option, especially for consumers seeking alternatives to traditional credit, though experts advise caution regarding potential debt accumulation and lack of consumer protections compared to credit cards.

title: Investment Giant Fidelity Offers New Metaverse and Crypto Payments ETFs
summary: Fidelity Investments, managing over $4.2 trillion in assets, has launched two new ETFs: the Fidelity Crypto Industry and Digital Payments ETF (FDIG) and the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET). These new offerings aim to expand Fidelity's presence in the digital ecosystem, catering to growing investor demand, especially from younger demographics, for exposure to crypto and metaverse industries. While FDIG does not offer direct crypto exposure, it invests in companies supporting the digital asset ecosystem, and FMET focuses on companies building out the metaverse.

title: Live Nation head says ‘speculative ticketing’ to blame for difficulty with buying event tickets
summary: Live Nation Entertainment President and Chief Financial Officer Joe Berchtold attributes difficulties in buying event tickets to "speculative ticketing" by secondary sellers. He explained that these sellers list tickets they don't yet possess and use bots to acquire them when sales officially open. Berchtold also highlighted Live Nation's push for a "Fair Ticketing Act" to combat these practices, make speculative ticketing illegal, and ensure transparent "all-in pricing" for consumers.

title: Apple lawsuit claims company fails to pay overtime compensation at correct rate
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Inc. alleging that the company fails to properly compensate its non-exempt employees for overtime. The lawsuit claims Apple does not include the value of vested restricted stock units in the regular rate of pay when calculating overtime, thereby violating the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). The plaintiff, Francis Costa, seeks to represent a nationwide class of current and former non-exempt Apple employees who were awarded and vested restricted stock units.

title: Is Google ending?
summary: This article explores whether Google's dominance in search and AI is coming to an end, citing challenges like failed product launches (Duplex), internal scandals, and a talent drain. The rise of competitors like OpenAI's ChatGPT and increased competition from Amazon and Apple in online search are highlighted as significant threats. Despite these setbacks, the article concludes that Google's vast resources, default search engine status, and strategic investments in AI mean it is still too early to write the company off.

title: SPDR S&P 500 (SPY) Stock: Breakout Leveraging Tech Stocks Rally?
summary: The SPDR S&P 500 (SPY) ETF has shown a positive resurgence in 2023, largely driven by gains in tech and semiconductor stocks, which comprise a significant portion of its portfolio. Technical analysis suggests a potential bullish breakout for SPY, with indicators pointing to an upcoming rally. Investors are advised to watch support levels and anticipate a rise leveraging the strength of its underlying tech holdings.

title: Top and Flop ETFs of March
summary: The U.S. stock market experienced extreme volatility in March, characterized by bank failures and a tech-driven rally. This article highlights the top and flop ETFs of the month, with technology and gold-related funds performing well, while regional bank and natural gas ETFs saw declines.

title: Apple wins appeal against UK's decision to investigate its mobile browser
summary: Apple Inc. has successfully appealed the UK's Competition and Market Authority (CMA) decision to investigate its mobile browser and cloud gaming services. The Competition Appeal Tribunal ruled that the CMA had no power to launch the probe so late, as it should have been initiated at the time of a related report in June. The CMA expressed disappointment, stating the ruling limits its ability to investigate markets efficiently.

title: Garmin Forerunner 265 review: Brilliant but far too expensive
summary: The Garmin Forerunner 265 is praised for its accuracy, new AMOLED screen, and improved training features like Training Readiness. However, the review criticizes its significant price increase of £130 over its predecessor, the Forerunner 255, making it a difficult recommendation despite its excellent performance. The article suggests that consumers might be better off with the older, cheaper model or other Garmin alternatives.

title: 12 Most Advanced Countries in the World In 2023
summary: This article identifies the 12 most technologically advanced countries in the world for 2023, based on internet and LTE usage, innovation capacity, and GDP allocated to R&D. South Korea, the USA, and Denmark lead the list, recognized for their robust tech sectors, strong educational systems, and significant investments in research and development. The article highlights that no single region dominates technological progress, underscoring the collaborative and diverse nature of global innovation.

title: iShares MSCI USA Quality Factor ETF (QUAL) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI USA Quality Factor ETF (QUAL), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, dividend data, and historical performance. As of the latest N-PORT filing on 2025-07-31, QUAL holds 125 stocks with NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple as its top positions. The ETF has a current dividend yield of 0.94% and a P/E ratio of 26.81.

title: ProShares Ultra QQQ 2x Shares (QLD) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of ProShares Ultra QQQ 2x Shares (QLD), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It highlights QLD's current portfolio value of $6.70 billion with top holdings like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, and details its performance and historical data. The article also lists recent trades and key fundamental data.

title: Schwab 1000 Index ETF (SCHK) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Schwab 1000 Index ETF (SCHK), detailing its stock price, holdings, dividend yield, and key financial statistics. As of November 30, 2025, SCHK held 994 stocks with a total value of $4.83 billion, with top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft. The ETF also shows various performance metrics, key statistics, and historical data like PE and PB ratios, and dividend information.

title: iShares Russell Top 200 Growth ETF (IWY) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares Russell Top 200 Growth ETF (IWY), covering its stock price, holdings, dividend yield, and key financial statistics. It highlights the ETF's top holdings as of December 31, 2025, which include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet. The report also details the ETF's performance, asset allocation, and historical data like price-to-earnings and dividend yields.

title: Apple and global suppliers expand renewable energy to 13.7 gigawatts
summary: Apple announced that its manufacturing partners now support over 13 gigawatts of renewable electricity globally, a 30% increase from the previous year. More than 250 suppliers across 28 countries are committed to using 100% renewable energy for Apple production by 2030, covering over 85% of its direct manufacturing spend. Apple also highlighted its $4.7 billion Green Bonds, which fund clean energy solutions, low-carbon design, energy efficiency, and carbon removal, contributing to the avoidance of 17.4 million metric tons of carbon emissions last year.

title: PGIM High Yield Bond Fund, Inc. (ISD) Stock Forecast & Price Prediction 2026–2030
summary: This article provides a comprehensive stock forecast for PGIM High Yield Bond Fund, Inc. (ISD) from 2026 to 2030 and beyond, utilizing technical analysis. It shows short-term predictions for the next five days and long-term monthly predictions up to 2057, indicating a generally bearish sentiment with expected price decreases over time. The forecast, based on moving averages and other indicators, suggests ISD will not reach significantly higher price targets like $100.

title: Frontdoor App Changes the Game of Home Repair and Maintenance
summary: Frontdoor, Inc. has launched a new app that aims to revolutionize home repair and maintenance by connecting homeowners with pre-qualified experts via video chat. The app, available in mid-April, offers various features including DIY tips, maintenance reminders, and discounts. A new campaign from Fallon and Chemistry supports the launch, which also marks a reunion of brand and agency leaders.

title: EMBD Stock Price | Global X Emerging Markets Bond ETF
