summary: This article provides detailed information about the Global X Emerging Markets Bond ETF (EMBD), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and various key statistics such as dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings. It also covers technical analysis ratings and performance metrics, offering a comprehensive overview for potential investors.

title: Green Visor Financial Technology Acquisition Corp. I (GVCI) Stock Live Price, Chart & News
summary: This article provides live stock price, chart, and news for Green Visor Financial Technology Acquisition Corp. I (GVCI). It shows the current stock price, market capitalization, trading volume, and performance metrics. The company's stock trades on NASDAQ and currently has a bullish price prediction according to the site.

title: Flaherty & Crumrine Total Return Fund Inc. (FLC) Stock Live Price, Chart & News
summary: This article provides live stock price, chart, and news for Flaherty & Crumrine Total Return Fund Inc. (FLC). It details current trading information, performance statistics, and fundamental data for FLC, which is trading on the NYSE. The article also includes a list of recent news headlines related to FLC from Seeking Alpha, highlighting past analysis and outlooks on the fund.

title: General Motors hates your iPhone
summary: General Motors has decided to remove CarPlay and Android Auto support from its future electric vehicles, opting instead for its own Android Automotive-based infotainment system. This decision, driven by GM's desire for increased control over data and subscription revenues, is criticized for prioritizing corporate interests over user convenience and potentially degrading the in-car experience. The article raises concerns about app compatibility, data syncing, and the long-term software quality in GM vehicles, questioning whether other automakers will follow suit or leverage CarPlay as a competitive advantage.

title: iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO), detailing its stock price, current holdings, and dividend yield. As of its latest N-PORT filing on September 30, 2025, IOO primarily invests in major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Broadcom. The ETF manages $7.30 billion in common equities with a 0% turnover rate, offering key statistics and historical performance data.

title: Apple says 'Hello Mumbai' at first India store launch
summary: Apple Inc. has revealed the appearance of its first retail store in India, located in Mumbai, which is expected to open in April. India has become a significant market for Apple, leading to the launch of an online retail store in 2020 and plans to expand manufacturing operations for products like iPhones, iPads, and AirPods within the country. The new store's design is inspired by Mumbai's iconic black and yellow taxis.

title: Apple sued to stop App Store clones of Baidu's Ernie AI chatbot
summary: Baidu has initiated legal action against Apple and various app developers in Beijing, seeking the removal of numerous unauthorized "Ernie bot" applications from the App Store. The Chinese tech giant claims that these apps are fake, as Baidu has not yet released an official application for its Ernie AI chatbot, which is similar to ChatGPT. Baidu has warned users that any Ernie app currently available is counterfeit and has urged caution against selling access codes for the bot.

title: Social Livestreaming Platform Bigo Live Removed From App Store
summary: Bigo Live, a social livestreaming platform, has been removed from the App Store. The article by Pandaily reports on this development.

title: Introducing Domino's® on Apple CarPlay®: The Easiest Way to Order Pizza on the Go
summary: Domino's Pizza has launched a new ordering feature through its iOS app on Apple CarPlay, allowing customers to easily order pizza on the go through "Tap to Order" or "Call to Order" options. This integration highlights Domino's leadership in combining technology with pizza ordering convenience, aiming to enhance customer experience and offering an alternative to traditional drive-thru waiting. The company reported global retail sales exceeding $17.5 billion in 2022, with a substantial portion from digital channels, underscoring its strong market presence and reliance on tech-driven solutions for ordering.

title: Domino’s Introduces In-Car Ordering
summary: Domino's Pizza has launched a new in-car ordering system through its iOS app on Apple CarPlay, allowing customers to place orders without waiting in drive-thru lines. This new feature, part of Domino's AnyWare ordering platforms, offers "Tap to Order" for saved meals or "Call to Order" for hands-free placement. Domino's is among the first quick-service restaurants to offer this convenient on-the-go ordering method.

title: Baidu sues Apple, app developers over fake Ernie bot apps
summary: Baidu has filed lawsuits against app developers and Apple Inc. over counterfeit versions of its AI-powered Ernie bot app appearing on Apple's App Store. The Chinese search engine giant clarified that no official Ernie app currently exists and warned against purchasing access codes. Baidu's Ernie bot, launched last month, is considered China's answer to ChatGPT.

title: Apple iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15 Pro Max Might Stick To Physical Instead Of Solid State Buttons
summary: Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reports that the iPhone 15 Pro and 15 Pro Max will likely retain physical buttons instead of moving to solid-state buttons due to unresolved technical challenges. This change might negatively impact Taptic Engine component suppliers like Cirrus Logic and AAC Technologies. However, it is not expected to delay mass production or shipments, and the solid-state button concept may be pushed to the iPhone 16 series.

title: TSMC’s Taiwan factory plans have not changed, says minister
summary: Taiwan's Economy Minister Wang Mei-hua stated that TSMC's investment plans for its new chip factory in Kaohsiung, Taiwan, remain unchanged, despite a media report suggesting a slowdown in expansion. Wang emphasized that TSMC continues to view Taiwan as its global R&D and manufacturing hub. TSMC had previously postponed plans to build advanced 7-nanometre chips at the Kaohsiung plant, though the facility will still produce 28-nanometre semiconductors.

title: Apple will use 100 percent recycled cobalt in batteries by 2025
summary: Apple announced a significant acceleration of its efforts to use recycled materials in its products, setting a goal to use 100% recycled cobalt in all Apple-designed batteries by 2025. By the same year, magnets in Apple devices will use entirely recycled rare earth elements, and all Apple-designed printed circuit boards will incorporate 100% recycled tin soldering and gold plating. This initiative is part of Apple's broader commitment to eventually make all products with only recycled and renewable materials and achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.

title: Legal Briefs: News From Around NH
summary: This article provides a round-up of legal news from New Hampshire. Key updates include the launch of a new cloud-based child support system by Conduent Inc. for the NH Department of Health and Human Services, the nomination of Associate Attorney General Anne M. Edwards for a Superior Court judgeship, and upcoming oral arguments before the Supreme Court regarding gerrymandering challenges. Additionally, three new attorneys have joined the Devine Millimet law firm.

title: 10 up-and-coming stocks, according to Gen Z
summary: A Piper Sandler report highlights ten stocks popular with Gen Z, who spend an estimated $67 billion annually. These companies, including Crocs, e.l.f. Cosmetics, and Lululemon, are favored by teens for their products and services, leading analysts to give them "overweight" ratings and strong price targets. The article details why each company resonates with Gen Z and their current stock performance.

title: Apple Triples India iPhone Output to $7 Billion in China Shift
summary: Apple Inc. significantly increased its iPhone production in India last fiscal year, reaching over $7 billion, which represents a nearly threefold increase. This expansion underscores Apple's strategy to diversify its manufacturing base and reduce its reliance on China, with India now producing almost 7% of global iPhones. This marks a substantial jump from 2021, when India contributed only an estimated 1% of the world's iPhones.

title: Apple Plans to Launch More Than Just Its New Headset at WWDC
summary: Apple's upcoming Worldwide Developers Conference in June 2023 is expected to be a major product launch event, featuring not only the anticipated mixed-reality headset but also new Macs, watchOS 10, and iOS 17. The article highlights other recent Apple news, including the passing of its communications chief, new Mac testing, and the opening of its first India stores.

title: Autodesk Technology Engagement Center breaks ground at CSUN
summary: California State University, Northridge has broken ground on the $50-million Autodesk Technology Engagement Center, which will house the Global Hispanic Serving Institution Equity Innovation Hub. This new facility aims to steer Latinos and other underserved communities into STEM careers through research labs, a maker space, and student showcase areas. Funded by Autodesk, the State of California, and Apple, the center will leverage XR technology and address the underrepresentation of the Latino community in the tech industry.

title: Ben & Jerry's ice cream 'scoopers' launch union drive
summary: Workers at the flagship Ben & Jerry's scoop shop in Burlington, Vermont, have initiated a union drive over concerns regarding pay, health benefits, and having a voice in staffing decisions. They are organizing with Workers United, the same union involved in the Starbucks organizing campaign. This move comes as the ice cream maker, known for its social justice values, faces increased unionization efforts across various companies.

title: Magna to engineer and build off-road electric vehicle for industrial group Ineos
summary: Magna International Inc. will engineer and build electric off-road vehicles for U.K.-based industrial conglomerate Ineos Group Ltd. Production is expected to start in 2026 at Magna's facility in Graz, Austria, expanding on their previous collaboration for Ineos's fossil-fuel powered Grenadier. Magna is also investing significantly in Canada to increase its EV-related production capacity.

title: Samsung competes with Apple on rising non-terrestrial network market
summary: Samsung is entering the non-terrestrial network (NTN) market, developing a modem chip for its Galaxy smartphones to rival Apple, Qualcomm, and MediaTek. The global NTN market, which enables communication via satellites in remote areas, is projected to grow significantly to $8.8 billion by 2026. Samsung's Exynos Modem 5300 will offer 5G NTN capabilities, competing with Apple's iPhone 14 series and upcoming solutions from Qualcomm and MediaTek.

title: YieldMax APLY ETF Seeks Income Through Call Options on Apple Stock
summary: The YieldMax APLY Option Income Strategy ETF (APLY) has launched, aiming to generate monthly income by selling call options on Apple (AAPL) stock. APLY, managed by Toroso Investments and ZEGA Financial, uses a synthetic covered call strategy to provide exposure to AAPL’s returns and income from option premiums, without directly investing in the stock. It operates with an expense ratio of 0.99% and maintains its strategy regardless of market conditions.

title: Big Tech Illegally Uses NDAs to Silence Would-Be-Whistleblowers, SEC Tips Allege
summary: The SEC has received whistleblower tips alleging that major tech companies, including an Apple Inc. subcontractor, Electronic Arts Inc., and Block Inc., are using illegally restrictive non-disclosure agreements (NDAs). These NDAs reportedly forbid employees from reporting misconduct to the SEC, in violation of Rule 21F-17 of the SEC Whistleblower Program. The law firm Kohn, Kohn & Colapinto LLP filed these complaints, highlighting a perceived willful disregard for the law given previous SEC enforcement actions against such agreements.

title: TSMC seeks $15B in U.S. support despite ‘unacceptable’ conditions
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is seeking $15 billion in U.S. government aid for its Arizona chip plants but finds some of the conditions attached to the CHIPS Act funding "unacceptable." The primary concern involves rules requiring profit-sharing if returns exceed projections, which TSMC chairman Mark Liu fears could complicate profit calculations and make the Arizona investment less attractive. Other conditions, like providing affordable childcare and restrictions on expanding manufacturing in "countries of concern" such as China, also pose challenges for chipmakers.

title: Semiconductor industry electricity consumption to more than double by 2030: study
summary: A Greenpeace East Asia report projects that global semiconductor manufacturing will consume 237 terawatt hours of electricity by 2030, more than doubling current levels and equating to Australia's 2021 electricity consumption. This energy-intensive industry, critical to the tech supply chain, is also set to emit 86 million tonnes of CO2e by 2030, exceeding Portugal's 2021 total emissions. The report criticizes major manufacturers like Samsung and TSMC for their reliance on fossil fuels and lack of ambitious 100% renewable energy commitments by 2030, urging them to accelerate renewable energy development in East Asia.

title: Evaluating ESG’s Role in Creating Value
summary: This article examines the increasing demand for ESG commitments from companies and evaluates their impact on financial performance. A Bain & Company study suggests that while not always directly correlated, ESG activities are associated with positive financial outcomes like revenue growth and improved EBITDA margins. The article emphasizes that ESG efforts, particularly in social and governance areas like diversity, contribute to the overall success and profitability of companies.

title: Eye on Tap to Pay: MagicCube Teams Up With ACI; Fiserv Offers Tap to Pay on Clover
summary: MagicCube and Fiserv are expanding the adoption of tap-to-pay technology on smartphones for merchants. MagicCube partnered with ACI Worldwide to enable small and mid-sized merchants to accept contactless payments with or without a PIN, enhancing payment acceptance without additional POS terminals. Simultaneously, Fiserv introduced Tap to Pay on iPhone for its Clover merchants, allowing them to accept contactless payments via the Clover Go iOS app, increasing flexibility and customer engagement.

title: 3 reasons why the Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) could be an excellent investment for beginners
summary: The Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) is presented as an excellent investment option for beginners due to its significant diversification across major developed markets and sectors, its strong long-term performance, and its low management fees. The ETF offers exposure to over 1,400 businesses globally, including tech giants, and has historically delivered solid returns while keeping costs down.

title: In Masimo v. Apple Trial Twist, Jurors Suddenly Learn Apple Hired Many Masimo Employees
summary: During the Masimo v. Apple trade secrets trial, a dramatic turn occurred when an Apple witness violated a pre-trial order, leading the judge to reverse his decision and allow Masimo to introduce new evidence. Jurors subsequently learned that Apple had hired at least seven Masimo employees, not just the two initially disclosed. This revelation, along with evidence of Apple's "Project Everest" targeting Masimo, allowed Masimo to present a fuller picture of Apple's hiring practices amidst closing arguments in the high-stakes $3 billion trial.

title: Elon Musk Threatens to Sue Microsoft Over Alleged Illegal Data Usage
summary: Elon Musk is threatening to sue Microsoft, alleging unauthorized use of Twitter's data for training its AI models. This comes after Microsoft stopped supporting Twitter on one of its advertising platforms due to Musk's high asking price for API access. Musk claims Microsoft "trained illegally using Twitter data" and sees this as an opportune moment for legal action, especially given Microsoft's $10 billion investment in OpenAI and the integration of GPT-4 into Bing.

title: Webull expands to Japan
summary: Webull Corporation has launched a local version of its trading app in Japan through Webull Securities (Japan) Co. Ltd., offering Japanese investors access to thousands of U.S. listed stocks and ETFs, including fractional shares. This expansion follows successful launches in Singapore and Australia, with plans to introduce Japanese securities, U.S. options, and Chinese and Hong Kong S.A.R. securities later in the year. The platform aims to attract both experienced and new investors with its convenient mobile trading tools and low commissions.

title: Starbucks, Ford, Apple Lead Another Busy Earnings Week
summary: Starbucks, Apple, Marriott, Uber, Ford, and CVS Health are among the major companies scheduled to report their quarterly earnings in the coming days. These reports will provide insights into consumer spending habits amid a slowing economy and persistent inflation. Most companies have already exceeded Wall Street's expectations for the quarter.

title: Forerunner 965, 265, 265S: Everything to know about Garmin’s first AMOLED display-toting running watches in India
summary: Garmin has launched its first AMOLED display running watches in India, including the Forerunner 965, 265, and 265S. These watches offer extensive tracking features for runners and triathletes, with the 965 boasting advanced mapping and longer battery life. The prices are Rs 67,490 for the Forerunner 965 and Rs 50,490 for the 265 series, and they are available both online and offline.

title: If Expense Ratio Really Mattered, Most of These ETFs Would Dominate
summary: Despite investors consistently indicating that expense ratio is the most important factor in selecting an ETF, several low- or no-cost ETFs with significant assets remain under the radar. Examples include the BNY Mellon US Large Cap ETF (BKLC) with a 0.00% expense ratio, the JPMorgan BetaBuilders US Equity ETF (BBUS) at 0.02%, and the SoFi Select 500 ETF (SFY) also at 0.00%. The article suggests advisors should investigate these cost-effective options for client portfolios given their stated preference for low expense ratios.

title: Masimo v. Apple – Judge Declares a Mistrial as Jury Hopelessly Deadlocked
summary: A mistrial has been declared in the $1.8 billion trade secret lawsuit between Masimo Corp. and Apple Inc. after the jury became hopelessly deadlocked, leaning significantly towards Apple's defense. Both companies are expected to pursue a new trial, with Masimo planning to retry the case and Apple seeking to dismiss the remaining claims. The article suggests Masimo may have been overconfident due to previous wins against Apple, while Apple's legal team presented a strong defense.

title: Apple fights $2 billion London lawsuit for 'throttling' millions of iPhones
summary: Apple is facing a substantial $2 billion lawsuit in London, accused of "throttling" millions of iPhones by covertly installing software updates to manage defective batteries. Consumer advocate Justin Gutmann, representing UK iPhone users, claims Apple concealed battery issues and limited performance. Apple denies these allegations, calling the lawsuit "baseless" and stating that their power management update only minimally affected performance for certain older models, while offering free battery replacements for a small number of iPhone 6s models.

title: Qualcomm outlook grim as smartphone sales stay weak
summary: Qualcomm Inc. forecast third-quarter revenue and profit below Wall Street estimates due to weak smartphone sales and excess chip inventory. The company's CEO noted a lack of meaningful recovery, particularly in China, and faces increased competition and a rise in used phone sales impacting premium device demand. Qualcomm's shares fell nearly 7% after the announcement.

title: Joe Kiani, Co-founder and CEO of Masimo, Talks About Fighting Corporate Giants and His Fixation on Reducing Preventable Hospital Deaths
summary: Joe Kiani, co-founder and CEO of Masimo, discusses his journey from an immigrant to a medtech innovator. He recounts how he started Masimo to create a more reliable pulse oximeter after encountering disinterest from his previous employer. Kiani also touches on Masimo's legal battles with Apple, its acquisition of Sound United, and his ongoing advocacy for reducing preventable hospital deaths and addressing homelessness.

title: CEO Tim Cook says layoffs are a 'last resort' and not something Apple is considering right now
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook stated that large-scale layoffs are a "last resort" and not currently being considered by the company, contrasting with many other Big Tech firms that have cut thousands of jobs. While not ruling out future job cuts, Apple is focusing on cost-cutting measures and a slower rate of hiring. The company remains highly profitable, reporting $24 billion in net income on $95 billion in revenue for its March quarter.

title: Amkor Technology seeks to open Vietnam factory in fourth quarter
summary: U.S.-based Amkor Technology Inc. expects its mega facility in Bac Ninh, Vietnam, to begin first-phase manufacturing in the fourth quarter of this year. This factory, covering 1.9 million square feet, will be among Amkor's largest globally and represents a $1.6 billion investment until 2035. The move highlights Vietnam's growing importance as a manufacturing and R&D hub for major foreign electronics manufacturers.

title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has been a fortress stock during recessions and bear markets. Here's how
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has proven to be a resilient investment, consistently outperforming the S&P 500 during recessions and bear markets due to its long-term focus and conservative investment strategies. The conglomerate's significant stake in Apple, coupled with its substantial cash reserves and insurance ventures, underscores its "fortress stock" reputation. This approach has led to impressive compounded annual gains for Berkshire shareholders over decades.

title: Apple stakes future growth on emerging markets, starting with India
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is betting on emerging markets, particularly India, for future growth after iPhone sales surprisingly rose despite a global smartphone slump. The company sees significant opportunity in these markets due to their youthful populations and low iPhone penetration, with India showing strong double-digit growth and becoming a major focus for Apple. The booming used iPhone market also contributes to Apple's market share gains in these regions.

title: Buffett says Apple is Berkshire portfolio's best business
summary: Warren Buffett stated that Apple Inc. is a better business than any other in Berkshire Hathaway Inc.'s portfolio, citing its dominant consumer product, the iPhone. Berkshire Hathaway's stake in Apple grew from $1 billion in 2016 to $151 billion by March 2023, accounting for 46% of its equity portfolio, and Buffett indicated the company could buy more shares. He views Apple primarily as a consumer products company rather than a technology company.

title: My Favorite BlackBerry of All Time Is One That Everyone Hated
summary: The author reminisces about the BlackBerry PlayBook, a 7-inch tablet launched in 2011, which he believes was ahead of its time despite being largely panned by critics. He highlights its innovative hardware design, multitasking-focused QNX software, and gestures that foreshadowed features in modern tablets. The article concludes that while the PlayBook was ultimately doomed by poor strategic decisions like the lack of core apps and third-party developer support, many of its ideas live on in today's successful tablets.

title: CEO pay continues to soar – new study
summary: A new study by Equilar reveals that median CEO pay at top US public companies increased by 7.7% to a record $22.3 million in 2022, despite negative S&P 500 returns. This rise was primarily driven by stock awards, leading to a median "pay ratio" of 288 times the median employee's compensation, significantly outpacing the 3.6% increase in average US private sector employee earnings. The study highlights a trend of incentivizing executives through substantial stock awards during uncertain economic times.

title: This Popular Brand Has Better Profit Margins Than Apple or Nike. Is the Stock a Buy?
summary: The article analyzes Yeti Holdings (NYSE: YETI), highlighting its impressive gross profit margins, which surpass those of Apple and Nike. The author examines Yeti's pricing power and quality products but expresses concerns about the unpredictable nature of future sales and potential growth limitations due to the durability of its products. Ultimately, the author concludes that Yeti's long-term outlook isn't inspiring enough to warrant an investment at this time.

title: IonQ Appoints Pat Tang as VP of Research & Development
summary: IonQ, a quantum computing leader, announced the appointment of Pat Tang as Vice President of Research and Development. Tang, previously an Engineering Vice President at Amazon Lab126 and a team lead at Apple, will lead the company's technical exploration with a focus on developing impactful technologies for IonQ's systems. His extensive experience in building and scaling innovative technologies is expected to be invaluable as IonQ continues to advance quantum computing.

title: YieldMax™ Launches NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF
summary: YieldMax™ has launched NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF, which seeks to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). This actively managed ETF由ZEGA Financial aims to provide substantial monthly income to investors, joining other YieldMax™ ETFs like TSLY, OARK, and APLY, and adheres to a gross expense ratio of 0.99%.

title: Best Buy Announces New Membership Programs to Save You Money
summary: Best Buy is introducing three new membership tiers, "My Best Buy," "My Best Buy Plus," and "My Best Buy Total," replacing its previous Totaltech program. These options cater to different customer needs, ranging from a free basic membership to a premium plan offering comprehensive tech support and extended protection. The changes aim to provide more flexible benefits to consumers, with current Totaltech members transitioning to the new tiers upon renewal.

title: Layoffs may cause organizational drag: Gartner
summary: According to Gartner research, layoffs can negatively impact shareholder returns within three years by leading to reduced employee morale, increased turnover, and loss of customers, despite initial cost savings. The report highlights that businesses often face immediate costs like severance payments and long-term issues such as higher contractor costs and demands for increased compensation from remaining staff. Gartner suggests alternative cost-cutting measures like voluntary reduced hours, executive pay cuts, hiring freezes, and internal redeployment to avoid "organizational drag."

title: Apple previews Live Speech, Personal Voice, and more new accessibility features
summary: Apple announced new accessibility features including Assistive Access for cognitive disabilities, Live Speech and Personal Voice for speech accessibility, and Point and Speak in Magnifier for users who are blind or have low vision. These features leverage advances in hardware and software, including on-device machine learning for privacy, and are part of Apple's ongoing commitment to making technology inclusive for everyone. The company also detailed various initiatives and events celebrating Global Accessibility Awareness Day, showcasing applications of these features and broader accessibility efforts across its products and services.

title: Apple investments with European suppliers surpass €20 billion annual spend
summary: Apple announced that its investments with European suppliers exceeded €20 billion in 2022, reaching a total of €85 billion over the last five years. These investments support over 4,000 European suppliers, fostering innovation in Apple products and advancing the green transition of European industry, while also supporting more than 2.6 million jobs across the region. The company is committed to achieving carbon neutrality across its supply chain by 2030, with many European partners joining its Supplier Clean Energy Program.

title: What you need to know about Temu, the online shopping app dominating download charts
summary: Online shopping app Temu, owned by Chinese company PDD Holdings Inc., has quickly risen to popularity in Canada with its aggressive marketing, low prices, and gamified shopping experience. While it offers bargain deals, experts caution consumers about product quality, potential environmental impact from shipping and returns, and data privacy concerns given its sister app's issues. The platform's "growth-at-all-costs" approach and referral schemes are disrupting the e-commerce market, leading to a "race to the bottom" that could hurt local businesses.

title: Steve Jobs: Apple Cofounder and Tech Innovator
summary: Steve Jobs, co-founder of Apple Inc., was an American inventor, designer, and entrepreneur responsible for pioneering revolutionary technologies like the iPhone and iPad. The article details his life from adoption and early education to co-founding Apple, his departure and subsequent ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, ultimately shaping modern technology. It also covers his personal life, battle with pancreatic cancer, and lasting legacy after his death in 2011.

title: Apple announces multibillion-dollar deal with Broadcom for components made in the USA
summary: Apple has announced a new multiyear, multibillion-dollar agreement with Broadcom to develop 5G radio frequency and wireless connectivity components. These components, including FBAR filters, will be designed and built in the USA, notably in Fort Collins, Colorado. This investment aligns with Apple's 2021 commitment to invest $430 billion in the U.S. economy, supporting American manufacturing and job growth while expanding 5G technology.

title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FMET)
summary: This article provides an overview of the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), including its price, holdings, and recent news. FMET tracks a market cap-weighted index of equities related to the metaverse, launched on April 19, 2022, and is managed by Fidelity. The ETF has seen a significant increase in share price since the beginning of 2025 and lists its top institutional shareholders and key holdings.

title: Examining the Importance of Dividend Growth
summary: This article contrasts dividend growth investing with high-yield strategies, highlighting its benefits like reduced volatility and steadily rising payouts. It uses Warren Buffett's investments in companies like Coca-Cola and American Express to demonstrate the long-term efficacy of reinvesting growing dividends. The piece concludes by suggesting dividend growth ETFs, such as the T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth ETF (TDVG), as an accessible option for retail investors.

title: Apple inks multi-billion-dollar deal with Broadcom for U.S.-made chips
summary: Apple has announced a multi-billion-dollar deal with Broadcom to source U.S.-made chips. Under the multi-year agreement, Broadcom will develop 5G radio frequency components, including FBAR chips, at facilities in the U.S., including Fort Collins, Colorado. This move signifies Apple's continued efforts to diversify its supply chains and invest in domestic manufacturing.

title: Burney U.S. Factor Rotation ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:BRNY) $BRNY
summary: This article provides details on the Burney U.S. Factor Rotation ETF (BRNY), including its current price, key statistics, holdings, and recent news. As of March 13, 2026, BRNY is trading at $48.24, with assets under management of $476.48 million and a net expense ratio of 0.79%. The ETF is an actively managed fund that dynamically shifts US equity exposure based on market phases and includes major holdings like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:HLAL) $HLAL
summary: The Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF (HLAL) tracks the FTSE USA Shariah index, focusing on Sharia-compliant US equities. The ETF, launched in 2019 and managed by Wahed, had a market capitalization of $736.85 million and assets under management of $758.04 million at the time of the article. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet, and it recently declared quarterly distributions.

title: New HP and Sonos Devices Accentuate the Ultimate Home Office Workstation
summary: This article reviews the HP E45c G5 DQHD Curved Monitor and Sonos Era 100 Speakers, highlighting how they enhance productivity in a home office setup. The author praises the monitor's ultrawide display and Device Bridge feature for seamless multi-system operation, and the Sonos speakers for their superior sound quality and easy integration. Both devices are presented as valuable investments for improving the efficiency and enjoyment of hybrid work environments.

title: Why Is Wearable Devices (WLDS) Stock Up 250%?
summary: Shares of Wearable Devices (WLDS) surged by 250% after the company announced preorders for its Mudra Band, a neural input interface that allows users to control digital devices via subtle finger movements. This technology has potential applications beyond the Apple Watch, including smartphones and AR/VR devices, addressing a key concern among investors regarding its market viability. Additionally, the company received a 180-day extension from Nasdaq to regain compliance with its minimum bid price rule.

title: Cadence Stock vs Synopsys Stock: The Winner Is?
summary: The article compares Cadence (CDNS) and Synopsys (SNPS), two leading electronic design automation (EDA) software providers, to determine which stock is a better investment. It analyzes their market share, financial performance, valuation, and their use of AI in a mature, well-penetrated market. The authors conclude that Synopsys has a slight edge due to its higher EDA market share, strong position in intellectual property, and marginally better valuation, despite both being considered overvalued.

title: Everything you need to know about Salesforce’s Q1 2024 financial results
summary: Salesforce, Inc. reported increased first-quarter adjusted earnings in Q1 2024 ($1.69 per share) and revenue growth of 11% year-over-year to $8.25 billion. All key operating segments showed growth, driven by its generative AI ecosystem including Einstein GPT, Slack GPT, and Tableau GPT. CEO Marc Benioff highlighted the role of the Salesforce GPT Trust Layer in securing customer data and enhancing enterprise automation.

title: American Express Is Approaching Generative AI Technology with Caution
summary: American Express is cautiously approaching generative AI technology, aiming to integrate it for enhanced personalization, proactivity, and predictive capabilities through partnerships rather than developing its own large language models. This cautious stance aligns with broader industry concerns, highlighted by an open letter from tech leaders urging a pause in "giant AI experiments" to establish safety protocols. Amex Digital Labs, established in 2017, serves as the company's innovation hub for product prototypes and predictive analytics.

title: Apple Dominates Berkshire’s Portfolio. The Tech Stock Is Near a Record.
summary: Apple stock is approaching a record high and has become the dominant holding in Berkshire Hathaway's investment portfolio. This article details the significance of Apple's performance for Warren Buffett's company.

title: Garmin debuts Pro versions of Fenix 7 and Epix 7 adventure smartwatches
summary: Garmin has launched Pro versions of its Fenix 7 and Epix 2 smartwatches, introducing new sizes and features. Both Pro models include a redesigned, more accurate heart-rate sensor, a built-in LED flashlight with variable intensities, and enhanced mapping features. The Fenix 7 Pro adds endurance and hill scores, while the Epix 2 Pro, which features an AMOLED display, gains additional activity options and a Red Shift mode.

title: Apple Bets On The Emerging Markets Growth Story
summary: Apple is increasingly focusing on emerging markets, particularly India, to drive growth amidst a challenging global smartphone market. The company achieved marginal growth in iPhone sales despite overall industry decline, with India seeing significant retail and manufacturing expansion plans. While services and financial technology offer long-term opportunities, the emerging markets strategy is a key component of Apple's immediate growth narrative, alongside ongoing share repurchases and dividend increases.

title: Apple, Microsoft Boost the S&P 500 Amid Tech Comeback
summary: Apple and Microsoft have significantly contributed to the S&P 500's gains in 2023, accounting for nearly half of the index's rise and reaching an all-time high combined weight of 14%. This tech rally, driven by robust earnings, has left many mutual funds underweight in major tech stocks. The article suggests leveraged ETFs like AAPU, MSFU, and TECL for traders looking to capitalize on the continued bullish trend in the tech sector.

title: High Beta ETF 'SPHB' Lifted by Heavy Tech Sector Tilt
summary: The Invesco S&P 500 High Beta ETF (SPHB) performed strongly in May, driven by its significant allocation to the information technology sector. While the broader S&P 500 saw modest gains, the S&P 500 High Beta index climbed 2.4% due to the tech sector's 9.5% rally. SPHB has also demonstrated strong long-term performance, outpacing the S&P 500 over a three-year period.

title: PayPal and Venmo users shouldn't store money on the apps long term
summary: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) has warned users against storing money long-term on payment apps like PayPal, Venmo, and CashApp, citing potential risks during a financial crisis. Unlike traditional bank accounts, funds held in these apps are generally not covered by FDIC insurance, leaving them vulnerable if the platform experiences financial distress. While some specific features like PayPal Savings or Apple Cash with identity verification may offer insurance, the default accounts typically do not.

title: Vimeo is tuning out its streaming TV apps
summary: Vimeo is discontinuing its dedicated streaming TV apps for Apple TV, Android TV, Amazon Fire TV, and Roku starting June 27th. Users can still watch Vimeo content on their TVs by casting from the Vimeo mobile app via Apple AirPlay 2 or Google Chromecast. This move aligns with Vimeo's strategic shift in recent years from a consumer-facing streaming destination to focusing on video-streaming services for businesses.

title: In challenge to Meta, Apple expected to unveil mixed-reality headset
summary: Apple is expected to announce a new mixed-reality headset at its annual software developer conference, positioning itself against Meta Platforms in the emerging market for virtual and augmented reality. While Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg has championed the "metaverse" and has products out, Apple has been more private about its augmented-reality efforts, which so far have been limited to existing devices. Analysts suggest Apple's device, rumored to cost around $3,000, is an interim step toward transparent augmented-reality glasses and aims to encourage software developers to create compelling "XR" applications where no "killer app" currently exists.

title: Introducing Apple Vision Pro: Apple’s first spatial computer
summary: Apple has unveiled Apple Vision Pro, its first spatial computer, which blends digital content with the physical world using a three-dimensional user interface controlled by eyes, hands, and voice. Running on the new visionOS, Vision Pro features an ultra-high-resolution display system, custom Apple silicon, and offers new ways to interact with apps, enjoy entertainment, relive memories, and connect via FaceTime. The device is designed with advanced materials, focuses on privacy, and will be available early next year starting at $3,499 (U.S.).

title: Is Temu Shopping App a Communist China-Based Scam That Spies on Users?
summary: This article investigates claims that the Temu shopping app, owned by Chinese company PDD Holdings, is a scam that spies on users. It critically examines assertions made in a widely shared USA Today column, confirming that while concerns about product quality, data practices, and the company's Chinese ties are valid, some claims about the app's ability to access phone data without consent are imprecise or relate to its sister app, Pinduoduo, which was found to contain malware. The article concludes that Temu offers legitimate cheap products but its parent company's business ethics and data privacy record raise significant concerns.

title: Cisco Shows Breakthrough Innovation Towards AI-First Security Cloud
summary: Cisco is advancing its AI-driven Security Cloud with new innovations designed to simplify cybersecurity operations and enhance security efficacy. Key announcements include the launch of Cisco Secure Access, a new Security Service Edge (SSE) solution for hybrid work, and a preview of generative AI capabilities for threat response and policy management. Additionally, Cisco is bolstering network security with the new Secure Firewall 4200 Series and Multicloud Defense, alongside new cloud-native application security features in Panoptica.

title: Big Tech Is Outpacing Small-Caps Amid Market Rally
summary: Big Tech is significantly outperforming small-cap stocks in the current market rally, with the Direxion Daily Technology Bull 3X ETF (TECL) up nearly 120% compared to the Direxion Daily Small Cap Bull 3X Shares (TNA) 3.7% gain year-to-date. This outperformance, particularly by companies like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, is despite lingering recession concerns. The article questions the sustainability of Big Tech's rally while suggesting that small-cap funds like TNA could offer future upside.

title: Apple, Amazon must face consumer lawsuit over iPhone, iPad prices, US judge rules
summary: A federal judge in Seattle has ruled that Apple and Amazon must face a consumer antitrust lawsuit accusing them of conspiring to artificially inflate the price of iPhones and iPads sold on Amazon's platform. The lawsuit alleges that a 2019 agreement between the two companies restricted the number of competitive resellers, a claim that will now proceed to evidence-gathering. Apple contends the agreement was to minimize counterfeit goods and is procompetitive.

title: Bank of America execs blew $93.6 billion. Here’s how they did it.
summary: Bank of America spent $93.6 billion on stock buybacks over the past five years, but its stock price declined slightly during that period, leading analyst Dick Bove to critique the strategy. The article suggests that while buybacks can benefit shareholders of consistently profitable and expanding companies like Apple, they were a wasteful expense for Bank of America. The author argues that corporations and analysts should stop referring to stock buybacks as a "return of capital" to shareholders.

title: Apple Vision Pro’s External Displays from LG Display; Internal Displays from Sony
summary: Apple's new Vision Pro extended reality device will feature internal micro-OLED displays supplied by Sony and external regular OLED displays supplied by LG Display. While Sony secured the more lucrative internal display contract, South Korean companies like Samsung Display and LG Display are actively developing their own micro-OLED technologies to compete in the growing XR market. Displays are the most expensive component of the Vision Pro, accounting for 48.1% of its retail price.

title: The 3 Best ETFs to Buy to Balance Risk and Reward
summary: This article recommends three ETFs for a balanced portfolio, particularly for those interested in a 60/40 equity-to-bond split. The suggested ETFs are the Vanguard Total World Stock Fund ETF (VT) for global equity diversification, the VanEck Morningstar SMID Moat ETF (SMOT) for small and mid-cap stocks with competitive advantages, and the Fidelity Total Bond ETF (FBND) for fixed-income exposure. The author references market analysis suggesting a potential rebound for the 60/40 portfolio and provides detailed insights into each ETF's holdings, performance, and expense ratios.

title: Labor Ruling Offers Unionization Path to Uber, Lyft Drivers
summary: A recent U.S. labor board ruling has made it easier for workers to be classified as employees rather than independent contractors, potentially opening the door for unionization efforts among gig workers at companies like Uber and Lyft. While gig companies argue this could impact their business model, the decision emboldens organizing campaigns, despite the inherent difficulties in unionizing a dispersed workforce. This ruling could lead to significant challenges for gig companies, potentially forcing them to negotiate with unions, though hurdles remain for successful organizing drives.

title: Sonos Era 300 Review: Spatial Audio Is a Game Changer
summary: The Sonos Era 300 is the company's first speaker designed specifically for spatial audio, aiming to revolutionize how we experience music beyond traditional stereo. While it boasts a unique design and offers good stereo performance with Wi-Fi 6 and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity, its key selling point is the immersive spatial audio experience, particularly with services like Amazon Music Unlimited and Apple Music. Although pricey at $449 and limited in spatial audio service support at launch, the Era 300 delivers impressive sound quality that justifies the investment, especially when considering its potential as rear surround speakers for a home theater setup.

title: "나스닥 떨어질 것"…서학개미 인버스 뭉칫돈
summary: Despite the NASDAQ reaching a 13-month high driven by companies like Nvidia and Apple, South Korean individual investors (Seohak Gaemi) are increasing their bets on a market downturn. They are heavily investing in ultra-high-risk inverse ETFs, such as ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ (SQQQ) and Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bear 3X (SOXS), which aim to triple the inverse daily performance of their respective indices. However, these investors have already incurred significant losses as the market continues to rise, prompting experts to warn against the high risks associated with such leveraged inverse products for long-term investment.

title: T. Rowe Price's New ETF, TCAF, Is Worthy of Appreciation
summary: T. Rowe Price launched a new actively managed ETF, TCAF, run by experienced portfolio manager David Giroux. TCAF builds on the success of Giroux's $50 billion T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation PRWCX mutual fund, which has consistently outperformed its peers but is closed to new investors and has incurred capital gains. TCAF differentiates itself by focusing solely on equities, holding approximately 100 stocks, and offering a more tax-efficient structure compared to mutual funds, making it an attractive option for advisors seeking active management.

title: Democratic Large Cap Core ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DEMZ)
summary: The Democratic Large Cap Core ETF (DEMZ) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with a price target of $41.72, suggesting a 0.31% potential upside. This rating is based on 651 analyst ratings covering 25 of the ETF's constituent companies. Holdings with strong ratings include NVIDIA and Microsoft, while Loews has a lower rating score.

title: Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (METV)
summary: This article provides an aggregate rating and price target for the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) based on Wall Street analyst ratings of its holdings. METV has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and a price target of $16.79, derived from 595 analyst ratings covering 18 companies within its portfolio. The top holdings include tech giants like Apple, Roblox, NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms, along with their individual analyst ratings and potential upsides.

title: FlexShares Quality Dividend Index Fund Review and Aggregate Rating (QDF) $QDF
summary: This article provides an aggregate rating and price target for the FlexShares Quality Dividend Index Fund (QDF), evaluating its holdings based on Wall Street analyst ratings from the past year. QDF received a "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating and an aggregate price target reflecting the average potential upside of its constituent stocks. The report also details the top 25 companies held by QDF, including their individual ratings and price targets.

title: Schwab US Large-Cap ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (SCHX)
summary: MarketBeat reports an aggregate "Moderate Buy" rating for the Schwab US Large-Cap ETF (SCHX), with an aggregate price target of $26.09. This rating is based on 849 analyst ratings covering 25 companies, representing 46.8% of the ETF's portfolio. The analysis highlights that the majority of underlying stock holdings are rated as "Buy" or "Moderate Buy."

title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (ACWI)
summary: MarketBeat provides an aggregate rating and price target for the iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) based on Wall Street analyst ratings of its holdings. As of March 3, 2026, ACWI holds a "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating with an average price target of $143.68. The report details the consensus ratings and individual analyst price targets for the top holdings within the ETF, including major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.

title: JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (JUSA)
summary: This article provides a detailed review and aggregate rating for the JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA). MarketBeat calculates the ETF's "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating and a price target of $61.93 by analyzing the weight and individual analyst ratings of its underlying stock holdings. The review also lists the top 25 holdings with their respective analyst ratings, price targets, and potential upside.

title: Analysts highlight Masimo’s advantages over Apple, Fitbit ahead of proxy vote
summary: Ahead of a crucial shareholder vote, analysts at Stifel have bolstered Masimo's position by highlighting the potential of its W1 watch to improve patient outcomes and significantly reduce costs. Despite Masimo ceding early ground in wearables to Apple and Fitbit, Stifel's conversations with clinicians suggest that W1's clinically validated pulse oximetry technology offers superior accuracy for patient monitoring, positioning it as a durable solution for hospital-to-home care. While the device's battery life and connectivity need improvement, its discreet design and Masimo's reputation may enable it to become a major player in the wearables market.

title: Sonos Five review: is Sonos' biggest, priciest wireless speaker its best?
summary: The Sonos Five is reviewed as a solid wireless speaker, praised for its bold, expansive sound and multi-room potential within the Sonos ecosystem. While it delivers room-filling audio, the review notes it can be overly bassy and lacks Bluetooth, suggesting that the newer Era 300 offers a more refined and expressive sound despite the Five's louder output.

title: Spotify has higher-priced subscription plan in the pipeline: report
summary: Spotify is reportedly planning to introduce a higher-priced "Supremium" subscription tier that will offer high-fidelity audio and additional audiobooks. This new plan, which was previously delayed, is expected to roll out to non-U.S. users in 2023, with American users gaining access later. The company recently increased its monthly active users to 515 million and premium subscribers to 210 million in the first quarter, and also announced an AI DJ feature.

title: MUSQ Global Music Industry Index ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (MUSQ)
summary: The MUSQ Global Music Industry Index ETF (MUSQ) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on analyst ratings for its holdings. The ETF's aggregate price target is $27.39, with its portfolio composed of companies like Live Nation Entertainment, Spotify Technology, and Tencent Music Entertainment Group. This review details the methodology for calculating ETF ratings and provides a breakdown of ratings for MUSQ's top holdings.

title: Top 10 LGBT manufacturing leaders
summary: This article highlights the top 10 LGBTQ+ leaders in the manufacturing sector, emphasizing the positive impact of diversity and inclusion on business performance. It showcases leaders from companies like Apple, Dow, and Land O’Lakes who are advocating for an inclusive workplace culture. The piece underscores how LGBTQ-inclusive policies lead to increased employee commitment, job satisfaction, and improved company reputation.

title: Road to UEFA Euro 2024™ Ramps up With European Qualifiers on FOX Sports and Fubo
summary: FOX Sports and Fubo have announced their comprehensive coverage plans for the third and fourth rounds of the UEFA European Qualifiers, critical for the UEFA Euro 2024™ tournament. From June 12-20, FOX Sports will broadcast 15 qualifying matches and three friendly matches across its various channels, while Fubo will exclusively stream 31 qualifying matches and two friendly matches. The coverage includes major national teams like England, France, Portugal, and Belgium, with FOX Sports employing a seasoned team of commentators and Fubo offering interactive features like FanView and Multiview for an enhanced viewing experience.

title: Lenovo ‘overall winner’ of UK FRAND trial as InterDigital confirms appeal
summary: The UK High Court has declared Lenovo the 'overall winner' in its FRAND dispute with InterDigital, determining a FRAND rate of $138.7 million plus $46.2 million in interest, making the total $184.9 million. The court found Lenovo's proposed per-unit rate much closer to the final decision than InterDigital's, and the payable lump sum 'substantially lower' than InterDigital's offer. Both parties have been granted permission to appeal this decision, indicating the case is not yet concluded.

title: Apple’s market value ends above $3 trillion for the first time
summary: Apple Inc.'s market value surpassed $3 trillion for the first time at the close of a trading session, driven by positive inflation data and confidence in its market expansion strategies. This milestone reflects a 49 percent gain in Apple shares for 2023, amid a tech stock rebound fueled by expectations of slower interest rate hikes from the US Federal Reserve and buzz around artificial intelligence. The company's financial resilience, strong balance sheet, and a recent augmented-reality headset launch contribute to its success, making it one of only five US companies with a valuation exceeding $1 trillion.

title: Apple, world's most valuable public company, is first to hit $3 trillion in market value
summary: Apple has become the first publicly-traded company to achieve a market value of $3 trillion or more at the close of Wall Street's trading day, with its stock price rising 2.21% to $193.78 on Friday. This historic milestone was attributed to its popular products and strong profits, with an analyst from Wedbush Securities suggesting its fair valuation could reach $3.5 trillion, potentially even $4 trillion by FY25, especially with upcoming products like the Vision Pro VR headset. The article also lists the world's ten largest companies by market cap, with Apple leading the pack.

title: How Apple Became a $3 Trillion Company
summary: This article chronicles Apple's journey from its founding in 1976 to becoming a $3 trillion company. It highlights key product launches like the Apple I, Macintosh, iPod, and iPhone, emphasizing the iPhone's central role in its financial success. The article also touches on leadership changes, challenges like supply-chain disruptions, and future innovations such as the Apple Vision Pro.

title: Proposal for new Costco store in region takes a step forward
summary: A proposal to bring a new Costco Wholesale Corp. store to Indian Land, S.C., has moved closer to reality after a second reading of a rezoning request was approved. The Lancaster County Council approved the request for a 28-acre site adjacent to The Exchange at Indian Land, a mixed-use development by Crosland Southeast. Three readings are required for final approval of the rezoning request.

title: Oakmark Equity and Income Fund: Second Calendar Quarter 2023
summary: The Oakmark Equity and Income Fund gained 5.3% in Q2 2023, outperforming the Lipper Balanced Fund Index, despite market concentration in large-cap tech stocks. The fund adjusted its portfolio by reducing technology exposure and initiating new positions, primarily in the healthcare sector, based on a value-driven investment philosophy. The article also reflects on the foundational principles of diversification in portfolio construction, influenced by economist Harry Markowitz, and the fund's approach to asset allocation.
authors: Colin Hudson|CFA; Adam D. Abbas; Michael A. Nicolas|CFA; Alex Fitch|CFA; Clyde S. McGregor|CFA

title: Apple Is the World’s First $3 Trillion Company
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first company to close with a market value above $3 trillion, demonstrating the enduring strength and resilience of the iPhone. This achievement highlights Apple’s significant growth from its origins, now valued at double Google and seven times Exxon Mobil, despite various global challenges.

title: Apple makes history as first $3 trillion company amid tech stock surge
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first company to reach a $3 trillion market value, driven by investor optimism about its expansion into new markets and a projected easing of interest rate hikes. This accomplishment is set against a backdrop of a broader tech stock surge, with other companies like Tesla and Nvidia also seeing significant gains due to advancements in AI and strategic partnerships. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance and its reputation as a safe investment, alongside the success of other tech giants.

title: Settlement checks in the mail from Roundup, EpiPen, Zoom, others
summary: Readers of Top Class Actions are reporting the receipt of settlement checks from seven different class action lawsuits. These settlements address various claims including false advertising for Roundup, spam calls from Bath Saver, privacy issues related to Yahoo data breaches, antitrust violations by EpiPen manufacturers, price-fixing for lithium-ion batteries, Apple iCloud storing data on third-party servers, and security failures by Zoom that led to "Zoom bombing." Claimants are receiving payments ranging from small amounts to several thousands of dollars depending on the settlement terms and individual claims.

title: Apple loses London appeal in 4G patent dispute with Optis
summary: London's Court of Appeal dismissed Apple's appeal in a patent dispute with Optis Cellular Technology LLC, ruling that Apple infringed two 4G telecommunications patents. Texas-based Optis sued Apple in 2019, alleging that the patents are essential to certain technological standards used in devices like iPhones and iPads. This ruling is the latest decision in a legal battle that has involved multiple trials and appeals in Britain.

title: Kopin Corp is the Technology Seen Behind Smart AR Glasses
summary: Kopin Corp (NASDAQ: KOPN), a high-resolution microdisplay maker, is highlighted as a key technology provider for smart AR glasses, including supplying displays for Vuzix and Alphabet. Despite an 80% drop from 2021 highs, the company has made significant advancements, such as achieving 3 million nits brightness for its 2K x 2K MicroLED displays, essential for AR in bright conditions. Kopin's Q1 2023 results showed increased product revenues, especially in defense, and the new CEO emphasized renewed focus on quality and efficiency to improve margins and achieve cash flow breakdown by year-end.

title: Apple’s Vision Pro Will Take Far Longer Than iPad, Watch to Spur Big Revenue
summary: Apple's Vision Pro headset is expected to take significantly longer than products like the iPod, iPhone, and iPad to become a major revenue driver for the company. The rollout plan for the device is anticipated to be slow, involving appointments and head scans for customers. This indicates a cautious approach from Apple regarding the market penetration and financial impact of the Vision Pro.

title: If You Like JEPI, You’ll Love This ETF With an 11.9% Dividend Yield
summary: This article introduces the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) as an alternative to the JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI), highlighting its 11.9% dividend yield. JEPQ invests in Nasdaq 100 stocks, focusing on tech giants, and uses a covered-call strategy similar to JEPI to generate income. While offering a high yield and potential downside protection, JEPQ has lagged behind a comparable Nasdaq ETF (QQQ) in total return during a bull market.

title: Apple executives fight subpoenas in shareholders' lawsuit against Qualcomm
summary: Apple Inc.'s chief operating officer and a top corporate lawyer are attempting to quash subpoenas in a private securities class action lawsuit against chipmaker Qualcomm. They argue that their previous testimony and documents provided in an FTC antitrust case against Qualcomm make further questioning irrelevant to the current shareholder litigation. Apple contends that Qualcomm is using the subpoenas to harass the company.

title: Apple opens store on China's WeChat platform
summary: Apple has opened an official store on Tencent's WeChat platform in China, expanding its retail presence in the country's second-largest economy. This move allows WeChat users to purchase Apple products directly through the dominant messaging app, following a trend of Chinese consumers increasingly using social media for shopping. The expansion comes as Apple recorded a significant market share in China's smartphone market despite an overall decline in sales.

title: SIT Investment Associates Inc Acquires Stake in Neuberger Berman High Yield Strategies Fund
summary: SIT Investment Associates Inc acquired 2,043,847 shares of Neuberger Berman High Yield Strategies Fund on July 12, 2023. The transaction, valued at $7.59 per share, had a 0.42% impact on SIT's portfolio. Despite the target company's stock being deemed significantly overvalued with poor financial health indicators by GuruFocus, SIT's investment may represent a long-term strategic move.

title: First-of-its-Kind App, TheStreet Powered by Tornado, Launches One-Stop Investing Experience
summary: TheStreet and Tornado have launched a new app specifically designed to provide a comprehensive investing experience for all levels of investors. The app integrates TheStreet's news and investing ideas with Tornado's technology and community, offering tools like expert Q&A, tailored notifications, S&P Capital IQ data, and portfolio optimization. This collaboration aims to empower investors with knowledge and tools, expanding reach to a new audience.

title: RSPT: Right Idea for More Balanced Approach to Tech
summary: The article discusses the growing concentration risk in the technology sector, highlighted by the outperformance of a few mega-cap stocks in the cap-weighted S&P 500 Information Technology Index. It presents the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF (RSPT) as a solution for investors seeking a more diversified approach, noting its strong year-to-date performance despite its equal-weighting strategy. The article suggests that historical trends of tech concentration reversing could favor equal-weighted strategies like RSPT.

title: For Long-Term EM Growth, India Answers the Bell
summary: India is projected to become the world's second-largest economy by 2075 and is already a leader in emerging markets. Investors can access this growth through ETFs like the VanEck India Growth Leaders ETF (GLIN), which focuses on companies with strong growth prospects and adheres to a growth-at-a-reasonable-price (GARP) methodology. India's economic growth, large democracy, and increasing role as a manufacturing hub—potentially surpassing China—further enhance its long-term appeal for investors.

title: For Long-Term EM Growth, India Answers the Bell
summary: India is positioned to become the world's second-largest economy by 2075 and is already among the top-performing emerging markets. The VanEck India Growth Leaders ETF (GLIN) offers investors exposure to Indian equities, utilizing a growth at a reasonable price (GARP) methodology. India's economic growth, a large and growing population, and government efforts to attract manufacturing contribute to its long-term investment appeal.

title: Unlocking Business Potential with Apple’s Vision Pro
summary: This eBook explores the revolutionary potential of Apple's Vision Pro headset for businesses, akin to the impact of the original iPhone. It delves into how the device can create enhanced human-centric digital experiences, improve user engagement, and foster new business models through spatial computing. The authors provide insights into navigating Apple's software and hardware integration to develop seamless, intuitive interactions for this new technology.

title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock saw a modest gain of 0.08% on Friday, closing at $190.69, marking its third consecutive day of increases. Despite this, the stock underperformed compared to its competitors, even as the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the NASDAQ Composite Index experienced a slight decline. The trading session was mixed overall for the stock market.

title: A breakdown of the changes to the Nasdaq-100 and what it means for mega-cap tech stocks
summary: Nasdaq announced a special rebalance of the Nasdaq-100 Index to dilute the weightings of mega-cap tech stocks that have outperformed in 2023. The seven largest stocks in the Nasdaq-100 will see their combined weighting reduced from 56% to 44%, with Nvidia and Microsoft being most affected. Historically, similar rebalances have not had a significant impact on stock prices.

title: Preparing for Upcoming QQQ Changes
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a special rebalance of its Nasdaq-100 Index (NDX), impacting ETFs like Invesco QQQ (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). This rebalance, effective July 24, aims to address concentration risk due to the large percentage holdings of tech giants like Microsoft and Apple. Investors can expect lower allocations to these mega-cap growth names, though the overall changes are not anticipated to be monumental, and the ETF structure should help avoid capital gains distributions for investors.

title: Apple maintains its spot as the world’s most valuable brand, per Kantar BrandZ
summary: Apple has retained its position as the world's most valuable brand according to Kantar BrandZ, valued at $880,455M, despite a 7% decline from 2022. The company's resilience is attributed to its strong brand equity, diversification into services, and ability to justify premium pricing. Apple's differentiation, purpose-driven initiatives, distinctive products, and iconic advertising are highlighted as key factors in its sustained success amidst challenging market conditions.

title: Report: Apple built ‘Apple GPT’ chatbot using internal AI development framework
summary: Apple Inc. has reportedly developed an internal AI framework called Ajax for building large language models and a ChatGPT-like chatbot internally known as "Apple GPT." Ajax, based on Google's open-source Jax technology and running on Google Cloud, has already been used to enhance features in Apple Maps, Siri, and search tools. Although employees are prohibited from using Apple GPT for customer-facing features, it's used for product prototyping, and the company plans a significant AI announcement next year.

title: Netflix Strategic Changes Led to User Base Expansion & Record Revenues in Q2 2023
summary: Netflix's strategic changes, including a crackdown on password sharing and the introduction of an ad-supported tier, significantly boosted its user base and revenue in Q2 2023. The company added 5.89 million subscribers and achieved record revenues of $8.187 billion, demonstrating the positive impact of these initiatives despite increasing competition in the direct-to-consumer market. Even with these successes, challenges remain, such as intense competition and the absence of live sports content, which are critical for long-term growth and investor satisfaction.

title: Nasdaq Is Reducing the Influence of Giant Tech Firms in a Popular Stock Market Index
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a "special rebalance" of its Nasdaq 100 index to reduce the dominance of giant tech firms like Apple and Microsoft. This move addresses the historic rally of a few mega-cap tech stocks, which have caused overconcentration in the index and potentially increased diversification risk for investors. The rebalance, effective July 24, will prompt funds tracking the index to adjust their portfolios, potentially leading to trading costs and minor market volatility.

title: How will the metaverse change our lives?
summary: This article explores the concept of the metaverse, its origins, and its potential impact on our lives, particularly within financial services. It delves into how the metaverse, driven by emerging technologies like Augmented Reality and Blockchain, could revolutionize banking with new e-commerce experiences and personalized financial assistance. The author also discusses the challenges and considerations, such as identity and fraud prevention, that need to be addressed as the metaverse develops.

title: Apple faces $1 billion UK lawsuit by apps developers over app store fees
summary: Apple is facing a 785-million-pound ($1 billion) class action lawsuit in the UK brought by over 1,500 app developers. The lawsuit alleges that Apple's App Store fees, ranging from 15% to 30% for in-app purchases, are excessive and abusive due to its monopoly on app distribution. Sean Ennis, a former OECD economist, is leading the legal action, claiming the charges harm both developers and app buyers.

title: Introducing Norton Genie - Real-Time AI-powered Scam Detection at Your Fingertips
summary: Norton has launched Norton Genie, a real-time AI-powered scam detection tool designed to help users identify and avoid increasingly sophisticated scams in texts, emails, websites, and social media posts. Available now in early access in the US, Genie combines Norton's cybersecurity expertise with advanced AI trained on millions of scam messages. The tool offers fast, free, and easy scam analysis, evolving protection, and trusted advice on how to respond to potential threats.

title: Why is Netflix lagging FAANG stocks?
summary: While other FAANG stocks have seen significant rallies in 2023, Netflix has lagged, raising questions about its inclusion in the group. Despite a subscriber boost from a password crackdown, the company faces challenges from ongoing strikes and has not highlighted AI in its earnings, unlike its tech counterparts. The article discusses investment approaches for FAANG stocks through ETFs.

title: Apple, Meta, Google argue they're not 'bookies' in casino app appeal
summary: Apple, Meta, and Google are appealing a 2022 ruling that found them potentially liable for facilitating purchases of virtual chips in casino game apps, arguing that this interpretation of Section 230 could jeopardize the entire internet economy. They contend that holding platforms liable for payment processing related to third-party content would force extensive monitoring and is a misinterpretation of existing legal precedents. The companies emphasize they act as publishers offering payment services, not creators of the allegedly illegal apps or chips, and that their conduct in these cases is distinct from prior rulings.

title: Mutual Fund Top Trumps – TMT
summary: This article compares two TMT mutual funds, the BlackRock Next Generation Technology fund and the Janus Henderson Global Technology and Innovation fund, using the "Top Trumps" card game format. The comparison focuses on factors like 3-year performance, assets under management, alpha, volatility, ongoing charges, and information ratio. Ultimately, the Janus Henderson fund wins the comparison 4:2, and details on both funds' investment strategies, sector, and geographic breakdowns are provided.

title: Small Account Options Trading: How to Scale
summary: This article discusses strategies for scaling up trading capital in small accounts, focusing on options trading. It explores two primary approaches: increasing an individual position's risk/buying power requirement or increasing the number of contracts, while emphasizing the importance of understanding risk and probability of loss. The author advises widening the spread while keeping the number of contracts low to balance premium and risk, ensuring sustainable growth rather than just profitability.

title: Is QYLD ETF’s 11.3% Dividend a High-Yield Trap?
summary: This article examines the Global X NASDAQ 100 Covered Call ETF (QYLD) which offers an impressive 11.3% monthly dividend yield. While appealing for income investors, the article suggests that QYLD's covered call strategy, despite holding top tech stocks, leads to significantly lower long-term total returns compared to a standard Nasdaq 100 ETF like QQQ, primarily due to capping upside potential and higher fees. It concludes that while QYLD provides consistent income, QQQ is a superior long-term wealth-building investment.

title: Pixar, Apple and Nvidia are teaming up to promote a 3D file format that could help boost Apple's upcoming Vision Pro
summary: Pixar, Apple, Nvidia, Adobe, and Autodesk are collaborating to promote OpenUSD, a 3D file format. This initiative aims to streamline the creation of 3D content, potentially benefiting game development based on Pixar movies and boosting Apple's upcoming Vision Pro mixed-reality headset by making 3D environments more accessible.

title: Best Augmented Reality & Virtual Reality Stocks In 2023: December List
summary: This article identifies top Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) stocks for 2023, acknowledging the market's slow but potential growth, similar to the early days of smartphones. It emphasizes investing in stable, well-capitalized companies that are involved in AR/VR but do not solely depend on it for revenue, such as Alphabet, Apple, Autodesk, Meta, and Microsoft. The article also highlights the importance of risk management, portfolio diversification, and continuous monitoring of emerging trends in this evolving technological landscape.

title: Apple’s Goldman Sachs-Backed Savings Account Tops $10 Billion in Deposits
summary: Apple and Goldman Sachs announced that their high-yield savings account has accumulated over $10 billion in US deposits since its launch in April. This achievement highlights the product's success, with 97% of users opting to deposit their Apple Daily Cash rewards into the account, which offers a 4.15% interest rate. The growth comes amidst rumors of a potential split between the two companies.

title: Garmin Fenix 7X Pro Solar Review | A new pinnacle in ultra-premium sports smartwatches
summary: The Garmin Fenix 7X Pro Solar is reviewed as a robust and premium sports smartwatch, offering extensive features for outdoor activities and impressive battery life. Despite its high price, the watch stands out with its durable design, advanced tracking capabilities, and an integrated LED flashlight, making it a strong competitor in the luxury sports watch market. The review highlights its excellent battery longevity and enhanced accuracy for outdoor tracking as key selling points for sports enthusiasts.

title: S&P 500 analysis: US stocks drop after Fitch downgrade
summary: US stocks, particularly the S&P 500, dropped following Fitch's unexpected downgrade of the US credit rating from AAA to AA+. This marks the first such downgrade in 12 years and comes amidst fiscal deterioration and high government debt. The article highlights that while the market is in an uptrend, unlike the 2011 downgrade, the potential for a sharper sell-off exists, especially with upcoming economic data like the nonfarm payrolls report and earnings from tech giants set to influence market sentiment.

title: Haus raises $17M Series A led by Insight Partners to build the future of growth intelligence
summary: Haus, a growth command center utilizing causal inference, experimentation, and AI, has successfully raised $17 million in Series A funding. The round was led by Insight Partners, with participation from existing investors, to further expand its team and develop integrated causal analytical tools. This funding aims to help companies measure and drive profitable growth amid challenges like cookie depreciation and increasing consumer privacy regulations.

title: Apple sees sales slump continuing, shares drop 2% despite beating sales expectations
summary: Apple shares dropped 2% after the company forecast a continued sales slump for the current quarter, despite beating Wall Street's sales and profit targets in the fiscal third quarter. While services and China sales were strong, weaker-than-expected iPhone sales disappointed investors, raising concerns about future volume growth. Apple also revealed increased R&D spending, partly driven by generative AI development.

title: Apple Loses Historic $3 Trillion Crown as Sales Disappoint
summary: Apple Inc. saw its market capitalization fall decisively below $3 trillion after its fourth-quarter outlook raised concerns about weak demand for its products. Shares dropped 4.8% on Friday, wiping out over $160 billion in market value, marking its largest decline since September. This follows Apple becoming the first company to hit a $3 trillion valuation in June.

title: Apple Pay reportedly coming to Chile this month after long delay
summary: Apple Pay is reportedly launching in Chile in August 2023, after several previous delays and false starts since 2021. Local banks, including Santander and Banco de Chile, are expected to support the service, though some concerns remain about meeting Apple's operational and security standards. Chile would join several other Latin American countries where Apple Pay has already launched.

title: Exploring two covered call strategies by Global X ETFs
summary: This article explores two covered call ETF strategies offered by Global X ETFs, designed to provide alternative income solutions in a potentially rising-rate environment.
