title: Unity 6 debuts next year with AI-powered innovations and platform updates
summary: Unity announced its upcoming Unity 6 platform, set to launch in 2024, featuring significant AI innovations and platform updates. Key announcements from the Unite conference include the early access of Unity Muse for content creation, a preview of Unity 6, and the introduction of Unity Cloud. These updates aim to address developer feedback and enhance the game development process following recent controversies over pricing changes.

title: Warren Buffett Has Bought Shares of This Stock for 21 Consecutive Quarters -- and It's Not Apple
summary: Warren Buffett has consistently invested in Berkshire Hathaway's own stock for 21 consecutive quarters, authorizing over $72 billion in repurchases since mid-2018. This strategy, enabled by a change in buyback clauses, aims to reward long-term shareholders by increasing their ownership stakes and potentially boosting earnings per share. While Apple constitutes nearly half of Berkshire's invested assets, the frequent repurchase of Berkshire Hathaway shares itself highlights Buffett's conviction in his own company.

title: AT&T’s Black Friday Deals on the iPhone 15 Pro & More
summary: AT&T announced its Black Friday deals for the holiday season, offering significant savings on various Apple products including the iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone 14, iPhone 14 Pro, iPhone SE 3rd Gen, iPad, and Apple Watch. Deals include trade-in options, no trade-in required offers, and low monthly payments, catering to both new and existing customers. Additionally, AT&T highlighted benefits like flexible shopping, unlimited plans with 5G access, AT&T Fiber deals, and special discounts for military, veterans, teachers, nurses, and physicians.

title: Network of Lies review: Brian Stelter on Fox News, Trump and Dominion
summary: Brian Stelter's book "Network of Lies" examines Fox News's role in spreading election conspiracies following the 2020 election, leading to significant legal and financial repercussions, such as the Dominion settlement. The review highlights Rupert Murdoch's recent step down, the Murdochs' balancing act of profitability and audience satisfaction, and the ongoing influence of Donald Trump on the network and Republican politics. Despite the controversies, the article suggests little change is expected from Fox News as it navigates complex defamation lawsuits and competitive political landscapes.

title: Apple’s In-House Technologies Team Still Has Plenty of Work to Do
summary: This article discusses Apple's intensive efforts to design and manufacture its own core hardware components, such as cameras, screens, modems, and batteries, for its products. It also touches on Apple's adoption of the RCS texting standard, the revelation of its revenue share from the Google search deal, and a key health executive's move to Oura. The piece highlights that Apple's in-house technologies team has extensive work ahead in its strategic initiative to control major parts of its devices.

title: Venmo, Cash App users sue Apple over peer-to-peer payment fees
summary: Venmo and Cash App customers have filed a proposed class-action lawsuit against Apple Inc. alleging the company abused its market power in mobile peer-to-peer payments, leading to "rapidly inflating prices." The lawsuit claims Apple's agreements with PayPal's Venmo and Block's Cash App violate U.S. antitrust law by limiting "feature competition," including prohibiting decentralized cryptocurrency technology. This case adds to Apple's ongoing antitrust legal challenges, seeking an injunction that could force Apple to divest or segregate its Apple Cash business.

title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF With the Nasdaq At An All-Time High? History Offers a Clear Answer.
summary: This article discusses the Invesco QQQ ETF and whether it's a good investment despite the Nasdaq Composite being at an all-time high. It analyzes historical data of the Nasdaq's performance after reaching new highs, suggesting that further gains are common. The article concludes that dollar-cost averaging into QQQ is a sensible strategy regardless of market highs, as it tracks the Nasdaq 100 which tends to rise over time.

title: Gaming Industry Charts a Rebound in 2023
summary: The gaming industry is experiencing a significant rebound in 2023 driven by strong console sales, new game releases, and innovations in mobile and cloud gaming. This recovery, following a dip in 2022, is further supported by anticipation of increased M&A activity and strategic moves by major players like Microsoft, Apple, and Netflix, positioning the sector for continued growth into 2024. The article highlights strong U.S. sales, hardware as a primary catalyst, and key factors like M&A and technological advancements shaping the industry's future trajectory.

title: Sonos Readies $400-Plus Headphones to Rival Apple and Bose, TV Set-Top Box
summary: Sonos Inc. is planning a significant expansion into new product categories, including headphones priced over $400 and a TV set-top box. These new offerings are expected to compete with products from Apple, Bose, and Roku, marking a strategic move beyond its traditional smart speakers and sound bars. The headphones could launch as early as April, with the set-top box following by the end of 2024.

title: InterDigital’s Alain Mourad Elected Chair of New ETSI Industry Specification Group on Integrated Sensing and Communications
summary: Dr. Alain Mourad, Head of InterDigital’s Wireless Lab Europe, has been elected Chair of the newly formed ETSI Industry Specification Group (ISG) on Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC). This group aims to establish the technical foundation for ISAC technology and its standardization in 6G, defining use cases and developing evaluation roadmaps. The ISG will also study privacy, security, and sustainability aspects of ISAC within the 6G framework.

title: Department of Justice and Apple Reach $25 Million Landmark Agreement
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced a $25 million agreement with Apple Inc. to resolve allegations of discriminatory hiring practices related to the permanent labor certification (PERM) program. Apple's recruitment practices for PERM positions, which included not advertising on its public-facing career website and requiring mailed paper applications, deterred U.S. workers. The settlement includes $6.75 million in civil penalties and $18.25 million for a backpay fund for victims, and mandates Apple to align PERM recruitment with its standard practices.

title: OpenAI Says Sam Altman to Return as CEO in Dramatic Reversal
summary: Sam Altman is set to return as CEO of OpenAI after being ousted less than a week prior, following intense negotiations and pressure from investors like Microsoft and nearly all of the company's employees. The agreement includes a new initial board of directors led by Bret Taylor, with Larry Summers and Adam D’Angelo, and an internal investigation into the conduct that led to Altman's dismissal. This dramatic reversal comes after widespread calls for Altman's reinstatement and highlights the significant influence of investors and employees on the future of the AI startup.

title: Warren Buffett-Led Berkshire Hathaway Sells Its Entire Procter & Gamble Stake. Should You?
summary: Berkshire Hathaway fully exited its small position in Procter & Gamble, likely due to consolidating its portfolio and P&G's premium valuation compared to other opportunities like Apple. Despite Berkshire's sale, P&G is still considered a worthwhile investment for risk-averse individuals due to its strong brand development, pricing power, and commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The article suggests that while P&G's valuation is high, its other characteristics make it a solid choice for passive income and portfolio stability.

title: Sonos is reportedly working on a streaming video TV set top box, along with headphones
summary: Sonos is reportedly expanding its product line beyond speakers and soundbars. The company isSaid to be working on over-the-ear headphones for a March 2024 launch, possibly priced between $400 and $500, and high-end earbuds. Additionally, Sonos is reportedly developing an Android-based streaming video TV set-top box, codenamed "Pinewood," which could launch in late 2024 or early 2025.

title: Deere must face US farmers' 'right-to-repair' lawsuits, judge rules
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Deere & Co must face consolidated lawsuits from farmers accusing the agricultural machinery maker of unlawfully conspiring to restrict maintenance and repair services. The judge rejected Deere's arguments for dismissal, stating that the plaintiffs met the legal thresholds to pursue their antitrust claims. This case is part of a broader "right-to-repair" movement challenging restrictive repair practices across various industries.

title: Amkor to build $2 billion semiconductor testing plant in Peoria. 2,000 jobs promised
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to build a $2 billion advanced semiconductor packaging and testing facility in Peoria, Arizona, creating approximately 2,000 jobs. The plant will partner with TSMC, testing chips produced for Apple Inc., and is expected to begin production within two to three years. This investment is seen as a significant step in bolstering the U.S. semiconductor supply chain and is supported by the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Amkor Announces U.S. Advanced Packaging and Test Facility In Peoria
summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. announced a $2 billion investment to build an advanced packaging and test facility in Peoria, Arizona, creating 2,000 new jobs. This facility will be the largest outsourced advanced packaging facility in the US, bolstering America's national security and semiconductor supply chain. Apple will be the first and largest customer, and Amkor has applied for CHIPS funding to support the project.

title: AI ETFs see huge performance dispersion in 2023
summary: Artificial Intelligence ETFs have shown significant performance dispersion in 2023, with top strategies more than doubling the returns of the lowest performers. This disparity is largely attributed to the heavy weighting of certain ETFs towards US equities and the "magnificent seven" tech stocks. While these mega-cap companies have driven much of the AI theme's returns, there is still potential for pure-play AI companies to join the rally.

title: $2 billion semiconductor testing and packaging facility planned for Peoria
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to build a $2 billion semiconductor testing and advanced packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona, near the TSMC plant. This facility, expected to create 2,000 jobs, will test and package chips for Apple and aims to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain. The project is seen as a crucial step for Arizona's semiconductor ecosystem and national security.

title: Apple announces expanded partnership with Amkor for advanced silicon packaging in the U.S.
summary: Apple announced an expanded partnership with Amkor, designating Apple as the first and largest customer for Amkor's new manufacturing and packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This facility will package Apple silicon produced at the nearby TSMC fab, strengthening Apple's commitment to American manufacturing. Amkor plans to invest approximately $2 billion in the project, creating over 2,000 jobs, as part of Apple's broader commitment to invest $430 billion in the U.S. economy.

title: Who says corporate bonds are dull? Apple’s bonds have seen equity-like returns in the past month
summary: Apple Inc. bonds have generated equity-like returns for investors in the past month, with prices climbing as Treasury yields have tumbled. This rally, fueled by hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2024, has led to significant gains in the broader fixed-income market, with the Bloomberg US Aggregate Bond Index seeing its biggest monthly gain since May 1985.

title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock rose by 0.31% to $189.95 on Thursday, outperforming the mixed stock market session which saw the Dow Jones Industrial Average rise and the NASDAQ Composite Index fall. The company's shares closed $8.28 below their 52-week high.

title: Apple to become largest customer of new $2B chip packaging facility in Arizona
summary: Apple announced it will be the primary customer for Amkor Technology Inc.'s new $2 billion chip packaging facility in Arizona, which will package chips produced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) at a nearby complex. This facility will play a crucial role in assembling and protecting the next generation of processors for various Apple products. The investment underscores Apple's commitment to American manufacturing and will create over 2,000 jobs.

title: Cognizant Technology Solutions to lay off employees tied to Apple in Austin
summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions announced layoffs impacting employees linked to Apple in Austin. The contractors affected will receive pay while exploring other positions within Cognizant. This move adds to a series of technology sector layoffs in Austin, though Central Texas is noted to be somewhat sheltered from broader industry cuts.

title: One ETF that focuses on employee happiness is outperforming the market this year
summary: An ETF focused on companies with high employee happiness, the Harbor Human Capital Factor U.S. Large Cap ETF (HAPI), has outperformed the S&P 500 this year, climbing nearly 25% compared to the benchmark's less than 20% gain. The fund's strategy is based on Irrational Capital's extensive employee surveys and publicly available data, creating a "human capital score" that is considered a novel and uncorrelated investment factor. While the large-cap HAPI fund includes major tech stocks contributing to its performance, a similar small-cap ETF (HAPS) is also available, offering potential for future growth.

title: Walmart stops advertising on X, adding to growing list of companies
summary: Walmart has ceased advertising on X, joining several other major companies like Apple, Disney, and IBM, citing that it found other platforms to better reach its customers. This decision was part of a gradual reduction in advertising on X, not a sudden halt, according to a source familiar with the situation and an X executive. The social media platform has faced a significant exodus of advertisers due to concerns over antisemitic content, exacerbated by recent controversies involving Elon Musk's comments and his subsequent strong reaction to advertisers.

title: Amkor to invest US$2bn in US chip packaging
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to invest US$2 billion to build an advanced semiconductor packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This new plant will create up to 2,000 jobs and is expected to start production within two to three years, primarily packaging chips made for Apple by TSMC in Phoenix. The investment aims to bolster the US’s chipmaking capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign packaging capacity, and has applied for Chips Act funding.

title: Sonos roadmap reveals multiple audio launches intended in 2024
summary: Sonos is planning an "aggressive" roadmap of new audio products and software updates for 2024, including new in-ceiling speakers, headphones, a second-generation Roam, a high-end amplifier, and a high-end soundbar. A set-top box and subwoofer are also planned for late 2024 or 2025 releases. The company aims to revive sales through these launches, despite facing skepticism in competitive markets.

title: This Growth-Focused ETF Makes for an Excellent Long-Term Investment
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) is highlighted as an excellent long-term investment, having seen a 46% gain this year due to its focus on growth stocks. QQQ tracks the NASDAQ-100 Index, which includes 100 of the largest non-financial companies on the NASDAQ, with a heavy concentration in technology giants like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The ETF offers investors diversified exposure within the tech sector and has historically shown strong performance, more than doubling in value over five years, making it suitable for maximizing returns.

title: 美股科技股“疯牛”行情未完待续? AI仍是引爆明年行情的关键
summary: Wedbush機構預計美股科技股在2024年將上漲至少20%，主要得益於人工智能（AI）和雲計算支出的大幅增長。分析師將AI比作互聯網的“1995時刻”，預計未來十年AI支出規模將達到1萬億美元，並點名英偉達和微軟將引領這一趨勢。儘管宏觀經濟不確定性存在，Wedbush仍堅定看好科技行業，並預計納斯達克100指數明年在高科技巨頭帶領下將大漲20%以上。

title: Amex CEO talks co-brand partnerships after Apple reports
summary: American Express CEO Steve Squeri discussed co-branded partnerships, emphasizing that Amex's "premium" card designation means it doesn't align with all potential partners, a point highlighted amidst reports of Amex potentially replacing Goldman Sachs as Apple's credit card partner. Squeri noted that Amex seeks partnerships that offer strong value propositions, target premium customers, and provide distribution while yielding "premium economics." Analysts have speculated on several potential suitors for Apple's card partnership, including Amex, JPMorgan Chase, Synchrony Financial, Capital One, and Bread.

title: Why Investors Should Keep An Eye on Small-Cap Stocks
summary: This article discusses how the increasing concentration of market-cap-weighted indexes, particularly driven by large-cap tech stocks, is creating historical levels of concentration. It explores the implications for the size premium, suggesting that higher market concentration leads to increased expected returns for small-cap stocks due to capital misallocation and reduced investor demand for smaller firms, making them an attractive area for investors.

title: Who’s ready for a Gamestop mutual fund? Not this broker
summary: Wedbush analysts are critical of GameStop's decision to delegate investment authority to CEO Ryan Cohen, allowing him to invest the company's capital in equities and other investments. Analyst Michael Pachter suggests that if GameStop believes in its own shares, it should use excess cash for buybacks instead. The move is compared to AMC's "embarrassingly stupid" investment in Hycroft Mining, which saw significant losses.

title: Tesla’s Dojo Supercomputer Head Exits in Blow to Efforts
summary: Ganesh Venkataramanan, the head of Tesla's Dojo supercomputer project, has left the company after five years, posing a significant setback to the automaker's self-driving technology endeavors. Peter Bannon, a long-time Tesla director and former Apple executive, has taken over leadership of the project. This departure comes as Tesla plans to invest over $1 billion in Dojo by the end of 2024, a project analysts believe could add $500 billion to Tesla's valuation.

title: TIDAL cuts 10% of staff as parent company Block enforces cost-saving measures
summary: Music streaming service TIDAL has laid off 10% of its workforce, impacting about 40 employees, as part of parent company Block Inc.'s cost-saving measures. This move aligns with Block CEO Jack Dorsey's November letter to investors, stating the company's intent to cap payroll at 12,000 employees. The layoffs at TIDAL reflect a broader trend of staff reductions across the tech and music industries, following similar cuts by companies like Spotify, Amazon, Google, and Meta.

title: CastleArk Enters ETF Market With Large Growth ETF CARK
summary: CastleArk has launched its first exchange-traded fund, the CastleArk Large Growth ETF (CARK), an actively managed ETF focused on large-cap growth companies. The fund targets 25-30 leading growth companies, identifying competitive strength and seeking to optimize risk through quantitative inputs. CastleArk aims to provide an actively managed alternative to index-heavy portfolios, especially given the concentration of "Magnificent Seven" stocks in the large-cap growth sector.

title: Just 3 of the "Magnificent Seven" Have Outperformed the S&P 500 Since 2022. Here's the One I'd Buy for 2024 and 2025.
summary: This article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks against the S&P 500 since 2022. It identifies Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple as the only three that have outperformed the S&P 500. The author argues that Alphabet, despite underperforming recently, is the best buy for 2024 and 2025 due to its valuation, potential in digital advertising, and cloud segment growth.

title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:WISE)
summary: The Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE) tracks an index of companies involved in AI-related industries, with a concentrated portfolio of 89 holdings. Launched on December 8, 2023, the ETF currently has $33.55 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.35%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Advanced Micro Devices, Intel, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: 'You have to be difficult': How Gap and J.Crew icon Mickey Drexler became the king of mall-brand retail
summary: This article details how Mickey Drexler, a prominent figure in retail, transformed Gap and revitalized J.Crew, known for his unconventional leadership. He also founded Old Navy and Madewell, served on Apple's board, and heavily influenced Alex Mill, a company started by his son. Drexler is characterized by his micromanagement style and significant impact on several successful brands.

title: The Nasdaq-100 Has Had a Monster 2023 So Far. Is It Too Late to Invest in It Before 2024 Arrives?
summary: The Nasdaq-100 has seen a significant rally of 45% in 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming other major U.S. indexes. Despite this strong performance, the article suggests that it's not too late to invest, emphasizing a long-term approach and dollar-cost averaging to mitigate short-term volatility. It highlights the Invesco QQQ ETF as a common way to invest in the Nasdaq-100, composed of innovative companies poised for continued growth in industries like AI, EVs, and biotech.

title: Apple supplier Corning to set up Rs 1,000 crore Gorilla Glass facility in Tamil Nadu
summary: Corning Inc., a key Apple supplier and Gorilla Glass manufacturer, plans to invest Rs 1,000 crore in a new manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu, India. This facility, a collaboration with Indian partner Optiemus Infracom, will be located near other Apple suppliers and is expected to start operations in 2024, initially producing 30 million pieces of cover glass for smartphones. The move comes after Corning chose Tamil Nadu over Telangana, seeking central government incentives under the SPECS scheme.

title: How Google’s Epic Legal Defeat Threatens the App-Store Industry’s $200 Billion Business Model
summary: Google's recent legal defeat against Epic Games threatens the $200 billion app store industry, which includes Apple. A jury found Google's app store practices anticompetitive, which could lead to significant changes in commission rates and developer options. This verdict could also impact Apple's similar business model, despite its previous victory against Epic, as regulators and lawmakers globally scrutinize app store rules and consumer sentiment leans against the power of tech giants.

title: Buffett's Berkshire Slashes Losing HP Bet by Nearly 60% in 3 Months
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has significantly reduced its stake in HP by 57% since mid-September, selling off nearly 60% of its holdings and cutting its ownership from over 12% to about 5%. This move comes as HP has been a losing bet for Berkshire, with the firm selling shares below its initial purchase price from early 2022. Berkshire has been actively paring back its stock portfolio recently, accumulating a record cash pile.

title: DGRW Has Hallmarks of a Great Dividend ETF
summary: The WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) is highlighted as a strong dividend ETF due to its focus on companies' return on assets and return on equity, rather than just yield. This strategy has allowed DGRW to outperform the S&P 500 and other dividend indexes, especially in high-interest-rate environments. The ETF's approach aims for reduced volatility, potential capital appreciation, and diversification, including a significant allocation to tech stocks.

title: The Apple Inc (AAPL) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Apple Inc. (AAPL) to determine if the stock is undervalued. Based on the DCF analysis, Apple's stock appears overvalued with a DCF value of $118.68 per share compared to a current market price of $198.08, resulting in a negative margin of safety of -66.91%. The author emphasizes that this is a preliminary analysis and investors should conduct further research.

title: US Chamber, Comcast, tech group split over net neutrality
summary: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Comcast Corp are opposing the Federal Communications Commission's proposal to reinstate net neutrality rules, arguing it would harm investment and innovation. Conversely, major tech firms like Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms support the reinstatement to preserve open internet access. The FCC's move to regain regulatory oversight, following Democrats taking majority control, aims to ensure a "fast, open, and fair" internet, while some states also have existing net neutrality laws.

title: Focus: Exxon's low US tax payments ruffle Biden's climate agenda
summary: Exxon Mobil's income tax payments to the U.S. government have significantly decreased to 3% over the past five years, mainly due to deductions passed under former President Donald Trump, frustrating the Biden administration's climate agenda. This low tax rate, much lower than major companies in other sectors, highlights how current tax codes impede efforts to fund green energy initiatives. Experts suggest Exxon could maintain these low tax rates for several more years, even as the government seeks more revenue for climate action, partly because President Biden's new minimum corporate tax struggles with implementation and still accounts for the Trump-era accelerated depreciation deductions.

title: Call translation services new battleground in AI race
summary: The coming year will see a surge in real-time phone call translation services driven by AI, with Korean tech firms like SK Telecom and Samsung Electronics entering the fray. SK Telecom is launching its "A. call translator" service within its existing AI app, supporting four languages initially and expanding to 11. Samsung, conversely, plans to integrate its generative AI model "Samsung Gauss" directly into its new Galaxy S24 phones, offering on-device translation without external internet access, emphasizing privacy and reduced latency.

title: Apple Races to Tweak Software Ahead of Looming US Watch Ban
summary: Apple Inc. is rapidly working on software adjustments to its Apple Watch series to circumvent a looming U.S. ban. The changes primarily target the blood oxygen level measurement feature, which a company called Masimo Corp. claims infringes on its patents. These modifications include altering algorithms for oxygen saturation detection and data presentation to consumers.

title: Qorvo to sell Chinese assembly, test factories to Luxshare ...
summary: Qorvo Inc. has agreed to sell its assembly and test facilities in Beijing and Dezhou, China, to Luxshare Precision Industry Co. Ltd. The deal includes Luxshare acquiring the facilities' assets, operations, and workforce, while Qorvo will continue to be supplied through a long-term agreement. This move allows Qorvo to streamline its manufacturing network while Luxshare expands its role in the electronics manufacturing services sector, particularly within the Apple supply chain.

title: Apple terminates credit-card and savings partnership with Goldman Sachs
summary: Apple Inc. has initiated the termination of its credit card and savings account partnership with Goldman Sachs Group Inc., proposing to end the collaboration within 12 to 15 months. This move follows Goldman Sachs' struggles with its consumer banking division, which has incurred losses despite the strong performance of Apple Card and Apple Savings. Apple is now seeking a new banking provider, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. and American Express Company emerging as top contenders.

title: Why Apple Had to Disable the Blood-Oxygen Sensor in Its Watches
summary: Apple has begun selling new versions of its latest Apple Watch models with the blood oxygen sensing feature disabled. This action stems from a patent dispute with Masimo Corp., a medical technology company, which successfully argued that Apple violated its patents. A US International Trade Commission ruling and subsequent appeals court decision led Apple to remove the feature rather than face a sales ban on its Watch Series 9 and Ultra 2 models.

title: Meta: Sell it? Buy it?
summary: Meta's stock price has experienced significant volatility in recent years, oscillating from a high of $384 in August 2021 to below $100 in October 2022, before rebounding to around $340. This article explores the company's journey from being considered a "sell" to a "buy," focusing on challenges such as Apple's privacy policy changes and competition from TikTok, and how Meta has responded by investing heavily in AI and re-evaluating its metaverse strategy. The future success of Meta is tied to its AI applications across its businesses, especially in advertising and new ventures.

title: U.S. Fitness Tracker Market Size, Growth and Trends [2030]
summary: The U.S. fitness tracker market was valued at USD 13.69 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach a significant size by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 18.9% during the forecast period. This growth is driven by the increasing prevalence of chronic diseases, high adoption of fitness monitoring devices, and technological advancements like integration with smartphones and advanced features. Despite potential inaccuracies, the market sees strong demand, particularly for smartwatches, with key players continuously innovating to meet consumer needs.

title: Why These 3 Semiconductor Stocks Should Be on Your Radar in 2024
summary: The article highlights three semiconductor stocks—Qualcomm (QCOM), United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), and Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS)—that are poised for growth in 2024, driven by advancements in AI, electric vehicles, and 5G technology. It evaluates each company's market position, financial health, and growth catalysts, emphasizing their undervaluation and innovative contributions to the industry. The piece suggests these stocks offer high risk-reward opportunities for investors looking to capitalize on the expanding semiconductor market.

title: Intellexa and Cytrox: From fixer-upper to Intel Agency-grade spyware
summary: Cisco Talos has conducted an in-depth analysis of Intellexa, a spyware vendor previously known as Cytrox, detailing its operational paradigms, procedures, and the evolution of its Predator spyware. The analysis reveals that Intellexa's Predator spyware continues to evade detection, offers persistence even after device reboots, and demonstrates a sophisticated business model with high-priced solutions targeting state-sponsored agencies. Despite public disclosures and the US adding Intellexa to its Entity List, the company effectively continues its operations by adapting to patched exploits and maintaining plausible deniability through its sales and delivery methods.

title: QARP ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:QARP
summary: This article provides an analysis of the Xtrackers Russell 1000 US Quality at a Reasonable Price ETF (QARP), detailing its investment strategy, holdings, and performance. QARP focuses on US large-cap companies with a blend of quality and value metrics, giving twice as much weight to quality. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.19% and aims to provide exposure to the Russell 1000 Slowdown Factor Index.

title: PLDT warns public on AI-generated scams
summary: PLDT and Smart Communications are warning the public about the rise of AI-generated scams, which are becoming increasingly sophisticated and convincing. Criminals are leveraging AI to create polished messages and visuals that mimic official communications, making it harder for individuals to distinguish genuine messages from fraudulent ones. PLDT advises checking the source of emails and texts and adhering to the basic rule of not communicating with strangers to avoid falling victim to these scams.

title: Apple files appeal after Biden administration allows US ban on watch imports
summary: Apple has appealed a U.S. import ban on its watches, which stems from a patent dispute with medical monitoring company Masimo. The ban, allowed by the Biden administration, targets Apple Watches with blood-oxygen level features. Apple has also requested an emergency halt to the ban while awaiting a decision from U.S. Customs and Border Protection on redesigned versions of its watches.

title: T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF): A Promising Active Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF) has shown significant growth since its launch in June 2023, accumulating over $600 million in assets. The fund focuses on high-quality U.S. large-cap companies, employing a bottom-up stock selection approach with top holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. TCAF's investment philosophy emphasizes strong performance in areas like technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary.

title: How I used the Garmin epix Pro to train for Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend
summary: The author details their experience using the Garmin epix Pro to train for and complete Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend, which involves finishing three races (Sprint, Super, Beast) in one weekend. The article highlights how the smartwatch's features like training readiness, body battery, heart rate monitoring, and race prediction were crucial for their rigorous training regimen and successful completion of the demanding events. The author expresses that the Garmin epix Pro not only enhanced their athletic performance but also motivated them toward healthier habits and served as a worthy investment in their fitness journey.

title: Here Are All 49 Stocks Warren Buffett Holds for Berkshire Hathaway's $371 Billion Portfolio
summary: This article details the 49 stocks held in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, valued at $371 billion. It highlights that over 85% of the assets are concentrated in eight major holdings like Apple and Bank of America, while also listing other billion-dollar investments and smaller holdings attributed to Buffett's lieutenants. The piece emphasizes Buffett's preference for financial and cyclical businesses, his valuation-driven approach, and recent investments in homebuilders.

title: Apple's stock underperformed top tech peers in 2023 due to longest revenue slide in 22 years
summary: Apple's stock underperformed its mega-cap tech peers in 2023, experiencing its longest revenue slide in 22 years with four consecutive quarters of declining sales. This decline was attributed to a combination of a weak economic environment for smartphones and PCs, and company-specific issues such as the lack of new iPad models and an Apple Watch sales ban. Despite a 49% stock rally for the year, Apple lagged behind competitors like Nvidia, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft.

title: What to expect from Sonos in 2024 – headphones, new Arc and a big surprise
summary: In 2024, Sonos is set to expand its product line significantly, venturing into new categories like premium over-ear headphones and potentially a streaming video box. The company also plans major upgrades to its existing portfolio, including an advanced voice control system (Voice 2.0), a second-generation Roam, and a new flagship Sonos Arc soundbar. These moves follow a challenging financial year for Sonos, indicating a strategy focused on growth and diversification.

title: Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:ZZZ)
summary: The Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF (ZZZ) is an exchange-traded fund launched on December 28, 2023, by Onefund, which invests in a 75/25 blend of US large-cap stocks and front-month CME Bitcoin futures. It does not directly invest in Bitcoin's spot price. The ETF has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy," assets under management of $6.02 million, and a net expense ratio of 1.01%.

title: Apple Pay
summary: This article from Barclays Private Bank details how to use Apple Pay with Barclays debit cards. It covers the benefits of Apple Pay such as fast, secure, and contactless payments for any amount, and provides instructions for adding cards, setting up, paying in-store, online, and in-app, as well as managing transactions and cards. Eligibility requirements for using Apple Pay with Barclays are also outlined.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing to delay chip production in Phoenix for a year. Here's why
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) announced a one-year delay in the start of chip production at its Phoenix facility, pushing the timeline to 2025 due to a lack of skilled employees for specialized equipment installation. The company also anticipates higher production costs in Phoenix compared to Taiwan and is sending more specialized workers to address the issues. This delay comes as TSMC reported its first quarterly profit decline in nearly four years amid lower demand for consumer electronics.

title: The Cheap Trick Alphabet Is Using To Boost Its Share Price
summary: Alphabet's 20-for-1 stock split, following similar moves by Apple and Tesla, is unlikely to fundamentally change the company's valuation but aims to make shares more accessible to smaller investors and potentially gain entry into the Dow Jones Industrial Average. While some tech giants saw significant share price boosts post-split, Alphabet's future success might be tempered by changing market conditions. The article suggests Amazon could be the next tech giant to undertake a similar split to boost its stock.

title: How Much Longer Can US Stocks Keep Beating The World?
summary: US stocks have outperformed global markets for nearly 14 years, a period attributed to tech giants and resulting in higher valuations compared to other developed economies. The article questions the sustainability of this dominance and suggests ETFs for investors who anticipate a shift, allowing exposure to non-US developed markets.

title: Apple plans to expand retail business into China
summary: Apple is planning a significant expansion and revitalization of its retail chain, with 53 new, relocated, or remodeled stores globally over the next four years. The focus is heavily on the Asia-Pacific region, particularly China and India, while also upgrading locations in the US and Europe. This strategy aims to strengthen Apple's brand in growth markets and improve the customer experience.

title: Sophia Velastegui
summary: Sophia Velastegui is Senior Vice President and Chief Product Officer of Aptiv, an automotive and autonomous tech company, where she drives innovation and growth. She previously held significant roles at Microsoft, Google/Alphabet, Apple, and Applied Materials, and is recognized for her board directorships and contributions to AI and emerging technologies. Velastegui is an award-winning engineer with degrees from Georgia Tech and UC Berkeley, and has furthered her corporate governance expertise at Stanford Law and Harvard Business School.

title: The Financial Success of Apple Inc.
summary: This article examines the financial success of Apple Inc., focusing on key financial indicators such as net income, operating cash flow, free cash flow, and capital structure. It highlights Apple's consistent growth in profitability and operational efficiency, demonstrating how the company manages investments and financing to maintain sustainable growth. The article concludes that Apple's strong financial position, driven by innovative products and effective financial management, solidifies its leadership in the competitive technology industry.

title: IYW: Bill Gates Bought This Tech ETF. Should You?
summary: This article examines Bill Gates' recent acquisition of the iShares U.S. Technology ETF (IYW) in the third quarter, as revealed by 13F filings. It highlights IYW's strong long-term performance, comprehensive portfolio of top U.S. tech companies, and analyst ratings, while also noting its expense ratio compared to peers. The piece suggests that Gates' investment indicates continued opportunity in the tech sector.

title: Qualcomm, Iridium Take Aim at Satellite-to-Mobile Phone Market
summary: Qualcomm and Iridium Communications are collaborating to introduce technology enabling satellite messaging for smartphone users in areas without cellular service. This partnership will initially offer a satellite-based emergency service for Android devices equipped with Qualcomm's premium chips. The move aims to bring satellite connectivity to a broader market, similar to the feature available on newer iPhones.

title: Apple Inc bets big on India as it opens first flagship store
summary: Apple Inc. has opened its first flagship store in Mumbai, India, marking a significant expansion into the world's second-largest smartphone market. CEO Tim Cook was present for the launch, emphasizing the company's commitment to India as both a growing market and a potential manufacturing hub. This move aligns with India's government efforts to boost local manufacturing, with Apple aiming for 25% of its global production to come from India within five years.

title: Miami doesn’t need to be the Silicon Valley of the South
summary: Miami has been hyped as a "new Silicon Valley" or "Silicon Valley of the South," but this article argues it should be recognized for its unique strengths rather than compared to Silicon Valley. The author highlights Miami's advantages, including favorable tax laws, a supportive tech ecosystem, increasing VC attention, strong foreign investment, a focus on AI, and its role as a bridge to Latin America for nearshoring. Miami's distinct features allow it to stand as its own specialized tech hub.

title: Markets in Focus: Concentrating on Diversification
summary: The article discusses the increasing concentration in equity markets, exemplified by the "Magnificent Seven" stocks, and the diminished diversification benefits of bonds, especially since 2020. It suggests that investors reassess top-down allocations and broaden equity exposure across diverse regions, sectors, and styles to create more resilient portfolios. The MSCI Adaptive Multi-Factor Allocation Model indicates an overweight to value and low size as of December 31, 2023, with Europe and China trading at lower historical valuations.

title: Mindlessly scroll on bike? ‘Tiktok fitness powered by Peloton’, stock soars
summary: Peloton has announced a partnership with TikTok to create a new fitness hub called #TikTokFitness, powered by Peloton. This collaboration aims to bring short-form fitness videos, live classes, instructor series, and celebrity collaborations to TikTok's vast audience. Following the announcement, Peloton's stock shares rose by 7%, signaling a positive market reaction to their efforts to recover from recent financial downturns, including another recent partnership with YouTube TV.

title: Google and Samsung Set To Use Qualcomm's New VR Chip, Could Compete With Apple and Meta
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) recently unveiled its new Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 2 Platform chip for virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR). Google (GOOGL) and Samsung are slated to integrate this chip into their upcoming VR/AR experiences, aiming to challenge market leaders like Apple (AAPL) and Meta Platforms (META). This development could intensify competition in the growing VR headset market, especially following Apple's Vision Pro and Meta's dominance in 2023.

title: FNGU ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FNGU
summary: This article provides an analysis of the MicroSectors FANG+ 3X Leveraged ETNs (FNGU), detailing its key statistics such as Assets Under Management ($1.47 billion), expense ratio (2.60%), and the fact that it does not pay dividends. It highlights FNGU's structure as a geared product with daily resets, making it suitable for short-term trading rather than long-term investment due to compounding effects. The ETF offers 3x exposure to an index of FANG companies and other similar businesses, with constituents being equally weighted.

title: Aeva to supply sensors to Daimler Truck in $1 billion deal
summary: Aeva Technologies has secured a $1 billion deal to supply lidar sensors to Daimler Truck for self-driving trucks, which are anticipated to be on U.S. roads by 2027. Aeva's sensors are designed to detect the speed of distant objects, aiding in collision avoidance. The agreement underscores continued interest in robotic trucking for long-haul routes despite challenges faced by self-driving passenger vehicle technology.

title: FTI Consulting adds trio to Strategic Communications team
summary: FTI Consulting has expanded its Strategic Communications practice in India by hiring Salonie Chawla as a Director, Snehil Singh as a Senior Consultant, and Shruti Vakhariya as a Consultant. These new additions bring diverse experience from government engagement, public policy, and financial communications to strengthen the team's capabilities in handling complex policy challenges and industry-side advisory for clients. The firm anticipates increased demand for such expertise given the disruptive business landscape expected in the coming 24 months.

title: Best Socially Responsible Investment Apps of January 2024
summary: This article highlights the best socially responsible investment (SRI) apps for January 2024, detailing their features, pros, and cons. It explains what SRI is, differentiates it from ESG investing, and provides expert insights on the advantages and disadvantages of using these apps. The article reviews top platforms like IMPACT, Betterment, Sustainfolio, and Ellevest, among others, to help investors align their financial goals with their ethical values.
authors: Rickie Houston|Elias Shaya and Tessa Campbell

title: Software Glitch Behind Recalled App Could Drastically Increase Insulin Delivery
summary: A software glitch in a recalled app has the potential to drastically increase insulin delivery, posing a significant health risk. This issue was highlighted on January 10, 2024, by Brian Bossetta.

title: SKYX’s All-in-One Smart Platform is Everything from a Smart Speaker to a Smoke Detector
summary: SKYX Platforms Corp. debuted its patented All-in-One Smart Platform at CES 2024, aiming to make existing homes technologically advanced. This platform integrates multiple smart home features like a smoke/CO detector, smart speaker, Wi-Fi repeater, and various lighting controls, all controllable via app and voice commands. The product installs easily into a ceiling and supports major smart home ecosystems, with SKYX also renewing a global licensing agreement with GE.

title: Iridium Is Offering Its Own Smartphone-to-Satellite Service to Companies
summary: Iridium has launched its own smartphone-to-satellite service, "Project Stardust," after its partnership with Qualcomm ended. This new service will allow original equipment manufacturers and carriers to offer text messaging and emergency SOS capabilities through Iridium's global low Earth orbit satellite constellation, adhering to 3GPP 5G standards. Initially focusing on data messaging, Iridium plans to begin testing in 2025 with service expected to start in 2026.

title: Bracken Darrell reimagined some of the world's biggest brands. Now he has plans to do it again
summary: Bracken Darrell, the interim CEO of Vans and former CEO of Logitech, is known for his ability to reinvent brands through design. Now six months into his role at VF Corp (owner of brands like Vans, North Face, and Supreme), he aims to apply his turnaround expertise to the company. Darrell previously revitalized Logitech's product line and culture, emphasizing the importance of design in corporate success.

title: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management Expand T-REX Single-Stock ETF Suite with Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft Offerings
summary: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management have expanded their T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite by introducing new offerings for Apple (CBOE: AAPX), Alphabet (CBOE: GOOX), and Microsoft (CBOE: MSFX). These ETFs provide seasoned traders with 200% daily targeted exposure to these major tech companies. The expansion aims to offer advanced tools for precise positioning in the tech giants, building on the success of earlier NVIDIA and Tesla single-stock ETFs which have attracted over $100 million in assets since their October 2023 launch.

title: Upcoming Events
summary: REX Shares, in collaboration with Tuttle Capital Management, has expanded its T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite with new offerings providing 200% daily targeted exposure to Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft. These ETFs (AAPX, GOOX, MSFX) are designed for seasoned traders to give leveraged exposure to major tech stocks. The T-REX suite, which debuted in October 2023, now covers diverse tech sectors and has already attracted over $100 million in assets.

title: Taiwan's strength in semiconductors could be its 'Achilles heel,' economist says
summary: An economist warns that Taiwan's heavy reliance on semiconductor exports, particularly to China, could become its "Achilles heel." Paul Cavey of East Asia Econ advises Taiwan to diversify its economy, as China's growing capacity for chip production could significantly impact Taiwan's economic future. This vulnerability is further exacerbated by the ongoing U.S.-China chip war and efforts by Western countries to restrict China's access to advanced chip technology.

title: Optimizing Communication and Data Sharing in Multi-Core SoC Designs
summary: This article discusses the evolution of interconnects in Systems-on-Chip (SoCs), from shared buses to Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures, highlighting their role in managing data flow and enhancing performance. It delves into the importance of cache coherence for data consistency in multi-core designs and the challenges engineers face in optimizing communication and data sharing as semiconductor technology advances. The article also mentions Arteris' Ncore Cache Coherent Interconnect as a solution.

title: Jabil announces collaboration with Fintech Leader Revolut
summary: Jabil Inc. announced its ongoing collaboration with Revolut, a digital banking pioneer, to support the neobank's growth and global expansion in merchant acquiring. Jabil provides custom, pre-certified Point-of-Sale (POS) hardware platforms, including the Revolut Reader, which enables merchants to accept various in-store and on-the-go payments. The partnership highlights Jabil's role in accelerating market entry and providing comprehensive product design, development, and manufacturing solutions for Revolut's innovative payment solutions.

title: Microsoft Monetizing Copilot, Round 2; Synopsys Pays Big for Ansys
summary: Microsoft is expanding the monetization of its AI-powered Copilot by removing the minimum seat requirement for Microsoft 365 Copilot subscriptions, allowing single users to purchase it. Simultaneously, Synopsys has agreed to acquire Ansys in a significant $35 billion cash and stock deal, marking a major merger in the chip design and engineering simulation software industries for 2024.

title: Unlocking The Potential Of Pure Research At InterDigital
summary: This article examines InterDigital's role as a pure research facility, led by CEO Liren Chen, and its significant contributions to wireless technology, including current work on 5G advanced and future plans for 6G with AI integration. It highlights InterDigital's impressive growth, long-standing partnerships with companies like Apple and Amazon, and its unique approach to fostering innovation among its largely inventor-based workforce. Chen emphasizes the importance of curiosity, competency, and impact in driving both individual and company success, showcasing InterDigital's continued influence on global digital communication.

title: Supreme Court rejects Apple's appeal on App Store fees amid Epic Games legal battle
summary: The Supreme Court has rejected Apple's appeal regarding its App Store fees, allowing a lower court order to take effect. This decision could force Apple to permit app developers to offer alternative payment options, potentially reducing Apple's commission revenue. The ruling stems from an antitrust lawsuit filed by Epic Games, although Epic's broader antitrust claims against Apple were also rejected.

title: IDC: Apple sold more smartphones than Samsung for the first time in 2023
summary: Apple Inc. surpassed Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. to become the world's largest smartphone manufacturer by unit shipments in 2023, according to a preliminary report by IDC. This marks the first time since 2010 that Samsung did not hold the top position. Despite an overall decline in global smartphone shipments for the year, Apple saw an increase in iPhone sales, particularly strong in the fourth quarter, whereas Samsung experienced a decline.

title: Disney+ On Apple Vision Pro Ushers In A New Era Of Storytelling Innovation And Immersive Entertainment
summary: Beginning February 2, Disney+ will be available on Apple Vision Pro, offering subscribers a new immersive streaming experience. Users can transform their spaces into iconic Disney environments and watch dozens of popular movies in 3D, leveraging advanced technology developed through the collaboration of Disney and Apple teams. This collaboration aims to bring fans closer to beloved stories and characters through groundbreaking technology.

title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Apple Inc.'s stock experienced a 0.52% decline, closing at $182.68 on Wednesday, marking its second consecutive day of losses. This occurred during an overall negative trading session where the NASDAQ Composite fell by 0.59% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.25%. Despite the daily loss, the article title suggests Apple's stock performance still outpaced the broader market.

title: What Does 2024 Hold for the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" stocks, including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla, drove 73% of stock market gains in 2023 due to superior earnings growth and profit margins. While some analysts predict a pullback in 2024 due to potential overvaluation and cyclicality, others believe their strong forecasted earnings growth could lead to continued outperformance, especially while interest rates remain elevated. The article highlights contrasting fortunes early in 2024, with Nvidia surging and Tesla facing its worst January since its IPO.

title: 5 key themes from the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show
summary: The 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) highlighted five key technological themes: AI as a pervasive enabler across all tech, advancements in smart home devices with unified communication standards like Matter, the evolution of immersive technology anticipating the Apple Vision Pro, the future of the automotive industry focusing on driver experience and electric vehicles, and the widening scope of health technology, including AI-powered mental wellness tools and wearables. These innovations provide a glimpse into the technological future.

title: Your S&P 500 Index Fund Has Higher Risk Than You May Think
summary: This article argues that the S&P 500 index fund, traditionally seen as diversified, now carries higher risk due to its concentration in a few technology stocks. It highlights concentration, valuation, and inflation risks, suggesting investors re-evaluate their S&P 500 holdings and consider more value-oriented, truly diversified investments.

title: More Computer Magazine Lore: A 1983 Nastygram from Wayne Green
summary: This article provides additional lore surrounding computer magazines, focusing on Wayne Green, a colorful and controversial figure in tech publishing. It details his eccentric career, his contentious relationship with figures like Steve Jobs and David Bunnell, and his impact on early computer magazines like Byte, PC World, and Macworld. The post highlights a "nastygram" Green sent in response to PC World's premiere issue, shedding light on the competitive landscape of the 1980s computer magazine industry.

title: Higher S&P 500 Earnings Could Benefit This Active ETF
summary: The NEOS S&P 500 High Income ETF (SPYI) is poised to benefit from expected higher corporate earnings in 2024, as forecasted by Goldman Sachs. SPYI, an actively managed ETF, focuses on call options and holds "Magnificent Seven" stocks, offering investors tailor-made holdings, risk mitigation, and tax-efficient monthly income. Its active management removes the need for constant supervision and provides a single solution for a core equity portfolio.

title: Corning's Indian JV signs $120 mln investment deal with Tamil Nadu state
summary: Corning's Indian joint venture, Bharat Innovative Glass Technologies, has signed an agreement with the Tamil Nadu state to invest 10 billion rupees ($120.4 million) to establish a new facility. This plant will manufacture front cover glass for mobile phones and phone parts, highlighting India's growing importance as a manufacturing hub amid a global shift in supply chains away from China. The investment is part of larger efforts by the Indian government to promote local manufacturing and has attracted other major electronics manufacturers and Apple suppliers.

title: The Best Desktop PCs: Early 2024
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to the best pre-built desktop PCs available in early 2024, categorizing them for different needs such as gaming, all-in-one solutions, and mini PCs. It offers detailed recommendations including specific models like the Corsair Vengeance for gaming, HP Envy 34 AIO for creative work, and Apple Mac Mini M2 for compactness. The guide also discusses considerations between building a PC and buying pre-built, and factors like value, upgradeability, and space constraints.

title: ‘Find My®’ Alternative Guide
summary: The article provides a guide to alternatives to Apple's "Find My" app. It highlights Life360 as a location sharing and family safety application.

title: Behind Target’s ‘Winning Retail Formula’
summary: Target Corp. actively expanded and renovated its stores in 2023, opening 21 new locations and remodeling over 160. The company's strategy focuses on a superior shopping experience through large-format stores, expanded assortments, enhanced fulfillment services, and strategic partnerships. Target plans further investments and new locations in 2024, continuing its growth trajectory in the retail sector.

title: Apple device management software company Jamf plans to layoff 6% of employees
summary: Jamf, an Apple device management software company, announced a workforce reduction plan to lay off approximately 6% of its full-time employees. This move is intended to reduce operating costs, improve operating margins, and achieve profitable growth. The company expects to incur between $6.6 and $8.2 million in charges for severance and related expenses, primarily in the first quarter of 2024.

title: Companies are struggling to keep private data safe from generative AI, Cisco says
summary: A new Cisco report indicates that despite corporate bans and restrictions on generative AI tools, employees are still entering private company data into them. The survey of 2,600 privacy and security professionals found that a significant number of companies have either banned or limited AI usage due to concerns about data leaks and intellectual property infringement. This highlights a struggle for companies to balance the benefits of AI with the imperative to protect sensitive information.

title: 10 Best Project Management Software for Windows
summary: This article reviews the 10 best project management software solutions compatible with Windows, comparing their features, pricing, and specific strengths suitable for different team needs. It highlights tools like monday.com for customized workflows, Smartsheet for collaboration, Microsoft Project for scheduling, and Jira for software development, providing guidance on how to choose the right software based on budget, team size, and essential features. The article also addresses common FAQs regarding Windows project management tools, including Microsoft Project's capabilities and its transition to the upgraded Microsoft Planner.

title: Newegg Debuts Newegg Refreshed Program to Offer Quality Resale Products at Low Prices
summary: Newegg has launched "Newegg Refreshed," a new program offering refurbished tech products at competitive prices, including Apple goods, graphics cards, and gaming PCs. The program also integrates Newegg's graphics card trade-in service and provides a 90-day return guarantee. Newegg emphasizes both cost savings for customers and environmental benefits through product reuse.

title: AI & The Dragon: What Trends Will Dominate 2024?
summary: In 2024, Emerging Markets (EM) are expected to rebound, driven by potential Fed rate cuts and a weakening US dollar, with China and Brazil presenting more attractive valuations than the "Magnificent Seven" and other EM ex-China countries. The article suggests focusing on long-term trends and strategic allocations to specific growth sectors within EM. China's "Year of the Dragon" is anticipated to bring a gradual recovery in consumer sentiment and real estate, supported by government policies and significant expected EPS growth.

title: What Gentex’s Acquisition Of eSight Means For The Electronic Eyewear Market
summary: Gentex Corporation has acquired Toronto-based eSight, a move that could significantly impact the electronic eyewear market for the visually impaired. This acquisition follows a partnership that led to the eSight Go, a new electronic eyewear system that is more affordable, lighter, and offers improved performance compared to its predecessor. Gentex's expertise in electro-optical products and manufacturing capabilities is expected to drive down costs and enhance the technology for sight enhancement wearables.

title: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) Seeks FDA Nod for Next-Gen da Vinci 5
summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) has applied for FDA 510(k) clearance for its next-generation da Vinci 5 multiport surgical robot and expects approval and launch later this year. The company is actively preparing manufacturing facilities for the new system while also engaging regulators in Japan and South Korea for approval. This development comes as the company's shares lost value a year ago due to the absence of a new multiport system announcement.

title: Popular Inc. reports $541M in net income in 2023, $94.6M in 4Q
summary: Popular Inc. reported a net income of $541.3 million for 2023, down from $1.1 billion in 2022, with $94.6 million in the fourth quarter. These figures include a $71.4 million expense due to a special FDIC assessment. Excluding this assessment, adjusted net income for 2023 was $586.6 million, and CEO Ignacio Álvarez noted stable net interest income, loan growth, and strong client activity despite market disruptions.

title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock underperformed on Monday, closing down 0.36% at $191.73, despite a generally positive trading session for the broader market. This marked the fourth consecutive day of losses for Apple, contrasting with gains seen in the NASDAQ Composite Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

title: XLK ETF: A Better Way To Invest In Microsoft But Volatility Comes First (NYSEARCA:XLK)
summary: The Technology Select Sector ETF (XLK) has appreciated over 20% since October 2023, now trading above $200. The author maintains a positive outlook, attributing potential growth to top holdings like Microsoft and other software companies benefiting from AI's move into exploitation. Despite volatility risks from Fed policies and Apple's earnings, XLK is considered undervalued compared to Microsoft and Nvidia, with a target price around $223.

title: Gogoro Unveils New Flagship Smartscooter, Pulse
summary: Gogoro Inc. (Nasdaq: GGR) has unveiled its new flagship Smartscooter, the Pulse, which features an aerodynamic design and a hyperdrive powertrain enabling it to reach 0-50km/h in 3.05 seconds. The Pulse integrates advanced features such as an active-matrix lighting system, a 10.25-inch panoramic HD touch display powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon QWM2290 digital chassis, and Apple Wallet and Apple Find My integration. The Smartscooter is expected to begin shipping in Taiwan in late Q2 2024, aiming to set a new standard for high-performance electric two-wheelers.

title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares fell 1.92% to $188.04 on Tuesday, marking its fifth consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred on a mixed trading day, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the NASDAQ Composite Index falling. The article highlights Apple's stock decline relative to its competitors and broader market movements.

title: Corning beats quarterly sales estimates, signals improving demand for fiber optics
summary: Corning exceeded Wall Street's fourth-quarter adjusted sales estimates, driven by increasing demand for optical fiber cables used in data centers for generative AI applications. Despite a slight dip in optical communications segment sales, the company observes "green shoots" in the hyperscale data center space. Although Q4 earnings met expectations, Corning provided a conservative profit outlook for the current quarter, expecting it to be the low point of the year.

title: 2027 the Target for Transition Away from 1D GTIN, Toward Digital Link 2D Barcodes
summary: The packaging industry is targeting 2027 for a significant transition from the traditional 1D GTIN barcode to the more versatile Digital Link 2D barcode. This shift, driven by increasing consumer demand for information, regulatory requirements, and supply chain traceability needs, will enable a single QR code to provide different information to various stakeholders (consumers, retailers, supply chain partners). The "Sunrise 2027" program aims to prepare the necessary infrastructure, primarily software changes, for this industry-wide adoption.

title: Payments firm PayPal to reduce global workforce by 9% in 2024
summary: Payments firm PayPal Holdings is set to cut approximately 2,500 jobs, or 9% of its global workforce, in 2024 through layoffs and the elimination of open roles. This decision, announced by CEO Alex Chriss, aims to "right-size" the company for efficiency and profitable growth amidst competitive pressures and a need to improve margins. The move follows similar cost-cutting measures by competitor Block.

title: Tile vs. AirTag™: Which Bluetooth Tracker Should You Choose?
summary: This article asks the question of whether a Tile or an AirTag is the better Bluetooth tracker. However, the provided content is incomplete, only listing the title and a company name, so no further details can be extracted.

title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares rose 1.33% to $186.86 on Thursday, breaking a six-day losing streak. Despite this gain, the stock underperformed compared to the broader market, as the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw larger percentage increases on the same day.

title: Alcoa to supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminum, including metal from ELYSIS™ technology
summary: Alcoa will supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminum rod, including metal produced using the breakthrough ELYSIS™ technology, which eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from the traditional smelting process. Nexans will be the first cable manufacturer globally to utilize metal from this process in various cable types. This collaboration further strengthens the long-standing relationship between the two companies, with Alcoa already supplying Nexans with EcoLum™ low-carbon aluminum.

title: Box Brings Your Content to Life with the Box App for Apple Vision Pro
summary: Box Inc. has launched a new app for Apple Vision Pro, leveraging spatial computing to transform how users interact with content in the enterprise. The Box app for Apple Vision Pro offers immersive experiences for various industries, including construction, retail, manufacturing, and education. It provides secure collaboration and productivity features, integrating with Box's Content Cloud, and will be available for free on the App Store for visionOS on February 2, 2024.

title: Aptiv to cut stake in Motional, self-driving JV with Hyundai
summary: Aptiv, a US autonomous driving technology company, has decided to significantly reduce its stake in Motional, a self-driving joint venture with Hyundai Motor Group, and will cease injecting additional capital. Motional, which has accumulated 1 trillion won ($750 million) in deficits, had requested further funding from both partners. This decision puts pressure on Hyundai Motor Group to decide on its own additional capital injection, highlighting the ongoing financial and commercial challenges in the autonomous vehicle industry.

title: What Stock Investors Need to Know About Meta’s New Dividend
summary: Meta Platforms announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, alongside its fourth-quarter earnings, leading to a significant stock surge. While the dividend provides a more balanced capital return program, share repurchases will remain the primary method for returning cash to shareholders. The initial dividend yield is modest compared to peers and the broader market index, but Morningstar analysts expect it to grow.

title: Disney Heads into the Future on the Apple Vision Pro
summary: The Walt Disney Company is launching Disney+ on Apple's Vision Pro, marking a new chapter in immersive storytelling. This integration allows viewers to experience Disney+ content in virtual environments like Star Wars' Tatooine or Marvel's Avengers Tower, and watch 3D versions of popular films. The partnership highlights Disney's long-standing focus on blending technology with creativity to enhance entertainment experiences.

title: Apple, Skechers fall; Amazon, Deckers Outdoor rise, Friday, 2/2/2024
summary: On Friday, February 2, 2024, several major stocks experienced significant price changes. Apple and Skechers saw declines due to lukewarm forecasts, while Amazon, Deckers Outdoor, Meta Platforms, and Clorox rose following strong financial results or optimistic outlooks. Microchip Technology and Columbia Sportswear also faced drops after giving disappointing forecasts.

title: Vishay Unveils New High-Speed Photodiode for Wearable Devices
summary: Vishay Intertechnology has expanded its optoelectronics portfolio with the VEMD2074, a new high-speed silicon PIN photodiode designed for wearable devices. This photodiode offers improved visible light sensitivity, low capacitance, and quick switching times, making it ideal for accurate signal detection in applications like pulse oximetry and optical heart rate detection. Its small size, flexibility in sensor placement, and cost-effectiveness facilitate integration into compact and price-sensitive products such as smartwatches and earbuds.

title: Markets Brief: Will the ‘Magnificent Seven’ Stocks Keep Carrying the Market After Earnings?
summary: The article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" stocks after their latest earnings reports, noting that while most beat forecasts, market reactions were mixed due to high expectations. It discusses whether these tech giants can continue to drive market gains in 2024, given their current valuations and the potential for a broader market rally. The article also provides a detailed recap of individual company performances and analyst commentary.

title: These ETFs could help investors reduce Big Tech exposure
summary: Investors who are overly concentrated in Big Tech stocks are looking for ways to diversify their portfolios. Equal-weight ETFs like the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF and the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF are suggested as options to spread risk beyond top-heavy Magnificent Seven companies, as advisors note nervousness about Big Tech valuations.

title: What investors need to know about Meta’s new dividend
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, which will be paid on March 26 to shareholders of record on February 22. This move is seen as a complement to existing share repurchase programs, which will remain the primary method of returning capital to shareholders. While the market reacted positively, sending Meta's stock up 20%, the initial dividend yield of less than 0.5% is modest compared to its mega-cap peers and the Morningstar Dividend Composite Index.

title: ¿Cuáles son los CEDEAR más elegidos por los argentinos?
summary: Certificados de Depósito Argentinos (CEDEAR) are a popular investment option in Argentina for diversifying portfolios and hedging against peso devaluation by investing in foreign companies. In January, Coca-Cola (KO) was the most chosen CEDEAR, followed by Apple (AAPL) and Tesla (TSLA). Recent changes by Banco Comafi regarding conversion ratios aim to make CEDEARs more accessible to a wider range of investors by lowering entry prices.

title: ESPN, Fox, and Warner Bros. Discovery Team Up to Offer Joint Sports Streaming Service
summary: Walt Disney's ESPN, Fox, and Warner Bros. Discovery are collaborating to launch a joint sports streaming service for U.S. customers available in the fall. This new service aims to combine their sports offerings, including professional and college games, to better compete with rivals like Comcast and Apple who have seen success in sports streaming. The app will be available standalone or bundled with Disney+, Hulu, or Max, consolidating vast sports rights under one platform.

title: ITOT ETF: Investing Well Doesn’t Have to Be Complicated
summary: The iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT) offers a straightforward, low-cost way to invest in a broad range of U.S. companies, providing exposure to 90% of the total domestic stock market. With an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03% and a track record of strong double-digit annualized returns over the past decade, ITOT is presented as an effective long-term portfolio building block due to its wide diversification across 2,589 stocks. Despite its simplicity, it has delivered significant wealth building for investors since its inception in 2004.

title: ITOT ETF: Investing Well Doesn’t Have to Be Complicated
summary: The iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT) is presented as an effective, uncomplicated, and low-cost investment option for long-term growth. The article highlights ITOT's strong historical performance with double-digit annualized returns, wide diversification across 2,589 U.S. stocks, and an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03%. It emphasizes the fund's lack of concentration risk due to its extensive holdings and its ability to provide exposure beyond just large-cap stocks.

title: One Store attracts $50 mn investment from Digital Turbine
summary: South Korean app store One Store has secured a $50 million investment from US-based mobile platform company Digital Turbine, with an initial $10 million for new shares. This partnership aims to facilitate One Store's overseas expansion and business growth, leveraging Digital Turbine's global reach and app advertising services. The investment raises One Store's corporate value to over 920 billion won ($694 million), nearing the 1 trillion won mark, and follows previous strategic investments from DTCP and Krafton Inc.

title: ADT welcomes Silicon Valley veteran Gilles Drieu as new CTO
summary: ADT has appointed Gilles Drieu, a veteran of Silicon Valley tech giants like Google, Apple, and Adobe, as its new Chief Technology Officer. Drieu will work alongside Chief Product Officer Naveen Chhangani and Chief Information Officer Sam Jaddi to accelerate ADT's innovation in smart home security offerings. His experience includes leading the development of HTML5-enabled web browsers at Apple and pivotal work on Chromecast, Home, and Nest products at Google.

title: Apple defeats lawsuit claiming it overpaid CEO Tim Cook, others
summary: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit against Apple alleging overpayment of CEO Tim Cook and other top executives through miscalculated performance-based stock awards. The judge ruled that Apple's compensation disclosures met securities law requirements and found no proof of improper actions by the board, also noting the plaintiffs did not allow sufficient time for the board to address their objections before filing suit. The lawsuit, brought by a Teamsters-affiliated pension fund, claimed Apple overvalued restricted stock units in 2021 and 2022.

title: DLN: Large-Cap Monthly Dividend Payer Not So Hot
summary: The WisdomTree US LargeCap Dividend ETF (DLN) is rated a "Hold" due to its lower dividend yield and growth rate compared to other dividend-paying ETFs. While DLN offers monthly income and is well-diversified across approximately 300 companies, its current concentration in technology and large-cap focus present a limitation given the potential for future small-cap growth. The article suggests that while large-cap growth has dominated, changing market dynamics could favor value and small-cap opportunities.

title: Nvidia Overtakes Amazon in Market Value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon in market value, making it the fourth most valuable US-listed company earlier in the day. The chipmaker's rally, driven by exploding demand for AI chips, has seen its shares jump 46% this year. While Nvidia closed slightly below Amazon's valuation, the event marks a significant milestone fueled by the AI boom.

title: Meta settles lawsuit over haptic feedback tech in Quest headsets
summary: Immersion, a company specializing in haptic feedback technology, has settled a lawsuit with Meta over Meta's use of touch-feedback in its Quest VR headsets. The settlement involves Meta licensing Immersion's patents for its hardware, software, VR, and gaming products, though the terms were not disclosed. Immersion has a history of pursuing patent infringement lawsuits against major tech companies like Microsoft, Sony, Apple, and Valve.

title: Michael Burry Covers Bet Against Chips ETF, Buys Health and Tech Stocks
summary: Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management has changed its investment strategy, abandoning its bearish bet against semiconductor companies by closing its position against the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX). Instead, the firm nearly doubled its holdings to 25 positions, acquiring shares in health and technology sectors, and holds no bearish positions as of the end of 2023. Burry, known from "The Big Short," had previously bet against the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indexes.

title: Relx Share Price Spiked Up 3% but Later Fell on FY 2023 Results
